sugar: Preheat the oven to 350°F.

To make the compote, put the berries, caster sugar and 1 tbsp water in a saucepan over a medium-high heat. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring from time to time, until the berries are softened and syrupy. Set aside to cool.
Put the egg yolks and brown sugar in a large mixing bowl. Using an electric whisk, whisk on a high speed for 3 minutes.
Put the egg whites in a second large mixing bowl and whisk, with clean beaters, on a low speed for 2 mins or until foamy. Gently fold the egg white mixture into the beaten yolks, one-third at a time.

Put the oil in a nonstick, ovenproof frying pan over a low heat. Swirl to distribute the oil, then pour in the egg mix and gently spread it evenly in the pan with a spatula or spoon. Cook for 3 mins, then transfer to the oven and bake for 2-3 mins more, until puffed, set and very lightly golden.
Carefully transfer the omelette to a serving plate. To serve, spoon the compote onto the omelette.
Mix lime juice and water. Rinse prawn with water and pat dry with paper towers.
Add the lime water, salt, and sugar to the prawns and mix well.
Marinate for 15 minutes. Heat up a wok and add coconut oil. Drop the prawn in, and fry for 1-2 minutes on each side.
Dish out and serve immediately.
In a saucepan, combine low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth, sliced mushrooms, diced tofu, sliced bamboo shoots, minced garlic, and minced ginger.
Bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for about 5 minutes or until the mushrooms and tofu are tender.
Stir in rice vinegar, chili garlic sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and salt.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes to combine the flavors.
Top with sliced green onions.
Combine the ingredients for the teriyaki marinade in small bowl and whisk together.
Cut the tempeh into triangles or dice — it really doesn’t matter; the triangles look cool but this will taste amazing either way.
Heat the teriyaki marinade in a stir-fry pan or medium skillet.
When it begins to bubble, add the tempeh and stir quickly to coat all sides.
Turn the heat up to medium-high and cook, stirring often, until the marinade is completely reduced and coats the tempeh nicely, about 8 minutes.
Continue to cook until the tempeh begins to brown here and there, another minute or two.
Drizzle in some sriracha if you’d like a spicier effect; otherwise just sprinkle in the sesame seeds.
Serve on its own as a protein-packed side dish, arrange on a rice bowl with a vegetable or two; or toss into a stir-fry at the last minute.
In a small bowl, mix the active dry yeast, sugar, and warm water. Let it sit for about 5-10 minutes until it becomes frothy.
In a mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour and salt.
Pour the yeast mixture into the dry ingredients and mix to form a dough.
Knead the dough on a floured surface until it becomes smooth. You can add more flour if needed to prevent sticking.
Roll the dough into a round shape, about 6-7 inches in diameter.
Place the dough on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Drizzle olive oil over the dough and spread it evenly.
Sprinkle the thinly sliced red onion over the dough.
Sprinkle ground thyme (za'atar) evenly over the topping.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 12-15 minutes, or until the dough is cooked through and the edges are lightly browned.
Place the mango, peach, pineapple, onion, red pepper, yellow pepper, and cilantro in a mixing bowl.
Stir in the garlic, jalapeno, lime juice, salt, sugar, and water. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving.
In a small bowl, mix the flour and instant yeast together.
In a separate bowl, combine the lukewarm milk and granulated sugar. Stir until the sugar dissolves, then add the beaten egg and a pinch of salt. Mix well.
Gradually add the flour mixture to the milk mixture, stirring continuously until a smooth batter forms.
Cover the batter with a clean cloth and let it rest in a warm place for about 15-20 minutes to allow it to rise.
Preheat a non-stick poffertjes pan or a small round pancake pan over medium heat. Brush each cavity with melted ghee to prevent sticking.
Spoon the batter into each cavity of the pan, filling them about halfway. Cook for a few minutes until small bubbles start to form on the surface.
Using a skewer or fork, carefully flip each poffertje over and cook for an additional minute or until golden brown.
Transfer the cooked poffertjes to a plate and continue cooking the remaining batter in batches until finished.
Heat the oil in a large pan over a medium-high setting.
Sauté the finely chopped onion until golden.
Add the crushed garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the garlic doesn't smell raw anymore.
Mix in the next four ingredients (ground coriander, turmeric, cumin, and cayenne) and toast for two minutes stirring often.
Add the crushed tomatoes, chickpeas and vegetable stock.
Increase the heat to high and once boiling, lower to medium-low to maintain at a simmer for 10 minutes, stirring every now and then.
Add 1/2 tsp salt, the sugar, and the spinach. If the spinach is frozen increase the heat until the curry is bubbling away again.
Simmer for an additional five minutes.
Add the garam masala, lemon juice and coconut milk and stir.
Sprinkle over the chopped cilantro leaves and serve hot with pita, if desired.
Place your steamer rack in a wok on high heat.
Pour enough water so that the top of the rack isn't submerged, and start boiling water.
Take the rack out, separate and lay out noodles (12 oz) on top of the rack.
Once boiling, set the steamer rack + noodles back in the wok and cover for 10 minutes. Leave the stove on high heat.
If you're using a steamer rack without holes (i.e. steaming on a plate where the steam can't easily access the bottom, steam for 2-3 extra minutes.)
Once the 10 minutes is up, quickly dump the noodles in the water for 15-30 seconds.
Drain noodles through a colander, and spend about 1-2 minutes fluffing and separating the noodles with a chopstick.
Let it cool for 3-5 minutes.
We'll wash and chop our vegetables:
Mix dark soy sauce (1 tbsp), light soy sauce (1 tbsp), oyster sauce (1 tbsp), sugar (2 tsp), water (1 tbsp) in a bowl until the sugar dissolves.
We'll be adding the sesame oil later.
We'll start by heating our wok to around 350-400°F.
Depending on your stove, this should take around 2-3 minutes.
.Make room for the bean sprouts by pushing the noodles aside in the wok
Set the bean sprouts in the wok, and then cover them with the noodles.
This helps trap some of the heat inside to help them cook more evenly and quickly.
Let the bean sprouts cook underneath the noodles for 30-45 seconds.
Add the green onions (everything, the cooked roots + raw shoots) and onions, and cook for about 1-2 minutes.
Continue separating the noodles with chopsticks and a spatula.
Pour in the sauce mix, and mix everything around for 1 minute.
We'll want to let the sauce evaporate as much as we can.
Add sesame oil (1 tsp), and mix and separate the noodles for another 1-2 minutes, and then we can start plating.
Transfer the noodles onto a plate, and call your loved ones over.
Time to eat!
Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper and set aside.
In the bowl of your stand-up mixer add the warm water and sugar, sprinkle the yeast on top, let sit for 5 minutes, then stir to combine.
Roll the dough out into a large rectangle. Spread the pizza sauce on top.
Sprinkle the mozzarella cheese evenly over the top. Then add the sliced salami, ham, and pepperoni. Brush the edges of the dough with the egg.
Then roll up the dough like a jelly roll. Pinch the edges together and tuck under. Brush the top of the dough with the egg wash. Sprinkle with oregano.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until bubbly and golden. Remove from oven and slice.
Melt some butter in a pan.
Add the garlic and sauté until fragrant and light brown.
Add the shrimps and sauté for 2 minutes.
Sprinkle some chili flakes.
Add the ketchup and sugar.
Stir gently until well-combined.
Season with salt and pepper.
Stir for 30 seconds until the shrimps are well-coated with the sauce.
Transfer the sweet and spicy shrimp to a serving plate.
Sprinkle some chopped spring onions on top before serving.
Serve it over hot steaming rice.
Enjoy!
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the brown sugar, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the brown sugar and garlic mixture evenly over both sides of the chicken thighs.
Heat the olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Once the oil is hot, add the chicken thighs to the skillet.
Sear the chicken thighs for about 2-3 minutes on each side until they are nicely browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven.
Roast the chicken thighs in the oven for about 15-20 minutes or until they are cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Remove the skillet from the oven.
Let the chicken thighs rest for a couple of minutes before serving.
In a large skillet heat oil over medium-high heat. Add the green beans to the oil and stir fry them for 8 minutes.
While still over the heat add the garlic, chili peppers, sugar and soy sauce to the pan and toss for 1 minutes longer. Serve warm.
Preheat your oven to 450°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, active dry yeast, sugar, and salt.
Add plain Greek yogurt and warm water to the dry ingredients.
Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for a few minutes until it's smooth.
Roll the dough into a round or oval shape, about 1/4 inch thick.
Place the rolled-out dough on a baking sheet or oven-safe skillet.
Bake in the preheated oven for 3-5 minutes, or until the naan puffs up and turns lightly golden on the surface.
Combine the fennel, celery, cucumbers and shallots in a salad bowl. Add the olive oil and lemon juice, add salt and pepper to taste, and toss.
Transfer to a serving plate, add fresh mint leaves and serve.
Add the shallots, chili, ginger, garlic and shrimp paste to a blender and blend until smooth.
Heat the coconut oil in a wok until hot. Add the blended paste, lemon juice and sugar, and cook them until the oil separates.
When the oil separates, season with salt to taste, add the shrimp. Cook for 1-2 minutes and turn off the heat.
Transfer to a plate and serve.
Boil salted water, add the pasta to cook for 3-5 min.
Drain the pasta under cold water until it is no longer hot, you can drizzle a little olive oil on top to coat to keep it from sticking.
Transfer the pasta to a large mixing bowl and add the rest of the ingredients.
Toss to combine.
Drizzle the vinaigrette on top and toss until all of the ingredients are evenly coated with the dressing.
Whisk all ingredients together until combined.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil. Add the noodles and boil for 1-2 minutes, careful not to overcook them. Reserve 5 tbsp noodle water, drain the noodles and set aside.
To a non-stick pan over medium heat, add coconut oil. Add kimchi and fry until juices from the kimchi have been released. Add garlic and stir fry for 30-60 seconds.
Add the noodles to the pan, followed by kimchi juice, chicken powder, sugar, sesame oil, and water. Mix everything to coat well.
Add scallions and mix well. Top the noodles off with shredded cheddar cheese, allowing it to melt. Serve hot.
Separate egg yolks from whites, placing yolks in a medium bowl and whites in a small bowl.
Add rice to bowl with yolks and mix to break up any clumps and coat each grain with yolks (take your time with this as any clumps will cook together); set aside.
Stir 2 tablespoons of oil into egg whites and season lightly with salt.
Heat a dry large nonstick skillet over high.
Add egg white mixture and cook, pushing around constantly with chopsticks or a heatproof rubber spatula, until gently set, about 30 seconds.
Transfer to a plate.
Wipe out skillet if needed.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in same skillet over medium-high.
Season fish with salt and cook, undisturbed, until golden brown underneath, about 2 minutes.
Turn and cook just until lightly browned on the other side and opaque throughout, about 1 minute.
Transfer to another plate.
Wipe out skillet.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in skillet over medium-high.
Cook ginger, stirring, until very fragrant, about 20 seconds.
Add onion and season with a pinch of salt.
Cook, stirring, until onion is translucent, about 1 minute (remove from heat if onion starts to go past golden brown).
Add reserved rice mixture, sprinkle with sugar, and season with salt.
Toss to combine, then cook, undisturbed, until rice is beginning to warm and crisp underneath, about 1 minute.
Push some of the rice to the side to clear a few inches in skillet.
Drizzle a bit of oil into the clearing.
Add scallion white and pale green parts and garlic and cook, stirring, until just softened and fragrant, about 45 seconds.
Toss into rice mixture and cook, tossing occasionally, until warmed through and rice is crisp and chewy, about 3 minutes.
Return cooked egg whites to pan and cook, tossing and breaking up with spatula until distributed.
Return salmon to pan and toss once to combine.
Divide among plates and top with furikake and scallion greens.
Preheat grill to medium (grill side, not hot plate) or a heavy based pan over medium heat.
It's important only to use medium heat because otherwise the eggplant will burn before it cooks through
Combine the Miso Glaze ingredients and whisk to combine and remove lumps.
Brush the cut side of each eggplant with the oil.
Do it 2 to 3 times to use up all the oil.
Place the cut side down on the grill and cook for 4 minutes until it turns a light golden brown.
Turn over, and cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
While it is cooking, use a spoon to spread a generous amount of Miso Glaze on the cut side of each eggplant.
Squeeze the side of the eggplant with your tongs (or fingers if you can handle the heat!) to check for doneness - it should be squishy and soft.
Turn over and grill just for 30 seconds to 1 minute, just to caramelise the glaze.
Remove from grill, garnish with shallots and sesame seeds and serve immediately with bread.
cream: Brown the ground beef and onion in a skillet over medium-high heat for 10 minutes.
Stir in the garlic and Italian seasoning. Add the tomato sauce, chicken broth, cream, and gnocchi to the pan, and give it a good stir.
Once the sauce starts to bubble again, cover the pan and reduce the heat to medium. Cook for 5 minutes.
Uncover the pan, stir it, and let it cook for another few minutes, or until the gnocchi has cooked through and is nice and soft and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the Parmesan cheese and salt and pepper as needed. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Fry the bacon in butter on medium heat until crispy. Add the spinach and stir until wilted. Remove the pan from the heat and set aside.
Whisk the eggs and cream together and pour into baking dish.
Add the bacon, spinach, tomato, mushrooms, and cheese on top and place in the middle of the oven.
Bake for 20 minutes or until set in the middle and golden brown on top.
In a saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the pan. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the onions turn translucent.
Add drained and rinsed white beans to the pan along with the vegetable or chicken broth.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low.
Let the beans simmer in the broth for about 5-7 minutes until they are heated through and tender.
Remove the pan from the heat.
Using a blender or an immersion blender, carefully blend the bean mixture until smooth and creamy.
Season with dried thyme, salt, and pepper to taste.
Add cream. Reheat the soup if needed before serving.
Fry the bacon in a large pan until the desired crispiness. Drain from the fat, chop and set aside.
Mix onion powder, garlic powder in a small bowl.
In a large pot add water, chicken broth, cauliflower with a pinch of salt and ground black pepper. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Simmer, add butter and cream to the cauliflower and mix well. Then add the cheese and stir slowly until melted.
Add 2/3 of the cooked bacon and season with the mixed spices and herbs. Blend in a blender and season with salt and black pepper. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Divide the cauliflower cheese soup into bowls. Top the soup with the remaining crispy bacon. Drizzle with olive oil and serve.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the pasta according to the instructions on the package. Reserve some pasta water before draining.

Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a large pan and fry the chicken for 6 minutes on medium-high heat until golden and slightly crispy. Remove the chicken bits from the pan with a slotted spoon and set aside.

Turn the heat down to medium and melt the butter in the same pan. When the butter has melted, add the mushrooms to the pan and saute them for 4 minutes. Add garlic and salt and cook for one minute until fragrant. Turn the heat up, add the wine, and cook for 2 minutes until the alcohol evaporates.
Lower the heat and stir in the single cream. Cook for 2 minutes until the sauce thickens a bit, then add the cream cheese and stir until combined. Add the chicken bits back to the pan.
Transfer the pasta to the pan and toss it well with the sauce. If the sauce looks too thick, add a bit of pasta water. Season to taste, and serve while warm.
Melt the butter in a non-stick pan over medium low heat.  Add the mushrooms and a pinch of salt.  Saute them untill they begin to turn golden brown.
Remove the mushrooms from the pan directly onto a serving plate.
Mix the egg, cream and oil together very well.  Pour the egg mixture into the pan and scramble the eggs.
Add the cooked eggs to the mushrooms and mix.
In a large skillet, add olive oil and heat it over medium high heat. When the pan is hot, add chicken and cook for about 5 minutes on each side.
Season chicken with salt and pepper right inside the pan. Remove it onto a plate and add the sliced mushrooms in.
Add in salt. In about 5 more minutes, your mushrooms should look nice and moist, and dark. Once cooked, remove them from the pan.
To make the sauce, add butter to the pan, allow it to melt and then cook garlic in it until slightly browned  Add 1 tablespoon flour, cooking cream, and broth.
Add salt slowly and taste the sauce to make sure the salt level is to your liking.
Add spinach to the sauce and allow it to wilt. Add the chicken and mushrooms back into the pan and mix in well. Serve.
Season the sliced chicken breast with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced chicken breast to the skillet and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side until golden brown and cooked through.
In a small bowl, mix crème fraîche and minced garlic until well combined.
Once the chicken is cooked, reduce the heat to low and pour the crème fraîche mixture over the chicken in the skillet.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce is heated through.
Sprinkle chopped fresh parsley over the chicken.
Cook spaghetti according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Season shrimp with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add shrimp to the skillet and cook until they turn pink and are cooked through, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Reduce heat to low and stir in pesto sauce and light cream.
Cook for another minute, stirring gently, until the sauce is heated through and slightly thickened.
Add cooked spaghetti to the skillet and toss to coat evenly with the creamy pesto shrimp sauce.
Cook for another minute to heat through.
Remove from heat and transfer to a plate.
Drizzle olive oil in skillet and heat it up. Sprinkle salt and pepper over the salmon filet. Cook the salmon about 5 minutes on each side until done.
Meanwhile get a large skillet and melt the butter with the crushed garlic. Mix in the cream cheese, cooking cream and parmesan cheese.
Whisk and cook until the cheeses melt and everything is blended.
When the salmon is done, chop up into pieces. Whisk it into cheese sauce.
Place zucchini noodles on a plate. Top with sauce and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Take the steaks out of the fridge 20 minutes before planning to cook.
Crush the pepper using a rolling pin, mortar and pestle or the side of a hardy knife.
Just before cooking, sprinkle both sides of steak generously with salt and black pepper.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat until smoking. Add steaks and cook the first side for 2 minutes, flip and cook the other side for 2 minutes.
Stack the steaks on top of each other, then use tongs to sear the fat strip.
Transfer to plate, cover loosely with foil to rest while you make the sauce.
Add brandy or cognac into the same skillet and let it simmer rapidly, scraping the bottom of the pan.
Add broth, bring to simmer and let it simmer rapidly for 2 to 3 minutes or until it reduces by half. Turn heat down to medium.
Add cream and pepper, stir, then simmer for 1 ½ to 2 minutes until it thickens (do not let it boil rapidly).
Transfer steaks to warmed serving plates. Spoon over sauce and add rice.
Preheat oven to 200°F.
Put cheese in a bowl. Pour in the cream and the wine, enough to obtain a creamy consistency after mixing the ingredients.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the asparagus and broccoli then cook for 10 minutes.
Heat the butter in a heavy pan over medium heat. Add the filets then saute for 3 minutes on each side for medium-rare, turning them once.
Put the filets and vegetables on the plates. Cover with the sauce and serve.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Gently heat the oil in a deep frying pan, add the onion and pancetta and fry for 3-4 minutes until golden.
Add the mushrooms, turn up the heat and fry for 3-4 minutes.
Add the garlic and fry for another minute. Add the chicken and crème fraîche and bring to a boil.
Simmer for 5 minutes until thickened slightly. Stir in the spinach and allow to wilt for 30 seconds.
Use to fill 8 pancakes, roll up and lay in an ovenproof dish, top with the cheese and bake for 15-20 minutes until golden.
Serve with a salad.
Cook the eggs sunny side up.
Place two fried tortillas on a plate and spread the refried beans onto each.
Grill the Smithfield Anytime Favorites Ham Steaks until slightly browned and add on top of the beans.
Add the eggs on top of the Smithfield Anytime Favorites ham steaks.
Add the peas to the salsa roja and stir briefly. Add the salsa roja with the peas on top of the eggs.
Top with jalapeno slices, cilantro, and queso fresco. Serve Immediately.
Pat salmon fillets dry on both sides with a paper towel then season with salt and pepper.
Heat butter in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
Tilt and rotate the pan so the butter evenly coats the bottom of the pan.
When the pan is hot, carefully add the salmon skin side down.
Cook, undisturbed,  for about 4-5 minutes and the salmon flesh looks opaque about ½ way up the sides.
Carefully flip the salmon and cook for 3-4 minutes more or until the salmon is cooked to your liking  (about 120 degrees F internal temperature for medium-rare).
Transfer the salmon to 4 plates.
To the same pan, add green onions and cook until soft, about 3 minutes.
Add in cream, milk, and herbed cream cheese.
Cook, continuing to stir until cheese has melted completely.
Add spinach and toss with tongs until the spinach is wilted.
Add parmesan cheese.
Continue tossing until spinach is evenly coated, parmesan cheese is melted and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Divide the spinach among the 4 servings, spooning sauce over the spinach and salmon.
Garnish with chopped green onions if desired.
In Dutch oven or large saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Cook leeks, potatoes, onion,s alt and pepper, stirring often,for 10 minutes
Stir in broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes or until potatoes are tender.
Puree soup, in batches, using a blender until very smooth. Stir in cream; season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cook corn according to the package directions and set aside.
Melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in flour. Cook until bubbly, about 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the cream and milk. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens. Turn heat to low and add salt, and pepper. Stir in cooked corn.
Pour the corn mixture into a small greased casserole and top with hParmesan. Broil until bubbly.
Heat a large frying pan over high heat. Add the cream and lemon juice. Bring to a boil. Cook for 3 minutes.
Add the zucchini, salmon, half the lemon zest and season with salt and pepper. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add peas, cream and stock and bring to the boil, stirring. Simmer for 5 minutes. Season to taste,
Add chicken, spinach and parmesan, stirring to combine. Cook for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook fusilli in a large saucepan of boiling water according to pack instructions. Drain well. Return to pan. Toss sauce with pasta and serve.
Drizzle a little oil into a large, high-sided frying pan set over medium heat for 2 minutes.
When the pan is hot, add the chicken and garlic, and fry for 10 mins, stirring often so it doesn’t burn.
Pour in 900ml boiling water, then add the stock cube and pasta along with some seasoning. Bring to the boil, then cover and simmer for 8 mins.
Stir in the broccoli for the last 4 mins of cooking time, and replace the lid.
After 4 mins, remove the lid and reduce the heat to low, then stir in the crème fraîche.
Remove the pan from the heat and stir through the lemon juice, parmesan, and mint leaves.
Serve with some extra parmesan, if you like.
oil: Place pork chops on a paper towel to absorb any excess moisture. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Whisk egg and soy sauce together in a small bowl. Place flour on a small plate and bread crumbs in another.

Dredge a pork chop in the flour, pressing it in using your fingertips to cover all crevices along the surface. Turn to evenly coat all sides. Dip the pork into the egg mixture, coating completely. Transfer immediately to the bowl of bread crumbs, pressing in to evenly coat. Repeat process with the second pork chop.

Melt the butter in a small frying pan over high heat. Once it begins to foam, stir it and watch it carefully, it will brown in 1-2 minutes. Stir the butter a few times to ensure it browns evenly. Remove the pan from the heat. Add the olive oil to the pan then and set it aside for the butter to cool slightly.

In a medium-sized bowl, whisk the maple syrup, balsamic vinegar, apple cider vinegar, and mustard. Slowly drizzle in the brown butter and oil, whisking the entire time. Once all the oil and butter have been added, whisk the dressing quickly for several seconds so it becomes creamy.
Store the dressing at room temperature until you are ready to use it.

Put the cabbage, carrots and spring onions in a large bowl and stir to combine. Mix the mayonnaise with the mustard in another small bowl and drizzle over. Fold everything together to coat in the creamy sauce, then season with salt and pepper.

Heat oil in a wide pan or wok on medium-high heat to 350°F. Lower a pork chop into the oil. Fry until bottom side is golden, 2 to 3 minutes. Turn; cook until other side turns golden and pork is slightly pink in the center.
Pick up the cutlets and hold them on their side for a few seconds to let the oil drip off. Drain on a paper towel.
Place the cutlets on the plate and serve them with brown dressing and coleslaw.
Heat the oil in a large pan over a medium-high setting.
Sauté the finely chopped onion until golden.
Add the crushed garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the garlic doesn't smell raw anymore.
Mix in the next four ingredients (ground coriander, turmeric, cumin, and cayenne) and toast for two minutes stirring often.
Add the crushed tomatoes, chickpeas and vegetable stock.
Increase the heat to high and once boiling, lower to medium-low to maintain at a simmer for 10 minutes, stirring every now and then.
Add 1/2 tsp salt, the sugar, and the spinach. If the spinach is frozen increase the heat until the curry is bubbling away again.
Simmer for an additional five minutes.
Add the garam masala, lemon juice and coconut milk and stir.
Sprinkle over the chopped cilantro leaves and serve hot with pita, if desired.
To start making smoked tofu and grilled vegetable salad recipe, heat a tsp of oil in the grill pan on medium heat.
Add the vegetables to the hot pan and stir-fry till vegetables are cooked and yet firm to touch.
Turn off the heat.
Transfer the vegetables to a bowl and set aside while you smoke the tofu.
To prepare the smoked tofu, gently press the tofu with a kitchen towel to remove the excess water or moisture content.
Heat a tsp of olive oil in a pan, add the cut tofu and stir fry for a few seconds until warm.
Turn the heat to low and allow the tofu to roast a bit. While it is roasting and we will proceed to prepare the coal.
To smoke the tofu, we will need a few pieces of large coal. Place the coal on the flame and allow it to become red hot.
Once you notice that the coal is red hot, place the hot coal on a heatproof small bowl.
Place this bowl on the deep pan where you have kept the tofu, add a tsp of ghee over the hot coal. It will begin to start smoking.
At this stage, cover the deep dish pan and allow the tofu to absorb the smoke flavors.
Once the smoke has dissipated, turn off the heat on the tofu pan and the smoked tofu is ready to be served with the salad.
To prepare the dressing for the salad, in a salad mixing bowl whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger and garlic until well combined.
Add the grilled vegetables and smoked tofu to this and stir well until everything is coated.
Serve at room temperature.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the white fish fillet on a piece of aluminum foil large enough to wrap the fish.
Season the fish with jerk seasoning, olive oil, lime juice, salt, and pepper. Rub the seasoning evenly over the fish.
Fold the aluminum foil over the fish to create a packet and seal the edges.
Place the foil packet on a baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Carefully open the foil packet to release the steam.
Serve hot. Enjoy your Jamaican jerk fish!
Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add halloumi slices to the pan and fry on each side until golden.
Spread mashed avocado onto the toasted bread.
Sprinkle with lime juice, and chili flakes.
Top with fried halloumi.
Roughly tear the chapatis by hand.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds.
When the seeds start crackling, add the green chilies, curry leaves and chopped onions.
Sauté them for 1-2 minutes.
Now add the potatoes, carrots, salt and turmeric.
Stir and cover. Let it cook for 2-3 minutes, till the potatoes and carrots are tender.
Add a tablespoon of water, peas and rest of the spices.
Stir and then add the crumbled chapati.
Add the lemon juice, stir and cover and cook it for a minute.
Take off the lid and cook for another one minute.
Garnish with chopped coriander.
It tastes great by itself and goes well when paired with yoghurt.
Firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and roast cottage cheese.
Roast until the paneer turns golden brown. keep aside. In the same oil, add ½ bell pepper, ½ onion and saute on high flame.
Once they slightly shrink, keep the onions and bell pepper aside. With the remaining oil add ½ tsp cumin, cloves.
Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. Further add 1 onion, 1 inch ginger and 2 clove garlic.
Saute until onions shrink and softens. Now add 2 tomato and saute until tomatoes soften and turn mushy.
Cool completely and transfer to the blender. Blend to smooth paste adding water if required.
Filter the puree making sure to have a smooth silky puree. keep aside.
Firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp butter and saute 1 bay leaf, ½ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and ¼ tsp cumin powder.
Saute until the spices turn aromatic. Now add in prepared onion tomato puree, 1 tsp salt and mix well.
Cover and cook for 5-10 minutes or until the puree thickens. Add in ¾ cup water and mix well adjusting consistency as required.
Now add in fried paneer, bell pepper and onion. mix well. Cover and simmer for 3 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well.
Additionally, add 2 tbsp coriander, mix well.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Put oil and ghee in a large frying pan over medium high heat and melt ghee
Add ginger, garlic and onion to the frying pan and sauté for about 10 minutes until the onion becomes yellowish and very soft with some browning around the edge.
Add the remaining Curry Base ingredients and cook for another 1 minute, mixing the ingredients well.
Add water, potato and carrot to the curry base and cook for a couple of minutes. Add wine, turn the heat up and mix well.
When it starts boiling, add tamarind, salt, tomato paste, vegetable stock powder, red chili powder and mix.
Place a lid on and cook for about 7 minutes, then add zucchini.
Cook further 8 minutes or so with a lid on until the vegetables are cooked through and the curry becomes a béchamel sauce consistency
Mix the curry occasionally to ensure that it does not stick to the bottom
In a small bowl, mash together avocado, olive oil from the canned sardines, lemon juice, salt, pepper, and chili flakes.
Enjoy the avocado mash alongside sardines.
Finely shred red and white cabbage with a mandoline slicer.
Cut the broccoli into small florets.
Dice the cherry tomatoes.
Cube the cucumber into small cubes.
Combine all the ingredients in a bowl.
Combine ingredients for dressing.
Season salad with salt and pepper, to taste.
Add dressing to the salad.
Mix salad.
Mix flour, water, sugar, a tablespoon of vegetable oil, pinch of salt, milk and egg to form a watery batter.
Get a non-stick pan, scoop in batter like pancakes and fry while turning.
When fried, whisk your eggs and keep aside, add your minced meat in the center of each crepe.
Fold from sides and roll.
Dip in whisked eggs and roll in breadcrumbs.
Repeat each method for each crepe and deep fry till golden brown.
Serve with dipping of your choice.
Preheat the oven to 355°F for raw potato hash browns.
Get a non-stick baking sheet and a round cookie cutter or an egg ring ready.
Place grated potatoes and remaining ingredients in a bowl. If using raw potatoes, set the mixture aside for 10 minutes.
After 10 minutes, place the raw potato mixture in the middle of a clean muslin cloth or kitchen towel and squeeze all the excess moisture out.
Drain the water away and you will end up with potato starch stuck to the bottom of the bowl, scoop it up and add it back to the mixture.
Break compacted mixture up with a fork or your hands after squeezing the liquid out.
Bake the raw potato hash browns for 20 minutes and parboil ones for about 10 then carefully detach from the tray using a flat spatula.
Bake for a further 15-20 minutes. Serve with vegan sour cream/creme fraiche or ketchup – whichever takes your fancy.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground beef, seasoning with salt & pepper and cook 5 minutes until lightly browned.
Add garlic, taco seasoning and lime juice, cooking another 5 minutes until fully cooked.
Divide beef mixture among avocados then add cheese, and garnish with scallions.
Turn the Instant Pot to saute mode and heat oil. Add whole spices–cardamom, cloves, black pepper, and cumin. Saute for 30 seconds.
Add onion and mix well. Saute for 2 minutes covered with glass lid. Add ginger, garlic, turmeric, red chili powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, and salt, mix well.
Add chicken and water. Mix well to evenly coat all the spices to the chicken. Close Instant Pot with pressure valve to sealing.
Pressure Cook for 8 minutes followed by 5-minute Natural Release. Open the Instant Pot, add a few spoonfuls of hot curry to the yogurt and mix well.
Cook on Saute mode to thicken the sauce. Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the package instructions. Set aside to cool a bit, then fluff the rice using a fork.
Serve the curry alongside rice with a garnish of cilantro.
Combine water and flax seeds in a small bowl. Refrigerate until mixture thickens and has an egg-like consistency, 15 minutes.
Stir in almond milk, banana, zucchini, and vanilla extract.
Mix flour, cocoa powder, brown sugar baking blend, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and sea salt together in a bowl.
Pour in flax mixture, stir until batter is just combined
Heat a large griddle over medium heat and oil with coconut oil. Drop batter onto the griddle and cook until bubbles form and bottoms are golden brown.
Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 4 to 6 minutes. Transfer to a baking rack to cool. Repeat with remaining batter. Garnish with blueberries.
Bring water to a boil, add chickpeas, baking soda and salt and cook the chickpeas until they get soft, over low heat.
In a dutch oven saute chopped meat with oil till it is brown. Then add onions and saute for 2 more minutes over medium heat.
Stir in peppers and tomato paste. Add one cup of hot water and salt, and then simmer till the meat is soft.
Transfer the cooked chickpeas into the pot. Simmer for 10 minutes over low heat. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 325°F.
To prepare your meat, slice it into 1 inch chunks, and season it with the little salt and pepper.
Add the ghee to a dutch oven or oven-friendly pot with a thick bottom on medium/high heat until melted.
Add the chuck roast and brown in batches being careful not to overcrowd the dutch oven, about 6 minutes per batch.
Remove and set the browned meat aside.
Add the diced onion to the dutch oven/pot, and lower the heat to medium – sauté until softened and translucent, about five minutes.
At this point, add the garlic and sauté for another minute, then return the meat (and its juices) to the dutch oven/pot.
Add the allspice, bay leaves, and stir in enough beef broth to mostly cover the meat, about four cups.

Cover the dutch oven/pot and put it in the oven for 1½
hours, or until the meat is soft. After an hour, add the carrots.
While the meat is cooking, peel and slice the potatoes, then put them in a pot with cold water.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium and simmer until easily pierced with a fork, about eight minutes.
Remove the meat and carrots and put them in a covered dish that retains heat.
Remove and discard the bay leaves.
Put the dutch oven on the stove on medium-high heat and reduce the liquid until about half of it has reduced – should take about five minutes.
Next, chop up a couple of your cooked potato pieces and add them to the liquid.
Lastly, using an immersion blender, blend the liquid/onions/potatoes to create a thicker stew.
Return the meat and carrots to the stew, and stir in the fresh chopped parsley.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Serve each plate stew, the remaining potatoes, and pickled beets.
Turn Instant Pot to sauté(more) mode and heat oil.
Add cumin seeds and toast them for 30 seconds.
Add turmeric powder and mix well.
Add ginger and garlic, mix well.
Add onion and sauté for a minute.
Cook covered for 2 minutes with a glass lid on.
Add tomatoes, red chili powder, garam masala, salt and ground coriander .
Mix well.
Add ground chicken, breaking it up with a spatula.
Add ½ cup of water (add only 2 tbsp of water for a thicker curry).
Secure the lid, close the pressure valve and cook for 10 minutes at high pressure.
Open the valve to quick-release any remaining pressure.
Press the sauté button to reduce or boil off any extra liquid if needed.
Garnish with cilantro and serve hot with potato rolls or burger buns lightly toasted with some ghee on a griddle.
In a medium mixing bowl, stir together the almond flour, coconut flour, coconut, flax, cinnamon, nutmeg, baking soda and salt.
Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl with an electric hand mixer or using a stand mixer, beat together the oil, almond butter and vanilla extract at medium speed until well combined, about 1 minute.
Beat in the egg on low and mix until well incorporated.
Stir in the flour mixture until well combined.
Then stir in the raisins.
Refrigerate for at least 30-60 minutes or until the dough is firm and easy to roll into balls.
Move the oven rack to the bottom position in the oven.
Preheat the oven to 350 °F and line a baking sheet with a piece of parchment paper.
If you use the Nordic Ware Insulated Baking Sheet, you don't need to use parchment paper.
Roll the dough into 8 balls.
You can also roll them into smaller balls but then you need to reduce the baking time.
Place 4 inches apart on the prepared baking sheet.
Press the cookies down lightly with the palm of your hand.
Bake for 14-18 minutes or until the surface of the center of the cookies no longer appears wet.
They'll be very soft but will continue to cook as they sit on the cookie sheet.
Let cool completely on the baking sheet.
Store in an airtight container for up to 3 days.
Add Jasmine rice, water and salt to a stainless steel bowl and keep aside.
Separate the coconut cream and watery portion of the coconut milk and keep aside.
Turn the Instant Pot to Saute mode and heat oil.
Add the massaman curry paste and saute for 10 to 15 seconds.
Add chicken and mix well with the curry paste.
Add potatoes, onions, cashews, fish sauce, brown sugar and salt.
Add ½ cup of the separated water from the coconut milk and stir everything well.
Carefully place the tall trivet inside the Instant Pot. Place the prepared rice bowl on the trivet.
Close the Instant Pot lid with pressure value to sealing. Cook on manual/pressure cook (Hi) for 5 mins followed by 10 minute natural pressure release.
Open the Instant Pot, take out the rice bowl and trivet using heat resistant mitts. Stir in the reserved coconut cream.
Set the Instant Pot to Saute mode and bring the curry to a gentle boil. Enjoy hot over steamed jasmine rice.
vinegar: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a cupcake tray with 12 cupcake liners. Set aside.
Sift the flour into a mixing bowl and add sugar, pea proteins, baking soda, salt and matcha powder. Mix together.
Prepare your vegan buttermilk by adding 1 tbsp white vinegar to a measuring jug and then adding soy milk.
Add the yogurt, oil and vanilla extract and mix into a batter. Don’t overmix.
Divide the batter evenly between the 12 cupcake liners in your cupcake tray.
Bake for 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center of one of the cupcakes comes out clean. Serve with some blueberries.
Season the filet mignon generously with salt and black pepper on both sides.
Heat the ghee in a skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the filet mignon and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or until desired doneness.
Remove the filet mignon from the skillet and set it aside on a plate. Cover loosely with foil to keep warm.
In the same skillet, add the balsamic vinegar and bring it to a simmer over medium heat. Allow it to reduce by half, stirring occasionally, for about 2-3 minutes.
Once the balsamic glaze has thickened, remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the olive oil until well combined.
Pour the balsamic glaze over the filet mignon and serve immediately.
In a large bowl combine red cherry tomatoes, onion, cucumber, ciabatta bread and basil leaves.
Make dressing with black pepper, olive oil, white wine vinegar and salt, mix until combined.
Add the dressing to the salad and toss gentnly.
Preheat a grill to medium-high heat 375°F.
Rub tilapia fillets with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder and grill for 5 minutes on each side.
Serve with tomatoes, bell peppers, seasoned with salt, pepper, olive oil and vinegar.
Add egg yolks, lemon juice, salt, and cayenne to a blender or food processor and pulse until combined.
Slowly drizzle in the butter and continue to run the blender until the sauce thickens.
Bring a large pot with a few inches of water to a slight simmer. Add a dash of vinegar to the pot.
Let eggs poach in not-quite-boiling water for 3 minutes, or until the whites of the eggs are set and the centers are still soft.
Toast the slices of bread. Top bread with sliced avocado, salad mix, poached egg. Spoon hollandaise sauce over and serve.
Place the potatoes in a medium-size pot and cover them with water.
Salt the water and bring to the boil over medium-high heat. Continue cooking until potatoes are tender, about 15 minutes.
Cook the bacon in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Once crisp, place on a kitchen towel-lined plate and crumble into small pieces.
Whisk in the vinegar, oil, pepper, dill, and salt and stir until thick.
Add the sliced, cooked potatoes and toss to coat. Top with the crumbled bacon.
Season meat with salt, pepper, paprika powder, and cumin. Heat 1 tsp oil in a frying pan. Fry the meat over high heat for 5 minutes until crumbly. Set aside.
Mix all vegetables with oil, vinegar and season with salt and pepper. Add minced meat and mix. Serve.
Place the eggs in a saucepan and cover them with cold water. Bring the water to a boil over medium-high heat.
Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and simmer for 9-12 minutes, depending on your desired yolk consistency (9 minutes for soft-boiled, 12 minutes for hard-boiled).
Drain the hot water and transfer the eggs to a bowl of ice water to cool completely.
In a small saucepan, combine the white vinegar, water, salt, black peppercorns, and any optional seasonings.
Heat the mixture over medium heat until it comes to a simmer. Allow it to simmer for a couple of minutes to infuse the flavors.
Place the peeled eggs in a clean glass jar or container that has a lid.
Carefully pour the hot pickling liquid over the eggs, ensuring they are completely covered.
Seal the jar or container with the lid.
Refrigerate the pickled eggs for at least 24 hours to allow the flavors to develop. They can be kept for up to two weeks in the refrigerator.
Heat the frying pan to high and lightly toast the bread on both sides.
Spread 1 tsp mustard over one side, then top with the ham and cheese.
Whisk the remaining mustard and vinegar with 1 tsp oil and seasoning to make the dressing for the spinach.
Grill the croque for 3 mins, or until the cheese is bubbling and turning golden.
Meanwhile, heat a non-stick frying pan.
Add a little oil, then crack in the egg and gently fry to your liking.
When cooked, lift it on top of the toast.
Toss the dressing with the spinach, and cornichons if using,  Plate the entire meal and serve immediately.
In a small skillet bring water to a boil and cook eggs for 7 minutes, until they are hard-boiled. When cooked, peel and half them.
Mix all ingredients in a bowl and place on a plate, then serve.
In a large bowl, mix all powder ingredients, butter, egg, garlic and ginger paste, scallion, cilantro, almond flour, seasoning, and ground chicken.
When ready to cook, mix in tomatoes. Wet your palms with a little oil or water and shape the chicken mixture into round patties.
Heat oil on medium heat in a skillet and, once oil is hot, place the chicken patties in oil and let them fry for 5 minutes. Flip and cook for another 5 minutes.
Remove to a paper towel-lined plate. Place on a plate, topped with sliced tomatoes. Serve along with salad mix, seasoned with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, and vinegar.
Rinse the chickpeas and soak for 8 hours or overnight in enough water to cover.

Drain and rinse, then transfer to a medium saucepan and cover with fresh water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until the chickpeas are tender. Drain and transfer to a large bowl.
Meanwhile, rinse the lentils and transfer to a medium saucepan. Cover with 4 cups of water, then bring to a boil.
Drain and add to the bowl of chickpeas. Add the onion, red pepper and dried tomatoes, and pour with olive oil, vinegar and salt and pepper.
Toss until everything is evenly coated with the dressing. Now add the fresh parsley and toss again.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.
Place all the sauce ingredients apart from the cornflour and water in a saucepan on medium heat. Whisk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Mix the cornflour and water in a bowl then add the slurry to the sauce, continuing to whisk it all together until it is thick.
Place the tofu chunks into a bowl with the cornflour, garlic, ginger, salt and pepper. Toss well so all the edges of the tofu is fully coated in the flour.
Add the tofu and fry for around 10 minutes, flipping occasionally, until golden brown and crispy.
Pour over the majority of the sauce, stir the tofu and leave to heat through for a minute.
Bring water to a boil and put in squids. Cook on low heat for 5 minutes. Cool and cut squids into rings. Grate the cheese and place squids in the salad bowl.
Add garlic, mayonnaise, salt, pepper, and vinegar. Mix all together and serve.

To scald the tomatoes put a large pot of salted water on the stove and bring to a boil. Cut a small cross at the bottom of each tomato and when the water is boiling add the tomatoes for 30 to 60 seconds.
Afterwards, remove immediately and place in a cold water bath to stop the boiling process. The skin will peel right off.
Cut the cores of the tomatoes and add the rest to a blender. Blend for about 30 seconds then add the bread. Let the bread soak in the tomato juice for about 5 minutes.

To the blend add a vinegar, salt and garlic and blend until the soup has an even texture. Slowly add the olive oil while blending at a moderate speed. Add 1 hardboiled egg and blend until incorporated.
Serve and garnish with diced ham and 1 diced hardboiled egg.
Marinate the meat with the marinade, let stand 1 hour in refrigerator.
Stir ingredients pickles, let stand 2 hours in refrigerator.
Mix sauce ingredients well, set aside.
Heat the ghee.
Saute onion until fragrant and enter the tomato.
Stir until wilted.
Pour the water.
Let it boil.
Put in the meat then leave until the color changes.
Add soy sauce, worcestershire sauce, nutmeg powder, pepper, and salt. Leave it until the meat is cooked.
Serve the meat with a complementary, pickles and sauces.
In a skillet place slices of zucchinis. Cook until tender on both sides and set aside.
In a small bowl mix together parsley, olive oil, garlic, vinegar, salt and pepper.
Mix zucchini with garlic mixture and set on a plate. Serve.
Preheat oven to 300°F.
Bake bread cubes for 15 minutes.
Combine the olive oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper in a jam jar. Shake vigorously. Alternatively, combine ingredients in a small bowl and whisk to combine.
Combine the bread and chopped vegetables in a large mixing bowl. Pour the vinaigrette over top and use a spatula to thoroughly combine.
Garnish with arugula and serve.
Mix together the onion, cod, bell peppers, tomato and olives. In a small bowl mix the vinegar, oil, salt and pepper, to form the dressing.
Mix the vegetables with the dressing and serve immediately.
Brown chicken in oil in a large skillet over medium high heat. Add red bell pepper, carrot and garlic and stir fry for 1 to 2 minutes.
In a small bowl, combine cornstarch and soy sauce and mix together; pour mixture into the skillet.
Add along with the pineapple and liquid, vinegar, sugar and ginger. Stir together and bring to a full boil. Serve with rice and garnish with chives.
mirin: Preheat the oven to 450°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook according to package instructions. Set aside.
In a 13 x 9 baking dish, whisk ginger, mirin, soy sauce, vinegar and sesame oil. Add the chicken to the sauce, turning the pieces so they are completely coated.
Bake chicken, skin side up, for 30 minutes or until golden brown. Serve along with rice.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Use thinly sliced pork belly, stir-fry pork or pork mince.
Lightly season the pork with salt and pepper, and coat with potato starch flour.
To make the sauce, place the grated ginger, soy sauce, mirin and sake (or water) in a small bowl and mix.
Heat small mount of oil in a frying pan over medium high heat, cook pork and onion.
When the pork is cooked and onion is soft, add the sauce and stir to combine well.
Half fill a bowl with hot cooked rice and cover it with the pork mixture.
Sprinkle some finely chopped spring onion on top.
Serve with rice.
Cut Chicken Fillets into 1-1½ inch size pieces.
Combine ginger, garlic, soy sauce, rice wine in a bowl, add chicken pieces, massage, and set aside.
Place 1 egg in ½ cup and add cold water to make it ½ cupful.
Combine flour in a separate bowl, and egg & cold water, and mix lightly to make tempura batter.
Heat Oil from 325°F to 350°F.
Coat the chicken pieces with flour, dip into tempura batter and fry until batter is light golden and crispy.
Take them out and lay them on a rack to drain the oil.
Place all the sauce ingredients in a saucepan and bring to a boil, then simmer for a few minutes.
Half fill a bowl with freshly cooked rice, place chicken, lettuce leaves and spring onion on top, drizzle the sauce over and enjoy.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, mirin, sake (or dry white wine), grated ginger, and minced garlic.
Heat ghee in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the chicken thighs to the skillet and cook until browned on both sides.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the chicken.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 10-12 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through, flipping the chicken halfway through.
Once cooked, remove the chicken from the skillet and let it rest for a minute.
Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
Mix all the ingredients for the sauce in a small bowl and set aside.
Remove your udon noodles from its package and add it to a pot of boiling water.
Frozen udon noodles can be added directly to the pot of boiling water. Stir gently until your udon noodles are separated and pliable.
Rinse and drain your noodles under cold water. If not using right away, drizzle on a little bit of oil to keep them from sticking.
Heat your pan over high heat and add the oil. Add in the ground meat and stir fry for 1 minute before adding the rest of your vegetables.
Stir fry together for 2 to 3 minutes and then add in the noodles and sauce. Continue stir frying for about 3 minutes or until well combined.
Serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile put the ginger, soy, mirin, garlic, chilli flakes  and 1 tsp of the sesame oil in a food processor and blend until fine.
Pour the marinade into a bowl, add the meat, mix well and leave to sit while you prepare the onion.
Heat the remaining sesame oil in a large wok or frying pan until very hot. Add the onion and stir-fry for a few mins.
Add the beef and the marinade, stirring constantly until it’s cooked through, about 5 mins. Serve with rice and garnish with onions.
Make the sauce: In a small bowl, mix together corn starch and 1 tablespoon of water to make slurry.
Set aside. In a small pot, whisk together all ingredients and 2 tablespoons of water until combined. Bring to a boil and lower heat to a simmer.
Whisk in the corn starch slurry and cook 1-2 minutes, until thickened. Set aside in a warm place.
In a steamer set, steam broccoli and carrots over boiling water for 4-5 minutes, until tender with a little bite.
Toss with a splash of soy sauce and mirin. Set aside in a warm place.
Cook the chicken: In a large skillet, heat 1 teaspoon of sesame oil and 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium high heat.
Add diced chicken and cook until golden and cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Pour half of the teriyaki sauce over the chicken and cook 1 more minute. Assemble the bowls:
Top with broccoli, carrots, and chicken. Drizzle with remaining teriyaki sauce, sesame seeds and chives.
Combine and boil soy sauce, sake, and mirin in a small pan, remove from heat immediately and let cool.
If the tuna is in a block, cut tuna sashimi into ½" thick pieces.
Marinade tuna pieces in a cooled soy sauce mixture for 10-15 minutes.
Serve rice in a bowl, and top with marinated tuna, sesame seeds, wasabi, and roasted seaweed.
Pour some of the marinade sauce over rice if you want.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In another bowl, whisk together 1/2 cup of dashi soup, 1 tablespoon of soy sauce, 1 tablespoon of mirin, and 1/2 tablespoon of sugar.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add 4 ounces of thinly sliced beef and cook until it's no longer pink.
Add 1/2 small thinly sliced onion to the skillet and sauté until the onion is soft and translucent.
Pour the sauce mixture into the skillet with the beef and onions. Stir and simmer for a couple of minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Push the beef and onion mixture to one side of the skillet and crack 1 egg into the empty side.
Cover the skillet with a lid and cook until the egg reaches your desired level of doneness.
Once the egg is cooked, remove the skillet from heat.
To serve, scoop the beef and onion mixture over the cooked rice in the bowl.
Top the Gyudon with the cooked egg.
Fill a large pot with lightly salted water and bring to a rolling boil. Drop in soba noodles and return to a boil for about 7 minutes, drain.
Meanwhile, combine soy sauce, oyster sauce, and mirin in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add peas, onion, and green beans and saute until softened but still crisp, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add soba noodles and toss with the cooked vegetables. Mix in soy sauce mixture and cook for an additional 1 to 2 minutes
Add two parts water to one part rice and cook over low to medium-low heat in a saucepan until all the water has been absorbed and rice is tender (15 to 20 minutes).
Cut steak into thin slices and set aside. You may want to freeze the steak for 10 minutes to make slicing it a little easier.
Cut the stems off the enoki mushrooms, and slice them horizontally into three equal parts.
In a small bowl, add 2 tablespoons of mirin, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, 2 tablespoons of sake, 3 tablespoons of dashi (or water), and set aside.
Cut green onion into thin slices. Separate the green part in one bowl, and the white part of the onion in another bowl.
Heat pan on medium/low and add the white part of the onion with mirin, soy sauce, sake, and dashi, and cook for 1 minute.
Add slices of beef and cook until medium.
Add enoki mushrooms and half of the green onions and cook for 1 minute.
Crack an egg, separating the yolk from the white, and carefully add the white part of the egg in the middle of the pan first.
After 30 seconds, add the yolk, and cook for an additional 1 minute on medium.
Place beef on the edges of the rice bowl and top with egg in the middle.
Sprinkle on the remaining green onion, sesame seeds, black pepper, and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Gather all the ingredients.
Thinly slice the onions, cut the green onions into thin slices (save for garnish), and cut the meat into 3 inches pieces.
Heat the oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the onion until tender, about 3-5 minutes.
Add the meat to the pan, and cook until meat is no longer pink.
Add sake, mirin, and soy sauce.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 3-5 minutes.
If you like to add the egg, slowly drizzle the beaten egg over the beef.
Cook covered until the egg is almost done (don't overcook it).
Remove from the heat.
In a large donburi bowl, add steamed rice and put the beef and egg mixture on top
If you'd like, drizzle over remaining sauce.
Top with green onion.
Enjoy!
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile toast sesame seeds in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat once sesame seeds are a golden brown.
Combine miso paste, mirin, soy sauce, ginger, and honey in a small bowl. Mix well. Stir in toasted sesame seeds.
Set an oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler. Line a baking pan with foil and lightly grease with oil.
Coat salmon well in the miso sauce and place skin-side up on the baking pan and broil in the preheated oven, 10 to 15 minutes.
In a small bowl, stir together the soy sauce, mirin, and sesame oil. Divide into two equal parts. Stir the rice vinegar into one part and set aside as a dipping sauce.
Spread the sesame seeds out on a plate. Coat the tuna steaks with the remaining soy sauce mixture, then press into the sesame seeds to coat.
Heat olive oil in a cast iron skillet over high heat until very hot. Place steaks in the pan, and sear for about 30 seconds on each side.
Serve immediately, garnished with lemon slices.
In a saucepan heat the dashi and whisk in the miso pastes.
Bring to a simmer and add the tofu, scallions, and mirin, if using.
Remove from the heat and serve immediately.
In a saucepan, combine the water and kombu.
Bring to a simmer, uncovered, over medium heat.
Remove the kombu and discard.
Bring the liquid to a boil, add the bonito flakes and immediately remove the pan from the heat.
Allow the dashi to sit undisturbed for 2 minutes.
Strain through a fine mesh strainer into a medium bowl.
Discard the bonito flakes.
Use immediately or store, covered, in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Preheat grill to medium (grill side, not hot plate) or a heavy based pan over medium heat.
It's important only to use medium heat because otherwise the eggplant will burn before it cooks through
Combine the Miso Glaze ingredients and whisk to combine and remove lumps.
Brush the cut side of each eggplant with the oil.
Do it 2 to 3 times to use up all the oil.
Place the cut side down on the grill and cook for 4 minutes until it turns a light golden brown.
Turn over, and cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
While it is cooking, use a spoon to spread a generous amount of Miso Glaze on the cut side of each eggplant.
Squeeze the side of the eggplant with your tongs (or fingers if you can handle the heat!) to check for doneness - it should be squishy and soft.
Turn over and grill just for 30 seconds to 1 minute, just to caramelise the glaze.
Remove from grill, garnish with shallots and sesame seeds and serve immediately with bread.
In a large bowl, stir together the soy sauce, sugar, sesame seeds, sesame oil, shallots, garlic, and mirin.
Add the meat, and stir to coat. Cover and refrigerate for 12 to 24 hours.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Fry the meat for 5 to 10 minutes, or until no longer pink.

In a bowl, combine soy sauce, mirin, honey, minced garlic, grated ginger, and black pepper. Add the chicken cubes to the marinade and toss to coat. Let marinate for at least 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

While the chicken is marinating, prepare the miso ranch dip. In a small bowl, mix together Greek yogurt, white miso paste, rice vinegar, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper until well combined. Set aside.
Preheat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated chicken cubes onto the soaked bamboo skewers.
Grill the chicken skewers for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked through and slightly charred.
Once cooked, remove the chicken skewers from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season the beef steak with salt and black pepper to taste.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Once the skillet is hot, add the steak and sear for 1-2 minutes on each side, or until desired doneness is reached.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, mirin, sake, and grated ginger to make the sauce.
Pour the sauce over the steak in the skillet.
Allow the sauce to simmer and reduce for about 1-2 minutes, while continuously basting the steak with the sauce.
Remove the steak from the skillet and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
For the teriyaki sauce, mix together soy sauce, tsuyu, sake, sweet rice wine, and honey in a small pot.
Add the garlic, ginger and bring the sauce to boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Discard the garlic and ginger, set aside. Heat oil in a large heavy bottom skillet over med-high heat.
Add the onion and cook until they are just softened, about 2-3 minutes. Transfer to another plate; set aside.
Add the pork belly to the hot skillet and let them sear for 2-3 minute, then turn to the other side and cook for another 1 minute.
Pour the sauce over pork belly and let the sauce bubble. Reduce the sauce until it thickens, about 2-3 minutes. Remove from the heat.
To serve, put individual serving amount of rice in each bowl, put onion over the rice and top with pork belly slices.
Sprinkle chopped green onion and top with an egg yolk.
Serve immediately.
ham: In a small saucepan, melt the ghee over medium heat. Add the diced onion, celery, and minced garlic, and sauté until softened, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the diced potato, diced ham, and broth to the saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the potato is tender.
Stir in the milk and continue to simmer for another 2-3 minutes.
Using a potato masher or fork, mash some of the cooked potato in the soup to thicken it slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
Place the chicken breast between two sheets of plastic wrap or parchment paper. Use a meat mallet or rolling pin to pound the chicken breast to an even thickness, about 1/4 inch thick.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper on both sides.
Place a slice of ham and a slice of Swiss cheese on one half of the chicken breast.
Fold the other half of the chicken breast over the ham and cheese to create a stuffed chicken breast.
Secure the edges of the chicken breast with toothpicks to hold the filling in place.
Place the flour, beaten egg, and breadcrumbs in three separate shallow dishes.

Dredge the stuffed chicken breast in the flour, shaking off any excess. Then dip it into the beaten egg, allowing any excess to drip off. Finally, coat it evenly with breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray. Place the breaded chicken breast in the basket.
Place the broccoli florets in a microwave-safe bowl with a little water. Microwave for 2-3 minutes, or until the broccoli is slightly tender.
Add the partially cooked broccoli to the air fryer basket around the chicken breast.
Air fry at 375°F for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the chicken is golden brown and cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the chicken and broccoli from the air fryer and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the air-fried Chicken Cordon Bleu with the steamed broccoli and your favorite side dishes.
Peel the white asparagus.
Wash and finely chop the fresh parsley and set aside.
Boil the potatoes for about 10 to 15 minutes. Poke them with a fork to make sure they're fully cooked.
Boil the eggs for about 7 minutes.
Place the peeled asparagus in the pan filled with the prepared water.
Don't let it come to a boil, let it simmer on medium-low heat for about 15 minutes. They are done when you can slightly bend them.
Turn down the heat and let them wait in the water until they are ready to serve. Serve weith hollandaise.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
While the air fryer is heating up, prepare the chicken breast. Lay it flat on a clean surface and season it with salt, pepper, paprika, and garlic powder.
Place one slice of ham and one slice of Swiss cheese on top of the chicken breast.
Place one slice of ham and one slice of Swiss cheese on top of the chicken breast.
In a shallow dish, combine the breadcrumbs with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Roll the stuffed chicken breast in the breadcrumb mixture, coating it evenly.
Lightly coat the air fryer basket with cooking spray to prevent sticking.
Place the breaded chicken roll into the air fryer basket, seam side down.
Cook in the preheated air fryer for about 18-20 minutes or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) and the exterior is crispy and golden brown.
Carefully remove the chicken from the air fryer, let it rest for a minute, then remove any toothpicks before slicing.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Lightly grease a 6-inch pie dish with cooking spray.

Lay one sheet of phyllo dough in the prepared dish, allowing the edges to hang over the sides. Lightly brush the sheet with cooking spray or melted butter. Repeat with the remaining sheets, layering them and brushing each with cooking spray or melted butter.

In a mixing bowl, combine the diced ham, salami, provolone cheese, mozzarella cheese, Parmesan cheese, ricotta cheese, egg, dried oregano, dried basil, salt, black pepper, and diced tomatoes. Mix until well combined.
Spoon the filling mixture into the phyllo dough-lined dish, spreading it out evenly.
Fold the overhanging edges of the phyllo dough over the filling to create a rustic crust.
Bake the pizza rustica in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set.
Remove the pizza rustica from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
In a bowl, beat the eggs until well mixed. Stir in the chopped ham and shredded cheese. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add the olive oil. Pour the egg mixture into the skillet.
Cook for a few minutes until the edges start to set.
Carefully fold the omelette in half with a spatula.
Continue cooking for another minute or so until it's fully set but still moist inside.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Meanwhile bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook lasagna noodles in boiling water until al dente. Drain, and cut noodles in half crosswise.
Melt ghee in a skillet over medium heat. Saute garlic just until fragrant. Stir in cream. Gradually mix in milk, and season with thyme. Simmer sauce gently until thick. Remove from heat.
Grease a baking dish. Layer noodles, sauce, asparagus, ham, and mozzarella cheese in three layers, each starting with noodles, and ending with shredded cheese on the top.
Cover the dish, and cook in the oven for 10 minutes, or until cheese is melted and bubbly. Let stand for 5 to 10 minutes before serving.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Grease a muffin tin with oil.
In a medium bowl whisk together eggs, heavy cream, salt, pepper, tomatoes, ham, and zucchini.
Pour into tin, garnish with cheddar, and bake for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F .

Split each zucchini in half, lengthwise, and remove the seeds. Sprinkle with salt and let sit for 10 minutes. Then blot off the drops of liquid with paper towels and place the halves in a greased baking dish.
Chop the ham coarsely and mix with cottage cheese, sour cream, red onion, tomato and spices. Add a third of the grated cheese. Salt and pepper to taste.
Distribute the mixture into the halves of zucchini and sprinkle the remaining cheese on top. Bake for approximately 20–30 minutes.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add spaghetti and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente, drain and set aside.
In a large skillet over medium heat, melt butter and add garlic, onion, mushrooms and ham. Saute mixture until onions are translucent.
In a large bowl toss cooked spaghetti with vegetable mixture. Garnish with cheese and parsley.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Roll out the puff pastry sheet on a lightly floured surface and press it into a 6-inch tart or pie pan, trimming any excess.
Spread the diced ham evenly over the bottom of the pastry shell.
Sprinkle the shredded cheese over the ham.
In a small bowl, whisk together the milk and egg until well combined. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Pour the egg mixture over the ham and cheese in the pastry shell.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, or until the quiche is set and the pastry is golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Bring a large pot of slightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook accordingly to instructions. Drain and set aside.
Cut the ham into very small pieces. Fry in a pan over high heat for 8 minutes. Set aside. Cut the bell pepper into small cubes.
Combine Greek yogurt, lemon juice, thyme, olive oil, and salt. Mix well until everything is combined.
In a large bowl, add cooked pasta, ham, diced bell pepper, green beans. and lemon mayonnaise dressing and stir well. Transfer to small bowls and serve.
Sift flour and baking powder into a bowl. Add sliced spring onions, chopped parsley, corn and ham. Season with salt and pepper and stir to combine.
Make a well in the centre, add eggs and milk, then stir to form a batter.
Heat a frying pan, add a film of oil and drop heaped tablespoonfuls of the batter into the pan. Cook for 3 minutes on each side.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Whisk together eggs and milk and set aside. Heat ghee in small non-stick saute pan over medium-high heat adn add spinach, cook for 3 minutes, then add egg mixture.
When eggs begin to set, add ham and cheese and place into oven for 3-4 minutes, until fluffy and golden. Serve immediately.
Heat the frying pan to high and lightly toast the bread on both sides.
Spread 1 tsp mustard over one side, then top with the ham and cheese.
Whisk the remaining mustard and vinegar with 1 tsp oil and seasoning to make the dressing for the spinach.
Grill the croque for 3 mins, or until the cheese is bubbling and turning golden.
Meanwhile, heat a non-stick frying pan.
Add a little oil, then crack in the egg and gently fry to your liking.
When cooked, lift it on top of the toast.
Toss the dressing with the spinach, and cornichons if using,  Plate the entire meal and serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Press the pie crust into a small tart pan or pie dish.
Sprinkle diced ham and shredded Swiss cheese evenly over the bottom of the pie crust.
In a small bowl, beat eggs and heavy cream together until well combined.
Season the egg mixture with salt, pepper,.
Pour the egg mixture over the ham and cheese in the pie crust.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, or until the quiche is set and golden brown on top.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and rinse with cold water.
Add ham, cheese, pineapple chunks, and onion to a large bowl.
In another small bowl, whisk the garlic powder, vinegar, salt, pepper, Greek yogurt, and pineapple juice until creamy.
Pour the dressing over the salad, and mix to combine.
Preheat your oven to 425°F.
Place the pizza crust on a baking sheet or pizza stone.

Spread the ricotta cheese evenly over the pizza crust. Scatter the torn ham pieces over the ricotta cheese.

Arrange the asparagus pieces on top of the ham.
Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella cheese over the toppings.
Drizzle a little olive oil over the pizza, and season with salt and pepper to taste. Add red pepper flakes if you like some heat.
Transfer the pizza to the preheated oven and bake for about 12-15 minutes or until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbly and slightly browned.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add the pasta, and cook for 8 minutes, or until tender. Drain.
Meanwhile, heat the oil in a small skillet over medium-high heat. Fry the ham in the oil until browned, stirring constantly.
Remove from the heat and set aside. When the pasta is done and drained, mix in the ham and Swiss cheese until cheese is melted. Top with shredded cheese.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Place the bread on the trays. Spray with a little olive oil. Bake, turning once, for 10 minutes
Meanwhile, prepare pesto cream cheese. In a bowl, stir cream cheese with a fork to soften it up. Add pesto and mix well to incorporate.
Spread the pesto on the bread, top with mozzarella and ham. Serve.
mustard: Mix mayonnaise, mustard, and cayenne together in a bowl to make the sauce.
Trim off ends of bread. Cut loaf in half and evenly split bread to make tops and bottoms of 2 sandwiches. Spread each half on both sides generously with the mayo-mustard sauce.

Divide sandwich ingredients between the two bottom halves in this order: 2 slices Swiss cheese, 3 slices ham, hot cooked pork, pickle slices, and 2 more slices of Swiss cheese. Place tops on sandwiches.
Melt butter in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Place sandwiches in skillet and press down with a heavy weight, toast until bread is crisp, 3-4 minutes per side.
Place pork chops on a paper towel to absorb any excess moisture. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Whisk egg and soy sauce together in a small bowl. Place flour on a small plate and bread crumbs in another.

Dredge a pork chop in the flour, pressing it in using your fingertips to cover all crevices along the surface. Turn to evenly coat all sides. Dip the pork into the egg mixture, coating completely. Transfer immediately to the bowl of bread crumbs, pressing in to evenly coat. Repeat process with the second pork chop.

Melt the butter in a small frying pan over high heat. Once it begins to foam, stir it and watch it carefully, it will brown in 1-2 minutes. Stir the butter a few times to ensure it browns evenly. Remove the pan from the heat. Add the olive oil to the pan then and set it aside for the butter to cool slightly.

In a medium-sized bowl, whisk the maple syrup, balsamic vinegar, apple cider vinegar, and mustard. Slowly drizzle in the brown butter and oil, whisking the entire time. Once all the oil and butter have been added, whisk the dressing quickly for several seconds so it becomes creamy.
Store the dressing at room temperature until you are ready to use it.

Put the cabbage, carrots and spring onions in a large bowl and stir to combine. Mix the mayonnaise with the mustard in another small bowl and drizzle over. Fold everything together to coat in the creamy sauce, then season with salt and pepper.

Heat oil in a wide pan or wok on medium-high heat to 350°F. Lower a pork chop into the oil. Fry until bottom side is golden, 2 to 3 minutes. Turn; cook until other side turns golden and pork is slightly pink in the center.
Pick up the cutlets and hold them on their side for a few seconds to let the oil drip off. Drain on a paper towel.
Place the cutlets on the plate and serve them with brown dressing and coleslaw.
Heat the ghee in a small pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pot. Cook until the onions are translucent and fragrant.
Sprinkle the flour over the onions and garlic. Stir well to combine and cook for another minute to cook off the raw flour taste.
Slowly whisk in the chicken or vegetable broth, ensuring there are no lumps.
Pour in the milk and continue stirring until the mixture thickens slightly.
Add the Dijon mustard and whole grain mustard to the pot. Stir well to combine.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Continue cooking the soup over low heat for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Remove from heat and ladle the Mosterdsoep into a bowl.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
, Boil pasta for 3-5 min or to al dente.
Drain pasta and reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
