While pasta is cooking, in a large saucepan (4 quart) or medium pot melt butter over medium heat then add flour, cook 1 minute whisking constantly.
While whisking slowly pour in milk, add in mustard and garlic powder.
Increase heat slightly, then bring mixture to a gentle simmer whisking constantly.
Reduce heat to low add in cream cheese and whisk until melted.
Remove saucepan from heat add in the shredded cheeses half at a time, and whisk to melt, while returning to warm heat as needed to melt fully.
Season sauce with salt to taste.
Toss pasta with cheese sauce, thin with pasta water a splash at a time as needed.
Serve immediately.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F
Divide the ground beef into a 1/4 pound patty.
Season both sides of the patty with salt and pepper.
Place the hamburger bun in the air fryer basket, cut side down, and the seasoned beef patty beside it.
Air fry the hamburger bun and beef patty at 375°F for 8-10 minutes, flipping the patty halfway through, until the beef is cooked to your desired level of doneness and the bun is toasted.
Once the beef patty is cooked, place the slice of cheese on top of it and return it to the air fryer for an additional 1-2 minutes to melt the cheese.
Assemble your cheeseburger by placing the beef patty with melted cheese on the toasted bun.
Add any desired toppings such as lettuce, tomato, onion, pickles, ketchup, or mustard.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Heat butter in a skillet and saute onion, garlic, zucchini and mushrooms until softened. Drain and set aside.
Heat butter in a skillet and saute onion, garlic, zucchini and mushrooms until softened. Drain and keep aside.
In a large bowl whisk eggs and then add all the remaining ingredients to the bowl, mix with the hand whisker to combine well. Pour the mixture over the veggie mixture, and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove from oven add a bit of basil, let it cool and then cut into wedges to serve.
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, Dijon mustard, 1 tablespoon water and 1/2 teaspoon each salt and pepper.

Heat oil in a skillet and fry the bacon pieces for 4 to 5 minutes. Add eggs and cook, stirring with rubber spatula every few seconds, to desired doneness, 2 to 3 minutes for medium-soft eggs. Fold together with bacon, spinach, and Gruyere cheese.
Preheat a sandwich press or a skillet over medium heat.
Slice the Cuban bread or French bread in half horizontally.
Spread mustard on one side of each bread slice.
Layer the roasted pork, ham, Swiss cheese, and dill pickle slices on one bread slice.
Top with the other bread slice to form a sandwich.
Lightly brush the outside of the sandwich with olive oil.
Place the sandwich in the preheated sandwich press or skillet and cook until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is melted, about 3-5 minutes per side.
Remove from the heat and let it cool for a minute before slicing.
Serve the Classic Cuban Midnight Sandwich hot.
Combine tuna, mayonnaise, tomatoes, lime juice, red onion, mustard, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Mix well. Serve in avocados.
Marinate the meat with the marinade, let stand 1 hour in refrigerator.
Stir ingredients pickles, let stand 2 hours in refrigerator.
Mix sauce ingredients well, set aside.
Heat the ghee.
Saute onion until fragrant and enter the tomato.
Stir until wilted.
Pour the water.
Let it boil.
Put in the meat then leave until the color changes.
Add soy sauce, worcestershire sauce, nutmeg powder, pepper, and salt. Leave it until the meat is cooked.
Serve the meat with a complementary, pickles and sauces.
Fill a small saucepan with water and bring it to a boil. Add the smoked sausage to the boiling water and simmer for about 10 minutes, or until heated through.
While the sausage is simmering, slice the baguette or bread roll lengthwise, leaving one side intact. Spread mustard on the inside of the bread, to taste.
Once the smoked sausage is heated through, remove it from the water and drain. Place the smoked sausage on the bed of sauerkraut, inside the bread.
Close the bread, pressing it gently to hold the ingredients together. Serve the Broodje Rookworst immediately as a tasty and satisfying meal.
Slice the croissants in half lengthwise. Spread a thin layer of mustard on each cut slice. Place lettuce leaves on the bottom slices of the croissants.
Top with cheese, ham and tomatoes, then close the croissant with the second half. Enjoy.
In small bowl, mix mustard and mayonnaise. Open the sliced bread, spread the mixture on both pieces.
Add the lettuce, meats and tomatoes, close the sandwich with the second half of bread and enjoy.
Place the turkey ham slices on bottom half of bun.
Layer the sliced avocado, lettuce, and tomato on top of the turkey ham.
Spread mustard on the other half of bun and place it on top to form a sandwich.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve immediately.
Mix olive oil, garlic, and mustard and toss turkey in this sauce.
On wooden skewers thread turkey and bell pepper. Place in a pan and ccok for 10 minutes on each side.
Place the potatoes in a skillet, cover with cold water and bring to the boil. Cook until tender, about 15 minutes, drain and cut into chunks.
Place tuna in a small bowl. Use a fork to break the chunks into small pieces. Add potato chunks, tomatoes, celery, scallion and baby spinach and mix together.
Add the olive oil and mustard, to moisten the tuna to your preference. Sprinkle with sea salt and freshly ground pepper, and enjoy.
Heat oven to 400 degrees F/fan 350 degrees F/gas 6.
Mix the cheeses and mustard together.
Cut a slit into the side of each chicken breast, then stuff with the mustard mixture.
Wrap each stuffed chicken breast with 2 bacon rashers – not too tightly, but enough to hold the chicken together.
Season, place on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 mins.
Serve and enjoy with baked potatoes.
Place the eggplant slices on a plate, and cook in the microwave for about 5 minutes, or until the centers are cooked.
Meanwhile melt 1 tbsp ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook mushrooms for 3 minutes then set aside.
Melt the remaining ghee and fry eggplant slices until lightly toasted on each side, and place one slice of cheese onto each one. Cook until cheese has melted, and remove from the skillet.
Place eggplant on hamburger buns, and top with lettuce, bell pepper, onion and dress with mustard.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Grease a baking dish with ghee.
Cook ground beef and onion in a large, nonstick skillet over medium heat until beef is browned and onions are soft and translucent, 5 to 10 minutes.
Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper. Stir in 1/2 cup Cheddar cheese until well combined.
Remove from heat. Add ketchup, mayonnaise, pickles, and mustard. Transfer mixture to buttered baking dish and top with remaining 1/2 cup Cheddar cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted, 7 to 10 minutes.
Mix together the tuna, mayonnaise, mustard, relish, orange juice, pecans, garlic salt, onion powder, and black pepper.
Cover and refrigerate until ready to use. Serve with bread.
corn: In a nonstick skillet, heat oil over medium high heat and cook chicken with salt and pepper for about 8 minutes or until golden brown.
In a bowl, whisk together egg whites and milk. Stir in spinach and corn. Stir in cooked chicken.
Return skillet to medium low heat and add egg mixture, stirring gently.
Cook, lifting edges with spatula to let egg flow under to cook for about 6 minutes or until golden brown and set on top.
Preheat your air fryer to 400°F.
Cut the ear of corn into "ribs" by slicing it lengthwise into quarters or smaller pieces.
Place the corn ribs in a bowl and drizzle with olive oil, then season with salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder, and chili powder to taste. Toss to coat evenly.
Arrange the seasoned corn ribs in a single layer in the air fryer basket.
Air fry the corn ribs for about 10-12 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through cooking, until they are tender and slightly crispy.
Once done, remove the corn ribs from the air fryer and transfer them to a serving plate.
Serve the corn ribs hot with lime wedges for squeezing over the top and garnish with chopped cilantro, if desired.
Enjoy your delicious corn ribs!
In a large pot, place the potatoes, celery and leaves, bacon and onion. Pour in enough water to cover. Bring to a boil.
Remove the celery leaves, and stir in the bouillon cubes until dissolved. Mix in the corn. Reduce heat to medium-low, and cook 25 minutes. Serve with a basil leaf garnish.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil and brown ground meat. Drain excess liquid.
Add bell pepper, corn, and seasonings. Saute over medium heat until just crisp-tender, about 5 minutes.
Place the mixture in tortillas and garnish with some parsley.
Cook corn according to the package directions and set aside.
Melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in flour. Cook until bubbly, about 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the cream and milk. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens. Turn heat to low and add salt, and pepper. Stir in cooked corn.
Pour the corn mixture into a small greased casserole and top with hParmesan. Broil until bubbly.
In a medium bowl, stir together the tuna, eggs, corn and mayonnaise. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Place half of the mixture onto 1 slice of bread and the other half on another slice of bread. Top with remaining slices of bread. Serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add rice, cook and stir until heated through, 2 to 3 minutes
Stir in soy sauce and sesame oil. Add onion, corn, peas and carrot, cook and stir until tender, 5 to 10 minutes.
In a salad bowl, combine diced avocado, diced red onion, corn, sliced cucumber, and halved cherry tomatoes.
Drizzle olive oil and balsamic vinegar over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss gently to combine all the ingredients.
Serve immediately as a fresh and flavorful salad.
In a salad bowl, combine rocket leaves, diced tomato, corn, diced cucumber, and diced sun-dried tomatoes.
Drizzle olive oil and balsamic vinegar over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss gently to combine all the ingredients.
Serve immediately as a flavorful and nutritious salad.
Heat the oven to 450°F.
Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spread half of a bag of tortilla chips in an even layer.
Sprinkle half of the cheddar cheese over the chips. Repeat for another layer. Bake until the cheese is melted and bubbly, about 5 minutes
Meanwhile, add the avocado to a small mixing bowl. Season using salt and pepper to taste, add olive oil, 1/2 of the lime juice, and mash with a fork.
Prepare the pico de gallo by mixing the diced tomatoes, cilantro, lime juice, white onion, and jalapeno in a separate mixing bowl. Set aside.
Remove from the oven and evenly top with warm corn, guacamole, and pico de gallo.
In a medium size mixing bowl add the black-eyed peas, black beans, corn, tomatoes, bell pepper, and red onion. Stir to combine.
In a jar, add olive oil, vinegar, salt and lemon juice, shake to combine. Pour the dressing over the veggies and stir to combine. Add in the avocado, give it another mix and enjoy.
Combine olive oil, vegetable oil, cider vinegar, and sugar in a saucepan, bring to a boil, remove from heat, and cool to room temperature.
Stir pinto beans, black-eyed peas, corn, onion, celery, red bell pepper, and cilantro together in a large bowl.
Pour cooled oil mixture over bean mixture and toss to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate, stirring occasionally, for 24 hours.
Preheat oven to 355°F.
In a mixing bowl, add the zucchini, corn, butter, salt and pepper and mix well.
Add the eggs, oregano and baking powder and mix well.
Add the coconut flour and mix until the batter becomes thick. Fold through the cheddar cheese.
Bake for 25 minutes, until the muffins are golden.
In a skillet, heat the ghee (or vegetable oil) over medium heat.
Add the chopped spring onions and thawed corn kernels to the skillet. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they are tender.
Sprinkle the chili powder over the corn and spring onions, and season with salt and black pepper. Stir to coat evenly.
Place one corn tortilla in the skillet.
Spread half of the shredded cheese evenly over the tortilla.
Spoon the sautéed corn and spring onion mixture over the cheese.
Sprinkle the remaining cheese on top.
Place the second corn tortilla on top to create a quesadilla.
Cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side until the tortillas are crispy, and the cheese is melted.
Remove the quesadilla from the skillet and let it cool for a minute before cutting it into wedges.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine canned corn kernels, cornmeal, plain Greek yogurt, milk, honey, and salt. Mix until well combined.
Lightly grease an oven-safe dish with cooking spray or olive oil spray.
Pour the corn mixture into the greased dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is set.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a minute.
Serve your Awesome and Easy Creamy Corn Casserole as a delicious side dish.
Cook the ear of corn by grilling or boiling until tender. Once cooked, let it cool slightly.
Cut the kernels off the cob into a bowl.
Add the mayonnaise, lime juice, and chili powder to the bowl with the corn kernels. Mix well to combine.
Sprinkle the grated Cotija cheese over the top of the esquites.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro, if desired.
Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Add garlic and onion, cook for 5 minutes until translucent but not golden. Add carrots and stir through. Turn heat up to high and add wine.
Stir then let it simmer rapidly until it's mostly evaporated. Add flour and stir for 30 seconds - it will become sludgy!
Slow pour in broth while stirring to dissolve paste, then stir in milk. Add potato, corn, salt and pepper.
Bring to simmer then turn heat down so it's simmering gently.
Cook, stirring every now and then, for 8 minutes or until potato is almost cooked.
Add fish and peas, simmer for 3 minutes or until fish is just cooked (should flake easily).
Adjust salt and pepper to taste. If soup is too thick, add a touch of water or milk.
Serve, garnished with green onions and crusty bread on the side for dunking!
In a small skillet heat olive oil, add onion and cook for 3 minutes.
Add vegetable broth, salt, pepper and 2 cups of water.
Add beans, corn, peas, broccoli and cauliflower florets. Bring to a boil and simmer for 25 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Combine ingredients in a large bowl. Scoop mixture into mini muffin tins coated to prevent sticking.
Press the mixture down with the back of a spoon to tightly compact in each muffin tin well.
Bake for 20 minutes or until firm and starting to brown.
Bring a pot of water to the boil. Cook the rice to package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool. Once cooled, fluff the rice using a fork.
In a separate pan, add olive oil and heat on medium heat. Add onion, a pinch of salt, and saute for 2-3 minutes. Add zucchini, saute for additional 3 minutes.
Add tomatoes, and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes. A minute or so before finishing the sauce off, add corn and stir the mixture well. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Divide the rice between plates, top with vegetable sauce and enjoy.
lemon: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a lightly greased baking dish.
Season the salmon fillets with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until the salmon flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is cooking, prepare the Creamy Garlic Dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together minced garlic, Greek yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Once the salmon is done, remove it from the oven and let it cool slightly.
In a large salad bowl, arrange the mixed lettuce and tomato slices.
Place the cooked salmon fillets on top of the salad.
Squeeze lemon wedges over the salmon and salad for extra flavor.
Drizzle the Creamy Garlic Dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad gently to combine all the ingredients.
In a small bowl, combine the drained and rinsed chickpeas with 1/2 teaspoon of smoked paprika and 1/2 teaspoon of cumin. Toss to coat the chickpeas in the spices.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the seasoned chickpeas to the skillet.
Sauté the chickpeas for about 4-5 minutes until they become slightly crispy and browned. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Remove from heat and let them cool.
In a salad bowl, place 2 cups of chopped lettuce.
Add the thinly sliced radishes, halved cherry tomatoes, and sliced cucumber to the bowl with the lettuce.
Drizzle the salad with the juice of half a lemon and 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the sautéed chickpeas have cooled slightly, add them to the salad.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined.
Preheat your grill to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, ground cumin, paprika, ground coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture evenly over both sides of the chicken breasts.
Grill the chicken breasts for about 6-8 minutes per side or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F. Remove from heat and let them rest for a few minutes before slicing.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the Cauliflower 'Rice' Tabbouleh.
Cut the cauliflower into florets and place them in a food processor. Pulse until the cauliflower resembles rice grains.
In a large bowl, combine the cauliflower 'rice', chopped parsley, diced tomato, and diced bell pepper.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the dressing over the cauliflower 'rice' mixture and toss to combine.
In a saucepan, bring 4 cups of salted water to a boil. Add 1 cup of orzo pasta and cook for 8-10 minutes or until al dente.
Drain and rinse with cold water to cool.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooked and cooled orzo, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, finely chopped red onion, sliced Kalamata olives, and crumbled feta cheese.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil and the juice of 1 lemon.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss the salad to combine all the ingredients.
Optionally, garnish with fresh parsley.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
Place the seasoned chicken breasts on a baking sheet.
Bake the chicken in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes or until they reach an internal temperature of 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the salad. In a large bowl, combine the torn lettuce, cherry tomatoes, and croutons.
Squeeze the juice of both lemons into a small bowl, and whisk in the olive oil to make a lemon vinaigrette.
Once the chicken is cooked, let it cool for a few minutes, then slice it into thin strips.
Drizzle the lemon vinaigrette over the salad.
Toss the salad to coat the ingredients with the dressing.
Top the salad with the sliced chicken.
Place the cubed potato in a pot of boiling water and cook for about 10-12 minutes or until the potato cubes are tender but still firm. Drain and let them cool.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled potato cubes, halved cherry tomatoes, sliced black olives, chopped roasted red peppers, and fresh Italian herbs.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss everything together to combine.
Cut the lemon in half and squeeze the juice into a blender or mixing glass.
Add cold water to the blender.
If desired, add honey for sweetness.
Add Greek yogurt to the blender.
Blend the mixture until smooth and creamy.
If using, add ice cubes to a glass.
Pour the creamy lemonade over the ice cubes.
Serve immediately and enjoy!
Preheat a gas grill to high. Drizzle salmon and peppers with oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Place them on a grill. Grill the salmon until browned, 5 minutes on each side. Cook the peppers, turning frequently, until tender and well browned, 10 minutes total.
Place them on a plate and serve with arugula and slices of lemon.
Heat oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Fry prawns in the hot oil until golden. Remove to paper towels to drain.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the chile sauce, pomegranate juice, Sriracha hot sauce,tomato sauce, soy sauce, lemon juice and lemon zest.
Pour the sauce into a wok, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Cook until the sauce has reduced by about 10 percent and has thickened slightly.
Mix the prawns into the sauce, and toss to coat. Serve on top of spinach and sprinkle with red chili pepper.
In a small pot, bring 1 cup of water to a boil.
Stir in the couscous, remove the pot from heat, cover it, and let it sit for about 5 minutes or until the couscous has absorbed the water.
Fluff the couscous with a fork.
In a mixing bowl, combine the cooked couscous, lemon juice, lemon zest, olive oil, and fresh herbs.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss everything together until well combined.
Serve your Lemony Herb Couscous as a delicious and flavorful side dish.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a bowl, combine the rolled oats, baking powder, and ground cinnamon.
Add the lemon juice, lemon zest, unsweetened applesauce, and honey or maple syrup to the dry ingredients. Mix until well combined.
Gently fold in the fresh blueberries into the oat mixture.
Lightly grease a muffin tin or ramekin with oil.
Spoon the oatmeal mixture into the muffin tin or ramekin, filling it to the top.
Place the tin or ramekin in the preheated oven and bake for about 20-25 minutes or until the oatmeal cakes are firm and slightly golden on top.
Once baked, remove the oatmeal cakes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Carefully remove the oatmeal cakes from the muffin tin or ramekin.
In a large skillet, heat oil over medium high heat until hot. Add fillets and squeeze ½ of the lemon's juice over the tops.
Sprinkle with pepper to taste. Cook for 4 minutes and turn. Squeeze with the remaining lemon's juice and sprinkle with pepper to taste.
Continue to cook until fillets flake easily with a fork. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the 4 ounces of salmon fillet with a drizzle of 1/2 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and pepper to taste.
Place the seasoned salmon fillet on a baking sheet.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for about 12-15 minutes, or until it flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is baking, reheat or cook 1/2 cup of brown rice if it's not already cooked.
In a serving bowl, place the 1/2 cup of cooked brown rice.
Once the salmon is done baking, remove it from the oven and squeeze the juice of 1/2 lemon over it.
Place 1 cup of fresh spinach on top of the brown rice.
Top the spinach with the baked salmon fillet.
Drizzle the remaining 1/2 tablespoon of olive oil over the salmon and spinach.
Heat half of the oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the asparagus and cook for 5 minutes, turning them half way.
Remove cooked asparagus from pan and transfer to a platter, set aside. Take the remaining olive oil and pour it into a mixing bowl.
Whisk in lemon juice, salt and pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings. Drizzle lemon dressing over the prepared asparagus and sprinkle with cheese.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the boneless, skinless chicken breast with salt and black pepper to taste.
Heat the olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken breast to the skillet and cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side, until it's browned.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the chopped artichoke hearts and capers. Sauté for about 1-2 minutes to heat them through.
Pour in the chicken broth and lemon juice, scraping the bottom of the skillet to deglaze and pick up any flavorful bits.
Return the cooked chicken breast to the skillet.
Cover the skillet with a lid or aluminum foil.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for about 15-20 minutes.
Remove the Chicken with Artichokes and Capers from the oven.
Brush both sides of the salmon fillets with the olive oil. Add the lemon zest to the fish. Add the garlic, salt, and pepper.
Place the fillets on a grill. Cook for 6minutes per side, depending on the thickness of your fillets. Serve immediately.
In a pot, combine spaghetti, broccoli florets, minced garlic, olive oil, vegetable broth, and lemon juice.
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat.
Once boiling, reduce the heat to simmer and cover the pot. Let it cook for about 10-12 minutes or until the pasta is al dente, and the broccoli is tender.
Check the pot occasionally and stir to prevent sticking. If needed, you can add a little more vegetable broth.
Season the pasta with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the pasta is cooked, remove the pot from the heat.
Stir in grated Parmesan cheese and mix until well combined.
Garnish with fresh parsley if desired.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Place the chicken breast in the skillet and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side until golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and transfer it to a plate. Cover to keep warm.
In the same skillet, add the minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds, stirring constantly.
Pour in the chicken broth, lemon juice, and lemon zest. Bring it to a simmer and let it cook for 2-3 minutes to reduce slightly.
Return the chicken to the skillet and spoon some of the lemon sauce over the chicken.
Cook for an additional minute to let the flavors meld.
Remove from heat, garnish with fresh parsley if desired, and serve.
Use a pair of kitchen shears to cut lengthwise about halfway into the shrimp, without cutting through the tail.
Devein, pat dry, and place in a large bowl. Toss shrimp with 2 teaspoons oil and season with salt, pepper, paprika, and cayenne. Marinate in the refrigerator, 20 minutes.
Sprinkle shrimp with rice flour and toss until fully coated.
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat remaining ¼ cup oil until shimmering.
Add shrimp in a single layer and fry until shrimp are pink and rice flour is golden, Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate.
Melt butter over medium heat until it begins to foam, then add garlic. Cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant and golden, 2 to 3 minutes. Add shrimp and toss until fully coated.
Serve with lemon wedges. Spoon over remaining garlic butter.
In a mixing bowl, combine the drained and rinsed chickpeas with diced cucumber.
Add crumbled feta cheese to the bowl.
Squeeze the juice of half a lemon over the ingredients in the bowl.
Drizzle olive oil into the mixture.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and dill for added flavor.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined.
pecan: In a bowl, combine the chopped kale, thinly sliced apple, crumbled feta cheese, pecan halves, and pomegranate seeds.
In a small bowl or jar, whisk together the dressing ingredients: olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, salt, and black pepper to taste.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad gently to ensure that all the ingredients are coated with the dressing.
coconut: Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper. Line a second cookie sheet with several layers of paper towels.
Mix coconut flour, onion powder, and garlic powder in a small bowl. Whisk eggs thoroughly in a second bowl.
Grind macadamia nuts in a food processor until coarsely chopped. Empty ground nuts into a third bowl, mix in coconut until blended.
Place each shrimp in the coconut flour mixture, lightly coating on all sides.
Then dip in the egg, and finally in the macadamia-coconut mixture. Place on the parchment-lined cookie sheet, repeat with remaining shrimp.
Heat ghee in a large skillet over medium heat until melted, add shrimp. Cook, turning often, until golden brown and evenly cooked.
Drain shrimp on paper towels. Season with salt and pepper and serve.
In a food processor, add the oats, and pulse for 20 seconds in order to break the oats down slightly.
Add the rest of the ingredients and pulse until fully combined.
Roll into 12 balls and refrigerate, then serve.
In a food processor, combine the chopped dried apricots, almond butter, and ghee.
Pulse until the mixture becomes a paste-like consistency.
Transfer the mixture to a mixing bowl.
Add the unsweetened shredded coconut, mixed seeds, unsweetened protein powder, and a pinch of salt to the bowl.
Mix everything together until well combined.
Line a small dish or container with parchment paper, leaving some excess paper hanging over the edges for easy removal.
Transfer the mixture into the lined dish and press it down firmly to create an even layer.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour to allow the bars to set.
Once set, use the parchment paper overhang to lift the bars out of the dish.
Cut the mixture into individual protein bars.
Combine cashew nuts, apricots, coconut, and dates in a food processor bowl. Pulse ingredients until mixture is crumbly.
Add citrus zest, flour, spices, and salt to mixture. Pulse ingredients to combine well, and process on high speed until mixture starts to stick together.
Line a tray with parchment paper. With clean hands, shape the mixture into balls. Garnish with some sesame seeds and store in refrigerator,
Blend cashews and coconut in a food processor, add dates and pulse until combined.
Add coconut oil and pulse until mixture is thick and sticks together.
Transfer to a sheet of waxed paper, form into a square, folding sides of waxed paper over the top. Refrigerate until solid, at least 30 minutes.
Place frozen berries, banana, protein powder, strawberries, coconut, and chia seeds in a blender, pulse briefly to combine. Pour in orange juice, blend until smooth.
Preheat your air fryer to 370°F.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper on both sides.
In a shallow dish, mix together the shredded coconut and breadcrumbs.
Dip the chicken breast into the beaten egg, allowing any excess to drip off.
Coat the chicken breast evenly with the coconut and breadcrumb mixture, pressing gently to adhere.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the breaded chicken breast in the air fryer basket.
Air fry at 370°F for 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the chicken is cooked through and the coating is crispy and golden brown.
Once cooked, remove the chicken from the air fryer and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the air-fried coconut chicken hot with your favorite side dishes.
Place chia seeds in a bowl.
Whisk coconut milk, unsweetened coconut milk, agave nectar, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and salt together in a bowl, pour over chia seeds and stir well.
Allow coconut milk-chia seed mixture to soak until thickened, at least 20 minutes, or cover bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
Stir and top with blueberries and coconut shavings.
Prepare oatmeal according to package directions on stove top or in microwave, using oats and water.
Peel and slice banana in half. In a separate bowl, mash half banana with a fork and stir into the oatmeal along with coconut extract. Stir in honey. Slice the other half of banana into slices.
Top oatmeal with coconut and banana slices.
Add coconut, roasted gram lentils, garlic, chili, cumin seeds, sugar, lemon juice and water to a blender and blend until you get smooth chutney.
Add more water if needed to get to the desired consistency.
Take out the chutney in a serving bowl.
Heat oil in a tadka pan over medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and allow them to pop.
Turn the heat off and then add asafetida/hing and curry leaves.
Be careful as you add curry leaves as the oil may splatter.
Pour the tadka over the chutney and stir in.
Serve with pita bread.
Combine the pandan leaf and water in an electronic blender and blend for a minute.
Wrap the pandan in a cheese cloth or muslin cloth and squeeze to extract the juice or strain through a fine sieve.
Set aside.
Sieve the flour into a mixing bowl.
Make a well in the center and crack in the egg.
Slowly stir in the salted coconut milk.
Set aside.
Combune pandan leaf and water in a pot.
Add in the grated coconut and corn starch and continue to cook for a few minutes.
Dish out into a bowl.
Place almond milk, chia seeds, kale and 1 banana into a blender and blend until smooth.
Place into bowl and top with sliced banana, shredded coconut, pumpkin seeds, and blackberries.
In a shallow bowl, season flour with salt and pepper. In another shallow bowl, combine bread crumbs and coconut. Place eggs and flaxseed oil in a third shallow bowl.
Working with one at a time, dip prawns in flour, then eggs, then coconut mixture.
Place prawns in the basket of an air fryer and heat to 400°F . Bake until prawns are golden and cooked through, 10 to 12 minutes. Work in batches as necessary.
In a high-speed blender, food processor or mixing bowl, combine your ingredients and blend until a dough is formed.
Using your hands, form them into small balls. Put in almonds and roll balls in extra shredded coconut.
Squeeze the juice from half a lemon. Mix the lemon juice with the nut butter, coconut oil and maple syrup. Optionally, add lemon zest.
Add the desiccated coconut, proteins, and mix until you have a dough-like mixture that you can shape into truffles.
Roll the truffles in desiccated coconut and refrigerate.
In a saucepan, combine the rolled oats and coconut milk.
Add the diced mango to the oats and coconut milk mixture.
Place the saucepan over medium heat and bring the mixture to a gentle boil, stirring occasionally.
Once it starts boiling, reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for about 5-7 minutes or until the oats are cooked and the mixture thickens to your desired consistency.
Stir in the shredded coconut and a pinch of salt.
Remove the saucepan from the heat.
Transfer the Coconut-Mango Oats to a bowl.
Stir oats, chia seeds, pepitas, pineapple, peanut butter, honey, coconut, mango, blueberries, flax, and wheat germ together in a large bowl. Roll into balls.
Sift cocoa powder into a medium deep mixing bowl.
Add vanilla extract and pinch of salt.
Whisk to combine until smooth.
Add the coconut milk on top and whisk to combine.
Pour the chia seeds on top of the mixture and whisk to combine one more time.
Cover and refrigerate until the mixture thickens, and becomes pudding like, at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
In a shallow bowl, whisk together egg. In another shallow bowl, mix together coconut, almond flour and salt.
Dredge chicken strips in the whisked egg, then straight to the coconut mixture. Coat on all sides then place on a plate. Repeat with all chicken strips.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the strips to the pan. Cook for 1 minute on both sides. Place strips on a cooling rack.
Place baking sheet in the oven and cook for 10 minutes or until no pink remains inside the chicken. Let cool on baking sheet before serving.
Combine oats, almond milk, coconut, chia seeds, cinnamon, protein powder, and salt in a jar. Stir well. Mix in chopped banana.
Cover and refrigerate, 8 hours to overnight. Add more almond milk to taste just before serving.
vanilla: Combine all ingredients into a blender and blend until consistency is smooth.
Top smoothie with bits of walnuts and a sprinkle of cinnamon.
Combine water, apple, and cranberries together in a bowl; bring to a boil.
Cook mixture until cranberries burst and apple is tender, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium and stir oats, cinnamon, nutmeg, and vanilla into apple mixture.
Cook, stirring continuously, until water is absorbed and oatmeal thickens, 2 to 3 minutes.
Squeeze the oranges to obtain 1/2 cup of fresh orange juice. Remove any pulp or seeds.
In a small bowl, mix the fresh orange juice with honey or maple syrup. Adjust the sweetness to your liking. Add vanilla extract if desired and stir to combine.
Pour the orange mixture into a popsicle mold designed for a single serving.
Insert a popsicle stick into the mold.
Place the mold in the freezer and let it freeze for at least 4-6 hours or until the popsicle is completely frozen.
Preheat oven to 350ºF.
Spray coconut oil on Madeleine pan to prevent sticking.
In a large bowl, add dry ingredients and whisk to blend.
In a small bowl, add wet ingredients and mix well. Pour them into dry ingredients and mix.
Put a spoonful of dough into each Madeleine pan cake mold.
Bake for 10 minutes until done and serve.
In a large bowl, whisk together all ingredients.
Let batter sit for 10 minutes to allow liquids to absorb. Grease and heat your griddle to low-medium heat.
Using a 1 tablespoon measuring spoon, scoop batter onto griddle. Cook for 3-4 minutes, flipping when edges look dry. Cook for another 1-2 minutes on the other side.
Serve warm with blueberries, fresh fruit.
Combine oats and water in a microwave-safe bowl and cook in the microwave for about 1 1/2 minutes.
Stir in cocoa powder, and vanilla extract. Top off with as much milk as desired.
Sift flour, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and nutmeg together in a large bowl.
Whisk eggs, squash, shallots, and 1/2 tsp vanilla extract together in a separate bowl, stir into flour mixture until just mixed. Fold currants into batter.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Scoop about 1/4 cup batter and drop into hot oil.
Cook pancakes until crisp and lightly browned, 6 to 8 minutes per side. Repeat with remaining batter.
Mix sour cream, vanilla extract together in a bowl. Serve atop the squash pancakes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F and line an 8x8-inch baking pan with parchment paper.
Lightly spray the exposed pan sides with nonstick cooking spray.
Make the fruit filling: In a medium bowl, mix together the strawberries, rhubarb, lemon juice and vanilla.
Make the crumble: In a food processor, combine the oats, nuts, almond flour, flaxseed, cinnamon, and salt and pulse until just combined.
Add the oil and pulse again. Add the water and pulse again.
Press ⅔ of the crumble into the baking pan to form a crust. Bake 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown and firm.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 15 minutes.
Spread the fruit filling over the crust, sprinkle with the remaining crumble, and garnish with some rolled oats.
Bake for an additional 20 minutes, or until the fruit is soft and the crumble topping is lightly crisp. Let cool completely before slicing.
If not serving the same day, store the bars covered in the refrigerator.
In a mixing bowl whisk water, milk, eggs, and vanilla together.
Add flour, sugar, and salt and beat with whisk until smooth (this can be done in a blender too).
Heat a crepe pan or 8-inch skillet over medium-high heat.
Brush the pan with a little of the melted butter.
Pour ¼ cup of the batter into the middle of the pan and quickly rotate the pan so the batter spreads evenly to cover the bottom of the pan.
Cook for about 20 seconds until the edges look done.
Loosen the edges with the spatula and then flip the crepe.
Cook for 10-15 seconds and remove onto a plate.
Repeat with remaining batter and stack crepes with a paper towel between each.
Spread peanut butter on one half of each crepe then place raspberries on half of the peanut butter and fold the crepes into fourths.
In a large bowl, add flour, cocoa powder, pea protein powder, baking powder, pumpkin pie spice, and salt. Whisk together.
Add the pumpkin puree, vanilla, and almond milk. Stir to combine with a spatula.  Do not overmix.
Follow instructions on waffle maker for amount and length of time to cook. Repeat until all the batter is used.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter and flour a large 9" bread pan and set aside.
Whisk together flour, cinnamon, baking soda, and salt in a small bowl until combined. Set aside.
In a stand mixer or using a hand mixer, beat together sugars, oil, and eggs for about 1-2 minutes until slightly fluffy. Add vanilla and sour cream.
Fold in the grated zucchini. Add the remaining dry flour ingredients. Stir to combine and pour into prepared 9" bread pan. Bake for 50-60 minutes.
Let cool for about 5-10 minutes and remove from pan. Serve either cool or warm.
Line a 8x8 inch baking dish with parchment paper.
In a food processor, combine cashews, almonds, pecans, walnuts and sunflower seeds.
Pulse a few times to break up the nuts, but you still want to have some larger pieces.
Pour processed nuts into a large bowl. Add shredded coconut, chia seed and cacao nibs to the bowl.
In a small pot over medium low heat, stir together almond butter, coconut oil, salt, vanilla and cinnamon until melted together.
Pour mixture over the nuts and mix immediately. Press granola into the parchment lined baking pan. Freeze for 30 minutes or until firm.
Lift the granola bar slab out of the baking pan by lifting up the parchment paper. Cut into rectangles.
Serve straight from the freezer or defrost for 15 minutes.
Whisk together bananas and eggs in a large bowl.
Add almond flour, cinnamon, vanilla, baking powder, baking soda, and salt and mix well.
Stir in walnuts.
Pour about ⅙ of the batter onto a skillet greased with coconut oil or grass-fed butter over medium heat.
Allow to cook until bubbles start to form on the top of the pancake, then flip to cook other side.
Top with additional banana slices, walnuts, nut butter, maple syrup, etc.
In a pot over medium-low heat, whisk together almond milk, water, vanilla, cinnamon, and a pinch of salt. Bring to the boil gradually.
Add oats, banana and apple. Heat mixture to the boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Serve hot, topped with chopped walnuts, slices of peach and banana.
cinnamon: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the lentils in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Toss the cubed raw squash with a little olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast the squash in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until it becomes tender and slightly caramelized.
In a mixing bowl, whisk together the honey, ground cinnamon, apple cider vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper. This is your salad dressing.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the chopped raw kale, cooked lentils, chopped red onion, roasted cubed squash, chopped pecans, and dried cranberries.
Pour the prepared salad dressing over the ingredients in the bowl.
Toss the salad to coat all the ingredients with the dressing. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a medium saucepan, combine the green or brown lentils and 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 20-25 minutes.
While the lentils are cooking, toss the diced pumpkin with a drizzle of olive oil, ground cinnamon, salt, and black pepper on a baking sheet.
Roast in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the pumpkin is tender and slightly caramelized. Remove from the oven and let it cool.
In a large salad bowl, place the fresh spinach leaves and sliced sun-dried tomatoes.
Add the cooked lentils and roasted pumpkin to the salad bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dressing ingredients: olive oil, honey, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients to ensure they are well coated with the dressing.
De-seed the bell peppers and chop into 1” square pieces. Cut the firm tofu into 1” square pieces.⁠ Heat a barbecue or grill pan until hot.⁠
Meanwhile, mix the seasoning spices and oil in a small bowl. Thread the vegetables and tofu, alternating, onto the skewers and brush with the marinade.⁠
Cook the skewers on each side until the vegetables are cooked. Turn and repeat on all sides. Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
In a blender, combine the onion, ginger, and garlic and blend into a smooth puree with 2 tbsp or more of water.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and cook for 1 minute. Add bay leaf and cook until the bay leaf changes color.
Add the pureed onion and cook until the onion mixture is dry and does not smell raw. Stir occasionally to avoid sticking, 4 minutes.
Add cinnamon, garam masala and cayenne and mix well. Add the coconut milk, mango pulp, salt, and mix well.
Mix, cover and cook until the sauce comes to a boil, 5 minutes. Add chickpeas and mix in. Reduce the heat to low medium and cook for 10 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf and discard. Serve with rice.
In a bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, and ground cinnamon.
Add applesauce, almond milk, and the egg to the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined.
Gently fold in the grated apple.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Spoon batter onto the skillet to form pancakes of your desired size.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface, then flip and cook the other side until golden brown.
Repeat until all the batter is used.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
Mix and divide oats, milk, protein powder, yogurt, chia seeds, cinnamon and 1 shredded carrot.
Let it sit in the fridge for 2 hours to overnight. Top with the remaining shredded carrot and raisins.
In a non-stick pan over medium heat, sauté the apple slices until softened.
Add oats, almond butter, chopped nuts, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and a dash of salt to the pan.
Stir well and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the oats are heated through.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and enjoy.
In a mixing bowl, combine the rolled oats, honey, and chopped nuts.
Add a pinch of cinnamon and cocoa powder for extra flavor.
Mix the ingredients together until well combined and the mixture sticks together.
Take a small portion of the mixture and flatten it in your palm.
Place a small amount of peanut butter in the center of the flattened mixture.
Gently fold the edges of the mixture over the peanut butter, shaping it into a ball. Ensure the peanut butter is fully enclosed within the mixture.
Repeat the process to make additional stuffed energy balls until all the mixture is used.
Place the formed balls on a plate or tray lined with parchment paper.
Refrigerate the energy balls for at least 30 minutes to set.
Once chilled, your Peanut Butter Stuffed Energy Balls are ready to enjoy as a delicious and energizing snack!
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Place the parsley in the bowl of a food processor and pulse until finely chopped, remove and set aside.
In the same food processor bowl used to chop the parsley, place the quartered onion and pulse until finely chopped.
Drain the onions slightly to release excess moisture. Return the parsley back to the bowl of the food processor with the onions.
Add the ground beef to the food processor in addition to all the spices.
Run the food processor on pulse once or twice until ingredients are well combined and form a pasty meat mixture.
Transfer the mixture from the food processor to a large bowl and form the Kafta into an oblong shape about 1 inch in thickness.
You can form them on a wooden skewer or without. Grease the grates of a gas grill or indoor grill pan, and preheat it to medium-high heat.
When heated through, transfer the kafta to the grill. Grill for 4-5 minutes per side, depending on your preferred level of doneness.
Serve hot off the grill with rice and dipping yogurt.
Combine all ingredients into a blender and blend until consistency is smooth.
Top smoothie with bits of walnuts and a sprinkle of cinnamon.
Layer apple, pineapple, cashew butter, coconut, flax meal, vanilla extract, and cinnamon into a blender; add almond milk. Blend mixture until smooth.
Preheat the oven to 375°F­­. Grease 12 muffin cups or line with paper liners.
Mix carrots, banana, zucchini, yogurt, and eggs together in a large bowl until well combined.
Whisk flour and baking soda together in a separate bowl. Mix oats, pecans, cherries, cinnamon, salt, and ginger into flour mixture until all ingredients are coated in flour.
Stir banana mixture into flour mixture until just combined. Spoon batter into the prepared muffin cups, filling each 2/3 full.
Bake in the preheated oven until tops spring back when lightly pressed, about 18 to 22 minutes. Cool in the tin for 10 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile fry the onions in olive oil until browned. Add the cinnamon. Continue stirring and add the paprika, curry powder, salt, and garlic. Continue stirring for 2 minutes.
Add chicken pieces and tomato. Add enough water to just cover the chicken. Simmer until chicken is done, about 20 minutes.
Just before serving, add the lemon juice and the cayenne pepper. Simmer for 5 more minutes.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Warm the oil and 3 tbsp honey in a large saucepan, then pour in the oats, cinnamon, and nuts.
Add the egg white, then tip out onto a large baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes, until the oats are toasted brown, then remove.
Put the plums on a metal roasting tray and top with sugar. Roast for 10 minutes.
Remove the fruit from the tray, leaving behind the caramelized juices.

Pour in the apple juice, and heat on low-medium heat, stirring the juices to loosen the caramel. Once it has dissolved into the juice, simmer for 3 minutes, until the syrup has thickened, then pour into a bowl to cool.
To serve, divide yogurt among bowls, top with granola and roasted fruit.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Place the halved butternut squash on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or aluminum foil, cut side up.
In a bowl, mix together the cooked rice, chopped apple, chopped pecans, dried cranberries, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Fill each butternut squash cavity with the rice and apple mixture.
Optionally, sprinkle a bit of cinnamon over the stuffed squash for added flavor.
Cover the baking sheet with foil and bake the stuffed squash in the preheated oven for about 40-50 minutes or until the squash is tender when pierced with a fork.
Once cooked, remove the stuffed butternut squash from the oven.
Serve your delicious apple & pecan stuffed butternut squash as a hearty and flavorful single-serving meal!
Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, celery, and carrot to the pot. Cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic, ground turmeric, ground cumin, ground cinnamon, salt, and pepper. Cook for another 1-2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes to the pot. Cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring to a simmer.
Add dried lentils and canned chickpeas to the pot. Stir well to combine.
Cover the pot and let the harira simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Remove from heat and let it rest for a few minutes.
Ladle the harira into a bowl.
Pour the vegetable broth into a soup pot, and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Stir in the curry powder, cumin, cinnamon, and ginger, add the carrots.
Reduce heat to a simmer, and cook until the carrots are tender, about 20 minutes, stirring often.
Strain out the carrots from the broth, and place carrots into a blender, filling the pitcher no more than halfway full.
Pour about 1/4 cup vegetable broth into the blender to allow the carrots to puree. Add yeast.
Hold down the lid of the blender with a folded kitchen towel, and carefully start the blender and leave it to puree.
Puree in batches until smooth, if needed, return the pureed carrots to the vegetable broth.
Alternately, you can use a stick blender and puree the carrots right in the cooking pot.
Stir in the coconut milk and the water. Bring back to a simmer, sprinkle with cilantro to serve.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, mix together ground lamb, breadcrumbs, chopped fresh parsley, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Form the mixture into small meatballs, about 1 inch in diameter.
Place the meatballs on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until the meatballs are cooked through and browned.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced eggplant to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and red pepper flakes, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add diced tomatoes and low-sodium chicken broth to the skillet. Bring to a simmer.
Add the baked lamb meatballs to the skillet with the spicy eggplant tomato sauce.
Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove from heat and garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
Serve the little lamb meatballs in spicy eggplant tomato sauce hot.
nutmeg: De-seed the bell peppers and chop into 1” square pieces. Cut the firm tofu into 1” square pieces.⁠ Heat a barbecue or grill pan until hot.⁠
Meanwhile, mix the seasoning spices and oil in a small bowl. Thread the vegetables and tofu, alternating, onto the skewers and brush with the marinade.⁠
Cook the skewers on each side until the vegetables are cooked. Turn and repeat on all sides. Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook elbow macaroni in the boiling water, about 8 minutes. Drain. 3 minutes before cooking add squash
Place cooked squash in the bowl of a food processor; process until pureed. Transfer to a large bowl.
Stir in cooked pasta, milk, cottage cheese, Cheddar cheese, Parmesan cheese, olive oil, bouillon, nutmeg, cayenne pepper, sea salt, and black pepper until completely blended and creamy.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Lightly grease a 6-inch pie dish or tart pan with cooking spray.
Lay one sheet of phyllo dough in the prepared dish, allowing the edges to hang over the sides. Lightly brush the sheet with cooking spray or melted butter.
Repeat with the remaining sheets, layering them and brushing each with cooking spray or melted butter.
In a bowl, whisk together the eggs and milk until well combined.
Stir in the chopped spinach and shredded cheddar cheese. Season with salt, black pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg, if desired.
Pour the egg mixture into the phyllo dough-lined dish.
Fold the overhanging edges of the phyllo dough over the filling to create a rustic crust.
Bake the quiche in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the center is set and the top is golden brown.
Remove the quiche from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
Peel the potato and cut it into quarters.
Boil the potato quarters in a pot of salted water until tender, about 15-20 minutes.
Drain the cooked potato quarters and let them cool slightly.
Mash the cooked potato quarters until smooth.
In a mixing bowl, combine the mashed potato, all-purpose flour, salt, and ground nutmeg. Mix until well combined and a dough forms.
Divide the dough into small portions and shape each portion into a dumpling.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil.
Carefully drop the potato dumplings into the boiling water.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and cook the dumplings for about 10-12 minutes, or until they float to the surface and are cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the dumplings from the water using a slotted spoon and transfer them to a serving plate.
Melt the ghee in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the cooked potato dumplings to the skillet and toss to coat them evenly with the ghee.
In a mixing bowl, combine ground pork, breadcrumbs, milk, diced onion, egg, chopped parsley, ground nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Shape the mixture into small meatballs.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the meatballs to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
Serve the Frikadeller hot with your favorite side dishes, such as potatoes and lingonberry sauce.
Heat oil in a large stockpot over medium-low heat.
Add garlic; cook 10 seconds or until fragrant.
Add broth, chicken, Parmesan cube, salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
Bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium, and simmer 10 minutes.
Remove chicken; set aside. Remove Parmesan cube; discard.
Return broth mixture to a boil.
Add pasta; simmer 9 minutes or until pasta is tender.
Cut chicken-breast halves into thin strips; return to soup.
Stir in spinach, and simmer 1 minute or until wilted.
Divide soup evenly among 4 bowls; sprinkle with ¼ cup shaved Parmesan cheese.
Add olive oil to a large pot over medium heat. Stir in diced onion and minced garlic. Cook until the onion starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Next, stir in Arborio rice and toss to coat the rice in oil. Allow the rice to toast for 1 minute.
Stir in miso paste, fresh thyme leaves, ground sage, pumpkin, ground nutmeg, and pepper.
Add white wine and stir continuously until the wine is mostly absorbed into the rice.
Once most of the liquid is absorbed, begin adding in the vegetable broth in ½ cup increments.
Stir continuously until most of the liquid is absorbed before adding in the next ½ cup of broth. Cook for 20 minutes.
Then stir in canned pumpkin puree, almonds, fresh spinach and fresh lemon juice. Stir until the spinach wilts down.
Preheat oven to 375°F and butter ramekins and place on a baking tray.
Cook the spinach in a pan, according to the packet instructions. Drain well and set aside to cool. Once cooled, squeeze out any water and chop it finely.
Heat the butter in the pan the spinach was cooked in. Add the spinach and stir in the seasoning and nutmeg. Remove from the heat and stir in the yogurt.
Divide the creamy spinach between the ramekins and press down in the centres to make dips for the eggs to sit in.
Carefully break eggs into ramekin and season well. Bake for 15 minutes, or until the eggs are just cooked.
In a pot over medium heat, add olive oil.
Sauté diced onion and minced garlic for about 2-3 minutes until they soften.
Add diced butternut squash and apple to the pot and cook for about 5 minutes until they start to soften.
Pour in vegetable broth and add ground cinnamon and a pinch of nutmeg.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Let the soup simmer for about 15-20 minutes until the squash and apples are tender.
Once the squash and apples are cooked, pour in milk.
Using an immersion blender or carefully transferring to a blender, blend the soup until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook in the boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 10 minutes. Drain.
Combine butternut squash, soy milk, and vegetable broth in a pot, bring to a boil and cook until squash is tender, about 12 minutes.
Puree with an immersion blender. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg. Stir in cooked macaroni and garnish with chopped parsley. Garnish with basil.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the canned pumpkin puree, almond flour, coconut flour, baking powder, ground cinnamon, ground nutmeg, ground ginger, and a pinch of salt. Mix well.
Add the vanilla extract and crack in the egg. Stir until all the ingredients are fully combined and you have a smooth batter.
Line a muffin tin with a single muffin liner or use a silicone muffin cup.
Pour the muffin batter into the liner, filling it about two-thirds full.
Place the muffin tin in the preheated oven and bake for 18-20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of a muffin comes out clean.
Remove the muffin from the oven and allow it to cool for a few minutes before enjoying.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a skillet and cook shallot until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add hazelnuts and cook until toasted, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat and cool for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, bring a pot of lightly salted water to a boil and cook broccoli until softened, about 3 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water. Drain well.
Combine shallot-hazelnut mixture, nutritional yeast, broccoli, parsley, and dill in the bowl of a food processor, pulse until blended.
Pour 2 tablespoons olive oil in slowly, with the processor running, until spread is smooth.
Add more olive oil if needed. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg. Serve with crackers or bread.
Oil a skillet and cook the chicken for 7 to 8 minutes. Season well. Add zucchini noodles and cook for 3 minutes more.
Melt ghee in a medium saucepan. Cook garlic until fragrant, about 2 minutes. Add heavy cream and cream cheese.
Slowly add Parmesan cheese, stirring constantly until well incorporated and sauce thickens, 5 to 7 minutes. Stir in salt, nutmeg, and white pepper.
Combine water, apple, and cranberries together in a bowl; bring to a boil.
Cook mixture until cranberries burst and apple is tender, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium and stir oats, cinnamon, nutmeg, and vanilla into apple mixture.
Cook, stirring continuously, until water is absorbed and oatmeal thickens, 2 to 3 minutes.
In a bowl or jar, combine the rolled oats, grated carrot, Greek yogurt, almond milk, maple syrup or honey, cinnamon, nutmeg, and vanilla extract.
Mix all the ingredients together until well combined.
Cover the bowl or jar with a lid or plastic wrap and place it in the refrigerator.
Let the mixture sit in the fridge overnight or for at least 4-6 hours to allow the oats to soften and the flavors to meld.
Once ready to eat, give the oats a stir. If it's too thick, you can add a splash of milk to reach your desired consistency.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion with the paprika and cayenne pepper and cook until softened, 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic clove and cook for 1 more minute. Add the tomato purée, tomato paste, water, fresh coriander, salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and cook for 5 more minutes.
Meanwhile make the fish balls in a food processor. Chop the onion in the food processor, then add the rest of the ingredients. Grind for 1 minute.
Form the fish mixture into balls and place them in the tomato sauce, making sure that they aren't touching one another.
Cover the skillet and let the fish balls simmer in the sauce for 20 minutes, turning them over after 5 minutes to cover them in the sauce. Serve warm.
Cut each slice of bread into thirds to make sticks. Cut a piece of parchment paper to fit the bottom of the air fryer basket.
Preheat air fryer to 360°F.
Stir together eggs, milk, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and nutmeg in a bowl until well combined.
Dip each piece of bread into egg mixture, making sure each piece is well submerged.
Shake each bread stick to remove excess liquid and place in a single layer in the air fryer basket.
Cook in batches, if necessary, to avoid overcrowding the fryer.
Cook for 5 minutes, turn bread pieces, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Whisk together flour, protein powder, baking powder, cinnamon, ground ginger, salt and nutmeg.
In a separate bowl, stir together milk, pumpkin puree, melted butter, and egg. Fold mixture into dry ingredients.
Heat some oil in a skillet over medium heat. Pour in ¼ cup batter for each pancake. Cook pancakes about 3 minutes per side.
Serve with walnuts and syrup.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Toss the thinly sliced butternut squash and minced garlic with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread them on a baking sheet.
Roast in the preheated oven for about 10-15 minutes until slightly tender.
Roast in the preheated oven for about 10-15 minutes until slightly tender.
In a bowl, mix ricotta cheese, grated Parmesan cheese, egg, chopped fresh basil, and nutmeg until combined.
Spread marinara sauce on the bottom of a small baking dish.
Layer half of the roasted butternut squash slices, followed by half of the cooked ground beef, and half of the cheese mixture. Repeat the layers.
Cover with foil and bake for 20 minutes. Remove foil and bake for an additional 10 minutes.
Let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Cut the cauliflower into roughly equal chunks. Steam for 8 minutes.
While it’s steaming, chop the garlic and put half the oil in a frying pan along with the garlic and fry for 3 mintues. Then turn the heat off but let the pan sit there.
When the cauliflower is ready put it into a bowl and add the garlic and oil infusion, the rest of the oil, nutritional yeast, nutmeg, mustard and salt.
Give it a quick blend with a hand blender, then serve immediately.
parsley: Preheat your grill or a grill pan to 375°F.
Season the boneless, skinless chicken breast with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Grill the chicken breast for about 6-8 minutes per side, or until it's cooked through and has nice grill marks. Make sure the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, you can grill the small pita bread pieces until they are crispy and lightly browned.
Once the chicken is done, remove it from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped romaine lettuce, diced cucumbers, diced tomatoes, diced onions, chopped fresh parsley, and chopped fresh mint.
Add the grilled chicken strips to the salad.
Drizzle the salad with olive oil and fresh lemon juice.
Toss everything together to ensure the salad is evenly coated with the dressing.
Rinse the quinoa thoroughly under cold water in a fine-mesh strainer.
In a saucepan, combine the rinsed quinoa and 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the quinoa is cooked and the water is absorbed. Remove from heat and let it cool to room temperature.
In a large salad bowl, combine the cooked and cooled quinoa, diced cucumber, diced tomato, diced red onion, and chopped fresh parsley. Toss to mix well.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dressing ingredients: extra-virgin olive oil, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad to ensure all the ingredients are coated with the dressing.
Sprinkle crumbled feta cheese on top.
Cook the lentils in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the diced eggplant on a baking sheet, drizzle with a bit of olive oil, and season with salt and black pepper
Roast in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until the eggplant is tender and slightly caramelized. Remove from the oven and let it cool.
In a large salad bowl, combine the cooked and cooled lentils, diced tomato, and finely sliced red onion.
Add the roasted eggplant to the salad.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dressing ingredients: olive oil, balsamic vinegar, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper to taste.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad gently to ensure that all the ingredients are coated with the dressing.
Garnish with fresh parsley leaves.

In a food processor, combine the chickpeas, diced red onion, 1 garlic, fresh parsley, fresh cilantro, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, pepper, baking powder, and 2 tablespoons of flour. Pulse until the mixture forms a coarse paste. If needed, add the extra tablespoon of flour to achieve the right consistency.
Form the mixture into 8 falafel patties.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the falafel patties and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until they are golden brown and crispy.
While the falafel is cooking, prepare the salad. In a large bowl, combine the torn lettuce, thinly sliced red onion, corn kernels, diced tomatoes, and diced cucumber.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the Lemon-Tahini Dressing by combining tahini, fresh lemon juice, water, 1 minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
Once the falafel is cooked, remove them from the skillet or oven.
Divide the salad between two plates or bowls.
Top each salad with four hot homemade falafel patties.
Drizzle the Lemon-Tahini Dressing over the salad.
Serve immediately and enjoy your delicious Homemade Falafel Salad with Lemon-Tahini Dressing.
In a large mixing bowl, combine drained black beans, diced cucumber, chopped red onion, diced tomatoes, chopped fresh parsley, and cooked corn kernels.
In a small bowl, whisk together the lime juice and olive oil.
Pour the lime and olive oil dressing over the salad.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined and evenly coated with the dressing.
Let the salad chill in the refrigerator for about 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Preheat your grill to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, ground cumin, paprika, ground coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture evenly over both sides of the chicken breasts.
Grill the chicken breasts for about 6-8 minutes per side or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F. Remove from heat and let them rest for a few minutes before slicing.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the Cauliflower 'Rice' Tabbouleh.
Cut the cauliflower into florets and place them in a food processor. Pulse until the cauliflower resembles rice grains.
In a large bowl, combine the cauliflower 'rice', chopped parsley, diced tomato, and diced bell pepper.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the dressing over the cauliflower 'rice' mixture and toss to combine.
In a saucepan, bring 4 cups of salted water to a boil. Add 1 cup of orzo pasta and cook for 8-10 minutes or until al dente.
Drain and rinse with cold water to cool.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooked and cooled orzo, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, finely chopped red onion, sliced Kalamata olives, and crumbled feta cheese.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil and the juice of 1 lemon.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss the salad to combine all the ingredients.
Optionally, garnish with fresh parsley.
In a large salad bowl, place the thinly sliced kale leaves.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes and thinly sliced red onion to the kale.
Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the vegetables.
Season the salad with a pinch of salt and black pepper.

In a separate bowl, prepare the Green Goddess dressing. Combine mayonnaise, Greek yogurt, chopped parsley, chopped chives, chopped tarragon, minced garlic, lemon juice, and white wine vinegar. Mix until well combined.
Taste the dressing and adjust the seasoning with salt and black pepper. If the dressing is too thick, you can thin it with a little water until it reaches your desired consistency.
Drizzle the Green Goddess dressing over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients in the salad bowl to coat them with the dressing.
In a medium saucepan, bring 2 cups of water to a boil.
Add the pearl couscous, reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10-12 minutes or until the couscous is tender.
Remove the cooked couscous from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked and cooled couscous, diced cucumber, cherry tomatoes, diced red onion, and chopped fresh parsley.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Toss everything together to combine.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the drained and rinsed chickpeas, finely chopped red onion, diced tomatoes, sliced cucumber, and chopped fresh parsley.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon juice for dressing, adjusting the amount to your preference.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss the salad to combine all the ingredients.
Place the cubed potato in a pot of boiling water and cook for about 10-12 minutes or until the potato cubes are tender but still firm. Drain and let them cool.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled potato cubes, halved cherry tomatoes, sliced black olives, chopped roasted red peppers, and fresh Italian herbs.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss everything together to combine.
In a bowl, place the fine bulgur wheat.
Pour enough boiling water over the bulgur to cover it and let it sit for about 15-20 minutes, or until it's softened. Drain any excess water.
In the same bowl, combine the softened bulgur, chopped fresh parsley, diced tomatoes, diced cucumbers, diced onions, canned chickpeas, and crumbled feta cheese.
In a small bowl, whisk together the fresh lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper to create the dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the tabbouleh.
Toss everything together to ensure the tabbouleh is evenly coated with the dressing.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook until al dente according to the package instructions.

Heat a dry skillet over medium heat. Add bacon slices and cook until the fat renders out and the bacon is crispy. Transfer the bacon on to a paper towel to drain the fat and cool, then chop into bits.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Saute garlic for 30 seconds or so, add tuna and bacon bits and toss to coat everything evenly.
Drain the pasta, reserving 3 tablespoons of the pasta water, and transfer it to the skillet. Stir everything well, add the pasta water and some fresh cracked pepper.
Divide the pasta between two plates, add the grated Parmesan cheese and a drizzle of olive oil. Enjoy.
In a bowl, combine lean ground beef or turkey with garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned ground meat and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, until browned and cooked through.
Warm the whole wheat pita bread in the microwave or oven.
Place the cooked ground meat on the warmed pita bread.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, diced cucumber, and diced red onion.
Drizzle plain Greek yogurt over the toppings.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Toss the tomatoes, cucumbers, and lemon juice in a bowl with 2 tsp of the salt. Set aside.
Put the couscous into a large bowl. Bring the water, 2 tbsp of olive oil, flax seeds, the remaining salt, cayenne, and herb stems to a boil over high heat.
Pour the liquid over the couscous, stir to separate any clumps. Cover with a lid, set aside for 5 minutes. Remove the herb stems and fluff with a fork.
Add the tomato mixture, scallions, mint, parsley, and pine nuts. Drizzle the remaining oil over the salad, toss and serve.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add the spaghetti, and cook until al dente. Toss with 1/4 cup olive oil, cover and keep warm.
Place another 1/8 cup olive oil in a skillet, and heat over medium heat. Stir in the garlic and flaxseed oil, and cook just until golden, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add the sardines, and cook 1 minute more. Stir in the bread crumbs, stir in the remaining olive oil.
Stir in the parsley and pepper, and remove from the heat.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the sole fillet with salt and pepper on both sides.
In a bowl, combine lump crabmeat, Panko breadcrumbs, chopped fresh parsley, minced garlic, lemon juice, and paprika. Mix well to create the stuffing.
Lay the sole fillet flat on a baking sheet or oven-safe dish. Spoon the crabmeat stuffing onto the center of the sole fillet.
Carefully fold the edges of the sole fillet over the stuffing, creating a neat packet. You can secure it with toothpicks if needed.
Drizzle olive oil over the top of the stuffed sole.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 12-15 minutes or until the sole is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove the stuffed sole from the oven and let it rest for a minute or two.
In a small bowl, whisk the eggs with a fork and season with salt and pepper, to taste. Set aside.
Heat a small skillet over medium heat and add the oil. Pour in the egg mixture and cook until almost set, about 5 minutes.
Place the chopped tomato and feta on one half of the omelet and fold the other half over top. Remove from heat and top with parsley.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Place hashbrown in each case of a muffin tin, and smush them down to form a base. Bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
Place 1 tbsp of beans on top of each cooked hash brown, followed by a few pieces of sausages and bacon.
Beat the eggs and milk together and pour some into each cup, being careful not to overfill. Top with grated cheese, parsley, and pepper.
Bake for 5 minutes, and serve.
Preheat your air fryer to 400°F for about 3-5 minutes.
Season the steak bites with salt and black pepper.
Place the seasoned steak bites in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
Cook the steak bites in the air fryer for 6-8 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through cooking, or until the steak reaches your desired level of doneness.
While the steak is cooking, melt the ghee in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Once the steak is cooked, transfer it to a serving dish and pour the garlic ghee over the steak bites.
Toss the steak bites in the garlic ghee until evenly coated.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Serve immediately and enjoy!
chili: To make the paste, heat a little of oil in a frying pan, add diced onion and 1 tsp fresh or dried chilli, and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
In a blender combine garlic, remaining oil, then add ground coriander,  cumin, garam masala, tomato sauce, salt, and the fried onion. Blend to a smooth paste.
Cook the paste in a medium saucepan for 2 minutes over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally so it doesn’t stick.
Tip in chickpeas, tomatoes, and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add coconut cream with a little water, cook for 5 minutes more. Serve garnished with parsley.
In a small pot, heat olive oil until starting to smoke, then remove from heat. Meanwhile, in a medium jar, whisk ginger, garlic, chilis, sesame seeds and some salt.
Slowly pour hot oil over the spices, letting it sizzle through evenly, streaming gradually and carefully to control the overflow. Whisk and let cool.
Use the flat size of your knife to smash each piece of cucumber.
In a large bowl, toss smashed cucumbers with chili oil, sesame oil and season with some salt. Serve immediately.
In a large skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. Add peppers, onion and garlic. Cook and stir 10 minutes or until tender.
Add beans, chili, cumin and oregano. Heat through. Sprinkle with cilantro and serve.
Combine the chopped seafood, pinch of salt and a pinch of ground black pepper in a small bowl. Mix it well and set aside.
Combine flour, starch, salt, and stock in a large enough to accommodate the green onions. Mix with a whisk until smooth.
Heat 2 tbsp of oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and swirl to coat evenly.
Add the green onions to the batter to coat them. Using tongs or your hand, place them side by side in the skillet.
Add the seafood to the leftover batter in the bowl. Using your hands or tongs, spread the battered seafood on top of the battered scallions, scraping out any excess batter remaining in the bowl.
Add the sliced chili and pour the beaten eggs over top of the pancake in the skillet.
Reduce the heat to medium and cook for about 6 minutes, until the bottom is light brown and crispy.
Lift one edge of the pancake, and cook on another side for 3 minutes.
Transfer to a large plate, with the egg and seafood side up.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
