Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and minced chili to the skillet. Sauté for a few seconds until fragrant.
Add ground chicken to the skillet and cook until it's no longer pink, breaking it into smaller pieces as it cooks.
Stir in low-sodium soy sauce, fish sauce, and sugar. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and well-coated with the sauce.
Add fresh Thai basil leaves to the skillet and cook for about 1-2 minutes until they wilt.
Season with a pinch of salt to taste.
Serve the Pad Krapow Gai over cooked jasmine rice.
Add dry pasta to the pot of boiling salted water. Cook uncovered according to package directions, about 10 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a saute pan. Add onion, garlic and chili. Cook for 3 minutes.
Add tuna and stir in tomatoes, olives, salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer and reduce heat.
Serve pasta and top with sauce.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
In a skillet, add the olive oil to the skillet over medium heat. Saute the minced onion until fragrant.
Add spinach into the skillet and let it cook until it’s wilted.
Add the cumin, coriander, harissa, and salt and pepper. Stir together well and let cook for another 1-2 minutes.
Drizzle oil in the bottom of the skillet. Pour the spinach mixture into the skillet. Using a spoon, press the back of the spoon into parts of the spinach mixture.
Gently crack the eggs into these parts of the mixture. Cook in the oven until the egg whites are set and the yolk is a little runny. This should take about 20 minutes.
Top with parsley and chili. Serve and enjoy.
Take a bowl and add chicken breast, egg, cornflour,  flour,  red chili powder, salt and mix all ingredients together.
Take a non stick pan and shallow fry the marinated chicken. Fry chicken for 5 minutes.
After frying chicken, take it out from the pan and keep it aside for 5 minutes.
Take a wok and add curry leaves, green chilies, bell pepper, spring onion, garlic, ginger and stir fry all the vegetables for 3 minutes.
Make CornStarch by adding 1 tbsp cornflour in 1 cup of water and add it in the wok.
Add in soya sauce, red chili sauce, vinegar, and keep it mixing for 2 minutes till sauce become thicker.
Add chicken, and garnish with spring onion and sesame seeds.
Add the shallots, chili, ginger, garlic and shrimp paste to a blender and blend until smooth.
Heat the coconut oil in a wok until hot. Add the blended paste, lemon juice and sugar, and cook them until the oil separates.
When the oil separates, season with salt to taste, add the shrimp. Cook for 1-2 minutes and turn off the heat.
Transfer to a plate and serve.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium-high heat. Saute onion and bell pepper until tender and golden, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, curry paste, chili garlic sauce, garam masala, curry powder, and salt. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring often, until fragrant.
Next add lentils, broth, and tomatoes. Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer gently, stirring occasionally. Cook until lentils are soft but not mushy.
Add juice from 1/2 a lime and stir. For an ultra creamy soup, feel free to add all the coconut milk to the soup.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and chili, and add some extra chili garlic sauce if you like things spicy. Enjoy!
In a large bowl toss together, lime juice, chili, coriander stalks, yogurt, onion, carrot and cabbage.
Whisk all the eggs together in a bowl, with a splash of water to loosen and a pinch of sea salt and black pepper.
Put a large non-stick frying pan on a medium-low heat. Once fairly hot, put in a tiny drizzle of oil followed by a quarter of the egg mixture.
Slide it on to a plate, spoon over a quarter of the vegetable slaw, then gently fold up the sides and roll over. Serve with a few extra coriander leaves.
Heat a large pot over medium heat. Once hot, add oil, onion, cumin, and one-third of the salt.
Add garlic, ginger, cilantro, and green chilies to a mortar and pestle and grind into a rough paste. Then, add to the pan with the onions.
Next add ground coriander, chili powder, and turmeric and stir to coat. Add pureed tomatoes and chickpeas and remaining salt.
Increase heat to medium high until it reaches a rolling simmer, then reduce heat to low or medium-low and maintain a simmer, 15 minutes.
Remove from heat and add lemon juice and garam masala. Stir to mix, then let cool slightly before serving. Garnish with lemon juice and cilantro.
Add the oil to a pan and when it's semi hot, add the garlic, onion, seeds and chili. Add the minced pork after 3 minutes.
Cook for 5 minutes, then add the soy sauce and cook for 5 more minutes. Serve topped with chives.
Heat up some vegetable oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add ground beef and saute for 3 minutes. before adding chili and onions.
Add tomato paste to saucepan and continue to saute for approx. 3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Add canned tomatoes, sweet corn, and kidney beans. Stir well to combine.
Leave to simmer for approx. 15 minutes. over low-medium heat. Stir occasionally. Season to taste with salt, and pepper.
Sieve the flour and the salt. Add the water and whisk it well. Add the eggs, Kimchi, Kimchi liquid and chilies. Mix them well.
Pre heat a skillet until well heated. Put a generous amount of cooking oil into the pan.
Scoop out the pancake mixture with a ladle, pour it out on the pan. Make sure it is evenly spread.
Turn the pancake over when 70% of the pancake is cooked. Add green onions.
When both sides are cooked, slice it into easy to bite-size. Serve.
Heat olive oil in a saucepan over medium-high heat, add onion and garlic and stir occasionally until tender, 5 minutes.
Add ginger, cook until fragrant, 2 minutes, add spices (except garam masala) and stir until fragrant.
Add stock, tomato, cover and simmer until starting to become tender, 5 minutes.
Add cauliflower, cover and simmer until tender, 5 minutes.
Stir in garam marsala, and serve.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
Place the hot dog in the bun. Spread the canned chili over the hot dog.
Sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese on top.
Place the assembled chili dog in the oven and bake for about 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the hot dog is heated through.
Season meat with salt, pepper, paprika powder, and cumin. Heat 1 tsp oil in a frying pan. Fry the meat over high heat for 5 minutes until crumbly. Set aside.
Mix all vegetables with oil, vinegar and season with salt and pepper. Add minced meat and mix. Serve.
Cook the macaroni pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the ground beef until browned.
Stir in the cooked macaroni pasta and chili until well combined and heated through.
Sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese over the mixture and stir until melted.
Transfer the chili mac and cheese to a serving bowl.
Top with diced tomatoes and onions.
Serve hot.
Place all the veggies (onion, red bell pepper, mushrooms, garlic, ginger and chili), broth, coconut milk and sugar in a large pot.
Bring it to a boil and then cook over medium heat for about 5 minutes. Add the tofu and cook for 5 minutes more.
Remove from the heat, add the soy sauce, lime juice and parsley. Stir and serve.
thyme: Place the cubed potato in a pot of boiling water and cook for about 10-12 minutes or until the potato cubes are tender but still firm. Drain and let them cool.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled potato cubes, halved cherry tomatoes, sliced black olives, chopped roasted red peppers, and fresh Italian herbs.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss everything together to combine.
Position rack in the lower third of oven; preheat to 450°F. Place a large rimmed baking sheet in the oven to preheat.
Combine mustard, thyme, oil, salt and pepper in a small bowl; spread the mixture evenly on chicken.
Toss sweet potatoes and onions in a bowl with the remaining oil and salt and pepper.
Carefully remove the baking sheet from the oven and spread the vegetables on it. Place the chicken on top of the vegetables.

Return the pan to the oven and roast, stirring the vegetables once halfway through, until the vegetables are tender and beginning to brown and an instant-read thermometer inserted into a chicken thigh registers 165°F, 30 to 35 minutes.
In a pot, bring the broth and water to a boil over medium-high heat.
Add the uncooked egg noodles, shredded rotisserie chicken, chopped carrots, celery, onion, dried thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for about 10-12 minutes, or until the noodles and vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the heavy cream and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
De-seed the bell peppers and chop into 1” square pieces. Cut the firm tofu into 1” square pieces.⁠ Heat a barbecue or grill pan until hot.⁠
Meanwhile, mix the seasoning spices and oil in a small bowl. Thread the vegetables and tofu, alternating, onto the skewers and brush with the marinade.⁠
Cook the skewers on each side until the vegetables are cooked. Turn and repeat on all sides. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Rub the spice mixture evenly over the chicken thighs, ensuring they are well coated.
Place the seasoned chicken thighs on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and juices run clear.
Once cooked, remove the chicken thighs from the oven and let them rest for a few minutes.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add the celery, onion, and garlic and saute for about 5 minutes. Add in the vegetable broth and water.
Add in the raw cashews, bay leaf, and thyme. Bring to a simmer and cook for about 15 minutes. Remove the bay leaf, then blend.
Make sure to blend very well so that the cashews are completely blended. Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately, garnished with dill.
In a pot or skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sliced mushrooms. Cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the lamb stew meat to the pot and brown on all sides, about 5-6 minutes.
Stir in the diced potatoes, sliced carrots, beef or vegetable broth, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low.
Cover and cook for 30-35 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
Adjust seasoning with more salt and pepper if needed.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season both sides of the pork chop with salt, pepper, and dried thyme.
Add the pork chop to the skillet and sear for about 2-3 minutes on each side until it's browned.
Remove the pork chop from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced potatoes and onions. Cook for about 5 minutes, or until they start to brown.
Return the seared pork chop to the skillet, placing it on top of the potatoes and onions.
Pour the low-sodium chicken broth into the skillet.
Cover the skillet and simmer for about 10-15 minutes.
Serve the skillet pork chop with potatoes and onions.
Heat ghee in a pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and garlic; cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add sliced mushrooms and thyme; cook for another 5-6 minutes until tender.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to purée the soup until smooth.
Stir in heavy cream, salt, and black pepper. Heat through but do not boil.
Garnish with fresh parsley.
Boil broth in a pot and keep warm.
Heat the oil in another pot over medium heat. Cook the onion, thyme and garlic for 2 minutes. Add the rice and saute, stirring, for 2 minutes.
Ladle in 1 cup of hot broth and continue to cook the rice until almost all the liquid has evaporated, stirring often. Continue adding broth and wait for it to evaporate.
When the rice is cooked, mix in the cream cheese and season with salt and pepper. Mix well so everything is combined. Serve immediately.
Pierce spaghetti squash halves all over using a fork. Place in a microwave-safe dish and cook in a microwave oven until softened, about 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat and cook onion until soft and translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add ground beef, tomato sauce, peanut butter, oregano, paprika, thyme, tomato paste, turmeric, salt, and pepper and mix well. Simmer until sauce for 5 to 10 minutes.
Shred squash flesh using a fork and distribute among 4 plates. Serve with the sauce.
Preheat your oven to 400°F. Grease a glass or ceramic baking dish.
Wash and dry the mushrooms. Use a paring knife to gently remove the stems.
In a medium bowl, use a rubber spatula to thoroughly mix the cream cheese with the salt and the spices. Mix in the gruyere.
Spoon the mixture into the mushroom caps.
Place the mushrooms in the prepared baking dish. Bake until the cheese has melted and the mushrooms are tender, 10 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging. Make it according to how you make sushi rice.
Season the steak generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat ghee in a skillet over medium-high heat until hot.
Add the steak to the skillet and sear for 3-4 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or longer according to your preference.
Add butter, minced garlic, and fresh thyme or rosemary to the skillet. Baste the steak with the melted butter and aromatics for another minute.
Remove the steak from the skillet and let it rest on a cutting board for a few minutes.
Slice the steak against the grain into thin slices.
Take a small amount of sushi rice and shape it into an oval-shaped mound using your hands.
Place a slice of aburi steak on top of each rice mound.
Serve the aburi steak with soy sauce, wasabi, pickled ginger, and sesame seeds for garnish.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a bowl, combine ground turkey, breadcrumbs, diced onions, grated zucchini, diced red bell peppers, minced garlic, buttermilk, egg, dried thyme, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Divide the mixture and shape it into muffin-sized portions.
Place the meatloaf muffins in a muffin tin or on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Allow the buttermilk turkey meatloaf muffins to rest for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat your oven to 375°F
In a skillet over medium heat, add a small amount of olive oil.
Sauté diced onions until they turn translucent.
Add the mixed frozen vegetables and cook for 2-3 minutes until they begin to thaw.
Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir to coat evenly. Cook for 1-2 minutes to cook out the raw taste of the flour.
Slowly pour in the chicken broth while stirring continuously to avoid lumps.
Add milk (or non-dairy alternative) and dried thyme to the skillet, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens and begins to simmer.
Stir in the diced cooked chicken breast and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove the skillet from heat.

If your skillet is oven-safe, place it in the preheated oven for about 10-15 minutes or until the mixture is bubbly and slightly golden on top. If your skillet isn't oven-safe, transfer the mixture to a small baking dish before baking.
Once done, let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat the grill to medium.
Season the shrimp with salt, pepper, garlic, oregano, and paprika in a medium bowl. Toss to combine.
In a small bowl, combine the melted butter, garlic, lemon juice. Brush lemon butter sauce on both sides of shrimp, reserving some for later.
Grill for 3 minutes on each side and serve.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Place the diced sweet potato and sliced onion on a baking sheet.
Rub the chicken breast or thigh with olive oil and season with paprika, salt, and pepper. Add optional herbs if desired.
Place the seasoned chicken on the baking sheet with the sweet potatoes and onions.

Roast in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes or until the chicken is thoroughly cooked (reaches an internal temperature of 165°F or 74°C) and the sweet potatoes are tender, and the onions are caramelized.
Once done, remove from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Meanwhile bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook lasagna noodles in boiling water until al dente. Drain, and cut noodles in half crosswise.
Melt ghee in a skillet over medium heat. Saute garlic just until fragrant. Stir in cream. Gradually mix in milk, and season with thyme. Simmer sauce gently until thick. Remove from heat.
Grease a baking dish. Layer noodles, sauce, asparagus, ham, and mozzarella cheese in three layers, each starting with noodles, and ending with shredded cheese on the top.
Cover the dish, and cook in the oven for 10 minutes, or until cheese is melted and bubbly. Let stand for 5 to 10 minutes before serving.
In a mixing bowl, combine ground venison, ground sage, dried thyme, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Divide the mixture into small portions and shape them into patties.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and add a little oil.
Cook the sausage patties in the skillet until browned and cooked through, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Once cooked, remove the sausage patties from the skillet and drain on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Serve hot with your favorite breakfast sides.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the seasoned chicken breast to the skillet and cook until golden brown on both sides and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes per side. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add sliced mushrooms and cook until they are tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and dried thyme, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Pour in the low-sodium broth and bring to a simmer.
Return the cooked chicken breast to the skillet and cook for another 2-3 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Remove from heat and let it rest for a few minutes.
Garnish with chopped dill.
Serve the chicken with mushrooms hot.
oregano: In a large bowl, combine the chopped lettuce, diced tomatoes, chopped red onion, crumbled feta cheese, chickpeas.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the salad.
Toss everything together to coat the salad ingredients with the dressing.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a large salad bowl, combine the cooked and cooled rice, fresh arugula, and diced tomato.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dressing ingredients: olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, minced garlic, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper to taste.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad gently to ensure that all the ingredients are coated with the dressing.
Place the cubed potato in a pot of boiling water and cook for about 10-12 minutes or until the potato cubes are tender but still firm. Drain and let them cool.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled potato cubes, halved cherry tomatoes, sliced black olives, chopped roasted red peppers, and fresh Italian herbs.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss everything together to combine.
Heat oven to 350°F.
Tip the orzo into a medium casserole dish, then stir in the oil and oregano.
Tip in the chopped tomatoes, then refill the can halfway with water and pour that in too.
Give everything a good mix, season, then cover and bake for 20 minutes until the pasta is almost cooked.
Take it out of the oven and give the orzo a stir. Remove the foil and return to the oven for a further 5 minutes.
Take the orzo out of the oven and tear the mozzarella over the top, then grill until melted and bubbling.
In a bowl, combine lean ground beef or turkey with garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned ground meat and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, until browned and cooked through.
Warm the whole wheat pita bread in the microwave or oven.
Place the cooked ground meat on the warmed pita bread.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, diced cucumber, and diced red onion.
Drizzle plain Greek yogurt over the toppings.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken breast and cook until golden brown and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes per side. Remove from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add cherry tomatoes, bell peppers, carrots, minced garlic, dried oregano, and dried basil. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender-crisp, about 5-6 minutes.
Pour broth into the skillet and stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Add the cooked farfalle pasta to the skillet and toss to coat in the sauce.
Slice the cooked chicken breast and add it back to the skillet, stirring to combine with the vegetables and pasta.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until everything is heated through.
In a pot, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, minced garlic, and diced bell pepper to the pot, sautéing until softened (about 2-3 minutes).
Add chicken to the pot, cooking until they are browned on all sides (about 3-4 minutes).
Stir in rinsed rice, tomato sauce, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and dried oregano, ensuring the rice and chicken are well coated.
Pour in broth, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the chicken is tender.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
In a small pot or saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced onion, sliced eggplant, and sliced zucchini to the pot.
Sauté the sliced vegetables for about 5 minutes or until they start to soften.
Add the diced fresh tomato and canned diced tomatoes to the pot. Stir to combine.
Pour in the low-sodium vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Sprinkle dried oregano, salt, and pepper over the stew.
Cover the pot and let the stew simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and the flavors have melded together.
Heat grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, combine oil, oregano, and red pepper flakes. Brush all over eggplants and season with salt and pepper.
Grill eggplants until tender and slightly charred, about 3 minutes per side. Top grilled eggplants with parsley, then squeeze lemon juice over.
In a bowl, combine the ground turkey, finely chopped onion, minced garlic, dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Form the mixture into a turkey burger patty.
Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Cook the turkey burger patty in the preheated skillet for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until it's cooked through and browned.
In a separate bowl, combine the grated cooked beetroot, grated cucumber, red wine vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Mix everything together to make the beetroot relish.
Serve the burkey in you bun with the vegetables, patty and relish.
In a bowl, combine olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Add the chicken cubes to the marinade and coat them evenly.
Let the chicken marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours in the refrigerator.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated chicken cubes onto skewers, alternating with vegetables if desired.
Grill the chicken kabobs for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until cooked through and slightly charred.
Remove the chicken kabobs from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat; stir in onion and garlic.
Cook and stir until the onion has softened and turned translucent, about 5 minutes, add chicken and cook for 5 minutes more
Add tomatoes, chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Stir in chicken broth and water, bring to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer and cook for 5 to 10 minutes.
Add black beans, hominy, cilantro, and jalapeno pepper. Simmer until heated through, at least 10 minutes.
Prepare the beef by trimming fat, and dicing it into one-inch cubes. Toss into the slow cooker, and sprinkle with pepper, salt, garlic powder, and oregano.
Stir to coat the beef cubes. Top with the sliced onion and cook on high for 4-6 hours.
20 minutes before the hearts are ready, bring a pot of water to a boil and cook the rice to the package instructions.
Serve over rice.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Lay one sheet of phyllo dough flat on a clean surface and lightly coat with cooking spray or brush with olive oil.
Place the second sheet of phyllo dough on top and press gently to adhere.
Spread pizza sauce evenly over the phyllo dough.
Sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese over the sauce.
Evenly distribute chopped turkey pepperoni, chopped bell peppers, and chopped onions over the cheese.
Sprinkle dried oregano over the toppings.
Roll up the layered phyllo dough tightly into a log.
Slice the rolled phyllo dough into 1-inch pinwheels.
Place the pinwheels on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Lightly coat the tops of the pinwheels with cooking spray or olive oil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 10-12 minutes, or until the pinwheels are golden brown and crispy.
Allow the pinwheels to cool slightly before serving.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
Wash the zucchini and slice it into thin rounds.
In a shallow bowl, combine almond flour, grated Parmesan cheese, garlic powder, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Lightly grease the air fryer basket with cooking spray or olive oil.
Dip each zucchini slice into the almond flour mixture, coating both sides evenly.
Place the coated zucchini slices in the air fryer basket in a single layer, making sure they are not overlapping.
Air fry the zucchini chips at 375°F for 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway through, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Once cooked, remove the zucchini chips from the air fryer and let them cool slightly before serving.
Pierce spaghetti squash halves all over using a fork. Place in a microwave-safe dish and cook in a microwave oven until softened, about 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat and cook onion until soft and translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add ground beef, tomato sauce, peanut butter, oregano, paprika, thyme, tomato paste, turmeric, salt, and pepper and mix well. Simmer until sauce for 5 to 10 minutes.
Shred squash flesh using a fork and distribute among 4 plates. Serve with the sauce.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
In a baking dish or oven-safe skillet, combine the cherry tomatoes, feta cheese cubes, olive oil, dried oregano, red pepper flakes (if using), salt, and pepper. Toss until well coated.
Place the baking dish in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the tomatoes are softened and the feta cheese is golden and creamy.
While the tomatoes and feta are baking, cook the orecchiette pasta according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Once the tomatoes and feta are done baking, remove them from the oven and stir until well combined.
Add the cooked orecchiette pasta to the baking dish with the tomato-feta mixture and toss until the pasta is coated.
Garnish with chopped fresh basil before serving.
Preheat a grill or stovetop grill pan.
In a bowl, combine ground turkey, chopped spinach, crumbled goat cheese, garlic powder, black pepper, and dried oregano. Mix well.
Divide the mixture and shape it into a patty.
Grill the turkey burger patty for about 5-7 minutes per side or until fully cooked.
Essemble the burger with the remianing ingredients.
In a small pot or saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced zucchini, red bell pepper, yellow bell pepper, and diced onion to the pot.
Sauté the vegetables for about 5 minutes, or until they begin to soften.
Add the diced tomatoes, dried oregano, salt, and pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
Pour in the low-sodium vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let the stew simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and the flavors have melded together.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the whole wheat ziti pasta according to the package instructions until it's al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a small oven-safe dish, add the cooked ziti pasta.
Stir in low-sodium tomato sauce, part-skim ricotta cheese, and season with dried oregano, salt, and black pepper.
Top the mixture with shredded part-skim mozzarella cheese.
Drizzle a little olive oil over the top.
Place the dish in the preheated oven and bake for about 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is bubbly and golden.
Serve your Baked Ziti hot.
lime: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking sheet and season with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until it flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is baking, in a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, and sesame seeds to create the dressing.
In two serving plates, divide the mixed greens and arrange the halved cherry tomatoes and sliced red bell pepper.
Once the salmon is cooked, let it cool slightly and then flake it into bite-sized pieces.
Place the flaked salmon on top of the prepared salads.
Drizzle the Asian Ginger Sesame Dressing over the salads.
Garnish each plate with slices of lime.
In a mixing bowl, combine 1 cup of drained and rinsed black beans, 1 cup of halved cherry tomatoes, and 1/2 cup of corn kernels.
Add 1/2 diced avocado to the bowl.
Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of chopped fresh dill over the ingredients.
Squeeze the juice of half a lime into the bowl to add a zesty flavor.
Season the salad with a pinch of salt and pepper to taste. If you like it spicy, you can also add 1/4 teaspoon of chili powder.
Toss all the ingredients together until they are well combined.
In a large mixing bowl, combine drained black beans, diced cucumber, chopped red onion, diced tomatoes, chopped fresh parsley, and cooked corn kernels.
In a small bowl, whisk together the lime juice and olive oil.
Pour the lime and olive oil dressing over the salad.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined and evenly coated with the dressing.
Let the salad chill in the refrigerator for about 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Rinse 1 cup of wild rice under cold water. In a saucepan, combine the rinsed wild rice with 2 cups of water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for about 40-45 minutes or until the rice is tender and most of the water is absorbed. Fluff the rice with a fork and let it cool.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooked and cooled wild rice, diced papaya, and lamb's lettuce.
Squeeze the juice of 1 lime over the salad ingredients.
Add the sliced cucumber to the bowl.
Toss the salad to combine all the ingredients. Add liem wedges to the side.
Mix the chicken with the lime juice, chilli powder and garlic in a zipper bag, leave to marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan on medium-high, fry the chicken for 2-3 minutes, or until cooked through. Transfer the chicken to a plate and set aside.
Add the rest of the olive oil to the frying pan. Add the sliced onion and bell peppers, season with salt and pepper, and stir fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add the chicken back to the pan to warm it up.
Meanwhile warm the tortillas in a separate non-stick pan on medium heat. Once nice and hot, remove from the heat.
Divide the vegetable-chicken mixture and add it to the warm tortillas. Enjoy immediately.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Season chicken breast with salt and pepper, place it on a baking sheet, and roast for 20-25 minutes until cooked through.
Remove chicken from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing it thinly.
Warm corn tortillas in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 1 minute on each side.
Place sliced chicken on warmed tortillas.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomato, avocado slices, chopped onion, and cilantro.
Squeeze lime juice over the tacos.
Boil the noodles in salted water for 3 mins until soft. Drain and rinse in cold water. To make the sauce, mix soy sauce, fish sauce and lime
Heat a wok with half the oil. Add the prawns and spring onions, and cook, stirring quickly, for 1 min or until the prawns turn pink.
Push to the side and add the remaining oil. Add the egg and let sit for 30 secs, then scramble until cooked. Add bean sprouts.
Add the noodles and sauce, and cook, stirring continuously, for 3 mins or until everything is hot.
Toss chopped avocado with juice from 1 small lime.
In a large bowl, combine avocado, cucumbers, tomatoes, black beans, corn, and cilantro.
Stir in olive oil, red wine vinegar, salt, and pepper and stir to coat.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Place corn tortillas on a baking sheet and bake for 5-7 minutes until crispy.
Season shrimp with salt and pepper.
Heat cooking spray or oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook shrimp for 2-3 minutes per side until pink and cooked through.
Place cooked shrimp on crispy tortillas.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomato, avocado slices, chopped onion, and cilantro.
Squeeze lime juice over the tostadas. Top with jalapeno.
Serve hot.
Place clam juice and oregano in a saucepa, bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Add shrimp and stir just until shrimp turn pink and are not quite cooked through, about 2 minutes.
Transfer shrimp to a bowl with a slotted spoon. Reserve liquid. Let shrimp cool to room temperature.
Place chopped tomatoes, cucumbers, celery, red onion, and jalapenos is a mixing bowl.
Add ketchup, cooking liquid from the shrimp, lime juice, cilantro, and hot sauce. Gently mix until ingredients are well blended.
Chop shrimp into bite-size pieces, leaving a few whole for garnishes. Stir chopped pieces into the vegetable mixture.
Wrap mixing bowl with plastic wrap. Place reserved shrimp in a bowl, wrap with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate shrimp cocktail and reserved shrimp until thoroughly chilled, 2 to 3 hours.
If mixture seems a bit thick, add a little tomato juice or pickle juice. Stir in avocado cubes.
Serve, if possible, in clear glass bowls. Top with reserved whole shrimp and some cilantro leaves.
Add the cucumber to a bowl with the yogurt. Add in the remaining ingredients. Stir well to combine.
In a medium bowl, combine the tuna, mustard, lemon juice, water, parsley, salt and pepper. Mix in the egg. Form the mixture into patties.
Heat the butter in a cast iron on medium high. Gently place the patties in the pan, and cook until nicely browned, 3 minutes per side.
Place avocado and garlic in a medium-sized bowl. Cut the lime in half and squeeze both halves into the bowl with the avocado. Add salt and pepper.
Using a fork gently mash the ingredients together. Serve in a small bowl next to the tuna patties.
In a bowl, combine the diced tomato, chopped onion, minced jalapeño pepper, and chopped cilantro.
Squeeze the lime juice over the mixture and sprinkle with salt.
Gently toss everything together until well combined.
Let the pico de gallo sit for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve as a topping for tacos, salads, grilled meats, or enjoy with tortilla chips.
In a medium sized pot bring water to boil, add rice and cook according to box instructions. Remove from heat and set aside.
Add pieces of chicken breasts, olive oil, paprika, cumin, ground chili pepper, salt and black pepper. Mix well until chicken is coated with spices and oil.

Heat a non-stick pan on medium heat.
Add chicken in pan and cook for 10 minutes on each side until thoroughly cooked and set aside.
In a large bowl add salad mix, corn, beans, rice, tomato and cooked chicken.
For salsa finely chop cilantro and onion. Add in a small bowl and garnish with vinegar, lime juice and salt. Mix well until everything is combined.
Add salsa, yogurt and cheese on top.
Pour cleaned lentils into a medium sauce pan. Add 3 cups of water. Bring lentils and water to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 10 minutes.
While the lentils are cooking, bring a separate small pot of water to a boil. Place the tomatoes in the boiling water and blanch for one minute.
Remove the tomatoes to a bowl to cool. Once cool, peel the tomatoes and cut out and discard the tough stem end. Mash them, and set aside.
In a medium saucepan, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions. Cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add garlic paste and cook for 1 minute more, stirring continuously, making sure that the garlic does not burn. Add turmeric and stir.
To the onions and spices, proteins, add the cooked lentils along with the lentil cooking water. Add salt. Cook for 10 minutes.
Add lime juice and tomatoes. Cook for 3 more minutes. Adjust salt if necessary.
Stir in chopped cilantro and remove from heat. Garnish with more chopped cilantro.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
In a bowl, toss the finely chopped cauliflower florets with olive oil, chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, lime juice, salt, and pepper until well coated.
Spread the seasoned cauliflower on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Roast in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes until the cauliflower is tender and slightly browned.
Remove the cauliflower from the oven and set it aside.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Place one tortilla in the skillet and sprinkle half of the shredded cheese on top of the tortilla.
Add the roasted chile-lime cauliflower on top of the cheese.
Sprinkle the remaining cheese over the cauliflower and cover it with the second tortilla.
Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side until the tortilla turns golden brown and the cheese melts.
Once cooked, remove the quesadilla from the skillet and let it cool for a minute before slicing.
Place the rinsed lentils, vegetable stock, tomatoes, and salt in a stockpot.
Heat the oil in a small skillet over high heat and add the mustard and cumin seeds. Fry until the mustard seeds begin to pop, about 10 seconds.
Reduce the heat to medium-high and add the garlic. Fry for another 20 seconds. Add the onion and fry until soft, about 5 minutes.
Add the turmeric, coriander, cinnamon, and cloves and stir to combine. Scrape the contents of the skillet into the stockpot. Stir to combine and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for about 20 minutes, or until the lentils are done and the soup is thick, stirring occasionally.
Before serving, add the lime juice, according to taste, and garnish with cilantro
Rub the poblano pepper with olive oil. Roast over the gas flame of your stove, turning often, until the skin is black, 7 to 10 minutes.
Place pepper into a paper bag and seal; set aside to cool, about 10 minutes.
Combine cucumber and onion in a bowl. Set aside.
Take cooled poblano from the bag and scrape off the burned skin. Remove stem and seeds, roughly chop the pepper.
Place tomatoes, poblano, jalapeno, garlic, lime juice, sugar, salt, pepper, and cumin into the bowl of a food processor and pulse until thick and not runny.
Add cucumber and onion, pulse 3 to 4 times to incorporate. Refrigerate until chilled, about 30 minutes. Serve.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the white fish fillet on a piece of aluminum foil large enough to wrap the fish.
Season the fish with jerk seasoning, olive oil, lime juice, salt, and pepper. Rub the seasoning evenly over the fish.
Fold the aluminum foil over the fish to create a packet and seal the edges.
Place the foil packet on a baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Carefully open the foil packet to release the steam.
Serve hot. Enjoy your Jamaican jerk fish!
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile mix together the Cajun seasoning, lime juice and olive oil. Add the shrimp, coat with the marinade. Marinate in the refrigerator for 20 minutes.
Preheat a skillet on medium heat, and lightly oil. Remove the shrimp from the marinade, and shake off excess. Discard the remaining marinade.
Cook the shrimp until they are bright pink on the outside and the meat is no longer transparent in the center, about 2 minutes per side.
cilantro: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Toss the diced sweet potatoes in a little olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread them on a baking sheet and roast for about 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly caramelized.
While the sweet potatoes are roasting, prepare the tamarind dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together tamarind concentrate, maple syrup, olive oil, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Once the sweet potatoes are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool slightly.
In a mixing bowl, combine the roasted sweet potatoes, finely chopped red onion, fresh cilantro,, toasted pecans, cucumber, arugula and carrots.
Drizzle the tamarind dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasonings, if needed.

In a food processor, combine the chickpeas, diced red onion, 1 garlic, fresh parsley, fresh cilantro, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, pepper, baking powder, and 2 tablespoons of flour. Pulse until the mixture forms a coarse paste. If needed, add the extra tablespoon of flour to achieve the right consistency.
Form the mixture into 8 falafel patties.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the falafel patties and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until they are golden brown and crispy.
While the falafel is cooking, prepare the salad. In a large bowl, combine the torn lettuce, thinly sliced red onion, corn kernels, diced tomatoes, and diced cucumber.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the Lemon-Tahini Dressing by combining tahini, fresh lemon juice, water, 1 minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
Once the falafel is cooked, remove them from the skillet or oven.
Divide the salad between two plates or bowls.
Top each salad with four hot homemade falafel patties.
Drizzle the Lemon-Tahini Dressing over the salad.
Serve immediately and enjoy your delicious Homemade Falafel Salad with Lemon-Tahini Dressing.
Bring broth to a boil. Reduce heat to medium and simmer for about 5 minutes. Add mushrooms and shrimp and cook until shrimp have heated through, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the fish sauce, lime juice, green onion, cilantro and chile pepper. Taste and adjust salt and pepper if needed.
Soup should be sour, salty, spicy and hot. Serve imemdiately.
In a large pot, heat the oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and bell pepper and cook until softened.
Add garlic, ginger and curry paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the broth, coconut milk, and fish sauce.
Bring to a boil.
Add the shrimp and rice noodles.
Cook until shrimp is pink and opaque.
Stir in the lime juice and serve immediately, topped with optional garnishes if using.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a small baking dish, spread a thin layer of enchilada sauce on the bottom.
Cut one of the corn tortillas into smaller pieces and place them in the baking dish to cover the bottom.
Layer half of the black beans, corn, and diced bell peppers on top of the tortilla pieces.
Sprinkle half of the shredded cheese over the beans and veggies.
Pour a little more enchilada sauce over the cheese layer.
Repeat the layers using the remaining tortilla, black beans, corn, bell peppers, and cheese.
Top it off with the remaining enchilada sauce.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for about 20-25 minutes, until the casserole is heated through and the cheese is melted.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 5 minutes to slightly brown the cheese on top.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a couple of minutes.
Garnish with chopped cilantro.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat; stir in onion and garlic.
Cook and stir until the onion has softened and turned translucent, about 5 minutes, add chicken and cook for 5 minutes more
Add tomatoes, chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Stir in chicken broth and water, bring to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer and cook for 5 to 10 minutes.
Add black beans, hominy, cilantro, and jalapeno pepper. Simmer until heated through, at least 10 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Season chicken breast with salt and pepper, place it on a baking sheet, and roast for 20-25 minutes until cooked through.
Remove chicken from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing it thinly.
Warm corn tortillas in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 1 minute on each side.
Place sliced chicken on warmed tortillas.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomato, avocado slices, chopped onion, and cilantro.
Squeeze lime juice over the tacos.
Preheat the oven to 400°F .
Spread the tortilla chips in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Sprinkle shredded cheese evenly over the tortilla chips.
Top with cooked black beans, diced tomatoes, diced bell peppers, sliced black olives, and diced onions.
Place the baking sheet in the oven and bake for 5-7 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the nachos from the oven and top with diced avocado, chopped cilantro, and a dollop of sour cream.
Serve immediately with optional toppings on the side.
Cook fusilli pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a food processor, combine cilantro leaves, peanuts, garlic, olive oil, and lemon juice. Blend until smooth.
Toss cooked fusilli pasta with cilantro pesto until evenly coated.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with grated Parmesan cheese.
Serve hot.
Toss chopped avocado with juice from 1 small lime.
In a large bowl, combine avocado, cucumbers, tomatoes, black beans, corn, and cilantro.
Stir in olive oil, red wine vinegar, salt, and pepper and stir to coat.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the onion and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, 5 minutes.
Add coriander, cumin and turmeric and cook, stirring, for 2 minutes.
Pulse tomatoes in the food processor until finely chopped. Add to the pan along with salt.
Reduce heat to maintain a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
Add chickpeas, spinach, and garam masala, reduce heat to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes more.
Serve topped with cilantro.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Place corn tortillas on a baking sheet and bake for 5-7 minutes until crispy.
Season shrimp with salt and pepper.
Heat cooking spray or oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook shrimp for 2-3 minutes per side until pink and cooked through.
Place cooked shrimp on crispy tortillas.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomato, avocado slices, chopped onion, and cilantro.
Squeeze lime juice over the tostadas. Top with jalapeno.
Serve hot.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Combine chili powder and cocoa powder in a bowl, sprinkle over both sides of pork loin
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and brown pork loin on both sides, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer pork and juices to a baking dish.
Roast in the preheated oven until pork is slightly pink in the center, about 12 minutes.
Combine mango, shallot, cilantro, and lemon juice in a bowl. Slice pork loin and top with juices from the baking dish and mango mixture. Top with cheese slices.
Slice the cauliflower head in half and use a knife to remove all the florets.
Insert the grater adapter into your food processor and start feeding the cauliflower florets through the top.
Heat the olive oil in a large pan and sauté the diced onion and garlic for a few minutes, until its translucent.
Add the cauliflower rice to the pan and sauté for another 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Add in the lemon juice and zest, and chopped cilantro. Toss everything together and serve immediately.
Place clam juice and oregano in a saucepa, bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Add shrimp and stir just until shrimp turn pink and are not quite cooked through, about 2 minutes.
Transfer shrimp to a bowl with a slotted spoon. Reserve liquid. Let shrimp cool to room temperature.
Place chopped tomatoes, cucumbers, celery, red onion, and jalapenos is a mixing bowl.
Add ketchup, cooking liquid from the shrimp, lime juice, cilantro, and hot sauce. Gently mix until ingredients are well blended.
Chop shrimp into bite-size pieces, leaving a few whole for garnishes. Stir chopped pieces into the vegetable mixture.
Wrap mixing bowl with plastic wrap. Place reserved shrimp in a bowl, wrap with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate shrimp cocktail and reserved shrimp until thoroughly chilled, 2 to 3 hours.
If mixture seems a bit thick, add a little tomato juice or pickle juice. Stir in avocado cubes.
Serve, if possible, in clear glass bowls. Top with reserved whole shrimp and some cilantro leaves.
Slice top and bottom ends off oranges; cut off peel and white pith, following the shape of the fruit.
Cut orange segments from the membrane to remove and roughly chop.
Place in a medium bowl. Discard peel, pith, and membrane.
Stir in onion, cilantro, chile, salt, and 1 tbsp lime juice.
Sprinkle both sides of snapper evenly with Cajun seasoning.
Heat oil in a large nonstick or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the snapper; cook until golden brown and cooked through, 3 to 4 minutes per side.
Transfer to a plate and flake into large pieces using 2 forks. Sprinkle with the remaining 1 tbsp lime juice.
Divide the snapper and cabbage evenly among warm tortillas. Top with the orange salsa.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
Preheat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
In a bowl, mix together harissa paste, olive oil, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to make a marinade.
Pat dry the chicken with paper towels and then coat them with the harissa marinade.
Once the grill is hot, place the chicken on the grill and cook for about 6-7 minutes per side, or until fully cooked and charred on the edges.
Remove the chicken from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the grilled harissa chicken thighs with lemon wedges on the side for squeezing over the top, and garnish with fresh cilantro if desired.
Pour the vegetable broth into a soup pot, and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Stir in the curry powder, cumin, cinnamon, and ginger, add the carrots.
Reduce heat to a simmer, and cook until the carrots are tender, about 20 minutes, stirring often.
Strain out the carrots from the broth, and place carrots into a blender, filling the pitcher no more than halfway full.
Pour about 1/4 cup vegetable broth into the blender to allow the carrots to puree. Add yeast.
Hold down the lid of the blender with a folded kitchen towel, and carefully start the blender and leave it to puree.
Puree in batches until smooth, if needed, return the pureed carrots to the vegetable broth.
Alternately, you can use a stick blender and puree the carrots right in the cooking pot.
Stir in the coconut milk and the water. Bring back to a simmer, sprinkle with cilantro to serve.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place diced butternut squash on a baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat evenly.
Roast the butternut squash in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until tender.
While the squash is roasting, heat the black beans in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add a pinch of salt and a dash of chili powder for extra flavor.
Warm the corn tortillas either on a dry skillet or in the microwave for about 20 seconds.
Assemble the tacos: Place the warmed tortillas on a plate. Spread the warmed black beans over the tortillas.
Top with the roasted butternut squash, diced onion, diced tomatoes, shredded lettuce, and chopped cilantro. Add cotija sheese and greek yogurt.
Preheat oven on broiler to 350°F. Oil a 9 x 13 inch pan with olive oil.
In a small saucepan, bring enchilada sauce to a simmer. Pour 1/2 cup enchilada sauce in the bottom of the baking dish, and swirl it around to cover the bottom.
Place a tortilla on a microwave-safe plate. Spread 1/4 shredded cheese over the tortilla, and sprinkle black beans over the cheese. Microwave for 20-30 seconds, until the cheese is melted.
After microwaving, carefully roll the tortilla into a tube, and lay it seam-side down in the baking dish. Repeat for all tortillas.
Pour the hot enchilada sauce over the rolled enchiladas, and sprinkle about remaining cheese over the top.
Place baking dish under the hot broiler for about 5 minutes, until cheese is melted and bubbly. Sprinkle with cilantro.
sake: Add two parts water to one part rice and cook over low to medium-low heat in a saucepan until all the water has been absorbed and rice is tender (15 to 20 minutes).
Cut steak into thin slices and set aside. You may want to freeze the steak for 10 minutes to make slicing it a little easier.
Cut the stems off the enoki mushrooms, and slice them horizontally into three equal parts.
In a small bowl, add 2 tablespoons of mirin, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, 2 tablespoons of sake, 3 tablespoons of dashi (or water), and set aside.
Cut green onion into thin slices. Separate the green part in one bowl, and the white part of the onion in another bowl.
Heat pan on medium/low and add the white part of the onion with mirin, soy sauce, sake, and dashi, and cook for 1 minute.
Add slices of beef and cook until medium.
Add enoki mushrooms and half of the green onions and cook for 1 minute.
Crack an egg, separating the yolk from the white, and carefully add the white part of the egg in the middle of the pan first.
After 30 seconds, add the yolk, and cook for an additional 1 minute on medium.
Place beef on the edges of the rice bowl and top with egg in the middle.
Sprinkle on the remaining green onion, sesame seeds, black pepper, and serve.
Combine and boil soy sauce, sake, and mirin in a small pan, remove from heat immediately and let cool.
If the tuna is in a block, cut tuna sashimi into ½" thick pieces.
Marinade tuna pieces in a cooled soy sauce mixture for 10-15 minutes.
Serve rice in a bowl, and top with marinated tuna, sesame seeds, wasabi, and roasted seaweed.
Pour some of the marinade sauce over rice if you want.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Gather all the ingredients.
Thinly slice the onions, cut the green onions into thin slices (save for garnish), and cut the meat into 3 inches pieces.
Heat the oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the onion until tender, about 3-5 minutes.
Add the meat to the pan, and cook until meat is no longer pink.
Add sake, mirin, and soy sauce.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 3-5 minutes.
If you like to add the egg, slowly drizzle the beaten egg over the beef.
Cook covered until the egg is almost done (don't overcook it).
Remove from the heat.
In a large donburi bowl, add steamed rice and put the beef and egg mixture on top
If you'd like, drizzle over remaining sauce.
Top with green onion.
Enjoy!
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, sake (or dry white wine), grated ginger, and minced garlic to create the marinade.
Add the chicken pieces to the marinade, ensuring they are well coated.
Let the chicken marinate for at least 15-20 minutes.
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F,
Coat the marinated chicken pieces with cornstarch.
Place the coated chicken in the air fryer basket, ensuring they are not overcrowded.
Air fry for 12-15 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through, or until the chicken is golden brown and cooked through.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Use thinly sliced pork belly, stir-fry pork or pork mince.
Lightly season the pork with salt and pepper, and coat with potato starch flour.
To make the sauce, place the grated ginger, soy sauce, mirin and sake (or water) in a small bowl and mix.
Heat small mount of oil in a frying pan over medium high heat, cook pork and onion.
When the pork is cooked and onion is soft, add the sauce and stir to combine well.
Half fill a bowl with hot cooked rice and cover it with the pork mixture.
Sprinkle some finely chopped spring onion on top.
Serve with rice.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, mirin, sake (or dry white wine), grated ginger, and minced garlic.
Heat ghee in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the chicken thighs to the skillet and cook until browned on both sides.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the chicken.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 10-12 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through, flipping the chicken halfway through.
Once cooked, remove the chicken from the skillet and let it rest for a minute.
Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, mirin, sake (or dry white wine), grated ginger, and minced garlic to create the teriyaki sauce.
Place the chicken thighs in a baking dish.
Pour the teriyaki sauce over the chicken, ensuring each piece is coated.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is fully cooked, basting with the sauce halfway through.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small saucepan, combine the dashi, soy sauce, mirin, sake. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion to the saucepan and cook until softened, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the sliced chicken thigh to the saucepan and simmer until the chicken is cooked through, about 3-4 minutes.
In a small bowl, beat the egg.
Pour the beaten egg evenly over the chicken and onion mixture in the saucepan. Cover and simmer for another 1-2 minutes until the egg is set.
Once the egg is cooked to your desired consistency, remove the saucepan from heat.
Serve the oyakodon hot over a bowl of cooked rice.
Garnish with chopped green onions and nori if desired.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
For the teriyaki sauce, mix together soy sauce, tsuyu, sake, sweet rice wine, and honey in a small pot.
Add the garlic, ginger and bring the sauce to boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Discard the garlic and ginger, set aside. Heat oil in a large heavy bottom skillet over med-high heat.
Add the onion and cook until they are just softened, about 2-3 minutes. Transfer to another plate; set aside.
Add the pork belly to the hot skillet and let them sear for 2-3 minute, then turn to the other side and cook for another 1 minute.
Pour the sauce over pork belly and let the sauce bubble. Reduce the sauce until it thickens, about 2-3 minutes. Remove from the heat.
To serve, put individual serving amount of rice in each bowl, put onion over the rice and top with pork belly slices.
Sprinkle chopped green onion and top with an egg yolk.
Serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, sake (or dry white wine), mirin, grated ginger, and minced garlic to create the marinade.
Thread the chicken pieces onto the soaked bamboo skewers.
Brush the chicken skewers with the marinade, reserving some for basting during cooking.
Heat ghee or olive oil in a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Grill the chicken skewers for 3-4 minutes on each side or until they are fully cooked, basting with the reserved marinade.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season the beef steak with salt and black pepper to taste.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Once the skillet is hot, add the steak and sear for 1-2 minutes on each side, or until desired doneness is reached.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, mirin, sake, and grated ginger to make the sauce.
Pour the sauce over the steak in the skillet.
Allow the sauce to simmer and reduce for about 1-2 minutes, while continuously basting the steak with the sauce.
Remove the steak from the skillet and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, mirin, sake (or dry white wine), grated ginger, and minced garlic to create the teriyaki sauce.
Place the chicken breast in a skillet or pan over medium heat.
Pour the teriyaki sauce over the chicken, ensuring it is well coated.
Cook the chicken for 6-8 minutes on each side or until fully cooked.
Shred the cooked chicken using two forks, pulling it apart into bite-sized pieces.
Drizzle any remaining teriyaki sauce over the pulled chicken and mix well.
Remove from heat and let it rest for a minute.
Preheat grill to medium (grill side, not hot plate) or a heavy based pan over medium heat.
It's important only to use medium heat because otherwise the eggplant will burn before it cooks through
Combine the Miso Glaze ingredients and whisk to combine and remove lumps.
Brush the cut side of each eggplant with the oil.
Do it 2 to 3 times to use up all the oil.
Place the cut side down on the grill and cook for 4 minutes until it turns a light golden brown.
Turn over, and cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
While it is cooking, use a spoon to spread a generous amount of Miso Glaze on the cut side of each eggplant.
Squeeze the side of the eggplant with your tongs (or fingers if you can handle the heat!) to check for doneness - it should be squishy and soft.
Turn over and grill just for 30 seconds to 1 minute, just to caramelise the glaze.
Remove from grill, garnish with shallots and sesame seeds and serve immediately with bread.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Fill a large skillet with about 1 inch of water and bring to a boil. Poach fish just until cooked on the outside, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer fillets to a broiler pan.
In a small bowl, stir together the miso paste, sake, sesame seeds, sesame oil, water, soy sauce and rice vinegar. Spread this over the tops of the salmon fillets.
Bake for 15 minutes in the preheated oven, or until almost cooked through. Switch the oven to broil, and broil about 5 minutes. Cut fillets into portions to serve.
Adjust the oven rack to the middle position and preheat the oven to 400ºF.
Combine the panko and oil in a frying pan and toast over medium heat until golden brown.
Transfer panko into a shallow dish and allow to cool. Cut the onions into thin slices and the mitsuba into small pieces.
Remove the extra fat and make a couple of slits on the connective tissue between the meat and fat.
This will allow Tonkatsu to stay nice and flat and prevent it from curling up.
Pound the meat with a meat pounder, or if you don’t have one then just use the back of a knife to pound.
Mold the extended meat back into the original shape with your hands.
Sprinkle salt and freshly ground black pepper. Dredge each pork piece in the flour to coat completely. Pat off the excess flour.
Beat one egg in a bowl and coat the pork with the beaten egg. Finally, coat with the toasted panko.
Press on the panko flakes to make sure they adhere to the pork.
lace the pork on the prepared baking sheet lined with parchment paper or even better if you have an oven-safe wire rack.
Bake at 400ºF until the pork is no longer pink inside, about 20 minutes.
Remove the tonkatsu from the oven and cut into 1-inch pieces (so you can eat with chopsticks).
Combine Seasonings in a liquid measuring cup or bowl. This amount could be more than you need, depending on the frying pan size you decide to use.
You can store the extra in a mason jar in the refrigerator for up to a week. Beat one egg in a bowl.
In a large stockpot, cover pork bones with cold water;
Over medium heat, bring the pot to a boil, then remove and strain the water off the bones and rinse them in cold water;
Put the bones back into the stockpot and cover with water bringing it to a boil. Reduce the heat to medium, add an onion, and cover;
Keep the pork bones cooking over medium heat and adding water as needed to keep them covered for 10-12 hours;
After 10-12 hours, remove the bones, strain the broth, and allow it to cool; Refrigerate for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 4 months;
In a large Ziploc bag, combine the soy sauce, sake, mirin, garlic, sesame oil, and green onions;
Put the pork belly into the bag of marinade and seal removing all of the air;
Place the bag into a large pot or container of water with a sous vide attachment and cook for 10 hours at 170°F;
Remove from the sous vide and discard the bag and marinade;
Once cooled completely, slice into 8-12 slices;
You will use 4 slices for the servings of ramen, but you can freeze the additional pork belly for up to 4 months;
Hard boil 4 eggs and set aside to peel and slice just before serving;
Simmer the miso, sake, mirin, and salt for 5 minutes in a small saucepan then set aside. Cook your noodles according to package directions;
Fry the sliced pork in a large skillet until brown on all sides; Divide the miso tare into four bowls;
Top the miso with ½ cup broth and mix together with the miso tare;
Top with noodles, additional 1 ½ cups broth, an egg, a slice of pork, and green onions;
Serve with your favorite ramen toppings.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Fill a large skillet with about 1 inch of water and bring to a boil. Poach fish just until cooked on the outside, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer fillets to a broiler pan.
In a small bowl, stir together the miso paste, sake,  sesame seeds, sesame oil, water, soy sauce and rice vinegar. Spread this over the tops of the salmon fillets.

Bake for 15 minutes in the preheated oven, or until almost cooked through. Switch the oven to broil, and broil until the top is browned and bubbly, about 5 more minutes. Cut fillets into portions to serve.

Lightly pound the pork to tenderize. If there is connecting tissue separating red meat and a band of fat, cut the tissue every 1”. This will prevent the meat from curling when cooked. Sprinkle with salt and pepper on both sides of the meat.
Coat the meat with flour, 2 eggs and breadcrumbs.
Heat oil to 338 °F and fry the meat for 2 minutes. Turnover and fry for another 2 minutes. Turn over again and fry for 1 minute, then put aside on kitchen paper.
Cut each tonkatsu into ¾" wide strips. Mix the sauce ingredients (dashi, soy sauce, mirin, sake)

In a very small fry pan or skillet, about 7", add half of the sauce and half of the sliced onions and cook over medium-high heat. When the sauce starts boiling, reduce heat to medium and add tonkatsu in the center. Cook for 2 minutes.

Beat an egg and pour it over with a swirling motion from the center towards the outer edge. Place a lid on and cook for 30 seconds or until the egg is almost cooked but slightly runny. Turn the heat off.
Bring a large pot of lightly-salted water to the boil, add the rice and cook according to the package instructions.
Slide the katsu-don topping including the sauce onto the rice in a bowl. Turn the heat off and serve.
Place the miso, sake, honey, ginger, soy sauce and sesame oil in a large bowl and whisk to combine. Add the salmon fillets to the bowl and marinate in the fridge for at least 20 minutes.
Preheat the broiler. Coat a sheet pan with olive oil and place the salmon filets on it. Spoon any excess marinade over the top.
Broill for 8 to 10 minutes ot until the salmon is opaque
orange: In a mixing bowl, combine masa harina, orange juice, orange zest, olive oil, and a pinch of salt. Mix well until a smooth dough forms. Adjust sweetness with stevia or other non-sugar sweetener if desired.
Take a soaked corn husk and spread a thin layer of the masa mixture onto the center, leaving some space around the edges.
Fold the sides of the corn husk over the masa mixture to enclose it.
Fold up the bottom of the corn husk to form a closed bottom.
Repeat with the remaining masa mixture and corn husks.
Place the tamales in a steamer basket.
Steam the tamales over boiling water for about 45-60 minutes, or until the masa is firm and cooked through.
Remove the tamales from the steamer and let them cool slightly before serving.
Serve the sweet orange tamales warm.
Place the diced beet and potato in a pot, cover with water and bring to the boil. Simmer for 10-12 minutes or until the potatoes are nice and tender. Drain and set aside to cool.
In a large mixing bowl, add the carrot, apple, peas and now cooled beet and potato, season with salt and pepper to taste, drizzle with olive oil and orange juice and toss. Serve slightly chilled.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Rinse and pat the sole dry with a paper towel.
Set the sole on top of the lined baking sheet.
Lightly cover the top of each fish with breadcrumbs then line with orange slices and top with lemon pieces, chili peppers, basil and parsley.
In a small bowl combine the extra virgin olive oil, salt, pepper and paprika.
Drizzle the mixture over the sole. Bake for 20-25 minutes.
The sole is done when the meat has become opaque and flakes when scraped with a fork.
In a medium skillet bring salted water to a boil. Add rice and cook according to package directions, for 10 minutes. Fluff with a fork and set aside.
In a medium pot, add orange juice, vinegar, soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and red chili flakes. Heat for 3 minutes.
In a small bowl whisk together 1 tbsp cornstarch with 2 tbsp of water and form a paste.
Add to orange sauce and whisk together. Continue to cook for 5 minutes. Add orange zest.
Dip chicken pieces in egg mixture and then flour mixture. Place on plate.
Cook several chicken pieces at a time for 3 minutes and place on a paper towl.
Toss chicken with orange sauce and serve with rice.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the chicken cubes with salt and pepper.
Add the chicken cubes to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes per side.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, rice vinegar, orange juice, orange zest, minced garlic, minced ginger, and crushed red pepper flakes.
Add the sliced red bell pepper to the skillet with the cooked chicken.
Pour the sauce mixture over the chicken and bell pepper in the skillet.
Stir well to coat the chicken and bell pepper evenly with the sauce.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving plate.
Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds.
Serve the spicy orange chicken with red bell pepper hot and enjoy!
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Preheat your grill to medium-high heat.
In a bowl, combine the diced red bell pepper, onion, tomato and orange segments.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, honey, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper to create the dressing.
Pour the dressing over the red pepper and orange mixture. Toss to combine.
Season the salmon fillet with salt and pepper.
Place the salmon fillet on the preheated grill. Grill for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until the salmon is cooked to your desired doneness.
While the salmon is grilling, spoon the red pepper and orange relish over the top.
Once the salmon is cooked, remove it from the grill and transfer it to a plate.
In a bowl or jar, pour in the orange juice.
Add chia seeds and vanilla extract to the orange juice.
Stir the mixture thoroughly until the chia seeds are well combined.
Cover the bowl or jar and refrigerate for at least 2-3 hours, or ideally overnight, to allow the chia seeds to absorb the liquid and form a pudding-like consistency.
Once the pudding has set, give it a good stir.
Serve chilled and enjoy your refreshing orange chia pudding topped with orange!
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Grease a muffin tin with a little olive oil.
In a food processor beat the eggs until fluffy. Stir in the chicken, pepper, zest, kale, and add the seasoning.
Transfer into the prepared muffin tin. Place in the oven for 20 minutes and serve.
Put all the fruit into a deep pot and cover with 6 cups of water. Bring to boil on high.
Add honey, cloves, and cinnamon. Lower to medium and cook an additional 30 minutes, or until the fruit is soft.
Serve compote hot, or keep in fridge until it cools down.
To a mixing bowl, add kiwis, berries and some orange. Drizzle some fresh orange juice on top and toss well to combine. Serve chilled.
Combine all ingredients in a blender and blend.
Pour into glass and serve with a slice of lemon on top.
Wash the kale leaves thoroughly. Peel and segment the orange.
Place the kale leaves, orange segments, banana into a blender.
Blend until smooth, gradually adding water as needed to achieve your desired consistency.
In a bowl, mix orange juice, soy sauce, and rice vinegar.
In a wok or skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add thinly sliced chicken to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 3-4 minutes).
Pour the orange juice mixture over the chicken, stirring to coat evenly.
Toss in bell pepper, carrot and onion, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
In a pot with boiling water add the tagliatelle.  Follow the instruction of your spaghetti brand. It usually takes about 8 minutes to cook.
Place the oil in a pan and let it warmup. Saute the onion and garlic.
Roast the jalapeno by toasting by bathing it in a little oil and then placing it straight into the stove flame.
In the blender add the cream, the milk, salt, and jalapeno.
Blend and the taste. Add more jalapeno if you prefer it spicier or add more salt to taste.
Get rid of the water were the pasta cooked and place the pasta back in the pot. Add the lender mix to the pasta as well as the cheese.
Mix all together and garnish with orange, arugula, and few slices od jalapeno.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend until creamy and smooth. Serve immediately.
Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
Zest and juice the orange into a small bowl.
Add olive oil, fresh thyme leaves, salt, and pepper to the bowl and mix well to combine.
Season the beef steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Brush the orange and thyme mixture over the steak, coating it evenly.
Grill the steak for 3-4 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or longer to your desired level of doneness.
Remove the steak from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes.
Slice the steak against the grain and serve.
Optionally, you can drizzle any remaining orange and thyme mixture over the sliced steak for extra flavor.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper, folding up the edges to keep sauce in.
Combine orange juice, oil, zest, soy sauce, and honey in a bowl. Whisk well.
Pour a few spoonfuls of sauce on the prepared baking pan. Place salmon skin up and pour remaining sauce over it.
Bake for about 5 minutes and carefully flip salmon skin down. Spoon some sauce over the top of the salmon.
Bake for 20 minutes, until salmon, is done.
In a blender, puree the spinach, banana, 2/3 cup of blueberries, yogurt, orange juice and honey. Blend until smooth.
Transfer the smoothie into a bowl, top with banana slices, kiwi and a sprinkling of matcha powder before enjoying.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Place the salmon fillet on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Drizzle 1/2 tbsp olive oil over the salmon and season with salt and pepper.
Roast the salmon in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until it flakes easily with a fork.

While the salmon is roasting, prepare the citrus salsa verde. In a bowl, combine the chopped orange segments, lemon juice, lime juice, chopped cilantro, parsley, and capers. Drizzle with remaining olive oil and toss to combine.
Once the salmon is cooked, remove it from the oven and transfer it to a serving plate.
Spoon the citrus salsa verde over the roasted salmon.
Serve immediately.
Layer orange, mango, cashew butter, coconut, chia seeds, and flax into a blender, add orange juice.
Cover and blend mixture until smooth, adding water for a thinner smoothie.
coriander: In a food processor, combine the chickpeas, diced red onion, 1 garlic, fresh parsley, fresh cilantro, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, pepper, baking powder, and 2 tablespoons of flour. Pulse until the mixture forms a coarse paste. If needed, add the extra tablespoon of flour to achieve the right consistency.
Form the mixture into 8 falafel patties.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the falafel patties and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until they are golden brown and crispy.
While the falafel is cooking, prepare the salad. In a large bowl, combine the torn lettuce, thinly sliced red onion, corn kernels, diced tomatoes, and diced cucumber.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the Lemon-Tahini Dressing by combining tahini, fresh lemon juice, water, 1 minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
Once the falafel is cooked, remove them from the skillet or oven.
Divide the salad between two plates or bowls.
Top each salad with four hot homemade falafel patties.
Drizzle the Lemon-Tahini Dressing over the salad.
Serve immediately and enjoy your delicious Homemade Falafel Salad with Lemon-Tahini Dressing.
Preheat your grill to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, ground cumin, paprika, ground coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture evenly over both sides of the chicken breasts.
Grill the chicken breasts for about 6-8 minutes per side or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F. Remove from heat and let them rest for a few minutes before slicing.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the Cauliflower 'Rice' Tabbouleh.
Cut the cauliflower into florets and place them in a food processor. Pulse until the cauliflower resembles rice grains.
In a large bowl, combine the cauliflower 'rice', chopped parsley, diced tomato, and diced bell pepper.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the dressing over the cauliflower 'rice' mixture and toss to combine.
Heat oil in a large pan and add the onion and garlic and cook for 2 minutes. Add the spices and stir well.
Add the chopped tomatoes, drained chickpeas and salt and pepper. Reduce the heat and simmer for 8 minutes.
2 minutes before it has finished cooking, add the spinach and coriander and stir well. Cook for 2 minutes or until the spinach has wilted.
Serve with chopped coriander.
Heat the olive oil in a small pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until it becomes translucent.
Add the minced garlic, harissa paste, ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook for a minute to release the flavors.
Add the lamb pieces and brown them on all sides.
Stir in the cauliflower florets, sliced carrots, and continue to cook for a few minutes.
Pour in the beef broth and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer, cover the pot, and let it cook for about 20-25 minutes, or until the lamb is tender, and the vegetables are cooked.
Adjust the seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
