Cut the eggplant in half lengthwise. Score the flesh in a crisscross pattern, then brush it with olive oil and place it on a baking sheet.
Roast the eggplant in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until it becomes tender and slightly browned. Remove it from the oven and let it cool.

While the eggplant is cooling, prepare the grape leaves. If they are from a jar, rinse and drain them. If they are fresh, blanch them in boiling water for a few seconds and then cool them under cold water.
In a bowl, combine the cooked rice, diced tomatoes, diced onions, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Once the roasted eggplant is cool enough to handle, scoop out the flesh, leaving the skin intact.
Chop the roasted eggplant flesh and add it to the rice mixture. Mix everything together.
Place a grape leaf on a flat surface, vein side up, and trim the stem if necessary.
Place a small portion of the eggplant and rice mixture in the center of the grape leaf.
Fold the sides of the grape leaf over the filling, then roll it up to encase the filling.
Place the stuffed grape leaf in an oven-safe dish.
Repeat the process with the remaining grape leaves and filling.
Drizzle olive oil over the stuffed grape leaves.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until the Dolma is heated through.
Preheat oven to 360°F.
Use a small spoon to remove the flesh from the centre of the zucchini. Finely chop the flesh and reserve in a bowl.
Line a baking tray with baking paper. Oil zucchini shells with olive oil and place cut-side down on the lined tray. Cover with foil. Bake for 15 minutes or until just tender.
Meanwhile, heat a large frying pan over high heat. Add the mince and cook, stirring with a wooden spoon to break up any lumps, for 5 minutes or until the mince changes colour.
Add the reserved zucchini flesh, all the seasoning and tomato. Reduce heat to medium. Simmer for 10 mins or until the sauce thickens slightly. Season.
Turn the zucchini shells cut-side up. Spoon mince mixture into each shell and sprinkle with cheddar. Bake for 10 mins or until cheddar melts and is golden brown. Sprinkle with coriander to serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, combine the plain Greek yogurt, ground cumin, ground paprika, ground coriander, ground cayenne pepper, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Add the cubed chicken pieces to the yogurt and spice mixture. Toss to coat the chicken evenly.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers.
Heat the olive oil in a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Place the chicken skewers on the hot grill pan or skillet.
Grill the chicken kebabs for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are cooked through and have nice grill marks.
Remove the chicken kebabs from the grill and let them rest for a minute.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and add olive oil.
Remove the sausage from its casing and crumble it into the skillet. Cook until it's browned and cooked through.
While the sausage is cooking, beat the eggs in a bowl and season with ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Once the sausage is cooked, remove it from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the finely chopped red onion and sauté until it becomes soft and translucent.
Pour the seasoned beaten eggs into the skillet with the sautéed onions.
Sprinkle the crumbled sausage and shelled pistachios on top of the eggs.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for about 8-10 minutes, or until the eggs are set.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Combine the peas, the juice of 1 lemon, the vegetable stock, salt, and pepper in a blender. Puree until smooth.
Grease a baking dish with the olive oil. Place the salmon in the dish and turn to coat with the oil. Sprinkle each piece with the seasoning mix and the remaining juice of 1 lemon.
Bake for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through.
Pour the puree on a serving platter. Place the salmon on top, and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, carrot, and celery to the pot. Cook until vegetables are softened.
Add minced garlic, curry powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, and ground turmeric to the pot. Cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour broth into the pot and bring to a simmer.
Add cooked rice and diced cooked chicken breast to the pot. Cook for a few minutes until heated through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a bowl, mix chicken pieces with yogurt, garlic, turmeric, cumin, coriander, and chili powder.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add marinated chicken pieces and cook for 5-6 minutes until browned.
Add chopped tomato and cook for another 5-7 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Stir in heavy cream, garam masala, and salt; cook for 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a food processor, combine the canned chickpeas, diced onion, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, ground paprika, baking powder, salt, and black pepper.
Process the mixture until it forms a coarse paste.
Shape the mixture into a small patty or ball with your hands.
Place the patty or ball on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle olive oil over the top of the patty or ball.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until the falafel is firm and slightly crispy on the outside.
Remove the baked falafel from the oven.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, combine the mashed sweet potato, canned chickpeas, finely chopped red onion, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, and pepper.
Add the breadcrumbs to the mixture and combine until it's well incorporated.
Shape the mixture into small falafel balls.
Place the falafel on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle olive oil over the falafel.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the falafel is crispy and cooked through.
In a separate bowl, combine the shredded cabbage, grated carrot, grated apple, mayonnaise, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Mix everything together to create the coleslaw.
Serve!
Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced onion, minced garlic, and minced ginger to the skillet. Cook until the onions are softened and translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chicken leg pieces to the skillet and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5-6 minutes.
Stir in ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, and garam masala. Cook for another 1-2 minutes.
Add the rinsed basmati rice to the skillet and stir to combine with the chicken and spices.
Pour in low-sodium chicken broth and season with salt and pepper to taste. Bring to a simmer.
Cover the skillet with a lid and reduce the heat to low. Let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the chicken is tender.
Remove from heat and let it sit, covered, for an additional 5 minutes.
Fluff the chicken biryani with a fork before serving.
Serve the chicken biryani hot.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Remove the leaves and stem from the whole cauliflower, leaving the core intact.
In a mixing bowl, combine the olive oil with the ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, turmeric, salt, and pepper.
Brush the spiced olive oil mixture all over the whole cauliflower, ensuring it's evenly coated.
Place the cauliflower on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.

Roast in the preheated oven for about 40-50 minutes, or until the cauliflower is tender and the outer layer is nicely browned. You can check for doneness by inserting a knife into the center to ensure it's tender.
Serve your Spiced Whole Cauliflower Roast as a side dish or a light meal.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, combine the plain Greek yogurt, ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, paprika, chili powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the bite-sized chicken pieces to the yogurt and spice mixture. Toss to coat the chicken evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the marinated chicken pieces to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are browned and cooked through.
Once the chicken is cooked, reduce the heat to low.
Add the tomato sauce and milk to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Let the mixture simmer for about 5 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken and the flavors to meld.
In a mixing bowl, combine ground pork with all the spices and apple cider vinegar. Mix well to combine.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the seasoned ground pork mixture to the skillet and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Once cooked, remove from heat and drain any excess fat if necessary.
Serve the homemade Mexican chorizo hot as desired, such as in tacos, burritos, or scrambled eggs.

In a mixing bowl, combine the mashed potato, finely chopped onion, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, pepper, chopped fresh cilantro, breadcrumbs, and egg. Mix well until fully combined.
Divide the mixture into equal portions and shape each portion into a small cutlet.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Place the cutlets in the skillet and cook until golden brown and crispy on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove from the skillet and drain on paper towels.
Serve the Potato Cutlets hot with your favorite dipping sauce or side dish.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a food processor, combine the cooked beetroot, canned chickpeas, finely chopped red onion, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, and pepper.
Pulse the mixture until it forms a coarse paste.
Transfer the beetroot and chickpea mixture to a bowl and add the breadcrumbs. Mix until it's well combined.
Shape the mixture into small falafel balls.
Place the falafel on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle olive oil over the falafel.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the falafel is crispy and cooked through.
In a pot, heat ghee or olive oil over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic to the pot, sautéing until softened (about 2-3 minutes).
Add diced chicken to the pot, cooking until it's browned on all sides (about 3-4 minutes).
Stir in red curry paste, ground coriander, and turmeric powder, ensuring the chicken is well coated.
Add diced butternut squash and sliced red bell pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Pour in chicken broth and coconut milk, bringing the soup to a simmer.
Let it simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and the squash is tender.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the Red Curry Chicken and Squash Soup.
dill: Start by cooking 1/2 cup of dry couscous. In a small saucepan, bring 1/2 cup of water to a boil. Stir in the 1/2 cup of dry couscous.
Remove the saucepan from heat, cover it with a lid, and let it sit for 5 minutes to allow the couscous to absorb the water.
After 5 minutes, fluff the couscous with a fork to separate the grains. Allow it to cool.
After 5 minutes, fluff the couscous with a fork to separate the grains. Allow it to cool.
Drizzle 1 tablespoon of extra-virgin olive oil and 1 tablespoon of fresh lemon juice over the ingredients in the bowl.
Season the couscous salad with salt and black pepper to taste.
Toss all the ingredients together until they are well combined.
In a mixing bowl, combine 1 cup of drained and rinsed black beans, 1 cup of halved cherry tomatoes, and 1/2 cup of corn kernels.
Add 1/2 diced avocado to the bowl.
Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of chopped fresh dill over the ingredients.
Squeeze the juice of half a lime into the bowl to add a zesty flavor.
Season the salad with a pinch of salt and pepper to taste. If you like it spicy, you can also add 1/4 teaspoon of chili powder.
Toss all the ingredients together until they are well combined.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add the celery, onion, and garlic and saute for about 5 minutes. Add in the vegetable broth and water.
Add in the raw cashews, bay leaf, and thyme. Bring to a simmer and cook for about 15 minutes. Remove the bay leaf, then blend.
Make sure to blend very well so that the cashews are completely blended. Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately, garnished with dill.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a small ovenproof skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the thinly sliced potatoes and cook for about 5-7 minutes until they start to soften and turn slightly golden. Season with salt and pepper.
Stir in the chopped spring onions and cook for another 2 minutes until they become tender.
In a bowl, whisk the eggs and then pour them over the potatoes and spring onions in the skillet.
Sprinkle the shredded cheese and chopped dill evenly over the top.
Let the mixture cook on the stovetop for about 2 minutes to set the bottom.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for approximately 10-12 minutes or until the frittata is set and the top is lightly golden.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F
Place the salmon fillet on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Season with salt, pepper, and half of the lemon juice.
Bake for about 12-15 minutes or until the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is baking, prepare the quinoa according to the package instructions.
In a bowl, toss the mixed vegetables with olive oil, minced garlic, dried dill, salt, and pepper.
Spread the vegetables evenly on a separate baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for about 10-15 minutes or until they are tender and slightly charred.

Once the salmon, quinoa, and roasted vegetables are ready, assemble the dish on a plate. Place the cooked quinoa on one side, the baked salmon on another side, and the roasted vegetables on the remaining space.
Drizzle the remaining lemon juice over the salmon and serve.
Cook the pasta in a large saucepan of boiling water following packet directions or until just al dente. Drain the pasta.

Meanwhile, heat a large frying pan over medium heat. Add the spring onion and cook for 1 minute. Add the cream and bring to a simmer. Add the mustard, horseradish and lemon juice and cook, stirring occasionally, for 2-3 minutes.
In a separate pot with water poach the eggs by creating a whirl in the water and cracking them in.
Add the pasta mixture, salmon and dill to the sauce in the pan and gently toss to combine. Remove from heat. Season.
In a bowl, combine the diced salmon, diced avocado, finely chopped red onion, and capers.
Add the lemon juice and olive oil to the mixture. Gently stir to combine.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve immediately, garnished with fresh dill.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a small oven-safe dish, place the smoked salmon slices and warm them in the preheated oven for about 3-4 minutes.

While the salmon is warming, prepare the hollandaise sauce. In a bowl, mix together the Greek yogurt, Dijon mustard, fresh dill, a squeeze of lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Adjust the seasoning to your taste.
Fill a small saucepan with water and bring it to a gentle simmer. Crack the egg into a small bowl.
Create a gentle whirlpool in the simmering water by stirring it with a spoon, and carefully slide the egg into the center of the whirlpool.
Poach for about 3-4 minutes for a runny yolk.
While the egg is poaching, assemble the dish. Place the toasted English muffin on a plate.
Remove the warmed smoked salmon from the oven and place it on top of the English muffin halves.
Carefully remove the poached egg from the simmering water with a slotted spoon, allowing any excess water to drain, and place it on top of the smoked salmon.
Drizzle the dill-infused Greek yogurt sauce over the poached egg.
Season with a pinch of salt and pepper if desired.
Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly oil 12-hole round bottomed patty tins. Use a 3 inch cutter to cut rounds from the pastry and line the tins with the pastry rounds.
Divide the salmon, herbs and cream cheese evenly between the pastry shells.
Whisk the eggs and cream together and carefully pour into the shells. Bake for 20 mins until puffed and golden brown. Lift quiches from tins and then place on a wire rack to cool.
Melt ghee in a skillet over medium heat. Pour beaten eggs into the skillet, let cook undisturbed until the bottom of the eggs begin to firm, 1 to 2 minutes.
Stir cottage cheese and dill into eggs and season with black pepper. Cook and stir until eggs are nearly set, 3 to 4 minutes more.
Preheat oven to 400°F .
Rinse salmon, and arrange in a baking dish. Sprinkle salt, pepper, onion powder, and dill over the fish and potatoes. Oil over top.
Bake in preheated oven for 20 to 25 minutes. Salmon is done when it flakes easily with a fork.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the seasoned chicken breast to the skillet and cook until golden brown on both sides and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes per side. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add sliced mushrooms and cook until they are tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and dried thyme, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Pour in the low-sodium broth and bring to a simmer.
Return the cooked chicken breast to the skillet and cook for another 2-3 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Remove from heat and let it rest for a few minutes.
Garnish with chopped dill.
Serve the chicken with mushrooms hot.
Preheat your grill or grill pan over medium heat.
Lightly coat the grill or grill pan with cooking spray or a small amount of olive oil.
Season the salmon fillet with salt and pepper.
Place the seasoned salmon fillet on the grill and cook for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
While the salmon is cooking, toast the two bun.
In a small bowl, mix together the Greek yogurt, lemon juice, and chopped fresh dill to make the dill sauce.
Once the salmon is cooked, remove it from the grill.
Spread the dill sauce on one side of each slice of toasted bread.
Place the grilled salmon fillet on the bottom part of the bun.
Top with lettuce leaves, onion slices and tomato slices if desired, then place the top bun.
Serve immediately.
Add the cucumber to a bowl with the yogurt. Add in the remaining ingredients. Stir well to combine.
Add the unpeeled potatoes to a large pot and cover with water. On high heat, bring the pot to boil. Add 1 tsp salt. Lower the heat and cook the potatoes on low about 15 minutes.
Discard the water. Fill the pot with cold water and start peeling the potatoes. Mash the potatoes with protein powder until puree, with a masher.
Heat a pan on medium heat and add oil. Add the chopped onions saute for 1 minute. Add the mushrooms, 1 tsp salt, and pepper and continue to saute for 10 minutes. Set aside.
Add flour to the potato puree and mix well.
Dust a surface with flour. Add 1 tbsp potato dough, place 2 tsp mushroom mixture to the center and fold the edges to cover the mushroom stuffing. Form a patty with both hands.
Heat a large pan with 1 tbsp oil on medium heat. Add the patties to the pan. Fry each side for 5 minutes.
Serve seasoned with dill, rosemary and sour cream.
In a small bowl, combine the Greek yogurt, mayonnaise, chopped fresh dill, lemon juice, and minced garlic.
Stir until well mixed.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the dill dip to a serving bowl.
Serve with vegetable sticks, crackers, or chips.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the leek and saute for 3 minutes.
Then add the asparagus and saute another 2 minutes. Then add the frozen peas, carots, potatoes, salt and pepper, and saute another minute.
In a large bowl, whisk the eggs, yogurt, salt and pepper. Pour the eggs over the half of the vegetables in the pan. Cook on the stove for 3 minutes.
Add the remaining vegetables on top, then dollop with the cheese. Transfer to the oven and cook for 15 minutes.
Garnish with dill before serving.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Season the salmon fillet with salt and pepper.
Drizzle olive oil over the salmon and sprinkle chopped fresh dill on top.
Place lemon slices on top of the salmon.
Wrap the salmon in aluminum foil, ensuring it is fully sealed.
Place the foil-wrapped salmon on a baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove the salmon from the oven and carefully unwrap it from the foil.
Place the potatoes in a medium-size pot and cover them with water.
Salt the water and bring to the boil over medium-high heat. Continue cooking until potatoes are tender, about 15 minutes.
Cook the bacon in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Once crisp, place on a kitchen towel-lined plate and crumble into small pieces.
Whisk in the vinegar, oil, pepper, dill, and salt and stir until thick.
Add the sliced, cooked potatoes and toss to coat. Top with the crumbled bacon.
Pulse cauliflower florets in a food processor until chopped into rice-sized pieces, about 10 seconds.
Transfer cauliflower 'rice' to a large bowl; add lemon juice, olive oil, and garlic.
Add tomatoes, scallions, mint, parsley, salt, pomegranate seeds and pepper and mix well.
paprika: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Toss the diced sweet potatoes in a little olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread them on a baking sheet and roast for about 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly caramelized.
While the sweet potatoes are roasting, prepare the tamarind dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together tamarind concentrate, maple syrup, olive oil, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Once the sweet potatoes are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool slightly.
In a mixing bowl, combine the roasted sweet potatoes, finely chopped red onion, fresh cilantro,, toasted pecans, cucumber, arugula and carrots.
Drizzle the tamarind dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasonings, if needed.
Preheat your grill to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, ground cumin, paprika, ground coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture evenly over both sides of the chicken breasts.
Grill the chicken breasts for about 6-8 minutes per side or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F. Remove from heat and let them rest for a few minutes before slicing.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the Cauliflower 'Rice' Tabbouleh.
Cut the cauliflower into florets and place them in a food processor. Pulse until the cauliflower resembles rice grains.
In a large bowl, combine the cauliflower 'rice', chopped parsley, diced tomato, and diced bell pepper.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the dressing over the cauliflower 'rice' mixture and toss to combine.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
In a bowl, mix together the grated sweet potato, olive oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Shape the mixture into small tots or cylinders.
Place the sweet potato tots in the air fryer basket in a single layer, making sure they are not touching.
Air fry the sweet potato tots at 375°F for 12-15 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Once the sweet potato tots are cooked, remove them from the air fryer and serve hot.
Enjoy your delicious and healthy sweet potato tots as a snack or side dish!
Preheat your air fryer to 400°F.
In a bowl, combine the grated sweet potato, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Take small portions of the sweet potato mixture and shape them into tots.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the sweet potato tots in the air fryer basket, making sure they are not touching each other.
Air fry at 400°F for 8-10 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through, or until the tots are crispy and golden brown.
Once done, remove the sweet potato tots from the air fryer and serve hot.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the sole fillet with salt and pepper on both sides.
In a bowl, combine lump crabmeat, Panko breadcrumbs, chopped fresh parsley, minced garlic, lemon juice, and paprika. Mix well to create the stuffing.
Lay the sole fillet flat on a baking sheet or oven-safe dish. Spoon the crabmeat stuffing onto the center of the sole fillet.
Carefully fold the edges of the sole fillet over the stuffing, creating a neat packet. You can secure it with toothpicks if needed.
Drizzle olive oil over the top of the stuffed sole.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 12-15 minutes or until the sole is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove the stuffed sole from the oven and let it rest for a minute or two.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the ground turkey until browned. Break it into crumbles as it cooks.
Add diced onions and bell peppers to the skillet. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are softened.
Stir in kidney beans, diced tomatoes, and tomato sauce.
Season the mixture with chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine.
Let the chili simmer for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chili to thicken.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Preheat a skillet over medium heat.
Rub the chicken breast with olive oil, paprika, garlic powder, chili powder, salt, and pepper.
Cook the seasoned chicken breast in the preheated skillet for about 4-5 minutes per side or until fully cooked through. Remove from heat and let it rest for a few minutes.
While the chicken is resting, prepare the vegetables by washing and slicing them.
Lay the large lettuce leaf flat on a plate.
Slice the cooked chicken breast into thin strips.
Place the chicken strips on top of the lettuce leaf.
Add the shredded carrots, bell pepper and sliced cucumber on top of the chicken.
In a small bowl, mix together the Greek yogurt and sriracha.
Drizzle the yogurt sauce over the chicken and vegetables.
Wrap the chicken and vegetables in a tortilla.
Serve immediately.
Preheat your air fryer to 380°F.
In a bowl, toss the sliced turkey breast, bell pepper, and onion with olive oil, chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.
Place the seasoned turkey and vegetables in the air fryer basket.
Air fry at 380°F for 8-10 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through, until the turkey is cooked through and the vegetables are tender and slightly charred.
Warm the tortillas in the air fryer for about 1 minute, if desired.
Once cooked, remove the turkey and vegetables from the air fryer.
Fill the tortillas with the turkey and vegetable mixture.
Cook the elbow macaroni according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the ground beef, breaking it apart with a spoon, until browned.
Add the chopped onion and bell pepper to the skillet and cook until they soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the canned diced tomatoes, beef broth, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for about 10 minutes, allowing the flavors to blend.
Add the cooked elbow macaroni to the skillet and stir until everything is combined and heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add diced onions and minced garlic. Sauté until the onions become translucent.
Stir in diced bell peppers and diced tomatoes. Cook for a few minutes until they start to soften.
Add canned chickpeas and season with ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Cook for another few minutes to infuse the flavors.
Pour in light coconut milk and simmer for about 5-7 minutes until the curry thickens.
Serve your Brazilian Coconut Chickpea Curry over the cooked rice.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a bowl, combine the olive oil, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add the beef cubes to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the seasoning mixture.
Thread the seasoned beef cubes onto skewers, alternating with the red onion, cherry tomatoes, zucchini slices, and eggplant pieces.
Place the skewers on the preheated grill and cook for about 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until the beef is cooked to your desired doneness and the vegetables are tender.
Remove the skewers from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the beef shish kabobs hot with your favorite sides.
Make zucchini noodles using a spiralizer. Mash avocado in a small bowl and mix in pesto and nutritional yeast until smooth.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add zucchini noodles; cook and stir until softened, 5 to 7 minutes.
Stir in avocado pesto mixture. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese on top before serving
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix together breadcrumbs, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Dredge the chicken breast in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing the breadcrumbs onto the chicken to adhere.
Place the coated chicken breast on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the coating is golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let the chicken rest for a few minutes.
Preheat an air fryer to 360°F according to manufacturer's instructions.
Stir kale leaves off the stems and chop into small pieces. Place in a large bowl, add olive oil and seasonings. Toss until well coated.
Add a layer of kale leaves to the air fryer basket. Cook for 3 minutes. Shake the basket and pour kale out onto a cutting board or baking sheet to cool and crisp up.
Repeat with remaining kale, working in batches to prevent it from steaming and getting soggy.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
While the air fryer is heating up, prepare the chicken breast. Lay it flat on a clean surface and season it with salt, pepper, paprika, and garlic powder.
Place one slice of ham and one slice of Swiss cheese on top of the chicken breast.
Place one slice of ham and one slice of Swiss cheese on top of the chicken breast.
In a shallow dish, combine the breadcrumbs with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Roll the stuffed chicken breast in the breadcrumb mixture, coating it evenly.
Lightly coat the air fryer basket with cooking spray to prevent sticking.
Place the breaded chicken roll into the air fryer basket, seam side down.
Cook in the preheated air fryer for about 18-20 minutes or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) and the exterior is crispy and golden brown.
Carefully remove the chicken from the air fryer, let it rest for a minute, then remove any toothpicks before slicing.
Preheat your air fryer to 350°F.
Season both sides of the baby back ribs with salt, pepper, and your favorite dry rub seasoning.
Place the seasoned ribs in the air fryer basket, bone side down.
Air fry the ribs at 350°F for 25-30 minutes.
After the initial cooking time, remove the ribs from the air fryer and brush them with barbecue sauce if desired.
Return the ribs to the air fryer and continue to cook for an additional 5-10 minutes, or until the ribs are cooked through and tender.
Once cooked, remove the ribs from the air fryer and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook elbow macaroni in the boiling water for 8 minutes, drain.
Brown bacon pieces in a large, oven-proof skillet over medium heat. Remove with a slotted spoon. Pour off all but 1 tablespoon of bacon grease.
Saute shallots in skillet over medium heat until translucent. Return cooked bacon to skille, stir in broccoli.
Add cooked pasta and cheddar cheese and mix until cheese has melted.
Combine bread crumbs, 1 tbsp ghee, paprika and cheese in a small bowl and sprinkle over the top of the macaroni and cheese.
Bake until topping is lightly browned, 5 to 10 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the chicken breast with salt, pepper, chili powder, cumin, and paprika.
Place the seasoned chicken breast on a baking sheet and roast it in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes. Then, shred it into bite-sized pieces.

While the chicken is roasting, you can prepare the rest of the soup. In a saucepan over medium heat, add the diced onion, diced bell pepper, and minced garlic. Sauté for a few minutes until the vegetables start to soften.
Stir in the canned black beans, canned corn kernels, diced tomatoes, and shredded chicken.
Add the low-sodium chicken broth and enchilada sauce to the saucepan. Stir well to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and let it cook for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Taste the soup and adjust the seasoning with more salt and pepper if needed.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix together low-sodium soy sauce, honey, ketchup, apple cider vinegar, minced garlic, grated fresh ginger, paprika, and black pepper to create the marinade.
Place the chicken breast in a resealable plastic bag and pour the marinade over it. Seal the bag and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to marinate.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Remove the chicken from the marinade, allowing any excess to drip off.
Place the chicken on a baking sheet or in an ovenproof dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through, with an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C), and the exterior is caramelized.
Serve your Huli Huli Chicken hot.
In a bowl, combine thec 2 tbsp Greek yogurt, soaked saffron (saffron soaked in hot water), ground cumin, ground paprika, ground turmeric, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to create a marinade.
Add the chicken cubes to the marinade, ensuring they are well-coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Preheat your grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and grease it with oil.
Thread the marinated chicken cubes onto skewers.
Grill the chicken kabobs for about 10-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are cooked through and have a nice char.
Create a sidesalad with the onion, cucumber and remaining greek yogurt.
cumin: In a small bowl, combine the drained and rinsed chickpeas with 1/2 teaspoon of smoked paprika and 1/2 teaspoon of cumin. Toss to coat the chickpeas in the spices.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the seasoned chickpeas to the skillet.
Sauté the chickpeas for about 4-5 minutes until they become slightly crispy and browned. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Remove from heat and let them cool.
In a salad bowl, place 2 cups of chopped lettuce.
Add the thinly sliced radishes, halved cherry tomatoes, and sliced cucumber to the bowl with the lettuce.
Drizzle the salad with the juice of half a lemon and 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the sautéed chickpeas have cooled slightly, add them to the salad.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Toss the diced sweet potatoes in a little olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread them on a baking sheet and roast for about 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly caramelized.
While the sweet potatoes are roasting, prepare the tamarind dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together tamarind concentrate, maple syrup, olive oil, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Once the sweet potatoes are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool slightly.
In a mixing bowl, combine the roasted sweet potatoes, finely chopped red onion, fresh cilantro,, toasted pecans, cucumber, arugula and carrots.
Drizzle the tamarind dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasonings, if needed.

In a food processor, combine the chickpeas, diced red onion, 1 garlic, fresh parsley, fresh cilantro, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, pepper, baking powder, and 2 tablespoons of flour. Pulse until the mixture forms a coarse paste. If needed, add the extra tablespoon of flour to achieve the right consistency.
Form the mixture into 8 falafel patties.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the falafel patties and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until they are golden brown and crispy.
While the falafel is cooking, prepare the salad. In a large bowl, combine the torn lettuce, thinly sliced red onion, corn kernels, diced tomatoes, and diced cucumber.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the Lemon-Tahini Dressing by combining tahini, fresh lemon juice, water, 1 minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
Once the falafel is cooked, remove them from the skillet or oven.
Divide the salad between two plates or bowls.
Top each salad with four hot homemade falafel patties.
Drizzle the Lemon-Tahini Dressing over the salad.
Serve immediately and enjoy your delicious Homemade Falafel Salad with Lemon-Tahini Dressing.
Preheat your grill to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, ground cumin, paprika, ground coriander, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture evenly over both sides of the chicken breasts.
Grill the chicken breasts for about 6-8 minutes per side or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F. Remove from heat and let them rest for a few minutes before slicing.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the Cauliflower 'Rice' Tabbouleh.
Cut the cauliflower into florets and place them in a food processor. Pulse until the cauliflower resembles rice grains.
In a large bowl, combine the cauliflower 'rice', chopped parsley, diced tomato, and diced bell pepper.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the dressing over the cauliflower 'rice' mixture and toss to combine.
Preheat a skillet over medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, mix the chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Toss the sliced beef with half of the spice mixture.
Add olive oil to the hot skillet and cook the beef until it reaches your desired doneness, about 3-5 minutes for medium-rare. Remove from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced bell pepper and onion along with the remaining spice mixture. Cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the cooked beef back into the skillet with the peppers and onions, stir to combine, and cook for an additional minute.
Warm the tortillas in the skillet or microwave.
Serve the beef fajita mixture on the warm tortillas.
Heat oil in a large pan and add the onion and garlic and cook for 2 minutes. Add the spices and stir well.
Add the chopped tomatoes, drained chickpeas and salt and pepper. Reduce the heat and simmer for 8 minutes.
2 minutes before it has finished cooking, add the spinach and coriander and stir well. Cook for 2 minutes or until the spinach has wilted.
Serve with chopped coriander.
In a medium-sized pot, combine the dried lentils, vegetable broth, diced carrots, diced onion, diced tomatoes, diced potatoes, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Place the pot over medium-high heat and bring the mixture to a boil.
Once it's boiling, reduce the heat to low and cover the pot with a lid.
Let the stew simmer for about 20-25 minutes or until the lentils, potatoes, and vegetables are tender.
Check for seasoning and adjust salt and pepper to your taste.
In a small saucepan, combine the pinto beans, vegetable broth, ground cumin, and chili powder.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beans are heated through and the liquid has reduced slightly.
Using a potato masher or fork, mash the beans to your desired consistency.
Stir in olive oil and salt to taste.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beans are thickened.
Serve the refried beans hot with your favorite toppings.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to the boil. Add the rice, cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a small bowl mix the chili powder, cumin, pepper, and salt. Sprinkle over both sides of the chicken breasts.
In a large nonstick skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Cook the chicken on both sides until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Add beans, corn, and salsa to the skillet. cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is fully cooked.
Remove chicken from pan, cut into slices, serve with beans, corn, and rice.
Heat the cooked chickpeas in a skillet over medium heat.
Season chickpeas with chili powder, ground cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
Warm the corn tortillas in the skillet or microwave.
Divide the seasoned chickpeas between the tortillas.
Top with sliced avocado, shredded lettuce, salsa, and thinly sliced radish.
Garnish with optional toppings as desired.
Serve the chickpea tacos hot.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced onions, diced bell peppers, and minced garlic to the skillet. Cook until vegetables are softened.
Add ground turkey to the skillet and cook until browned.
Season the turkey with chili powder, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Warm the tortillas in the skillet or according to package instructions.
Divide the cooked turkey mixture between the tortillas.
Add your choice of toppings, such as lettuce, tomato, salsa.
Rinse the dry lentils under cold water and set them aside.
In a medium-sized pot, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced onions and minced garlic to the pot. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the onions are translucent.
Add diced carrots to the pot and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Pour in the vegetable broth and add the rinsed lentils, diced tomatoes, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for about 20-25 minutes or until the lentils are tender.
Stir in chopped kale and cook for an additional 5 minutes until the kale is wilted.
Adjust the seasoning if needed.
Ladle the Vegan Lentil Stew into a bowl.
Garnish with fresh parsley.
Heat the olive oil in a small pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until it becomes translucent.
Add the minced garlic, harissa paste, ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook for a minute to release the flavors.
Add the lamb pieces and brown them on all sides.
Stir in the cauliflower florets, sliced carrots, and continue to cook for a few minutes.
Pour in the beef broth and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer, cover the pot, and let it cook for about 20-25 minutes, or until the lamb is tender, and the vegetables are cooked.
Adjust the seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Cut the eggplant in half lengthwise. Score the flesh in a crisscross pattern, then brush it with olive oil and place it on a baking sheet.
Roast the eggplant in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until it becomes tender and slightly browned. Remove it from the oven and let it cool.

While the eggplant is cooling, prepare the grape leaves. If they are from a jar, rinse and drain them. If they are fresh, blanch them in boiling water for a few seconds and then cool them under cold water.
In a bowl, combine the cooked rice, diced tomatoes, diced onions, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Once the roasted eggplant is cool enough to handle, scoop out the flesh, leaving the skin intact.
Chop the roasted eggplant flesh and add it to the rice mixture. Mix everything together.
Place a grape leaf on a flat surface, vein side up, and trim the stem if necessary.
Place a small portion of the eggplant and rice mixture in the center of the grape leaf.
Fold the sides of the grape leaf over the filling, then roll it up to encase the filling.
Place the stuffed grape leaf in an oven-safe dish.
Repeat the process with the remaining grape leaves and filling.
Drizzle olive oil over the stuffed grape leaves.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until the Dolma is heated through.
In a pot or saucepan, combine diced sweet potatoes, onion, bell pepper, and minced garlic.
Add black beans, diced tomatoes (with juices), vegetable broth or water, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for about 15-20 minutes or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Preheat oven to 360°F.
Use a small spoon to remove the flesh from the centre of the zucchini. Finely chop the flesh and reserve in a bowl.
Line a baking tray with baking paper. Oil zucchini shells with olive oil and place cut-side down on the lined tray. Cover with foil. Bake for 15 minutes or until just tender.
Meanwhile, heat a large frying pan over high heat. Add the mince and cook, stirring with a wooden spoon to break up any lumps, for 5 minutes or until the mince changes colour.
Add the reserved zucchini flesh, all the seasoning and tomato. Reduce heat to medium. Simmer for 10 mins or until the sauce thickens slightly. Season.
Turn the zucchini shells cut-side up. Spoon mince mixture into each shell and sprinkle with cheddar. Bake for 10 mins or until cheddar melts and is golden brown. Sprinkle with coriander to serve.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a small baking dish, layer one corn tortilla at the bottom.
Spread a layer of black beans over the tortilla.
Add a layer of diced tomatoes, diced bell peppers, and diced onions.
Sprinkle some of the shredded cheese on top.
Season the layer with chili powder, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Repeat the layering process with the second corn tortilla, remaining black beans, tomatoes, bell peppers, onions, and cheese. Top with any remaining seasonings.
Pour the tomato sauce evenly over the top.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly, and the tortillas are crisp.
Serve your Vegetarian Mexican Lasagna hot.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat and cook onion, garlic, and ginger until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add chickpeas, tomatoes, coconut milk, and sweet potato. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low and simmer until tender, about 15 minutes.
Season with garam masala, cumin, turmeric, chile flakes, and salt. Add spinach right before serving.
In a pot or saucepan, heat a bit of olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced chicken and cook until it's no longer pink, about 5-7 minutes.
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the cooked chicken. Sauté for another 2-3 minutes until the onion softens.
Stir in the white beans, corn, broth, ground cumin, chili powder, dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to blend.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
In a small pot or saucepan, brown the ground beef over medium heat until no longer pink.
Add diced onion and bell pepper to the pot and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in diced tomatoes, cooked kidney beans, and diced habanero pepper.
Pour in beef broth and bring the chili to a simmer.
Season with chili powder, ground cumin, salt, and pepper.
Let the chili simmer for about 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
dijon: Place the potatoes in a pot with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil, and cook for about 10 minutes, or until easily pierced with a fork.
Drain, and transfer to a large bowl to cool.
In a medium bowl, mix the sour cream, mayonnaise, mustard, vinegar, eggs, pickle, celery, green onions, and hot sauce.
Season with dill, garlic powder, onion salt, salt, and pepper. Pour over the potatoes, and gently toss to coat. Chill at least 3 hours in the refrigerator before serving.
serrano: Bring a large pot of water to boil. Cook tomato, serrano chile, and garlic for 5 minutes. Drain and let cool slightly. Peel tomato.
Combine tomato, serrano chile, garlic, salt, and pepper in a blender; blend until smooth.
Heat oil in a small skillet over high heat. Fry tortillas individually until soft but not crispy, about 10 seconds.
Spread 2 tablespoons refried beans on each tortilla. Add an egg and top with tomato sauce. Serve immediately.
Preheat an outdoor grill for high heat and lightly oil the grate.
Mix ground beef, beer, serrano pepper, and butter together in a bowl, form into 4 patties.
Cook the burgers on the preheated grill for 5 minutes. Flip and continue cooking for 3 minutes.
Top each burger with 1 slice of cheese and cook until burgers are no longer pink in the center and the juices run clear, about 2 minutes more.
Assemble the burgers on buns with tomatoes, lettuce and onion.
Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Cook and stir rice and onion in the hot oil until browned, about 5 minutes, season with salt, cumin, and pepper.
Pour water over the rice mixture. Stir tomato sauce and chicken bouillon into the water.
Increase heat to medium-high, place a cover on the saucepan, and bring to a boil.
Add serrano chile pepper and continue cooking at a boil for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium-low until the rice is tender and the water is absorbed, 10 to 15 minutes more.
Put mayocoba beans into a large container with enough cool water to cover by several inches. Let beans soak 8 hours to overnight.
Drain mayocoba beans and rinse thoroughly, put into a large stockpot.
Pour enough water into the pot to cover beans by a few inches, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and place a cover on the pot, and cook at a simmer until beans are soft in the center, about 90 minutes.
While the beans simmer, heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook and stir onion in hot oil until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes and serrano pepper and simmer until the tomatoes have softened slightly, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium-low and continue cooking until the liquid begins to thicken, about 10 minutes more. Remove from heat.
Cook and stir bacon in a large, deep skillet over medium-high heat until very crispy, about 10 minutes.
Remove bacon from skillet with a slotted spoon and drain on a plate lined with paper towel.
Cook ham cubes in the bacon fat in the skillet until browned, about 5 minutes, remove with slotted spoon to the plate with the bacon to drain.
Once the beans have cooked, stir the tomato mixture, bacon, ham, beer, jalapeno peppers, cilantro, and bouillon granules into the beans, season with sea salt.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the beans are completely softened, about 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 450°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Remove the husks from the tomatillos and rinse under cool water to remove the stickiness.
Place the tomatillos, onion and pepper on the baking sheet, drizzle with the olive oil and a generous pinch of salt and toss.
Place the wrapped garlic on the pan.
Roast for 15 minutes or until the tomatillos are soft.
Unwrap the garlic from the foil, peel it, and place in the bowl of a food processor.
Add the roasted vegetables, lime juice and cilantro and pulse. If your salsa is too thick, add 1 to 2 tablespoons water to thin to desired consistency.
Season to taste.
Serve with tortilla chips or with your favorite mexican recipe.
Wrap tofu in paper towels and place heavy books on top for 30 minutes to dry out tofu.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Grease a baking sheet.
Chop pressed tofu into 1-inch cubes and place on the prepared baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10 minutes. Turn tofu and bake an additional 10 minutes until crispy.
Cook ramen in boiling water according to package directions.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat. Cook serrano pepper and ginger until softened, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add vegetable broth, carrots, green onions, broccoli, sesame oil, soy sauce, and baked tofu.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add cooked ramen noodles and simmer 5 more minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat the oil in a dutch oven over high heat.
Add the beef and sear on all sides for about 5 minutes.
Reduce the heat to medium, add the bacon, onions, garlic, serrano pepper and saute for another 5 minutes.
Add the spices, stir for a minute, then add the red wine to deglaze the pan.
Reduce the heat to low then add the chicken stock, tomato sauce, apple cider vinegar and prunes.
Cover the pot with a lid and cook for 30 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf, put lid back on and simmer for another 30 minutes, or until the beef is tender.
Garnish with cilantro or parsley if desired.
Enjoy with hot rice.
apples: Preheat the oven to 200°F.
Line two baking sheets with parchment paper. Using an apple corer, core the apples, then slice them into thin slices using a mandolin. Place the slices in a mixing bowl with water and lemon juice.
Drain the water, dry the apples and lay them on the baking sheet. Sprinkle with half of the ground cinnamon. Place in the oven, and bake for 2 hours or until apples are dry and crisp.
About half way through flip the apples over and sprinkle with more cinnamon. Remove from the oven, allow to cool completely, and store in an airtight container.
Combine strawberries, apples, yogurt, pineapple juice, orange juice, and milk in a blender; blend until smooth.
In a medium serving bowl combine kale, apple, pecans, bacon, and cranberries.
In a small food processor combine shallot, vinegar, juice, mustard, salt, pepper and oil for the dressing. Pulse until creamy and emulsified.
Toss salad in dressing and serve immediately.
Put all the fruit into a deep pot and cover with 6 cups of water. Bring to boil on high.
Add honey, cloves, and cinnamon. Lower to medium and cook an additional 30 minutes, or until the fruit is soft.
Serve compote hot, or keep in fridge until it cools down.
Apple Filling: Slice 4-5 Granny Smith Apples, set aside. We used this apple slicer.
Melt one stick of butter in pan on stovetop, then add in the apples. Add sweetener, vanilla, cinnamon, and stir.
Allow apples to soften and mixture to thicken, about 5-7 minutes. Sweetener and butter will thicken to a deeper brown. Pour mixture into your baking dish, set aside.
Almond Flour Crisp: Preheat oven to 350°F. Melt one stick of ghee, set aside.
In a separate bowl combine almond flour, sweetener, xanthan gum, and salt. Add melted butter to the dry mixture.
This should form small moist clumps.
Top the apples with crisp topping, spreading it out to make sure surface of apples is completely covered with the crisp mixture.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until the topping is slightly browned and slightly bubbling.
Heat coconut oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add sliced apples to the skillet.
Sprinkle ground cinnamon, vanilla extract, and a dash of salt over the apples.
Saute the apples until they are softened and lightly browned.
Transfer the sauteed apples to a plate.
Enjoy this healthy and flavorful sauteed apple dish!
In a pot over medium heat, add olive oil.
Sauté diced onion and minced garlic for about 2-3 minutes until they soften.
Add diced butternut squash and apple to the pot and cook for about 5 minutes until they start to soften.
Pour in vegetable broth and add ground cinnamon and a pinch of nutmeg.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Let the soup simmer for about 15-20 minutes until the squash and apples are tender.
Once the squash and apples are cooked, pour in milk.
Using an immersion blender or carefully transferring to a blender, blend the soup until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a saucepan, combine apples, water, sugar, and cinnamon. Cover, and cook over medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes.
Allow to cool, then mash with a fork or potato masher.
Preheat the air fryer to 180°F.
Thinly slice apple with a mandolin. Place as many apple slices in the air fryer basket as you can, making sure they do not touch.
Cook in the air fryer for 12 minutes, working in batches if necessary.
Pull the basket out, turn apple slices over, and cook until lightly browned on the other side, 8 to 12 minutes more. Immediately sprinkle with chile-lime seasoning.
In a non-stick pan over medium heat, sauté the apple slices until softened.
Add oats, almond butter, chopped nuts, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and a dash of salt to the pan.
Stir well and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the oats are heated through.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and enjoy.
Bring a small pot of water to the boil. Add the eggs, boil for 10 minutes, then transfer them to ice cold water to quickly cool off. Peel, slice and set aside.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium-low heat. Add a glug of olive oil, then the bread cubes. Cook until golden brown, about 3-5 minutes, flipping occasionally.
Add the mayonnaise, half the olive oil, Italian seasoning and lemon juice to a small mixing bowl. Season using salt and pepper and mix well to combine.
Heat the remaining olive oil over medium heat in a non-stick pan. Season the scallops using salt and pepper and cook 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they're no more translucent.
Add the greens to a large mixing bowl, add the dressing, eggs and apples, mix well, then transfer to a serving bowl. Add the scallops and croquettes and enjoy.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 400˚F.
Place the oats in a food processor or blender and blend until a coarse flour is formed.
To a large mixing bowl add the blended oats, baking powder, proteins, cinnamon, salt. Mix and set aside.
Roughly chop the apples, add to the food processor, and pulse blend until the apples are finely minced.
To a separate mixing bowl add the bananas and mash them with a fork or potato masher but leave a few chunks.
Then add the apples, erythritol, lemon juice, vanilla, and water. Stir to combine.
Combine the wet mixture with the dry mixture, stir until well combined, and pour equal amounts of the batter into each section of the muffin pan.
Bake for 25 minutes.
Mix oats, water, milk, honey, vanilla extract, and salt together in a rice cooker.
Cook oats in rice cooker until desired consistency is reached, 10 to 15 minutes. Top with blueberries and apples.
Mix all bananas, apples, strawberries, oranges, kiwis and cherries in a bowl, divide 4 times and pour over lemon juice.
Add the sliced apples to a large mixing bowl, add honey and lemon juice, toss to coat.
Add the celery, raisins, and nuts. Season to taste with salt. Chill for 1 hour or overnight. Serve on lettuce.
Mix sour cream, lemon juice, honey, and poppy seeds together in a medium bowl.
Add chicken, apples, pecans, celery, green onions, parsley, toss to combine. Season with salt and pepper.
Place lettuce on a bun and top with salad.
Place diced potatoes and beets in a medium pot, cover with water, cover with a lid and boil until tender. Drain and add cooked potatoes to a mixing bowl. Cool slightly.
Add diced beets, apples, onion, pickle, and ¼ cup of water. Mash slightly with a potato masher. Consistency should be smooth with chunks.
Whip vegan cream cheese and soymilk with an electric mixer until smooth and fluffy.
Fold vegan whipped cream cheese mixture into salad and stir just until well combined. Season as desired with salt and black pepper. Serve immediately.
Combine bananas, apples, yogurt, protein powder, water and half of the cinnamon in a high-powdered blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately, topped with the remaining cinnamon.
Wash and prepare all the ingredients.
Using a juicer, process lemons, carrots, apples, and beets.
Collect the juice in a glass.
Stir well to combine the flavors.
Enjoy this refreshing and healthy zinger juice for a nutrient-packed breakfast!
applesauce: Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a bowl, combine the rolled oats, baking powder, and ground cinnamon.
Add the lemon juice, lemon zest, unsweetened applesauce, and honey or maple syrup to the dry ingredients. Mix until well combined.
Gently fold in the fresh blueberries into the oat mixture.
Lightly grease a muffin tin or ramekin with oil.
Spoon the oatmeal mixture into the muffin tin or ramekin, filling it to the top.
Place the tin or ramekin in the preheated oven and bake for about 20-25 minutes or until the oatmeal cakes are firm and slightly golden on top.
Once baked, remove the oatmeal cakes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Carefully remove the oatmeal cakes from the muffin tin or ramekin.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
Grease a small oven-safe ramekin or baking dish with ghee or line it with parchment paper.
In a mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, ground ginger, ground cinnamon, and a pinch of salt.
In a separate microwave-safe bowl, melt the ghee and honey (or maple syrup) together in the microwave for a few seconds until smooth.
Stir in the milk and unsweetened applesauce into the ghee and honey mixture.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until you have a smooth batter.
Transfer the batter to the prepared ramekin, spreading it evenly.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until the Yorkshire Parkin is golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove the Parkin from the oven and let it cool in the ramekin for a few minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a muffin tin with paper liners.
In a large bowl, combine oats, applesauce, mashed banana, almond milk, and vanilla extract. Mix well.
Add ground cinnamon, baking powder, baking soda, and salt to the mixture. Stir until combined.
Fold in the chopped apple and chopped nuts into the batter.
Spoon the batter into the prepared muffin tin, filling each cup about 2/3 full.
Bake in the preheated oven for 18-20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center of a muffin comes out clean.
Bake in the preheated oven for 18-20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center of a muffin comes out clean.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging. Cook broccoli in a different pot for 5 minutes.
Oil a large nonstick skillet with olive oil and place over medium-high heat. Cook and stir chicken in the hot skillet until chicken is no longer pink.
Pour BBQ sauce and applesauce over chicken, stir to coat. Reduce heat to medium and cook until heated through, about 5 minutes. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a bowl, combine whole wheat flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt.
Add applesauce, honey and almond milk to the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined.
Gently fold in the diced apple.
On a floured surface, shape the dough into a round disc, about 1 inch thick.
Cut the disc into 4 equal wedges.
Transfer the wedges to the prepared baking sheet.
Bake for 15-18 minutes or until scones are golden brown.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
Roll out the pastry dough and cut it into a square.
Place the peeled and cored apple in the center of the dough square.
Fill the apple core with applesauce, sprinkle ground cinnamon, and add chopped nuts.
Fold the pastry dough over the apple, sealing it to form a dumpling.
Place the dumpling in a baking dish.
If desired, drizzle honey over the top for sweetness.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes or until the pastry is golden brown.
Allow the apple dumpling to cool slightly before serving.
In a mixing bowl, combine the applesauce, almond milk,  oil, and vanilla extract. Mix until well combined.
Add the flour, baking powder, and a pinch of salt to the wet ingredients. Stir until just combined. Do not overmix; it's okay if the batter is slightly lumpy.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and lightly coat with cooking spray or olive oil.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface of the pancakes and the edges look set, about 2-3 minutes.
Flip the pancakes and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until golden brown on both sides.
Repeat with the remaining batter.
In a small bowl, mix together the diced apple, ground cinnamon, ground nutmeg, lemon juice, and unsweetened applesauce until the apples are well coated.
Preheat your air fryer to 350°F.
Cut each sheet of phyllo dough into four equal squares.
Place one square of phyllo dough on a flat surface and spoon a quarter of the apple mixture into the center.
Fold the edges of the phyllo dough over the apple mixture to form a small pie. Repeat with the remaining phyllo dough and apple mixture to make four mini pies.
Lightly spray each mini pie with cooking spray.
Place the mini pies in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
Air fry at 350°F (175°C) for 8-10 minutes, or until the phyllo dough is golden brown and crispy.
Once cooked, remove the mini pies from the air fryer and let them cool slightly before serving.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine grated zucchini, egg, unsweetened applesauce, olive oil, and vanilla extract.
In a separate bowl, whisk together almond flour, baking powder, ground cinnamon, and a pinch of salt.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, stirring until well combined.
Pour the batter into a greased mini loaf pan or ramekin.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Allow the zucchini bread to cool slightly before serving.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a muffin tin with a paper liner.
In a bowl, mix whole wheat flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt.
In a separate bowl, combine applesauce, honey, almond milk, egg, and vanilla extract.
Add wet ingredients to dry ingredients, stirring until just combined.
Gently fold in the diced apple.
Spoon batter into the muffin tin.
Bake for 18-20 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.
Allow muffin to cool before enjoying this delicious, healthy treat!
In a small bowl, toss the diced apple with lemon juice, ground cinnamon, and ground nutmeg until evenly coated.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
Roll out the puff pastry sheet and cut it into two equal squares.
Place half of the seasoned diced apples in the center of each puff pastry square.
Spoon 1/2 tablespoon of unsweetened applesauce over the apples in each pastry square.
Fold the pastry squares over the apples to form rectangles, and press the edges to seal.
Lightly spray both sides of each pastry with cooking spray.
Place the pastries in the air fryer basket, leaving space between them.
Air fry at 375°F for 10-12 minutes, or until the pastries are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the pastries from the air fryer and let them cool slightly before serving.
In a bowl or jar, combine the rolled oats, unsweetened applesauce, almond milk, honey, ground cinnamon, ground nutmeg, and vanilla extract.
Mix all the ingredients together until well combined.
Cover the bowl or jar with a lid or plastic wrap and refrigerate it overnight or for at least 4-6 hours to allow the oats to soften and the flavors to meld.
Once ready to eat, give the oats a stir. If it's too thick, you can add a splash of milk to reach your desired consistency.
Top the Apple Pie-Inspired Overnight Oats with chopped apple.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine 1/2 cup rolled oats, 1/4 cup unsweetened applesauce, 2 tablespoons honey or maple syrup, 1/4 teaspoon vanilla extract, and 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon. Mix well to form a dough.
Gently fold in 1/4 cup fresh raspberries and 1/4 cup fresh blueberries into the dough. Be careful not to crush the berries.
Form the dough into a single cookie on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until the cookie is lightly golden.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Preheat a waffle iron according to manufacturer's instructions, oil the inside with coconut oil.
Whisk almond milk, applesauce, egg, chia seeds, and vanilla extract together in a bowl,; let sit until chia seeds start to thicken the mixture, about 2 minutes.
Whisk flour, oats, flax seed meal, baking powder into almond milk mixture until batter is smooth.
Scoop 1/2 cup batter into the preheated waffle iron and cook until crisp and golden, about 5 minutes per waffle. Repeat with remaining batter. Garnish with strawberries.
Lightly grease a pie pan.
Beat applesauce in a bowl. Add oats, milk, baking powder, oil, and salt; mix well. Fold fruit into oat mixture.
Pour oat mixture into the prepared pie pan; top with cinnamon. Refrigerate for 8 hours to overnight.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Bake in the preheated oven until oatmeal is firm, 35 to 50 minutes.
With the rack in the middle position, preheat the oven to 350°F. Oil a muffin tin or line it with paper liners.
In a bowl, combine both flours with the baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon and salt.
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, applesauce and oil. Using a wooden spoon, stir in the zucchini, pumpkin seeds, raisins and dry ingredients.
Divide the batter among the muffin cups. Bake for 25 minutes. Let cool on a wire rack before serving.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Spray a 12-cup muffin tin with oil, or line it with cupcake liners.
Mix the flour, baking powder, baking soda, nutmeg, walnuts and salt and whisk well together.
In a larger bowl, place all remaining ingredients and use a whisk to beat the mixture for 2 minutes or until well-integrated.
Add half the flour mixture, mix well, using a rubber spatula to scrape the bottom and sides of the bowl, and then add the remaining half. Mix well.
Using an ice cream scoop or a tablespoon, divide the batter equally into the cups. Bake in the preheated oven 25 minutes.
Let the muffins cool in the tin on a rack for five minutes, then unmold them and let them continue cooling on the rack.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
Boil or steam the diced sweet potato until tender. Drain any excess water.
In a bowl, mash the sweet potatoes with a fork or potato masher.
Add applesauce, olive oil, chopped pecans, ground cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt. Mix well.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes or until the top is golden and the casserole is heated through.
Allow the Sweet Potato Casserole to cool slightly before serving.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Steam or boil the sweet potato cubes until they are tender but still firm.
Mash the cooked sweet potato in a mixing bowl.
Add unsweetened applesauce, chopped pecans or walnuts, cinnamon, nutmeg, and a pinch of salt to the mashed sweet potato.
Mix all the ingredients together until well combined.
Transfer the mixture into a small baking dish or oven-safe dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until the top is slightly golden and the casserole is heated through.
Turn on a multi-functional pressure cooker and select Saute function. Heat oil and add onion.
Saute until translucent, about 2 minutes, be careful not to brown the onion. Move onion to the side of the pot.
Add a layer of butternut squash to the bottom of the pot. Brown slightly, about 4 minutes.
Add remaining butternut squash, curry powder, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Mix and continue cooking until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour broth and applesauce into the pot with the butternut squash mixture. Close and lock the lid, set knob to sealing.
Select high pressure and set timer for 15 minutes. Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build.
Release pressure using the natural-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, 10 to 40 minutes. Unlock and remove lid.
Mix pumpkin puree and ghee into the pot with the soup. Blend with a stick blender. Add salt. Mix thoroughly.
horseradish: Combine first 4 ingredients in processor.
Blend until just smooth, occasionally scraping down sides of work bowl.
Season spread with salt and pepper.
Transfer to small bowl.
Serve with baguette rounds.
Mix tuna, mayonnaise, yellow mustard, horseradish, lemon juice, garlic powder, and salt together in a bowl.
Bring a large pot of water to boil. Cook the shrimp until just opaque throughout, about 3 minutes.
Drain and transfer to a bowl of ice water to cool. Roughly chop the shrimp.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the yogurt, lemon juice, horseradish and black pepper.
Add the shrimp, scallions, celery, cucumber, and dill, and toss to combine. Spoon into the lettuce leaves and serve.
Place the eggs in a small saucepan and cover them with water.
Bring the water to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the eggs from the saucepan and place them in a bowl of ice water to cool.
Once cooled, peel the eggs and slice them in half lengthwise. Remove the yolks and place them in a small bowl.
Mash the egg yolks with a fork until they are smooth.
Add the mayonnaise, prepared horseradish, Dijon mustard, salt, and black pepper to the mashed yolks. Stir until well combined.
Spoon or pipe the yolk mixture into the egg white halves.
Sprinkle the deviled eggs with paprika.
Serve immediately or refrigerate until ready to serve.

Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add shallot and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes. Add broth, asparagus, preotein powder, potato, horseradish and salt and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer until the vegetables are tender, about 15 minutes.
Puree with a blender and serve hot.
Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil, add potatoes and cook for 12 to 15 minutes until tender.
Heat grill to medium-high. In large bowl, whisk together oil, vinegar, horseradish, celery seeds, and ¼ teaspoon salt. Toss with tomatoes, celery, and onion.
Season pork chops with ½  teaspoon each salt and pepper and grill until golden brown and just cooked through, 5 to 7 minutes per side.
Fold parsley into tomatoes and serve over pork, potatoes and greens.
In a medium bowl, whisk together lemon zest, salt, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, thyme, and cayenne.
Pat chicken wings dry and place in a large bowl.
Add spice mixture and toss to coat.
Heat grill or grill pan to medium heat.
Oil grill grates with vegetable oil.
Add wings and cook, stirring occasionally, until skin is crisp and meat is cooked through 15 to 20 minutes.
In a medium bowl, whisk together mayo, lemon juice, mustard, horseradish, chives, and hot sauce.
Serve wings hot with dipping sauce and rice, and cucumbers on the side.
Heat olive oil over medium-heat. Add the potatoes, celery, onions, garlic and leek. Cook for 2 minutes. Throw the veggies into the pot and cover them with water.
Add bay leaf and cook for 15 minutes. Remove the leaf and blend the soup until it is smooth. Mix in the cream and horseradish. Season with salt and pepper.
Serve immediately, along with boiled eggs.
Combine half of the butter, green onion, horseradish and dill weed until blended. Cover and refrigerate.
Preheat barbecue on medium heat for 10 minutes. Place each cod fillet on a piece of heavy-duty foil. Trim each piece of foil so it is slightly larger than fillet.
Brush melted butter over cod, sprinkle with salt and pepper. With lid down, cook cod until fish flakes easily with a fork, about 10 minutes per inch of thickness.
Serve topped with horseradish dill butter.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil, and brush the foil with ghee.
In a medium bowl, mix together the vinegar, water, mayonnaise, horseradish, salt and pepper until well blended.
Coat each piece of chicken heavily with the sauce, and place onto the prepared baking sheet.
Bake, uncovered, for about 45 minutes in the preheated oven. Baste occasionally with remaining sauce.
Chicken is done when the juices run clear, and the meat is no longer pink. Top with sesame seeds and parsley.
Heat the oven to 375 degrees F (Fan).
Place the potatoes (do not peel them) and celeriac in a large pot of cold water with a little salt and bring to the boil for.
Simmer until they’re just cooked through. (15-20 minutes).
Drain and cool, then remove the skins and cut the potatoes and celeriac into 3/16 in-thick slices.
Heat a pan over a medium heat and add 1 tbsp of butter and the sunflower oil.
Fry the onions with a good pinch of salt for 2 minutes until really soft and lightly golden, remove from the pan to cool.
Finely slice the fresh salmon.
Remove a few stems of dill from 1 bunch to garnish then chop the remainder.
Grease the dish you are going with a little butter.
Crack the eggs into a jug and whisk lightly.
Add the milk and cream and whisk again, set to one side.
Place a layer of potato and celeriac in the dish, sprinkle with half of the dill and half of the onions.
Season lightly with salt and white pepper.
Now add a layer of the fresh and smoked salmon.
Proceed with the next layer of potato and celeriac, making sure you leave the best slices of potato for the top layer, add the remaining onion and dill, season.
Now add the remaining salmon.
Arrange the potato slices on the top.
Pour over the egg and milk mixture.
Sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
Melt the remaining butter and pour over the breadcrumbs.
Finally grind a little white pepper on the top.
Place in the oven for 45-50 minutes, or until golden brown and the egg mixture has set.
Serve with a sprinkling of fresh grated horseradish, lemon wedges.
berries: Preheat your blender or food processor to 0°F.
Place the frozen mixed berries in the preheated blender or food processor.
Add the Greek yogurt to the blender.
If you prefer a sweeter taste, you can add a tablespoon of honey to the mixture. You can also add a touch of vanilla extract for extra flavor.
Blend the ingredients until you have a smooth and creamy mixture. If the mixture is too thick, you can add a splash of water or milk to help with blending.
Once blended, transfer the frozen berry yogurt to a bowl or cup.
In a small bowl, combine 2 tbsp ground flax or chia with 6 tbsp water.
Stir together and set aside to thicken. In a large bowl, whisk together the quinoa flour, baking powder, cinnamon and salt.
In a large measuring cup or medium size bowl, combine almond milk, and vanilla extract.
Pour the liquid mixture into the large bowl with flour. Add the walnuts, carrots, and melted coconut oil. Stir together until a smooth batter forms.
Allow batter to rest as you preheat the waffle iron. Lightly grease the iron then fill with batter.
Cook until your iron says the waffles are ready, or possibly a little longer so that the waffles are golden with crisp edges.
Place cooked waffles on a cooling iron as you cook the rest of the batch.
Reheat in the toaster or oven for extra crispy edges. Serve warm with mixed frozen berries.
Place frozen berries, banana, protein powder, strawberries, coconut, and chia seeds in a blender, pulse briefly to combine. Pour in orange juice, blend until smooth.
Place frozen fruit, coconut milk and spinach into the blender.
Blend until smooth.
Pour into your dish of choice.
Decorate with your favourite smoothie bowl toppings!
Enjoy eating this gorgeous kiwi smoothie bowl.
Blend ingredients in high powdered blender until creamy and smooth. Adding more orange juice if needed for consistency. Pour into serving glasses and enjoy.
