In a small saucepan, combine the cooked rice and milk.
Place the saucepan over medium heat and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the rice simmer in the milk, stirring occasionally, for about 5-7 minutes or until it thickens to your desired porridge consistency
Stir in the ghee (or vegetable oil) and a pinch of salt.
Remove the saucepan from heat. Pour the rice porridge into a serving bowl.
Top with berries.
In a bowl or jar, combine the rolled oats and almond milk.
Stir well until the oats are fully coated with the milk.
Add the mixed berries on top of the oat mixture.
Cover the bowl or jar and refrigerate it overnight, allowing the oats to soak and soften.
In the morning, take out your muesli from the refrigerator.
Give it a good stir to mix the softened oats and the berries.
Whisk together the wheat flour, egg whites, milk, salt, and oil in a large bowl until smooth.
Lightly coat a skillet with cooking spray and place over medium heat. Pour 1/4 cup of the batter into the skillet.
Tilt the pan in a circular motion to allow the batter to spread to the edges. Cook until the bottom is light brown, about 2 minutes.
Flip the crepe and place 2 tablespoons of the mixed berries in the center of the crepe; cook another 2 minutes.
Fold the crepe in half and remove with a spatula to a serving plate.
In a blender, combine mixed berries, plain kefir, banana.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour the berry-kefir smoothie into a glass.
Serve your refreshing berry-kefir smoothie immediately.
Preheat a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the filet steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Add a little olive oil to the skillet.
Place the filet steak in the skillet and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until it reaches your desired level of doneness.
While the steak is cooking, mash the mixed berries with a fork or blend them slightly to create a sauce-like consistency.
Once the steak is cooked to your liking, remove it from the skillet and let it rest for a few minutes.
In the same skillet, add the mashed berries and sliced peach. Cook for about 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce is heated through and the peach are softened.
Serve the filet steak with the warm berry and peach sauce on top.
Add the berries, one banana, Greek yogurt, almond milk, and almond butter to a blender and blend until smooth.
Top with bluberries, sliced banana, chia seeds and sliced almonds.
Combine coconut milk, rolled oats and maple syrup and bring to a boil, reduce heat and simmer until fully cooked.
When it is ready add the oatmeal in a serving bowl.
Top with berries, shredded coconut and chia.
Serve immediately.
Enjoy!
Add spinach, yogurt, frozen banana slices, protein powder, mixed berries, and milk to a blender.
Cover and process on medium speed for about 30 seconds, using the tamper as needed. Increase to high speed for 15 seconds until thick and smooth.
Gather your ingredients: mixed berries, banana, Greek yogurt, and almond milk.
Add the mixed berries to the blender.
Peel the banana and break it into chunks. Add the banana pieces to the blender.
Measure out the Greek yogurt and pour it into the blender with the berries and banana.
Pour in the almond milk.
Blend all the ingredients together until smooth and well combined.
Pour your refreshing mixed-berry breakfast smoothie into a glass.
Add ice cubes, pineapple, mango, strawberries, banana slices, yogurt, pineapple juice, orange juice, and coconut milk to a blender.
Cover and process on medium speed for about 30 seconds.
Increase to high speed for 15 to 30 seconds until thick and smooth.
Adjust with more coconut milk, pineapple, or orange juice as needed to reach the desired consistency. Serve.
Preheat your waffle iron / machine.
Mix the flour, baking powder, and sugar in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, mix the plant milk, vegan yoghurt and vanilla extract.
When the waffle iron is hot, tip the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until smooth.
Pour about a quarter of the mixture into the waffle machine, then cook until crispy and golden.
Repeat until all the mixture has been used up, and serve immediately drizzled with maple syrup and fruit.
In a bowl, mix together all the ingredients (leave some berries to garnish) until you get a smooth batter.
Preheat and lightly grease a frying pan.
Take a ladle half full of batter and pour it in the middle of the pan, spreading very lightly.
Cook for 1 minute then add on a lid to make the pancakes fluffy.
Cook for another minute then flip and leave for about 15 seconds.
Repeat until you've used all the batter.
Top with berries to serve.
artichoke hearts: To a large mixing bowl, add peppers, artichoke hearts and torn basil. Season with salt and pepper to taste, drizzle some olive oil on top and sprinkle some pine nuts before serving.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté garlic in hot oil until garlic starts to brown.

Add artichoke hearts and cook, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes. Stir in bread crumbs and 1/2 Parmesan cheese; cook and stir until heated through. Transfer 1/2 of the artichoke mixture to pie crust.
Pour beaten eggs over artichoke mixture in crust and sprinkle with remaining Parmesan cheese. Spoon remaining artichoke mixture on top and sprinkle with mozzarella cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven until crust begins to brown, about 45 minutes.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, 8 to 10 minutes.
While the pasta is cooking, heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add artichoke hearts, garlic, and basil; cook and stir until heated through, about 5 minutes.
Drain pasta. Add to the skillet and toss with artichoke mixture.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the boneless, skinless chicken breast with salt and black pepper to taste.
Heat the olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken breast to the skillet and cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side, until it's browned.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the chopped artichoke hearts and capers. Sauté for about 1-2 minutes to heat them through.
Pour in the chicken broth and lemon juice, scraping the bottom of the skillet to deglaze and pick up any flavorful bits.
Return the cooked chicken breast to the skillet.
Cover the skillet with a lid or aluminum foil.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for about 15-20 minutes.
Remove the Chicken with Artichokes and Capers from the oven.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Rinse the herbs and shake dry. Reserve some thyme sprigs and then pluck the leaves from the remaining sprigs.
Rinse the peppers, cut in half, remove the seeds and pith and cut into strips.
Peel the onions and garlic. Quarter the onion and mince the garlic.
Rinse the lemon in hot water, wipe dry and quarter.  Drain the artichoke hearts and cut in half.
Rinse the fish, pat dry and score the skin in a diamond pattern.
Season inside and outs with salt and pepper, drizzle with lemon juice and brush with a little olive oil. Stuff with the sage.
Arrange the vegetables, ½ the lemon wedges, the saffron and the thyme in a large baking dish.
Add the fish, drizzle with the remaining olive oil and white wine, season with salt and pepper and bake in the oven for about 25 minutes.
Remove then arrange the remaining lemon wedges over top.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a small oven-safe dish, combine the chopped artichoke hearts, Greek yogurt, grated Parmesan cheese, olive oil, garlic powder, dried basil, salt, and pepper.
Mix all the ingredients until well combined.
Place the dish in the preheated oven and bake for about 15-20 minutes, or until the dip is hot and bubbly, and the top is lightly browned.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a minute.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Drizzle olive oil over the cut sides of the spaghetti squash and season with salt and pepper.
Place the squash halves, cut side down, on a baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven for 30-40 minutes or until the squash is tender and easily pierced with a fork.
While the squash is baking, heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Add chopped spinach, artichoke hearts, and mushrooms to the skillet. Cook until the vegetables are tender.
Once the squash is done, use a fork to scrape the flesh into spaghetti-like strands.
Mix the vegetable mixture with the spaghetti squash strands.
Fill the hollowed squash shells with the mixture.
Top each filled squash half with a mixture of Parmesan and mozzarella cheese.
Broil for 2-3 minutes or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Serve your cheesy spinach & artichoke, mushroom stuffed spaghetti squash hot.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Lay out the filo pastry sheets and cut each sheet into 4 squares.
In a mixing bowl, combine the cooked and drained spinach, chopped artichoke hearts, grated Parmesan cheese, Greek yogurt, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Place a spoonful of the spinach and artichoke mixture in the center of each filo square.
Fold the corners of each filo square over the filling to create a cup shape.
Place the filled filo cups on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until the filo pastry is golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
In a mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour and sparkling water to create a batter. Stir until you have a smooth consistency.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and add the olive oil.
Dip each artichoke heart into the batter, ensuring it's well-coated.
Carefully add the battered artichoke hearts to the hot skillet.
Fry the artichoke hearts for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until they become golden brown and crispy.
Season the fried artichoke hearts with salt and pepper while they are still hot.
Transfer the batter-fried artichoke hearts to a plate.
In a small bowl, mix together the plain Greek yogurt, fresh dill, fresh lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Serve the Batter-Fried Artichoke Hearts with the Yogurt Dill Dip on the side for dipping and a wedged lemon.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions in boiling salted water until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet over medium heat, add a splash of olive oil.
Add the chopped red pepper to the skillet and sauté for 2-3 minutes until slightly tender.
Add the cooked pasta to the skillet along with the drained tuna, sliced olives, and chopped artichoke hearts.
Stir everything together and let it cook for an additional 2-3 minutes to heat the ingredients through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat the broiler.
In a medium bowl, mix marinated artichoke hearts, Romano cheese, red onion and mayonnaise.
Top French baguette slices with equal amounts of the artichoke heart mixture. Arrange slices in a single layer on a large baking sheet.
Broil in the preheated oven 2 minutes, or until toppings are bubbly and lightly browned.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Combine vegan cream cheese, nutritional yeast and coconut yogurt in a bowl and stir to combine.
Mix in spinach, vegan Parmesan, garlic, and Italian seasoning. Fold in artichoke hearts and season with salt and pepper.
Grease the bottom and sides of an 8x8-inch oven-safe baking dish with olive oil. Pour in spinach mixture and spread out evenly. Top with vegan mozzarella.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is beginning to brown, about 25 minutes. Remove from oven and serve warm with crackers.
Combine artichokes with feta cheese, green onions, lemon juice, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Spoon into pita halves. Divide chicken slices and add to pita pockets.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
On a large, rimmed baking sheet, place bell peppers cut side-up and drizzle with olive oil, then season with salt and pepper.
In a large bowl, add tuna, artichoke hearts, spinach, cream cheese, half mozzarella, Parmesan, sour cream, flaxseed oil and garlic.
Season with more salt and pepper and mix until well combined.
Divide chicken mixture between pepper halves, top with remaining mozzarella, and bake until cheese is melty and peppers are tender, about 25 minutes.
Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil.
Cook the fusilli for 3-5 min or until it's tender.
Fire up the grill and get in nice and hot. While the grill is heating, season the chicken breasts well with salt and pepper.
Grill the chicken for 3-5 minutes on each side or until it's cooked through. Set aside to cool a bit before slicing.
Meanwhile, add the chickpeas, lemon juice, sesame paste and some salt and pepper to a food processor. Process until the hummus is smooth, check for seasoning.
Once the pasta is cooked, drain it, then divide it between 4 serving plates. Top with the grilled chicken, add hummus and artichoke hearts and a healthy drizzle of olive oil. Enjoy.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Remove the stems from the Portobello mushrooms and gently scoop out the gills to create a hollow space.
In a bowl, mix together chopped spinach, chopped artichoke hearts, grated Parmesan cheese, plain Greek yogurt, minced garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Stuff the prepared spinach and artichoke mixture into the Portobello mushroom caps, dividing it evenly between the two mushrooms.
Place the stuffed mushrooms on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for approximately 18-20 minutes or until the mushrooms are tender and the filling is slightly golden on top.
Once done, remove the stuffed Portobello mushrooms from the oven.
Season both sides of chicken breasts with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Place chicken in skillet; cook, turning once to brown each side. Remove chicken from pan, and set aside.
Pour tomatoes into pan; cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, and incorporating any brown bits from bottom of pan.
Stir in pesto and artichokes, and return chicken to pan. Cover, and reduce heat to medium. Simmer for 5 to 10 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instrucions on the packaging.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet over medium heat, add the chopped artichoke hearts, chopped spinach, and minced garlic.
Stir in the low-fat cream cheese, grated Parmesan cheese, and chicken or vegetable broth.
Cook and stir until the cheese is melted and the mixture is heated through.
Add the cooked pasta to the skillet and toss to coat the pasta evenly with the spinach and artichoke mixture.
Season with salt, pepper, according to your taste.
Bring a large pot of salted water to boil; add pasta and boil until al dente according to packaging instructions, 8 to 10 minutes. Drain well and rinse with cold water.
In a large mixing bowl, combine pasta, artichoke hearts, tomatoes, onion, corn, basil, oregano, garlic, salt and pepper; toss well.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours. Before serving, season the pasta dish with salt and pepper to taste.
To assemble, thread 1 artichoke, 1 piece of salmon, and repeat on each toothpick.
whole grain bread: Rinse onions and green chilies under running water. Slice the onions thin. Fine chop green chilies and garlic.
Pluck and rinse coriander leaves in a bowl of water, a few times. Drain and fine chop. Set aside.
Remove the long stems if any  from the mushrooms.
Rinse them under running water and slice them slightly thick, not too thin. Break the eggs to a bowl and add salt and pepper.
Beat until frothy and keep aside. Heat 1 tsp oil in a 6 inch pan.
Add cumin, when they sputter, add garlic & green chili. Sauté them for 30 to 60 seconds. Then add onions, mushrooms.
Sauté them on a slightly high heat until cooked and roasted. Ads coriander leaves. Mix and remove to a small plate.
To the same pan, add the rest of the oil and reduce the flame to medium. Pour the beaten eggs.
Cook until the eggs are almost set, yet slightly runny, spread the roasted onions and mushrooms.
Flip and cook on the other side until eggs are cooked well. Remove to a serving plate.
Serve mushroom omelette hot with toasted bread. Serve and enjoy with bread,
Bring a small pot of water up to a steady simmer. Crack the eggs into two separate cups.
Add a splash of white wine vinegar to the pot and stir the water to create a whirlpool in the centre, then slide in one of the eggs.
Poach the egg in the simmering water for 3 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and drain on a plate lined with kitchen paper. Repeat with remaining eggs.
Place the avocado and smoked salmon on the bread and add a squeeze of lemon juice. Top with the poached eggs and a pinch of salt and pepper and serve.
Add egg yolks, lemon juice, salt, and cayenne to a blender or food processor and pulse until combined.
Slowly drizzle in the butter and continue to run the blender until the sauce thickens.
Bring a large pot with a few inches of water to a slight simmer. Add a dash of vinegar to the pot.
Let eggs poach in not-quite-boiling water for 3 minutes, or until the whites of the eggs are set and the centers are still soft.
Toast the slices of bread. Top bread with sliced avocado, salad mix, poached egg. Spoon hollandaise sauce over and serve.
Heat a grill pan over medium-high heat or heat a grill to medium heat.
Brush the halloumi slices with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, coating both sides of the cheese.
Place the halloumi on the grill pan or grill and cook on each side for 2 to 3 minutes or until grill lines form and the cheese is melting.
Toss the sliced cucumbers, halved tomatoes, greens, and mint together.
Spread them across a large, low bowl or large plate.
Mix the remaining olive oil, lemon juice, sea salt, and pepper together.
Drizzle across the salad.
When the halloumi is done cooking, add it to the salad and serve with bread immediately.
Mash together all ingredients in a bowl with a fork until combined well. Serve with bread.
Remove the avocado flesh into bowl. Add the lime juice, salt, and pepper, and mash using a fork.
Spread the cream cheese first onto the bread, then spread the avocado mash.
Put a small pan of water onto boil. Crack your first egg into a saucer. Give the water a gently stir and slide the egg into the water.
Repeat with the second egg. Cook eggs for 3 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on a tea-towel.
Place salmon on each slice of toast and top with an egg.
Smash the garlic clove, sprinkle with 1 teaspoon of salt and with the flat side of a large knife, mash and smear to a coarse paste.
Put the garlic paste in a large bowl and stir in the olive oil and lemon juice.
Add the tomatoes, cucumber, pepperoncini, and dill and toss.
Give a couple of generous grinds of black pepper.
Toss again, and set aside.
Preheat a grill pan or nonstick skillet to medium high.
Spray the chicken paillards lightly with the olive oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Grill the chicken, in batches if necessary, to avoid crowding the pan, turning once, until cooked through, about 2 minutes per side.
Place each paillard on a plate and serve topped with the tomato-cucumber salad.
Serve with bread.
In a large bowl, combine the chicken, pineapple and grapes.
In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise and curry; pour over chicken mixture; toss to coat.
Cover and refrigerate until serving.
Serve with bread.
Toast the bread and spread the cream cheese on the cooled bread slices, top with mashed avocado, then slices of smoked salmon.
Season with a squeeze of lemon, and salt and pepper, to taste. Garnish with black sesame seeds and serve.
Pop your diced tomatoes and cucumber in a serving bowl and give everything a stir until evenly distributed.
Optional step: Add in your optional ingredients such as red pepper, olives, olive oil, vinegar, and salt & pepper if you're using them and stir through again.
Grate that cheese right over the bowl until well and truly covered, and add flat-leaf parsley.
Serve immediately with bread.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Lightly brush a rimmed baking sheet with oil; set aside.
Tear bread into large pieces; place in food processor.
Pulse until large crumbs form.
Drizzle with oil; pulse once or twice, just until crumbs are moistened.
Season pork chops generously with salt and pepper; spread one side of each chop with 1 teaspoon jam.
Dividing evenly, sprinkle bread crumbs over jam, and pat them on gently.
Transfer pork, coated side up, to prepared baking sheet.
Bake until crust is golden and pork is opaque throughout, 14 to 16 minutes. Serve immediately.
Spread the keto chocolate hazelnut spread on each slice of toast.
Layer the bananas and strawberries on top, alternating bananas, and strawberries.
Mix cabbage and carrots in a large ceramic or glass jar at least 1 gallon in volume.
Stir salt into water in a large bowl until dissolved; pour over cabbage mixture which should be completely submerged.
Use an inverted small saucer to keep pressure on cabbage to keep it submerged in the liquid.
Cover container with a clean kitchen towel.
Poking holes through the cabbage mixture to the bottom of the container with the handle of a wooden spoon to help gas escape once a day, keep on counter at room temperature for 3 days.
Pour a small amount of the liquid from the container into a small bowl; stir sugar into the liquid until dissolved.
Return the liquid to the container. Cover container, again assuring the vegetables are submerged in liquid.
Keep on counter for 2 days more, again poking holes into the mixture to help trapped gasses escape daily.
Transfer vegetables and liquid to jars, seal, and refrigerate between uses.
Serve with bread and cranberries.
Combine 1 tbsp white vinegar in skillet filled with about 2 inches simmering water.
Crack 4 large eggs into separate small bowls/ramekins; season each bowl with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Gently slip first egg into simmering water at the 12 o'clock position. Adjust heat as needed to maintain gentle simmer.

Set timer for 4 ½ minutes:
- After 30 sec, add second egg at 3 o'clock.
- After another 30 sec, add third egg at 6 o'clock.
- After another 30 sec, add fourth egg at 9 o'clock.
Remove first egg after 1 ½ min left on timer using fish spatula/slotted spoon; place on towel-lined plate and blot dry. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Repeat procedure every 30 sec clockwise for remaining eggs.
Toast two slices of whole-grain bread; drizzle each slice with 1 tsp olive oil.
Place 2 prepared eggs on each toast slice and serve immediately.
Heat a small saucepan over medium-low heat and add the olive oil.
Add the thinly sliced onion to the saucepan and sauté for about 5 minutes, or until they start to become translucent.
Sprinkle the sugar over the onions and continue to cook for another 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the onions are caramelized and golden brown.
Pour in the low-sodium beef broth and Worcestershire sauce. Bring the mixture to a simmer and let it cook for an additional 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together. Season with black pepper.
While the soup is simmering, preheat your oven to 350°F.
Place the slice of whole-grain bread on a baking sheet and toast it in the preheated oven for about 5 minutes, or until it becomes crispy.
Ladle the hot onion soup into an oven-safe bowl.
Place the toasted bread slice on top of the soup.
Sprinkle the shredded Swiss cheese over the bread.
Transfer the bowl to the oven and broil for about 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is bubbly and lightly browned.
Rinse the eggplant and pat dry. Brush it with little oil all over.
Then make few slits all over the eggplant with a knife. In 3 of those slits, insert a large clove of garlic.
Put the eggplant directly on heat and roast, turning often for around 10-12 minutes until completely roasted.
Once roasted use a pair of tongs to remove the eggplant from heat and wrap in an aluminium foil to cool.
Once cooled, remove the skin. Meanwhile also chop the roasted garlic.
Transfer the roasted eggplant to a bowl and mash using a fork or potato masher. Set it aside.
Heat oil in a pan on medium heat.
Once the oil is hot, add chopped garlic (different from the ones used while roasting the eggplant), ginger and green chili.
Saute until they start changing color, around 2 minutes. Then add in the chopped onion and cook for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Don't brown them. Add the chopped tomatoes and mix. Cook the tomatoes for around 5 minutes until very soft and you notice oil oozing out of the masala.
Add the mashed roasted eggplant into the pan along with the chopped roasted garlic and mix well. Add the red chili powder and mix
Also add the coriander powder and salt and mix to combine.
Cook the bharta for another 5 minutes on medium-low heat, stirring often. Stir in the chopped cilantro and mix. Remove pan from heat.
1/2 cup mint chiffonade
Place spring mix in a large bowl.
Top with strawberries and walnuts.
Sprinkle Gorgonzola and mint chiffonade over the top.
For the dressing.
Combine all ingredients in a glass jar with a lid.
Place the lid on the jar and shake vigorously until the dressing has emulsified.
It should look homogeneous in appearance.
Serve salad with bread and dressing on the side or drizzle over the top just before serving.
In a small bowl, mash the avocado, add the olive oil, lemon juice, season with salt and pepper, mix and set aside.
In a small skillet, fry each egg in the other half of the oil on medium heat until your desired doneness. Season the eggs with salt and pepper to taste.
Meanwhile, toast the sliced bread.
Spread half of the avocado mixture on to each toast, season with a pinch of sea salt, and top with a fried egg. Serve right away.
Heat olive oil in a non stick frying pan.
When the oil is hot, add garlic and fry for a few seconds, till they turn slightly brown.
Add dried parsley, rosemary and oregano in the pan and immediately add the egg whites in the pan.
Simmer the heat to medium low.
Add salt and pepper and keep mixing the whites with a spatula so that they don't form lumps.
Switch off the heat once the whites are 80% done. They will cook further with the heat in the pan.
Serve hot with whole grain bread toast.
Heat a pan over medium heat and fry bacon to desired crispness.
Drain off some of the grease, then fry egg in same pan, about 2 minutes per side, depending on whether or not you want yolk runny.
Whip feta with a hand mixer or in a blender until light an fluffy.
Set bacon and egg aside while you prep avocado.
Peel avocado skin, and carefully slice the avocado in half width wise.
Slice a tiny bit off the wider end of the avocado so it can sit on a plate.
Layer with whipped feta, tomato slice, bacon, egg and the top half of the avocado.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and bread, if desired.
pepper sauce: Cook bacon in a large skillet and over medium heat, turning occasionally, until crispy, 10 to 12 minutes.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate with a slotted spoon, leaving bacon grease in the skillet. Crumble bacon when cool to the touch.
Add celery and garlic to the skillet and cook in the bacon grease until celery is tender, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add cauliflower, water, and onion powder, cover skillet to steam cauliflower. Cook, stirring every so often, until cauliflower is tender, about 5 minutes.
Add milk, 1 3/4 cups chicken broth, and hot sauce. Bring to a boil.
Meanwhile, whisk flour into the remaining chicken broth in a small bowl and drizzle into the boiling chowder.
Add Cheddar cheese and 1/2 of the crumbled bacon. Stir until smooth and cheese has melted.
Season with salt, pepper, and hot sauce. Ladle into bowls and top with remaining bacon and green onion.
In a medium bowl, stir together the tuna, eggs, and oats until blended.
Mix in shallots, hot pepper sauce, and salt.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Spoon tablespoonfuls of the tuna mixture into the pan, and flatten slightly.
Cook until browned on each side, about 3 minutes per side. Garnish with shallots and serve.
Set an oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place bell peppers on the prepared baking sheet. Place in the preheated oven and broil, until the skins begin to bubble and turn dark brown.
Remove and place browned peppers in a bowl. Cover with aluminum foil and set aside to cool. Leave the broiler on.
Meanwhile, whisk together eggs, hot pepper sauce, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Brown chorizo in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat, breaking up any large pieces as you go, about 4 minutes.
Add egg mixture to the skillet and cook until eggs have set, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in salsa.
Fill each pepper with chorizo-egg mixture and sprinkle with Cheddar cheese.
Place in the oven and broil until cheese melts, about 1 minute. Serve immediately.
In a large bowl, mix the ground beef, blue cheese, chives, hot pepper sauce, Worcestershire sauce, black pepper, salt, and mustard. Cover, and refrigerate for 2 hours.
Preheat grill for high heat. Gently form the burger mixture into about 12 patties.
Oil the grill grate. Grill patties 5 minutes per side, or until well done. Serve on rolls with lettuce, tomato and onion.
Prepare wings, Combine chicken wings, soy sauce, and Italian dressing in a large resealable plastic bag.
Coat chicken wings with marinade, squeeze out excess air, and seal the bag. Marinate in the refrigerator, 4 hours to overnight.
Preheat an outdoor grill for medium-high heat and lightly oil the grate.
Prepare sauce, Melt ghee in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir in hot pepper sauce and soy sauce until combined. Remove from heat and set aside.
Remove chicken wings from marinade and pat dry, discard any remaining marinade.
Cook on the preheated grill, turning occasionally, until chicken is browned and no longer pink, 25 to 30 minutes.
Place grilled wings in a large bowl. Pour sauce over wings, toss until well-coated.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Mix orange juice, soy sauce, hot sauce, ginger, and garlic together in a small bowl to make the sauce.
Season chicken with salt, pepper and italian seasoning. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chicken strips and brown on both sides, 1 to 2 minutes per side.
Pour in the orange sauce. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, until sauce thickens and chicken is no longer pink in the centers, 8 to 10 minutes.
Plate the chicken and top with green onion.
Cook and stir ground chicken in a large oiled skillet for about 10 minutes. Drain excess grease.
Whisk soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, ginger,  hot pepper sauce, and sesame oil in a bowl and pour over chicken.
Cook and stir chicken mixture with sauce over low heat until flavors have blended, 5 to 10 minutes.
Form lettuce leaves into cup shapes and fill with chicken mixture. Top lettuce wraps with shredded carrots and sprinkle with green onion.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packagung. Let it sit for 10 minutes.
Cut off the bottom quarter of each nori sheet, reserve for another use.
Combine chopped tuna, flaxseed oil, mayonnaise, green onions, and hot sauce in a bowl.
Center 1 sheet of nori on a bamboo sushi mat. Wet your hands. Spread a thin layer of rice on the nori using your hands.
Press into a thin layer, leaving a 1/2-inch space at the bottom edge.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds. Arrange 1/4 of the tuna mixture in a line across the rice, about 1/3 of the way down from the top of the sheet.
Wet the uncovered edge of the nori. Lift the top end of the mat and firmly roll it over the ingredients.
Roll it forward to make a complete roll. Repeat with remaining ingredients.
Slice the rolls into 3/4-inch pieces using a wet knife. Serve immediately or refrigerate until serving.
Whip cream cheese with flaxseed oil in a bowl with an electric hand mixer.
Add onion, capers, chives, lemon zest and juice, seafood seasoning, parsley, Worcestershire, and hot sauce.
Mix well. Add tuna and mix until well blended.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Clean the crab and cut into 6 pieces, drain the crab and discard the water seeping out of the crab.
Pick out the tamale (or mustard) inside the crab shell, set aside. Discard the shell.
Mix all the ingredients in the Sour and Spicy Sauce in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat up a wok and add the oil.
When the oil is heated, add the minced garlic and ginger and stir fry until aromatic or when they turn slightly brown.
Add the crab and the tamale into the wok, stirring for about 10 seconds.
Add the Sour and Spicy Sauce, stir to blend well with the crab.
Cover the wok with its lid and let cook for about 4-5 minutes.
Uncover the wok and slowly add the cornstarch mixture into the sauce. Stir the sauce until it thickens.
Add the egg into the sauce and stir a few times so the egg is distributed evenly in the sauce.
Cook for another 30 seconds or so, add salt to taste if needed.
Use a potato masher to crush garbanzo beans in a large mixing bowl.
Add celery, onion, vegan mayonnaise, nutritional yeast, salt, garlic powder, olive oil, pickle juice, vinegar, and lemon juice.
Season with paprika, nori, seafood seasoning, hot sauce, cayenne pepper, and dill. Mix thoroughly.
Serve on toasted bread.
Combine buttermilk and hot sauce in a shallow dish, mix to combine.
Combine tapioca flour, ground garlic, and 1/2 black pepper in a resealable plastic bag and shake to combine.
Beat egg in a shallow bowl.
Mix flour, salt, brown sugar, garlic powder, paprika, onion powder, oregano, and remaining black pepper in a gallon-sized resealable bag and shake to combine.
Dip chicken thighs into the prepared ingredients in the following order.
Buttermilk mixture, tapioca mixture, egg, and flour mixture, shaking off excess after each dipping.
Preheat an air fryer to 380°F.  Line the air fryer basket with parchment paper.
Place coated chicken thighs in batches into the air fryer basket and fry for 10 minutes.
Turn chicken thighs and fry until chicken is no longer pink in the center and the juices run clear, an additional 10 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic, cook 2 to 3 minutes. Add tomatoes, cook 2 to 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add remaining ingredients, cook 2 to 3 minutes or until shrimp turn pink. Serve over cooked rice.
Lightly butter the insides of the buns or rolls and line with lettuce leaves. Set aside.
In a medium bowl, stir together the mayonnaise, lime juice, hot pepper sauce, salt and pepper until well blended.
Mix in the green onion and celery, then lightly mix in the lobster so it just gets coated without falling apart.
Stuff the lobster filling into the buns and sprinkle parsley, basil or tarragon lightly over the filling
Remove skin and bones from fish. In a medium bowl, mix fish with onion and hot pepper sauce with a fork, breaking fish into small pieces.
Mix in mayonnaise. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Garnish with dill and serve with bread.
Place the potatoes in a pot with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil, and cook for about 10 minutes, or until easily pierced with a fork.
Drain, and transfer to a large bowl to cool.
In a medium bowl, mix the sour cream, mayonnaise, mustard, vinegar, eggs, pickle, celery, green onions, and hot sauce.
Season with dill, garlic powder, onion salt, salt, and pepper. Pour over the potatoes, and gently toss to coat. Chill at least 3 hours in the refrigerator before serving.
Preheat an outdoor grill for high heat. Lightly oil a sheet of aluminum foil with cooking spray.
Mash black beans in a medium bowl with a fork until thick and pasty.
Finely chop bell pepper, onion, and garlic in a food processor. Stir chopped vegetables into mashed beans.
Stir together egg, protein powder, chili powder, cumin, and chili sauce in a small bowl.
Add to the mashed beans and stir to combine. Mix in oatmeal until the mixture is sticky and holds together.
Divide the mixture into four patties and place on the prepared foil.
Grill on the preheated grill for about 8 minutes on each side.
Mix roma tomatoes, sweet onion, ketchup, tomato and clam juice cocktail, salsa in a glass salad bowl.
Add cilantro, salsa verde, lime juice, hot pepper sauce, and garlic, cover and refrigerate until cold, about 1 hour.
Cut tails from shrimp, if present, and gently fold into tomato mixture, stir avocado into shrimp cocktail.
In a mixing bowl, combine peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, curry powder, garlic and hot pepper sauce.
Place the chicken breasts in the marinade and refrigerate. Let the chicken marinate at least 2 hours, overnight is best.
Preheat a grill to high heat.
Weave the chicken onto skewers, then grill for 5 minutes per side.
Slice salmon fillets into 4 equal sections, width-wise. Rotate each section 90 degrees and slice lengthwise, about 1/4-inch thick.
Remove pin bones with needle-nose pliers. Don't slice bony sections near the center of the fillet.
Stir soy sauce, molasses, Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, black pepper and hot sauce together in a bowl.
Place salmon in the marinade, ensuring every strip is covered. Cover bowl and refrigerate for 4 hours.
Remove salmon from bowl and drain liquid using a colander. Pat the salmon dry using paper towels.
Place each salmon strip in a dehydrator and run the dehydrator according to manufacturer's instructions until desired doneness is reached, about 6 hours.
cherry tomatoes: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking sheet and season with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until it flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is baking, in a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, and sesame seeds to create the dressing.
In two serving plates, divide the mixed greens and arrange the halved cherry tomatoes and sliced red bell pepper.
Once the salmon is cooked, let it cool slightly and then flake it into bite-sized pieces.
Place the flaked salmon on top of the prepared salads.
Drizzle the Asian Ginger Sesame Dressing over the salads.
Garnish each plate with slices of lime.
In a small bowl, combine the drained and rinsed chickpeas with 1/2 teaspoon of smoked paprika and 1/2 teaspoon of cumin. Toss to coat the chickpeas in the spices.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the seasoned chickpeas to the skillet.
Sauté the chickpeas for about 4-5 minutes until they become slightly crispy and browned. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Remove from heat and let them cool.
In a salad bowl, place 2 cups of chopped lettuce.
Add the thinly sliced radishes, halved cherry tomatoes, and sliced cucumber to the bowl with the lettuce.
Drizzle the salad with the juice of half a lemon and 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the sautéed chickpeas have cooled slightly, add them to the salad.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
Place the seasoned chicken breasts on a baking sheet.
Bake the chicken in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes or until they reach an internal temperature of 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the salad. In a large bowl, combine the torn lettuce and halved cherry tomatoes.
Once the chicken is cooked, let it cool for a few minutes, then slice it into thin strips.
Top the salad with the sliced chicken.
Add the sliced ripe avocados to the salad.
Drizzle balsamic vinegar over the salad.
Toss the salad to mix the ingredients.
In a mixing bowl, combine 1 cup of drained and rinsed black beans, 1 cup of halved cherry tomatoes, and 1/2 cup of corn kernels.
Add 1/2 diced avocado to the bowl.
Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of chopped fresh dill over the ingredients.
Squeeze the juice of half a lime into the bowl to add a zesty flavor.
Season the salad with a pinch of salt and pepper to taste. If you like it spicy, you can also add 1/4 teaspoon of chili powder.
Toss all the ingredients together until they are well combined.
Boil the potatoes for 10-15 minutes.
In a large salad bowl, arrange the mixed salad greens as the base of your salad.
Place the drained tuna in the center of the greens.
Arrange the hard-boiled egg halves, cherry tomatoes, Kalamata olives, capers, and sliced red potatoes around the tuna.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dressing ingredients: olive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and black pepper to taste.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
In a large salad bowl, start with a base of mixed greens.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes to the greens.
Toss in the diced cucumber, and yellow bell pepper.
Sprinkle the thinly sliced red onion over the vegetables.
Tear the fresh basil leaves into pieces and add them to the salad.
Add crumbled feta cheese for extra flavor.
In a small bowl, whisk together the extra-virgin olive oil and balsamic vinegar to create a simple dressing. Season with a pinch of salt and black pepper to taste.
Drizzle the dressing evenly over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients in the salad bowl to combine and coat them with the dressing.
Taste and adjust the seasoning or dressing to your preference.
In a saucepan, bring 4 cups of salted water to a boil. Add 1 cup of orzo pasta and cook for 8-10 minutes or until al dente.
Drain and rinse with cold water to cool.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooked and cooled orzo, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, finely chopped red onion, sliced Kalamata olives, and crumbled feta cheese.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil and the juice of 1 lemon.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss the salad to combine all the ingredients.
Optionally, garnish with fresh parsley.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season the chicken breast with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Bake the chicken breast in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, or until it's cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the pasta according to the package instructions. Once cooked, drain and set it aside to cool.
Once the chicken is done, let it rest for a few minutes, then dice it into bite-sized pieces.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked pasta, diced chicken, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, red bell pepper, baby spinach or arugula, black olives, and red onion.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, balsamic vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper. This is your salad dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
Place the seasoned chicken breasts on a baking sheet.
Bake the chicken in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes or until they reach an internal temperature of 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the salad. In a large bowl, combine the torn lettuce, cherry tomatoes, and croutons.
Squeeze the juice of both lemons into a small bowl, and whisk in the olive oil to make a lemon vinaigrette.
Once the chicken is cooked, let it cool for a few minutes, then slice it into thin strips.
Drizzle the lemon vinaigrette over the salad.
Toss the salad to coat the ingredients with the dressing.
Top the salad with the sliced chicken.
In a large salad bowl, place the thinly sliced kale leaves.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes and thinly sliced red onion to the kale.
Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the vegetables.
Season the salad with a pinch of salt and black pepper.

In a separate bowl, prepare the Green Goddess dressing. Combine mayonnaise, Greek yogurt, chopped parsley, chopped chives, chopped tarragon, minced garlic, lemon juice, and white wine vinegar. Mix until well combined.
Taste the dressing and adjust the seasoning with salt and black pepper. If the dressing is too thick, you can thin it with a little water until it reaches your desired consistency.
Drizzle the Green Goddess dressing over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients in the salad bowl to coat them with the dressing.
In a medium saucepan, bring 2 cups of water to a boil.
Add the pearl couscous, reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10-12 minutes or until the couscous is tender.
Remove the cooked couscous from the heat and let it cool to room temperature.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked and cooled couscous, diced cucumber, cherry tomatoes, diced red onion, and chopped fresh parsley.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Toss everything together to combine.
Cook the quinoa in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a large salad bowl, start by adding the arugula as the base.
Divide the drained and rinsed white beans evenly between the two servings and place them on top of the arugula.
Divide the halved cherry tomatoes and diced bell pepper evenly between the servings and arrange them over the beans.
Sprinkle the red onion over the vegetables in each serving.
Spoon the cooked and cooled quinoa into each salad, dividing it evenly.
In a small bowl, whisk together the extra-virgin olive oil and balsamic vinegar to create a simple dressing. Season with a pinch of salt and black pepper to taste.
Drizzle the dressing evenly over both servings of the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients in each serving's salad bowl to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasoning or dressing to your preference.
Place the cubed potato in a pot of boiling water and cook for about 10-12 minutes or until the potato cubes are tender but still firm. Drain and let them cool.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled potato cubes, halved cherry tomatoes, sliced black olives, chopped roasted red peppers, and fresh Italian herbs.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss everything together to combine.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Prepare the tofu by draining it and cutting it into small cubes.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.

In a bowl, combine the cubed tofu with 2 tablespoons of low-sodium soy sauce, 2 tablespoons of rice vinegar, 1 tablespoon of sesame oil, and 1/2 teaspoon of grated ginger. Toss to coat the tofu evenly.
Spread the marinated tofu cubes on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Bake the tofu in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until it's lightly browned and slightly crispy.

While the tofu is baking, assemble your salad bowls. Divide 1 cup of cooked rice, 1/2 cup of thawed peas, 1/2 cup of broccoli florets, 1/2 cup of halved cherry tomatoes, and 1/2 cup of shelled edamame between two serving bowls.
When the tofu is done baking, remove it from the oven and allow it to cool slightly.
Once the tofu has cooled, distribute the baked tofu cubes evenly over the salad bowls.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cook quinoa according to package instructions and allow it to cool.
Cook edamame by boiling for 3-5 minutes, then drain and cool.
In a large salad bowl, combine the cooled quinoa, cooked edamame, lettuce, cherry tomatoes, and dried cranberries. Add olive oil and balsamic vinegar.
Toss everything together gently.
Serve the Edamame Salad immediately as a delicious and healthy meal.
Toss the tomatoes, cucumbers, and lemon juice in a bowl with 2 tsp of the salt. Set aside.
Put the couscous into a large bowl. Bring the water, 2 tbsp of olive oil, flax seeds, the remaining salt, cayenne, and herb stems to a boil over high heat.
Pour the liquid over the couscous, stir to separate any clumps. Cover with a lid, set aside for 5 minutes. Remove the herb stems and fluff with a fork.
Add the tomato mixture, scallions, mint, parsley, and pine nuts. Drizzle the remaining oil over the salad, toss and serve.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken breast and cook until golden brown and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes per side. Remove from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add cherry tomatoes, bell peppers, carrots, minced garlic, dried oregano, and dried basil. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender-crisp, about 5-6 minutes.
Pour broth into the skillet and stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Add the cooked farfalle pasta to the skillet and toss to coat in the sauce.
Slice the cooked chicken breast and add it back to the skillet, stirring to combine with the vegetables and pasta.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until everything is heated through.
In a small bowl, whisk the eggs with a fork and season with salt and pepper, to taste. Set aside.
Heat a small skillet over medium heat and add the oil. Pour in the egg mixture and cook until almost set, about 5 minutes.
Place the chopped tomato and feta on one half of the omelet and fold the other half over top. Remove from heat and top with parsley.
Cook the linguine pasta according to the package instructions until it's al dente. Drain and set it aside.
In a skillet or frying pan, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic and red pepper flakes to the hot oil. Sauté for about 30 seconds or until the garlic is fragrant, being careful not to brown it.
Add the prawns to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side or until they turn pink and opaque.
Toss in the halved cherry tomatoes. Sauté for an additional 1-2 minutes until the tomatoes soften.
Season the prawns and tomato mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Add the cooked linguine to the skillet and toss everything together to combine.
Once the linguine is heated through, transfer the Easy Prawn Linguine to a plate.
Finely chop roasted red peppers and sun-dried tomatoes, crumble feta, mince garlic and herbs. Combine feta with roasted red peppers and sun-dried tomatoes.
Make a small incision in the middle of the thickest part of the chicken breast. Carefully, continue working your knife into the chicken breast, creating a pocket.
Stuff each chicken breast with the feta mixture. Season all sides of the chicken breasts with salt and pepper and set aside.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat. Once the oil is nice and hot, add chicken breasts into the pan and cook for approximately 5 minutes until golden brown.
Turn the heat down to medium-low, turn the chicken breasts to the other side and cook for an additional 5 minutes or so, until fully cooked through.
Set chicken aside to rest for 2-3 minutes, then serve with spinach, cashews, cherry tomatoes and avocado.
chicken thighs: In a bowl, combine the turmeric powder, coriander powder, salt, minced garlic, and lime juice to create a marinade.
Rub the marinade all over the chicken piece. Make sure it's evenly coated. Let the chicken marinate for at least 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

In a small pan, heat about 1/4 inch of vegetable oil over medium-high heat. You can test if the oil is hot enough by dipping the end of a wooden spoon into the oil – if it bubbles around the wood, it's hot enough.
Carefully place the marinated chicken piece into the hot oil.
Fry the chicken until it's golden brown and crispy. This typically takes around 10-15 minutes, but the cooking time may vary depending on the size of the chicken piece.
Once the chicken is cooked through and crispy, remove it from the oil and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the Ayam Goreng hot, garnished with sliced cucumber.
Position rack in the lower third of oven; preheat to 450°F. Place a large rimmed baking sheet in the oven to preheat.
Combine mustard, thyme, oil, salt and pepper in a small bowl; spread the mixture evenly on chicken.
Toss sweet potatoes and onions in a bowl with the remaining oil and salt and pepper.
Carefully remove the baking sheet from the oven and spread the vegetables on it. Place the chicken on top of the vegetables.

Return the pan to the oven and roast, stirring the vegetables once halfway through, until the vegetables are tender and beginning to brown and an instant-read thermometer inserted into a chicken thigh registers 165°F, 30 to 35 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Rub the spice mixture evenly over the chicken thighs, ensuring they are well coated.
Place the seasoned chicken thighs on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and juices run clear.
Once cooked, remove the chicken thighs from the oven and let them rest for a few minutes.
In a pot, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, minced garlic, and diced bell pepper to the pot, sautéing until softened (about 2-3 minutes).
Add chicken to the pot, cooking until they are browned on all sides (about 3-4 minutes).
Stir in rinsed rice, tomato sauce, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and dried oregano, ensuring the rice and chicken are well coated.
Pour in broth, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the chicken is tender.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Whisk lemon juice, olive oil, Dijon mustard, garlic, salt, and pepper together in a bowl. Set marinade aside.
Place chicken thighs into a large resealable plastic bag. Pour marinade over chicken and seal bag. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
Preheat an air fryer to 360°F.
Remove chicken from marinade and pat dry with paper towels. Place chicken pieces in the air fryer basket, cooking in batches if necessary.
Fry until chicken is no longer pink at the bone and the juices run clear, 22 to 24 minutes. Dress with lemon wedges.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Season chicken breasts and thighs with salt and pepper.
Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken in the hot butter until golden brown, 5 to 10 minutes.
Transfer to a baking dish. Add feta cheese and garlic. Mix heavy cream and pesto together in a bowl. Pour over the chicken mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven, uncovered, until top is golden brown, 20 to 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add chicken pieces and cook for 5-6 minutes until browned on all sides.
Add garlic, paprika, ground coriander, stir well.
Add chopped tomato and bell pepper; cook for another 5-7 minutes until vegetables are tender and chicken is cooked through.
Season with salt and pepper.
Stir in fresh cilantro and parsley before serving.
Preheat an outdoor grill to 350°F. Oil the grate.
Season each side of the chicken thighs with salt and pepper. Place inner side of each chicken thigh on the preheated grill.
Bush the top of each with 1 tablespoon barbecue sauce. Grill undisturbed for 3 minutes, watching for flare-ups.
Move chicken pieces to another part of the grill if flare-ups occur.
Turn chicken pieces and brush each side with an additional 1 tablespoon barbecue sauce.
Grill, turning the pieces three more times, about 6 minutes per side. Serve with additional sauce.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix diced chicken with ground turmeric, ground coriander, chili powder, salt, and black pepper.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic to the pan, sautéing until softened (about 2-3 minutes).
Add the seasoned chicken to the pan, cooking until it's browned on all sides (about 3-4 minutes).
Pour in coconut milk and chicken broth, stirring to combine.
Lower the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the flavors meld.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve the Burmese Chicken Curry over cooked jasmine rice.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, whisk together hoisin sauce, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, minced garlic, red pepper flakes (if using), salt, and pepper.
Place the chicken thighs in a shallow dish or a resealable plastic bag.
Pour the hoisin glaze over the chicken thighs, ensuring they are evenly coated. Marinate for about 15-20 minutes at room temperature.
Heat an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the chicken thighs skin side down and sear for about 2-3 minutes until browned.
Flip the chicken thighs skin side up and transfer the skillet to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken thighs are cooked through, and the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Once done, remove the chicken thighs from the oven and let them rest for a few minutes.
Garnish with parsley.
In a large skillet, add olive oil and heat it over medium high heat. When the pan is hot, add chicken and cook for about 5 minutes on each side.
Season chicken with salt and pepper right inside the pan. Remove it onto a plate and add the sliced mushrooms in.
Add in salt. In about 5 more minutes, your mushrooms should look nice and moist, and dark. Once cooked, remove them from the pan.
To make the sauce, add butter to the pan, allow it to melt and then cook garlic in it until slightly browned  Add 1 tablespoon flour, cooking cream, and broth.
Add salt slowly and taste the sauce to make sure the salt level is to your liking.
Add spinach to the sauce and allow it to wilt. Add the chicken and mushrooms back into the pan and mix in well. Serve.
Combine chicken pieces, garlic powder, black pepper, curry powder, ginger, soy sauce, cinnamon and salt in a large bowl. Let marinate for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle tempura batter mix over chicken, toss to coat lightly and evenly.
Heat your air fryer to 350°F. Cook chicken in batches until golden brown, about 4 minutes. Drain on paper towels.
Increase the air fryer temperature to 400°F. Cook Thai basil leaves until crisp, about 1 minute. Drain on paper towels.
Cook chicken a second time, 2 to 3 minutes per batch. Drain on paper towels. Serve with basil leaves.
Combine sugar, soy sauce, water, onion, garlic, pepper, and sesame oil in a blender, process until well blended.
Put chicken into a large glass or ceramic bowl. Pour marinade over top and toss to evenly coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and marinate in the refrigerator for 12 hours.
Cook the rice a pot of water accoridng to the intrucstions on the packaging.
Preheat an outdoor grill for medium-high heat and lightly oil the grate.
Remove chicken from the marinade and shake off excess. Discard the remaining marinade.
Cook chicken on the preheated grill until slightly crisp on the outside, no longer pink in the center, and the juices run clear, about 4 to 8 minutes per side.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together the pure maple syrup, soy sauce, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to create the marinade.
Place the chicken thigh in a shallow dish and pour the marinade over it, ensuring the chicken is evenly coated. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
Remove the chicken thigh from the marinade and shake off any excess.
Place the chicken thigh in the air fryer basket.
Air fry at 375°F for 18-20 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the chicken is cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
Once cooked, remove the chicken thigh from the air fryer and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the air-fried maple chicken thigh hot with your favorite side dishes.
Season the chicken thigh with salt and pepper.
In a small saucepan, cook the chopped bacon over medium heat until crispy. Remove bacon from the pan and set aside.
In the same saucepan, add the chicken thigh, skin side down, and cook until browned on both sides. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic to the saucepan and cook until softened.
Stir in sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their juices.
Sprinkle all-purpose flour over the vegetables and stir to combine.
Pour in chicken broth and dry red wine, and bring to a simmer.
Return the chicken and bacon to the saucepan, cover, and simmer for about 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Serve hot.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Mix bread crumbs and dry soup mix together in a bowl.
Mix mayonnaise and garlic paste together in a separate bowl. Microwave on high to thin out consistency, for 20 seconds.
Brush chicken breasts with the mayonnaise mixture. Cover with the crumb mixture. Place on a baking sheet.
Bake on the middle rack of the preheated oven until chicken is no longer pink in the center and juices run clear, about 20 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the chicken thigh with salt, pepper, dried thyme, and dried rosemary.
In an oven-safe skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Place the seasoned chicken thigh in the skillet and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown.
In a bowl, mix together chicken broth, white wine, and Dijon mustard.
Pour the mixture over the chicken in the skillet.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for about 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
Check the internal temperature of the chicken; it should reach 165°F (74°C).
Remove the skillet from the oven, spoon the sauce over the chicken.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add chopped onions and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add chopped onions and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Transfer the cooked onions and mushrooms to the slow cooker.
Season the chicken thigh pieces with salt, pepper, and dried thyme.
Place the seasoned chicken thigh pieces on top of the onions and mushrooms in the slow cooker.
Pour broth over the chicken and vegetables in the slow cooker.
Cover and cook on low for 4-5 hours or on high for 2-3 hours, until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
If desired, you can thicken the stew by mixing 1 tablespoon of all-purpose flour with a little water to make a slurry. Stir the slurry into the stew during the last 30 minutes of cooking.
Serve the chicken and mushroom stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
In a small skillet, cook the chopped bacon over medium heat until crispy. Remove the bacon from the skillet and set aside.
Season the chicken thigh with salt and black pepper.

In the same skillet with the bacon fat, sear the chicken thigh skin-side down until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes. Flip and sear the other side for an additional 2-3 minutes. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced mushrooms and pearl onions. Cook until the mushrooms are browned and the onions are softened, about 4-5 minutes.
Return the cooked bacon to the skillet. Pour in the red wine and broth, and add the dried thyme. Stir to combine.
Return the chicken thigh to the skillet, nestling it among the mushrooms and onions.
Cover the skillet and let the mixture simmer over low heat for about 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Once the chicken is cooked, remove it from the skillet and place it on a serving plate.
Increase the heat to medium-high and let the sauce reduce and thicken for a few minutes.
Pour the sauce over the chicken and garnish with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Set the Instant Pot to sauté mode and heat olive oil.
Add diced onion and minced garlic, sautéing until softened (about 2-3 minutes).
Add chicken pieces to the Instant Pot, cooking until they are browned on all sides (about 3-4 minutes).
Stir in ground cumin, ground coriander, ground cinnamon, and ground turmeric, ensuring the chicken is well coated.
Add diced butternut squash and fresh spinach to the Instant Pot.
Pour in water, and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Close the Instant Pot lid, set it to manual high pressure for 8 minutes, and let it naturally release for 5 minutes before quick releasing.
cocoa powder: In a mixing bowl, combine the rolled oats, honey, and chopped nuts.
Add a pinch of cinnamon and cocoa powder for extra flavor.
Mix the ingredients together until well combined and the mixture sticks together.
Take a small portion of the mixture and flatten it in your palm.
Place a small amount of peanut butter in the center of the flattened mixture.
Gently fold the edges of the mixture over the peanut butter, shaping it into a ball. Ensure the peanut butter is fully enclosed within the mixture.
Repeat the process to make additional stuffed energy balls until all the mixture is used.
Place the formed balls on a plate or tray lined with parchment paper.
Refrigerate the energy balls for at least 30 minutes to set.
Once chilled, your Peanut Butter Stuffed Energy Balls are ready to enjoy as a delicious and energizing snack!
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Combine chili powder and cocoa powder in a bowl, sprinkle over both sides of pork loin
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and brown pork loin on both sides, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer pork and juices to a baking dish.
Roast in the preheated oven until pork is slightly pink in the center, about 12 minutes.
Combine mango, shallot, cilantro, and lemon juice in a bowl. Slice pork loin and top with juices from the baking dish and mango mixture. Top with cheese slices.
In a small pot, combine the rolled oats and water or milk.
Add the mashed ripe banana, cocoa powder, and a pinch of salt to the pot.
Place the pot over medium heat and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low and let the oatmeal simmer for about 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches your desired consistency.
If desired, add maple syrup or honey for sweetness and stir well.
Once cooked to your preferred consistency, remove the chocolate banana oatmeal from the heat.
Transfer the oatmeal to a bowl and top with sliced banana.
Add all ingredients to blender and blend until you reach the desired consistency.
You can add extra water in case you need would like it thinner.  Less if you prefer your shake thicker.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook ground beef until browned and cooked through.
Add beef broth, tomato sauce, apple cider vinegar, cocoa powder, cinnamon, cumin, allspice, cayenne pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for about 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until flavors are well combined and sauce thickens.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the Cincinnati chili over cooked spaghetti.
Top with shredded cheddar cheese, chopped onion, and/or kidney beans.
Pour cocoa powder into a microwave-safe bowl and stir in cinnamon, and salt.
Add hot water and stir thoroughly until cocoa powder has dissolved. Stir in cold water until well combined. Stir in oats and banana until mixed well.
Microwave for 2 minutes. Remove, stir, and allow oatmeal to cool and thicken.
Pour cashew milk into a saucepan and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Add oats and reduce heat to a simmer.
Stir in cocoa powder, salt, and cinnamon until well combined. Add protein powder and stir once more.
Cook until oatmeal has desired consistency, 1 to 2 minutes more. Pour oatmeal into a bowl and top with banana.
Make the granola first. Preheat the oven to 300 °F.
Mix the dry ingredients - oatmeal, 1 tbsp cocoa powder, and salt - together in a medium mixing bowl.
Stir to combine.
Add vanilla extract to the dry ingredients. Stir to combine.
Melt coconut oil in the microwave until completely liquid (approximately 30-40 seconds). Stir to combine.
Melt honey in the microwave until completely liquid (approximately 15-20 seconds).
Stir to combine.
Line a 9 x 13 inch cookie sheet with parchment paper.
Spread a thin layer of the mixture on the cookie sheet.
Bake for 20 minutes, stirring halfway through.
Remove from oven and allow to completely cool for 30 minutes.
While the granola is baking, mix the banana, avocado, almond milk, remaining cocoa powder and the ice cubes in a blender.
Pour into bowls, and top with cooled granola
In a bowl or jar, combine the chia seeds, cocoa powder, and almond milk.
Stir the mixture thoroughly until the cocoa powder is fully incorporated. Add honey.
Cover the bowl or jar and refrigerate it for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the chia seeds to absorb the liquid and thicken.
Once the pudding has thickened to your desired consistency, remove it from the refrigerator.
Top the cocoa-chia pudding with fresh raspberries before serving.
Enjoy your delicious and nutritious Cocoa-Chia Pudding with Raspberries!
Mix the banana, the avocado, the milk, the cocoa powder and the ice cubes in a blender.
Pour into bowls and add some homemade granola.
Complete with some toppings.
Heat a frying pan. Add oats, and dry roast them on medium heat for 5 minutes. Remove from the pan and set aside to cool.
In the same pan, add walnuts and dry roast on medium heat for 5 minutes. Remove from the pan and set aside to cool.
Once oats and walnuts are cooled, add them into a food processor. Grind the oats and walnuts until they are coarsely ground.
Add dates, cocoa powder, maple syrup and chia seeds. Process until everything is well combined, stick together, and becomes almost like a dough.
If it is not coming together or is a little dry, add one or two tablespoon of water. Scoop the dough with your hand, form it into small balls and serve.
Combine oats and water in a microwave-safe bowl and cook in the microwave for about 1 1/2 minutes.
Stir in cocoa powder, and vanilla extract. Top off with as much milk as desired.
Place all the ingredients into a high speed blender. Process until smooth. If desired add ice and process until crushed. Serve immediately.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
In a high powered blender, mix together the almond milk, half of the strawberries, dates, cocoa powder, protein powder, vanilla, and cinnamon until well incorporated.
In a bowl, mix together the almond milk mixture with the chia seeds very well. Let side 5 minutes, then stir again.
Serve cooled, topped with the remaining strawberries.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add chopped onion and minced garlic. Cook until softened, about 2-3 minutes.
Add chicken breast to the skillet and cook until browned on both sides, about 4-5 minutes per side.
In a small bowl, mix together cocoa powder, ground cumin, ground cinnamon, and chili powder. Sprinkle the spice mixture over the chicken in the skillet.
Pour broth into the skillet and bring to a simmer. Cover and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and shred it using two forks.
Return the shredded chicken to the skillet and simmer for an additional 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Top with sliced onion and cotija.
Sift cocoa powder into a medium deep mixing bowl.
Add vanilla extract and pinch of salt.
Whisk to combine until smooth.
Add the coconut milk on top and whisk to combine.
Pour the chia seeds on top of the mixture and whisk to combine one more time.
Cover and refrigerate until the mixture thickens, and becomes pudding like, at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight.
Add ingredients to a mixing bowl.
Whisk to combine.
Let rest covered in the fridge overnight. Top with mango.
Add all ingredients except blueberries to a mixing bowl.
Whisk to combine.
Let rest covered in the fridge overnight.
Top with berries and enjoy.
fish fillets: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion with the paprika and cayenne pepper and cook until softened, 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic clove and cook for 1 more minute. Add the tomato purée, tomato paste, water, fresh coriander, salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat to low, and cook for 5 more minutes.
Meanwhile make the fish balls in a food processor. Chop the onion in the food processor, then add the rest of the ingredients. Grind for 1 minute.
Form the fish mixture into balls and place them in the tomato sauce, making sure that they aren't touching one another.
Cover the skillet and let the fish balls simmer in the sauce for 20 minutes, turning them over after 5 minutes to cover them in the sauce. Serve warm.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Rub 4 brown paper bags (or parchment paper) inside and out with oil to grease.
Separate fish into 4 fillets and sprinkle with the lemon rind and lemon juice over the fish.
Top each with shallot, salt, and 1 tbsp butter each and cover evenly.
Divide the fish among the bags.
Place the bags on a large baking tray.
Fold in the ends and tuck under to seal.
Bake for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine peas and 1/4 cup water in a medium saucepan.
Cover and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Cook until peas are tender, about 6-8 minutes, stirring occasionally.  Drain peas and return to the pan.
Add in 2 tbsp butter, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, and pepper to taste.
Divide the peas among serving plates.
Top with the fish and drizzle over the juices.
Sprinkle with sea salt flakes and serve with lemon wedges, if desired.
Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Add garlic and onion, cook for 5 minutes until translucent but not golden. Add carrots and stir through. Turn heat up to high and add wine.
Stir then let it simmer rapidly until it's mostly evaporated. Add flour and stir for 30 seconds - it will become sludgy!
Slow pour in broth while stirring to dissolve paste, then stir in milk. Add potato, corn, salt and pepper.
Bring to simmer then turn heat down so it's simmering gently.
Cook, stirring every now and then, for 8 minutes or until potato is almost cooked.
Add fish and peas, simmer for 3 minutes or until fish is just cooked (should flake easily).
Adjust salt and pepper to taste. If soup is too thick, add a touch of water or milk.
Serve, garnished with green onions and crusty bread on the side for dunking!
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile bring the tomatoes, cumin, turmeric, salt, and water to a boil in a four quart saucepan, reduce heat to medium and maintain a simmer.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium-high heat, cook the fish in the oiled skillet until golden brown, 2 to 3 minutes per side. Transfer the fish to the saucepan.
Heat a separate skillet over medium heat, and toast the mustard seeds, cumin seeds and fennel seeds until fragrant.
Stir the spices into the saucepan. Simmer until the flavors integrate, about 10 minutes more. Serve hot.
Heat oil and add cumin seeds. When they splutter, add onions and ginger-garlic pastes. Stir fry till fat separates.
Add the garam masala, coriander powder, chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt and yogurt and saute till fat separates.
Mix in the fish and cook over high heat till opaque. Mix in the browned onions, coriander, green chillies and biryani masala.
To make the rice, heat oil and add cloves, peppercorns, cinnamon and cardamoms. Mix well and cook till rice is tender but still holds its shape.
To serve, put the fish layer at the bottom of the dish, cover with the rice and sprinkle the saffron-milk mixture.
Keep or over low heat for about 15 minutes. Mix to break up the layers and serve.
navy beans: Place basil leaves, 1 garlic clove, Parmesan, olive oil, pumpkin seeds, salt, and pepper in the bowl of a food processor and pulse until smooth.
Heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add leeks and saute for 5 minutes. Lower the heat if the leeks are starting to brown.
Add the remaining garlic. Stir and cook for 2 more minutes.
Add asparagus, zucchini, green beans, and navy beans. Stir and cook for one minute.
Add the broth. Add salt and pepper to taste. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a gentle simmer and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add kale and continue simmering for another 5 minutes.
Add peas and pesto and cook for 2 more minutes. Serve with lemon juice.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In an ovenproof dish, combine canned navy beans, chopped bacon, diced onions, low-sodium tomato sauce, honey, apple cider vinegar, salt, and black pepper.
Stir to mix all the ingredients.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 45-60 minutes, or until the beans are tender and the flavors are well combined.
Serve your Homemade Baked Beans with Bacon hot.
In a small saucepan, combine the canned navy beans, low-sodium tomato sauce, water, white vinegar, paprika, onion powder, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Stir everything together to combine.
Place the saucepan over medium heat and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Once it's simmering, reduce the heat to low and let it cook for about 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the flavors meld.
Taste and adjust the seasonings if needed.
Serve your homemade copycat Heinz Baked Beans hot with bread.

In a medium saucepan,  cook the lightly salted water and spelt grains until it boils. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for 45 minutes to 1 hour or until the spelt is tender. Drain, transfer to a large bowl and set it aside to cool.
Once the spelt is cool, add navy beans, cucumber, bell pepper and the herbs. Toss to combine.
In a separate bowl, combine the balsamic vinegar, the salt and pepper and the olive oil. Whisk until emulsified.
When ready to serve, pour the dressing over the salad and toss to coat. Serve at room temperature.
Transfer soaked navy beans and soaking water to a saucepan; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer until tender, approximately 1 to 2 hours. Drain and reserve the cooking liquid.
Preheat the oven to 325°F.
Arrange 1/2 of the beans in the bottom of a 2-quart casserole dish. Place 1/2 of the bacon strips over the beans and sprinkle 1/2 of the onions over top. Repeat layers once more.
Combine ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, salt, pepper, and dry mustard in a large saucepan over medium heat; bring to a boil.
Pour sauce over the beans. Pour in just enough reserved cooking liquid to cover the beans. Cover the casserole dish with a lid or aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for 1 1/2 hours. Remove the lid and continue to cook, checking every 1/2 hour or so.
Add more cooking liquid if necessary to prevent the beans from getting too dry, until beans are tender, 1 1/2 to 2 1/2 more hours.
curry powder: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the salmon fillet with salt and pepper.
Place the salmon fillet in the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until cooked through.
In a small saucepan, combine coconut milk, curry powder, and turmeric. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce heat and stir in yogurt until well combined. Let simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Serve the pan-fried salmon with the curry cream sauce poured over the top.
Combine the milk, flour and chili powder in a deep bowl, mix well and keep aside.
Heat the oil in a broad non-stick pan, add the cumin seeds and peas and saute on a medium flame for 30 seconds.
