Add the curry and grated onion and saute on a medium flame for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and saute on a medium flame for 1 minute.
Add the milk mixture and salt, mix well and cook on a slow flame for 6 minutes, while stirring occasionally.
Serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile fry the onions in olive oil until browned. Add the cinnamon. Continue stirring and add the paprika, curry powder, salt, and garlic. Continue stirring for 2 minutes.
Add chicken pieces and tomato. Add enough water to just cover the chicken. Simmer until chicken is done, about 20 minutes.
Just before serving, add the lemon juice and the cayenne pepper. Simmer for 5 more minutes.
Whisk eggs with curry powder, ground turmeric and coconut oil until they are combined. Add in sliced beans.
Add oil to pan over medium heat and swirl the pan to distribute oil evenly. As oil lightly smokes, turn heat to low. Give the egg mixture a good stir before adding to the pan.
Using a turner or a flat-topped spoon to lift and stir the loosely set egg mixture, forming soft folds of scrambled eggs. Cook for 2 minutes.
Serve hot.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Rinse the dried lentils thoroughly and drain them.
In a saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced onions and diced bell peppers. Sauté for a few minutes until they start to soften.
Stir in minced garlic, curry powder, ground cumin, and ground coriander. Cook for about a minute until fragrant.
Add the rinsed lentils, vegetable broth, and diced tomatoes.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and cover the saucepan.
Let the lentil curry simmer for about 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are soft and the flavors have melded.
Stir occasionally and add a little water if it becomes too thick.
Serve your Lentil Curry hot with rice.
Pour the vegetable broth into a soup pot, and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Stir in the curry powder, cumin, cinnamon, and ginger, add the carrots.
Reduce heat to a simmer, and cook until the carrots are tender, about 20 minutes, stirring often.
Strain out the carrots from the broth, and place carrots into a blender, filling the pitcher no more than halfway full.
Pour about 1/4 cup vegetable broth into the blender to allow the carrots to puree. Add yeast.
Hold down the lid of the blender with a folded kitchen towel, and carefully start the blender and leave it to puree.
Puree in batches until smooth, if needed, return the pureed carrots to the vegetable broth.
Alternately, you can use a stick blender and puree the carrots right in the cooking pot.
Stir in the coconut milk and the water. Bring back to a simmer, sprinkle with cilantro to serve.
Heat coconut oil in a pot over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add green beans and saute until nearly tender, about 3 minutes. Pour in stock and bring to a boil. Add salmon, curry powder, and garlic powder.
Add coconut cream, reduce heat, and simmer until salmon flesh flakes easily with a fork, 3 to 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper, garnish with basil and chili.
Bring a pot of water to a boil. Turn the heat to low so there's no bubbles. Use a skimmer to slowly and gently place the eggs in the pot.
Turn the heat back to high and boil the eggs for 12-14 minutes. Transfer the eggs to an ice water bath for a few minutes to stop the cooking process and cool.
Once your eggs are cool to touch, peel and discard the eggs shells. Grate the hard boiled eggs.
To a mixing bowl, add grated eggs, mayonnaise, grated carrots, green onion, curry powder, salt and pepper to taste, mix well. Serve over toasted bread.
Combine chicken pieces, garlic powder, black pepper, curry powder, ginger, soy sauce, cinnamon and salt in a large bowl. Let marinate for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle tempura batter mix over chicken, toss to coat lightly and evenly.
Heat your air fryer to 350°F. Cook chicken in batches until golden brown, about 4 minutes. Drain on paper towels.
Increase the air fryer temperature to 400°F. Cook Thai basil leaves until crisp, about 1 minute. Drain on paper towels.
Cook chicken a second time, 2 to 3 minutes per batch. Drain on paper towels. Serve with basil leaves.
In a large bowl, combine the chicken, pineapple and grapes.
In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise and curry; pour over chicken mixture; toss to coat.
Cover and refrigerate until serving.
Serve with bread.
In a small skillet bring water to a boil. Add eggs and cook until they are hard-boiled. Peel them and chop.
Put slices of bacon on a pan and cook until it's crispy.
Mash up the chopped egg a bit with a fork. Mix together eggs, mayonnaise, bacon, and onion.
Sprinkle with salt, pepper and curry powder to taste. Mix with a spoon.
Put a layer of lettuce on one slice of toast, spread the egg mixture on top of the lettuce, put another slice of lettuce and slice of toast on top.
In a mixing bowl, combine the diced chicken breast, diced apple, diced pineapple, and diced celery.
In a small bowl, mix the light mayonnaise and curry powder together until well combined.
Pour the curry mayo mixture over the chicken and fruit mixture.
Gently toss everything together until the chicken and fruit are evenly coated with the dressing.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Adjust the seasoning if needed.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Put oil and ghee in a large frying pan over medium high heat and melt ghee
Add ginger, garlic and onion to the frying pan and sauté for about 10 minutes until the onion becomes yellowish and very soft with some browning around the edge.
Add the remaining Curry Base ingredients and cook for another 1 minute, mixing the ingredients well.
Add water, potato and carrot to the curry base and cook for a couple of minutes. Add wine, turn the heat up and mix well.
When it starts boiling, add tamarind, salt, tomato paste, vegetable stock powder, red chili powder and mix.
Place a lid on and cook for about 7 minutes, then add zucchini.
Cook further 8 minutes or so with a lid on until the vegetables are cooked through and the curry becomes a béchamel sauce consistency
Mix the curry occasionally to ensure that it does not stick to the bottom
First, we'll bring a pot of water to boil.
Boil the noodles for about 1-2 minutes, and work to spread the noodles apart with chopsticks the entire time.
Then, we'll drain the noodles into a colander.
The goal is to get the noodles to be pretty dry, so shake the colander a few times to get excess water.
Continue separating the noodles with chopsticks for 30-60 seconds. Cover the colander with a plate.
This helps the noodles get to the perfect texture, allowing them to cook a bit longer without introducing excess moisture.
For the chicken, Cut the chicken into little strips. Place the strips in a bowl. Marinate the chicken with cornstarch and water.
Using our hands, we'll mix and massage the bowl for 20-30 seconds.
We'll also crack and beat eggs. For the shrimp, as long as they're peeled and deveined, we don't have to do anything to them.
After washing our vegetables, we'll be cutting carrots, onions, and celery into slices and strips.
For bell pepper, use several different colors of bell peppers, but if you want to keep it simple, you can just use an entire, single bell pepper.
Chop green onions  into roughly 1 inch pieces. We'll also be adding bean sprouts later as well.
The amounts and types of vegetables are really up to you. Anything goes with Singapore Noodles (with some caveats):
Try to avoid veggies with higher water content (like bok choy), since this will lead to a more soggy dish.
Try to avoid having an excessive amount of vegetables, since even lower water content vegetables will leak out water.
We'll create our sauce by mixing curry powder, oyster sauce, salt and water.
As written, the amount of curry powder my dad uses is pretty mild, so if you want a stronger flavor and/or more yellow finish, feel free to increase the amount of curry powder.
Curry powder is absolutely essential for Singapore noodles.

With so many options, the type of curry powder you use for this dish really impacts the final flavor profile, so you might want to experiment with several options until you land on something you love.
When cooking this dish, along with other noodle stir-fries, we'll want to get our wok really hot before adding oil.
Heat the wok for 2-3 minutes on high heat. While we wait, we'll continue loosening up our noodles. Once the wok is hot enough, add 1-2 tsp of oil.
Pour in the eggs: cook the eggs very gently, leaving the first side intact for 20-30 seconds before starting to flip and scramble the eggs.
We want the eggs to be really soft. In total, we're cooking the eggs for about a minute here, and tossing them back in later.
Transfer the eggs onto a plate, add oil: Next, we'll add another tbsp of a high smoke point oil like oil, and roll it around the pan.
Cook the chicken + shrimp: We'll be cooking the chicken now, so remix the chicken + marinade around with chopsticks for a few seconds.
Cook the chicken for 30-45 seconds before adding the shrimp.
Add the shrimp, and cook both meats until the shrimp starts to turn orange, and the chicken starts to turn white.
Transfer the meat onto a plate, add 1 tbsp of oil. Cook the vegetables for about 60-90 seconds.
Transfer the vegetables onto a plate, add 1 tbsp of oil. Cook the noodles for about 1-2 minutes before flipping them.
After flipping, cook the noodles for another 2-3 minutes, occasionally moving them around with your chopsticks.
Add the sauce and cook the noodles for another 2 minutes before adding everything else back in.
Add everything else in: Over the course of about 2 minutes, we'll be progressively adding the vegetables, followed by the chicken and shrimp, the char siu, bean sprouts, and lastly, some eggs.
Evenly mix the ingredients in well with the noodles. Add sesame oil, and mix everything for another 30-45 seconds.
In a pot over medium heat, add olive oil.
Sauté the chopped onion and minced garlic until they're softened and fragrant, for about 3-4 minutes.
Add the diced squash to the pot and continue cooking for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Sprinkle curry powder and ground turmeric over the squash mixture, stirring well to coat the ingredients with the spices.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat and let it simmer for about 15-20 minutes or until the squash becomes tender.
Using an immersion blender or a regular blender, blend the mixture until smooth.
Return the blended soup to the pot if using a regular blender.
Stir in the coconut milk and let the soup simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan or wok over a high heat. Add the onion and fry for 5 mins. Stir in the carrots, pepper, pak choi and cook for 5 mins more.
Add the garlic, curry powder and soy sauce, and cook for another minute.
Add the udon noodles and reserved brine, and stir in 2-3 tbsp hot water until the noodles are heated through. Divide between bowls and serve. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
In a medium skillet bring salted water to a boil. Add rice and cook according to package directions. Fluff with a fork and set aside.
In a medium bowl, toss the chicken with the oil and season with salt and pepper.
Heat a pan over medium heat and add 1 tbsp olive oil. Let the pan heat for 2 minutes then add the chicken and cook, stirring, until the chicken is cooked.
Add the onion, garlic, curry, and ginger and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion softens, about 2 minutes.
Add the carrots and cook 1 more minute until they've softened slightly. Stir in the celery, rice, peas and chopped pineapple.
Season the rice with salt and pepper and serve.
Combine sweet potatoes and beef broth in a pot; cook over medium-high heat until tender, 5 to 10 minutes.
Mash sweet potatoes into the broth using a potato masher.
Mix coconut milk, shallot, maple syrup, curry powder, sea salt, chili powder, and paprika into sweet potato mixture.
Cook and stir until heated through, 5 to 10 minutes. Reduce heat to low and blend soup with a hand blender until smooth.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a pan, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add minced garlic, diced onions, and bell peppers to the pan. Sauté for about 3-5 minutes until the vegetables are softened.
Add curry powder, turmeric, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper to the pan. Stir to coat the vegetables in the spices.
Add diced tomatoes and cooked chickpeas to the pan. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Pour in coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer. Let it simmer for about 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Taste and adjust the seasoning if necessary.
Once the curry has thickened and the flavors have melded, remove the pan from heat.
Transfer the curry to a serving bowl. Serve with rice.
Garnish with fresh cilantro.
Grease a nonstick pan, or add a little oil to prevent sticking. Saute tomatoes, spinach, and garlic over medium heat until lightly browned.
Drain the tofu, add it to the pan. Add the spices and salt. Cook, stirring occasionally until extra liquid has evaporated and it looks like scrambled eggs.
Stir in the spinach until it wilts, then serve.
hamburger buns: In a bowl, combine the ground turkey, finely chopped onion, minced garlic, dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Form the mixture into a turkey burger patty.
Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Cook the turkey burger patty in the preheated skillet for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until it's cooked through and browned.
In a separate bowl, combine the grated cooked beetroot, grated cucumber, red wine vinegar, salt, and pepper.
Mix everything together to make the beetroot relish.
Serve the burkey in you bun with the vegetables, patty and relish.
Preheat your grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
In a bowl, gently mix together the lean ground beef, crumbled blue cheese, salt, and pepper.
Form the mixture into a patty.
Grill the burger patty for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
While the burger is cooking, toast the whole wheat hamburger bun.
Once the burger is cooked, place it on the toasted bun.
Top with arugula.
Serve immediately.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Cook and stir lean ground beef in the hot skillet until some of the fat starts to render, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add onion and bell pepper; continue to cook until vegetables have softened and beef is cooked through, 3 to 5 more minutes.
Stir in ketchup, mustard, and garlic powder; season with salt and pepper. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes.
Load the hamburger bun.
In a large bowl, mix the ground beef, blue cheese, chives, hot pepper sauce, Worcestershire sauce, black pepper, salt, and mustard. Cover, and refrigerate for 2 hours.
Preheat grill for high heat. Gently form the burger mixture into about 12 patties.
Oil the grill grate. Grill patties 5 minutes per side, or until well done. Serve on rolls with lettuce, tomato and onion.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add meatless grounds and break up the clumps with a spoon until crumbly. Cook until browned and heated through, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to low. Add ketchup, brown sugar, apple cider vinegar, ground mustard, dill, and pepper.
Mix well, cover, and let simmer until heated through, 3 to 4 minutes. Adjust seasonings if necessary. Serve on hamburger buns.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F
Divide the ground beef into a 1/4 pound patty.
Season both sides of the patty with salt and pepper.
Place the hamburger bun in the air fryer basket, cut side down, and the seasoned beef patty beside it.
Air fry the hamburger bun and beef patty at 375°F for 8-10 minutes, flipping the patty halfway through, until the beef is cooked to your desired level of doneness and the bun is toasted.
Once the beef patty is cooked, place the slice of cheese on top of it and return it to the air fryer for an additional 1-2 minutes to melt the cheese.
Assemble your cheeseburger by placing the beef patty with melted cheese on the toasted bun.
Add any desired toppings such as lettuce, tomato, onion, pickles, ketchup, or mustard.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add bacon and cook on both sides until slightly crispy or to desired crispness.
Set aside on paper towels to drain.
Divide meat into 4 equal parts and press loosely into patties about 3/4 inch thick.
Lightly season with salt and pepper.
Heat grill to medium-high or a heavy cast-iron skillet works great for this too.
Cook burgers about 5 minutes then gently flip over.
While burgers are cooking, split open buns and gently toast.
Place cheese on cooked side of burgers and cover with lid.
Continue cooking for 3 to 4 minutes or to desired doneness.
Assemble cheeseburgers on bottom buns and top with cooked bacon, tomato slices and lettuce.
Finish with top buns and enjoy.

In a skillet over medium-high heat, add the lean ground beef and diced onion. Cook and break apart the beef with a spatula as it browns, usually for about 5-7 minutes or until it's fully cooked and crumbled.
Season the cooked beef and onions with salt and black pepper to taste. Mix well.
While the beef is cooking, you can lightly toast the hamburger bun in a toaster or oven if desired.
Once the beef is cooked and seasoned, remove it from the heat.
Place the loose meat mixture onto the bottom half of the hamburger bun.
Top with sliced pickles.
Place the top half of the bun over the pickles to make a sandwich.
Preheat your grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Season the ground lamb with salt and black pepper to taste, and form it into a patty.
Lightly grease the grill or grill pan with cooking spray or olive oil.
Place the lamb patty on the grill and cook for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Toast the hamburger bun on the grill for about 1-2 minutes, until lightly golden.
Assemble the burger by placing the cooked lamb patty on the bottom half of the toasted bun. Add cheese.
Add lettuce, tomato slices, onion slices, and cucumber slices on top of the patty, if desired.
Place the top half of the bun on top of the toppings.
Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the fish fillet with salt and pepper.
Dip the fish fillet into the beaten egg, ensuring it's coated on all sides.
Then, coat the fish fillet with breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Place the breaded fish fillet on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for about 10-12 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the breadcrumbs are golden brown.
While the fish is baking, lightly toast the hamburger bun in a toaster or on a skillet.
Spread tartar sauce on the bottom half of the toasted bun.
Place the cooked fish filet on top of the tartar sauce.
Top the fish with a slice of cheese.
Finihs building the burger with lettuce, onion and tomato.
Close the sandwich with the top half of the hamburger bun.
Preheat the grill to high heat.
In a large bowl, mash the bread and milk together with a fork until it forms a chunky paste.
Add the salt, pepper, garlic, Worcestershire sauce and ketchup and mix well.
Add the ground beef and scallions and break the meat up with your hands.
Mix everything together until evenly combined.
Divide the mixture into 8 equal portions and form compact balls.
Flatten the balls into 3/4-inch patties about 4-1/2 inches across.
Form a slight depression in the center of each patty to prevent the burgers from puffing up on the grill.
Oil the grilling grates.
Grill the burgers, covered, until nicely browned on the first side, 2 to 4 minutes.
Flip burgers and continue cooking for a few minutes more until desired doneness is reached.
Before serving, toast the buns on the cooler side of the grill if desired. Build the burger.
Combine onion and both bell peppers in the bowl of a food processor, pulse until blended into a uniform size but not liquid.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onion-bell pepper mixture, carrot, and zucchini. Cook and stir until soft and heated through, 6 to 8 minutes.
Add broth and lentils; bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered and stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender, about 15 minutes.
Stir in tomatoes, chili powder, mustard, vinegar, maple syrup, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer until thickened, about 10 minutes. Serve on buns.
Season pork with salt, pepper, and any desired spices, then cook it in a pressure cooker for about 45 minutes or until tender. Shred the cooked pork with a fork.
Toast the whole wheat hamburger bun.
Assemble the burger by placing lettuce and the shredded pork on the bottom half of the bun.
Drizzle with barbecue sauce if desired.
Cover with the top half of the bun.
Preheat an outdoor grill for high heat and lightly oil the grate.
Combine ground beef, Worcestershire sauce, liquid smoke, and garlic powder in a medium bowl, lightly mix until just combined.
With minimal handling, form mixture into a patty. Brush oil onto both sides of patty, then season with salt.
Cook patty on the preheated grill until no longer pink in the center, about 5 minutes per side.
Construct the burger with the remaining ingredients.
Prepare the keto burger buns.
Brush a batch of buns with one egg yolk mixed with a tbsp of water.
In a bowl, mix the ingredients for the burger patties: beef, minced garlic, onion powder, apple cider vinegar, sea salt and black pepper.
Using your hands, create 4 equal patties, ½ to ¾ inch (1¼ to 25/32 inch) thick. Pierce the patties with a fork several times.
This will loosen the patties and help them cook equally without curling or getting tough while enabling maximum caramelization. And set aside.
Prepare the burger sauce by mixing the mayonnaise, ketchup, pickle juice, salt and pepper. And set aside.
To brown the keto burger buns, cut them widthwise.
Brush them with melted butter and cook in a hot skillet cut side down for about a minute or until browned and crispy. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
Grease the hot skillet with a tbsp of ghee and add the burger patties.
Cook over high heat, until a crust forms on the bottom and the burgers are easy to flip on the other side. 2-3 min
Once flipped, cook until the crust forms on the bottom and the sides are browned. 2-3 min
Cook the patties. The thicker the patties, the longer they will take to cook. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
In the same pan, cook the bacon slices.
Cut each of the slices lengthwise and cook until browned and crispy.
To assemble the burger: Spread about 2 tsp of the burger sauce on each bun halve.
Top each the bottom half with 4 slices of pickles and add a quarter of the shredded lettuce.
Add a burger patty, 2 onion slices, 1 slice of tomato and 1 slice of cheese.
Place under a broiler for a minute until the cheese is melted. Otherwise, you can add the cheese directly on top of the burger patty while still hot.
Finally, top with 2 slices of cooked bacon and add the top half of the burger bun.
Enjoy! Serve with leftover burger sauce, or low-carb ketchup and homemade mustard.
Optionally, serve with more pickles.
In a bowl, thoroughly mix minced tuna, bread crumbs, green onion, carrot, ginger, cilantro, flaxseed oil, tomato sauce, soy sauce, cumin, salt, pepper, and egg.
Cover, and refrigerate for 30 minutes and preheat the oven broiler.
Form tuna mixture into 4 patties, and place in a broiler pan on top oven rack.
Cook, uncovered, 4 minutes per side, or until easily flaked with a fork. Serve on hamburger buns with lettuce and tomato.
almond extract: Stir together almond milk, chia, maple syrup and almond extract in a small bowl. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

When ready to serve, stir the pudding well. Spoon about half the pudding into a serving glass and top with half the blueberries and almonds. Add the rest of the pudding and top with the remaining blueberries and almonds.
Mash half the banana in a saucepan. Whisk almond milk, almond extract, cinnamon, and salt with the mashed banana until smooth; bring to a boil and stir oats into the mixture.
Reduce heat to medium-low and cook at a simmer until the oats are tender and the moisture has been absorbed to your desired consistency, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer oatmeal to a bowl.
Dice remaining banana half. Top oatmeal with banana and more cinnamon, as desired.
In a large bowl, whisk flour, baking powder, salt and cinnamon. In another bowl, whisk almond milk, half of the ricotta, egg yolks, lemon juice and extract until blended.
Add to dry ingredients, stirring just until moistened. In a clean small bowl, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry. Fold into batter. Gently stir in half of the raspberries.

Lightly grease a griddle, heat over medium heat. Pour batter by ¼ cup full onto griddle. Cook until bubbles on top begin to pop and bottoms are golden brown. Turn and cook until second side is golden brown.
Serve with maple cream, additional raspberries and ricotta.
vegetable bouillon: Melt ghee in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Stir in orzo and garlic and saute until lightly browned.
Add spinach and season with dried Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Mix water into the saucepan. Stir in vegetable bouillon until dissolved. Top with Parmesan and cover, reduce heat, and simmer 12 minutes.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil and add rice. Cook according to instructions and set aside.
Bring water to boil in a saucepan. Throw in spinach, gently stir to ensure its well soaked in water. Cook for about a minute, strain and let cool.
In a medium saute pan, heat oil. Add in onion and let cook until translucent and starts to turn slightly brown.
Add in garlic, spinach and let cook for 5 minutes, then mix with sauce. Let cook on medium heat for about 10 minutes.
Rinse and pad dry salmon with paper towel and season with salt. Heat stove top grill, drizzle olive oil and sear skinless side of salmon for 3 minutes. Flip and cook for another 3 minutes.
Serve on spinach stew with rice.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile heat the oil in a very large nonstick frying pan, add the onions and ginger, and cook for 5 mins. Add the curry powder and allspice.
Pour in the tomatoes plus a can of water, the coconut milk, thyme and bouillon.
Add the sweet potatoes, beans and peppers, cover the pan and simmer for 20-25 mins or until all the vegetables are tender.
Heat  3/4 vegan ghee in a medium-sized pot over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and cook until onion is soft and translucent, 3 to 5 minutes.
Add caraway seeds, nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper and cook for another minute. Add water and mix in bouillon.
Bring soup to a boil, about 5 minutes. Add potatoes, adjusting water as needed to cover potatoes by a few inches.
Reduce heat to a simmer and cook until potatoes are soft, but not mushy, 15 to 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, toss cubed bread in a pan with remaining vegan ghee over medium heat.
Sprinkle salt, pepper, oregano, onion powder, and garlic powder on top.
Cook, shaking the pan occasionally to cook evenly, until crouton are crisped, about 10 minutes. Serve with soup and garnish with parsley.
Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Cook and stir rice and onion in the hot oil until browned, about 5 minutes, season with salt, cumin, and pepper.
Pour water over the rice mixture. Stir tomato sauce and chicken bouillon into the water.
Increase heat to medium-high, place a cover on the saucepan, and bring to a boil.
Add serrano chile pepper and continue cooking at a boil for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium-low until the rice is tender and the water is absorbed, 10 to 15 minutes more.
Combine water and bouillon in a pot; bring to a boil. Boil until bouillon cubes have disintegrated fully.
Reduce heat, add scallions, and simmer for 30 seconds. Add kale and tofu, cook until kale has softened to desired texture, 3 to 5 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a large soup pot over medium-high heat; cook onion and garlic until soft, 2 to 3 minutes.
Stir in kale and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes. Stir in water, potatoes, beans, tomatoes, vegetable bouillon, parsley, and Italian seasoning.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until potatoes are cooked through, about 25 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
After peeling, very thinly slice the ginger.
This helps the flavor come out, and also allows the ginger to become soft so it can be eaten along with the soup.
The shallots need peeled, and can be roughly sliced or diced.
Add the avocado oil to a pot over medium heat, and add the ginger and shallots.
Saute for about 5 minutes to start releasing the flavors.
Add the water and vegetable bouillon, and gently boil for about 20 minutes.
Do not replace the water that evaporates.
Adding the coconut milk will add more volume without diluting the flavors of the soup.
Add the thinly sliced chicken, and allow it to poach in the broth for about 5 minutes.
Add the carrots, asparagus, and mushrooms, and continue to simmer for 5-10 minutes.
While the soup is finishing, add the chili sauce or chilis, salt, and optional fish sauce (I omit the fish sauce most often) to taste.
Add additional water if the soup is too thick for your tastes.
Serve garnished with lime wedges and cilantro.
This soup is great served with bread.
Put mayocoba beans into a large container with enough cool water to cover by several inches. Let beans soak 8 hours to overnight.
Drain mayocoba beans and rinse thoroughly, put into a large stockpot.
Pour enough water into the pot to cover beans by a few inches, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and place a cover on the pot, and cook at a simmer until beans are soft in the center, about 90 minutes.
While the beans simmer, heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook and stir onion in hot oil until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes and serrano pepper and simmer until the tomatoes have softened slightly, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium-low and continue cooking until the liquid begins to thicken, about 10 minutes more. Remove from heat.
Cook and stir bacon in a large, deep skillet over medium-high heat until very crispy, about 10 minutes.
Remove bacon from skillet with a slotted spoon and drain on a plate lined with paper towel.
Cook ham cubes in the bacon fat in the skillet until browned, about 5 minutes, remove with slotted spoon to the plate with the bacon to drain.
Once the beans have cooked, stir the tomato mixture, bacon, ham, beer, jalapeno peppers, cilantro, and bouillon granules into the beans, season with sea salt.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the beans are completely softened, about 30 minutes.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add celery and garlic; saute until slightly softened, about 4 minutes.
Add water and bouillon cubes; stir until bouillon dissolves. Add soy milk, nutritional yeast, potatoes, and onion. Simmer for 15 to 20 minutes.
Add all corn, parsley, paprika, and pepper. Simmer until potatoes are soft and flavors have blended, 30 to 40 minutes more.
Spray the inside of a stockpot with oil, place over medium heat. Cook and stir garlic in the stockpot until lightly browned, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add onion, carrot and about 2 tablespoons vegetable broth to garlic; cook and stir until onion is translucent, 5 to 10 minutes.
Stir kale into onion mixture; cook until slightly wilted, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add chickpeas, remaining vegetable broth, vegetable bouillon, and curry powder, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer until heated through, about 15 minutes. Add almond milk and cook until heated through, 2 to 3 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Place a rack in the bottom of a large roasting pan.
Strip husks from corn and set aside for tamales. Remove kernels from one ear for filling. Reserve other ears for another use.
Prepare filling. Heat coconut oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and saute until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add black beans, diced tomatoes and salsa. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes.
Stir in reserved corn kernels and cook for 3 minutes. Remove from the heat.
Prepare dough: Combine 3 cups water and bouillon in a small bowl, stir to dissolve.
Mix masa, baking powder, and salt together in a large bowl. Stir in melted coconut oil until well combined.
Mix in bouillon. Add more water if needed to make a soft, spongy dough.
Smooth a 3-inch square of dough onto a corn husk. Place 1 to 2 tablespoons filling into the center of the dough.
Fold the sides of the husk in over the filling, then repeat with the top and bottom, you may need to use more than one husk per tamale.
Use thin pieces of husk as string to tie tamale together; it doesn't have to be perfect. Repeat to make remaining tamales.
Set tamales on the prepared rack. Pour about 2 cups water into the bottom of the roasting pan, making sure tamales are well above the water.
Cover the pan tightly with several sheets of aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for 35 to 45 minutes. Remove from the oven and let sit, untouched, for 15 to 20 minutes before serving.
Heat oil in a large pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add tortilla strips in batches and fry until brown and crispy, 2 to 3 minutes per batch.
Fry pasilla chiles in the same pan, being careful not to burn them, until browned, about 2 minutes. Remove from oil and drain on paper towels.
Combine 1 fried pasilla pepper, tomatoes, onion, garlic, and oregano in a blender, blend until smooth. Strain through a sieve into a bowl.
Heat 1 teaspoon oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add tomato mixture and cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in water and bring to a boil. Stir in chicken granules and parsley.
Cover and simmer over low heat for about 15 minutes. Remove from heat and discard parsley.
Divide fried tortilla strips among 4 bowls. Pour soup on top of tortilla strips and garnish with diced avocado and cotija cheese.
Cut remaining fried pasilla peppers into thin strips and sprinkle over soup. Add Mexican crema to each bowl.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain, reserve 1 cup of the pasta water and set aside.
Heat oil in a large covered skillet over medium heat. Add the asparagus and cook for a minute or two. Add ¼ cup water and quickly cover.
Allow the asparagus to steam for 2 minutes. Uncover and add the mushrooms. Add a splash of water if it seems dry and cover. Cook for about 2 minutes.
Uncover, add the garlic, and cook for a minute or two. Add the vegetables to the pasta and combine well. Keep warm on the lowest heat setting.
Put pasta water into a small saucepan. Place it over medium high heat and add the bouillion, basil and nutritional yeast.
As it comes to a boil, whisk together the milk and arrowroot in a small bowl and add it to the saucepan. Cook, stirring, until it boils and thickens slightly.
Pour it over the pasta and vegetables, and stir to coat. Serve immediately.
Saute carrots, celery, onion, leek, and shallot in a soup pot over medium-high heat until onion is translucent and carrots lose their crunch, 7 to 10 minutes.
Pour broth over vegetables in the pot and bring to a rolling boil. Dissolve bouillon into broth, reduce heat to a simmer.
Add parsley, bay leaves, thyme, nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper. Cook until carrots are soft, about 10 minutes more.
Add pasta and seitan and cook until pasta is al dente, another 7 to 10 minutes.
sourdough bread: For the hollandaise, put the egg yolks, 2 tbsp butter, lemon juice and a tablespoon of cold water water into a heavy-based pan and place over the lowest possible heat.
Using a balloon whisk, immediately begin whisking until the butter has melted and has combined with the egg yolks and liquid.
Once the butter has completely melted, turn up the heat ever so slightly and keep whisking continuously until the sauce has thickened.
For the poached egg and asparagus, bring a pan of salted water to the boil and cook the asparagus in the boiling water for 2 minutes.
Reduce the heat to the lowest setting, until the water is barely simmering. Crack the eggs into a cup and gently lower into the water, tipping out the eggs.
Cook for 3 minutes, or until the whites have set and the yolks are cooked to your liking. Remove and drain on kitchen paper.
In a medium skillet heat butter and add asparagus and sliced mushrooms. Season with salt and pepper and cook for 5 minutes.
Toast the bread and top with mushrooms, asparagus, eggs and hollandaise sauce.
In a small bowl, stir together cheese, basil and pepper until thoroughly combined.
In a medium bowl, stir together tomatoes, olive oil, salt and basil.
Slice bread on slices and toast it in toaster until golden brown. Rub 1 side of each toast slice with cut side of garlic. Brush with remaining olive oil.
Just before serving, spread toast slices with cheese mixture and top with tomato mixture. Sprinkle remaining basil on top.
Heat  3/4 vegan ghee in a medium-sized pot over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and cook until onion is soft and translucent, 3 to 5 minutes.
Add caraway seeds, nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper and cook for another minute. Add water and mix in bouillon.
Bring soup to a boil, about 5 minutes. Add potatoes, adjusting water as needed to cover potatoes by a few inches.
Reduce heat to a simmer and cook until potatoes are soft, but not mushy, 15 to 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, toss cubed bread in a pan with remaining vegan ghee over medium heat.
Sprinkle salt, pepper, oregano, onion powder, and garlic powder on top.
Cook, shaking the pan occasionally to cook evenly, until crouton are crisped, about 10 minutes. Serve with soup and garnish with parsley.
Soak cashews in a bowl of water, 8 hours to overnight.
Drain cashews and discard water. Place cashews, nutritional yeast, and salt in the bowl of a food processor.
Blend until smooth and creamy, 10 to 15 minutes. Add yogurt, mix until well combined. Stir in chopped chives. Serve on top of sourdough bread.
In a small mixing bowl, combine the two cheeses, add pepper to taste and mix well.
Spread ¾ tbsp butter on one side of each slice of bread. With butter side down, top each slice of bread with ½ cup cheddar.
In a skillet over medium heat, melt the remaining butter. Add two slices of bread, butter side down. Cook until bread is golden and cheese is starting to melt, about 2 minutes.
Flip one piece of bread on top of the other and continue to cook until cheese is runny, about 30 seconds more.
Repeat for the second sandwich, wiping skillet clean if necessary. Cut the sandwich in half and enjoy.
Place bacon pieces in a large Dutch oven pot over medium heat and cook until bacon is crisp and the fat is rendered out.
Remove bacon pieces and set aside, leaving the fat in the pot. Add butter and chopped onion, cook until onions are tender, about 4 minutes.
Add garlic and continue to cook for another 30 seconds, sprinkle flour over the ingredients in the pot and stir until smooth.
Add the diced potatoes along with chicken broth, milk, heavy cream, salt and pepper, stir well.
Bring to a boil and cook until potatoes are tender when pierced with a fork, about 10 minutes.
Using a hand blender, puree the soup until it thickens, simmer for an additional 15 minutes.
Serve topped with bacon, scallions and bread.
Heat a frying pan, add the bacon and fry until golden and crispy. Take the bacon out of the pan with a draining spoon and set aside.
Afterwards, poach the eggs–pour boiling water (4 in in depth) into a small pan, over high heat. Add a dash of vinegar. When water boils again, break egg into pan.
As the water comes back to the boil, gather egg white round yolk, using a draining spoon. Turn down heat. Simmer for a minute for a soft egg, longer for a firmer one.
Lift the egg out of the pan with the draining spoon and set aside.
To a small mixing bowl, add the olive oil, white wine vinegar and mustard, add salt and pepper to taste and mix well.
To serve, divide the greens between to serving plates, add the bread, place two poached eggs on each slice of bread, add the avocado. Drizzle with olive oil dressing before serving.
Heat the oil in a fry pan over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant, 30-60 seconds.
Add the crumbled tofu, almond milk and all spices (yeast, onion powder, salt, turmeric powder and black pepper), stir to combine.
Cook for about 6-8 more minutes or until the tofu is heated all the way through and the milk has evaporated.
Taste the scramble, and adjust seasonings.
Serve over toasted bread alongside cherry tomatoes.
Toast bread in a toaster, toaster oven, or beneath a broiler, set aside. Heat 1 teaspoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.

Add kale, stir to coat, then cook, stirring occasionally, until the kale begins to soften, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic, teaspoon salt, teaspoon pepper, and red pepper flakes, stir and cook 1 additional minute.
Remove from heat, stir in the feta cheese, then cover to keep warm. In a small skillet, heat the remaining teaspoon olive oil over medium.
Gently crack eggs into skillet and sprinkle with a little extra salt and pepper. Cook until whites are nearly set, about 1 minute.
Cover skillet, remove from heat, and let stand until whites are set but yolks are still soft, about 3 minutes. Place the kale spread on top of toast, then top with a fried egg, serve immediately.
cayenne pepper: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
In a blender, combine the onion, ginger, and garlic and blend into a smooth puree with 2 tbsp or more of water.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and cook for 1 minute. Add bay leaf and cook until the bay leaf changes color.
Add the pureed onion and cook until the onion mixture is dry and does not smell raw. Stir occasionally to avoid sticking, 4 minutes.
Add cinnamon, garam masala and cayenne and mix well. Add the coconut milk, mango pulp, salt, and mix well.
Mix, cover and cook until the sauce comes to a boil, 5 minutes. Add chickpeas and mix in. Reduce the heat to low medium and cook for 10 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf and discard. Serve with rice.
In a large mixing bowl, mix together the raw cabbage, onion, and almond flour.
In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs, soy sauce, pepper, and garlic. Stir the egg mixture into the cabbage mixture. Mix well.
Heat a nonstick pan over medium heat and add 2 tbsp olive oil. Scoop the mixture onto the pan and flatten with a spatula.
Cook the pancakes until browned on both sides, about 3 minutes per side.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Place the parsley in the bowl of a food processor and pulse until finely chopped, remove and set aside.
In the same food processor bowl used to chop the parsley, place the quartered onion and pulse until finely chopped.
Drain the onions slightly to release excess moisture. Return the parsley back to the bowl of the food processor with the onions.
Add the ground beef to the food processor in addition to all the spices.
Run the food processor on pulse once or twice until ingredients are well combined and form a pasty meat mixture.
Transfer the mixture from the food processor to a large bowl and form the Kafta into an oblong shape about 1 inch in thickness.
You can form them on a wooden skewer or without. Grease the grates of a gas grill or indoor grill pan, and preheat it to medium-high heat.
When heated through, transfer the kafta to the grill. Grill for 4-5 minutes per side, depending on your preferred level of doneness.
Serve hot off the grill with rice and dipping yogurt.
Preheat your oven to 400°F. Grease a glass or ceramic baking dish.
Wash and dry the mushrooms. Use a paring knife to gently remove the stems.
In a medium bowl, use a rubber spatula to thoroughly mix the cream cheese with the salt and the spices. Mix in the gruyere.
Spoon the mixture into the mushroom caps.
Place the mushrooms in the prepared baking dish. Bake until the cheese has melted and the mushrooms are tender, 10 minutes.
Add olive oil to a large pot over medium heat. Stir in diced onion and minced garlic. Cook until the onion starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Next, stir in Arborio rice and toss to coat the rice in oil. Allow the rice to toast for 1 minute.
Stir in miso paste, fresh thyme leaves, ground sage, pumpkin, ground nutmeg, and pepper.
Add white wine and stir continuously until the wine is mostly absorbed into the rice.
Once most of the liquid is absorbed, begin adding in the vegetable broth in ½ cup increments.
Stir continuously until most of the liquid is absorbed before adding in the next ½ cup of broth. Cook for 20 minutes.
Then stir in canned pumpkin puree, almonds, fresh spinach and fresh lemon juice. Stir until the spinach wilts down.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the leek and saute for 3 minutes.
Then add the asparagus and saute another 2 minutes. Then add the frozen peas, carots, potatoes, salt and pepper, and saute another minute.
In a large bowl, whisk the eggs, yogurt, salt and pepper. Pour the eggs over the half of the vegetables in the pan. Cook on the stove for 3 minutes.
Add the remaining vegetables on top, then dollop with the cheese. Transfer to the oven and cook for 15 minutes.
Garnish with dill before serving.

In a food processor, combine the drained and rinsed chickpeas, 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon juice, 1 tablespoon of tahini, minced garlic clove, 1 tablespoon of extra-virgin olive oil, 1/4 teaspoon of ground cumin, and a pinch of salt.
Process the mixture until smooth, which may take 1 to 2 minutes. If it's too thick, you can add 1 to 2 tablespoons of water while the food processor is running to reach your desired consistency.
Taste the hummus and adjust the seasoning by adding more salt or lemon juice if needed.
Once the hummus is smooth and well-seasoned, transfer it to a small bowl.
Preheat your oven to 425°F
In a mixing bowl, toss the cauliflower florets with 2 tablespoons of olive oil until they are evenly coated.

Add 1/2 teaspoon of ground cumin, 1/2 teaspoon of paprika, 1/4 teaspoon of cayenne pepper (adjust to your desired level of spiciness), salt, and black pepper to taste. Toss the cauliflower to coat it in the spices and olive oil.
Spread the seasoned cauliflower in a single layer on a baking sheet.

Roast in the preheated oven for approximately 20-25 minutes or until the cauliflower is tender and has golden brown edges. Make sure to stir or flip the cauliflower halfway through the roasting time for even cooking.
Once roasted, serve the spiced cauliflower with a side of hummus.
Add egg yolks, lemon juice, salt, and cayenne to a blender or food processor and pulse until combined.
Slowly drizzle in the butter and continue to run the blender until the sauce thickens.
Bring a large pot with a few inches of water to a slight simmer. Add a dash of vinegar to the pot.
Let eggs poach in not-quite-boiling water for 3 minutes, or until the whites of the eggs are set and the centers are still soft.
Toast the slices of bread. Top bread with sliced avocado, salad mix, poached egg. Spoon hollandaise sauce over and serve.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil, cook the rotini at a boil until tender yet firm to the bite, about 10 minutes, drain.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground turkey and break it up with a wooden spoon.
Add chili powder, garlic, cumin, cayenne pepper, oregano, and black pepper, mix thoroughly.
Cook and stir until turkey is browned and crumbly, 5 to 7 minutes.
Add corn, diced tomatoes and chiles, green bell pepper, onion, celery, and carrot; mix thoroughly.
Place lid on skillet. Cook until vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally, about 8 minutes.
Add tomato sauce, let simmer until thickened, about 5 minutes. Transfer turkey mixture to a casserole dish and add the cooked pasta.
Mix everything together. Top with cheese. Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted, about 10 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Preheat your grill to medium-high heat, around 375-400°F.
In a small bowl, combine the olive oil, paprika, cayenne pepper, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
In a small bowl, combine the olive oil, paprika, cayenne pepper, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Brush the shrimp skewer with the spice and olive oil mixture, making sure to coat them evenly.
Place the shrimp skewer on the preheated grill.
Grill the shrimp for about 2-3 minutes per side, or until they turn pink and have grill marks. Cooking times may vary depending on the size of the shrimp, so keep an eye on them.
Once the shrimp are cooked through and slightly charred, remove them from the grill.
Combine chili powder, thyme, smoked paprika, oregano, cayenne pepper, cumin, onion powder, and salt in a bowl.
Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium heat, cook and stir onion in hot ghee until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook until soft, about 2 minutes, stir shredded cabbage and sliced carrot into onion and garlic.
Cook and stir cabbage mixture until cabbage starts to soften, about 5 more minutes.
Stir spices into cabbage mixture and cook, stirring often, until spices are evenly mixed in and cabbage is tender, 3 more minutes.
In a bowl, combine the Greek yogurt, tomato paste, grated ginger, minced garlic, garam masala, ground turmeric, ground cumin, cayenne pepper, and salt. Mix well to form a marinade.
Add the chicken pieces to the marinade and toss to coat evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours.
In a skillet, heat the ghee over medium heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
Serve the chicken hot with naan bread.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Add onions to a large bowl and separate. Add in all the spices, salt, chickpea flour and rice flour. Sprinkle in the oil and mix in.
Add in water, just a little bit at a time so that you get a pasty pakora mix. Take spoonfuls of this mixture and place on a parchment lined baking sheet.
Bake for 17 minutes. Once the pakoras are cooked, let them cool for a few minutes.
Mix tomato paste, vinegar, onion powder, oregano and some salt in a bowl. Serve with pakoras.
In a bowl, combine the chicken pieces with the ground cumin, paprika, ground coriander, ground turmeric, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper. Make sure the chicken is well-coated with the spices.
Thread the seasoned chicken pieces onto a skewer.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, around 375-400°F.
Brush the chicken kebabs with olive oil.
Grill the chicken kebabs for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are cooked through and have nice grill marks.
While the chicken is grilling, prepare the garlic yogurt sauce. In a small bowl, mix together the Greek yogurt, minced garlic, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Once the chicken kebabs are cooked, remove them from the grill.
Serve the Spiced Chicken Kebabs with the Garlic Yogurt Sauce on the side.
Preheat the air fryer to 360°F for 3-5 minutes.
Slice about 1/2 inch off the top of the onion and peel the outer skin off. Cut about 1/2 inch off the root end of the onion, ensuring the onion sits flat on the cutting board.
Use a sharp knife to make vertical cuts about 1/4 inch apart from the top of the onion down, stopping about 1/2 inch from the root end. Make 4-6 cuts around the onion to create "petals."
In a small bowl, mix together breadcrumbs, olive oil, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cayenne pepper (if using), salt, and pepper.
Carefully open the "petals" of the onion and sprinkle the breadcrumb mixture between them, ensuring it gets into each layer.
Lightly coat the onion with non-stick cooking spray.
Place the onion in the air fryer basket.
Air fry for 20-25 minutes, until the onion is golden brown and crispy, and the petals are tender.
Serve the air fryer blooming onion hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook ground beef until browned and cooked through.
Add beef broth, tomato sauce, apple cider vinegar, cocoa powder, cinnamon, cumin, allspice, cayenne pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for about 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until flavors are well combined and sauce thickens.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the Cincinnati chili over cooked spaghetti.
Top with shredded cheddar cheese, chopped onion, and/or kidney beans.

In a bowl, combine soy sauce, lime juice, grated ginger, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, cayenne pepper (if using), salt, and pepper. Add the chicken strips to the marinade and toss to coat. Let marinate for at least 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

While the chicken is marinating, prepare the peanut sauce. In a small saucepan over low heat, combine peanut butter, coconut milk, soy sauce, lime juice, grated ginger, honey (if using), and cayenne pepper (if using). Stir until well combined and smooth. If the sauce is too thick, add water gradually to reach the desired consistency. Remove from heat and set aside.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat. Thread the marinated chicken strips onto the soaked bamboo skewers.
Grill the chicken skewers for about 2-3 minutes per side, or until cooked through and slightly charred.
Serve the grilled chicken satay with the homemade peanut sauce on the side for dipping.
Stir shrimp, oil, cilantro, juice, garlic, tequila, cayenne pepper, and salt in a bowl. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate shrimp in marinade for 15 minutes.
Preheat an outdoor grill for high heat and lightly oil grate. Remove shrimp from bowl and thread onto skewers. Discard marinade.
Cook on the preheated grill until shrimp turn pink, 2 minutes per side. Serve immediately.
Heat a small amount of cooking oil in a medium-sized pot over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced chorizo sausage to the pot and sauté for about 3-4 minutes until it's browned and has released some of its flavorful oils. Remove the chorizo from the pot and set it aside.
In the same pot, add a bit more oil if needed. Sauté the diced onions, red bell pepper, and green bell pepper for about 3-4 minutes until they become tender.
Add the diced potatoes to the pot and cook for another 2-3 minutes, allowing them to start softening.
Return the cooked chorizo to the pot and add the canned diced tomatoes, chicken or vegetable broth, paprika, and cayenne pepper. Stir to combine.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Simmer the Chorizo Potato Stew for about 15-20 minutes or until the potatoes are tender and the flavors meld together.
Once the stew is ready, transfer it to a serving bowl.
Heat oil in a pot over medium heat.
Sauté diced onion, red bell pepper, and minced garlic until softened.
Stir in ground cumin, chili powder, and cayenne pepper.
Let the soup simmer for about 10-15 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Optionally, use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup for a smoother texture.
Serve hot.
red cabbage: Place the veg stock, garlic and ginger in a blender and whizz until smooth.
If your blender isn't big enough, do this in two batches and make sure to keep the lid on tight.
Pour the blended stock into a medium-sized pot and bring to the boil.
Once it's boiling, add the noodles and cook as per the packet instructions.
Chop the button mushrooms or roughly tear the oyster mushrooms.
Add the oil to a frying pan set on a high heat, then add the mushrooms along with the soy sauce and cook for 3 minutes, stirring regularly.
Once the noodles are cooked, remove them from from the stock with a slotted spoon and divide them between four large bowls, then pour over the broth.
Now layer up the bowls by placing some of the red pepper strips, grated carrot, red cabbage and spring onions in each bowl, along with some beansprouts.
Each bowl should be very colourful and rainbow-like in terms of colour.
Top with the fried mushrooms, then top each bowl with a sprinkle of sesame seeds and serve with a lime wedge.
Add boiled eggs, 1 per serving.
In a large bowl, mix ginger, garlic, soy sauce, and sesame oil.
Place tofu in the mixture, and marinate 1 hour in the refrigerator.
Toss the carrots, cabbage and salad with the tofu, and marinade to serve.
Season the chicken leg with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken leg to the skillet and cook for about 5-6 minutes on each side or until cooked through and no longer pink in the center. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add shredded red cabbage, sliced red onion, minced garlic, ground cumin, and paprika. Cook for about 5 minutes or until the vegetables are tender.
Pour in broth and stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Return the cooked chicken leg to the skillet and let it simmer in the cabbage mixture for a few minutes.
Combine shrimp, cabbage, red cabbage, carrot, mint, coriander, 2 tablespoons lime juice and fish sauce in a large bowl.
Place 1 rice paper round in a medium bowl of lukewarm water for 15 seconds or until just soft. Place on a clean tea towel or paper towel
Arrange 1/4 cup of the shrimp mixture along the centre of rice paper round. Fold ends in and roll up firmly to enclose filling. Repeat with remaining rice paper rounds and filling.
To cook the shrimp, heat a skillet over medium-high heat and add olive oil. Add the large, peeled and deveined shrimp to the hot oil.
To season the shrimp, add garlic powder, chili powder, cayenne pepper, ground black pepper, sea salt, and granulated onion to the pan and stir to evenly cover the shrimp with the seasoning.
Cook the shrimp, stirring frequently, until the shrimp is golden on the outside and no longer transparent on the inside.
Lay a corn tortilla flat on a clean surface. Pile several shrimp onto the center of the tortilla, followed by the pico de gallo, and then drizzle guacamole over the top.
Oil a skillet over medium-high heat and cook the chicken for 7 to 10 minutes.
Meanwhile cook the noodles according to package directions. Drain, run under cold water to cool, then transfer back to pot.
Mix together ginger, soy sauce, canola oil and hot sauce to make the dressing. Toss the noodles with dressing, then fold in the chicken.
Add red cabbage and tomatoes. Serve with crushed peanuts and dress with parsley.
Add shredded cabbage and carrots to a large bowl.
Stir together mayonnaise, apple cider vinegar, salt and pepper in a separate bowl, then combine.
Toss well to coat the veggies, then serve.
Combine cabbage, carrot, mayonnaise, cranberries, walnuts, milk, cider and vinegar in a bowl; stir well. Cover and refrigerate until chilled, about 4 hours.
Slice top and bottom ends off oranges; cut off peel and white pith, following the shape of the fruit.
Cut orange segments from the membrane to remove and roughly chop.
Place in a medium bowl. Discard peel, pith, and membrane.
Stir in onion, cilantro, chile, salt, and 1 tbsp lime juice.
Sprinkle both sides of snapper evenly with Cajun seasoning.
Heat oil in a large nonstick or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the snapper; cook until golden brown and cooked through, 3 to 4 minutes per side.
Transfer to a plate and flake into large pieces using 2 forks. Sprinkle with the remaining 1 tbsp lime juice.
Divide the snapper and cabbage evenly among warm tortillas. Top with the orange salsa.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
Combine yogurt, sugar, lemon juice, vinegar, salt, pepper and hot sauce in a large bowl. Add cabbage and toss until fully coated.
Combine chicken and barbecue sauce in a medium microwavable bowl, toss until chicken is coated. Microwave on High until heated through, about 1 minute.
Heat tortillas according to package directions. Fill each tortilla with some chicken and top with the slaw and avocado. Garnish with cilantro and serve.
Bring a pot of lightly salted water to the boil. Add the black rice and cook it to its packet instructions. Drain, drizzle with olive oil and set aside.
Heat ½ of the olive oil in a non-stick pan on medium heat. Add the shredded cabbage, season with salt and pepper and saute for 3-5 minutes, stirring every now and then.
Remove the cabbage from the pan, add the other ½ of the olive oil and add the kale. Season lightly with salt and pepper and saute for an additional 3-5 minutes. Remove from the heat and set aside.
Divide all the components between two serving bowls, add ½ burrata on each, drizzle with some olive oil and freshly squeezed orange juice. Enjoy.
Cook the quinoa in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a stacked steamer, add the broccoli to the bottom stack and the edamame on the top stack. Steam for 3 to 4 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the meantime, heat the olive oil in a large skillet on medium heat.
Cook the sliced tempeh for 1 to 2 minutes on each side or until golden brown. Set aside.
Using the same skillet, toss in the kale with a splash of water and sauté for 2 to 3 minutes or until bright green and glossy.
Make the Dressing In a small bowl, whisk together the tahini, lemon juice, water and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Set aside.
Assemble the bowl by adding 1 cup of quinoa into each bowl.
Arrange the broccoli, edamame, avocado around the bowl.
Top with sliced tempeh, drizzle with lots dressing and garnish with pickled ginger.
Finely shred red and white cabbage with a mandoline slicer.
Cut the broccoli into small florets.
Dice the cherry tomatoes.
Cube the cucumber into small cubes.
Combine all the ingredients in a bowl.
Combine ingredients for dressing.
Season salad with salt and pepper, to taste.
Add dressing to the salad.
Mix salad.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and add the olive oil.
Season the fish fillet with salt and black pepper on both sides.

Place the seasoned fish fillet in the hot skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes per side or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork. Cooking times may vary depending on the thickness of the fillet.
While the fish is cooking, warm the corn tortillas in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 1-2 minutes per side or until they are heated through and slightly browned.
Once the fish is done, remove it from the skillet and break it into smaller pieces with a fork.
Assemble your Fish Tacos: Place the shredded cabbage or lettuce on the warm corn tortillas. Top with the cooked fish, diced tomatoes, diced red onions, and chopped cilantro.
Squeeze fresh lime juice over the taco filling for extra flavor.
Serve your Fish Tacos immediately with lime wedges on the side.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced red cabbage and onion to the skillet. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly softened.
Pour in apple cider vinegar and vegetable broth. Add caraway seeds if using.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cover the skillet and let the cabbage simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until tender, stirring occasionally.
Once the cabbage is tender and most of the liquid has evaporated, remove from heat.
Serve hot as a side dish.
To make the cabbage slaw, put the cabbage, cilantro, green onion and jalapeno into a bowl.
Combine the lime juice and mayonnaise. Season with salt and pepper. Add it to the cabbage mixture and toss to coat.
To make the chipotle sauce, combine mayonnaise, sour cream, 1 chipotle, lime juice and garlic in a blender. Pulse until smooth. Season with salt and pepper.
To make the salmon, put it onto a greased baking sheet. Brush with a little olive oil. Combine the chili powder, cumin and oregano.
Season the salmon with salt and pepper. Sprinkle with the seasoning. Bake for 10 minutes, remove and flake into large chunks.
To serve, fill tortillas with some salmon, top with slaw and drizzle with the chipotle sauce.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water with quinoa to the boil. Cook according to package instructions.
Add the flaxseed meal to a small bowl. Add 1 ½ tablespoons of warm water and mix well. Set in the fridge for 10 minutes.
In a larger bowl, combine flaxseed meal mixture, cooked quinoa, chickpeas, spinach, flour, curry powder, cumin, salt and pepper to form the burger mixture. Form burger patties from the mixture.
In a skillet heated over medium heat, add an olive oil and cook burgers until each side is golden brown, around 3 minutes per side.
Mix halved cherry tomatoes, spinach, bell pepper and red cabbage in a bowl. Place patties on a plate and serve with mixed vegetable.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.

For sauce, melt a tablespoon of butter into a saucepan over moderately low heat, and add chopped onions.   Cook until softened, add wine, and simmer the mixture for 2 minutes. Add the tomatoes, balsamic, rosemary, and salt and pepper.  Leave to slowly simmer, stirring occasionally, until thick and the liquid is almost evaporated.
For preparing rolls, bring two pots of water to boil. Using a sharp knife cut deep cone-shaped incisions into bottom of each cabbage. Removes cores.

Add whole green cabbage to one pot of boiling water.  Add red cabbage to the second pot of boiling water, boil for 10 minutes. Place cabbage into an ice bath, remove and drain on paper towel and let cool. Once cooled, gently pull off the leaves and remove the thick ribs from the leaves.  In a large bowl, mix together ricotta cheese, mozzarella cheese, egg, bell peppers, corn and scallions.

Spread a thick layer of sauce onto the bottom of a shallow baking dish. Pat your cabbage leaves dry and scoop 2 tablespoons of the cheese sauce into the top end of the leaves, then tuck in the ends and roll. Arrange rolls on top of the sauce. Cover the baking dish with foil and bake in the middle of the oven for 50 minutes.
Serve hot.
Heat oil in a pan. Add garlic and onion. Cook until the onion starts turning brown. Add cabbage, potato, ginger, pepper, and salt. Cook for 7 minutes.
Add vegetable stock and protein powder. Mix and cover the pan. Cook for 20 minutes. Blend the soup with a hand blender until smooth. Boil for about a minute and then switch off the gas.
Add almond milk and mix. Add lime juice and chopped dill. Serve warm.
Preheat the oven to 375°F. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spray it with nonstick spray.
In a medium skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion and saute until tender, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Transfer the onion and garlic to a medium bowl and cool slightly. Stir in the chicken, and parsley.
Form the mixture into approximately 2 tbsp size balls and transfer to the prepared baking sheet. Bake the meatballs until firm and fully cooked, 15 minutes.
Cut cauliflower into small pieces and use the grater attachment to grate the cauliflower into “rice.”
Saute in a large skillet over medium heat in 1 tbsp oil. Cover with a lid so the cauliflower steams and becomes more tender. Cook for a total of 5 minutes.
Place cauliflower rice into a bowls, top with meatballs, cabbage, cucumbers and cherry tomatoes.
rigatoni pasta: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta at a boil until tender yet firm to the bite, about 11 minutes, drain.
Whisk peanut butter, rice vinegar, soy sauce, lemon juice and sriracha sauce together in a large bowl until well-combined.
Add pasta and toss to coat completely. Garnish with parsley.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Meanwhile, place avocados, Parmesan, spinach, garlic, lime juice, salt and pepper in a food processor and pulse until chopped. Continue processing while gradually adding oil in a steady stream.
When the pasta is cooked, mix in the avocado sauce and the chopped tomatoes. Toss to coat and season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.
Heat the oven to 400°F.
Toss the broccoli and garlic in 1 tbsp of the olive oil on a roasting tray and roast in the oven for 10-12 minutes, until softened.
Tip the pasta into a pan of boiling, salted water. Cook according to packet instructions and drain.
Tip the parsley, basil, pine nuts, lemon juice and parmesan into a blender. Once the broccoli is done, set aside a few of the smaller pieces.
Squeeze the garlic from its skin, add to the blender along with the rest of the broccoli, pulse to a pesto and season well.
Toss the pasta with the pesto. Add the reserved broccoli florets, split between two bowls and top with a little extra parmesan.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook until al dente according to package instructions; drain.
In a skillet heat oil and saute garlic over medium heat for 30 seconds. Tear the sausages into the skillet and brown them.
Once browned, add frozen peas and cook for 5 minutes. Add butter, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Toss with cooked pasta and top with Parmesan cheese shavings and fresh cracked pepper before serving.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Roast pine nuts in a pan without oil for 2 minutes. Add to a blender along with lemon juice, protein powder, garlic, salt, pepper, avocado and basil and blend until smooth.
Mix pasta with sauce. Serve immediately.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat; cook and stir garlic until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes.
Add eggplant; cook, stirring constantly, until eggplant is softened, about 5 minutes. Add tomatoes and juice; cook until sauce is slightly reduced, about 20 minutes.

Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rigatoni in the boiling water, stirring occasionally until cooked through but firm to the bite, about 13 minutes. Drain and transfer to a serving bowl.
Pour sauce over pasta.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil over high heat. Add rigatoni pasta and cook until tender yet firm to the bite, 8 to 10 minutes. Drain and keep warm.

Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and pepper, and cook in the hot oil until lightly browned on all sides, and no longer pink in the center, about 5 minutes. Remove the chicken from the skillet and keep warm.
Stir onion and garlic into the skillet. Cook and stir until onion softens, about 5 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, roasted red peppers, and hot cherry peppers. And bring to a simmer. Stir in cooked chicken and heavy cream; simmer for 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in cooked pasta.
Serve garnished with Parmesan cheese.
broccoli florets: In a small mixing bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, sugar, vinegar and mustard, season with salt and pepper to taste, and mix well.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the broccoli, grapes, chicken, walnuts, and optional additions if using any, toss well to combine.
Pour half the dressing over the broccoli salad, toss and taste. Season with additional salt and pepper, if necessary.
Serve immediately, or cover and refrigerate the salad for an 1 hour before serving for an even better taste.
In a medium saucepan over medium heat, heat olive oil. Add onion and sauté until translucent, stirring often. Add garlic and cook, for 30 seconds.
Add stock, broccoli, and carrots, then cover with a lid. Simmer until broccoli is tender, about 10 minutes.
In a high-speed blender, combine the remaining ingredients. Blend at high speed until very, very smooth. Pour mixture into broccoli mixture and stir until smooth.
Add plenty of sea salt and freshly cracked pepper to taste. Spoon into serving bowls and serve immediately.
In a liquid measuring cup or small bowl combine egg whites, the ½ tsp dill, the salt, and hot pepper sauce.
Beat with a fork until combined, but not frothy.
In an 8-inch nonstick skillet with flared sides heat oil over medium-high heat. Pour egg white mixture into skillet. Cook over medium heat.
As mixture sets, run a spatula around edges of skillet, lifting egg white mixture so the uncooked portion flows underneath.
Continue cooking and lifting edges until egg white mixture is set but is still shiny.
Top half of the omelet with cheese, salmon, and, if desired, broccoli.
Lift and fold the unfilled half of omelet over filling. Gently slide omelet out of skillet onto a plate.
Sprinkle with additional dill and serve with sour cream and enjoy with bread.
To make this superfood recipe, preheat oven, 400°F.
Par-boil the potatoes in their skins for 8-10 mins, drain and rinse with cold water to cool.
Set aside.
Melt 50g of margarine in a pan, add onion, garlic, ground white pepper and stir-fry for three mins.
Add the broccoli and continue to stir-fry for another five mins.
Add the spinach and cook until it begins to wilt.
Grease a deep 1.3ltr baking dish with some margarine and spoon the stir-fried vegetable mixture evenly over the base.
Place the salmon strips on top of the mixture, scatter over the lemon rind and parsley and pour over the cream.
Season well.
Remove the peel from the potatoes and grate coarsely to make rosti potatoes.
Top the salmon with the potato, season and dot over the remaining margarine.
Place on a baking sheet and bake for 30 mins or until the potatoes are brown and crispy.
Serve with rice, whole baby carrots, whole baby sweetcorn or sugar snaps.
cooking wine: Preheat oven to 425°F.
This can be done in a bamboo steamer similar to below, but it is easier en papillote.
Get yourself a large piece of parchment paper and fold it in half.
Pat the fish dry and remove any bones.
Sprinkle all over with salt and place on in the center of one half of the parchment.
Sprinkle the fish with the cooking wine.
Working from one end of the parchment, fold it over and roll it up and along to form a sealed parchment bundle - en papillote.
Place in a oven on a cookie sheet. Bake for 8-12 minutes, depending on the thickness of your fish.
Remove from the oven, open parchment and transfer gently to a plate including any juice.
Top with half of the green onion.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic, cook 2 to 3 minutes. Add tomatoes, cook 2 to 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add remaining ingredients, cook 2 to 3 minutes or until shrimp turn pink. Serve over cooked rice.
Combine shrimp, olive oil, red pepper flakes, salt in a bowl; toss to coat shrimp completely in oil and spices.
Heat a large skillet over high heat until very hot. Cook shrimp in a single layer in the hot skillet until the bottoms are browned, about 2 minutes.
Turn shrimp and cook until browned on the other side, about 1 minute more. Transfer shrimp to a clean bowl.
Heat the same skillet over medium heat. Cook and stir onion with a pinch of salt in the hot skillet until onions are slightly golden and translucent, 2 to 4 minutes.
Add garlic and oregano; cook and stir until fragrant, 15 to 30 seconds.
Pour cooking wine into onions; bring to a boil while scraping the browned bits of food off of the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon.
Add crushed tomatoes and lower heat to medium-low. Cook and stir until tomato mixture thickens and flavors blend, 10 to 15 minutes.
Return shrimp to the skillet along with any accumulated juices in the bowl.
Cook until shrimp are reheated, 2 to 4 minutes. Stir parsley and basil into sauce; season with salt, red pepper flakes.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Toss the diced eggplant with some olive oil (enough to coat the eggplant).
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and place the eggplant in a single layer on the parchment paper.
Bake for about 25 minutes until cooked and fully roasted (slightly browned, but not burned).
Heat 2-3 tbsp olive oil on a non-stick large pan on low-medium heat.
Add the thinly sliced white onions and an pinch of salt, and saute until they become completely translucent, slightly brown, and just caramelized (~25 minutes).
Be sure to mix frequently.
If you find that the onions start sticking to the bottom of the pan or start to burn, feel free to add a little more olive oil.
Once the onions are caramelized, de-glaze the pan by adding a slash of cooking wine to the pan.
Heat 3 tbsp of oil in a medium sized pot on medium heat.
Add 8 cloves minced garlic and some dried red crushed peppers (more or less to taste depending on spice preference).
Fry for ~30 sec. until fragrant.
Be careful not to burn them.
Add the can of diced tomatoes.
Once the sauce is simmering, reduce the heat to low-medium and simmer uncovered for about 15-20 minutes or until the sauce thickens and reduces to about 2 cups.
Add the chopped basil.
Season to taste with salt, pepper, and white sugar (if needed).
Cook for another 5 minutes.
Turn off the heat and set aside (note: if you are almost done with the recipe, feel free to keep heating the sauce at lowest setting possible to keep the sauce hot until the pasta is ready).
Set a large pot of water to boil.
Add a generous pinch of salt and a drizzle of olive oil.
Once the water is boiling, add the penne pasta and cook al dente.
Add the roasted eggplant to the de-glazed pan with caramelized onions.
Add the hot tomato sauce and cooked penne pasta.
Toss everything to combine.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with some freshly chopped basil.
Top with some fresh Parmesan cheese (omit for vegan option), and serve hot!
Heat ghee and olive oil in a skillet over medium-high until butter melts. Stir in parsley, basil, oregano, garlic, salt, lemon juice, and cooking wine.
Add chicken, cook and stir until chicken is browned, about 3 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium and cook, stirring occasionally, until chicken is no longer pink inside, about 10 more minutes.
Add asparagus; cook and stir until asparagus is bright green and just starting to become tender, about 3 minutes.
Stir in mushrooms and cook until they release their juices, about 3 more minutes. Serve hot.
In a pot of salted water, boil the pork until you see large areas of white foam. Drain and rinse it with cold water. Cut in 1-inch cubes. Set aside.

Over low heat, pour olive oil and sugar into the wok. Cook and stir until sugar is slightly melted and nicely caramelized, add boiled pork. Increase the heat to medium and cover wok. Stir until all the pork is incorporated into the mixture and lightly browned.
Turn heat back to low and add in the sauce ingredients and ginger. Stir just until incorporated, about 20 seconds. Add just enough water to cover the pork.
Add bay leaves and increase the heat to high until it boils, then cover the wok, turn heat down to medium/low and simmer for 40 minutes – 1 hour or until pork is tender.
Turn up to high heat until all the liquid is absorbed. Stir occasionally to prevent burning and add water if it gets too dry. Fold in salt and chicken base. Season to taste.
We'll start by trimming the fat off the edges of our beef (12 oz) fillet.
Then, we'll start cutting the beef into strips, about ⅖ inch thick and about 2 inches long.
We'll want to cut against the grain of the meat, which results in more tender bites.
