Then, we'll place our beef slices in a bowl of cold water and gently massage the meat.
This helps remove some of the myoglobin and the more "meaty" taste/smell from the beef.
After 1-2 minutes, drain the beef with a colander.
In a bowl, we'll mix black pepper (1 tsp), cornstarch (1 tbsp), water (1 tbsp), oyster sauce (1 tbsp), light soy sauce (1 tsp), Shaoxing cooking wine (1 tbsp), and red wine (1 tbsp).
Mix together until the marinade is even.
Before we mix the beef with the marinade, we'll want to squeeze as much fluid out as we can.
One of the main goals of this dish is to avoid having excess fluid or sauce pooling when we're done cooking it.
With our hands, we'll press and squeeze the beef against a colander to force any excess moisture out of the beef.
Then, we'll add the beef to the marinade and massage the meat for about 30-60 seconds.
This helps the beef absorb the marinade and become more tender.
For our vegetables, we'll be mincing garlic (2 cloves), and slicing up a variety of bell peppers (1) and red onion (½) for color.
For each bell pepper, we'll only be using about ¼ to ⅓ of each, so if you'd rather just stick to one color and use up a whole entire bell pepper, that's fine too.
If you're freestyling with the amounts and veering off the recipe, just make sure to be more conservative on the veggies and avoid using ones with high water content (like bok choy).
Bell peppers have lower water content, but if you cook an excess of them, your sauce will be too watery.
Next, we'll create the sauce for our black pepper beef.

We'll be mixing cornstarch (1 tbsp), water (1 tbsp), sugar (1 tsp), light soy sauce (1 tsp), dark soy sauce (1 tsp), red wine (1 tbsp), salt (½ tsp), oyster sauce (1 tsp), and Shaoxing cooking wine (1 tbsp).
We'll set our stove to high heat and let our wok heat up for about 2-3 minutes.
Once our wok is hot enough, we'll add about 2 tbsp of corn oil, or any oil with a high smoke point, until the oil is shimmering, or forming ripples across the surface
While we wait, keep the beef juicier: add oil (2 tbsp) to the beef. The oil helps seal the juices and trap them inside the beef.
Mix the beef around for about 20-30 seconds to evenly coat the oil. Add the beef, and cook for about 1 minute.
Once the beef starts to change color, transfer out of the wok. We're only looking to partially cook the beef here.
Add the minced garlic, and let it cook for about 20-30 seconds. Add the veggies, and cook for about 90 seconds.
Add the beef back in, and cook for about 45-60 seconds. Add the slurry sauce, and cook everything for another minute.
My dad doesn't always use the entire sauce, which is a judgment call that he makes since this recipe isn't supposed to have too much sauce pooling at the bottom of the dish. Add sesame oil.
Pour some black pepper (1 tsp) into your hand, and slowly pour it around the wok. Mix and cook for 45-60 seconds.
Cook and mix everything together for another minute. Try taste to see if it needs any adjustments. If it's good, we'll plate our dish and serve it with egg noodles.
wheat tortilla: Tear the tortillas into 1 in pieces.
In a medium-sized bowl whisk 6 large eggs with a pinch of salt.
Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the torn tortillas to the pan and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cook, stirring often, until they crisp up and start to make scraping sounds against the pan, about 8 minutes.
Reduce the heat to medium and pour in the eggs. Cook, stirring and scraping the bottom of the pan, until the tortillas are slightly more dry and no longer glossy, about 2 to 3 minutes.
Serve hot, with queso fresco and a sprinkling of fresh scallions.
Bring a pot of water to the boil. Add rice, cook to packet instructions, then strain and set aside. Place a non-stick pan on medium heat, add 1 tbsp olive oil.
Add chicken breasts, season with salt and pepper, cook for 4-6 minutes on each side, or until the chicken is done. Set aside to cool before cutting into strips.
To a small mixing bowl, add avocado, 1 tbsp olive oil, salt and pepper, shallot and lemon juice. Mash it well using a fork to make guacamole.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan. Add the rice, chicken, guacamole, tomatoes and black beans. Add a drizzle of olive oil and some black pepper before wrapping the burritos and enjoying.
In a large nonstick skiller, cook and stir spinach, green onions, cumin, lemon juice and garlic powder until spinach is wilted. remove from heat and stir in cheese.

Top half of each tortilla with spinach mixture and fold the other half over. Coat with olive oil and place the tortilla on the griddle and cook over medium heat until golden brown. Cut the quesadillas in half.
Crack the eggs into a small mixing bowl. Season with salt and pepper and whisk lightly. Heat half the olive oil in a non-stick pan. Add the eggs, cook whilst scrambling until done. Set aside.
Heat the remainder of the olive oil in a non-stick pan. Add the onion, a pinch of salt, and saute for 3-4 minutes. Add the bell pepper and cook for another 3-4 minutes.
Return the eggs to the pan to heat them up, then transfer the contents of the pan onto the tortilla. Sprinkle cheddar cheese on top, wrap into a burrito and enjoy.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet on medium heat and place the chicken in the skillet.
Cover with a lid and cook for 10 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pan and shred the chicken.
Combine chicken and beans in a bowl. Place tortillas on a bench and sprinkle cheese evenly over half of each tortilla.
Top with 1/3 cup chicken mixture and spread evenly. Fold tortilla over and press to enclose.
Place tortillas into the pan and cook for 2 minutes on each side. Cut tortillas in half and serve.
Place chicken breasts into a saucepan and pour in enough water to cover. Place over medium heat, bring to a boil and simmer until chicken meat is no longer pink, 10 minutes.
Transfer chicken breasts to a bowl, allow to cool, and shred the chicken meat with 2 forks.
Heat olive oil in a small saucepan over medium heat, and cook and stir 1 tbsp onion until translucent, 3 minutes.
Mix in the shredded chicken, cumin, chili powder, and tomato sauce and bring to a boil.
Place chicken mixture into tortillas and garnish with chopped tomato, onion, parsley and avocado.
Put the chicken breasts in a bowl and drizzle with oil. Make sure that they're well coated.
In a shallow dish, mix the dried bread crumbs with the spices until well combined. Coat each chicken breast in bread crumbs, and transfer to your air fryer basket.
Air fry in the air fryer at 390°F 10-12 minutes. After the first 7 minutes, open the air fryer turn the meat over and continue cooking for 3 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the air fryer, cool slightly and cut into strips.
Warm up the tortillas in a non-stick pan, add a handful of shredded lettuce, a sprinkling of tomatoes and the chicken strips. Drizzle with sour cream and enjoy!
orange juice: Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Cut the chicken into cubed chunks, then toss in a bowl with Cornstarch or Potato Starch.
Use enough to coat the chicken evenly.
Place in the Air Fryer and cook according to your Air Fryer Manual for chicken.
While the chicken is cooking, in a small saucepan, begin to make the sauce.
Add the soy sauce, orange juice, hoisin sauce, ground ginger, and garlic to the saucepan on medium-high heat.
Whisk this up until well combined.
Low boil is reached, whisk in the water and cornstarch.
Mix in the sesame seeds.
(The sauce should only take about 5 minutes or less to make on the stove and then an additional 5 minutes to thicken up.)
Remove the sauce from the heat and set aside for 5 minutes to thicken.
Once the chicken is done, remove from the air fryer and place in a bowl, and then coat with the sauce.
Serve topped over rice.
Mix molasses in the orange juice in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Stir in the cranberries and cook until they start to pop, about 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and place sauce in a bowl. It will thicken as it cools.
Season the sliced chicken thigh with salt and black pepper.
In a small bowl, mix together the broth, orange juice, orange zest, soy sauce, honey (if using), grated ginger, and minced garlic.
In a small skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced chicken thigh and sear until golden brown on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Pour the orange juice mixture over the sliced chicken in the skillet.
Pour the orange juice mixture over the sliced chicken in the skillet.
Once the chicken is cooked, remove it from the skillet and place it on a serving plate.
Increase the heat to medium-high and let the sauce reduce and thicken for a few minutes.
Pour the sauce over the sliced chicken and garnish with chopped green onion.
In a blender or food processor, combine the avocado, mango, chia, orange juice, and water. Whirl until smooth and there are no chunks left.
Divide among chilled glasses. Serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Mix orange juice, soy sauce, hot sauce, ginger, and garlic together in a small bowl to make the sauce.
Season chicken with salt, pepper and italian seasoning. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chicken strips and brown on both sides, 1 to 2 minutes per side.
Pour in the orange sauce. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, until sauce thickens and chicken is no longer pink in the centers, 8 to 10 minutes.
Plate the chicken and top with green onion.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanshile saute chicken in a large skillet over medium high heat until cooked through.
In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, garlic powder, pineapple juice, orange juice, pecans and pepper.
Pour over chicken. Let simmer for 10 to 15 minutes, or until sauce is thickened to taste. Garnish with parsley.
Combine strawberries, apples, yogurt, pineapple juice, orange juice, and milk in a blender; blend until smooth.

In a mixing bowl, combine masa harina, orange juice, orange zest, olive oil, and a pinch of salt. Mix well until a smooth dough forms. Adjust sweetness with stevia or other non-sugar sweetener if desired.
Take a soaked corn husk and spread a thin layer of the masa mixture onto the center, leaving some space around the edges.
Fold the sides of the corn husk over the masa mixture to enclose it.
Fold up the bottom of the corn husk to form a closed bottom.
Repeat with the remaining masa mixture and corn husks.
Place the tamales in a steamer basket.
Steam the tamales over boiling water for about 45-60 minutes, or until the masa is firm and cooked through.
Remove the tamales from the steamer and let them cool slightly before serving.
Serve the sweet orange tamales warm.
Layer cucumber, banana, ginger, and chia seeds in a blender; add orange juice. Blend mixture until smooth, adding water for a thinner smoothie.
In a bowl or jar, pour in the orange juice.
Add chia seeds and vanilla extract to the orange juice.
Stir the mixture thoroughly until the chia seeds are well combined.
Cover the bowl or jar and refrigerate for at least 2-3 hours, or ideally overnight, to allow the chia seeds to absorb the liquid and form a pudding-like consistency.
Once the pudding has set, give it a good stir.
Serve chilled and enjoy your refreshing orange chia pudding topped with orange!
In a large skillet, saute the carrots and cumin in the butter over low heat for about 2 minutes. Season with salt and pepper and coat with protein powder.
Add the orange juice and honey. Bring to a boil. Cover and simmer over high heat for about 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Adjust the seasoning. Sprinkle with the thyme and serve.
Peel the grapefruit.
Place grapefruit and all other ingredients in blender and process until smooth.
Pour into a glass and enjoy!

In a bowl, combine the thinly sliced beef, low-sodium soy sauce, fresh orange juice, orange zest, grated ginger, chili garlic sauce, minced garlic, and cornstarch. Mix well and let it marinate for 10-15 minutes.
Heat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add the oil.
Once the oil is hot, add the thinly sliced bell peppers to the skillet. Stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes or until they start to soften.
Push the bell peppers to one side of the skillet and add the marinated beef to the other side. Stir-fry the beef for about 2-3 minutes or until it's cooked through and slightly caramelized.
Combine the cooked beef and bell peppers in the skillet and stir-fry together for another minute to heat through.
Transfer the Spicy Orange Zest Beef with bell peppers to a serving plate.
Garnish with sliced green onions.
Squeeze the oranges to obtain 1/2 cup of fresh orange juice. Remove any pulp or seeds.
In a small bowl, mix the fresh orange juice with honey or maple syrup. Adjust the sweetness to your liking. Add vanilla extract if desired and stir to combine.
Pour the orange mixture into a popsicle mold designed for a single serving.
Insert a popsicle stick into the mold.
Place the mold in the freezer and let it freeze for at least 4-6 hours or until the popsicle is completely frozen.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Brush both sides of the fillets with oil. Place in a greased baking dish and then sprinkle with salt and pepper. Pour orange juice over the fish.
Bake, covered until fish just begins to flake easily with a fork or for 17-20 minutes.
Boil rice in a separate bowl for 5 minutes on high flame or until soft. Serve rice with salsa and cherry tomatoes on top. Garnish with lemon.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the chicken cubes with salt and pepper.
Add the chicken cubes to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes per side.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, rice vinegar, orange juice, orange zest, minced garlic, minced ginger, and crushed red pepper flakes.
Add the sliced red bell pepper to the skillet with the cooked chicken.
Pour the sauce mixture over the chicken and bell pepper in the skillet.
Stir well to coat the chicken and bell pepper evenly with the sauce.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving plate.
Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds.
Serve the spicy orange chicken with red bell pepper hot and enjoy!
Place all ingredients in a blender. Mix well for one minute or until smooth. If the smoothie is too thick, add water. Serve cooled.
In a blender combine frozen banana, orange juice, apple and milk. Blend until smooth. pour into glasses and serve.
Blend ice, yogurt, banana, whey, prunes and orange juice together in a blender until smooth.
In an oven-safe dish, bring 1 cup of water to a boil in a preheated 212°F (100°C) oven.
Add the couscous to the hot water, cover the dish with a lid, and place it back in the oven for 5 minutes.
After 5 minutes, remove the dish from the oven and fluff the couscous with a fork to separate the grains.
In the same dish, combine the cooked couscous, chopped fresh parsley, chopped fresh mint, and pomegranate seeds.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the orange juice, lemon zest, orange zest, and olive oil to create the dressing.
Pour the citrus dressing over the couscous mixture in the oven-safe dish.
Season with salt and pepper, and toss everything together until well combined.
Allow the flavors to meld for a few minutes in the residual heat.
dill weed: Use a potato masher to crush garbanzo beans in a large mixing bowl.
Add celery, onion, vegan mayonnaise, nutritional yeast, salt, garlic powder, olive oil, pickle juice, vinegar, and lemon juice.
Season with paprika, nori, seafood seasoning, hot sauce, cayenne pepper, and dill. Mix thoroughly.
Serve on toasted bread.
Place the potatoes in a pot with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil, and cook for about 10 minutes, or until easily pierced with a fork.
Drain, and transfer to a large bowl to cool.
In a medium bowl, mix the sour cream, mayonnaise, mustard, vinegar, eggs, pickle, celery, green onions, and hot sauce.
Season with dill, garlic powder, onion salt, salt, and pepper. Pour over the potatoes, and gently toss to coat. Chill at least 3 hours in the refrigerator before serving.
Preheat an air fryer to 400°F.
Toss sweet potato, olive oil, paprika, salt, pepper, and dill in a large bowl.
Place mixture into the preheated air fryer. Cook for 12 to 16 minutes.
Check and stir after 10 minutes and then every 3 minutes until crispy and browned.
Place eggs in a saucepan and cover with water.
Bring to a boil, remove from heat, and let eggs stand in hot water for 10 to 12 minutes.
While eggs are cooking, combine mayonnaise, relish, dill, mustard, salt, and pepper in a medium mixing bowl.
Remove eggs from hot water, cool under cold running water, and peel.
Halve eggs lengthwise; remove yolks and add to mayonnaise mixture.
Mix well.
Scoop mixture into a piping bag or plastic baggie.
Place in the refrigerator until more firm, 10 to 15 minutes.
Pipe the chilled mixture into the emptied and halved eggs.
Sprinkle paprika on top of each egg.
Serve immediately with bread or refrigerate for up to 7 days.
Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir in onion, and cook until it begins to brown.
Sprinkle with bouillon granules, and stir until the melted butter is absorbed.
Stir in salmon and cooking cream. Stir in dill, white pepper, and paprika. Cook about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat, and let cool slightly.
In a food processor or blender, puree salmon mixture. Return to saucepan over medium-low heat.
Stir in the port, and cook 5 minutes. Season with salt to taste. Garnish with 1 tbsp sour cream and parsley, and serve.
Fill a large pot with 4 quarts water.
Add chicken; bring to a boil.
Cook until chicken is coming apart, about 45 minutes.
Remove from heat; remove chicken from pot and transfer to a platter.
Put a lid on the pot and cover the chicken with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate both until chicken is safe to handle, about 40 minutes.
Tear meat off the chicken bones.
Skim excess fat off the top of the cooled broth.

Add olive oil and sautee carrots, celery, and onion for 5 minutes. Then add the last 2 quarts of water, chicken meat, cabbage, wine, bouillon, dill, butter, garlic, parsley, thyme, white pepper, and bay leaves.
Cook over medium heat until vegetables have softened, about 30 minutes.
Serve warm.
yellow onion: In a mixing bowl, add the kimchi. Use kitchen shears to cup it into small bits then add the rest of the ingredients.
Whisk and combine well. The batter will be on the thin side.
Preheat skillet, add oil and swirl it around. Take ½ cup of the batter and add it to the pan and immediately use the back of a spoon to spread it.
Cook over medium-low heat about 2 minutes on the first side and 1 minute on the second side. Repeat the process until you finish the batter.
In a large pot over medium heat, heat oil. Add carrots, celery, onion, and leek and cook until soft, 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, 1 minute more.
Add broth, water, thyme, rosemary, bay leaf, oregano, and pinch red pepper flakes. Bring to a boil, then add the turkey meat. Cook for 12-15 minutes, or until the meat is cooked through.
Take the turkey out, shred it with two forks and return it to the pot. Add the egg noodles. Cover and cook until pasta is cooked through, 7 minutes.
Discard thyme, rosemary, and bay leaf, and serve.
Preheat conventional oven to 350°F.
Squeeze pouch to separate rice; pour into a skillet. Add water and heat; stir to separate rice if necessary.
Transfer to a bowl, add lemon juice and 1½ tsp. salt (or to taste) and stir to combine.
Sweat onions in vegetable oil over medium heat until translucent and soft.
Add tomatoes and cook, stirring occasionally until slightly softened, approximately 2 minutes.
Add onion, tomatoes, capers, parsley, and olive oil to rice and gently stir to combine.
Hold in refrigeration until ready to serve. Season cod with salt and white pepper to taste.
Heat vegetable oil in skillet over high heat. Sear fish on presentation side until golden brown.
Carefully turn over fillets and place pan in oven, cooking fish to desired doneness.
To serve, plate 1 cup rice salad with 1 cod fillet.
Garnish cod with 1 tsp tapenade.
Cook and crumble the sausage and diced onions over medium-high heat until the onions, spinach, and carrots are softened and the sausage is cooked, for 10 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add oregano, cayenne and pepper. Stir to combine.
Add the chicken broth and use a silicone spatula to “clean” the bottom of the pot. Add the cream. Bring to a boil, reduce to a simmer.
Add the kale and tortellini and simmer for 5 minutes.
Transfer to serving bowls and top with a pinch of red pepper flakes.
Heat oil and half the butter in a large, deep frypan on medium-low heat. Cook onion and garlic for 5 minutes until soft.
Turn heat to medium-high, add rice and stir to coat. Add wine and stir for 1 minute.
Add stock a ladleful at a time, stirring and allowing each to absorb before adding the next, until rice is cooked but still al dente.
Add asparagus with a final ladleful of stock, stir in cheese, butter, and 1/2 cup water, cook for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, stir in rind and parsley. Season and serve with remaining parsley.
Place 4 eggs, milk, water, butter, salt and flour in a blender and mix until well combined. Place in the refrigerator and chill for at least 20 minutes.

Place the remaining 3 eggs in cold water, bring them to the boil, take off the heat and leave in hot water for 13 minutes. Afterwards run under cold water to cool. Grate the eggs using a cheese grater, set aside.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a non-stick pan on medium heat. Add the onion, saute for 3 minutes, add the port, season with salt and pepper and cook 5-8 minutes, or until the pork is cooked.
Heat some butter in a nonstick skillet on medium heat. Pour about ¼ cup of batter into the hot skillet and tilt the skillet until the batter spreads evenly.
Cook for about a minute, until slightly golden, flip the crepe over and cook for another 30 seconds on the second side. Set aside and cover with a damp towel.
Take a pancake, spread a tbsp of sour cream on it, add pork meat, some grated egg and a few slices of pickle, then roll tightly. Enjoy warm or cold.
Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil in a 6-inch cast-iron or oven-safe nonstick skillet over medium heat. Cook the onion, stirring often until very soft, about 10 minutes.
Transfer onion to a plate, wipe out skillet, and set aside to cool for at least 10 minutes. Combine chopped herbs and cooled onions in a medium bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together eggs, salt, baking powder, and turmeric until smooth. Fold into the herb mixture.
Heat remaining oil in the skillet over medium heat. Pour egg and herb mixture into the pan and spread around in an even layer.
Cover and cook for 8 minutes. Serve immediately.
Bring a pot of water to the boil. Cook the rice to package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool. Once cooled, fluff the rice using a fork.
In a separate pan, add olive oil and heat on medium heat. Add onion, a pinch of salt, and saute for 2-3 minutes. Add zucchini, saute for additional 3 minutes.
Add tomatoes, and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes. A minute or so before finishing the sauce off, add corn and stir the mixture well. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Divide the rice between plates, top with vegetable sauce and enjoy.
Boil the broth.
Pour the chicken broth in a large stockpot and place it over medium high heat.
Remove the tops of the guajillo peppers, then slice them in half and remove the seeds.
Add the garlic cloves, onion, guajillo peppers, oregano, cumin, and salt to the chicken broth.
Boil the mixture for 10-15 minutes until the guajillo peppers are soft. Blend the pozole broth.
Use a slotted spoon to remove the garlic cloves, onion, guajillo peppers, and 1 cup of chicken broth and place it in a blender.
Blend the mixture until it is pureed. Add the purè to the chicken broth and stir to combine.
Add the hominy and shredded turkey meat to the broth, and continue to cook for 2-5 minutes until it is heated through.
Prepare the toppings. Slice, dice, and arrange the toppings in separate bowls.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add the celery, onion, and garlic and saute for about 5 minutes. Add in the vegetable broth and water.
Add in the raw cashews, bay leaf, and thyme. Bring to a simmer and cook for about 15 minutes. Remove the bay leaf, then blend.
Make sure to blend very well so that the cashews are completely blended. Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately, garnished with dill.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil. Add the rice and cook to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a non-stick skillet or wok. Add the onion and saute for 3-4 minutes. Add the carrots and peppers and saute for another minute or two.
Add the meat, season with salt and pepper to taste, and cook for 6-7 minutes, or until the meat is cooked through. Add the tomatoes and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
Divide the rice between two plates, add the meaty sauce, top with parsley and serve.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the onion and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, 5 minutes.
Add coriander, cumin and turmeric and cook, stirring, for 2 minutes.
Pulse tomatoes in the food processor until finely chopped. Add to the pan along with salt.
Reduce heat to maintain a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
Add chickpeas, spinach, and garam masala, reduce heat to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes more.
Serve topped with cilantro.
In a large pot, heat the oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and bell pepper and cook until softened.
Add garlic, ginger and curry paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the broth, coconut milk, and fish sauce.
Bring to a boil.
Add the shrimp and rice noodles.
Cook until shrimp is pink and opaque.
Stir in the lime juice and serve immediately, topped with optional garnishes if using.
For the dip, mix sour cream with parsley to taste.
Set a large strainer over a bowl. In a food processor fitted with the shredding disk, shred the potatoes, carrot, onion and zucchini in batches.
Add each batch to the strainer. Discard any liquid in the bowl and transfer the shredded vegetables to a clean kitchen towel and squeeze out as much liquid as possible.
Add the shredded vegetables to the bowl and add the egg, protein powder and flour. Stir in some salt and pepper.
Heat a large skillet until very hot. Add half of the oil and heat until it shimmers. Scoop four ½ cup mounds of the vegetable mixture into the pan and use the bottom of the measuring cup to flatten.

Cook the cakes over moderately high heat until golden brown on the bottom, about 4 minutes. Turn the cakes, reduce the heat to medium and cook until golden brown and cooked through, about 4 minutes longer.
Cook the remaining cakes in the remaining oil. Serve immediately with the dip.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Place the parsley in the bowl of a food processor and pulse until finely chopped, remove and set aside.
In the same food processor bowl used to chop the parsley, place the quartered onion and pulse until finely chopped.
Drain the onions slightly to release excess moisture. Return the parsley back to the bowl of the food processor with the onions.
Add the ground beef to the food processor in addition to all the spices.
Run the food processor on pulse once or twice until ingredients are well combined and form a pasty meat mixture.
Transfer the mixture from the food processor to a large bowl and form the Kafta into an oblong shape about 1 inch in thickness.
You can form them on a wooden skewer or without. Grease the grates of a gas grill or indoor grill pan, and preheat it to medium-high heat.
When heated through, transfer the kafta to the grill. Grill for 4-5 minutes per side, depending on your preferred level of doneness.
Serve hot off the grill with rice and dipping yogurt.
Turn Instant Pot to saute mode and heat oil. Add cumin seeds and toast them for 30 seconds. Add turmeric powder and mix well. Add ginger and garlic, mix well.
Add onion and saute for a minute. Cook for 2 minutes with a glass lid on. Add tomatoes, red chili powder, garam masala, salt and ground coriander. Mix well.
Add ground chicken and break it with a spatula. Close Instant Pot with the pressure valve to sealing. Pressure Cook on for 4 minutes.
Garnish with cilantro and serve hot.
Chop the scallions and keep the whites and greens seperate. Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan. Add chopped onions along with half of the salt.
Sauté the onions on medium heat until they are translucent and golden brown.
Add ginger, garlic, chili and the white scallions to the onions. Cook for 2 minutes, mixing well.
Add tomatoes and turmeric to the pan and cook for 2 minutes. Whisk the eggs with milk and remaining salt.
Slowly pour the whisked eggs to the pan and keep mixing with a spatula for 5-6 minutes or until the eggs are cooked.
Add the garam masala and garnish with chopped cilantro and remaining scallions. Mix well.
Heat a griddle on medium heat. Apply ghee on both sides of sliced bread.

Cook the bread slices until both sides turn golden-brown
Enjoy egg bhurji with the ghee toasted bread!
In a large pot, heat olive oil.
Add garlic and onion; saute until soft. Add kale and saute, stirring, until wilted.
Add 3 cups of broth, 2 cups of beans, and all of the tomato, herbs, salt and pepper. Simmer 5 minutes.
In a blender or food processor, mix the remaining beans and broth until smooth. Stir into soup to thicken.
Simmer 15 minutes. Ladle into bowls; sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Put the chicken liver in the freezer for 5-10 minutes before cooking, so that it's easier to cut. Take it out and cut it into bite-sized pieces.
Heat the olive oil on medium heat in a non-stick frying pan. Add the onions and saute for 3-4 minutes, or until the onion starts to turn brown.
Add the chicken liver to the pan in a single layer and cook for 1-2 minutes, then flip, add the red wine, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes or until the liver is cooked through.
Take the meat off the heat, stir in the parsley and transfer it to a plate. Season with salt and pepper and serve.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium-high heat. Saute onion and bell pepper until tender and golden, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, curry paste, chili garlic sauce, garam masala, curry powder, and salt. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring often, until fragrant.
Next add lentils, broth, and tomatoes. Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer gently, stirring occasionally. Cook until lentils are soft but not mushy.
Add juice from 1/2 a lime and stir. For an ultra creamy soup, feel free to add all the coconut milk to the soup.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and chili, and add some extra chili garlic sauce if you like things spicy. Enjoy!
habanero pepper: In a small pot or saucepan, brown the ground beef over medium heat until no longer pink.
Add diced onion and bell pepper to the pot and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in diced tomatoes, cooked kidney beans, and diced habanero pepper.
Pour in beef broth and bring the chili to a simmer.
Season with chili powder, ground cumin, salt, and pepper.
Let the chili simmer for about 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Spray a baking dish with cooking spray.
Cook the rice in a pot of water accoriding to the instructions on the packaging.
Mix1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon lime juice, and 1/2 teaspoon chipotle powder in a large bowl.
Cover the bottom of the baking dish with the mixture and set aside.
Mix tomatoes, red onion, jalapeno peppers, habanero peppers, remaining lemon juice, remaining lime juice, and remaining chipotle powder together in a large bowl. Mix thoroughly.
Place salmon fillets on top of spinach in the baking dish and top with tomato mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven until fish flakes easily with a fork, about 14 minutes.
Combine mangoes, avocados, onion, and habanero pepper in a bowl and toss. Add cilantro and drizzle with lime juice. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium high heat.
Add chicken pieces into the oil and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side until slightly browned, they do not need to be cooked through;
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add the onion and garlic to the skillet and cook over medium heat for 2-3 minutes stirring occasionally.
To the skillet, add the tomato, bell peppers, curry powder, lime zest, lime juice, paprika, red pepper flakes, and habanero if desired and cook for 5-6 minute stirring occasionally.
Place the chicken back into the skillet and cover with chicken broth.
Stir to combine, reduce the heat, then cover and simmer for another 20 minutes stirring occasionally.
Season with salt and pepper to taste and then garnish with fresh parsley before serving over rice.
Combine tomatoes, onion, lime juice, cilantro, jalapeno pepper, habanero pepper, garlic, and ground black pepper in a food processor.
Pulse several times until desired texture is reached.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add diced bell peppers, habanero pepper, jalapeno pepper, and minced garlic to the skillet. Cook for 5-7 minutes, or until softened and slightly charred.
Remove from heat and transfer the cooked vegetables to a bowl.
Let the vegetables cool for a few minutes.
Once cooled, stir in lime juice and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped cilantro before serving.
Serve the spicy four-pepper salsa with your favorite chips or as a topping for tacos or grilled meats.
Cook the rice a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Combine pineapple, broth, mango, habanero, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and turmeric in a small saucepan. Simmer over medium heat, 10 to 15 minutes.
Pour sauce contents carefully into the bowl of a food processor or electric blender and mix until smooth. Set aside.
Heat a wok over high heat. Add vegetable oil to the hot wok, heat until shimmering, about 1 minute.
Add tofu and stir-fry until uniformly golden brown, about 5 minutes. Add sauce to tofu.
Taste and add salt as needed. Serve with rice and top with sesame seeds.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente, drain.
Melt ghee with olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add shallots, garlic and habanero pepper, and cook until lightly browned. Pour cream into a saucepan, and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Stir in the ingredients from the skillet, mix in the  black pepper, and simmer until thickened, 5 to 8 minutes. Stir in Parmesan cheese, and remove from heat.
coconut butter: In a pot, combine coconut milk and butter, a pinch of salt, and heat up over low to medium heat until the milk starts to simmer.
Once the milk simmers, add rice and give everything a good stir. Allow the rice pudding to simmer over low heat for about 25 minutes until thick and creamy.
Put frozen cherries and a ½ cup of water in a second saucepan and heat up over medium heat. Bring the cherries to a boil and simmer for 2 minutes, then remove from heat and set aside until serving.
Remove the cooked rice pudding from heat and add the protein powder. Serve along with the hot cherries.
Place all ingredients in a blender and puree until smooth. Serve immediately.
Heat a skillet over medium heat. Add the pecans and toast them while constantly stirring.
Transfer to a dish when the pecans are darker and smell toasty. They'll toast even more while cooling, roughly chop the pecans.
In a small saucepan, combine coconut butter, ghee (or butter), and coconut oil and heat over low heat until melted.
Stir in vanilla extract and sea salt, as well as stevia. Divide the chopped pecans into a silicone mold or ice cube tray of your choice.
Pour the heated butter and coconut mixture over the pecans, dividing it evenly. Freeze for at least 30 minutes until the bombs harden, enjoy!
pita bread: Preheat your grill or a grill pan to 375°F.
Season the boneless, skinless chicken breast with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Grill the chicken breast for about 6-8 minutes per side, or until it's cooked through and has nice grill marks. Make sure the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, you can grill the small pita bread pieces until they are crispy and lightly browned.
Once the chicken is done, remove it from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped romaine lettuce, diced cucumbers, diced tomatoes, diced onions, chopped fresh parsley, and chopped fresh mint.
Add the grilled chicken strips to the salad.
Drizzle the salad with olive oil and fresh lemon juice.
Toss everything together to ensure the salad is evenly coated with the dressing.
In a bowl, combine lean ground beef or turkey with garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned ground meat and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, until browned and cooked through.
Warm the whole wheat pita bread in the microwave or oven.
Place the cooked ground meat on the warmed pita bread.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, diced cucumber, and diced red onion.
Drizzle plain Greek yogurt over the toppings.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Spread a couple tablespoons of sauce on each pita, leaving a small crust around the edge. Top with tomatoes, onion, basil and feta.
Place the pitas one at a time directly on the rack of a toaster oven. Toast for 3-4 minutes.
Put a small skillet on medium heat. Add onions and saute for 2 minutes then pour in tomato sauce.
Make an indentation in 4 places and crack an egg into each one. Cover and cook for 5 minute and dress with parsley
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cut the pita bread into small squares and place them on a baking sheet.
Bake the pita squares for about 8-10 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown. Remove from the oven and let them cool.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped romaine lettuce, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, diced red onion, chopped parsley, and sliced radishes.
Add the cooled pita squares to the bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss everything together gently to combine.
Preheat your oven to 375°F
Place the small pita bread in the preheated oven and toast for about 5-7 minutes or until it becomes crispy. Remove it from the oven and break it into pieces.
In a small bowl, combine the plain yogurt, minced garlic, salt, and pepper. Mix well to create the garlicky yogurt.
In a separate pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the canned chickpeas to the pan and sauté for about 3-5 minutes until they are heated through and slightly crispy.
To serve, spread the garlicky yogurt on a plate.
Top it with the sautéed chickpeas a side of pita.
Garnish with paprika.
Mince garlic in the bowl of a food processor. Add black beans, 2 tbsp reserved liquid,  lime juice, tahini, cumin, salt and cayenne pepper
Process until smooth, scraping down the sides as needed. Add additional seasoning and liquid to taste. Garnish with cilantro and serve with pita.
Place edamame into a large pot and cover with salted water. Place over medium-low heat, bring to a simmer, and cook until tender, about 5 minutes, drain.
Puree garlic in food processor until minced. Add edamame, tahini, water, cilantro, lime juice, olive oil, kosher salt, cumin, and cayenne pepper, blend until smooth. Serve with pita.
Cook lentils per package directions.
Drain and rinse to cool.
In a food processor, finely chop garlic.
Add cooked lentils, tahini, lemon juice, oil, cumin, thyme, 3/4 teaspoon salt, and  1/4 teaspoon pepper, then process, scraping down the sides as necessary, until smooth.
For a thinner consistency, add water 1 tablespoon at a time, processing to reach desired consistency (do not add more than 3 to 4 tablespoon water, or it will become too thin).
Transfer to a bowl, drizzle with oil, and sprinkle with paprika or crushed red pepper flakes if desired.
Serve with pita bread and celery sticks for dipping.
Add coconut, roasted gram lentils, garlic, chili, cumin seeds, sugar, lemon juice and water to a blender and blend until you get smooth chutney.
Add more water if needed to get to the desired consistency.
Take out the chutney in a serving bowl.
Heat oil in a tadka pan over medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and allow them to pop.
Turn the heat off and then add asafetida/hing and curry leaves.
Be careful as you add curry leaves as the oil may splatter.
Pour the tadka over the chutney and stir in.
Serve with pita bread.
Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
In a bowl, combine ground turkey or chicken with dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Form the mixture into a patty.
Place the patty in the preheated skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side or until fully cooked.
While the patty is cooking, mix crumbled feta, Greek yogurt, and chopped fresh dill in a small bowl to make the herb-feta sauce.
Cut the whole wheat pita bread in half to form two pockets.
Once the patty is cooked, assemble the Greek-inspired burger by placing it inside the pita pocket.
Stuff the pita pocket with tomato, cucumber, and red onion.
Drizzle the herb-feta sauce over the burger and vegetables.
Add lettuce leaves for freshness.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped romaine lettuce, diced tomatoes, cucumbers, red bell peppers, red onions, fresh parsley, and fresh mint leaves.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and pepper to create the dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad and toss to combine.
Add the toasted and broken pita pieces to the salad and gently toss again.
In a bowl, combine lamb cubes with olive oil, minced garlic, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Mix well to coat the lamb evenly.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Grill the lamb skewers for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until cooked to desired doneness.
While the lamb is grilling, warm the whole wheat pita bread in the microwave or oven.
Remove the lamb skewers from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes.
Slide the cooked lamb off the skewers and onto the warm pita bread.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Place the whole-wheat pita bread on a baking sheet or oven-safe tray.
Spread the pizza sauce evenly over the pita bread, leaving a small border around the edges.
Sprinkle the shredded part-skim mozzarella cheese evenly over the sauce.
Distribute the diced bell peppers, sliced mushrooms, and pepperoni evenly on top of the cheese.
Sprinkle the dried oregano over the toppings for extra flavor.
Place the prepared pita pizza in the preheated oven.
Bake for about 8-10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted, and the edges of the pita bread are crispy and slightly golden.
Carefully remove the Pita Pizza from the oven using oven mitts.
Let it cool for a minute, then slice it into wedges or squares.
In a bowl, combine chicken slices, olive oil, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Toss to coat the chicken with the seasonings.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the seasoned chicken to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until it's cooked through and slightly browned.
In a separate bowl, mix low-fat Greek yogurt, minced garlic, and lemon juice to create a simple tzatziki sauce.
Warm the whole wheat pita bread in the skillet for about 10-15 seconds on each side.
Lay out the warm pita bread on a plate.
Place the cooked chicken on the pita bread.
Top with diced tomatoes and cucumbers.
Drizzle the tzatziki sauce over the gyro.
Fold the pita bread in half to create your Greek Chicken Gyro.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.

In a mixing bowl, combine the sliced zucchini, red bell pepper, red onion, and eggplant. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, 1/4 tsp ground cumin, 1/4 tsp ground coriander, and paprika. Toss to coat.
Spread the seasoned vegetables on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast the vegetables in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly caramelized.

While the vegetables are roasting, prepare the baked falafel. In a food processor, combine chickpeas, chopped onion, garlic, 1/2 tsp ground cumin, 1/2 tsp ground coriander, salt, and pepper. Pulse until well combined.
Form the falafel mixture into small patties.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Cook the falafel patties for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are golden and cooked through.
Warm the whole wheat pita bread in the oven for a few minutes.
To assemble the sandwich, spread plain Greek yogurt on the pita bread, place the roasted vegetables and baked falafel inside, and garnish with fresh parsley leaves.
Place the dried chickpeas in a bowl and cover them with water. Let them soak overnight. Drain before using.
In a food processor, add the soaked and drained chickpeas along with chopped parsley, cilantro, diced onion, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and baking soda.
Pulse the mixture until everything is well combined and forms a coarse paste. Scrape down the sides of the processor bowl if needed.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and stir in the flour. The mixture should hold together when formed into a ball. If it's too wet, add a bit more flour.
Shape the falafel mixture into small balls or patties using your hands. If the mixture is sticky, wet your hands slightly to prevent sticking.
Heat vegetable oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Make sure the oil is hot enough before frying the falafel.
Carefully place the shaped falafel into the hot oil, cooking them in batches to avoid overcrowding the pan. Fry for 2-3 minutes on each side or until they're golden brown and crispy.
Once done, transfer the fried falafel to a plate lined with paper towels to absorb excess oil.
Once the falafel is cooked, warm the whole-grain pita bread in the oven for a few minutes.
Carefully open the warm pita halves.
Stuff each pita half with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, diced cucumber, and diced red onion.
Place the baked falafel balls into the pita pockets on top of the vegetables.
Drizzle the prepared tzatziki sauce over the falafel.
Combine artichokes with feta cheese, green onions, lemon juice, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Spoon into pita halves. Divide chicken slices and add to pita pockets.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the eggplant slices on a baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil, and season with salt and pepper.
Roast the eggplant in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly crispy.
While the eggplant is roasting, warm the whole wheat pita bread in the oven for a few minutes.
To assemble the Sabich Sandwich, spread hummus and tahini sauce on the pita bread.
Place the roasted eggplant slices, sliced hard-boiled egg, tomato slices, and red onion on top.
Garnish with fresh parsley leaves.
Fold the pita bread and enjoy your Sabich Sandwich, a delightful and flavorful meal for one person!
Place chicken cutlets in a shallow baking dish. In a small bowl, whisk together lemon juice, 2 tbsp olive oil, oregano, and 2 cloves garlic.
Pour mixture over chicken cutlets and let marinate 10 minutes.
Heat grill pan over high heat.
Remove cutlets from marinade and season on both sides with salt and pepper.
Grill until cooked through, 3 minutes per side.
Let rest 5 minutes, then thinly slice.
In a large bowl, toss together cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, feta, and parsley. In a small bowl, whisk together red wine vinegar and remaining garlic clove.
Slowly add remaining 4 tbsp olive oil in steady stream, whisking constantly.
Season with salt and pepper.
Drizzle vinaigrette over salad and toss gently to combine.
Stuff each pita pocket half with grilled chicken and top with Greek salad.
maple syrup: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Toss the diced sweet potatoes in a little olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread them on a baking sheet and roast for about 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly caramelized.
While the sweet potatoes are roasting, prepare the tamarind dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together tamarind concentrate, maple syrup, olive oil, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Once the sweet potatoes are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool slightly.
In a mixing bowl, combine the roasted sweet potatoes, finely chopped red onion, fresh cilantro,, toasted pecans, cucumber, arugula and carrots.
Drizzle the tamarind dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasonings, if needed.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
In a bowl, toss the diced sweet potato with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Spread the seasoned sweet potato on a baking sheet in a single layer.

Roast the sweet potato in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until it's tender and slightly caramelized. Stir or flip the sweet potato halfway through for even cooking. Once done, remove it from the oven and let it cool.

While the sweet potato is roasting, prepare the tamarind dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together 1 tablespoon of tamarind paste, 1 tablespoon of maple syrup, 1 tablespoon of olive oil, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
In a serving bowl, place 2 cups of chopped kale.
Once the roasted sweet potato has cooled slightly, add it to the bowl with the kale.
Drizzle the tamarind dressing over the sweet potato and kale.
Toss all the ingredients together until the sweet potato and kale are coated with the dressing.
Place the strawberries, mango, and clementine slices in the blender. Let sit for a few minutes so that it starts to thaw slightly.
Add the orange juice, protein powder and maple syrup. Blend on high speed until smooth and creamy.
Serve immediately.
In a small pot, combine the rolled oats and water or milk.
Add the mashed ripe banana, cocoa powder, and a pinch of salt to the pot.
Place the pot over medium heat and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low and let the oatmeal simmer for about 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches your desired consistency.
If desired, add maple syrup or honey for sweetness and stir well.
Once cooked to your preferred consistency, remove the chocolate banana oatmeal from the heat.
Transfer the oatmeal to a bowl and top with sliced banana.
Preheat the oven to 375°F with a rack in the center of the oven. Butter the bottom of a 9×13 casserole dish.
In a small bowl, combine the filling ingredients: rolled oats, walnuts, protein powder, cranberries, and cinnamon.
Drizzle with maple syrup and melted butter and stir to combine. Cut pears in half lengthwise. Scoop out the seeds with a teaspoon.
Arrange pears in the buttered casserole dish and divide the filling evenly between pears. Lightly sprinkle cinnamon over pears.
Bake uncovered for 25 minutes. Serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together the pure maple syrup, soy sauce, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to create the marinade.
Place the chicken thigh in a shallow dish and pour the marinade over it, ensuring the chicken is evenly coated. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
Remove the chicken thigh from the marinade and shake off any excess.
Place the chicken thigh in the air fryer basket.
Air fry at 375°F for 18-20 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the chicken is cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
Once cooked, remove the chicken thigh from the air fryer and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the air-fried maple chicken thigh hot with your favorite side dishes.
Add all ingredients except lemon and water to a blender. Squeeze over the lemon juice and pour in half the water.
Close the lid tightly and mix for 2 minutes, until smooth. Then add the rest of the water and mix again for about 30 seconds.
Serve immediately.
Preheat an air fryer to 400°F.
Whisk together maple syrup, olive oil, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Add Brussels sprouts and toss to coat. Arrange Brussels sprouts in a single layer in an air fryer basket without overcrowding; work in batches, if necessary.
Cook for 4 minutes. Shake basket and cook until sprouts are deep golden brown and tender, 4 to 6 minutes more.
Whisk the eggs, milk and cinnamon together in a bowl until well-combined. Heat a non-stick fry pan over medium heat, melt the butter.
Dip the bread in the egg mixture, evenly coating both sides, shake off any excess mixture. Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side until lightly brown.
Serve French toast warm, with berries and drizzle of maple syrup.
Preheat your oven to 425°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add the milk, syrup, egg, and vanilla extract to the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined. Do not overmix; small lumps are okay.
Line a small baking sheet or pan with parchment paper or lightly grease it.
Pour the pancake batter onto the prepared baking sheet, spreading it evenly.
Arrange the sliced strawberries over the pancake batter.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until the pancake is puffed and golden brown.
Once done, remove from the oven and let it cool slightly before slicing.
Serve your delicious Strawberry Sheet-Pan Pancake warm.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile mix vegetable broth, maple syrup and soy sauce in a bowl.
Heat peanut oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add black beans, green onions, garlic, ginger. Saute until aromatic, about 1 minute.
Add chili bean paste and the soy sauce mixture. Cook and stir until thick, 3 to 5 minutes. Add tofu, cook until heated through, about 1 minute more.
Transfer cooked tofu to a serving dish drizzle sesame oil on top. Serve with cooked rice.
Pour the almond milk into a bowl. Add the oats and allow them to soak for 5 minutes.
Pour the oat mixture into a blender and add all of the other ingredients. Blend until smooth.
Serve immediatelay.
In the blender jar add all ingredients starting with milk first. Blend until you smooth consistency smoothie. If its too thick, you can add a little more milk and blend again.
Pour in serving glasses and top with blueberries and fresh mint, serve.
Whisk together eggs, milk, cinnamon and maple syrup. Heat a non-stick pan up to a moderate heat and add oil.
Pour in the egg mixture and cook until almost set, then flip and cook for a minute more. When the omelette is cooked, transfer to a plate.
Top half of the omelette with half of mandarin orange pieces and half of walnuts, then fold the omelette and repeat.
In a bowl or jar, combine the rolled oats, grated carrot, Greek yogurt, almond milk, maple syrup or honey, cinnamon, nutmeg, and vanilla extract.
Mix all the ingredients together until well combined.
Cover the bowl or jar with a lid or plastic wrap and place it in the refrigerator.
Let the mixture sit in the fridge overnight or for at least 4-6 hours to allow the oats to soften and the flavors to meld.
Once ready to eat, give the oats a stir. If it's too thick, you can add a splash of milk to reach your desired consistency.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Slice the peaches. Mix them with maple syrup, cinnamon, salt, vanilla extract, and lemon juice. Transfer the peaches and mixture to a pie pan and bake for 15 minutes.
While the peaches bake, combine the almond flour, cinnamon, salt, baking powder, and baking soda.
When the peaches in the oven are done, remove them from the oven and sprinkle liberally with the dry topping.
Place butter slices over the top of the cobbler. Place it in the oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove from the oven, let cool 5 minutes before serving.
Place all of the ingredients in your blender. Blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
In a mixing bowl, toss the Brussels sprouts and butternut squash with olive oil, salt, and pepper until coated.
Spread the Brussels sprouts and butternut squash mixture on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes or until the vegetables are tender and lightly browned.
While the veggies are roasting, in a small bowl, mix together the balsamic vinegar and maple syrup to make the dressing.
Once the vegetables are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool slightly.
Place a handful of spinach in a bowl.
Arrange the roasted Brussels sprouts and butternut squash on top of the greens.
Sprinkle chopped pecans and cranberries over the salad.
Drizzle the balsamic-honey dressing over the salad.
Mix and divide oats, milk, 3/4 cup yogurt, chia seeds, protein powder, 2 tsp cinnamon and maple syrup in cups. Let it sit in the fridge for 2 hours to overnight.
Before serving drizzle the rest of the yogurt and top with the remaining cinnamon and pecans.
Heat a skillet over medium heat.
Add diced onion, bell pepper, and minced garlic to the skillet. Sauté until they soften.
Add the shredded jackfruit to the skillet and stir to combine with the vegetables.
Pour in the tomato sauce or ketchup and tomato paste. Mix well.
Add maple syrup, chili powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Stir until all ingredients are well incorporated.
Let the mixture simmer for about 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the flavors meld and the jackfruit softens.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Once ready, spoon the Jackfruit Sloppy Joe mixture onto a hamburger bun or bread of your choice.
worchestire sauce: To prepare dressing, combine water. lemon juice, ground black pepper, Sriracha, Worcestershire sauce, olive oil, and garlic.
To prepare salad, combine croutons, 2 tablespoons cheese, shrimp, lettuce, onion and olives in a large bowl. Add dressing; toss well to coat.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
In a pot over medium heat, add thinly sliced onions, minced garlic, beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the soup simmer for about 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together and the onions to soften.
While the soup simmers, toast the baguette or bread slice in the oven for about 5 minutes until slightly crisp.
Sprinkle the shredded Gruyère cheese over the toasted bread slice.
Place the cheese-topped bread under the broiler for 1-2 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Once the soup is ready, ladle it into a bowl.
Place the Gruyère toast on top of the soup.
Cook chicken in an oiled skillet over medium-high heat for7 to 8 minutes.
Meanwhile in a large saucepan cook and stir broccoli, mushrooms, carrot, and onion in ghee 6-8 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Stir in basil, and pepper. Add milk and cream all at once, add Worcestershire sauce and bouillon granules.
Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Add chicken, heat through.
Whisk tomato sauce, soy sauce, sherry, Worcestershire sauce, and garlic together in a bowl until sugar has dissolved. Set katsu sauce aside.
Preheat an air fryer to 350°F.
Meanwhile, lay chicken pieces on a clean work surface. Season with salt and pepper.
Place beaten eggs in a flat dish. Pour bread crumbs into a second flat dish. Dredge chicken pieces in egg and then in bread crumbs.
Repeat by dredging chicken in egg and then bread crumbs again, pressing down so that the bread crumbs stick to the chicken.
Place chicken pieces in the basket of the pre-heated air fryer. Oil the top.
Air fry for 10 minutes. Flip chicken pieces over using a spatula and spray the tops with nonstick cooking spray.
Cook for 8 minutes more. Transfer chicken to a cutting board and slice. Serve with katsu sauce.
In a bowl, combine the canned crabmeat, softened cream cheese, finely chopped green onion, Worcestershire sauce, and soy sauce. Mix well until all ingredients are thoroughly combined.
Lay out a wonton wrapper on a clean, dry surface.
Place about a teaspoon of the crab and cream cheese mixture in the center of the wonton wrapper.
Moisten the edges of the wonton wrapper with a little water using your fingertip.
Fold the wonton wrapper diagonally to create a triangle, sealing the edges to enclose the filling. Press down firmly to ensure it's sealed.
Heat about 1 inch of  oil in a small frying pan or skillet to 350°F.
Carefully place the crab Rangoon into the hot oil using tongs or a slotted spoon.
Fry for about 1-2 minutes on each side or until they are golden brown and crispy. Be sure not to overcrowd the pan; fry in batches if necessary.
Remove the fried crab Rangoon with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Heat the oil in a frying pan over a medium-high heat. Add the onion and cook for 5 minutes, stirring often, until the onion softens.

Add the garlic and cook for a further minute. Add the carrot and mushrooms and cook for a further 3 minutes. Add the minced beef and cook for 5 minutes, until browned. Stir often, breaking up any large chunks of meat with a spatula.
Add the wine. Bring to the boil and let it bubble for 2 minutes, until reduced by three quarters.

Add the tomato puree, oregano, thyme, Worcestershire sauce, beef stock cube, tinned tomatoes, brown sugar, salt and pepper. Stir and bring to the boil. Turn down the heat and simmer for 20 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, bring a large pot of lightly-salted water to the boil, add the pasta and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
To serve, place pasta on to a plate, top with bolognese and parsley.
Whisk olive oil, ghee, onion, wine, Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, parsley, seafood seasoning, garlic, and black pepper together in a bowl, add shrimp.
Toss to coat evenly. Cover bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for flavors to blend, at least 15 minutes.
Preheat an outdoor grill for medium-high heat and lightly oil the grate. Coat steaks with 2 teaspoons olive oil, sprinkle with steak seasoning.
Cook steaks until they are beginning to firm and have reached your desired doneness, 5 to 7 minutes per side.
Remove shrimp from marinade and grill for 2 to 3 minutes per side.
In a large bowl, mix the ground beef, blue cheese, chives, hot pepper sauce, Worcestershire sauce, black pepper, salt, and mustard. Cover, and refrigerate for 2 hours.
Preheat grill for high heat. Gently form the burger mixture into about 12 patties.
Oil the grill grate. Grill patties 5 minutes per side, or until well done. Serve on rolls with lettuce, tomato and onion.
In a mixing bowl, combine canned crab meat, cream cheese, green onions, Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Place a spoonful of the crab mixture in the center of each wonton wrapper.
Moisten the edges of the wonton wrappers with water, then fold them over to form triangles, pressing the edges to seal.
Preheat the air fryer to 375°F for about 3-5 minutes.
Lightly coat both sides of the crab Rangoon with cooking spray.
Place the crab Rangoon in the air fryer basket in a single layer, making sure they are not touching.
Air fry the crab Rangoon for 6-8 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until golden brown and crispy.
Serve the air-fried crab Rangoon hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a large skillet over medium heat, cook beef and 1 cup onion, crumbling meat, until beef is no longer pink and onion is tender, 6-8 minutes; crumble meat; drain.
Stir in tomato sauce, Velveeta, mustard and Worcestershire sauce until cheese is melted, 4-6 minutes.
Transfer to a greased 13 x 9 inches or 3 ½ quarts baking dish.
Sprinkle with cheddar cheese and bacon.
Top with Tater Tots.
Bake, uncovered, 35-40 minutes or until bubbly.
Top with tomatoes, pickles and remaining onion.
In a medium bowl, stir together the olive oil, ranch dressing, Worcestershire sauce, rosemary, salt, lemon juice, white vinegar and pepper.
Let stand for 5 minutes. Place chicken in the bowl, and stir to coat with the marinade. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Preheat the grill for medium-high heat. Thread chicken onto skewers and discard marinade.
Lightly oil the grill grate. Grill skewers for 8 to 12 minutes, or until the chicken is no longer pink in the center, and the juices run clear.
Slice salmon fillets into 4 equal sections, width-wise. Rotate each section 90 degrees and slice lengthwise, about 1/4-inch thick.
Remove pin bones with needle-nose pliers. Don't slice bony sections near the center of the fillet.
Stir soy sauce, molasses, sugar, Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, black pepper, liquid smoke, and hot sauce together in a glass or plastic bowl until marinade is well-mixed.
Place salmon in the marinade, ensuring every strip is covered. Cover bowl and refrigerate for 4 hours.
Remove salmon from bowl and drain liquid using a colander. Pat the salmon dry using paper towels.
Place each salmon strip in a dehydrator and run the dehydrator according to manufacturer's instructions until desired doneness is reached, about 6 hours.
Check every few hours for doneness.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile melt ghee in a large deep non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken and cook, for 8 minutes.
Add the onion, mushrooms, and garlic. Saute 3 minutes or until slightly softened.
Stir in the soup, water, milk, lemon juice, and Worcestershire sauce; heat to a boil. Reduce the heat to medium. Cook, uncovered, stirring often, 8 minutes.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and carrot to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add the ground turkey to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Stir in frozen peas,  broth, tomato paste, and Worcestershire sauce. Simmer for a few minutes until the mixture thickens slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the turkey mixture to a small baking dish.
Spread the mashed potatoes evenly over the turkey mixture.
Use a fork to create ridges on the surface of the mashed potatoes.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the mashed potatoes are golden brown on top.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Season pork chops with pepper and salt. Dredge the meat into the flour, shake excess flour and then dip it into the beaten egg, shake excess.
Dredge in panko, then cover the meat evenly with panko breadcrumbs by patting with your hands to make the meat is throughly covered.
Working in batches, deep fry the cutlet, until golden on the bottom for about 3 minutes. Flip and cook on the other side for 3 minutes.
In a medium bowl stir ketchup, Worcestershire, soy and oyster sauce.
Serve sauce and pork chops with shredded cabbage.
Preheat your oven to 375°F .
In a mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, chopped onion, bell pepper, mushrooms, garlic, rolled oats, egg, Worcestershire sauce, dried thyme, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Divide the mixture into two equal portions and shape each portion into a mini meatloaf.
Place the mini meatloaves on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or aluminum foil lightly greased with cooking spray.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the mini meatloaves are cooked through and browned on the outside.
Remove from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the mini meatloaves with your favorite sides or sauce.
In a mixing bowl, combine lump crab meat, mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, Old Bay seasoning, Worcestershire sauce, panko breadcrumbs, and chopped fresh parsley. Mix until well combined.
Divide the crab mixture into two equal portions and shape each portion into a patty.
Preheat the air fryer to 375°F for about 3-5 minutes.
Lightly coat both sides of the crab cakes with cooking spray.
Place the crab cakes in the air fryer basket, making sure they are not touching.
Air fry the crab cakes for 10-12 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until golden brown and heated through.
Serve the air-fried crab cakes hot with your favorite sauce or lemon wedges.
Preheat oven on broiler setting.
To Make Hollandaise: Fill the bottom of a double boiler part-way with water. Make sure that water does not touch the top pan.
Bring water to a gentle simmer. Whisk together egg yolks, lemon juice, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, and 1 tablespoon water.
Add the melted ghee to egg yolk mixture 1 or 2 tablespoons at a time while whisking yolks constantly.
Continue whisking until all ghee is incorporated. Whisk in salt, then remove from heat. Place a lid on pan to keep sauce warm.
To Poach Eggs: Fill a large saucepan with 3 inches of water. Bring water to a gentle simmer, then add vinegar.
Carefully break eggs into simmering water, and allow to cook for 2 1/2 to 3 minutes.
Yolks should still be soft in center. Remove eggs from water with a slotted spoon and set on a warm plate.
While eggs are poaching, brown the bacon in a medium skillet over medium-high heat and toast the English muffins on a baking sheet under the broiler.
Top each one with a slice of bacon, followed by one poached egg. Drizzle with hollandaise sauce. Sprinkle with chopped chives and serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.

In a mixing bowl, combine ground beef, breadcrumbs, milk, diced onion, diced bell pepper, diced celery, egg, ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper. Mix until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a small greased loaf pan and shape it into a mini loaf.
Spread barbecue sauce evenly over the top of the meatloaf.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 30-35 minutes, or until the meatloaf is cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 160°F.
Remove from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Whip cream cheese with flaxseed oil in a bowl with an electric hand mixer.
Add onion, capers, chives, lemon zest and juice, seafood seasoning, parsley, Worcestershire, and hot sauce.
Mix well. Add tuna and mix until well blended.
pumpkin seeds: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Peel the pumpkin and cut the flesh into cubes. Do the same with the rutabaga. Peel the shallot and cut into wedges. Peel the garlic cloves.
Put everything in a baking dish. Add olive oil, salt and pepper. Roast in the oven for 30 minutes. Place the roasted vegetables in a pot.
Add vegetable stock, and bring to a boil. Let simmer for a couple of minutes. Remove from the stove. Add the butter, divided into cubes.
Mix the soup with a hand blender. Add salt and pepper to taste. Serve the soup with sour cream and roasted pumpkin seeds.
Place basil leaves, 1 garlic clove, Parmesan, olive oil, pumpkin seeds, salt, and pepper in the bowl of a food processor and pulse until smooth.
Heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add leeks and saute for 5 minutes. Lower the heat if the leeks are starting to brown.
Add the remaining garlic. Stir and cook for 2 more minutes.
Add asparagus, zucchini, green beans, and navy beans. Stir and cook for one minute.
Add the broth. Add salt and pepper to taste. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to a gentle simmer and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add kale and continue simmering for another 5 minutes.
Add peas and pesto and cook for 2 more minutes. Serve with lemon juice.
Place almond milk, chia seeds, kale and 1 banana into a blender and blend until smooth.
Place into bowl and top with sliced banana, shredded coconut, pumpkin seeds, and blackberries.
Preheat your oven to 300°F.
Remove the seeds from the pumpkin and separate them from any pulp or strings.
Rinse the seeds thoroughly under cold water to remove any remaining pumpkin flesh. Pat the seeds dry with a clean kitchen towel or paper towels.
In a small bowl, combine the pumpkin seeds, olive oil, salt, and any optional seasonings. Toss to coat the seeds evenly with the oil and seasonings.
Spread the seasoned pumpkin seeds in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Place the baking sheet in the preheated oven and roast the pumpkin seeds for about 30-35 minutes, or until they are golden brown and crispy. Stir or shake the pan occasionally for even roasting.
In a medium skillet bring water to a boil and cook eggs until they are hard-boiled, 5 minutes.
In a mixing bowl, mash 2 eggs with 1 peeled avocado.
Add in the juice of a lemon and plain yogurt, season with salt and pepper, and mix.
Spread avocado-egg mixture on toast slices and top with slices of radishes and avocado, pumpkin seeds, and halves of eggs.
Add the Greek yogurt to a bowl. Add the fresh blueberries and halved strawberries.
Add the berries and nuts and enjoy.
Spread the peanut butter over the bananas, top with seeds and nuts, slice and enjoy.
Add all ingredients to blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately, garnished with peppermint.
Stir oats, chia seeds, pepitas, pineapple, peanut butter, honey, coconut, mango, blueberries, flax, and wheat germ together in a large bowl. Roll into balls.
Bring water to a boil then stir in oats, cinnamon, vanilla, and pinch of salt. Cook about 5 minutes over medium heat and stir occasionally.
Mix in the almond butter, bananas and apples and remove from heat.
To serve, top with almonds and pumpkin seeds.
Prehet the oven to 350°F.
In a medium bowl add the eggs, milk, sugar, vanilla extract and ground cinnamon. Mix until fully combined.
Add bread that has been torn into 1 inch pieces, raisins and seeds. Mix until you see that all the bread pieces are in touch with the liquid.
Brush a small baking mould with oil or give it a light coating with oil spray. Transfer the bread pudding mix and spread evenly.
Bake for 10 minutes in the middle rack of the oven. Then transfer to the highest rack, turn on the broiler setting at high and bake about 3 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 400°F and place parchment paper on two baking sheets.
Gently toss tomatoes in olive oil and a dash of salt.
Place in a single layer on a baking sheet and bake for 10 minutes. Set aside.
Gently toss mushrooms in olive oil and place them in a single layer on a baking sheet. Sprinkle with salt to taste and lots of basil. Bake for 25 minutes.
Cool briefly and dice mushrooms.
If making your own pesto, blend all the pesto ingredients together using a food processor or blender. Set aside.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
While mushrooms and tomatoes roast, bake pizza crust for 7-10 minutes.
Add the pesto to the pizza crust, then fresh mozzarella, and melt the fresh mozzarella, approximately 5 minutes.
Top the melted cheese with the roasted tomatoes, roasted mushrooms, balsamic glaze and fresh basil.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook beets, green beans and brussels sprouts. Drain and set aside.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
When beets are cool, rub the skins off and discard. Chop beets into quarters. Add to the food processor along with the thyme, basil, milk, balsamic vinegar and garlic.
Pulse until smooth, adding more milk as necessary to make a pourable sauce. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour the sauce over the prepared noodles and cook over medium heat until warmed through, tossing to coat the noodles with the sauce.
Add the sprouts, green beans and pumpkin seeds and serve immediately.
With the rack in the middle position, preheat the oven to 350°F. Oil a muffin tin or line it with paper liners.
In a bowl, combine both flours with the baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon and salt.
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, applesauce and oil. Using a wooden spoon, stir in the zucchini, pumpkin seeds, raisins and dry ingredients.
Divide the batter among the muffin cups. Bake for 25 minutes. Let cool on a wire rack before serving.
Heat the oven to 400 degrees F.
Arrange the apricot halves cut side up on a roasting tray and sprinkle with olive oil, and plenty of salt and pepper.
Scatter the herbs over the apricots, then roast for about 20 minutes, until golden.
Lay the pumpkin seeds on a separate roasting tray, mix with the olive oil, paprika, and soy sauce, then season.
Roast in the same oven for 10-12 minutes, stirring halfway cooking, until nicely toasted and puffed up.
Set both the apricots and seeds aside to cool.
Meanwhile, shred the kale leaves with a large chopping knife, then put them in a large bowl with another tablespoon of olive oil and a big pinch of salt.
Massage the oil into the kale with your hands for three to four minutes, until it begins to wilt and soften.
Add the lettuce, tomatoes and mint leaves to the bowl.
To make the dressing, mix the mustard and vinegar in a jar with half a teaspoon of salt and olive oil.
Toss the salad in the dressing, taste, adjust seasoning to taste, salt or vinegar, as required.

Arrange on one large plate or divide between four individual plates, then top with the apricot halvesIf serving on one large plate, tear over the burrata and scatter with half the seeds (reserve the rest for another time).
If you are using individual plates, nestle a burrata in among the middle of the salad and scatter with seeds.
Serve straight away with bread.
Heat the olive oil in a soup pot over medium heat. Add the onion and celery. Saute for 5 minutes.
Add the pumpkin, garlic, rosemary, and salt. Pour in the vegetable stock, then simmer for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat. Blend the soup using a stick blender until pureed and creamy.
Stir in the heavy cream. Garnish with some heavy cream and pumpkin seeds, and serve.
Preheat oven to 250°F. Line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper.
Combine oil, seasoning, and salt in a bowl. Add seeds, stir with a spoon until coated. Spread in a single layer on the baking sheets.
Bake in the preheated oven, stirring occasionally, until golden brown and crisp, about 45 minutes.
In a small bowl combine pumpkin seeds, olive oil, and salt, and cook in hot olive oil for 5 minutes.
Place vegetables on a serving platter,
Top with prosciutto, roasted pumpkin seeds and garnish with basil leaves.
Heat olive oil in a small pot over medium heat.
Add diced onions and cook for about 2-3 minutes, or until they become translucent.
Add diced butternut squash, red lentils, ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, and ground cinnamon to the pot.
Stir and cook for another 2-3 minutes to infuse the spices and lightly toast the lentils.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for about 20-25 minutes, or until the butternut squash and lentils are tender.
Use an immersion blender or a regular blender to puree the soup until smooth.
Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve your Persian-Style Butternut Squash Soup hot, and garnish with chopped fresh cilantro, a dollop of yogurt and pumpkin seeds.
Place all ingredients into a blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
soy milk: Combine pumpkin puree, applesauce, apple juice, and soy milk in a blender; blend until smooth.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Boil cauliflower florets over high heat for 5 minutes, or until just tender. Drain and place into baking dish. Add uncooked grated cauliflower.
