Combine eggs and milk in a large bowl and beat with a fork until smooth. Add cheddar cheese, garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper.
Pour egg mixture over cauliflower. Top with reserved cheese. Bake for 25 minutes.
Place kale, banana, soy milk, flax seeds into a blender. Cover and purée until smooth. Serve over ice.
Stir flour, soy milk, egg, flax seeds, baking powder and vanilla extract together in a bowl.
Heat a lightly oiled griddle over medium-high heat. Drop batter by large spoonfuls onto the griddle and cook until bubbles form and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes.
Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 2 to 3 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.
Serve sprinkled with chopped walnuts and lemon juice.
Cook the pasta to al dente according to package instructions, then drain, reserving some of the pasta water.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the butter and olive oil. Once the butter has melted, add the onion and cook for 3 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for another minute. Add the tomato passata, dried basil, red chili flakes and salt and pepper to taste.
Simmer for 5 minutes and add cream and milk.
Add the cooked pasta, toss to combine and warm through for a few minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a cupcake tray with 12 cupcake liners. Set aside.
Sift the flour into a mixing bowl and add sugar, pea proteins, baking soda, salt and matcha powder. Mix together.
Prepare your vegan buttermilk by adding 1 tbsp white vinegar to a measuring jug and then adding soy milk.
Add the yogurt, oil and vanilla extract and mix into a batter. Don’t overmix.
Divide the batter evenly between the 12 cupcake liners in your cupcake tray.
Bake for 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center of one of the cupcakes comes out clean. Serve with some blueberries.
Blend soy milk, oats, strawberries, and banana in a blender until smooth. Add vanilla and blend again until smooth.
Pour into glasses and serve
Place diced potatoes and beets in a medium pot, cover with water, cover with a lid and boil until tender. Drain and add cooked potatoes to a mixing bowl. Cool slightly.
Add diced beets, apples, onion, pickle, and ¼ cup of water. Mash slightly with a potato masher. Consistency should be smooth with chunks.
Whip vegan cream cheese and soymilk with an electric mixer until smooth and fluffy.
Fold vegan whipped cream cheese mixture into salad and stir just until well combined. Season as desired with salt and black pepper. Serve immediately.
In a pan, melt some butter. Add the shallots and cook 3 minutes. Add all the mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the gnocchi and cook at high heat. Add the spices. Add the soy milk a bit of broth. Add the spinach. Continue to cook at high heat.
Add the cheese, vegan butter and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the milk, banana, powders, cashews and salt to a blender and blend for about three minutes until the cashews are nice and smooth.
Let chill in refrigerator or serve right away.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Oil a baking dish.
Place the potatoes in a pot, cover with cold water, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Turn the heat to medium-low, and boil the potatoes until tender, about 25 minutes, drain.
Stir the vegan sour cream, soy milk, olive oil, vegan cream cheese, and salt into the potatoes.
Mash with a potato masher until smooth and fluffy. Set the potatoes aside.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat, and cook and stir the onion, carrots, celery, peas, and tomato for about 10 minutes.
Stir in the Italian seasoning, garlic, and pepper. Reduce the heat to medium-low, and crumble the vegetarian ground beef substitute into the skillet.
Cook and stir, breaking up the meat substitute, until the mixture is hot, about 5 minutes.
Spread the vegetarian meat substitute mixture into the bottom of the baking dish, and top with the mashed potatoes, smoothing them into an even layer.
Sprinkle the potatoes with the shredded vegan cheese. Bake in the preheated oven until the cheese is melted and slightly browned and the casserole is hot, about 20 minutes.
Add all of the ingredients in a blender and blend.
Stir soy milk, water, quinoa, blueberries, chia seeds, and honey together in a slow cooker.
Cook on Low 6 to 8 hours. After cooking garnish with mint.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add celery and garlic; saute until slightly softened, about 4 minutes.
Add water and bouillon cubes; stir until bouillon dissolves. Add soy milk, nutritional yeast, potatoes, and onion. Simmer for 15 to 20 minutes.
Add all corn, parsley, paprika, and pepper. Simmer until potatoes are soft and flavors have blended, 30 to 40 minutes more.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Bring a water to a boil and prepare lasagna noodled according to package directions. Drain carefully and set aside on a towel.
Place the tofu, soymilk, garlic powder, lemon juice, basil, and salt in a food processor or blender and blend until smooth.
Cover the bottom of a baking pan with a thin layer of tomato sauce, then a layer of noodles.
Follow that with a layer of half the tofu and half the spinach. Continue, using half the remaining tomato sauce and noodles and the rest.
End with the remaining noodles covered by the remaining tomato sauce. Bake for 20 minutes.
Grind all ingredients excluding coconut milk and laksa leaves.
Heat some oil and saute till fragrant. Add stock, coconut milk and yong tau foo. Stir to mix. Bring to a boil, add salt to taste. Serve garnished with laksa leaves.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Cook potatoes in a large pot of boiling water for 20 minutes or until soft. Drain and cool.
Push the potatoes through a potato ricer into a large bowl or onto a floured bench top. Sprinkle over the salt.
Thoroughly combine the flour and potatoes, scrunching it together with your hands and forming into a dough.
Turn the dough onto a floured bench and roll into a large log shape. Divide into 8 pieces.
Roll each piece into a long rope. Then cut into small pieces. Place pieces onto a tray lined with a fresh towel until you are ready to cook them.
Heat oil in a medium pan and add the garlic. Lower the heat and cook garlic for approximately 1 minute.
Pour in the passata and salt. Cover and simmer for approximately 10 minutes.
In a medium pot over medium to high heat, pour in the soy milk and immediately whisk in the corn starch until dissolved.
Add the remaining ingredients and stir briskly to combine. Stir frequently until the sauce becomes thick.
In a large pot of salted boiling water, cook the gnocchi in batches. When the gnocchi float to the surface, carefully remove and drain.
Transfer gnocchi to the tomato sugo and stir to coat. Pour the gnocchi into a large baking or casserole dish. Dollop the mozzarella sauce on top.
Bake for 10 minutes and serve.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Heat the olive oil in a large wide skillet over a medium high flame. Add shallots and thyme and saute for 1 minute.
Add the leeks and continue cooking until they start to soften and begin to brown.
Add the mushrooms and a pinch of sea salt, cook, stirring until they start to darken and wilt, about 5 minutes.
Add the water and cauliflower, cover, and turn the heat down to low. Cook for 5 minutes.
Melt the butter in a heavy-bottomed saucepan. Stir in the flour and cook, stirring constantly, until the paste cooks and bubbles a bit.
Add the hot milk, continuing to stir as the sauce thickens. Bring it to a boil. Add salt and pepper to taste, lower the heat, and cook, stirring for 2 minutes.
Tip the vegetables into a baking dish and pour the bechamel sauce over them. Sprinkle with cheese and bake for 10 minutes.
Heat 1 tsp of olive oil in a skillet, over medium heat. When hot, add the sliced onion and saute for a minute, until translucent.
Add the sliced potatoes and stir. Let them cook for about 2 minutes, stirring gently. Add water and let them boil for about 10 minutes,
When potatoes are tender, drain the water and let them cool.
Using a food processor, mix flour, milk, 1 tsp olive oil, salt, and pepper until they're well incorporated, smooth, and sticky, for about 2 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the 'egg' mixture with the cooled potato and onion slices. Stir gently to incorporate.
Heat a non-stick large skillet over medium heat. Add the olive oil. Pour the tortilla mixture into the skillet, put a lid on it and let it cook for about 10 minutes.
Flip it on the other side and cook for 5 more minutes so it gets golden and crispy on both sides.
lemon zest: Combine cashew nuts, apricots, coconut, and dates in a food processor bowl. Pulse ingredients until mixture is crumbly.
Add citrus zest, flour, spices, and salt to mixture. Pulse ingredients to combine well, and process on high speed until mixture starts to stick together.
Line a tray with parchment paper. With clean hands, shape the mixture into balls. Garnish with some sesame seeds and store in refrigerator,
Preheat your air fryer to 400°F.
In a bowl, toss the shrimp with olive oil, lemon zest, black pepper, and salt until evenly coated.
Lightly grease the air fryer basket with cooking spray or olive oil.
Place the seasoned shrimp in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
Air fry the shrimp at 400°F for 5-6 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until they are pink and opaque.
Once cooked, remove the shrimp from the air fryer and transfer to a serving plate.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve hot with lemon wedges if desired.
Mash together all ingredients in a bowl with a fork until combined well. Serve with bread.
Cook the gnocchi according to package instructions until they float to the surface. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced bell peppers to the skillet. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the diced cherry tomatoes to the skillet and cook for another 2-3 minutes until they begin to release their juices.
Stir in the fresh spinach leaves and cook until wilted.
Add the cooked gnocchi to the skillet, along with the garlic powder, lemon zest, and lemon juice.
Pour the heavy cream into the skillet and toss everything together until well combined.
Cook for another 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and coats the gnocchi and vegetables.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove the skillet from heat and stir in the ricotta cheese until incorporated.
Preheat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Season the chicken breast with salt, pepper, and half of the lemon juice. Grill the chicken for about 6-8 minutes per side.
Remove from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing.
While the chicken is resting, prepare the quinoa according to the package instructions.
Toss the mixed vegetables and diced red onion with the remaining lemon juice, minced garlic, chopped fresh herbs, lemon zest, salt, and pepper. Spread the vegetables evenly on a baking sheet.
Roast the vegetables in the oven at 400°F for about 15-20 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly caramelized.
In a bowl, combine the cooked quinoa, roasted vegetables, and sliced grilled chicken. Toss gently to mix all the ingredients together.
Serve the grilled lemon herb chicken with quinoa and roasted vegetables warm and enjoy!
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F for 3 minutes.
While fryer is preheating, rinse tilapia fillets and pat dry with a paper towel.
Combine lemon zest, olive oil, pepper, salt, and garlic in a bowl. Rub spice mixture over the tilapia.
Sprinkle lightly with paprika. Spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray and place the filets inside, coated side up.
Air fry until fish can be flaked with a fork, 7 to 10 minutes.
Remove tilapia to a plate, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve with lemon wedges.
In a mixing bowl, toss shrimp with olive oil, lemon zest, black pepper, garlic powder, and salt until evenly coated.
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F  for about 3-5 minutes.
Place shrimp in the air fryer basket in a single layer, making sure they are not overcrowded.
Air fry the shrimp for 5-7 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through cooking, until shrimp are pink and cooked through.
Serve the air-fried shrimp hot with lemon slices.
Preheat the oven to 400­°F. Grease a 2 cup muffin tin, or line with paper liners.
In a large bowl, mix together the yogurt, lemon juice, egg whites, and lemon extract.
In a separate bowl, stir together the flour, baking powder, salt, and lemon zest.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry, and mix until just blended. Gently stir in the raspberries.
Spoon batter evenly into the prepared muffin cups
Bake for 15 to 17 minutes in the preheated oven, or until the top springs back when lightly touched. Cool muffins in the tin on a wire rack.
Combine salmon, shallot, olive oil, and brine in a bowl. Toss with a spoon until evenly combined.
Evenly distribute salmon mixture onto crackers. Top with lemon zest, chives, and cracked black pepper.
Mix kale, red onion, carrot and raisins in a large bowl.
Whisk yogurt,, lemon zest, and lemon juice together in a bowl, pour over the kale mixture and toss to coat.
Refrigerate 2 hours to overnight. Toos the salad and serve.
Spread some butter on the baguette slices and toast them in a dry pan over low heat.
In a small mixing bowl, combine the ricotta cheese, lemon zest and honey, mix well.
Spread the ricotta mixture on to the toasted bread and enjoy alongside fruit.
Toast the bread slice to your liking.
Spread Greek yogurt evenly over the toasted bread.
Sprinkle fresh blueberries on top of the yogurt.
Zest the lemon directly over the blueberries and yogurt.
Drizzle honey over the lemon-blueberry yogurt toast.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Rinse and drain chickpeas, patting them dry using paper towels.
In a large bowl, drizzle olive oil over chickpeas and add seasonings, tossing until chickpeas are completely coated.
Spread chickpeas out on a prepared baking sheet and roast in oven for 25 to 30 minutes, shaking pan halfway through.
While the chickpeas are roasting, whisk together lemon juice, zest, garlic, ginger, olive oil and salt/pepper.
Right before serving, toss dressing with the kale and top with roasted chickpeas and grated parmesan cheese.
Serve and enjoy!
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Rub 4 brown paper bags (or parchment paper) inside and out with oil to grease.
Separate fish into 4 fillets and sprinkle with the lemon rind and lemon juice over the fish.
Top each with shallot, salt, and 1 tbsp butter each and cover evenly.
Divide the fish among the bags.
Place the bags on a large baking tray.
Fold in the ends and tuck under to seal.
Bake for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine peas and 1/4 cup water in a medium saucepan.
Cover and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Cook until peas are tender, about 6-8 minutes, stirring occasionally.  Drain peas and return to the pan.
Add in 2 tbsp butter, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, and pepper to taste.
Divide the peas among serving plates.
Top with the fish and drizzle over the juices.
Sprinkle with sea salt flakes and serve with lemon wedges, if desired.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add fettuccine and cook al dente. Drain and set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp butter and olive oil in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Toss shrimp with garlic, lemon zest, salt and pepper.
Add to the heated pan and cook just until shrimp turns pink, about 2 minutes per side. Remove from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add bring remaining butter and cream to a simmer. Let simmer about 5 minutes.
Stir in parmesan cheese and season with salt and pepper.
Remove from the heat and stir in the shrimp with any juices. Toss with cooked fettuccine and garnish with parsley.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti in the boiling water, stirring occasionally, for 9 minutes. Add shrimp and cook for 3 minutes more. Drain.
Whisk together Parmesan cheese, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Add spaghetti and shrimp to the sauce.
Sprinkle with basil and lemon zest and toss to coat.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta at a boil for about 12 minutes, drain. Transfer to a large bowl.
Put cheese in the bowl of a food processor, add garlic into the food processor and process until the cheese and garlic are combined.
Add spinach, basil, walnuts, lemon juice, lemon zest, and seasoning process until the mixture comes together into a thick paste. Scrape down the sides of the bowl with a spatula.
In a small saucepan, bring the chicken broth to a simmer over medium heat.
Add shredded chicken breast to the simmering broth.
In a small bowl, whisk together the egg, milk, lemon juice, and lemon zest.
Slowly pour the egg mixture into the simmering broth while stirring continuously.
Continue to cook for about 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the soup thickens slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Pour the soup into a bowl and garnish with chopped fresh dill if desired.
Serve hot.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instrictions on the packaging.
Add oil to a large skillet. Add garlic and cook for one minute. Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Add chicken breast and cook 5-6 minutes on each side until fully cooked.Pour in the wine and allow to cook for one minute.
Add in the chicken stock and simmer until slightly reduced. Stir in lemon juice, lemon zest, and parsley.
Serve immediately with extra lemon slices and parsley for garnish. Enjoy it with rice.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, mix breadcrumbs with finely chopped chorizo and olive oil.
Place the cod fillet on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Press the chorizo breadcrumb mixture onto the top of the cod fillet, covering it evenly.
Bake the cod in the preheated oven for approximately 12-15 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the topping is golden and crispy.
While the cod is baking, prepare the lemon aioli. In a small bowl, combine Greek yogurt, lemon zest, lemon juice, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Once the cod is done, remove it from the oven and let it cool for a minute.
apple juice: Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Warm the oil and 3 tbsp honey in a large saucepan, then pour in the oats, cinnamon, and nuts.
Add the egg white, then tip out onto a large baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes, until the oats are toasted brown, then remove.
Put the plums on a metal roasting tray and top with sugar. Roast for 10 minutes.
Remove the fruit from the tray, leaving behind the caramelized juices.

Pour in the apple juice, and heat on low-medium heat, stirring the juices to loosen the caramel. Once it has dissolved into the juice, simmer for 3 minutes, until the syrup has thickened, then pour into a bowl to cool.
To serve, divide yogurt among bowls, top with granola and roasted fruit.
Combine pumpkin puree, applesauce, apple juice, and soy milk in a blender; blend until smooth.
In a medium serving bowl combine kale, apple, pecans, bacon, and cranberries.
In a small food processor combine shallot, vinegar, juice, mustard, salt, pepper and oil for the dressing. Pulse until creamy and emulsified.
Toss salad in dressing and serve immediately.
Combine oats, apple juice, water, and ½ tsp cinnamon in a saucepan. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook 3 minutes.
Remove from heat and let stand 3 minutes before serving. To serve, top with apples and cinnamon.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging. Drain and set aside.
Meanwhile oil a large nonstick skillet with olive oil, heat over medium-high heat.
Season chicken with Greek seasoning and add to hot skillet. Cook chicken for about 4 minutes per side.
Pour apple juice and lemon juice into the hot skillet and bring to a boil while scraping the browned pieces off the bottom of the skillet with a wooden spoon.
Cook sauce, stirring occasionally, until apple juice reduces and sauce thickens, about 10 minutes. Add chicken to skillet and stir until evenly coated with sauce.
Remove chicken to a plate with rice and garnish with tomatoes and parsley.
Place the brisket on a large baking sheet and pat dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together the salt, pepper, brown sugar, pepper flakes, garlic powder and cumin.
Rub the spice mix all over the brisket and let sit for 1 hour while you prepare the smoker.
In a barrel smoker, place a 9-by-13-inch aluminum pan underneath the grill grates on the side opposite the firebox.
Place another pan on top of the grate closest to the firebox and fill it with the water.
Light a chimney starter full of lump charcoal and let burn until bright red, about 10 to 15 minutes.
Fill the firebox with hot charcoal and top with 3 pieces of hickory wood.
Keep the lid open until the wood catches fire.
Using tongs, turn the wood and close the lid.
Maintain a temperature of 250º in your smoker.
Place the brisket on the grate directly over the empty aluminum pan.
Close the grill lid and smoke until the meat is dark in color, about 6 hours.
Every hour, add more wood to maintain the fire. In a spray bottle, combine the apple juice and apple cider vinegar, and liberally spray the brisket, working quickly as to not let heat escape.
Spray the brisket each time you refresh the wood.
After 6 hours, remove the brisket from the smoker and wrap tightly in butcher’s paper.
Return to the smoker and cook until the brisket has reached an internal temperature of 200º, another 2 to 3 hours.
Continue refreshing the wood every hour.
Remove the package and transfer to a cutting board.
Let the meat rest, still wrapped in the paper, for 1 hour.
Unwrap the brisket and slice against the grain.
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Cook and stir yams, onions, cabbage, tomatoes, peanuts, and garlic in the hot oil until the vegetables are slightly browned, 7 to 10 minutes
Pour tomato juice and apple juice into the Dutch oven; season with ginger, cayenne pepper, and salt. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook at a simmer for 1 hour.
Stir bell pepper and peanut butter into the mixture. Cook, stirring regularly, until the peanut butter is melted completely into the soup, about 30 minutes more.
Heat oven to 350°F. Trim fat from pork. Place pork in 13x9 in glass baking dish. Season well with salt and pepper.
In small skillet, heat preserves, apple juice, rosemary and garlic over medium heat, stirring frequently, until thickened.
Spoon ¼ cup apricot mixture onto pork, reserve remaining apricot mixture.
Bake uncovered 75 to 90 minutes or–if using a meat thermometer–until the thermometer reads 155°F. Take the meat out of the oven.
Cover pork with foil, let stand 15 to 20 minutes or until thermometer reads 160°F.
Cut pork into slices. Heat remaining apricot mixture, pour it over the meat and serve.
Place all the ingredients in a good quality blender, starting with the liquids and blend until smooth.
To serve, pour into glasses.
Heat a thick-bottomed medium large saucepan on medium heat. Melt the butter in the pan. Add the onion and the apple and lower the heat to low. Cover and cook for 10 minutes.
Add broccoli to the apple onion mixture. Add the stock and the juice. Increase the heat, bring to a boil, reduce the heat to maintain a low simmer, covered. Cook for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat. Working in batches, filling up the blender bowl to no more than one third full at a time and puree the soup.
Return soup to the pot, mix in the protein powder, reheat and adjust seasonings to taste. Serve warm.
In a saucepan, combine the beef, chopped apple, raisins, currants, chopped walnuts, and apple juice or cider.
Add the ground cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, and allspice to the saucepan.
Stir in the lemon zest, orange zest, lemon juice, and orange juice.
Cook the mixture over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the apples are tender and the liquid has reduced, about 15-20 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the brandy.
Let the mincemeat filling cool completely before using it in the pie.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Roll out the dough and line a small pie dish with it.
Fill the with the prepared mincemeat filling.
Place the pie dish on a baking sheet.
Brush the edges of the pie crust with melted ghee.
Roll out the remaining dough and cut it into thin strips to create a lattice pattern on top of the pie, if desired.
Bake the pie in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Heat a thick-bottomed medium large saucepan on medium heat. Melt the butter in the pan. Add the onion and the apple and lower the heat to low.
Cover and cook for 10 minutes, until onions are translucent and apples are softened. Add the broccoli florets and yogurt. Add the stock and the juice.
Increase the heat, bring to a boil, reduce the heat to maintain a low simmer, covered. Cook for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat. Puree the soup in a blender until smooth. Return soup to the pot, reheat and adjust seasonings to taste. Garnish with parsley to serve.
russet potato: Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Scrub the potato thoroughly and pierce it a few times with a fork.
If desired, rub the potato with a small amount of olive oil and sprinkle with salt. This step is optional.
Place the potato directly on the middle oven rack and bake for about 45-60 minutes, or until it's tender when pierced with a fork.
In a skillet over medium heat, add the chopped broccoli. Cook for 3-4 minutes until it starts to soften.
Add the cooked chicken to the skillet with the broccoli.
Pour the barbecue sauce over the chicken and broccoli in the skillet. Stir well to coat everything evenly. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Carefully slice open the baked potato lengthwise.
Fluff the insides with a fork.
Spoon the BBQ chicken and broccoli filling into the opened potato.
Top with cheese.
In a large pot  heat oil over medium heat. Add onion and cook until beginning to soften, about 5 minutes. Stir in garlic, then add potato and spinach. Pour over broth and bring to a boil.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until spinach is bright green, and potatoes are tender, 15 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Use an immersion blender to blend soup until smooth. Stir in  1/2 cup cream and flaxseed oil.
Garnish with the remaining cream and extra spinach.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Scrub the potato thoroughly and pierce it several times with a fork.
Place the potato directly on the middle oven rack and bake for about 45-60 minutes or until it's tender when pierced with a fork.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the ground beef until it's browned and cooked through, breaking it into smaller pieces with a spoon as it cooks.
Season the cooked beef with salt and pepper to taste.
Carefully slice open the baked potato lengthwise.
Fluff the insides with a fork.
Spoon the cooked ground beef into the opened potato.
Sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese on top of the beef-filled potato.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Wash the russet potato thoroughly and pat it dry. Use a fork to prick several holes around the potato.
Place the potato directly on the oven rack and bake for 45-60 minutes, or until the potato is tender when pierced with a fork.

While the potato is baking, prepare the corn salsa. In a bowl, combine the black beans, corn kernels, diced tomato, diced red onion, chopped cilantro, lime juice, chili powder, salt, and pepper. Mix well to combine.
Once the potato is baked and tender, carefully slice it open lengthwise.
Fill the baked potato with the prepared corn salsa and bean mixture.
Serve the stuffed potato with the flavorful corn salsa and beans.
Add Jasmine rice, water and salt to a stainless steel bowl and keep aside.
Separate the coconut cream and watery portion of the coconut milk and keep aside.
Turn the Instant Pot to Saute mode and heat oil.
Add the massaman curry paste and saute for 10 to 15 seconds.
Add chicken and mix well with the curry paste.
Add potatoes, onions, cashews, fish sauce, brown sugar and salt.
Add ½ cup of the separated water from the coconut milk and stir everything well.
Carefully place the tall trivet inside the Instant Pot. Place the prepared rice bowl on the trivet.
Close the Instant Pot lid with pressure value to sealing. Cook on manual/pressure cook (Hi) for 5 mins followed by 10 minute natural pressure release.
Open the Instant Pot, take out the rice bowl and trivet using heat resistant mitts. Stir in the reserved coconut cream.
Set the Instant Pot to Saute mode and bring the curry to a gentle boil. Enjoy hot over steamed jasmine rice.
Preheat an air fryer to 400°F.
Brush potato with oil, sprinkle with salt, and place in the air fryer basket.
Cook potato until very tender when pierced with a fork, about 1 hour. 30 minutes in add asparagus.
Cook broccoli in a pot of water for 10 to 12 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a large, heavy pot over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, 10 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Add the carrot, potato, lentil, and broth. Cover the pot and cook until the vegetables are very soft, 20 minutes.
Puree the soup in a blender or food processor until very smooth. Season with salt and pepper and serve.
In a large stockpot set over medium-high heat, melt the butter and olive oil together. Add the chopped bacon and fry for 5 minutes.
Stir in the onion and garlic, and saute for 3 minutes. Add flour and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Whisk in the chicken stock. Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low.
Stir in the salmon, potatoes and corn, and simmer for about 20 minutes.
Stir in the cream and Old Bay seasoning, and let simmer for 3 minutes to warm through. Adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper to taste, then spoon into bowls and serve.
Add the onion, garlic, and olive oil to a large soup pot and saute over medium heat for about 5 minutes, or until the onion is soft and translucent.
Once the onions are soft, add the carrots to the soup pot, along with the canned diced tomatoes, barley, basil, salt, pepper and vegetable broth.
Stir the contents of the pot to combine, place a lid on top, and bring the broth up to a boil. Once boiling, turn the heat down to medium-low. Simmer for 30 minutes.
Add potatoes, peanuts, green beans and corn. Stir to combine, and heat through. Finally, add the lemon juice to the soup and stir to combine. Serve immediately.
red potatoes: Boil the potatoes for 10-15 minutes.
In a large salad bowl, arrange the mixed salad greens as the base of your salad.
Place the drained tuna in the center of the greens.
Arrange the hard-boiled egg halves, cherry tomatoes, Kalamata olives, capers, and sliced red potatoes around the tuna.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dressing ingredients: olive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and black pepper to taste.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Preheat oven to 450°F.
Place the pork in a shallow roasting pan coated with cooking spray; arrange the potatoes, carrots and onion around pork.
Drizzle with oil.
Combine the seasonings; sprinkle over meat and vegetables.
Bake, uncovered, 25-35 minutes or until a thermometer reads 145°F and vegetables are tender, stirring vegetables occasionally.
Remove pork from oven; tent with foil.
Let stand 5 minutes before slicing.
Place the potatoes in a pot with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil, and cook for about 10 minutes, or until easily pierced with a fork.
Drain, and transfer to a large bowl to cool.
In a medium bowl, mix the sour cream, mayonnaise, mustard, vinegar, eggs, pickle, celery, green onions, and hot sauce.
Season with dill, garlic powder, onion salt, salt, and pepper. Pour over the potatoes, and gently toss to coat. Chill at least 3 hours in the refrigerator before serving.
Combine water, cashew milk, brown rice, red potatoes, cabbage, carrots and onion.
Add olive oil, garlic powder, salt, pepper, and onion powder in a very large pot over medium heat and bring to a boil.
Boil for 5 minutes, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 3 hours. Stir occasionally while simmering and add any extra seasonings as desired.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Saute leeks until translucent, 3 to 5 minutes.
Add potatoes, parsnips, turnip, celery, and carrots. Cook and stir until slightly tender and coated in oil, about 4 minutes.
Add garbanzo beans, vegetable broth and parsley. Bring stew to a boil.
Reduce heat to low. cook until vegetables are tender and stew thickens slightly, about 1 hour more. Add a little water if necessary. Season with salt and pepper.
Preheat your oven to 425°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the washed and quartered red potatoes with olive oil.
Season with salt, pepper, and dried rosemary. Toss the potatoes to ensure they are evenly coated with oil and seasonings.
Spread the seasoned red potato quarters out in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the red potatoes are tender and have crispy edges. You can test for doneness by piercing them with a fork; they should be fork-tender.
Serve your Roasted Red Potatoes as a delicious and satisfying side dish.
Combine onion, garlic, and oil in a large pot over medium heat. Cook, stirring frequently, until onions are soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add potatoes, vegetable broth, thyme, sage, nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper.
Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to medium-low and cover. Simmer until potatoes are fork-tender, about 8 minutes.
Stir in pumpkin puree and coconut milk until well combined. Simmer until warmed through, 3 to 5 minutes. Adjust seasonings and serve.
Place a rack near the center of the oven and preheat the oven to 350°F.
Place a piece of parchment paper or a silicone mat in a baking sheet and set aside.
Butterfly the chicken breast and using a meat mallet or rolling pin, flatten each chicken breast to about a ½ inch.
Spread a quarter of the mixture onto each chicken breast; top each with swiss cheese.
Carefully roll up the chicken and secure with toothpicks. Place the beaten eggs in a shallow dish.
In another shallow dish, combine Panko crumbs, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Dip each chicken breast in the eggs and then into the panko mixture. Make sure the breasts are well coated. Discard any extra mixture.
Place chicken on the prepared baking sheet. Add potatoes.
Bake 40-45 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink in center and the outside is golden brown.
Serve warm.
Preheat oven to 390°F.
In a saucepan place potatoes with enough water to cover and add 1 tsp salt. Heat to boiling on high. Simmer until tender, and drain.
In a skillet heat 1 tbsp olive oil. Add onion, pepper, and garlic, and cook 10 minutes or until vegetables are tender and golden, stirring occasionally.
In large bowl, with fork, mix eggs, and chorizo until well blended. Gently stir in potatoes and onion mixture.
In same skillet, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon oil on medium. Pour in egg mixture and place in oven. Bake for 20 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 320°F.
Place roast, fat side up, on a greased rack in a roasting pan. Pour water into the pan. Spoon the potatoes around the roast and season with ground cloves, black pepper, salt and rosemary.
Bake uncovered for 150-180 minutes, basting ocasionally, or until a food thermometer reads 320°F and potatoes are tender.
Baste and stir potatoes occasionally, if they are browning too fast, tend them with foil.
Let stand 10 minutes before slicing.
potato flour: Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Use thinly sliced pork belly, stir-fry pork or pork mince.
Lightly season the pork with salt and pepper, and coat with potato starch flour.
To make the sauce, place the grated ginger, soy sauce, mirin and sake (or water) in a small bowl and mix.
Heat small mount of oil in a frying pan over medium high heat, cook pork and onion.
When the pork is cooked and onion is soft, add the sauce and stir to combine well.
Half fill a bowl with hot cooked rice and cover it with the pork mixture.
Sprinkle some finely chopped spring onion on top.
macaroni pasta: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta in boiling water until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, brown beef and drain off excess fat. Add taco seasoning and water according to seasoning package directions and simmer for 10 minutes. Set aside.
Mix together macaroni and cheese by adding ghee and milk until desired consistency. Combine beef mixture with macaroni and cheese. Mix together and serve.
Cook the macaroni pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the ground beef until browned.
Stir in the cooked macaroni pasta and chili until well combined and heated through.
Sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese over the mixture and stir until melted.
Transfer the chili mac and cheese to a serving bowl.
Top with diced tomatoes and onions.
Serve hot.
Cook the macaroni pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a small saucepan, melt the ghee over medium heat.
Add the cooked macaroni pasta to the saucepan and stir to coat with the melted butter.
Reduce the heat to low and add the shredded cheddar cheese, shredded mozzarella cheese, and milk to the saucepan.
Cook, stirring constantly, until the cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth and creamy.
Season with salt, pepper, paprika, and cayenne pepper, to taste.
Cook pasta according to package directions. Drain. In a Dutch oven, brown ground beef over medium heat. Add chopped onion, and cook until onion is soft
Add tomatoes and tomato sauce; cook until pepper is soft. Serve sauce over pasta.
Cook the macaroni pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, about 10-15 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Sprinkle the flour over the onions and garlic, and stir to coat evenly. Cook for 1-2 minutes to remove the raw flour taste.
Gradually pour in the beef broth while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Cook until the sauce thickens, about 3-5 minutes.
Add the cooked macaroni to the skillet with the onion and sauce. Stir to combine.
Sprinkle the grated Gruyère cheese over the macaroni mixture and stir until the cheese is melted and the macaroni is well coated.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the French Onion Mac and Cheese to a serving bowl or plate.
Cook the macaroni pasta according to the package instructions. Once cooked, drain and let it cool completely.
In a bowl, combine the cooked and cooled macaroni pasta, diced cucumber, diced red bell pepper, diced cherry tomatoes, and diced red onion.
In a separate bowl, mash the ripe avocado. Add lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper to the mashed avocado and mix until creamy.
Pour the creamy avocado dressing over the macaroni and vegetables.
Toss everything together until the macaroni and veggies are evenly coated with the dressing.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Place the garlic and almonds in the bowl of a food processor fitted with the blade attachment and pulse until the almonds are a fine meal and the garlic is minced.
Add the beets, protein powder, olive oil, and vinegar and pulse until you have a smooth pesto-like sauce. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
When the pasta is ready, toss with the pesto. Serve warm, garnished with basil and walnuts.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the pasta and cook until 2 minutes short of al dente. Drain and set aside.

In a large deep skillet, cook the bacon over low heat for 10 minutes. With a slotted spoon, remove the cooked bacon from the skillet and transfer to a paper-towel-lined plate. Set aside. Drain all but 2 tablespoons fat from the skillet. Increase the heat to medium high.

Add the brussels sprouts to the pot with the reserved bacon fat and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring often, for 3 minutes. Add the garlic and lemon zest and cook 1 minute. Sprinkle the flour over the top and stir for 1 more minute.

Splash in the broth about ¼ cup at a time, stirring constantly to blend between additions and prevent lumps from forming. Let simmer for 3 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in the lemon juice, then the cooked pasta. Stir to coat evenly. Stir in the yogurt, half of the cheddar cheese, half of the mozzarella cheese, and 1 tbsp Parmesan cheese.
Scrape the pasta mixture into the prepared baking dish and sprinkle with the remaining cheddar and mozzarella, then the cooked bacon, then the remaining Parmesan. Bake for 30 minutes. Serve hot.
curry paste: Add the coconut cream to a saucepan and heat up to near boiling, stirring frequently. Mix in the curry paste and stir, combining the paste with the milk.
Add the sliced chicken and chicken stock and cook for 15 minutes. Toss in the vegetables and cook for 5 more minutes.
Serve immediately.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes or until onion softens.
Add curry paste and cook, stirring, for 1 minute or until fragrant.
Add the cauliflower, stock and water and bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes.
Transfer half the cauliflower mixture to a clean saucepan. Blend the remaining cauliflower mixture, until smooth.
Add to saucepan with unblended cauliflower mixture. Add coconut cream and stir over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat, garnish with parsley and season with salt .
Heat the oil in a deep saucepan, then soften the onion for 4-5 mins. Stir in the curry paste and cook for 1 min more until fragrant.
Add the grated sweet potato and stock, then bring quickly to the boil, simmering for 5 mins until the potato is tender.
Remove the soup from the heat, stir in the coconut and seasoning, then cool briefly before whizzing with a stick blender until smooth. Sprinkle with coriander.
Heat coconut oil in a medium skillet. Add onion and saute for a minute.
Add the garlic and ginger and let cook 30 seconds, then add the red curry paste and red pepper flakes. Stir to combine and let cook 1 additional minute.
Add the carrots and bell pepper and stir to coat with the spices and curry.
Stir in the coconut milk and chickpeas, then bring to a simmer over medium-high heat. Cook for 15 minutes.
Stir in the soy sauce and rice vinegar. Serve and garnish with chopped parsley.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over high heat. Cook pork in hot oil until completely browned, about 5 minutes.
In a small bowl mix together the coconut milk, basil and curry paste.
Pour stir-fry sauce into the saucepan, bring to a simmer, and cook until the pork is cooked through, about 15 minutes. Serve imemdiately.
Heat the olive oil in a pot on medium-high heat then add the onion ginger and garlic and let cook for 2 minutes.
Add the carrot and celery and cook for 5 minutes until all the veggies are tender.
Add in the lentils, rice, red curry paste, soy sauce lime juice, and vegetable broth and mix together.
Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and let simmer for 20 minutes until the lentils are tender.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat the oil in a large wok or frying pan on a medium heat. Pour in the egg and scramble. Set aside.
Heat the curry paste with 1 tbsp water until hot, then tip in the rice, stir to break up and toss to coat. Add the prawns and heat through.
Add back the scrambled eggs and line juice and stir through.
In a large pot over medium heat, pour in the oil and the chopped onion. Stir with a wooden spoon for a couple of minutes until the onions have browned.
Then add the garlic, ginger, and red curry paste to the pot. Continue stirring until they are well-incorporated.
Stir in the coconut milk, making sure to scrape the bottom of the pot and mix everything together.
Now add in the chopped zucchini pieces. Put the lid on the pot and allow it to cook for 5 minutes. Remove the lid and stir.
Pour the chickpeas and sugar into the pot and stir again. The curry should be gently bubbling in a simmer. Add the spinach. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add the soy sauce, chopped cilantro, and chives. Thoroughly stir once more and then serve.
Bring a pot of water to the boil. Add the rice and cook it to the package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool.
In a large skillet, saute meat and onion in oil until lightly browned, about 3 minutes. Add carrots and potatoes. Add 3 cups of water and bring to the boil.
Reduce heat, cover and simmer until meat and potatoes are tender, about 10 minutes. Add curry and saute to coat.
Simmer for another 5 minutes or so, stirring constantly if curry sauce becomes too thick, just add water. To serve, ladle the curry over rice.
Heat the oil in a large pan and cook the onions and garlic for 2 minutes.
Add the pork and sausage and fry for 3 minutes until the pork turns creamy white.
Stir in the coconut milk, water, and curry paste and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the soy sauce, fish sauce, cook for a minute and serve.
In a large soup pot, heat the oil over medium heat until shimmering. Stir in the onion, chili, bell pepper, sweet potato, and salt. Saute for 5 minutes.
Add the curry paste and stir until the vegetables are coated and the curry is fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the vegetable broth and water, and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a boil, then simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the chickpeas and kale. Stir to combine, and cook for a minute.
Heat the oil in a medium non-stick, ovenproof frying pan. Tip in the sliced onion and cook over medium heat for about 5 minutes until soft and golden.
Add the curry paste and fry for 1 min more, then tip in half the tomatoes. Cook until the mixture is thick and the tomatoes have all burst.
Add half the parsley to the eggs and season, then pour over the spicy onion mixture.
Stir gently once or twice, then cook over a low heat for 10 minutes until almost set.
Add lemon juice and garnish with parsley.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on tha packaging.
Meanwhile in a large wok or skillet heat oil over medium heat; saute onions until translucent. Stir in creamed corn and curry paste.
Cook, stirring regularly, for 5 minutes. As you stir, add salt, pepper and garlic.
Stir in chickpeas. Add spinach and cover. When spinach is tender, remove from heat and stir in basil. Garnish with parsley.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Spray a muffin pan with oil.
Place eggs, coconut milk, curry paste, flour and fish sauce in a large bowl. Whisk until well combined.
Add the chopped prawn and chopped basil. Stir to combine.
Divide mixture evenly among prepared muffin pans. Bake for 20 minutes or until golden and set. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a skillet or wok, heat a small amount of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced chicken and cook until it's no longer pink in the center.
Stir in the Panang curry paste and cook for a minute or two until it becomes fragrant.
Pour in the canned coconut milk and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the sliced bell peppers, zucchini, carrots, green beans, and bamboo shoots to the skillet.
Season with fish sauce.
Simmer for about 5-7 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and the sauce thickens slightly.
Serve your delicious Panang Curry over cooked rice.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat skillet or deep bottom pan. Add coconut oil and wait until oil melts.
Sauté finely chopped onions in hot coconut oil until onion is translucent. Add roughly chopped lemon grass.
Add minced garlic, red and green bell peppers. Cook for 2 minutes constantly stirring. Push veggies to one side of the pan.
Add washed and cleaned chicken things. Cook until chicken browns a bit and changes colour.
Mix chicken and veggies together. Continue cooking it for a minute more. Again gather chicken veggie mix to one side of the pan.
Scoop out curry paste to empty side of pan. Stir the paste and fry it in oil. Wait until oil separates from the paste.
Stir in unsweetened peanut butter, fish sauce. Cook stirring regularly for 2 minutes. Pour in thick coconut milk.
Let it cook for 5-7 minutes or until chicken is fully cooked.
Heat the oil in a skillet. Stir the curry paste into the hot oil. Cook and stir for a few seconds before adding the garlic and shallot.
Cook and stir the garlic and shallot until fragrant. Add the sardines and toss around in the pan to brown the skin a little bit and cover it well with the paste mixture.
Gently stir the coconut cream into the mixture and continue to toss to coat the sardines. Allow mixture to come to a boil until the sauce thickens, about 5 minutes.
Serve imemdiately.
Gut and clean the fish, then cut into medium sized pieces. Bring a pot of water to boil, then boil the fish for about 5 - 10 minutes until fully cooked.
Remove the fish from the water, drain fully, and leave to cool.
Once the fish is cool, carefully take off the skin and debone all the meat from the fish, and flake the fish in your fingers so it's almost like deboned minced fish.
Discard the bones and skin. Set aside the fish for later.
In a mortar and pestle, start with a pinch of salt, then pound the dry chilies first for about 10 minutes.
Then add the lemongrass, turmeric, garlic, and peppercorns, and pound for about 30 minutes until a relatively smooth paste.
Once the paste is pretty smooth, the final step is to add the shrimp paste, and pound and mix for another 5 minutes.
Set aside.
In a large pot or sauce pan, add all the coconut milk and curry paste, then turn on medium heat.
Add the minced fish, season with salt.
Keep stirring in one direction, and once it comes to a boil, turn down the heat.
Keep stirring gently for about 5 minutes once it comes to a boil.
Add Jasmine rice, water and salt to a stainless steel bowl and keep aside.
Separate the coconut cream and watery portion of the coconut milk and keep aside.
Turn the Instant Pot to Saute mode and heat oil.
Add the massaman curry paste and saute for 10 to 15 seconds.
Add chicken and mix well with the curry paste.
Add potatoes, onions, cashews, fish sauce, brown sugar and salt.
Add ½ cup of the separated water from the coconut milk and stir everything well.
Carefully place the tall trivet inside the Instant Pot. Place the prepared rice bowl on the trivet.
Close the Instant Pot lid with pressure value to sealing. Cook on manual/pressure cook (Hi) for 5 mins followed by 10 minute natural pressure release.
Open the Instant Pot, take out the rice bowl and trivet using heat resistant mitts. Stir in the reserved coconut cream.
Set the Instant Pot to Saute mode and bring the curry to a gentle boil. Enjoy hot over steamed jasmine rice.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Place the sweet potatoes directly on the rack and bake until tender enough to easily pierce with a fork. Remove from the oven and allow to cool.
Heat olive oil in a large saucepan or soup pot over medium heat. Add the onion and ginger, cook and stir for 5 minutes. Stir in the curry paste and heat for 1 minute.
Whisk in the coconut milk and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for about 5 minutes.
Remove the skins from the sweet potatoes and cut into bite size chunks. Add to the soup and cook for 5 more minutes.
Stir in lemon juice and season with salt. Ladle into bowls and garnish with a drizzle of sesame oil and a little bit of cilantro.
white cheese: In a small saucepan, combine shredded white cheese and milk over low heat.
Stir continuously until the cheese is melted and smooth.
Stir in garlic powder, onion powder, ground cumin, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Continue cooking for 1-2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the dip is heated through and reaches desired consistency.
Transfer the white cheese dip to a serving bowl and garnish with optional toppings if desired.
Combine the first 7 ingredients (milk through green chiles), using just 1/2 tablespoon of the taco seasoning, in a large bowl, stirring just until moist.
Pour mixture into a round pie plate (mine was glass) coated with cooking spray.
While corn is baking, toss the chicken in the remaining 1 tablespoon taco seasoning.
When corn is done – it will be just barely set and golden brown – pierce entire surface liberally with a fork (it might stick a little bit to the fork).
Pour enchilada sauce over top. Top with chicken; sprinkle with cheese.
Bake at 400 degrees F for 15 minutes or until cheese melts. Remove from oven; let stand 5 minutes.
Cut into 8 pieces; top each serving with cilantro and Cotija cheese.
chili powder: In a mixing bowl, combine 1 cup of drained and rinsed black beans, 1 cup of halved cherry tomatoes, and 1/2 cup of corn kernels.
Add 1/2 diced avocado to the bowl.
Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of chopped fresh dill over the ingredients.
Squeeze the juice of half a lime into the bowl to add a zesty flavor.
Season the salad with a pinch of salt and pepper to taste. If you like it spicy, you can also add 1/4 teaspoon of chili powder.
Toss all the ingredients together until they are well combined.
De-seed the bell peppers and chop into 1” square pieces. Cut the firm tofu into 1” square pieces.⁠ Heat a barbecue or grill pan until hot.⁠
Meanwhile, mix the seasoning spices and oil in a small bowl. Thread the vegetables and tofu, alternating, onto the skewers and brush with the marinade.⁠
Cook the skewers on each side until the vegetables are cooked. Turn and repeat on all sides. Serve immediately.
Mix the chicken with the lime juice, chilli powder and garlic in a zipper bag, leave to marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan on medium-high, fry the chicken for 2-3 minutes, or until cooked through. Transfer the chicken to a plate and set aside.
Add the rest of the olive oil to the frying pan. Add the sliced onion and bell peppers, season with salt and pepper, and stir fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add the chicken back to the pan to warm it up.
Meanwhile warm the tortillas in a separate non-stick pan on medium heat. Once nice and hot, remove from the heat.
Divide the vegetable-chicken mixture and add it to the warm tortillas. Enjoy immediately.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the ground turkey until browned. Break it into crumbles as it cooks.
Add diced onions and bell peppers to the skillet. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are softened.
Stir in kidney beans, diced tomatoes, and tomato sauce.
Season the mixture with chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine.
Let the chili simmer for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chili to thicken.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Preheat a skillet over medium heat.
Rub the chicken breast with olive oil, paprika, garlic powder, chili powder, salt, and pepper.
Cook the seasoned chicken breast in the preheated skillet for about 4-5 minutes per side or until fully cooked through. Remove from heat and let it rest for a few minutes.
While the chicken is resting, prepare the vegetables by washing and slicing them.
Lay the large lettuce leaf flat on a plate.
Slice the cooked chicken breast into thin strips.
Place the chicken strips on top of the lettuce leaf.
Add the shredded carrots, bell pepper and sliced cucumber on top of the chicken.
In a small bowl, mix together the Greek yogurt and sriracha.
Drizzle the yogurt sauce over the chicken and vegetables.
Wrap the chicken and vegetables in a tortilla.
Serve immediately.
Preheat your air fryer to 380°F.
In a bowl, toss the sliced turkey breast, bell pepper, and onion with olive oil, chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.
Place the seasoned turkey and vegetables in the air fryer basket.
Air fry at 380°F for 8-10 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through, until the turkey is cooked through and the vegetables are tender and slightly charred.
Warm the tortillas in the air fryer for about 1 minute, if desired.
Once cooked, remove the turkey and vegetables from the air fryer.
Fill the tortillas with the turkey and vegetable mixture.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat; stir in onion and garlic.
Cook and stir until the onion has softened and turned translucent, about 5 minutes, add chicken and cook for 5 minutes more
Add tomatoes, chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Stir in chicken broth and water, bring to a boil.
Reduce to a simmer and cook for 5 to 10 minutes.
Add black beans, hominy, cilantro, and jalapeno pepper. Simmer until heated through, at least 10 minutes.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a non-stick skillet over medium heat, cook the diced chicken breast until it's no longer pink and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Season with chili powder, cumin, salt, and black pepper while cooking. Remove the cooked chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add a bit of cooking spray if needed, and sauté the diced bell pepper and onion until they become tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and green chilies to the skillet. Stir in the low-sodium chicken broth. Let the mixture simmer for about 5 minutes to combine flavors and slightly reduce the liquid.
Stir in the cooked chicken pieces to the skillet and mix everything together.
In an oven-safe dish, layer half of the chicken and vegetable mixture.
Top with half of the corn tortilla strips and half of the shredded reduced-fat cheddar cheese.
Repeat the layers with the remaining chicken mixture, corn tortilla strips, and cheese.
Place the casserole in the preheated oven and bake for about 15-20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Preheat your air fryer to 350°F.
Season both sides of the baby back ribs with salt, pepper, and your favorite dry rub seasoning.
Place the seasoned ribs in the air fryer basket, bone side down.
Air fry the ribs at 350°F for 25-30 minutes.
After the initial cooking time, remove the ribs from the air fryer and brush them with barbecue sauce if desired.
Return the ribs to the air fryer and continue to cook for an additional 5-10 minutes, or until the ribs are cooked through and tender.
Once cooked, remove the ribs from the air fryer and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Combine the milk, flour and chili powder in a deep bowl, mix well and keep aside.
Heat the oil in a broad non-stick pan, add the cumin seeds and peas and saute on a medium flame for 30 seconds.
Add the curry and grated onion and saute on a medium flame for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and saute on a medium flame for 1 minute.
Add the milk mixture and salt, mix well and cook on a slow flame for 6 minutes, while stirring occasionally.
Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the chicken breast with salt, pepper, chili powder, cumin, and paprika.
Place the seasoned chicken breast on a baking sheet and roast it in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes. Then, shred it into bite-sized pieces.

While the chicken is roasting, you can prepare the rest of the soup. In a saucepan over medium heat, add the diced onion, diced bell pepper, and minced garlic. Sauté for a few minutes until the vegetables start to soften.
Stir in the canned black beans, canned corn kernels, diced tomatoes, and shredded chicken.
Add the low-sodium chicken broth and enchilada sauce to the saucepan. Stir well to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and let it cook for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Taste the soup and adjust the seasoning with more salt and pepper if needed.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Saute the onion, red bell peppers, jalapeno, mushrooms, tomatoes and corn for 10 minutes.
Season with black pepper, cumin, and chili powder. Stir in the black beans, chicken or vegetable broth, and salt, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium low. Remove 1½ cups of the soup to food processor or blender. Puree and stir the bean mixture back into the soup.
Enjoy hot.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a small bowl, mix together chili powder, ground cumin, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Place the salmon fillet on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Sprinkle the taco seasoning mixture evenly over the salmon fillet, pressing gently to adhere.
Lightly coat the salmon with cooking spray.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Serve the taco-seasoned salmon hot with your favorite side dishes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Bake the tortillas by placing them over two bars on the oven rack to create "hard shell" taco shells.
Mix minced onion, salt, chili powder, cumin, red pepper flakes, cayenne pepper, dried minced garlic, and oregano in a bowl.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Crumble ground beef into the hot skillet. Cook for 7 to 10 minutes. Drain and discard any excess grease.
Return ground beef to heat. Pour seasoning mixture and water over the beef and stir to combine. Bring to a simmer and cook until the moisture absorbs into the meat, about 5 minutes.
Place the beef onto the tortillas and add lettuce, tomato and cheese.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Combine chili powder and cocoa powder in a bowl, sprinkle over both sides of pork loin
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and brown pork loin on both sides, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer pork and juices to a baking dish.
Roast in the preheated oven until pork is slightly pink in the center, about 12 minutes.
Combine mango, shallot, cilantro, and lemon juice in a bowl. Slice pork loin and top with juices from the baking dish and mango mixture. Top with cheese slices.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced bell pepper and onion to the skillet and cook until they start to soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Season the shrimp with chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Push the vegetables to one side of the skillet and add the seasoned shrimp to the other side.
Cook the shrimp for 2-3 minutes on each side until they turn pink and opaque.
Once the shrimp are cooked through, mix them with the vegetables in the skillet.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook ground turkey until browned.
Add cooked quinoa, black beans, corn kernels, diced tomatoes, diced bell pepper, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture into a small baking dish.
Sprinkle shredded cheese on top.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly.
Preheat oven on 375°F.
Add the spinach to a non-stick pan over medium heat and stir until wilted. Add softened cream cheese and stir until well incorporated.
Let it cool slightly and combine with Parmesan cheese, mozzarella, minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
Use a sharp knife and slice through the chicken breast, creating a pocket. Season the chicken with chili powder, onion powder salt, pepper, garlic powder.
Fill the chicken with the spinach filling and fold it, and use toothpicks to seal the cream cheese.
Place the chicken breast into a baking dish, drizzeled with oil and bake for 25 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, cook and stir until onion is translucent.
Stir in the chili powder and cumin, and cook for about 30 seconds.
Add the rice, chicken broth and tomato paste, and bring to a boil. Transfer to a 1 quart casserole dish, and cover with aluminum foil or a lid.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, or until liquid has been absorbed and rice is tender.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place diced butternut squash on a baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat evenly.
Roast the butternut squash in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until tender.
While the squash is roasting, heat the black beans in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add a pinch of salt and a dash of chili powder for extra flavor.
Warm the corn tortillas either on a dry skillet or in the microwave for about 20 seconds.
Assemble the tacos: Place the warmed tortillas on a plate. Spread the warmed black beans over the tortillas.
Top with the roasted butternut squash, diced onion, diced tomatoes, shredded lettuce, and chopped cilantro. Add cotija sheese and greek yogurt.
pastry dough: Roll out the pastry dough and cut it into a square.
Place the peeled and cored apple in the center of the dough square.
Fill the apple core with applesauce, sprinkle ground cinnamon, and add chopped nuts.
Fold the pastry dough over the apple, sealing it to form a dumpling.
Place the dumpling in a baking dish.
If desired, drizzle honey over the top for sweetness.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes or until the pastry is golden brown.
Allow the apple dumpling to cool slightly before serving.
Finely chop the chicken, onions into small cubes.
In a bowl, combine the chicken, onions, pepper, and cumin and mix well.
Roll out the dough, and cut it into quarter pieces.
Place 3-4 tsp of filling put in the middle of each quarter piece and drape the remaining dough pieces over top.
Pinch the edges together and place them on a baking tray.
Whisk the egg yolk and brush the top of each Samsa with egg yolk, then sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Bake in a preheated oven for 30 minutes or until golden brown and serve hot.
smoked paprika: In a small bowl, combine the drained and rinsed chickpeas with 1/2 teaspoon of smoked paprika and 1/2 teaspoon of cumin. Toss to coat the chickpeas in the spices.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the seasoned chickpeas to the skillet.
Sauté the chickpeas for about 4-5 minutes until they become slightly crispy and browned. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Remove from heat and let them cool.
In a salad bowl, place 2 cups of chopped lettuce.
Add the thinly sliced radishes, halved cherry tomatoes, and sliced cucumber to the bowl with the lettuce.
Drizzle the salad with the juice of half a lemon and 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the sautéed chickpeas have cooled slightly, add them to the salad.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Rub the spice mixture evenly over the chicken thighs, ensuring they are well coated.
Place the seasoned chicken thighs on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and juices run clear.
Once cooked, remove the chicken thighs from the oven and let them rest for a few minutes.
In a pot, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, minced garlic, and diced bell pepper to the pot, sautéing until softened (about 2-3 minutes).
Add chicken to the pot, cooking until they are browned on all sides (about 3-4 minutes).
Stir in rinsed rice, tomato sauce, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and dried oregano, ensuring the rice and chicken are well coated.
Pour in broth, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the chicken is tender.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In an oven-safe skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken drumsticks and chorizo slices. Cook until they start to brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the diced potato, chopped onion, and chopped bell peppers to the skillet. Sauté for another 3-4 minutes.
Sprinkle the smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper over the mixture. Stir to coat everything evenly.
Place the skillet in the preheated oven and roast for about 30-35 minutes or until the chicken drumsticks are cooked through, and the potatoes are tender.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
In a small saucepan, bring the vegetable broth to a boil.
Gradually pour the instant polenta into the boiling liquid, stirring constantly to prevent clumps from forming.
Reduce the heat to low and continue to stir the polenta until it thickens, which should take about 3-5 minutes. Remove it from the heat.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add minced garlic to the skillet and sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and bell peppers to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Slice the prepared polenta into squares or desired shapes.
Add the polenta to the skillet with the vegetables, arranging it in a single layer.
Cook the polenta for 3-4 minutes on each side until it develops a light golden crust.
Season the polenta and vegetables with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika if using.
Preheat a grill to medium-high heat 375°F.
Rub tilapia fillets with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder and grill for 5 minutes on each side.
Serve with tomatoes, bell peppers, seasoned with salt, pepper, olive oil and vinegar.
Preheat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
In a bowl, mix together harissa paste, olive oil, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to make a marinade.
Pat dry the chicken with paper towels and then coat them with the harissa marinade.
Once the grill is hot, place the chicken on the grill and cook for about 6-7 minutes per side, or until fully cooked and charred on the edges.
Remove the chicken from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the grilled harissa chicken thighs with lemon wedges on the side for squeezing over the top, and garnish with fresh cilantro if desired.
In a medium skillet bring salted water to a boil, add lentils and cook until tender, 10 minutes. Drain.
Add the olive oil to a large soup pot over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic and saute until fragrant and softened, about 5 minutes.
Add tomato sauce, beans, lentils, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cover with a lid and allow to simmer for 15 minutes, until the beans are soft.
Puree with a hand blender or transfer to a stand alone blender and puree until smooth.
Combine barley and water in a small saucepot. Bring to a boil, lower to a simmer, and cook covered until the water has been fully absorbed. Turn off heat and set aside.
Cook sausage in a large pot over medium-high heat until browned. Remove sausage to a dish and set aside.

Lower heat to medium and melt butter in the pot.
Once melted, add the flour and whisk until incorporated with the butter.
Add the broth, and pumpkin and whisk until thickened, about 3 minutes. Add the cooked barley to the pot and stir.
Season with smoked paprika, salt and pepper. Stir to combine.
Turn off the heat, stir in the Parmesan cheese and serve, garnished with some more grated Parmesan.
Combine chili powder, thyme, smoked paprika, oregano, cayenne pepper, cumin, onion powder, and salt in a bowl.
Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium heat, cook and stir onion in hot ghee until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook until soft, about 2 minutes, stir shredded cabbage and sliced carrot into onion and garlic.
Cook and stir cabbage mixture until cabbage starts to soften, about 5 more minutes.
Stir spices into cabbage mixture and cook, stirring often, until spices are evenly mixed in and cabbage is tender, 3 more minutes.
In a pot, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, minced garlic, bell pepper, and celery to the pot, sautéing until softened (about 3-4 minutes).
Add sliced smoked sausage to the pot, cooking until it's browned (about 3 minutes).
Stir in shrimp, cauliflower rice, diced tomatoes, Cajun seasoning, dried thyme, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, ensuring everything is well combined.
Pour in broth, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes or until the shrimp is cooked through, and the cauliflower rice is tender.
Adjust the seasoning if necessary.
In a small bowl, mix together the seasoning ingredients. Place the sliced vegetables in a large bowl. Sprinkle with half the seasoning mix.
Place the chicken slices in another large bowl. Sprinkle with the remaining seasoning mix.
Heat 1 tbsp of the oil in a large, deep skillet over medium-high heat, about 2 minutes. Add the tomatoes, bell peppers and the onions. Cook, stirring often, about 5 minutes.
Remove the vegetables to a plate. Add the remaining oil to the skillet, then add the chicken slices. Stir-fry until the chicken is fully cooked, about 5 minutes.
Return the vegetables to the skillet. Stir-fry everything together for 2 more minutes, until heated through, and serve the chicken fajitas in bowls.
Place bacon on preheaed pan and cook until crispy, for 5 minutes.
Heat the oil in a large pot over a medium-low heat. Add the sliced garlic and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes. Then stir paprika.
Add the bread and cook, stirring, until lightly toasted, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the stock and 2 cups of water and bring nearly to the boil. Then reduce the heat to simmer.
Crack an egg into a small dish, make a small indentation in the soup and pour in the egg. Repeat with remaining eggs. Cover and cook 3 minutes.
Garnish with crispy bacon and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together the pure maple syrup, soy sauce, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper to create the marinade.
Place the chicken thigh in a shallow dish and pour the marinade over it, ensuring the chicken is evenly coated. Let it marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
Remove the chicken thigh from the marinade and shake off any excess.
Place the chicken thigh in the air fryer basket.
Air fry at 375°F for 18-20 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the chicken is cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
Once cooked, remove the chicken thigh from the air fryer and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the air-fried maple chicken thigh hot with your favorite side dishes.
Turn on a multi-functional pressure cooker and select Saute function. Melt butter in the pot.
Meanwhile, season chicken with garlic powder, salt, pepper, and paprika.
Quickly brown each chicken breast in the pot, rotating until browned on all sides, 5 to 7 minutes.
Add onions and cook 5 minutes. Add broth and garlic.
Change pressure cooker setting to Manual and cook chicken until no longer pink in the center and juices run clear, about 15 minutes.
In a heavy bottom soup pot, add the oil and heat on medium until it shimmers.
Add the onion and saute on medium low for 5 minutes or until just softened.
To the soup pot, stir in the corn, jalapenos, garlic, paprika, salt and potatoes. Pour in the broth and water. Stir and cook 15 minutes.
Transfer about 1 1/2 cups of the soup mixture to a blender and pour in the milk. Blend until smooth.
Transfer the blended soup back into the soup pot and cook on low for about 5 minutes just to warm through.
Ladle into soup bowls, add a few twists of fresh ground black pepper and garnish as desired.
In a medium skillet bring water to a boil, add eggs and cook until hard-boiled, about 8 minutes. Peel and chop.
In a pan fry bacon, until crispy, about 5 minutes.
In a bowl add yogurt, mustard, chives minced garlic, ground pepper, salt, smoked paprika, bell pepper, lemon juice. Whisk well until combined.
Add this mixture and bacon to a bowl, stir together and serve.
Place the chicken pieces in a bowl and pour buttermilk over them. Allow it to marinate for at least 30 minutes (or up to overnight in the refrigerator).
In a separate bowl, mix flour, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cayenne pepper (if using), salt, and black pepper.
Preheat the air fryer to 375°F (190°C).
Dredge the marinated chicken pieces in the flour mixture, ensuring they are well coated.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray or brush with a small amount of melted ghee.
Place the coated chicken pieces in the air fryer basket, ensuring they are not touching each other.
Air fry for 15-20 minutes, turning halfway through, until the chicken is golden brown and cooked through.
Heat olive oil in a skillet or paella pan over medium-high heat.
Add diced onions and bell peppers to the skillet. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the diced chicken thighs to the skillet. Cook for about 3-4 minutes until they are browned on all sides.
Stir in the Arborio rice and sauté for 1-2 minutes until the rice becomes slightly translucent around the edges.
Add the diced tomatoes, smoked paprika, saffron threads, salt, and black pepper to the skillet. Mix well.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cover the skillet. Let it simmer for about 10 minutes, or until the rice is mostly cooked and has absorbed most of the liquid.
Arrange the peeled and deveined shrimp and frozen peas over the top of the paella.
Cover the skillet again and continue to simmer for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the shrimp turn pink and the rice is fully cooked and tender.
Remove the skillet from heat and let it rest for a few minutes.
Combine sweet potatoes and beef broth in a pot; cook over medium-high heat until tender, 5 to 10 minutes.
Mash sweet potatoes into the broth using a potato masher.
Mix coconut milk, shallot, maple syrup, curry powder, sea salt, chili powder, and paprika into sweet potato mixture.
Cook and stir until heated through, 5 to 10 minutes. Reduce heat to low and blend soup with a hand blender until smooth.
flaxseed oil: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add the spaghetti, and cook until al dente. Toss with 1/4 cup olive oil, cover and keep warm.
Place another 1/8 cup olive oil in a skillet, and heat over medium heat. Stir in the garlic and flaxseed oil, and cook just until golden, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add the sardines, and cook 1 minute more. Stir in the bread crumbs, stir in the remaining olive oil.
Stir in the parsley and pepper, and remove from the heat.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, flaxseed oil,  lemon juice, and cumin. Season dressing with salt and pepper.
In a large serving bowl, combine avocados, tomatoes, basil and feta cheese. Gently toss with dressing and serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente; drain.
In a 1 1/2 quart glass casserole dish, layer noodles, 1/2 of cheese, 1/2 of the tuna, 1/2 of peas and 1/2 of the soup.
Repeat the layering with the remaining ingredients. Top the casserole with bread crumbs and drizzle with flaxseed oil.
Bake for 10 to 15 minutes.
Cook rice in a pot with water according to package instructions.
Heat 2 teaspoons olive oil in a wok or large frypan over medium- high heat. Add the egg and cook, stirring, for 1-2 minutes until softly scrambled. Remove egg from the pan and set aside.
Add remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil to the pan, then cook chilli and garlic, stirring, for 1 minute or until fragrant.
Add prawns and cook for 2-3 minutes until opaque. Return egg to the pan with rice, soy, sesame oil, sweet chilli sauce and flaxseed oil then cook stiring for 2 to 3 minutes.
Boil the pasta ina pot of water for 10 to 12 minutes or until al dente.
Heat olive oil in a deep skillet over medium heat and cook chile and garlic until lightly browned, about 1 minute.
