Add tuna, tomato paste, salt, and pepper and cook for 5 minutes.Add tomatoes and simmer until flavors are well combined, 5 to 10 minutes.
Serve with pasta, garnish with parsley and drizzle with flaxseed oil.
Pour almond or coconut milk into a blender and add chia seeds to allow the chia seeds to soak for 5 minutes.
Add all remaining ingredients and blend until chunks are liquified and the smoothie is the consistency you desire. Pour into large glasses and enjoy.
Cook pasta in a pot of water according to package instructions until al dente. In the last minute of cooking add broccoli.
Meanwhile, in a large pan over medium heat add olive oil and saute with garlic for 2 minutes.
Add chicken stock, 1 teaspoon salt, flaxseed oil, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper, and bring to a simmer.
Toss in prawn and saute until pink, about 3 minutes. Transfer prawn to a bowl and set aside.
Stir in cream, simmer on low for 5 minutes.
Toss in pasta until well combined, add back prawns and broccoli. Stir until warmed through.
In a shallow bowl, season flour with salt and pepper. In another shallow bowl, combine bread crumbs and coconut. Place eggs and flaxseed oil in a third shallow bowl.
Working with one at a time, dip prawns in flour, then eggs, then coconut mixture.
Place prawns in the basket of an air fryer and heat to 400°F . Bake until prawns are golden and cooked through, 10 to 12 minutes. Work in batches as necessary.
Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Add pasta and cook according to package directions. Drain.
In a large skillet over medium heat, heat sesame oil. Add green onions and cook until fragrant, 1 minute. Add shrimp, then season with salt and pepper. Cook for 3 minutes, then remove from skillet.
To same skillet, heat olive oil. Add sliced jalapeno and chili and cook until soft, 5 minutes, then add garlic and cook until fragrant, 1 minute more.
Pour in soy sauce, chicken broth, sweet chili sauce and rice vinegar adn whisk until combine. Simmer for 1 to 2 minutes then toss in pasta and shrimp, garnish with cilantro before serving.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packagung. Let it sit for 10 minutes.
Cut off the bottom quarter of each nori sheet, reserve for another use.
Combine chopped tuna, flaxseed oil, mayonnaise, green onions, and hot sauce in a bowl.
Center 1 sheet of nori on a bamboo sushi mat. Wet your hands. Spread a thin layer of rice on the nori using your hands.
Press into a thin layer, leaving a 1/2-inch space at the bottom edge.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds. Arrange 1/4 of the tuna mixture in a line across the rice, about 1/3 of the way down from the top of the sheet.
Wet the uncovered edge of the nori. Lift the top end of the mat and firmly roll it over the ingredients.
Roll it forward to make a complete roll. Repeat with remaining ingredients.
Slice the rolls into 3/4-inch pieces using a wet knife. Serve immediately or refrigerate until serving.
Whip cream cheese with flaxseed oil in a bowl with an electric hand mixer.
Add onion, capers, chives, lemon zest and juice, seafood seasoning, parsley, Worcestershire, and hot sauce.
Mix well. Add tuna and mix until well blended.
Combine all ingredients in a high-powdered blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Toast bread slices to desired doneness, 3 to 5 minutes.
Mash avocado in a bowl, stir in cilantro, lemon juice,  lemon zest, cayenne pepper, flaxseed oil and  salt.
Spread avocado mixture onto toast.
In a large bowl, mix together beans, tuna, beans, onion, olive oil, flaxseed oil, lemon juice and garlic. Season to taste with salt and black pepper. Top with cilantro.
In a food processor, combine salmon, shallot, capers, garlic, 3 teaspoons lemon zest, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, flaxseed oil and 3 tablespoons dill and pulse several times to combine.
Add egg white and panko crumbs and pulse until combined. Transfer to a bowl and divide salmon mixture into 8 patties.
Season salmon patties with salt and pepper. Heat and oil a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, then cook salmon patties until golden and cooked through, 2 to 3 minutes per side.
Serve salmon cakes with spinach leaves.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging,
In a medium pot of boiling water, simmer tofu for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and let drain in a colander lined with paper towels. When cool enough to handle, gently squeeze and pat dry.
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat 2 tablespoons oil. Add ginger and garlic and fry until fragnant.
Add tofu and let cook until golden on all sides. Season with salt and pepper, and remove from skillet and set aside.
Add mushrooms and edamame in the skilled and fry for 3 minutes or until tender, add the tofu and flaxseed oil and cook for another 2 minutes.
Serve on a plate with rice and garnish with red chilli pepper.
Stir flour, soy milk, egg, flax seeds, baking powder and vanilla extract together in a bowl.
Heat a lightly oiled griddle over medium-high heat. Drop batter by large spoonfuls onto the griddle and cook until bubbles form and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes.
Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 2 to 3 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.
Serve sprinkled with chopped walnuts and lemon juice.
In a large skillet over medium heat, heat olive oil. Add shrimp and season with salt and pepper. Cook until pink, 5 minutes, then remove from skillet.
Return skillet to heat and heat flaxseed oil. Add broccoli, carrots, and cauliflower and cook until soft, 7 minutes. Add garlic and ginger and cook until fragrant, 1 minute more.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, lime juice and a pinch of red pepper flakes. Add to skillet and toss to coat. Add shrimp and cook until heated through, 2 minutes.
Place all the ingredients and some water in a blender. Blend until smooth and serve immediately.
In a large pot of salted boiling water, cook pasta according to package directions until al dente. Drain, reserving ½ cup pasta water, and return to pot.
In a large skillet over medium heat, heat oil. Add onion and broccoli and cook until soft, 5 minutes, then add garlic and cook until fragrant, 1 minute more.
Add wine and cook until almost completely reduced, 5 minutes. Add heavy cream, lemon juice and flaxseed oil and cook until thickened, another 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Add salmon and cook until salmon is warmed through, 2 minutes, then toss sauce and pasta together. Add ¼ cup pasta water if sauce is too thick.
spring onions: Toss the tomatoes, cucumbers, and lemon juice in a bowl with 2 tsp of the salt. Set aside.
Put the couscous into a large bowl. Bring the water, 2 tbsp of olive oil, flax seeds, the remaining salt, cayenne, and herb stems to a boil over high heat.
Pour the liquid over the couscous, stir to separate any clumps. Cover with a lid, set aside for 5 minutes. Remove the herb stems and fluff with a fork.
Add the tomato mixture, scallions, mint, parsley, and pine nuts. Drizzle the remaining oil over the salad, toss and serve.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a small ovenproof skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the thinly sliced potatoes and cook for about 5-7 minutes until they start to soften and turn slightly golden. Season with salt and pepper.
Stir in the chopped spring onions and cook for another 2 minutes until they become tender.
In a bowl, whisk the eggs and then pour them over the potatoes and spring onions in the skillet.
Sprinkle the shredded cheese and chopped dill evenly over the top.
Let the mixture cook on the stovetop for about 2 minutes to set the bottom.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for approximately 10-12 minutes or until the frittata is set and the top is lightly golden.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Cook the noodles according to the packet instructions, then drain and tip into a bowl. Pour over the olive oil, tossing thoroughly to coat the noodles. Put to one side.
Heat the groundnut oil in a wok over a high heat. Once hot, add the garlic and stir-fry for 30 seconds before adding the prawns. Continue to stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Turn the heat down to medium and add both of the soy sauces and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Stir-fry for a further 2 minutes before spooning onto a plate and dressing with the spring onions.
Boil the noodles in salted water for 3 mins until soft. Drain and rinse in cold water. To make the sauce, mix soy sauce, fish sauce and lime
Heat a wok with half the oil. Add the prawns and spring onions, and cook, stirring quickly, for 1 min or until the prawns turn pink.
Push to the side and add the remaining oil. Add the egg and let sit for 30 secs, then scramble until cooked. Add bean sprouts.
Add the noodles and sauce, and cook, stirring continuously, for 3 mins or until everything is hot.
Boil some water in a large saucepan. Add 1 cup cold water and the udon noodles. Cook for 2 minutes or until al dente. Drain and leave in the colander.
Heat oil, add the onion and bean sprouts and saute for 5 mins until softened. Add spring onion and saute for 1 more minute.
Pour in the remaining oil and the noodles. Add soy sauce and mix.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile put the ginger, soy, mirin, garlic, chilli flakes  and 1 tsp of the sesame oil in a food processor and blend until fine.
Pour the marinade into a bowl, add the meat, mix well and leave to sit while you prepare the onion.
Heat the remaining sesame oil in a large wok or frying pan until very hot. Add the onion and stir-fry for a few mins.
Add the beef and the marinade, stirring constantly until it’s cooked through, about 5 mins. Serve with rice and garnish with onions.
Marinate the pieces with salt and ginger paste and keep aside for 5 minutes.
Mix cornflour, flour, ginger paste, salt in a bowl and make a batter using a little water.
Dip the paneer pieces in this batter and fry in hot oil until they turn golden.
Garnish with spring onions and serve.
Heat the oil in a non stick pan. Add finely chopped onions, spinach, and scallions, and saute for 3-4 minutes on a high heat.
Meanwhile, crack the eggs in a bowl. Add milk, black pepper and salt to taste, the crumbled feta cheese, and whisk until you get a frothy mixture.
Combine the sauteed greens mixture into the eggs, whisking it in quickly. Pour this mixture in the same pan and allow to cook on medium heat.
Once it looks nearly set, carefully turn it over to cook the other side. Flip the omelette on to a plate, serve hot.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Start by cooking the bulgur as instructed on the package.
When the bulgur is cooked, rinse the portabella mushrooms and place them on a baking sheet lined with baking paper.
Mix all the diced vegetables in a mixing bowl and add the cooked bulgur.
Season the bulgur and veggies with salt, pepper, nutritional yeast, tamari, cayenne pepper, smoked paprika, thyme, and garlic. Stir together.
Brush the portabella mushrooms with olive oil and top each mushroom generously with the bulgur topping.
Sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese on top and bake in the oven for 20 minutes or until the bulgur is golden and the mushrooms are ready.
Sift flour and baking powder into a bowl. Add sliced spring onions, chopped parsley, corn and ham. Season with salt and pepper and stir to combine.
Make a well in the centre, add eggs and milk, then stir to form a batter.
Heat a frying pan, add a film of oil and drop heaped tablespoonfuls of the batter into the pan. Cook for 3 minutes on each side.
Combine stock, ginger, garlic, half the spring onion and 2 cups water in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Bring to the boil. Add chicken. Cover. Reduce heat to low. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat. Set aside, covered, for 10 mins. Remove chicken and finely shred.
Combine cornflour and 1 tabelspoon cold water. Strain the broth. Discard solids. Return to pan. Bring to the boil.

Add corn, soy sauce and cornflour mixture. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes or until soup thickens slightly. Remove from heat. Gradually stir in egg in a thin, steady stream. Stir in the chicken. Season to taste.
Cut Chicken Fillets into 1-1½ inch size pieces.
Combine ginger, garlic, soy sauce, rice wine in a bowl, add chicken pieces, massage, and set aside.
Place 1 egg in ½ cup and add cold water to make it ½ cupful.
Combine flour in a separate bowl, and egg & cold water, and mix lightly to make tempura batter.
Heat Oil from 325°F to 350°F.
Coat the chicken pieces with flour, dip into tempura batter and fry until batter is light golden and crispy.
Take them out and lay them on a rack to drain the oil.
Place all the sauce ingredients in a saucepan and bring to a boil, then simmer for a few minutes.
Half fill a bowl with freshly cooked rice, place chicken, lettuce leaves and spring onion on top, drizzle the sauce over and enjoy.
Carefully cut the peel and all the pith off the oranges and slice them into round discs.
If they have seeds, remove these. Peel and cut the avocados into slices and or wedges.
Shave the fennel on a mandolin and slice the radishes very finely. Squeeze out all the juice from the leftover orange peels and collect this in a bowl.
Add the juice of half a lemon, the olive oil and salt and pepper and mix to emulsify *(I actually had enough orange juice so didn’t add the lemon juice, so you can decide)
On a large platter scatter the fennel across the bottom, Arrange the avo and orange slices and then scatter the spring onions and radish slices over the top.
Drizzle the dressing over just before you serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Use thinly sliced pork belly, stir-fry pork or pork mince.
Lightly season the pork with salt and pepper, and coat with potato starch flour.
To make the sauce, place the grated ginger, soy sauce, mirin and sake (or water) in a small bowl and mix.
Heat small mount of oil in a frying pan over medium high heat, cook pork and onion.
When the pork is cooked and onion is soft, add the sauce and stir to combine well.
Half fill a bowl with hot cooked rice and cover it with the pork mixture.
Sprinkle some finely chopped spring onion on top.
Serve with rice.
Heat the ghee in a pan, add the spring onions and zucchini and stir well. Cover and cook for 3 mins, add the peas and stock and return to the boil.
Cover and simmer for a further 4 mins, then remove from the heat and stir in the watercress and mint until they are wilted.
Puree in a food processor, adding yogurt with the second batch. Return to the pan, then add seasoning to taste. Serve hot or cold
Boil a kettle of water to pour over bulgur wheat. Place bulgur wheat into a large bowl, pour boiling water over it and cover immediately.
Let it sit for 20 minutes without taking the lid off.
Mix tomato paste, olive oil together. Once the bulgur wheat is ready, mix oil tomato paste mixture into it.
Now mix in diced vegetables, chopped herbs. Season with salt, pepper
Place pork chops on a paper towel to absorb any excess moisture. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Whisk egg and soy sauce together in a small bowl. Place flour on a small plate and bread crumbs in another.

Dredge a pork chop in the flour, pressing it in using your fingertips to cover all crevices along the surface. Turn to evenly coat all sides. Dip the pork into the egg mixture, coating completely. Transfer immediately to the bowl of bread crumbs, pressing in to evenly coat. Repeat process with the second pork chop.

Melt the butter in a small frying pan over high heat. Once it begins to foam, stir it and watch it carefully, it will brown in 1-2 minutes. Stir the butter a few times to ensure it browns evenly. Remove the pan from the heat. Add the olive oil to the pan then and set it aside for the butter to cool slightly.

In a medium-sized bowl, whisk the maple syrup, balsamic vinegar, apple cider vinegar, and mustard. Slowly drizzle in the brown butter and oil, whisking the entire time. Once all the oil and butter have been added, whisk the dressing quickly for several seconds so it becomes creamy.
Store the dressing at room temperature until you are ready to use it.

Put the cabbage, carrots and spring onions in a large bowl and stir to combine. Mix the mayonnaise with the mustard in another small bowl and drizzle over. Fold everything together to coat in the creamy sauce, then season with salt and pepper.

Heat oil in a wide pan or wok on medium-high heat to 350°F. Lower a pork chop into the oil. Fry until bottom side is golden, 2 to 3 minutes. Turn; cook until other side turns golden and pork is slightly pink in the center.
Pick up the cutlets and hold them on their side for a few seconds to let the oil drip off. Drain on a paper towel.
Place the cutlets on the plate and serve them with brown dressing and coleslaw.
In a skillet, heat the ghee (or vegetable oil) over medium heat.
Add the chopped spring onions and thawed corn kernels to the skillet. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they are tender.
Sprinkle the chili powder over the corn and spring onions, and season with salt and black pepper. Stir to coat evenly.
Place one corn tortilla in the skillet.
Spread half of the shredded cheese evenly over the tortilla.
Spoon the sautéed corn and spring onion mixture over the cheese.
Sprinkle the remaining cheese on top.
Place the second corn tortilla on top to create a quesadilla.
Cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side until the tortillas are crispy, and the cheese is melted.
Remove the quesadilla from the skillet and let it cool for a minute before cutting it into wedges.
Add the rice, a pinch of salt and the measured water to a pan with a lid and bring to a boil over a high heat. Cover and cook for 10 minutes.
In a bowl, combine the soy sauce, honey, ginger paste and garlic.
Heat a pan over a medium high heat with a drizzle of oil. Add the chicken pieces with a pinch of salt and fry for 5 minutes.
Add the teriyaki sauce and cook for 3 min further until the sauce is thickened and coating the chicken.
Cook the broccoli in lightly salted boiling water for 5 min or until tender. Drain once cooked.
Serve the chicken teriyaki over the rice with the broccoli to the side. Garnish with sesame seeds and the spring onion.
Preheat air fryer to 400°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on thepackaging.
Meanwhile combine tapioca starch, garlic powder, salt and pepper in a shallow dish. Dredge chicken wings through the mixture.
Line a baking sheet with a cooling rack and place chicken on rack to let excess dredge fall off. Lightly grease chicken with oil and place in the air fryer basket.
Cook in the preheated air fryer for 10 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through.
Combine tomato sauce, gochujang sauce, honey, and soy sauce in a small bowl and drizzle over the chicken pieces. Sprinkle with sesame seeds and onion.
fish sauce: Bring broth to a boil. Reduce heat to medium and simmer for about 5 minutes. Add mushrooms and shrimp and cook until shrimp have heated through, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the fish sauce, lime juice, green onion, cilantro and chile pepper. Taste and adjust salt and pepper if needed.
Soup should be sour, salty, spicy and hot. Serve imemdiately.
In a large pot, heat the oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and bell pepper and cook until softened.
Add garlic, ginger and curry paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the broth, coconut milk, and fish sauce.
Bring to a boil.
Add the shrimp and rice noodles.
Cook until shrimp is pink and opaque.
Stir in the lime juice and serve immediately, topped with optional garnishes if using.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, marinate thinly sliced chicken breast with soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce. Set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and minced ginger to the skillet and cook until fragrant.
Add sliced onion and sliced bell peppers to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add marinated chicken to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through.
Stir in fresh Thai basil leaves and cook until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, topped with egg made to taste, and with cooked rice on the side.
Boil the noodles in salted water for 3 mins until soft. Drain and rinse in cold water. To make the sauce, mix soy sauce, fish sauce and lime
Heat a wok with half the oil. Add the prawns and spring onions, and cook, stirring quickly, for 1 min or until the prawns turn pink.
Push to the side and add the remaining oil. Add the egg and let sit for 30 secs, then scramble until cooked. Add bean sprouts.
Add the noodles and sauce, and cook, stirring continuously, for 3 mins or until everything is hot.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, and fish sauce. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant.
Add sliced chicken breast to the skillet and cook until no longer pink.
Add sliced chicken breast to the skillet and cook until no longer pink.
Pour the sauce mixture into the skillet and stir to coat the chicken and vegetables evenly.
Add sliced red chili pepper and fresh basil leaves to the skillet. Cook for another minute until basil is wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the spicy basil chicken hot.
Heat the oil in a large pan and cook the onions and garlic for 2 minutes.
Add the pork and sausage and fry for 3 minutes until the pork turns creamy white.
Stir in the coconut milk, water, and curry paste and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the soy sauce, fish sauce, cook for a minute and serve.
Heat the chicken broth in a large soup pot. Add the lemongrass and simmer about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat and add the garlic, crushed red pepper flakes, lime juice, and mushrooms. Cook 5 minutes more.
Add the shrimp and red bell pepper. Cook another 5 minutes or until shrimp are cooked through.
Turn heat to low. Add the coconut milk and fish sauce. Taste and adjust any seasonings or ingredients.
Ladle into soup bowls and serve.
Toast the peanuts in a dry pan for just 2 minutes, until fragrant.
In a stone bowl, combine the fish sauce, chili, garlic, lime juice, and tamarind and use the pestle to pound them enough to mash them into a paste.
Add papaya and beans and mix well. Then add the tomatoes, peanuts, and mix.
Season the thinly sliced chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic and sliced chicken to the skillet. Sauté for about 3-4 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and no longer pink. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced red bell pepper and onion. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Return the cooked chicken to the skillet and add the sliced Thai chilies.
Stir in the fresh basil leaves, low-sodium soy sauce, fish sauce. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until the basil leaves are wilted.
In a wok or large carbon steel or stainless steel skillet, heat oil over high heat until just smoking. Move wok off-heat, add the garlic, and cook for 30 seconds.
Return wok to high heat, add the cabbage and peanuts, and cook, stirring and tossing rapidly to ensure garlic doesn’t burn and cabbage is evenly coated in oil.
Continue to cook and begin to carefully add a few drops of water, in ¼ tsp increments around the sides of the wok, for 2 minutes.
Add fish sauce around the sides of the wok, and continue to cook, tossing and stirring constantly, until fish sauce is fully absorbed into the cabbage, around 30 seconds.
Season to taste with pepper, transfer to a serving plate, and serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat wok or large skillet over medium heat.
Add oil and heat. Add garlic and onion and stir fry until fragrant and onion is just softened.
Add shrimp and toss; add Roasted Chili Paste and toss with shrimp.
Stir fry until shrimp is cooked through. Add fish sauce and toss.
Serve with rice and lettuce leaves.
Sprinkle arrowroot over the beef. Place inside a large sealable baggie and shake to evenly distribute over the beef.
Heat a large skillet to medium high heat. Once hot, add the oil and toss in the beef. Cook for 3 minutes or until just browned
Now cook the onion, bell pepper, and garlic for  5 minutes or until onion is softened and browned.
Bring the beef back to the skillet, toss in the coconut aminos, fish sauce, lime juice, and basil. Cook for another 3 minutes. Serve garnished with basil leaves.
In a bowl, mix all the ingredients of the filling and blend well.
The texture of the filling should be sticky. Stuff the filling into the empty crab shells using a spoon.
Smooth out the surface with the back of the spoon. Steam the stuffed crab for 5 minutes.
Discard the water that seeps out. Cool the stuffed crab to room temperature after steaming.
Heat up a wok with some oil. When the oil is heated, dip each crab shell into the beaten eggs.
Make sure the filling is well coated with the beaten eggs. Transfer the stuffed crabs into the wok and fry them.
As soon as the surface turns light to golden brown, dish out and serve immediately.
In a bowl, mix together low-sodium soy sauce, fish sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt and pepper to create the marinade.
Add the bite-sized beef cubes to the marinade and let them sit for about 10-15 minutes.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat until it's smoking hot.
Add the marinated beef cubes to the skillet, ensuring they are in a single layer to promote searing.

Sear the beef cubes for about 1-2 minutes without moving them, allowing them to develop a nice crust. Then, stir and cook for another 1-2 minutes until they are browned on all sides but still slightly pink in the center.
Add the minced garlic and minced ginger to the skillet and stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve your beef on top of lettue and tomato, and top with onion.
In a blender, combine all the ingredients except the chicken.
Pulse the blender until the shallot, cilantro, and garlic break down into a sauce like consistency.
Add the chicken strips into a gallon-sized zip-top bag and pour the prepared marinade over the chicken.
Zip the bag and squish to coat the chicken evenly.
Place the zip-top bag on a plate and refrigerate for at least 3 hours and ideally overnight.
PEANUT DIPPING SAUCE: Add all the ingredients for the peanut dipping sauce in a medium bowl and use a whisk to combine.
If your peanut sauce is thick, you can add in 1 tablespoon of hot water at a time to help thin it to desired consistency.
I only needed 1 tablespoon for this. Top with crushed peanuts if desired.
When you're ready to grill, preheat the grill on medium high for 5-10 minutes and oil the grates.
Meanwhile, remove the chicken pieces from the marinade and place the chicken (like an accordion) on skewers.
Pierce the chicken with the skewer and zig-zag the chicken through the skewers making sure to secure both ends on the stick.
Place the chicken on the hot grill, cover and cook for 4-7 minutes, flipping half way through.
The chicken is cooked through when it is slightly firm to the touch or registers 165ºF on a thermometer.
Remove, the chicken and serve immediately with the peanut dipping sauce.
Heat the fat on a high heat in a large skillet and tip in the pork.
Cook for 5 minutes until the liquid has all but evaporated and the pork is beginning to brown.
Add the ginger, garlic and shallots and cook for 3 minutes, stirring very frequently.
Now stir in the lime zest and juice, fish sauce, and cook a further minute.
Remove from the heat, throw in the herbs, chili flakes and give it all a good mix and serve.
In a wok or skillet, heat ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add thinly sliced chicken to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 3-4 minutes).
Add red curry paste to the wok, stirring to coat the chicken.
Toss in sliced red bell pepper, zucchini, and bamboo shoots (if using), stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
Pour in coconut milk and fish sauce, stirring to combine. Simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
In a large pot, heat the oil over moderately low heat.
Add the shallots, ginger, and jalapeños; cook, stirring occasionally, for 3 minutes.
Add the broth and water; bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the zests and mushrooms; simmer 5 minutes longer.
Add the lime juice, fish sauce, and swordfish to the soup.
Cook until the fish is just done, about 2 minutes.
Serve sprinkled with the tomatoes and cilantro, if using.
Enjoy with rice noodles.
In a pot, bring chicken broth to a boil over medium heat.
Add lemongrass, galangal or ginger, kaffir lime leaves, and chili pepper (if using). Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add chicken slices and mushrooms. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, and tomato wedges. Cook for another 2-3 minutes.
Season with salt to taste.
Remove lemongrass stalks and kaffir lime leaves.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh chopped cilantro.
Combine the rice, salt, and water in a medium pot. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce the heat, and simmer for 15 minutes. Remove from the heat and set aside.
Heat the oil over medium-low heat in a large nonstick pan. Add garlic, and jalapeno and cook, stirring frequently, until softened, 3 minutes.
Add the coconut milk, red curry paste, fish sauce and whisk together. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer until thickened, a few minutes.
Add the chicken and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally to promote even cooking, until the chicken is cooked through, 5  minutes.
Stir in the lime juice, season, and serve with quinoa.
celery stick: Chop the onion, carrots, and celery. Peel the potatoes and chop them into chunks. Heat the oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Toss the onion, carrots, and celery around in the oil. Sprinkle with salt and add pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally  for about 10 minutes.

Stir in the potatoes, garlic, bay leaves, and thyme. Cook for 5 minutes. Pour in the stock and bring to a boil. Reduce to a simmer and cook until you can easily pierce the potatoes with a fork, about 15 minutes.
Remove the soup from the heat. Take out the bay leaves and add heavy cream. Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth.
Cut the chorizo into small dice. Heat the olive oil in a pan and add chorizo. Cook, stirring occasionally for about 5 minutes.
Transfer the soup into the bowls and serve with chorizo on top. Garnish with chopped parsley.
Place all of the ingredients in your blender. Blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Add the butter to the saucepan with all of the rough-cut vegetables and allow to cook until they begin to soften.
Add the flour to the vegetables and mix well, to make a roux, allow this to cook for at least 5 minutes to get rid of the raw flour taste.
Add the seafood stock and blend. Add heavy cream and season. Allow chowder to simmer 20 minutes.
Add the chopped shrimp pieces and allow to cook in the chowder for 3 minutes. Before serving add in crab meat.
Wash the celery stalks and cut each in half.
Spread peanut butter in the hollow of the celery.
Top with raisins.
Heat pan over medium-high heat. Add oil and chicken and cook about 4 minutes. Reduce heat to medium.
Add tomatoes with juice, chili sauce, green pepper, celery, onion, garlic, basil, parsley, cayenne pepper, and salt.
Bring to a boil, and reduce heat to low and simmer, covered for 10 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Add your chopped cauliflower, sprinkle with salt and pepper and drizzle with the olive oil. Bake the cauliflower for 15 minutes.
Remove from the oven and let it cool to room temperature. In a small bowl, combine the dressing ingredients and whisk until smooth.
Once the cauliflower has cooled, add it to a bowl with the chopped onion, celery, pickles, and boiled eggs. Mix through the dressing.
Sprinkle with the chopped dill and serve immediately.
Heat olive oil in a pan on high heat and add onion, celery, brussels sprouts and potato. Add vegetable stock, cover and bring to boil for 15 minutes.
Add 1½ cup peas and kale. Cover and boil for 5 minutes. Remove from heat and puree in blender until smooth. Add salt to taste and garnish with parsley and the remaining peas.
Serve immediately.
Heat olive oil in a large saucepan, fry the onions until soft then add the garlic and ginger and fry for another minute.
Add celery, carrots, and potatoes and stir fry for about 3 minutes. Stir in the curry powder, paprika, and cumin, and cook,  for 1 minute. Add the bay leaves.
Pour in the tinned tomatoes, lentils and water. Bring to the boil, then turn the heat down to medium and allow the soup to simmer for 20 minutes
Remove the bay leaves and season with salt and ground black pepper to taste. Garnish with jalapeno and serve.
Preheat oven to 450°F.
Place beef bones, carrots, leek, onion, and garlic on a roasting pan or rimmed baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes.
Toss the contents of the pan and continue to roast until deeply browned, about 20 minutes more.
Fill a large (at least 6-quart) stockpot with 12 cups of water (preferably filtered).
Add celery, bay leaves, peppercorns, and vinegar.
Scrape the roasted bones and vegetables into the pot along with any juices.
Add more water if necessary to cover bones and vegetables.
Cover the pot and bring to a gentle boil.

Reduce heat to a very low simmer and cook with lid slightly ajar, skimming foam and excess fat occasionally, for at least 8 but up to 24 hours on the stovetop. (Do not leave on stovetop unattended, simply cool and continue simmering the next day.)
The longer you simmer it, the better your broth will be.
Add more water if necessary to ensure bones and vegetables are fully submerged.
Alternately, you can cook the broth in a slow cooker on low for the same amount of time.
Remove the pot from the heat and let cool slightly.
Strain broth using a fine-mesh sieve and discard bones and vegetables.
Let continue to cool until barely warm, then refrigerate in smaller containers overnight.
Remove solidified fat from the top of the chilled broth.
Broth can be stored for up to 5 days in the refrigerator and up to 6 months in the freezer.
Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil, add potatoes and cook for 12 to 15 minutes until tender.
Heat grill to medium-high. In large bowl, whisk together oil, vinegar, horseradish, celery seeds, and ¼ teaspoon salt. Toss with tomatoes, celery, and onion.
Season pork chops with ½  teaspoon each salt and pepper and grill until golden brown and just cooked through, 5 to 7 minutes per side.
Fold parsley into tomatoes and serve over pork, potatoes and greens.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl combine butter, garlic, and dried herbs and set aside. Season steaks generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Place a cast iron skillet ob medium heat and drizzle with oil. Sear steaks for 2-3 minutes on each side until nice and browned. Transfer to a plate and set aside.

Add potatoes, tomatoes, zucchini, red onion, and celery to the skillet. Season generously with salt and pepper, and saute 3-5 minutes until browned. Push vegetables to one side of the pan and return steaks to the other side of the pan.
Transfer to oven and cook for 15-20 minutes until potatoes are tender and steak is cooked to your preferred doneness.
Immediately after removing from the oven, place a dollop of the butter on each steak and serve.
Heat the olive oil in a large heavy bottomed pot over medium heat. Add the onions, carrots and celery and cook until soft and translucent, 5 minutes.
Add garlic, salt and pepper, cook until fragrant, 2 minutes.
Stir in the zucchini, beans, rice and bay leaves until well incorporated, about 1 more minute. Add the vegetable broth and water and bring mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to a low simmer and cook covered until rice is fluffy and vegetables are soft, 30 minutes.
Remove from heat, and stir in lemon juice. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.

Heat the oil in a saucepan over a moderate heat, add the onion and celery, and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes. Add tomatoes and spinach and stir well to combine. Cook for about 10 minutes.
Stir in the lentils and add half of the stock. Bring to the boil, then reduce the heat and cover. Simmer for 20 minutes and gradually add stock.
Spread the oats and cheese over the top. Place the dish in the oven and bake for about 10 minutes. Serve hot.
In a large pot, partially cover with lid and cook lightly salted water, beef and barley. Skim off any impurities that rise to the top to keep your soup clear.
Sautee pickles with oil and pepper for a few minutes on high heat. Add potatoes and carrots to the pot and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Add celery and saute for 5 more minutes. Stir in the tomato paste and mix well. Add to the soup and mix.
Toss in bay leaves, dill and more salt. Continue to simmer for another 2 minutes. Serve immediately.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a soup pot over medium high heat. Saute onion, celery, carrot, garlic and salt for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add tomato paste and stir for one minute. Add crushed tomatoes, beans, broth, rosemary, chili flakes and bay leaves. Stir well.
Bring to low boil then simmer on low to medium heat for 30 minutes, until carrots are soft.
Remove one cup of beans to a bowl and mash with a spoon. Stir back into pot. Add remaining oil and whisk into the soup. Serve immediately.
Heat a drizzle of oil in a large frypan and fry eggplant until golden brown. Drain on a paper.
Heat 1 tbsp of oil in the same pan and saute the onion for 2 minutes.
Add the celery and cook for a further minute. Stir through passata, capers, and olives. Simmer for 10 minutes on low, stirring occasionally.
Add the vinegar and simmer for 5 minutes.
While simmering the vegetables, lightly toast the pine nuts. Place pine nuts in a non-stick frypan and dry roast over a low heat for 2 minutes,.
Stir the toasted pine nuts and salt through the eggplant. Serve
Preheat air fryer at 400°F.
In a large pot, bring 4 quarts of salted water to a boil. Add carrots, celery and cabbage and blanche for 2 minutes until slightly softened. Transfer to an ice bath.
Once cool enough to handle, drain and squeeze the veggies using a kitchen towel until all of the water is removed. Set aside.
In a small bowl combine hoisin, soy sauce and sesame oil.
In a large skillet over medium-high, heat oil. Add ginger, hemp seeds and garlic and cook, stirring, 30 seconds. Add the drained veggies, season and saute for 2 minutes.
Place an egg roll wrapper on a clean surface in a diamond shape. Spoon 2 tbsp of filling onto the bottom third of the wrapper.
Fold up bottom half and tightly fold in sides. Gently roll, then seal fold with a couple drops of water, pressing to seal.
Brush each egg roll with olive oil then add to the fryer basket. Air fry for 10 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the egg rolls are golden brown and crispy.
Serve immediately.
Heat the butter over medium heat in a pot. Sautee the carrots, celery, onions, and garlic for 3 minutes.
Add in broth, chicken, parsley, black pepper and salt. Cover and let simmer for 30 minutes.
Pull the chicken out, shred with two forks or a pair of meat claws, and place back in the pan along for 5 minutes.
Garnish with parsley and serve.
Preheat the Oven to 350°F.
Mix all of the ingredients in a bowl and let rest for at least 2 hours in the refrigerator.
In a bowl set over an ice bath, whisk together all of the ingredients, excluding the olive oil.
While whisking continuously, slowly add the oil (be carefully not to over mix or the aioli may become bitter)
Pour into a squeeze bottle and keep refrigerated until service.
In a medium pot, heat about a quart of canola oil to 350°F.
Separate the four, whisked eggs and breadcrumbs into three bowls (in that order)
Season the panko breadcrumbs with the cayenne, salt and pepper.
Dredge the shrimp with flour and dust off excess.
Dip the shrimp in the egg and then the panko.
Firmly press the panko onto both sides of the shrimp.
Gently drop about 4-5 shrimp at a time into the hot oil.
Cook until golden brown; about 1-2 minutes.
Remove the shrimp from the oil with a nest or slotted spoon and transfer to paper towels to be rid of excess oil.
Lightly warm the buns on a sheet pan for about 3 minutes.
If need be, place the fried shrimp on a sizzle platter and reheat in the oven as well.
Gently open the buns and place a dollop of aioli on the bottom and place a shrimp on top (like glue).
Place a small cluster of the pickled vegetables on top of the shrimp and then drizzle with aioli.
Each rectangular plate should have 3 buns each.
Put celery, kiwi, protein powder, ginger, avocado, orange juice, water and lemon juice in a blender and blend for 2 minutes.
To serve, pour into glasses.
fish stock: In a large pot, melt butter over medium heat. Saute celery, onion, bay leaf, dill, salt and pepper for about 5 minutes.
Stir in potatoes and saute for 3 minutes. Add water and bring to a boil. Cover, reduce heat and boil until potatoes are tender.
Whisk flour into milk and stir together into pot. Bring to a simmer, stirring often.
Stir in seafood, lemon juice and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls and drizzle each serving with olive oil.
Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Add garlic and onion, cook for 5 minutes until translucent but not golden. Add carrots and stir through. Turn heat up to high and add wine.
Stir then let it simmer rapidly until it's mostly evaporated. Add flour and stir for 30 seconds - it will become sludgy!
Slow pour in broth while stirring to dissolve paste, then stir in milk. Add potato, corn, salt and pepper.
Bring to simmer then turn heat down so it's simmering gently.
Cook, stirring every now and then, for 8 minutes or until potato is almost cooked.
Add fish and peas, simmer for 3 minutes or until fish is just cooked (should flake easily).
Adjust salt and pepper to taste. If soup is too thick, add a touch of water or milk.
Serve, garnished with green onions and crusty bread on the side for dunking!
Peel and wash potatoes, carrot, and onion. Clean the celery and wash it as well. Cut potatoes into medium cubes. Finely chop the onion, carrot and celery.
Heat butter and oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Sauté onions, carrot, and celery in it for about 4 minutes. Add fish stock and bring to a boil.
Add potatoes and processed cheese to the pot. Simmer soup over medium heat for about 20 minutes. Cut salmon into small pieces and add to the soup.
Season the cheese soup with salt and pepper and simmer gently for another 7 minutes. Serve soup in bowls and garnish with parsley.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
For the teriyaki sauce, mix together soy sauce, tsuyu, sake, sweet rice wine, and honey in a small pot.
Add the garlic, ginger and bring the sauce to boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Discard the garlic and ginger, set aside. Heat oil in a large heavy bottom skillet over med-high heat.
Add the onion and cook until they are just softened, about 2-3 minutes. Transfer to another plate; set aside.
Add the pork belly to the hot skillet and let them sear for 2-3 minute, then turn to the other side and cook for another 1 minute.
Pour the sauce over pork belly and let the sauce bubble. Reduce the sauce until it thickens, about 2-3 minutes. Remove from the heat.
To serve, put individual serving amount of rice in each bowl, put onion over the rice and top with pork belly slices.
Sprinkle chopped green onion and top with an egg yolk.
Serve immediately.
Melt the butter in a large, heavy-bottomed pot and saute the leek for 5 minutes over medium heat until it softens.
Add the carrot and potatoes to the pot, then cover them with the fish stock. Continue to cook for 10 minutes until the potatoes are almost fork-tender.
Add the salmon chunks and double cream to the pot and simmer for 5 minutes, until the salmon is cooked through.
Stir in the parsley and simmer for another minute. Season to taste and serve.
Roast peppercorns, mustard, curry leaves and fenugreek seeds. Add them to a spice blender and grind to a fine powder.
Heat oil the oil in a pan on medium heat. Add onions, curry leaves, garlic and ginger. Stir fry for 2-3 minutes, adding a couple dashes of water if necessary.
Add dry masala and red chilli paste. Cook for 4 minutes before adding the stock. Add prawns and cook until tender.
Add lime juice and coconut milk, stir well and enjoy.
Place potatoes into a large pot and cover with salted water, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until tender, about 10 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and crushed red pepper.
Cook and stir until onion is soft, 5 to 7 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Increase heat to medium-high and add tomatoes, reserved potatoes, and white wine.
Bring to a simmer. Cook, breaking the tomatoes up with a spoon, 8 to 10 minutes. Pour in fish stock and return to a simmer.
Add cod to the pot, nestling the pieces down into the broth. Simmer for 2 to 3 minutes.
Add clams, shrimp, and calamari. Simmer until shrimp are pink and clams have opened, 3 to 5 minutes. Remove and discard any unopened clams.
Turn off the heat and stir in basil, parsley, lemon juice, lemon peel. Ladle into bowls to serve.
Cut lobster in half lengthwise, and remove any meat from the claws, tail, and head. Cut meat into pieces and place back into the shells.
Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium heat. Add shallot, cook and stir until tender.
Mix in fish stock, white wine, and double cream. Bring to a boil, and cook until reduced by half. Mix in parsley, lemon juice, mustard, salt, and pepper.
Preheat your oven's broiler.
Place stuffed lobster halves on a broiling pan or baking sheet. Spoon sauce over the meat and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Broil in the preheated oven until just golden brown, 3 to 4 minutes. Serve immediately.
Remove the skin from the salmon fillet.
To do this, grip the skin at the end nearest to you, then cut between the flesh and the skin, keeping the skin taut. Cut the flesh into 1inch cubes.
Pick the parsley leaves from the stems. Chop it finely. Place a saucepan over medium heat, then pour in the oil and add the sofrito. Cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the stock, bring to a boil, and add the lentils.
Season the salmon with salt and add to the pan.
After 1 minute, turn the salmon in the pan, being careful not to break it up, then season to taste with more salt if needed.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley into the pan and carefully stir in.
Put the garlic into a tall jar or beaker, then process to a paste using a hand-held blender.
Put a saucepan over medium heat and add the oil. Fry the garlic until browned.
Meanwhile, process the onion in the blender. Add to the pan with the garlic.
Lower the heat, add the herbs, then fry, stirring frequently, until the onion has browned. Add 4/5 of the tomatoes and cook for 30 minutes.
Add the remaining tomato, cook for 30 more minutes, then season with salt and pepper.
Put a large pot over medium heat, then add the olive oil. Add the crabs. Cook for 3-5 minutes.
Add the fish. Pour in the water and bring to a simmer. Skim the foam from the surface. Simmer for 20 minutes, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve.
Cool before putting into containers.
Wrap the saffron threads in aluminum foil and toast lightly in a hot frying pan for a few seconds, being careful not to let them burn. Cool.
Put the peeled garlic and the parsley leaves into a small bowl. Add the toasted saffron threads. Next, add the olive oil.
The process with a hand-held blender to make a coarse paste. Serve and enjoy with bread.
Melt butter over medium heat and add fish stock. Add the vegetables and let it boil for 15 minutes. Remove the vegetables from the soup.
In a small bowl mix lemon juice with curry, cayenne pepper, egg yolk and cream, and add to the soup.
Add fish and vegetables and let the soup simmer until the fish is cooked through, about 5 minutes. Season with salt and serve immediately.
In a small bowl using a fork, break up crab meat and mix with parsley. In a large saucepan over medium heat, saute the shallots in the olive oil for about 3 minutes.
Add the wine and allow the mixture to simmer for several minutes. Turn up the heat and stir the tomato paste into the stock. Mix well.
Also, add the bay leaves and thyme. Allow the mixture to simmer for about 20 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Place the crab meat in a large bowl. Remove the bay leaves from the sauce and pour over the crab. Serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Combine the fish, lime juice, oil, salt and pepper in a bowl.
Cover with cling wrap and refrigerate for 20 minutes.
Heat the 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over high heat.
Add the fish and cook until just cooked through and light golden brown.
Remove from the skillet and set aside.
Reduce the stove to medium-high and heat 1½ tbsp olive oil in the same skillet.
Add the garlic and onion and cook for 1½ minutes or until the onion is starting to become translucent.
Add the bell peppers and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the remaining broth ingredients.
Bring to simmer, then turn down to medium.
Cook for 15 to 20 minutes or until it thickens.
Adjust salt and pepper to taste.
Return the fish to the broth to reheat - about 2 minutes.
Stir through lime juice.
Garnish with cilantro/coriander and serve with rice or lime rice.
corn tortillas: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook lentils according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Stir together all the ingredients except the tortillas and cheese in a medium bowl. Warm a tortilla in a a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Once the tortilla is warm, place some cheese and some  lentil and veggie mixture onto the tortilla. Sprinkle with some cheese and place another tortilla on top.
Cook for about 2 minutes, pressing down with the back of your spatula to help seal the quesadilla as the cheese melts. Flip, and cook another 2 minutes.
Remove from heat and slice into triangles. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a small baking dish, spread a thin layer of enchilada sauce on the bottom.
Cut one of the corn tortillas into smaller pieces and place them in the baking dish to cover the bottom.
Layer half of the black beans, corn, and diced bell peppers on top of the tortilla pieces.
Sprinkle half of the shredded cheese over the beans and veggies.
Pour a little more enchilada sauce over the cheese layer.
Repeat the layers using the remaining tortilla, black beans, corn, bell peppers, and cheese.
Top it off with the remaining enchilada sauce.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for about 20-25 minutes, until the casserole is heated through and the cheese is melted.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 5 minutes to slightly brown the cheese on top.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a couple of minutes.
Garnish with chopped cilantro.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a non-stick skillet over medium heat, cook the diced chicken breast until it's no longer pink and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Season with chili powder, cumin, salt, and black pepper while cooking. Remove the cooked chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add a bit of cooking spray if needed, and sauté the diced bell pepper and onion until they become tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and green chilies to the skillet. Stir in the low-sodium chicken broth. Let the mixture simmer for about 5 minutes to combine flavors and slightly reduce the liquid.
Stir in the cooked chicken pieces to the skillet and mix everything together.
In an oven-safe dish, layer half of the chicken and vegetable mixture.
Top with half of the corn tortilla strips and half of the shredded reduced-fat cheddar cheese.
Repeat the layers with the remaining chicken mixture, corn tortilla strips, and cheese.
Place the casserole in the preheated oven and bake for about 15-20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Season chicken breast with salt and pepper, place it on a baking sheet, and roast for 20-25 minutes until cooked through.
Remove chicken from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing it thinly.
Warm corn tortillas in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 1 minute on each side.
Place sliced chicken on warmed tortillas.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomato, avocado slices, chopped onion, and cilantro.
Squeeze lime juice over the tacos.
Slice top and bottom ends off oranges; cut off peel and white pith, following the shape of the fruit.
Cut orange segments from the membrane to remove and roughly chop.
Place in a medium bowl. Discard peel, pith, and membrane.
Stir in onion, cilantro, chile, salt, and 1 tbsp lime juice.
Sprinkle both sides of snapper evenly with Cajun seasoning.
Heat oil in a large nonstick or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the snapper; cook until golden brown and cooked through, 3 to 4 minutes per side.
Transfer to a plate and flake into large pieces using 2 forks. Sprinkle with the remaining 1 tbsp lime juice.
Divide the snapper and cabbage evenly among warm tortillas. Top with the orange salsa.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place diced butternut squash on a baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle with chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat evenly.
Roast the butternut squash in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until tender.
While the squash is roasting, heat the black beans in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add a pinch of salt and a dash of chili powder for extra flavor.
Warm the corn tortillas either on a dry skillet or in the microwave for about 20 seconds.
Assemble the tacos: Place the warmed tortillas on a plate. Spread the warmed black beans over the tortillas.
Top with the roasted butternut squash, diced onion, diced tomatoes, shredded lettuce, and chopped cilantro. Add cotija sheese and greek yogurt.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and add the oil.
Add the diced bell pepper and diced onion to the skillet. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Crack the two large eggs directly into the skillet with the sautéed vegetables. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Scramble the eggs with the vegetables, cooking for about 2-3 minutes until they are fully cooked and slightly golden.
Warm the corn tortillas in a dry skillet or on a griddle until they are soft and pliable.
Divide the scrambled egg mixture evenly between the two tortillas.
Roll up the tortillas to form enchiladas and place them seam side down in an oven-safe dish.
Pour the red or green enchilada sauce over the top of the enchiladas.
Sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese over the enchiladas.
Place the dish in the preheated oven for about 10-15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the dish from the oven.
Pat salmon dry, if necessary, then evenly season all sides of the filets with salt, pepper, and Cajun spice.
In a large skillet over medium heat, heat oil. When oil is hot but not smoking, add salmon. Cook until about 5 to 6 minutes, then flip and cook for another 2 to 3 minutes.
Let rest and cool slightly, then flake into large pieces.
Make mango avocado salsa. In a medium bowl, add mango, avocado, tomatoes, flaxseed oil and lime juice. Season with salt and toss to combine.
Top tortillas with salmon, avocado salsa and chives.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a non-stick skillet over medium heat, sauté the diced bell peppers, onions, and tomatoes until they become tender. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Remove the cooked vegetables from the skillet and set them aside.
In the same skillet, using cooking spray, lightly cook the egg whites until they're scrambled and cooked through. Remove from heat.
Lay out the corn tortillas on a clean surface.
Divide the cooked vegetables and scrambled egg whites evenly between the tortillas.
Sprinkle shredded cheese over the fillings.
Roll up each tortilla tightly to form enchiladas.
Place the enchiladas seam-side down in an oven-safe dish.
Top the enchiladas with salsa.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 10-15 minutes, or until the enchiladas are heated through and the cheese is melted.
Combine first 5 ingredients in a blender or food processor; process until smooth.
Place chicken in a zip-top plastic bag. Add broth mixture.
Seal and marinate in refrigerator 2 hours, turning bag occasionally.
Preheat grill. Remove chicken from bag, discard marinade.
Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper. Place chicken and onion on grill rack coated with cooking spray.
Grill onion 3 minutes on each side or until onions are browned. Grill chicken 6 minutes on each side or until done.Cool 5 minutes.
Cut chicken into very thin slices.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Place 1 tortilla on a baking sheet coated with cooking spray. Sprinkle with 3 tbsp cheese, half of chicken, half of onion, and 1½ tsp cilantro.
Top with 1 tortilla. Sprinkle with remaining 3 tbsp cheese, chicken, onion, and 1½ tsp cilantro. Top with remaining tortilla.
Coat tortilla with cooking spray. Bake at 400°F for 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Let stand 5 minutes.
Cut into 4 wedges. Top each serving with 1 tsp sour cream.
Garnish with cilantro sprigs and serve with lime wedges, if desired.
Combine yogurt, sugar, lemon juice, vinegar, salt, pepper and hot sauce in a large bowl. Add cabbage and toss until fully coated.
Combine chicken and barbecue sauce in a medium microwavable bowl, toss until chicken is coated. Microwave on High until heated through, about 1 minute.
Heat tortillas according to package directions. Fill each tortilla with some chicken and top with the slaw and avocado. Garnish with cilantro and serve.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the shredded chicken, diced onion, diced green chilies, chopped cilantro, ground cumin, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Warm the corn tortillas in the microwave for about 10-15 seconds to make them pliable.
Place about half of the chicken mixture onto each tortilla and roll tightly to form a taquito.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the taquitos seam side down in the air fryer basket, making sure they are not touching.
Air fry for 8-10 minutes, until the taquitos are crispy and golden brown.
Remove the taquitos from the air fryer and let them cool slightly before serving.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and cook for 3 minutes.
Add vegetarian beef crumbles, chipotle peppers, adobo sauce, cumin, and onion powder. Cook and stir for 5 minutes.
Add cream cheese, nutritional yeast, and salt. Cook until cheese is melted and everything is evenly combined, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat.
Preheat an air fryer to 400°F.
Spoon a heaping tablespoon of mixture into the middle of each tortilla and roll up.
Working in batches, place a single layer of taquitos in the air fryer basket, seam-side down.
Spray the tops with cooking spray. Air fry for 6 minutes. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate. Repeat with remaining taquitos.
Pour oil into a pan on medium to medium-high and saute the peppers and onions until just softened.  Salt and pepper to taste.
Add the cooked chicken and cilantro to the pan and stir,
Once the chicken is hot, place 1/4 of the fajita mixture on each tortilla.  If you prefer your tortillas warm, you can place them in the microwave for 8-10 seconds.
Top each tortilla with fresh cilantro if desired and enjoy!
In a skillet, heat a small amount of cooking oil over medium-high heat.
Add the thinly sliced chicken breast and cook until it's no longer pink and has a nice sear, about 4-5 minutes.
Move the cooked chicken to one side of the skillet and add diced onions and minced garlic to the other side. Sauté for about 2 minutes until they become translucent and fragrant.
Stir in the chili powder and cumin, cooking for an additional minute to toast the spices.
Combine the cooked chicken, diced tomatoes, and the onion-garlic mixture in the skillet. Mix well.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth and simmer for another 5 minutes or until the mixture thickens. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Warm the tortillas in a dry skillet or microwave for a few seconds until they are soft and pliable.
To assemble the tacos, divide the chicken tinga mixture between the two tortillas.
Top with shredded lettuce, chopped fresh cilantro, low-fat Greek yogurt or sour cream, and salsa.
Cook the eggs sunny side up.
Place two fried tortillas on a plate and spread the refried beans onto each.
Grill the Smithfield Anytime Favorites Ham Steaks until slightly browned and add on top of the beans.
Add the eggs on top of the Smithfield Anytime Favorites ham steaks.
Add the peas to the salsa roja and stir briefly. Add the salsa roja with the peas on top of the eggs.
Top with jalapeno slices, cilantro, and queso fresco. Serve Immediately.
Cook and stir chorizo over medium high heat until well browned and crumbled.
Beat eggs in a bowl, and add to chorizo mixture. Reduce heat to medium-low, and continue cooking and stirring until eggs are scrambled.
Warm flour tortillas in the microwave for 30 seconds. Spoon mixture into the middle of each tortilla.
Roll up like a burrito and serve with sour cream.
To cook the shrimp, heat a skillet over medium-high heat and add olive oil. Add the large, peeled and deveined shrimp to the hot oil.
To season the shrimp, add garlic powder, chili powder, cayenne pepper, ground black pepper, sea salt, and granulated onion to the pan and stir to evenly cover the shrimp with the seasoning.
Cook the shrimp, stirring frequently, until the shrimp is golden on the outside and no longer transparent on the inside.
Lay a corn tortilla flat on a clean surface. Pile several shrimp onto the center of the tortilla, followed by the pico de gallo, and then drizzle guacamole over the top.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Meanwhile heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add beef and cook for 7 to 8 minutes. Remove.
Combine beef and cheese in a bowl. Use a fork to break up the beef and mix ingredients.
Begin spooning beef mixture into tortillas making a line down the middle of the tortilla. Roll tortilla around the filling and place in baking pan.
Cook until the tortillas are completely browned, 5 to 8 minutes per side.
Heat oil in a skillet. Add onion and cook until soft and translucent, 3 to 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add green chiles and Mexican tomato sauce; stir to combine. Add chicken and Mexican cheese blend.
Cook and stir until cheeses have melted and mixture is completely warmed, about 3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat tortillas in a skillet or directly on the grates of a gas stove until soft and pliable.
Place 3 tablespoons of chicken mixture down the center of each tortilla. Fold over and roll into taquitos.
Preheat an air fryer to 400°F.
Place taquitos in the air fryer basket, making sure they are not touching, and sprinkle with avocado oil.
Cook in batches if necessary. Cook until golden brown and crispy, 6 to 9 minutes.
Turn taquitos over, mist with avocado oil, and air fry for an additional 3 to 5 minutes.
vegetable oil: In a mixing bowl, combine the crumbled paneer, cooked peas, mashed potato, cumin seeds, garam masala, turmeric powder, chili powder, and salt. Mix well to combine.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the paneer and pea mixture to the pan and sauté for 2-3 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside to cool.

In a separate bowl, prepare the samosa dough by combining all-purpose flour, salt, and vegetable oil. Gradually add water and knead until a smooth dough forms. Cover with a damp cloth and let it rest for 15-20 minutes.
Divide the dough into small lemon-sized balls and roll each ball into a thin circle.
Cut each circle in half to form semi-circles.
Take one semi-circle and fold it into a cone shape, sealing the edges with water.
Fill the cone with the paneer and pea mixture, then seal the open edge using water to form a triangular samosa.
Repeat the process with the remaining dough and filling.
Heat vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Once the oil is hot, carefully add the samosas in batches and fry until they turn golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove the fried samosas from the oil and drain them on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Serve the paneer and pea samosas hot with your favorite chutney or dipping sauce.
Combine olive oil, vegetable oil, cider vinegar, and sugar in a saucepan, bring to a boil, remove from heat, and cool to room temperature.
Stir pinto beans, black-eyed peas, corn, onion, celery, red bell pepper, and cilantro together in a large bowl.
Pour cooled oil mixture over bean mixture and toss to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate, stirring occasionally, for 24 hours.
Heat oil in a skillet. Add onion and cook until soft and translucent, 3 to 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add green chiles and Mexican tomato sauce; stir to combine. Add chicken and Mexican cheese blend.
Cook and stir until cheeses have melted and mixture is completely warmed, about 3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat tortillas in a skillet or directly on the grates of a gas stove until soft and pliable.
Place 3 tablespoons of chicken mixture down the center of each tortilla. Fold over and roll into taquitos.
Preheat an air fryer to 400°F.
Place taquitos in the air fryer basket, making sure they are not touching, and sprinkle with avocado oil.
Cook in batches if necessary. Cook until golden brown and crispy, 6 to 9 minutes.
Turn taquitos over, mist with avocado oil, and air fry for an additional 3 to 5 minutes.
In a small saucepan, combine the quick-cooking grits, low-sodium chicken broth, skim milk, and a pinch of salt. Stir well.
Place the saucepan over medium-high heat and bring the mixture to a boil.
Once it boils, reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan with a lid, and simmer for about 5-7 minutes, or until the grits are creamy and thickened, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat.
While the grits are cooking, heat the vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and diced bell pepper to the skillet. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes, or until they start to soften.
Add the peeled and deveined shrimp to the skillet. Cook for about 2-3 minutes per side, or until they turn pink and opaque.
Sprinkle the smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper over the shrimp and vegetables. Stir to combine and cook for another minute.
Once the grits are ready, stir in the shredded cheddar cheese until it's melted and well incorporated.
Serve the creamy cheddar grits in a bowl, and top with the cooked shrimp, sautéed vegetables, and diced tomato.
Combine paprika, thyme, cumin, cayenne pepper, onion powder, black pepper, and salt together in a bowl. Transfer spice mixture to a flat plate.
Rub oil over each chicken breast until fully coated.
Roll each piece of chicken in blackening spice mixture, making sure to press down so spice sticks on all sides.
Let sit for 5 minutes while you preheat the air fryer.
Preheat an air fryer to 360°F.
Place chicken in the basket of the air fryer and cook for 10 minutes.
Flip and cook an additional 10 minutes. Transfer chicken to a plate and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Heat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the vegetable oil and shrimp to the hot skillet. Stir-fry the shrimp until they turn pink and opaque, about 2-3 minutes. Remove the shrimp from the skillet and set them aside.
In the same skillet, add the minced garlic and stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the shredded cabbage, thinly sliced carrot, and frozen peas to the skillet. Continue to stir-fry for 2-3 minutes or until the vegetables start to become tender.
Add the cold cooked jasmine rice to the skillet. Break up any clumps and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes to heat the rice through.
Return the cooked shrimp to the skillet.
In a small bowl, mix together the low-sodium soy sauce, gochujang, sesame oil, and black pepper.
Pour the sauce mixture over the rice and shrimp. Stir-fry for another 2 minutes to evenly distribute the flavors.
Season with additional soy sauce or gochujang to taste if desired.
Transfer the Korean Saewoo Bokkeumbap to a serving plate.
Garnish with thinly sliced green onions.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, cornstarch, vegetable oil, salt, and pepper.
Add thinly sliced beef to the marinade and let it sit for about 10 minutes.
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F for 3-5 minutes.
Place broccoli florets in the air fryer basket and cook for 5 minutes, shaking halfway through.
Remove the broccoli from the air fryer basket and set aside.
Place the marinated beef slices in the air fryer basket in a single layer. Cook for 5-7 minutes, shaking halfway through, until the beef is cooked through and slightly crispy.
Add the cooked broccoli back to the air fryer basket with the beef. Toss everything together and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes to heat through.
Serve the air fryer beef and broccoli hot, garnished with sliced green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
Cook the lo mein noodles according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet or wok, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds, until fragrant.
Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook until pink and opaque, about 2-3 minutes per side. Remove the shrimp from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the broccoli florets and sliced red bell pepper. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes, until tender-crisp.
Return the cooked shrimp to the skillet with the vegetables.
Add the cooked lo mein noodles to the skillet, along with the soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, and red pepper flakes, if using. Toss everything together until well combined and heated through.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving plate.
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve hot.
Mix tuna, mayonnaise, and salsa together in a bowl. Set aside.
Heat vegetable oil over medium-high heat in a frying pan. Add bread in batches and fry until golden and slightly crisp, 2 to 3 minutes.
Spread tuna mixture over tortillas evenly. Top with tomato, and onions. Garnish with olives and parsley.
In a shallow bowl, combine the cornmeal, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Dip the catfish fillet into the buttermilk, allowing any excess to drip off.
Coat the catfish fillet evenly with the cornmeal mixture, pressing the mixture onto the fish to adhere.
Heat the vegetable oil in a skillet or frying pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F .
Carefully place the coated catfish fillet into the hot oil.
Fry for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until the catfish is golden brown and crispy, and the internal temperature reaches 145°F.
Use a slotted spatula to remove the fried catfish from the oil and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Let it cool for a minute before serving.
Heat oil in a medium skillet. Add onion and cook until soft and translucent, 4 to 6 minutes.
Add chicken, soft cheese, diced chiles, chicken broth, taco seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Cook and stir until mixture is well combined and soft hceese has softened been incorporated.
Heat tortillas in a large skillet or directly on grates of a gas stove until soft and pliable.
Place 1/3 cup chicken mixture down the center of each tortilla and top with a heaping tablespoon of Mexican cheese.
Fold top and bottom of tortillas over the filling, then roll each into a burrito shape. Mist with cooking spray and place in the basket of an air fryer.
Air fry at 400°F for 4 to 6 minutes. Flip each chimichanga over, mist with cooking spray, and air fry until lightly browned, 2 to 4 minutes more.
Add prawns, yeast, chestnuts, green onions, ginger root, garlic and salt to a food processor.
Whisk egg white, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil together in a small bowl and drizzle over the top of the shrimp mixture.
Pulse until smooth, but not pureed, into a prawn mousse.
Trim crusts off the bread and equally spread the prawn mousse all the way to the edge of each bread slice.
Cut diagonally into halves, and lightly sprinkle with sesame seeds
Add oil to a pan and heat up. Add 4 of the toasts, prawn-side down, not crowding the pan.
Fry until toast is golden brown, 30 to 45 seconds, flip and cook bread side for another 15 seconds.
The toasts cook very quickly, so keep your eye on them. Repeat for next batch.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined rack and serve warm with sweet chili sauce.
Heat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the vegetable oil and diced chicken to the hot skillet. Stir-fry until the chicken is cooked through and no longer pink, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and stir-fry for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the mixed vegetables to the skillet and continue to stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, or until they start to become tender.
Add the cold cooked brown rice to the skillet. Break up any clumps and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes to heat the rice through.
Pour the low-sodium soy sauce and sesame oil over the rice and stir-fry for another 2 minutes to evenly distribute the flavors.
Season with black pepper and stir to combine.
Transfer the garlic chicken fried brown rice to a serving plate.
Garnish with thinly sliced green onions if desired.
Combine soy sauce, vegetable oil, chile paste, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and red chile flakes in a large bowl.
Whisk to completely dissolve chile paste. Add chicken and stir to coat.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 30 minutes and up to 2 hours.
Preheat grill for medium heat and lightly oil the grate. Grill the chicken until slightly charred and no longer pink in the center, 5 to 8 minutes per side.
In a large bowl, mix together beef, cabbage, carrot, and onion. Stir in the egg, salt, soy sauce, and vegetable oil.
Place a large teaspoonful of filling in the center of a dumpling skin. Moisten the edges of a wonton with a few drops of water.
Then fold the dumpling in half, and pinch the edges together to seal.
Create a ripple pattern along the pinched edge by pinching and gently pushing together small segments of it. Repeat with remaining dumplings.
Boil the dumplings in water until they float to the top, about 5 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Cook the egg sunny side up to desired doneness.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and add the vegetable oil.
Add the ground beef to the skillet and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, until it's browned and cooked through. Drain any excess fat.
In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, sesame oil, and garlic powder to create a sauce.
Pour the sauce over the cooked ground beef and stir to coat. Cook for an additional minute, then remove the beef from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced carrots and cook for about 2-3 minutes until they start to soften. Remove them from the skillet and set them aside.
Repeat the process with the sliced mushrooms, cooking for about 2-3 minutes until they become tender. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the baby spinach and cook briefly until it wilts, about 1-2 minutes. Remove and set aside.
To assemble your Bibimbap, start by placing the cooked rice in a bowl.
Arrange the cooked ground beef, carrots, cucumber, mushrooms, bean sprouts, and wilted spinach on top of the rice in separate sections.
Top your Bibimbap with a fried egg, cooked sunny-side up.
Garnish with sesame seeds.
Pre-heat oven to 450°F.
Wash carrots, remove the greens and cut them into even diagonal slices.
Place the carrots on a sheet pan, drizzle with 2 tablespoons olive oil. Add  1 teaspoon kosher salt and ½ teaspoon pepper. Toss well with your hands and spread out in one layer.
Bake for 15 minutes or until tender and lightly browned.
While the carrots cook, prepare the chicken. If your chicken breasts are thick, butterfly them or pound to 1/2 inch thickness.
Season both sides with salt and pepper; place on a foil or parchment-lined baking sheet.
Place the matzo breadcrumbs, salt, and pepper in a shallow dish or plate and stir to combine.
Place the eggs in another shallow dish or plate and whisk to blend,
Working with one piece of chicken at a time, coat in the eggs and allow the excess to drip off.
Then coat thoroughly in the matzoh breadcrumbs, turning a few times and pressing firmly to adhere.
Return to your baking sheet and repeat with remaining chicken.
Reduce oven heat to 375°F after you remove your carrots and set them aside.  Cover carrots in tented foil to keep warm.
Line a baking tray with foil or parchment; lay the breaded chicken on the tray. Drizzle each piece with a little oil.
Bake for 10 to 15 minutes on each side, or until the chicken is white in the middle, the juices run clear.
Serve with baked rainbow carrots.
In a small bowl, whisk together chicken broth, soy sauce, oyster sauce, rice vinegar, and cornstarch until smooth. Set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add diced onion, carrot, celery, and bell pepper to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are tender, about 5-7 minutes.
Add shrimp to the skillet and cook until they turn pink and opaque, about 2-3 minutes.
Stir the sauce mixture once more and then pour it into the skillet with the shrimp and vegetables. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens, about 1-2 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a medium bowl, stir together vinegar, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.
Heat oil in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Stir in sesame seeds, and reduce heat to medium. Cook until seeds are brown, about 5 minutes.
Remove seeds with a slotted spoon, and stir into vinegar mixture. Mix in cucumber, green onions, and carrot. Cover, and refrigerate at least 5 minutes.
Start by cleaning the soft-shell crab. Remove any gills, apron, and the face section. Rinse it under cold water and pat it dry with paper towels.
In a shallow bowl, combine the flour, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
In another shallow bowl, pour the skim milk.
Heat the vegetable oil in a skillet or frying pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F
Heat the vegetable oil in a skillet or frying pan over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F
Dip the cleaned soft-shell crab into the skim milk.
Coat the crab evenly with the flour mixture, pressing it onto the crab to adhere.
Carefully place the coated soft-shell crab into the hot oil.
Fry for about 2-3 minutes per side, or until the crab turns golden brown and crispy.
Use a slotted spatula to remove the fried soft-shell crab from the oil and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Let it cool for a minute before serving.
