cheese cheddar: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook lentils according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Stir together all the ingredients except the tortillas and cheese in a medium bowl. Warm a tortilla in a a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Once the tortilla is warm, place some cheese and some  lentil and veggie mixture onto the tortilla. Sprinkle with some cheese and place another tortilla on top.
Cook for about 2 minutes, pressing down with the back of your spatula to help seal the quesadilla as the cheese melts. Flip, and cook another 2 minutes.
Remove from heat and slice into triangles. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Place hashbrown in each case of a muffin tin, and smush them down to form a base. Bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
Place 1 tbsp of beans on top of each cooked hash brown, followed by a few pieces of sausages and bacon.
Beat the eggs and milk together and pour some into each cup, being careful not to overfill. Top with grated cheese, parsley, and pepper.
Bake for 5 minutes, and serve.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Combine pork sausage, Italian sausage and mozzarella cheese in a bowl.
Mix well and form into about 24 equal-sized meatballs. Arrange meatballs in a shallow baking pan.
Bake in the preheated oven meatballs are nicely browned for about 20 minutes. Serve and garnish with basil.
Preheat the oven to 425°F. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil.
Arrange bagels on prepared baking sheet, cut sides up. Spoon a thin layer of pizza sauce over each bagel half and sprinkle with cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted and pepperoni is lightly browned, about 6 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Unroll crescent dough and separate into triangles. Slice sausage into coin-sized pieces.
Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add eggs and sausage pieces and season with salt and pepper.
Cook, stirring, until cooked through, about 2 minutes. Place about 2 tablespoons of filling and some Cheddar cheese onto the crescent triangles.
Cook in the air fryer until golden brown, about 5 minutes. Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta in boiling water until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, brown beef and drain off excess fat. Add taco seasoning and water according to seasoning package directions and simmer for 10 minutes. Set aside.
Mix together macaroni and cheese by adding ghee and milk until desired consistency. Combine beef mixture with macaroni and cheese. Mix together and serve.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Place squash and onion in a large skillet over medium heat. Pour in water.
Cover, and cook until squash is tender, about 5 minutes. Drain well, and place in a large bowl.
Mix cracker crumbs and cheese together in a medium bowl. Stir half of the cracker mixture into the cooked squash and onions.
Mix eggs and milk together in a small bowl, then add to squash mixture.
Stir in 1/4 cup melted butter, and season with salt and pepper. Spread into the prepared baking dish.
Sprinkle with remaining cracker mixture, and dot with ghee.
Bake in the preheated oven until lightly browned, about 25 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Heat oil in large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion, and cook 5 minutes.
Add spinach and cook 5 minutes, or until spinach begins to wilt. Transfer vegetables to bowl and let cool. Add cheese.
Whisk milk and eggs in large bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Pour eggs over veggies and stir well to combine.
Divide the mixture evenly among prepared muffin cups and bake 20 minutes, or until puffed up and set. Serve with chopped green onion.
Set an oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add penne and cook for about 11 minutes. Drain pasta and return to the pot.
Meanwhile add oil in a pot and slowly add milk. ncorporate pumpkin, whisking until thoroughly combined.
Add garlic salt and 4 ounces vegan cheese. Mix until smooth. Fill casserole dishes with the pasta mixture. Top equally with remaining vegan cheese.
Broil in the preheated oven until cheese is melted, 1 to 3 minutes. Serve warm.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Line a large rimmed baking sheet with foil.
Place slices of bacon on the prepared baking sheet. Place bacon in the preheated oven and bake until partially cooked, but still pliable, about 8 minutes.
Remove from the oven, and reduce temperature to 350°F. Let bacon rest until cool enough to handle.
Oil 8 cups of a muffin pan. Whisk eggs together in a medium bowl. Wrap a slice of bacon around the inside of each prepared muffin cup.
Divide Cheddar cheese amongst the 6 muffin cups and top with beaten egg. Bake in the oven until eggs are set, 13 to 15 minutes.
Cook bacon in a large skillet and over medium heat, turning occasionally, until crispy, 10 to 12 minutes.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate with a slotted spoon, leaving bacon grease in the skillet. Crumble bacon when cool to the touch.
Add celery and garlic to the skillet and cook in the bacon grease until celery is tender, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add cauliflower, water, and onion powder, cover skillet to steam cauliflower. Cook, stirring every so often, until cauliflower is tender, about 5 minutes.
Add milk, 1 3/4 cups chicken broth, and hot sauce. Bring to a boil.
Meanwhile, whisk flour into the remaining chicken broth in a small bowl and drizzle into the boiling chowder.
Add Cheddar cheese and 1/2 of the crumbled bacon. Stir until smooth and cheese has melted.
Season with salt, pepper, and hot sauce. Ladle into bowls and top with remaining bacon and green onion.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Combine chili powder and cocoa powder in a bowl, sprinkle over both sides of pork loin
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and brown pork loin on both sides, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer pork and juices to a baking dish.
Roast in the preheated oven until pork is slightly pink in the center, about 12 minutes.
Combine mango, shallot, cilantro, and lemon juice in a bowl. Slice pork loin and top with juices from the baking dish and mango mixture. Top with cheese slices.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Fry the bacon in butter on medium heat until crispy. Add the spinach and stir until wilted. Remove the pan from the heat and set aside.
Whisk the eggs and cream together and pour into baking dish.
Add the bacon, spinach, tomato, mushrooms, and cheese on top and place in the middle of the oven.
Bake for 20 minutes or until set in the middle and golden brown on top.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Mix green beans and condensed soup together in a large microwave-safe bowl until well combined. Microwave on high until warm, 3 to 5 minutes.
Stir 1/2 cup Cheddar cheese into the green bean mixture. Microwave on high for 2 to 3 minutes.
Transfer mixture to a casserole dish and spread evenly over the bottom. Sprinkle French-fried onions over top, then sprinkle with remaining Cheddar.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted and the onions are just turning brown, about 10 minutes.
In a medium pot or saucepan, bring 6 cups of salted water to a boil. While waiting for the water to boil, prepare a large bowl with ice water.
Add broccoli florets to the boiling water and cook until tender, 1 minute. Remove with a slotted spoon and  immediately place in the prepared bowl of ice water.
When cool, spin the broccoli in a salad spinner. You will need to spin it at least twice. In a medium bowl, whisk to combine dressing ingredients.
Season to taste with salt and pepper. Serve immediately. Combine all salad ingredients in a large bowl and pour over dressing.
Toss until ingredients are combined and fully coated in dressing. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Combine almond flour, parsley and baking powder in a large bowl. Mix in Cheddar cheese by hand.
Create a small well in the middle of the bowl, add eggs and heavy whipping cream to the center.
Use a large fork, spoon, or your hands to blend in the flour mixture until a sticky batter forms.
Drop 8 portions of batter onto the prepared baking sheet. Bake in the preheated oven until golden, about 20 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Grease a muffin tin with oil.
In a medium bowl whisk together eggs, heavy cream, salt, pepper, tomatoes, ham, and zucchini.
Pour into tin, garnish with cheddar, and bake for 30 minutes.
Set an oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place bell peppers on the prepared baking sheet. Place in the preheated oven and broil, until the skins begin to bubble and turn dark brown.
Remove and place browned peppers in a bowl. Cover with aluminum foil and set aside to cool. Leave the broiler on.
Meanwhile, whisk together eggs, hot pepper sauce, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Brown chorizo in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat, breaking up any large pieces as you go, about 4 minutes.
Add egg mixture to the skillet and cook until eggs have set, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in salsa.
Fill each pepper with chorizo-egg mixture and sprinkle with Cheddar cheese.
Place in the oven and broil until cheese melts, about 1 minute. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil, cook the rotini at a boil until tender yet firm to the bite, about 10 minutes, drain.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground turkey and break it up with a wooden spoon.
Add chili powder, garlic, cumin, cayenne pepper, oregano, and black pepper, mix thoroughly.
Cook and stir until turkey is browned and crumbly, 5 to 7 minutes.
Add corn, diced tomatoes and chiles, green bell pepper, onion, celery, and carrot; mix thoroughly.
Place lid on skillet. Cook until vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally, about 8 minutes.
Add tomato sauce, let simmer until thickened, about 5 minutes. Transfer turkey mixture to a casserole dish and add the cooked pasta.
Mix everything together. Top with cheese. Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted, about 10 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Mix the flour, cheddar and baking powder together.
Whisk the eggs, and add the salt to the eggs, then add to the flour bowl. Add the melted butter to the flour bowl. Combine.
Add the milk and mix well. Stir in the crumbled feta. Stir in the chopped spinach
Spoon the mixture into your muffin tin, or into muffin cases. Throw a little extra cheddar on top of the muffins.
Bake for 20 minutes. Take out of the oven when the muffin tops are golden brown and cooked through.
onion salt: Place the potatoes in a pot with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil, and cook for about 10 minutes, or until easily pierced with a fork.
Drain, and transfer to a large bowl to cool.
In a medium bowl, mix the sour cream, mayonnaise, mustard, vinegar, eggs, pickle, celery, green onions, and hot sauce.
Season with dill, garlic powder, onion salt, salt, and pepper. Pour over the potatoes, and gently toss to coat. Chill at least 3 hours in the refrigerator before serving.
Slice each hot dog lengthwise into 3 thin slices.
In medium bowl, combine ricotta, egg, parsley, garlic salt and onion salt.
In another bowl combine the mozzarella and parmesan cheese.
In a 9 x13 inches baking pan, pour ⅓ of the pasta sauce.
Layer ½ of the hot dog slices in a single layer.
Then layer ½ of the ricotta mixture on top of the hot dogs, then ½ of the mozzarella/parmesan cheese mix.
Layer the next ⅓ the pasta sauce, remainder of the hot dog slices, and remainder of ricotta mixture.
Top with remaining pasta sauce and then mozzarella/parmesan cheese mixture.
Bake for about 45 minutes or until the lasagna is hot and bubbly and the cheese is lightly browned.
Top with the unnecessary parsley garnish if desired.
mustard powder: Brush the tuna with a little oil.
Heat a non-stick pan.
Add the tuna and cook for 1 min each side, or a min or so longer for a thicker fillet.
Transfer to a plate to rest. Halve and stone the avocado and scoop the flesh into a small bowl.
Add the mustard powder and vinegar, then mash well so that the mixture is smooth like mayonnaise. Stir in the capers.
Spoon into two small dishes and put on serving plates with the lettuce leaves, and tomatoes. Slice the tuna.
Spoon some ‘mayo’ on the lettuce leaves and top with tuna and cherry tomatoes and a few extra capers.
To eat, roll up into little wraps.
In a medium saucepan over medium heat, heat olive oil. Add onion and sauté until translucent, stirring often. Add garlic and cook, for 30 seconds.
Add stock, broccoli, and carrots, then cover with a lid. Simmer until broccoli is tender, about 10 minutes.
In a high-speed blender, combine the remaining ingredients. Blend at high speed until very, very smooth. Pour mixture into broccoli mixture and stir until smooth.
Add plenty of sea salt and freshly cracked pepper to taste. Spoon into serving bowls and serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add green beans and cook uncovered until bright green, about 3 minutes.
Drain in a colander and immediately immerse in ice water for several minutes to stop the cooking process. Drain and transfer to a large bowl.
Mix mustard powder and cold water together in a small bowl to make a paste. Stir in vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, sesame seeds and salt.
Pour mustard paste mixture over green beans, toss to coat.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 9x13-inch casserole dish.
Heat a skillet over medium heat; cook and stir sausage until crumbly and completely browned, about 10 minutes.
Transfer cooked sausage to the prepared casserole dish.
Cook and stir mushrooms in the same skillet over medium heat until mushrooms are lightly browned, 5 to 10 minutes.
Mix mushrooms, eggs, bread, milk, Cheddar cheese, ham, spinach, mustard powder, salt, ghee and basil together in a large bowl.
Pour over sausage and bake in the preheated oven until a knife inserted into the center of the strata comes out clean.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Crumble sausage into a medium skillet. Cook over medium heat until evenly brown, drain.
In a medium bowl, mix together mustard powder, salt, eggs and milk. Add the sausage, bread cubes, and cheese, and stir to coat evenly.
Pour into a greased 9x13 inch baking dish. Cover, and bake 45 to 60 minutes.
Uncover, and reduce temperature to 325°F. Bake until set.
Preheat the air fryer to 350°F.
Whisk together paprika, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, poultry seasoning, mustard powder, and pepper in a large bowl.
Toss in chicken wings and rub seasonings into chicken with your hands until fully coated.
Arrange wings in the air fryer basket, standing them up on their ends and leaning against each other and the wall of the basket.
Cook in the preheated air fryer until wings are tender inside and golden brown and crisp on the outside, about 35 minutes. Transfer wings to a plate and serve hot.
garlic powder: First, add your onion to a rimmed skillet over medium-high heat. Let the onions start to stick and then add a tbsp of water. Repeat this process for about 3 minutes.
Then add the bell peppers and mushrooms. Saute for a couple minutes.
Crumble tofu with your hand over the pan. You can also cut the tofu into chunks and scramble it in the pan with your cooking utensil.
Stir everything around, cook for a couple of minutes, add spinach if using, and stir some more. Garnish with scallions.
In a mixing bowl, add the kimchi. Use kitchen shears to cup it into small bits then add the rest of the ingredients.
Whisk and combine well. The batter will be on the thin side.
Preheat skillet, add oil and swirl it around. Take ½ cup of the batter and add it to the pan and immediately use the back of a spoon to spread it.
Cook over medium-low heat about 2 minutes on the first side and 1 minute on the second side. Repeat the process until you finish the batter.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced chicken and sauté for 2-3 minutes until it starts to brown.
Add the diced bell peppers, diced tomatoes, and frozen peas to the skillet. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the long-grain rice, paprika, saffron threads (if using), garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Pour the chicken broth into the skillet and give everything a good stir.

Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet with a lid, and simmer for about 10-12 minutes. Check the rice occasionally and add more broth if needed to prevent sticking.
Once the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed, remove the skillet from heat.
Let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a bowl, combine olive oil, chopped cilantro, lime juice, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Add shrimp to the bowl and toss to coat evenly.
Place the seasoned shrimp on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 6-8 minutes until shrimp are pink and cooked through.
Serve hot with your favorite side dishes.
In a mixing bowl, combine the thawed and drained spinach, Greek yogurt, sour cream, Parmesan cheese, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper.
Mix until well combined.
Serve immediately or refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve with your favorite vegetables, crackers, or bread for dipping.
Heat the cooked chickpeas in a skillet over medium heat.
Season chickpeas with chili powder, ground cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for 3-4 minutes.
Warm the corn tortillas in the skillet or microwave.
Divide the seasoned chickpeas between the tortillas.
Top with sliced avocado, shredded lettuce, salsa, and thinly sliced radish.
Garnish with optional toppings as desired.
Serve the chickpea tacos hot.
Preheat your air fryer to 390°F.
Wash the eggplant and pat it dry with paper towels. Slice the eggplant into rounds or strips, about 1/4 inch thick.
In a shallow bowl, mix together the flour, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the milk into another shallow bowl.
Place the breadcrumbs in a third shallow bowl.
Dredge each eggplant slice first in the flour mixture, then dip it into the milk, and finally coat it in the breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Place the breaded eggplant slices in a single layer in the air fryer basket, making sure they are not overlapping.
Lightly spray or brush the tops of the breaded eggplant slices with cooking spray or oil.
Air fry for 10-12 minutes, flipping halfway through the cooking time, until the eggplant is golden brown and crispy on the outside and tender on the inside.
Remove the battered air fried eggplant from the air fryer and serve hot.
Preheat your charcoal grill to high heat.
Season the ribeye steak generously with salt and black pepper on both sides.
Optional: If desired, brush the steak lightly with olive oil and sprinkle with garlic powder and dried herbs for extra flavor.
Once the grill is hot, place the steak directly over the coals.
Grill the steak for about 3-4 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or adjust cooking time according to your desired level of doneness.
Use tongs to flip the steak halfway through cooking.
Once the steak reaches your preferred level of doneness, remove it from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve hot.
In a large pot add a pinch of salt and bring water to a boil. Stir in the shrimp, cover, and remove the pot from heat.
Let sit for 5 minutes, until all the shrimp are cooked through to a bright pink shade.
In a large salad bowl, toss the spinach, arugula, tomatoes, avocados, and shrimp.
Drizzle with salt, garlic powder, olive oil, vinegar and sesame seeds.
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F for 3-5 minutes.
In a shallow bowl, combine the breadcrumbs, garlic powder, paprika, and salt.
Place the flour and beaten egg in separate shallow bowls.
Dip each okra slice into the flour, shaking off any excess.
Dip the floured okra slices into the beaten egg, allowing any excess to drip off.
Coat the okra slices in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing gently to adhere.
Place the breaded okra slices in a single layer in the air fryer basket.
Lightly spray the breaded okra slices with cooking spray.
Air fry for 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway through cooking, until the okra is crispy and golden brown.
Serve hot as a snack or side dish.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
, Boil pasta for 3-5 min or to al dente.
Drain pasta and reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
While pasta is cooking, in a large saucepan (4 quart) or medium pot melt butter over medium heat then add flour, cook 1 minute whisking constantly.
While whisking slowly pour in milk, add in mustard and garlic powder.
Increase heat slightly, then bring mixture to a gentle simmer whisking constantly.
Reduce heat to low add in cream cheese and whisk until melted.
Remove saucepan from heat add in the shredded cheeses half at a time, and whisk to melt, while returning to warm heat as needed to melt fully.
Season sauce with salt to taste.
Toss pasta with cheese sauce, thin with pasta water a splash at a time as needed.
Serve immediately.
Blend tofu, garlic powder, oregano, basil, and lemon juice in a blender until combined.
Stream olive oil into the tofu mixture while blending until your obtain the desired consistency.
Top sliced bread with tofu mixture.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the diced chicken breast, mixed vegetables, broth, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Transfer the chicken mixture to a small oven-safe dish.
In another bowl, mix together the biscuit mix and milk until a dough forms.
Drop spoonfuls of the biscuit dough on top of the chicken mixture in the dish.
Sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese over the biscuit dough.
Place the dish in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the biscuit topping is golden brown and cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the Chicken Cobbler from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
In a shallow bowl, combine almond flour, grated Parmesan cheese, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Place the beaten egg in another shallow bowl.
Dip each chicken tenderloin into the beaten egg, then coat it with the almond flour mixture, pressing gently to adhere the crumbs to the chicken.
Preheat your air fryer to 400°F.
Lightly grease the air fryer basket with cooking spray or olive oil.
Place the crumbed chicken tenderloins in the greased air fryer basket in a single layer, leaving space between each one.
Air fry the chicken tenderloins at 400°F for 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the chicken tenderloins from the air fryer and let them cool slightly before serving.
Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C).
In a bowl, toss diced potatoes with olive oil, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Spread the seasoned potatoes in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Roast in the preheated oven for 30-35 minutes or until the potatoes are golden and crispy, flipping halfway through.
Remove the oven-roasted potatoes from the oven.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, combine the grated zucchini, crumbled feta cheese, breadcrumbs, chopped fresh spinach, chopped fresh dill, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix everything together.
Shape the mixture into small tots or patties.
Place the tots on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the olive oil over the tots to help them crisp up in the oven.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the tots are golden brown and cooked through.
Remove from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Preheat an oven to 375°F. Grease a baking sheet liberally with olive oil.
Place the salmon fillets onto the baking sheet skin-side-down. Sprinkle the fillets with garlic powder, dill, salt, and pepper.
Lay the bacon strips over the fillets to cover completely. Do not overlap the bacon strips. Arrange them on a skewer.
Bake in the preheated oven until the salmon is no longer translucent in the center, 20 to 25 minutes.
Turn the oven on to broil and cook until the bacon has crisped, 1 to 2 minutes.
Preheat your oven to 325°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the torn kale leaves with olive oil. Toss well to coat the kale evenly with the oil.
In a small bowl, mix together the ground cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, a pinch of salt, and a pinch of black pepper.
Sprinkle the spice mixture over the kale leaves and toss again to ensure the kale is coated with the spices.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Spread the seasoned kale leaves in a single layer on the baking sheet. Make sure they are not crowded to allow for even crisping.
Place the baking sheet in the preheated oven and bake for about 10-15 minutes, or until the kale is crisp and the edges are slightly browned. Keep a close eye on them to prevent burning.
Remove the kale crisps from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Cook the elbow macaroni according to the package instructions until it's al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a small saucepan over low heat, warm the low-fat milk. Be careful not to boil it; just heat it gently.
Add the shredded sharp cheddar cheese and shredded mozzarella cheese to the warm milk. Stir until the cheeses are melted and the mixture is smooth.
Season the cheese sauce with garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Stir well to combine.
Add the cooked elbow macaroni to the cheese sauce. Stir until the macaroni is evenly coated with the cheese sauce.
In a bowl, combine shredded chicken, breadcrumbs, onion, bell pepper, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Mix in beaten egg until well combined.
Divide the mixture into two equal portions and shape each into a patty.
Heat cooking spray or oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Cook chicken cakes for 3-4 minutes on each side until golden brown and cooked through.
Serve hot as desired.
feta cheese: In a large bowl, combine the chopped lettuce, diced tomatoes, chopped red onion, crumbled feta cheese, chickpeas.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Pour the dressing over the salad.
Toss everything together to coat the salad ingredients with the dressing.
Start by cooking 1/2 cup of dry couscous. In a small saucepan, bring 1/2 cup of water to a boil. Stir in the 1/2 cup of dry couscous.
Remove the saucepan from heat, cover it with a lid, and let it sit for 5 minutes to allow the couscous to absorb the water.
After 5 minutes, fluff the couscous with a fork to separate the grains. Allow it to cool.
After 5 minutes, fluff the couscous with a fork to separate the grains. Allow it to cool.
Drizzle 1 tablespoon of extra-virgin olive oil and 1 tablespoon of fresh lemon juice over the ingredients in the bowl.
Season the couscous salad with salt and black pepper to taste.
Toss all the ingredients together until they are well combined.
Rinse the dry lentils under cold water.
In a saucepan, combine the rinsed lentils with 3 cups of water.
Place the saucepan over medium-high heat and bring the water to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and let the lentils simmer for about 20-25 minutes or until they are tender but still firm (you can taste test them).
Drain any excess water from the cooked lentils and let them cool to room temperature.
In a bowl, combine the cooked lentils, arugula, diced tomato, sliced black olives, and crumbled feta cheese.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the fresh lemon juice and extra-virgin olive oil to create a dressing. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Drizzle the lemony dressing over the salad.
Toss everything together to ensure the salad is evenly coated with the dressing.
Rinse the quinoa thoroughly under cold water in a fine-mesh strainer.
In a saucepan, combine the rinsed quinoa and 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the quinoa is cooked and the water is absorbed. Remove from heat and let it cool to room temperature.
In a large salad bowl, combine the cooked and cooled quinoa, diced cucumber, diced tomato, diced red onion, and chopped fresh parsley. Toss to mix well.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dressing ingredients: extra-virgin olive oil, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad to ensure all the ingredients are coated with the dressing.
Sprinkle crumbled feta cheese on top.
In a large salad bowl, start with a base of mixed greens.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes to the greens.
Toss in the diced cucumber, and yellow bell pepper.
Sprinkle the thinly sliced red onion over the vegetables.
Tear the fresh basil leaves into pieces and add them to the salad.
Add crumbled feta cheese for extra flavor.
In a small bowl, whisk together the extra-virgin olive oil and balsamic vinegar to create a simple dressing. Season with a pinch of salt and black pepper to taste.
Drizzle the dressing evenly over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients in the salad bowl to combine and coat them with the dressing.
Taste and adjust the seasoning or dressing to your preference.
In a saucepan, bring 4 cups of salted water to a boil. Add 1 cup of orzo pasta and cook for 8-10 minutes or until al dente.
Drain and rinse with cold water to cool.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooked and cooled orzo, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, finely chopped red onion, sliced Kalamata olives, and crumbled feta cheese.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil and the juice of 1 lemon.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss the salad to combine all the ingredients.
Optionally, garnish with fresh parsley.
In a large salad bowl, place the thinly sliced kale leaves.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes and thinly sliced red onion to the kale.
Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the vegetables.
Season the salad with a pinch of salt and black pepper.

In a separate bowl, prepare the Green Goddess dressing. Combine mayonnaise, Greek yogurt, chopped parsley, chopped chives, chopped tarragon, minced garlic, lemon juice, and white wine vinegar. Mix until well combined.
Taste the dressing and adjust the seasoning with salt and black pepper. If the dressing is too thick, you can thin it with a little water until it reaches your desired consistency.
Drizzle the Green Goddess dressing over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients in the salad bowl to coat them with the dressing.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a large salad bowl, combine the torn lettuce, cooked macaroni pasta, chickpeas, red onion, Kalamata olives, tomatoes and crumbled feta cheese.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together theolive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper. Taste and adjust the seasoning if needed.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss all the ingredients together gently to ensure even distribution of the dressing.
In a bowl, place the fine bulgur wheat.
Pour enough boiling water over the bulgur to cover it and let it sit for about 15-20 minutes, or until it's softened. Drain any excess water.
In the same bowl, combine the softened bulgur, chopped fresh parsley, diced tomatoes, diced cucumbers, diced onions, canned chickpeas, and crumbled feta cheese.
In a small bowl, whisk together the fresh lemon juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper to create the dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the tabbouleh.
Toss everything together to ensure the tabbouleh is evenly coated with the dressing.
Cook the quinoa in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a large salad bowl, place the fresh spinach leaves.
Add the sliced strawberries on top of the spinach.
Sprinkle the chopped nuts over the salad.
Scatter the crumbled feta cheese over the salad.
Add the cooked quinoa to the salad.
In a small bowl or jar, prepare the vinaigrette. Combine olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, salt, and black pepper. Whisk or shake well to emulsify the dressing.
Taste the vinaigrette and adjust the sweetness or seasoning according to your preference.
Drizzle the vinaigrette over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients in the salad bowl to coat them with the dressing.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Place the diced sweet potato on a baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil, and season with salt and pepper. Toss to coat the sweet potato evenly.
Roast the sweet potato in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until it's tender and slightly caramelized.
Remove the roasted sweet potato from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
In a bowl, combine the roasted sweet potato, cranberries, and crumbled feta cheese.
Toss everything together gently.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add finely chopped onion and minced garlic. Cook until softened, about 2-3 minutes.
Add chopped fresh spinach to the skillet and cook until wilted, about 2-3 minutes. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly.
In a bowl, mix together cooked spinach mixture and crumbled feta cheese. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Place one sheet of phyllo dough on a flat surface. Brush lightly with olive oil.
Place the second sheet of phyllo dough on top of the first sheet. Brush with olive oil.
Spoon the spinach and feta mixture onto the phyllo dough, leaving a border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the phyllo dough over the filling to form a rectangular parcel.
Transfer the spanakopita to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the phyllo dough is golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
In a small bowl, whisk the eggs with a fork and season with salt and pepper, to taste. Set aside.
Heat a small skillet over medium heat and add the oil. Pour in the egg mixture and cook until almost set, about 5 minutes.
Place the chopped tomato and feta on one half of the omelet and fold the other half over top. Remove from heat and top with parsley.
In a pan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add spinach and cook for 4 minutes. Add beans and cook for 3 more minutes. Season with salt and mix well.
To serve, place spinach and beans on toast, top with feta cheese and red pepper flakes.
Finely chop roasted red peppers and sun-dried tomatoes, crumble feta, mince garlic and herbs. Combine feta with roasted red peppers and sun-dried tomatoes.
Make a small incision in the middle of the thickest part of the chicken breast. Carefully, continue working your knife into the chicken breast, creating a pocket.
Stuff each chicken breast with the feta mixture. Season all sides of the chicken breasts with salt and pepper and set aside.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat. Once the oil is nice and hot, add chicken breasts into the pan and cook for approximately 5 minutes until golden brown.
Turn the heat down to medium-low, turn the chicken breasts to the other side and cook for an additional 5 minutes or so, until fully cooked through.
Set chicken aside to rest for 2-3 minutes, then serve with spinach, cashews, cherry tomatoes and avocado.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, flaxseed oil,  lemon juice, and cumin. Season dressing with salt and pepper.
In a large serving bowl, combine avocados, tomatoes, basil and feta cheese. Gently toss with dressing and serve immediately.
Spread a couple tablespoons of sauce on each pita, leaving a small crust around the edge. Top with tomatoes, onion, basil and feta.
Place the pitas one at a time directly on the rack of a toaster oven. Toast for 3-4 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In an oven-safe skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and diced onions to the skillet. Sauté until softened.
Add chopped spinach and Swiss chard to the skillet. Cook until wilted.
Stir in diced tomatoes and ground cumin. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Make two small wells in the vegetable mixture and crack an egg into each well.
Sprinkle crumbled feta cheese over the entire skillet.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 8-10 minutes or until the egg whites are set but the yolks are still runny.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove from the oven and serve your green shakshuka immediately.
Heat a saucepan on medium heat until the olive oil is shimmering. Add the chopped onion and garlic, saute for 3-4 minutes.
Once the onion is tender but still white, pour the veggie broth in. Add pumpkin and potatoes. Cook for 20-30 minutes.
Use a stick blender to puree the soup. Season to taste using salt and pepper, serve hot, topped with crumbled feta cheese.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
In a baking dish or oven-safe skillet, combine the cherry tomatoes, feta cheese cubes, olive oil, dried oregano, red pepper flakes (if using), salt, and pepper. Toss until well coated.
Place the baking dish in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the tomatoes are softened and the feta cheese is golden and creamy.
While the tomatoes and feta are baking, cook the orecchiette pasta according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Once the tomatoes and feta are done baking, remove them from the oven and stir until well combined.
Add the cooked orecchiette pasta to the baking dish with the tomato-feta mixture and toss until the pasta is coated.
Garnish with chopped fresh basil before serving.
white wine: Turn on the pressure cooker and set it to brown mode and add the olive oil, garlic, and onion. Cook the onion until is translucent.
Add the pumpkin and cook until the pumpkin and until it starts to brown.
Add the rice and stir to coat with the oil and cook for a few minutes.
Add the white wine and cook, stirring constantly until the wine is absorbed.
Add the vegetable stock and stir well before switching your pressure cooker into pressure cooker mode and use the Low-Pressure setting for 5 minutes.
When the time is up open the lid and add the baby spinach and parmesan and stir to combine.
Clean the mushrooms and slice them lengthwise. Boil the pasta in salted water according to package instructions
Add the olive oil to a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the mushrooms and cook until they begin to shrink and the moisture evaporates about 10 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour in the wine, stir, and let it cook for about 5 minutes. Add the tagliatelle, and 1/2 cup of the pasta water. Toss everything to coat.
Add the parsley and parmesan, then stir until everything is mixed well. Serve topped with grated parmesan cheese and garnish with parsley.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Make an envelope out of foil by folding a large rectangle in half, then securing each side by folding over twice.
Repeat to make two envelopes.
Fill each envelope with a fennel bulb, cut in half, 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp white wine, a little lemon zest and seasoning.
Fold over the top edges then bake in the oven for around 20 minutes, until the fennel is completely tender.
Meanwhile heat a little olive oil in a pan and sear the cod on all sides.
Transfer to a baking sheet, season and roast in the oven for 7-10 minutes until the cod flakes and is just cooked through.
Add the beans to a pan of boiling salted water and cook briefly for 3-4 minutes until just tender, but retaining some crunch.
Drain and refresh under cold water.
Serve the cod alongside the fennel and beans, pouring over the juices from the baked fennel parcels.
Sprinkle with a little lemon juice and chopped chives.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper on both sides.
Place a sage leaf (or sprinkle dried sage) on each side of the chicken breast.
Wrap each slice of prosciutto around the chicken breast, securing the sage leaves in place.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.

Add the wrapped chicken breast to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until the chicken is fully cooked and slightly caramelized. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, pour in the dry white wine and chicken broth. Stir, scraping the bottom of the skillet to release any browned bits.
Let the sauce simmer for about 3-4 minutes or until it reduces by half.
Return the cooked chicken to the skillet and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes to heat the chicken through.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente, drain and reserve.
Meanwhile, in a large skillet, heat 1 to 2 tablespoons of oil and add broccoli and salt and pepper to taste. Saute for 5 to 7 minutes.
Remove from skillet and toss with pasta, transfer and keep warm.
In the same skillet, heat 2 more tablespoons of oil and add shrimp and 2 cloves of sliced garlic. Cook for 1 to 2 minutes. Add the mixture to fetuccinne mix.
In the same skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil. Add 2 more cloves of sliced garlic and saute until golden.
Add soup and white wine to skillet. Stir over medium heat for 8 minutes or until warm. Pour over fettuccine and broccoli and add ground black pepper to taste, serve.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Saute onion and garlic in the oil until they start to become tender.
Add the mushrooms, and fry until they begin to shrink, about 3 minutes. Toss in the spinach and chickpeas, and fry, stirring constantly for a few minutes.
Add the vinegar, stirring constantly until absorbed, then stir in the white wine. Reduce heat, and simmer until the wine has almost completely absorbed.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, and sprinkle with fresh parsley. Serve hot.
Heat olive oil and butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add garlic and bell pepper and saute for 1 minute.
Add white wine, reduce 3 minutes. Stir in shrimp and Cajun seasoning. Cook 5 minutes.
Add lemon juice and cream. Stir and cook additional minute.
Add salt and pepper to taste, as well as parsley.
Add olive oil to a large skillet on a low-medium heat then add the chopped shallots. Saute the shallots until translucent but not browned.
Add the rice and stir to coat the grains in the oils then add the wine. Simmer for 2 minutes then add 1 ladleful of chicken stock.
Let the rice absorb the chicken stock whilst constantly stirring when it’s almost absorbed all of it add another ladle of stock, continue with the same process.
When you only have only 2 ladlefuls of stock left add the saffron then continue until all the stock is gone.
Season with salt and add the parmesan, and serve.
Add olive oil to a large pot over medium heat. Stir in diced onion and minced garlic. Cook until the onion starts to soften, about 3 minutes.
Next, stir in Arborio rice and toss to coat the rice in oil. Allow the rice to toast for 1 minute.
Stir in miso paste, fresh thyme leaves, ground sage, pumpkin, ground nutmeg, and pepper.
Add white wine and stir continuously until the wine is mostly absorbed into the rice.
Once most of the liquid is absorbed, begin adding in the vegetable broth in ½ cup increments.
Stir continuously until most of the liquid is absorbed before adding in the next ½ cup of broth. Cook for 20 minutes.
Then stir in canned pumpkin puree, almonds, fresh spinach and fresh lemon juice. Stir until the spinach wilts down.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the pasta according to the instructions on the package. Reserve some pasta water before draining.

Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a large pan and fry the chicken for 6 minutes on medium-high heat until golden and slightly crispy. Remove the chicken bits from the pan with a slotted spoon and set aside.

Turn the heat down to medium and melt the butter in the same pan. When the butter has melted, add the mushrooms to the pan and saute them for 4 minutes. Add garlic and salt and cook for one minute until fragrant. Turn the heat up, add the wine, and cook for 2 minutes until the alcohol evaporates.
Lower the heat and stir in the single cream. Cook for 2 minutes until the sauce thickens a bit, then add the cream cheese and stir until combined. Add the chicken bits back to the pan.
Transfer the pasta to the pan and toss it well with the sauce. If the sauce looks too thick, add a bit of pasta water. Season to taste, and serve while warm.
Heat oil and half the butter in a large, deep fry-pan on medium-low heat. Cook onion and garlic for 5 minutes until soft.
Turn heat to medium-high, add rice and stir to coat. Add wine and stir for 1 minute.
Add stock a ladleful at a time, stirring and allowing each to absorb, until rice is cooked.
Add asparagus with final ladleful of stock. Remove from heat, stir in rind of lemon and garnish with grated Parmesan.
Heat the oil in a large, frying pan and gently fry the shallots and garlic for 3 minutes until starting to soften.
Add the mushrooms to the pan. Turn up the heat and cook for 5 minutes until they have released their juices. Season well with salt and black pepper.

Add the gnocchi and then the white wine, and let it bubble for a minute, then pour over the vegetable stock, turn down to a medium simmer, cover with the lid and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Roughly chop the spinach, then stir it in, followed by the soy cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary, then serve immediately.
Clean and wash the fish and seafood. white fish should be cut into a one-inch piece.
Heat olive oil, add garlic, basil, parsley and saffron.
Cook for 30 seconds, (saffron is not traditional)
When you can smell the garlic, add wine and let cook for 2-3 minutes
Add diced tomatoes, salt and pepper.
Let cook for 10 min.
At this point, you can stop if you are making for later or next day.
In boiling tomato stew add fish and shrimps first, cover.
Let cook 3-4 min, do not stir just shake the pot.
Add calamari, baby scallops, clams and mussels (I had clams and mussels precooked, or you will start Brudet with clams first), cover
Let cook everything for next 2-3 minutes from time when start boiling again.
Take from stove and sprinkle with parsley.
Traditionally is serve with polenta or nice rustic bread.
In a medium size saucepan, bring the vegetable broth almost to a boil over high heat. Once it comes to this point, turn the heat down to the low setting.
Add the butter to a large saucepan over medium-low heat. Add the red bell pepper, peas, shallots, and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally for 2 minutes.
Add the rice to the pan and stir it so that all of the grains become coated with butter. Let it cook for 1 minute.
Add the wine to the pan and stir it in completely. Cook, stirring until it has been entirely absorbed by the rice.
Once the first addition of broth is absorbed, add another half cup of broth and stir until that has been absorbed as well. Keep repeating the process.
When you have used half of the broth, add the corn to the pan. Once the risotto has absorbed all of the broth is ready to serve.
Combine the ingredients for the teriyaki marinade in small bowl and whisk together.
Cut the tempeh into triangles or dice — it really doesn’t matter; the triangles look cool but this will taste amazing either way.
Heat the teriyaki marinade in a stir-fry pan or medium skillet.
When it begins to bubble, add the tempeh and stir quickly to coat all sides.
Turn the heat up to medium-high and cook, stirring often, until the marinade is completely reduced and coats the tempeh nicely, about 8 minutes.
Continue to cook until the tempeh begins to brown here and there, another minute or two.
Drizzle in some sriracha if you’d like a spicier effect; otherwise just sprinkle in the sesame seeds.
Serve on its own as a protein-packed side dish, arrange on a rice bowl with a vegetable or two; or toss into a stir-fry at the last minute.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente, drain. In another pot boil broccoli for 5 to 8 minutes.
In a medium saucepan, melt ghee over medium low heat, add wine, cheese, garlic, parslet, salt and pepper to taste. Simmer over low heat for 3 to 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Increase heat to medium high and add shrimp to saucepan, cook for about 3 to 4 minutes or until shrimp begins to turn pink.
Divide pasta into portions, add broccoli and spoon sauce on top, garnish with Parmesan cheese and fresh parsley, if desired.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add diced onions and cook for about 2-3 minutes until they become translucent.
Add diced mushrooms to the skillet and sauté for another 2-3 minutes until they soften.
Stir in Arborio rice and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the rice becomes slightly translucent around the edges.
If using white wine, pour it into the skillet and stir until it's mostly absorbed by the rice.
Begin adding the chicken or vegetable broth, one ladleful at a time, stirring frequently and allowing each addition to be mostly absorbed by the rice before adding more.
Continue this process for about 18-20 minutes or until the rice is creamy and cooked to your preferred level of doneness. You may not need all the broth.
Stir in the grated Parmesan cheese until it's fully melted and incorporated into the risotto.
Season the mushroom risotto with salt and black pepper to taste.
Place wine in a large heavy-based saucepan over high heat. Bring to the boil. Add mussels. Cover and cook for 5 minutes.
Remove mussel meat from the shell. Discard shells. Place mussel meat in a large glass bowl. Add parsley and paprika. Toss to combine.
Cook beans in a large saucepan of boiling salted water for 2 minutes, or until just tender. Drain, refresh under cold water.
Add to mussels, toss to combine. Whisk reserved cooking liquid, oil, juice in a small bowl.
Place in serving plates.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper on both sides.
Lay the sage leaves on top of the chicken breast.
Place a slice of prosciutto over each sage leaf.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Carefully add the chicken breast to the skillet with the prosciutto side facing down.
Cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until the chicken is cooked through and the prosciutto is crispy.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, pour in the dry white wine and low-sodium chicken broth.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and let it cook for a few minutes to reduce and thicken the sauce.
Return the chicken to the skillet briefly to heat it through and coat it with the sauce.
Season chicken. In large skillet, cook the chicken on a grill, 5 minutes per side.
Transfer to platter. Repeat with remaining chicken and oil. Add wine, tomatoes to skillet.
Simmer until tomatoes burst and sauce thickens slightly, 5 minutes. Off heat, swirl in butter. Season sauce and spoon over chicken.
pomegranate seeds: In a bowl, combine the chopped kale, thinly sliced apple, crumbled feta cheese, pecan halves, and pomegranate seeds.
In a small bowl or jar, whisk together the dressing ingredients: olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, salt, and black pepper to taste.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad gently to ensure that all the ingredients are coated with the dressing.
Pulse cauliflower florets in a food processor until chopped into rice-sized pieces, about 10 seconds.
Transfer cauliflower 'rice' to a large bowl; add lemon juice, olive oil, and garlic.
Add tomatoes, scallions, mint, parsley, salt, pomegranate seeds and pepper and mix well.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.

Place the poblano pepper directly on the oven rack and roast it for about 20-25 minutes, turning it occasionally until the skin is blistered and charred on all sides. This can also be done over an open flame or using a broiler.
Remove the roasted poblano pepper from the oven and immediately place it in a plastic bag. Seal the bag and let it sit for about 10 minutes. This will steam the pepper, making it easier to peel.
After 10 minutes, remove the pepper from the bag and peel off the charred skin. Cut a slit down one side of the pepper and carefully remove the seeds and membranes, leaving the pepper whole.
In a skillet over medium-high heat, cook the ground beef or turkey until browned and cooked through. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a separate skillet, sauté the diced onion until it becomes translucent.
Add the diced tomato, diced apple, and cooked ground meat to the skillet with the sautéed onion. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a mixing bowl, combine the Greek yogurt and chopped walnuts. Mix well.
Stuff the roasted poblano pepper with the meat and apple mixture.
Spoon the Greek yogurt and walnut mixture over the top of the stuffed poblano pepper.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and pomegranate seeds.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Toss the chicken breasts in oil, salt and ground black pepper. Bake in the oven for 20 to 25 minutes. Cool lightly and cut into strips.
On serving plates, arrange equal amounts of the lettuce, oranges, pomegranate seeds and pecans. Top with equal ammount of chicken and 1 tbsp of balsamic vinegar.
In a salad bowl, combine the fresh arugula, thinly sliced red onion, feta cheese, and pomegranate seeds.
In a small bowl, whisk together the extra-virgin olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad gently to combine the ingredients and evenly coat them with the dressing.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook the fettuccine according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a pan, saute the onion, hemp seeds and garlic in olive oil until they are translucent. Add the broccoli and pumpkin and cook for about 5 minutes.
Add pasta to the skillet with the vegetables, tossing to coat. Sprinkle with pomegranate seeds and serve immediately.
Rinse eggplants, cut lengthwise, and season with salt. Set aside.
Peel garlic and squeeze into a small bowl, mix with oil. Pat dry eggplants and brush with some garlic oil.
Grill on the hot grill for 10 minutes, turning often and brushing with remaining oil.
Arrange on plates and top each with feta, flax seeds, pomegranate seeds, pine nuts and cilantro. Season with salt and serve.
Bring water and salt to a boil. Whisk in grains and return to a boil. Reduce heat to low. Simmer, stirring often, for 30 minutes.
Top with milk, pomegranate seeds, pepitas, orange, and maple syrup. Serve immediately.
For the walnut paste, put walnuts into a food processor, along with parmesan, garlic, lemon and process with olive oil to make a smooth paste.  Season with salt and pepper.

Combine spinach and water in a large Dutch oven. Cover and cook over medium, turning spinach occasionally with tongs, for 9 minutes. Drain spinach in a colander set over a bowl, and let stand, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes.
orking in small batches, squeeze spinach with your hands to remove excess liquid until spinach is barely moist. Reserve 1 tablespoon liquid. Finely chop spinach.
Stir together walnut paste, vinegar, cilantro, parsley, and ground marigold until blended. If sauce is very thick, stir in reserved liquid.

Stir together chopped spinach and walnut sauce until thoroughly blended. Season with salt and pepper. Shape mixture into balls, and decorate each with some pomegranate seeds. Serve chilled or at room temperature.
In a bowl or jar whisk together white wine vinegar, apple cider vinegar, and olive oil.
Add the shallot and keep mixing until well blended.
Add spinach, broccoli, pomegranate seeds, blue cheese, pear, and cranberries to a salad bowl.
Drizzle dressing over salad.
Serve immediately after adding dressing.
Heat olive oil and butter in your casserole until butter starts foaming. Add the onions, cover the pot and cook over very low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.

Stir in the garlic, cinnamon, cumin, turmeric, nutmeg and salt, cooking for 1 minute. Add the lamb, stirring to coat in the aromatic spices. If the pan gets dry, add a little of the stock and scrape any browned bits in the pan. Add in the pomegranate seeds.
Pour in the stock and bring to a simmer, leave for 30 minutes. Taste and season with salt and pepper.
Heat ghee in a non stick pan. Add potatoes and fry until golden brown. Drain in a bowl.
Add salt, red chili powder, roasted cumin powder, chaat masala and lemon juice in the potatoes and toss well. Transfer the potatoes in the saving bowl.
Top with coriander chutney, tamarind chutney, curd, pomegranate seeds and finally with fresh coriander.
Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 390°F.
Wash, peel and cut all the vegetables into bite-sized chunks, add them to a baking tray.
Wash the outside and inside of the chicken and pat it dry with paper towel.
Add the rosemary, marjoram, turmeric, cayenne, thyme and basil to the olive oil and mix well. Season with salt and pepper to taste, then rub the mixture on the chicken and under its skin.
Put chicken breast side up on the vegetables, cut the lemon in half, and place one half in the chicken's cavity.
Slice the other half of the lemon into circles and add them to the vegetables, place the tray in the oven.
Roast the chicken and veg for 20 minutes, then baste it with the juice from the pan.
Reduce the heat of the oven to 350°F, add butter slice on top. Roast the chicken for another 90 minutes or until it is ready.
Let it rest at least 10-15 minutes before serving, serve with a handful of pomegranate seeds.
Sprinkle the gelatin over 2 tablespoons of water in a small dish and let stand a few minutes to soften and dissolve.
Heat the cream, honey and salt in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring all the time. Heat until very warm, but not boiling. Stir in the gelatin until it dissolves.
Remove from the heat and add the yogurt. Stir until smoothly incorporated into the cream.
Pour the cream into the molds, dividing evenly. Cover with plastic wrap and place in the refrigerator to chill and set, at least 1 hour.
About an hour before serving, bring the pomegranate juice and honey to a boil in a small saucepan. Cook until the syrup reduces to the consistency of maple syrup, 10 minutes.
Serve the panna cottas in their cups. Drizzle some pomegranate syrup over the panna cottas and sprinkle with pomegranate seeds.
Preheat grill for medium-low heat and lightly oil the grate.
Brush zucchini with olive oil. Sprinkle garlic powder, Italian seasoning, and salt over zucchini.
Cook on grill until beginning to brown, 3 minutes per side. Brush balsamic vinegar over the zucchini and continue cooking 1 minute more.
Serve immediately, drizzled with flaxseed oil and sprinkled with sesame seeds and pomegranate.
Combine chia seeds, milk, vanilla essence. Stir well until combined. Leave for 5 minutes.
Stir again to avoid any chia-lumps. Cover and leave in fridge for 2 hours.
Serve topped with blueberries, strawberries and pomegranate seeds.
Blanch the walnuts for 5 minutes, then remove the skins. Place in milk and let soak.
Meanwhile, place the chiles directly over the flame of a gas stove, or place under a hot broiler, to char the outside tough skin. Turn the chiles to char them on all sides.
Place the blackened chiles in a bowl, cover with a plate or damp clean towel, and let sit for 5 minutes. Discard the skin.
Make a slit in the side of each chile and carefully remove the seeds and veins. Rinse the chiles and pat them dry.
Drain the walnuts. Place the soaked and drained walnuts, the cheese, sour cream and cinnamon into a blender and purée until completely smooth.
Heat one tablespoon of the oil in a large wide saucepan on medium high heat. Brown the meat on at least one side, sprinkling the meat with salt as it cooks.
Remove meat to a bowl and set aside. Add the remaining oil to the pan and heat. Add the onion and cook until soft. Add pepper and garlic and cook 1 more minute.
Melt butter in the pan and return the ground meat to the pan, using use wooden spoon to break up any clumps. Add the crushed tomatoes and apple.
Stuff the chiles with the picadillo until they are well filled out. Place them on plates. To serve, cover the stuffed chiles with sauce and top with pomegranate seeds.

Whisk vinegar, lemon juice, cumin, protein powder, salt and pepper in a large bowl. Slowly whisk in oil until well combined. Add cabbage and carrots and toss to coat. Serve topped with cilantro and pomegranate seeds.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Slice off about ½ inch from the top of the eggplant. Lay it on the side and position the knife about ⅕ inch from the edge. Cut into long straight ⅕ inch slices.
Remove the bitterness from the eggplants by placing them flat and sprinkling them with salt. Let them rest and sweat for 5 minutes. Dry with kitchen towel.
Place dried eggplants on a baking tray with baking paper. Sprinkle them with oil and bake in the oven for 6 minutes, then turn them around and bake for 5 more.
In a  food processor, place the garlic cloves and protein powder with spices and bash into a paste. Then add walnuts and process again.
Transfer the walnut paste into a bowl, add hot water and gently mix until it creates a creamy filling. Next, mix it with chopped red chilis and coriander leaves.
Place a teaspoon of walnut filling on the end of the eggplant slice and spread it towards the front. Roll the eggplant slice into a nice roll.
Place an additional small amount of filling on top of the roll. Repeat with the rest of the baked eggplant slices. Serve immediately, topped with pomegranate seeds.
chicken stock: Prepare the sauce by adding the coconut sugar, vinegar, coconut aminos, sesame, pineapple juice, and chicken stock to a medium saucepan. Stir and bring to a boil.
Add chicken pieces and beaten egg to a large ziplock bag. Seal and shake to coat chicken.
Then add the arrowroot starch to the bag, shaking again to lightly coat all the chicken pieces.
Add oil to a large non-skillet. Add the coated chicken. Fry over medium heat, a couple of minutes on each side until the coating begins to crisp.
Add the sauce to the chicken. Cover and reduce the heat down to a simmer and allow the juices to soak into the chicken.
Cook pasta in a pot of water according to package instructions until al dente. In the last minute of cooking add broccoli.
Meanwhile, in a large pan over medium heat add olive oil and saute with garlic for 2 minutes.
Add chicken stock, 1 teaspoon salt, flaxseed oil, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper, and bring to a simmer.
Toss in prawn and saute until pink, about 3 minutes. Transfer prawn to a bowl and set aside.
Stir in cream, simmer on low for 5 minutes.
Toss in pasta until well combined, add back prawns and broccoli. Stir until warmed through.
Melt ghee in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, cook and stir until softened and browned, 10 to 15 minutes.
Stir chicken broth, squash, curry powder, salt, cumin, and cayenne pepper into the onion mixture and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer until squash is tender, about 15 minutes. Remove from the heat. Stir cream into the squash mixture.
Fill blender halfway with the squash mixture. Cover and hold the lid down with a potholder, pulse a few times before leaving on to blend.
Pour into a pot. Repeat with remaining squash mixture. Ladle soup into serving bowls.
Heat a large heavy-based saucepan over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken, stirring occasionally, for 3-4 minutes or until golden.
Add the vegetables to the pan and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes. Add the rice and stock and bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low.
Cover and cook for 12-15 mins or until the stock is absorbed and the rice is tender yet firm to the bite.
Cover and cook for 12-15 mins or until the stock is absorbed and the rice is tender yet firm to the bite. Set aside for 2 minutes to rest.
Season the fish fillets with salt and pepper. In a large nonstick skillet, heat 2 tbsp of the olive oil.
Add the fish, and cook over high heat until golden on the bottom, about 5 minutes.
Flip the fish and cook until golden and cooked through, about 5 minutes longer. Transfer to plate, cover and keep warm.
Add the remaining 2 tbsp of olive oil to the skillet along with the olives, caper berries, and garlic. Cook over moderate heat until fragrant.
Add stock and parsley and cook about 10 seconds. Spoon half of the sauce over the fish and pass the rest at the table.
Process cauliflower in batches by pulsing in a food processor until it has the consistency of rice.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Saute red bell pepper in hot oil until hot, about 2 minutes.
Add shallots and garlic and saute until soft, about 2 minutes
Stir cauliflower with the vegetables in the skillet; cook and stir until the cauliflower is hot, 3 to 5 minutes.
Pour chicken broth over the cauliflower mixture, season with salt and thyme. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for about 5 minutes.
Stir lemon zest and lemon juice into the mixture, continue cooking until the lemon flavor penetrates the cauliflower, about 2 minutes more.
Remove skillet from heat, add olives and parsley, and toss to mix.
Bring stock and tamari to a simmer in a large saucepan. Add chicken, reduce heat. Gently poach for 8-10 minutes until just cooked.
Remove chicken to a cutting board, allow to cool slightly. Cut into thin shreds.
Meanwhile, place noodles in a bowl, cover with boiling water. Soak for 8-10 minutes until tender.
Separate strands with a fork; drain. Rinse in cold water.
Add noodles and chicken to stock; simmer for 1 minute. Add cabbage and snow peas; simmer 30 seconds more.
Using tongs, transfer noodles and vegetables to serving bowls, forming a nest. Top with chicken. Ladle hot stock over. Sprinkle with onion and serve.
Add peas, cream and stock and bring to the boil, stirring. Simmer for 5 minutes. Season to taste,
Add chicken, spinach and parmesan, stirring to combine. Cook for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook fusilli in a large saucepan of boiling water according to pack instructions. Drain well. Return to pan. Toss sauce with pasta and serve.
In a large pot over medium heat, heat oil. Add carrots, celery, onion, and leek and cook until soft, 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, 1 minute more.
Add broth, water, thyme, rosemary, bay leaf, oregano, and pinch red pepper flakes. Bring to a boil, then add the turkey meat. Cook for 12-15 minutes, or until the meat is cooked through.
Take the turkey out, shred it with two forks and return it to the pot. Add the egg noodles. Cover and cook until pasta is cooked through, 7 minutes.
Discard thyme, rosemary, and bay leaf, and serve.
Add the coconut cream to a saucepan and heat up to near boiling, stirring frequently. Mix in the curry paste and stir, combining the paste with the milk.
Add the sliced chicken and chicken stock and cook for 15 minutes. Toss in the vegetables and cook for 5 more minutes.
Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to the boil. Add the rice and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool.
Heat 1 tablespoon of the coconut oil in a large skillet. Add the onion and saute for 2 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add chicken breasts and cook until brown on both sides, about 4 minutes per side. Add carrots, potatoes and cherry tomatoes and cook until lightly browned.
Stir in curry powder. Cook for 1 minute or until fragrant. Whisk in coconut milk and chicken stock. Gently simmer for 20 minutes or until potatoes and carrots are cooked through.
Remove from heat, serve with rice and garnish with cilantro.
Drizzle a little oil into a large, high-sided frying pan set over medium heat for 2 minutes.
When the pan is hot, add the chicken and garlic, and fry for 10 mins, stirring often so it doesn’t burn.
Pour in 900ml boiling water, then add the stock cube and pasta along with some seasoning. Bring to the boil, then cover and simmer for 8 mins.
Stir in the broccoli for the last 4 mins of cooking time, and replace the lid.
After 4 mins, remove the lid and reduce the heat to low, then stir in the crème fraîche.
Remove the pan from the heat and stir through the lemon juice, parmesan, and mint leaves.
Serve with some extra parmesan, if you like.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in a heavy based skillet or pot over medium high heat.
Add curry paste (and garlic, ginger and lemongrass extras, if using) and cook for 2 to 3 minutes until it mostly "dries out"
Add chicken broth and coconut milk, mix to dissolve paste. Curry in a jar seasonings, Add 1 tsp fish sauce.  Mix then bring to simmer.
Add chicken, stir then lower heat to medium so it's bubbling gently. Cook 7 minutes.
Add eggplants, cook 5 minutes until soft. Taste sauce.
Add snow peas, cook 2 minutes until a bit softened, then stir through basil and lime juice.
Sauce should have reduced but will still be a be on the thin side, not thick - that's how it's should be. DO NOT keep simmering - sauce will darken.
Serve curry over jasmine rice or roti bread with garnishes of choice.
Add rice and water to a pot and cook according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a medium bowl, whisk together soy sauce, chicken stock, honey, vinegar, garlic, sesame oil, cornstarch, sriracha, ginger, red pepper flakes and 1/4 cup water, set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat. Add steak and cook, flipping once, until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in broccoli and soy sauce mixture until tender and slightly thickened, about 3-4 minutes. Serve immediately.
Measure out ½ cup of the stock and mix with the cornstarch until dissolved.
Place the remaining chicken stock, ginger, soy sauce, green onions, mushrooms and white pepper in a pot and bring to a boil.
Stir in the corn starch slurry and reduce heat to a simmer.
Slowly pour in the beaten eggs while stirring the soup while stirring slowly.
The egg will spread out into ribbons.
Turn off the heat and garnish with a few more chopped green onions.
Serve immediately with bread.
Turn on a multi-functional pressure cooker and select Saute function. Add butter and melt. tir in garlic and saute until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Stir in orzo and 2 cups chicken stock, mix well. Cancel Saute function. Close and lock the lid.
Select high pressure according to manufacturer's instructions, set timer for 6 minutes. Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build.
Release pressure carefully using the quick-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, about 5 minutes.
Unlock and remove the lid. Add mushrooms and switch to Saute mode. Stir in Parmesan cheese, peas, garlic salt, and rosemary.
Stir in remaining 1/2 cup chicken broth 1 tablespoon at a time until the orzo takes on a creamy texture, about 3 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instrictions on the packaging.
Add oil to a large skillet. Add garlic and cook for one minute. Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Add chicken breast and cook 5-6 minutes on each side until fully cooked.Pour in the wine and allow to cook for one minute.
Add in the chicken stock and simmer until slightly reduced. Stir in lemon juice, lemon zest, and parsley.
Serve immediately with extra lemon slices and parsley for garnish. Enjoy it with rice.
Bring chicken stock and lentils to a boil in a large saucepan over high heat. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Stir in onion and garlic; cook until onion has softened and turned translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir onion mixture into lentils; season with cumin and cayenne. Continue simmering until lentils are tender, about 10 minutes.
Purée soup in a standing blender or with a stick blender until smooth. Stir in lemon juice and cilantro.
Boil the broth.
Pour the chicken broth in a large stockpot and place it over medium high heat.
Remove the tops of the guajillo peppers, then slice them in half and remove the seeds.
Add the garlic cloves, onion, guajillo peppers, oregano, cumin, and salt to the chicken broth.
Boil the mixture for 10-15 minutes until the guajillo peppers are soft. Blend the pozole broth.
Use a slotted spoon to remove the garlic cloves, onion, guajillo peppers, and 1 cup of chicken broth and place it in a blender.
Blend the mixture until it is pureed. Add the purè to the chicken broth and stir to combine.
Add the hominy and shredded turkey meat to the broth, and continue to cook for 2-5 minutes until it is heated through.
Prepare the toppings. Slice, dice, and arrange the toppings in separate bowls.
Cook the fresh noodles per the package instructions until al dente or there is a firm bite in the middle of the noodles.
Drain and set aside.
On high heat, heat up a pot with the olive oil. Add the garlic, onion and stir fry until aromatic.
Add the shrimp, clams, squid, scallops, gochujang and chili powder. Stir to combine well.
Add the broth, follow by the napa baggage and bok choy. Bring the soup to a quick boil. Add soy sauce and salt to taste.
Add the noodles into the soup, cook for 30 seconds, add the scallions. Turn off the heat and serve immediately.
greek yogurt: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a lightly greased baking dish.
Season the salmon fillets with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until the salmon flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is cooking, prepare the Creamy Garlic Dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together minced garlic, Greek yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Once the salmon is done, remove it from the oven and let it cool slightly.
In a large salad bowl, arrange the mixed lettuce and tomato slices.
Place the cooked salmon fillets on top of the salad.
Squeeze lemon wedges over the salmon and salad for extra flavor.
Drizzle the Creamy Garlic Dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad gently to combine all the ingredients.
In a large salad bowl, combine the chopped Romaine lettuce, chopped cooked chicken breast, chopped hard-boiled eggs, diced tomato, crumbled blue cheese, corn, crumbled bacon, and diced avocado.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste. Drizzle with greek yogurt.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined and evenly coated with the dressing.
In a large salad bowl, place the thinly sliced kale leaves.
Add the halved cherry tomatoes and thinly sliced red onion to the kale.
Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the vegetables.
Season the salad with a pinch of salt and black pepper.

In a separate bowl, prepare the Green Goddess dressing. Combine mayonnaise, Greek yogurt, chopped parsley, chopped chives, chopped tarragon, minced garlic, lemon juice, and white wine vinegar. Mix until well combined.
Taste the dressing and adjust the seasoning with salt and black pepper. If the dressing is too thick, you can thin it with a little water until it reaches your desired consistency.
Drizzle the Green Goddess dressing over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients in the salad bowl to coat them with the dressing.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
Place the seasoned chicken breasts on a baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until they are cooked through and no longer pink in the center.
While the chicken is roasting, cook the fusilli pasta according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool.
Once the chicken is done, let it cool slightly before slicing it into thin strips.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooked fusilli pasta, chopped Romaine lettuce, tomato, grated Parmesan cheese, and sliced chicken.
Drizzle the greek yogurt over the salad.
Toss everything together until the pasta, lettuce, and chicken are evenly coated with the dressing.
Cook the farfalle pasta according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain, rinse with cold water, and set aside to cool.
In a large salad bowl, combine the cooled farfalle pasta, chopped lettuce, sliced strawberries, and diced avocado. Toss gently to mix.
Season the sliced chicken breast with salt and black pepper.
In a skillet over medium-high heat, add a little olive oil. Cook the thinly sliced chicken breast for about 4-5 minutes on each side.

While the chicken is cooking, prepare the dressing. In a blender or food processor, combine all the dressing ingredients: 1 ripe avocado, fresh basil leaves, plain Greek yogurt, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, extra-virgin olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Blend until the dressing is smooth and creamy. If it's too thick, you can add a little water or more lemon juice to achieve your desired consistency.
Once the chicken is cooked, remove it from the skillet and let it cool for a few minutes. Then, slice it into thin strips.
Drizzle the creamy avocado dressing over the salad.
Gently toss the salad to coat all the ingredients with the dressing.
Top the salad with the sliced cooked chicken.
Cut the lemon in half and squeeze the juice into a blender or mixing glass.
Add cold water to the blender.
If desired, add honey for sweetness.
Add Greek yogurt to the blender.
Blend the mixture until smooth and creamy.
If using, add ice cubes to a glass.
Pour the creamy lemonade over the ice cubes.
Serve immediately and enjoy!
Rinse the seedless red grapes thoroughly.
In a blender, combine the grapes, Greek yogurt, frozen banana, and ice cubes. Add honey.
Blend all the ingredients until smooth and well combined.
Check the consistency, and if it's too thick, add a little more liquid (yogurt or almond milk) to reach your desired thickness.
Pour the grape smoothie into a glass and enjoy it chilled!
In a bowl or jar, combine the rolled oats, grated carrot, Greek yogurt, almond milk, maple syrup or honey, cinnamon, nutmeg, and vanilla extract.
Mix all the ingredients together until well combined.
Cover the bowl or jar with a lid or plastic wrap and place it in the refrigerator.
Let the mixture sit in the fridge overnight or for at least 4-6 hours to allow the oats to soften and the flavors to meld.
Once ready to eat, give the oats a stir. If it's too thick, you can add a splash of milk to reach your desired consistency.
Wash and prepare the fruits by slicing them into small pieces or chunks.
Take an ice cube tray or small paper cups and layer the bottom with a spoonful of yogurt.
Add a layer of chopped fruits over the yogurt in each section of the tray or cups.
Sprinkle a bit of granola on top of the fruit layer.
Insert a popsicle stick or skewer into each section, making sure it stands upright.
Place the ice cube tray or cups in the freezer for a few hours until the popsicles are frozen solid.
Once frozen, remove the popsicles from the tray or cups. If using cups, you may need to tear them away.
Peel the vegetables and grate the carrots, potatoes, onions and pumpkin, using the fine blades on a cheese grater.
Toss the vegetable mixture with a pinch of salt.
Place in a strainer and allow them to strain for 30 minutes, until they release any extra juices.
In a large bowl, add the eggs, flour, coriander, cumin seeds, mint and chervil.
Mix with a fork until combined.
Finally, crumble the feta cheese and add it to the mixture.
Press down on the vegetables with your hands for 1-2 minutes, so they can release any more juices.
Add them to the egg mixture.
Mix and season with salt and pepper.
Preheat oven to 300°F fan.
Add a generous amount of olive oil to a pan.
Place over medium heat.
The oil should be about 5-10 mm deep and its temperature should reach 350°F.
