Add spoonfuls of the mixture to the pan.
Make 2-3 fritters at a time.
Fry for 1-2 minutes.
Flip them over and fry for another 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Transfer to a plate and place them in the oven to keep them warm.
Serve with yogurt.
Preheat a panini press or skillet over medium heat.
In a bowl, mix together canned tuna, Greek yogurt, red onion, celery, and pickles. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Place one slice of bread on a clean surface. Top with the tuna mixture, followed by the slice of tomato and cheddar cheese. Place the second slice of bread on top.
Lightly coat the outside of the sandwich with cooking spray or olive oil.
Place the sandwich in the preheated panini press or skillet. Cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is melted.
Serve hot.
Preheat oven to 400°F.

Cook cauliflower in boiling, salted water for 10 minutes.  
Drain and allow to cool slightly.
In a medium bowl beat eggs well. Add yogurt, milk, and oregano. Mix well.

Arrange cauliflower in a lightly oiled baking dish.
Pour egg mixture over the top and sprinkle with cheddar.
Bake for 20 minutes, and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, combine the plain Greek yogurt, ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, paprika, chili powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the bite-sized chicken pieces to the yogurt and spice mixture. Toss to coat the chicken evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the marinated chicken pieces to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are browned and cooked through.
Once the chicken is cooked, reduce the heat to low.
Add the tomato sauce and milk to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Let the mixture simmer for about 5 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken and the flavors to meld.
Toast the bread slice to your liking.
Spread Greek yogurt evenly over the toasted bread.
Sprinkle fresh blueberries on top of the yogurt.
Zest the lemon directly over the blueberries and yogurt.
Drizzle honey over the lemon-blueberry yogurt toast.
Season the boneless, skinless chicken breast with salt and black pepper to taste.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and lightly grease it with cooking spray or a small amount of oil.
Place the seasoned chicken breast in the skillet and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until it's cooked through and no longer pink in the center.
Remove the cooked chicken breast from the skillet and let it cool slightly. Once cooled, slice it into thin strips.
Lay out the large whole-wheat tortilla on a clean surface.
Spread the hummus evenly over the tortilla, leaving a small border around the edges.
Place the sliced avocado, cooked turkey bacon, shredded lettuce, tomato slices, cucumber slices, and sliced chicken breast in the center of the tortilla.
Drizzle the yogurt over the ingredients.
Fold in the sides of the tortilla and then roll it up tightly, like a burrito.
Cut the California Club Chicken Wrap in half diagonally if desired.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, combine the minced garlic, chopped parsley, whole wheat breadcrumbs, grated Parmesan cheese, paprika, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Mix well.
Place your chicken breast between two sheets of plastic wrap or parchment paper. Use a meat mallet or rolling pin to gently pound the chicken until it's even in thickness.
Season the chicken breast with a little salt and pepper.
Roll the chicken breast in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing down gently to make sure the crumbs adhere.
Heat the olive oil in an ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the chicken breast and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for about 15-20 minutes or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
While the chicken is baking, prepare your mashed potatoes. Place the cubed potato in a pot of cold water. Bring it to a boil and cook until the potatoes are fork-tender. Drain.
Mash the cooked potatoes with a potato masher or a fork. Add 2-3 tablespoons of Greek yogurt and continue mashing until you achieve your desired consistency. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the chicken is cooked, remove it from the oven. Serve with mash.
Cook the onion on a medium heat in a big saucepan in olive oil for 5 minutes. Crush the garlic and add it to the onions and cook for 30 seconds.
Then add the diced tomatoes, yeast and the broth and let it simmer. After 20 minutes wash and cut the basil and add it to the soup.
Add the tomato paste and Greek yogurt and blend it smoothly using a hand blender. Add salt to taste and serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Season chicken breast with salt, pepper, and dried thyme.
In an oven-safe skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Place the seasoned chicken breast in the skillet and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown.
Add Brussels sprouts and minced garlic to the skillet. Sauté for 2 minutes.
Pour in chicken broth and bring to a simmer.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through and Brussels sprouts are tender.
Remove the skillet from the oven and place it back on the stovetop over medium heat.
Stir in Greek yogurt and let it simmer for 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.
Add the Greek yogurt to a bowl. Add the fresh blueberries and halved strawberries.
Add the berries and nuts and enjoy.
Wash the broccoli florets and carrot. Peel the carrot.
Use a food processor or box grater to shred the carrot. Alternatively, you can finely chop it with a knife.
In a small bowl, mix together Greek yogurt, apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard, honey, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Combine the broccoli florets and shredded carrot in a large mixing bowl.
Pour the dressing over the vegetables and toss until evenly coated.
Serve immediately or refrigerate until ready to serve.
cream of mushroom soup: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente; drain.
In a 1 1/2 quart glass casserole dish, layer noodles, 1/2 of cheese, 1/2 of the tuna, 1/2 of peas and 1/2 of the soup.
Repeat the layering with the remaining ingredients. Top the casserole with bread crumbs and drizzle with flaxseed oil.
Bake for 10 to 15 minutes.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente, drain and reserve.
Meanwhile, in a large skillet, heat 1 to 2 tablespoons of oil and add broccoli and salt and pepper to taste. Saute for 5 to 7 minutes.
Remove from skillet and toss with pasta, transfer and keep warm.
In the same skillet, heat 2 more tablespoons of oil and add shrimp and 2 cloves of sliced garlic. Cook for 1 to 2 minutes. Add the mixture to fetuccinne mix.
In the same skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of oil. Add 2 more cloves of sliced garlic and saute until golden.
Add soup and white wine to skillet. Stir over medium heat for 8 minutes or until warm. Pour over fettuccine and broccoli and add ground black pepper to taste, serve.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Mix green beans and condensed soup together in a large microwave-safe bowl until well combined. Microwave on high until warm, 3 to 5 minutes.
Stir 1/2 cup Cheddar cheese into the green bean mixture. Microwave on high for 2 to 3 minutes.
Transfer mixture to a casserole dish and spread evenly over the bottom. Sprinkle French-fried onions over top, then sprinkle with remaining Cheddar.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted and the onions are just turning brown, about 10 minutes.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook egg noodles in the boiling water, stirring occasionally about 5 minutes, drain.
Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Saute onion in hot ghee until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add ground beef to skillet in small chunks; cook and stir to break the beef into smaller pieces as it cooks until no longer pink, 5 to 7 minutes.
Stir mushrooms and garlic salt into the beef mixture. Pour mushroom soup and chicken soup over beef, stir, and cook until hot, about 5 minutes.
Add sour cream, stir until smooth, and cook until again hot, 2 to 3 minutes more. Pour beef mixture over the cooked egg noodles.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil, add pasta and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 10 minutes.
Meanwhile in a large, heavy skillet over medium heat combine mushroom soup, mushrooms, onion, garlic powder, salt, pepper, nutritional yeast, water and olive oil.
Stir until ingredients are well mixed. Reduce heat to low and simmer until thickened, about 10 minutes. Garnish with parsley.
Season pork chops with salt, pepper, and garlic powder to taste. In a large nonstick skillet, brown the chops over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and mushrooms, and saute for 1 minute. Pour cream of mushroom soup over chops. Add rosemary, cover skillet, and reduce temperature.
Simmer 20 minutes, or until chops are cooked through. Serve imemdiately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and pepper, cook in hot oil until browned, 3 to 5 minutes.
Stir onion with the chicken; continue cooking until the onion is translucent, 5 to 7 minutes.
Pour cream of mushroom soup and Marsala wine over the chicken mixture; cook until hot for 3 to 5 minutes.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente, drain.
In a large saute pan, melt ghee and saute onions and garlic until onions are almost translucent. Gradually pour in milk, stirring constantly.
Stir in mushroom soup and shrimp. Cook for 10 minutes or until shrimp are pink. Serve over pasta.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
In a large bowl combine spinach, cream cheese, mushroom soup and 1/2 of the fried onions; mix well.
Transfer mixture to a 2 quart casserole dish and sprinkle with remaining fried onions.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until heated through.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Cook and stir ground beef in a large skillet over medium heat for 7 to 10 minutes, season with salt and black pepper.
Stir cream of mushroom soup into the cooked ground beef; pour the mixture into a baking dish.
Layer tater tots evenly over the ground beef mixture; top with Cheddar cheese. Bake until tater tots are golden brown and hot, 30 minutes. Garnish with parsley.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Cook the pasta according to the instructions on the packaging or until al dente.
Season pork chops with salt and pepper to taste. Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium high heat.
Saute the pork chops in the butter for about 5 minutes per side.
In a separate medium bowl, combine the milk, water and soup. Place the pork in a baking dish and pour the soup mixture over the chops.
Add tomatoes and bake for 35 minutes. Serve with pasta. Garnish with parsley.
Preheat oven to 350°F and grease a casserole dish.
Put broccoli in a large microwave-safe bowl with a small amount of water in the bottom. Cook in microwave until tender, about 5 minutes.
Drain liquid from bowl. Stir mushroom soup, Cheddar cheese, and mayonnaise with the broccoli until evenly mixed, pour into prepared casserole dish.
Mix bread crumbs and melted butter together in a separate bowl, spread evenly over the broccoli mixture.
Bake in preheated oven until the surface is bubbling, 20 to 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
In a small bowl, combine the bread crumbs, potato, onion, egg, garlic powder, pepper and salt. Crumble beef over mixture and mix well.
Shape into 1 inch balls. Place in a shallow baking dish, coated with cooking spray. In a small bowl, combine the water, soup, mushrooms, sour cream, soup mix and pepper and pour over meatballs.
Bake for 30 minutes. Serve immediately, garnished with dill.
Place flour in a large bowl and toss beef until coated.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat, and, working in batches, brown beef.
Set aside.
Add diced shallot and cook for 2-3 minutes or until softened.
Add garlic, stirring frequently for 1 minute or until fragrant.
Mix in fire-roasted bell peppers, tomato paste, beef broth, Worcestershire, and browned beef, stirring to pick up additional flavor from the beef bits.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cover for 1 hour.
After 1 hour, stir in cream of mushroom soup and continue cooking for 30 minutes.
Enjoy!
Preheat an oven to 350°F.
Fill a large pot with lightly salted water and bring to a rolling boil over high heat. Once the water is boiling, stir in the pasta and return to a boil.
Cook the pasta uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the pasta has cooked through, but is still firm to the bite, about 5 minutes.
While the pasta is cooking, stir the tuna with flaxseed oil, soup, peas, onion, milk, salt, and pepper in a bowl until evenly mixed.
Gently stir in the cooked pasta, then pour the mixture into a baking dish. Sprinkle evenly with dry bread crumbs.
Bake in the preheated oven until the casserole is bubbling and the bread crumbs are golden brown, about 20 minutes.
almond butter: In a non-stick pan over medium heat, sauté the apple slices until softened.
Add oats, almond butter, chopped nuts, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and a dash of salt to the pan.
Stir well and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the oats are heated through.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and enjoy.
In a food processor, combine the chopped dried apricots, almond butter, and ghee.
Pulse until the mixture becomes a paste-like consistency.
Transfer the mixture to a mixing bowl.
Add the unsweetened shredded coconut, mixed seeds, unsweetened protein powder, and a pinch of salt to the bowl.
Mix everything together until well combined.
Line a small dish or container with parchment paper, leaving some excess paper hanging over the edges for easy removal.
Transfer the mixture into the lined dish and press it down firmly to create an even layer.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour to allow the bars to set.
Once set, use the parchment paper overhang to lift the bars out of the dish.
Cut the mixture into individual protein bars.
Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend on high until smooth.
Enjoy immediately or slightly chilled.
Wash the apple then core and slice. Spread slices with almond butter or serve on the side.
Add all ingredients to a high-speed blender and blend until smooth. Pour the smoothie in glasses and serve immediately.
Combine all ingredients in a high-powdered blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
Blend all the ingredients together in a high speed blender or food processor.
Scoop into a bowl and add your toppings of choice.
Peel your hard boiled egg. Gather the rest of your ingredients.
Add all of your ingredients to a high speed blender. Blend on high until fully incoporated, then serve immediately.
Blend all ingredients in a high speed blender until smooth. Serve immediately.
Mix the oats, cinnamon, almond butter, maple syrup and almond milk together the night before serving. Place in jars and refrigerate overnight.
Top with fresh berries before serving.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
Place all ingredients in a blender.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Serve immediately.
Combine bananas, pineapple, almond butter, and chia seeds in a blender, add water. Blend until smooth.
Bring water to a boil then stir in oats, cinnamon, vanilla, and pinch of salt. Cook about 5 minutes over medium heat and stir occasionally.
Mix in the almond butter, bananas and apples and remove from heat.
To serve, top with almonds and pumpkin seeds.
Place kefir in a blender. Add cherries, spinach, avocado, almond butter, ginger, olive oil and protein powder and chia seeds. Blend until smooth and pour into glass.
Wash the quinoa and add it to a pot. Cover with water and cook as per packet instructions. Drain and set aside to cool a bit. Afterwards, fluff using a fork.
Add the cooked quinoa and almond milk to a small sauce pan and stir over medium heat until heated through. Add cinnamon and mix well.
Transfer the quinoa into a bowl, top with banana slices, walnuts, blueberries and almond butter. Enjoy.
Add the berries, one banana, Greek yogurt, almond milk, and almond butter to a blender and blend until smooth.
Top with bluberries, sliced banana, chia seeds and sliced almonds.
Mix oats, almond butter, honey, flax seeds, and chia seeds together in a bowl, refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Scoop mixture into balls and place on a plate or baking sheet, store in refrigerator.
Add all ingredients to a high-speed blender and blend until smooth. Pour the smoothie in glasses and serve immediately.
chili paste: Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile heat 3 tbsp oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Stir-fry in the wok 5 minutes. Drain tofu on paper towels. Allow wok to cool, and wipe clean.
In a bowl, mix the soy sauce, orange juice, water and chili paste until smooth.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon oil in the wok, and stir-fry the broccoli until tender. Form a well in the center of the carrots, and pour in the sauce.
Bring sauce to a boil. Mix tofu into the wok, and continue cooking until coated with the sauce.
Cook the noodles according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a bowl, mix together low-sodium soy sauce, sesame oil, and Sichuan chili paste to make the sauce. Adjust the amount of chili paste to your preferred level of spiciness.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the thinly sliced pork chop with salt and pepper.
Add the pork to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until it's cooked through and slightly browned.
Remove the cooked pork from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add minced garlic and minced ginger. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped bok choy or Chinese cabbage and sliced green onions. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are slightly wilted.
Return the cooked pork to the skillet and pour the prepared sauce over the ingredients.
Toss everything together in the skillet to coat with the sauce.
Serve the spicy Sichuan pork and vegetable mixture over the cooked noodles.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat wok or large skillet over medium heat.
Add oil and heat. Add garlic and onion and stir fry until fragrant and onion is just softened.
Add shrimp and toss; add Roasted Chili Paste and toss with shrimp.
Stir fry until shrimp is cooked through. Add fish sauce and toss.
Serve with rice and lettuce leaves.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together olive oil, chili paste, honey, apple cider vinegar, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Place the chicken wings in a large mixing bowl.
Pour the chili honey mixture over the chicken wings and toss until they are evenly coated.
Place the coated chicken wings on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the wings are cooked through and crispy.
Once cooked, remove the wings from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Serve the hot honey chicken wings hot and enjoy!
Heat the oil in a large non-stick skillet or wok over medium heat. Add garlic and saute for 30 seconds. Add ground meat and cook until it's cooked through, about 4-5 minutes.
Turn heat down to medium-low. Stir in chili paste, vinegar, soy sauce, erythritol, red pepper flakes, ground ginger, black pepper and salt.
Cook for another 6-8 minutes. Spoon filling into lettuce, add additional soy sauce and red pepper flakes, enjoy.
Prepare rice noodles according to package directions, taking care they are a slightly crunchy/al dente because they will cook more in the sauce.
Rinse noodles in cold water, drain, and toss in with 1 tsp sesame oil. Set aside.
Add all of the Sauce Ingredients to a medium mixing bowl. Whisk together and set aside.
While rice noodles are soaking, heat 1 tbsp oil a deep large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
Once very hot, add chicken and let cook undisturbed for 1 minute then continue to stir fry just until cooked. Transfer to a plate. Wipe out the pan.
Heat one tbsp oil in the same skillet and heat over medium-high heat. Add bell peppers and shallots and sauté for 1 minute.
Add garlic and sauté for 1 additional minute.
Reduce heat to medium and pour beaten eggs over vegetables and cook, stirring constantly, just until scrambled.
Add sauce and noodles and increase heat to medium-high.
Toss until evenly combined and continue to stir and cook until noodles are al-dente 1-2 minutes.
Stir in chicken, bean sprouts, green onions and heat through. Taste and add additional chili sauce to taste. Top with cilantro and serve.
Melt the butter in a frying pan or wok, over medium-high heat. Add the pork strips, and stirfry for a couple of minutes, or until lightly browned.
Add the vegetables and chili paste, stirring for a couple of minutes, or until crisp-tender. Season with salt and pepper, to taste.
For serving, use a slotted spoon to distribute the stir-fry to plates, sprinkle with almonds and toss to coat.
Arrange the mixed raw vegetables and boiled egg on a plate.
In a separate bowl, combine the peanut sauce, chili paste (if using), and lime juice. Mix well.
Drizzle the peanut sauce mixture over the vegetables and egg.
Sprinkle a pinch of salt over the top, if desired.
Toss the salad gently to coat the vegetables and egg with the peanut sauce.
Serve your Gado Gado immediately.
Put salmon into a bowl and use a fork to flake and mix with the liquid from the can. Mix cucumber and capers with the salmon.
Stir mayonnaise, red wine vinegar, chile paste, and reserved liquid from the capers together in a bowl, add Sriracha hot sauce and adjust to taste.
Pour mayonnaise mixture over the salmon mixture and stir to coat. Spoon onto toasted bread to make sandwiches, add tomato and lettuce.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine olive oil, soy sauce, chili paste or sriracha sauce, rice vinegar, minced garlic, minced ginger, black pepper, crushed red pepper flakes, and salt.
Add the chicken wings to the bowl and toss until they are evenly coated with the marinade.
Arrange the chicken wings on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the wings are cooked through and crispy.
Once cooked, remove the wings from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Serve the spicy caramel chicken wings hot and enjoy!
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl combine the garlic, ginger and chile paste. Mix together and set aside.
In a second bowl mix together the orange juice, soy sauce and vinegar.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large skillet over medium high heat. Saute chicken for about 3 minutes, until browned. Remove from skillet and set aside.
Heat 1 teaspoon oil in skillet and saute bamboo shoots, broccoli, green beans and onion for about 3 minutes.
Stir in garlic/ginger mixture and saute for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Add chicken and orange juice mixture and cook, stirring, for 2 minutes
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a large skillet combine sesame oil, canola oil and chili paste, stir-fry for 30 seconds. Add garlic and stir fry an additional 30 seconds.
Add chicken and soy sauce and cook 5 minutes. Remove mixture from pan, set aside, and keep warm.
In the emptied pan combine the onion, cabbage, and carrots. Stir-fry for 2 minutes. Stir in the cooked noodles, and the chicken mixture. Serve topped with green onions.
Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper and set aside.
In large heavy bottom pot or dutch oven, saute shallot in a little oil over medium heat. Add ginger, lemongrass, and broth and simmer gently for 5 minutes.
Add the chicken and mushrooms and gently poach, simmering for 3 minutes.
Stir in the coconut milk, fish sauce, chili paste, and salt and bring to a gentle simmer. Add the lime juice and basil and mix well.
Adjust salt and lime and the spice level by adding more chili paste. Serve immediately.
Whisk together the water and yeast in a small bowl or liquid measuring cup.
Let it sit for a few minutes while you prepare your dry ingredients.
Stir the flour, sugar, salt, and baking powder together in a large mixing bowl.
Stir 1 tbsp of oil into the yeast and water mixture, then add the mixture to the dry ingredients.
Stir to form a soft dough.
Transfer the dough to a lightly floured work surface and knead it for about 7-8 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Lightly oil a medium bowl, roll the dough into a ball and place it in the bowl.
Cover with a towel and place it in a warm spot to rise until doubled in size, about 1-2 hours.
While the dough rises, mix the water, vinegar, sugar, and salt together in a medium bowl, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
Add the carrots, jalapeños, and cucumbers and allow the mixture to sit and pickle while you make the filling and finish making the buns.
To create the hoisin sauce, stir the soy sauce, vinegar, maple syrup, sesame oil, chili paste, and liquid smoke together in a separate small bowl. Set aside.
Coat the bottom of a large skillet with oil and place it over medium heat.
When the oil is hot, add the mushrooms to an even layer.
Cook the mushrooms for about 4 minutes, flip, and cook 4 minutes more until lightly browned.
Add a few teaspoons of the hoisin sauce to the skillet and flip a few times to coat the mushrooms.
Allow them to continue cooking until the sauce is thick and forms a sticky coating, about 3 minutes more.
Remove the skillet from heat and transfer the mushrooms to a plate.
Once the dough has doubled in size, divide it in half.
Remove half from the bowl and divide it into fifths.
Roll one of the fifths into a ball, then flatten it with your hand.
Use a rolling pin to roll the ball into a 4-5 inch round.
Brush the top with oil, fold it in half, and transfer it to a piece of parchment paper.
Make sure not to crowd the dough rounds.
Repeat for the remaining pieces of dough, then remove the other half of the dough from the bowl and repeat.
You should have 10 rounds.
Cover the dough rounds and let them rest for 15 minutes.
Transfer the parchment rounds to the bamboo steamer if they're not already there. They may not all fit — just work in batches if that's the case.
Layer the tiers in your steamer, place the lid on it, and place it over a large pot of water.
Bring the water to a boil and steam the buns for 8 minutes.
Very carefully remove the lid and remove the buns from the steamer.
Slather the insides of the buns with hoisin sauce, then stuff with mushrooms, pickled veggies, peanuts, scallions, and cilantro.
Serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Freeze pork tenderloin until firm around the edges, 30–45 minutes.
Combine chili paste, ginger, garlic, 2 tbsp soy sauce in a resealable plastic bag (or medium bowl if you aren’t feeling the plastic).
Thinly slice pork with a long sharp knife. Add to marinade, seal bag, and knead to thoroughly coat.
Let sit at least 10 minutes and up to 2 hours.
Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
When oil is very hot, add half of pork in a single layer; season very lightly with salt.
Cook, undisturbed, increasing heat to high if needed, until dark brown underneath, about 1 minute.
Toss pork, breaking up with tongs or a wooden spoon, and continue to cook, tossing, until cooked through, about 1 minute more.
Transfer to a plate.
Repeat with another 1 tbsp vegetable oil and remaining pork (you may want to briefly remove skillet from heat when adding more oil so it doesn’t spatter).
Wipe out skillet.
Heat remaining 1 tbsp vegetable oil in skillet over medium-high.
Add carrots and cook in a single layer, undisturbed, until beginning to soften and brown underneath, about 2 minutes.
Add collard greens and toss to wilt.
Cook, tossing occasionally, until vegetables are crisp-tender, about 2 minutes.
Combine vinegar and remaining 1 tbsp soy sauce in a small bowl.
To serve, divide rice among bowls and arrange pork and vegetables over.

Chop the onion in half. Then cut the onion into thin pieces. Also cut the spring onion into small pieces.
Cut pork into bite-sized pieces. Take out a few pieces of Kimchi and set aside for pan-frying.
Take out a large mixing bowl and put in the pork pieces. Mix in all of the listed ingredients for the marinade. Use your hands (and plastic gloves) to coat the meat thoroughly.
Take out a frying pan and put on a medium heat. If you have kimchi - cook that first. Once-pan is hot, drop in some sesame oil and cook the kimchi until it gets some browning on the tips.
Then put coconut oil in the the pan. Place in the pork pieces. Cook the pork until it is 90% cooked.
Then put in the onions. Once you see that the meat is fully cook (light sear on the meat) - turn off the heat.
Bring a large pot of lightly-salted water to the boil, add the rice and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Add the green onions in. Serve on top of rice and garnish with parsley.
Bring a medium saucepan of water to a boil. Meanwhile, heat a separate medium sauce pan over medium heat. Add the avocado and sesame oil.
When hot, add the onions, ginger and garlic and cook, stirring, until the onion is tender, about 4 minutes. Add the miso and chili paste and continue to cook, stirring, for 2 more minutes.
Add the broth and soy sauce, whisk until well combined. Increase heat and bring the broth to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat so that it is simmering
Carefully lower, using a spoon or ladle, the 2 eggs into the now boiling water, cook for exactly 7 minutes. Transfer the cooked eggs to a small bowl of ice water, do not drain the water.
Using the same boiling water, cook the noodles according to package instructions. Add shrimp and bok choy to the ramen broth mixture.
Cook, stirring, until the shrimp is cooked through and nice and pink and the bok choy is wilted and tender, about 3 minutes.
Place cooked ramen noodles in large bowl. Add the shrimp and bok choy around the noodles. Ladle the spicy miso ramen broth over all contents in the bowl.
Slice an egg in half and place on top of the soup. Sprinkle with scallions and serve.
Place onion, ginger, garlic and spinach in food processor. Pulse, stopping to stir up everything a few times.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add tofu. Cook about 10 minutes, until tofu cubes begin to brown lightly.
Sprinkle turmeric over tofu and toss a few more times to coat the tofu cubes.
Add spinach mixture, broth, lemon juice, cumin, garam masala, chili paste, and salt. Stir a few times to mix everything up.
Reduce heat to medium and allow to simmer about 10 minutes, until most of the liquid has cooked off.
Add coconut milk, and cook another minute. Mixture should be thick and creamy. Garnish with chopped parsley and sliced chili.
Combine chili paste, honey, ginger, garlic, two tablespoons soy sauce, and teaspoons sesame oil in a resealable plastic bag.
Meanwhile bring a large pot of water to the boil and cook the rice to the package instructions.
Add the pork to the marinade, seal the bag, and shake to thoroughly coat. Let sit at least 10 minutes and up to 2 hours.
Heat 1 tablespoon coconut oil oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high. Add half of the pork in a single layer, season very lightly with salt.
Cook the pork for about a minute, increasing heat to high if needed, until dark brown underneath. Repeat for the 2nd half of the marinated pork.
Wipe the skillet clean, add the remaining coconut oil and heat over medium-high heat. Add the carrots, cook until they begin to soften, about 3 minutes.
Combine vinegar and the remaining soy sauce and sesame oil in a small bowl.
Divide the rice between bowls, add pork and carrots, and enjoy!
Boil the water and add cloves, cardamom, ½ teaspoon black pepper and salt. Then add rice cook it till it is 70% cooked and drain the excess water.

Heat butter in a pan and add cumin, bay leaf and cayenne pepper. Then add ginger and garlic paste and chili paste. Saute for a min before adding the thinly sliced onions. Spring onions and fry them till they are golden brown. Then add salt, ½ teaspoon black pepper and lemon juice, mix well for 2-3 mins.

Place the first layer of cooked onions at the bottom of the pan, add a layer of rice and again the layer of cooked onions. Then seal the pan tightly with aluminium foil and place a heavy lid on top. Cook the biryani on medium heat for 5-10 mins.
Before serving, garnish with coriander.
all purpose flour: Preheat the oven to 450º F.
Place the flour, 1/4 cup of the Parmesan, the thyme leaves and the salt in the bowl of a food processor. Pulse a few times to combine.
Add the butter and pulse until a coarse meal forms. Sprinkle in 3 tablespoons of water and pulse until moist clumps form.
Transfer to a work surface and gently pull the dough together, kneading ever so slightly until the dough comes together into a ball.
Flatten into a disk and wrap in plastic wrap and chill for a least an hour and up to 1 day ahead.
Layer the cherry tomatoes into a colander and sprinkle some coarse salt in between the layers. Allow to drain for at least 45 minutes and up to an hour.
Remove the dough from the fridge and allow it to warm up slightly about 5 minutes. Transfer the parchment and dough to a baking sheet and return to the fridge.
Remove the tomatoes from the colander and drain them further by laying the tomatoes on several layers of paper towel. Well drained tomatoes make for a crisp tart.
Remove the dough from the fridge. Scatter the remaining 1 cup of finely grated Parmesan cheese over the base of the tart, leaving a 2 inch wide border.
Fold the edges of the dough over the tomatoes, pleating as you go. Every so often tilt the colander to allow the juices trapped between the layers to drain freely.
Season with a few grinds of black pepper and a drizzle of the olive oil. Bake in the preheated oven until the tart is golden about 30 minutes. Allow to cool slightly before serving.

In a large bowl, beat together the cottage cheese, eggs and salt and mix until smooth, then add the milk and stir until creamy. Add the flour and baking powder and stir just until blended. Stir in the melted butter and raspberries.

Preheat a griddle or skillet over medium heat and brush with butter or oil or spray with nonstick spray. Drop batter onto the pan and cook until bubbles begin to break the surface and the edges no longer appear wet, then flip using a thin spatula and cook until golden on the other side as well.
Melt the butter in a medium-sized saucepan over medium heat. Stir in the sliced mushrooms and cook for 7 minutes.
Stir in the milk and flour, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil and begins to thicken.
Add the cheese and stir until completely melted. Transfer to a plate, garnish with parsley and serve.
Rinse shredded potatoes until water is clear, then drain and squeeze dry.
Place shreds in a bowl, and mix in the onion, flour, and egg until evenly distributed.
Heat oil and place potatoes into the pan. Cook until nicely browned on the bottom, flip and brown on the other side.
Remove from pan, and drain on paper towels. Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Mix the flour, cheddar and baking powder together.
Whisk the eggs, and add the salt to the eggs, then add to the flour bowl. Add the melted butter to the flour bowl. Combine.
Add the milk and mix well. Stir in the crumbled feta. Stir in the chopped spinach
Spoon the mixture into your muffin tin, or into muffin cases. Throw a little extra cheddar on top of the muffins.
Bake for 20 minutes. Take out of the oven when the muffin tops are golden brown and cooked through.
Preheat air fryer to 390°F. Spray basket of the air fryer with cooking spray and place eggs into the basket.
Combine yogurt, chutney, mayonnaise, salt, pepper, curry powder, and cayenne in a small bowl. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Divide pork sausage into 6 even portions. Flatten each portion into a thin patty. Place one egg in the middle and wrap the sausage around, sealing all sides. Set aside.
Place flour into a bowl and beaten eggs into another bowl. Place breadcrumbs on a plate.
Dip each wrapped egg into flour, then into beaten egg, letting the excess drip off. Roll in bread crumbs and place onto a plate.
Cook for 12 minutes, turning eggs over halfway through. Repeat with remaining eggs. Serve immediately.
For the dip, mix sour cream with parsley to taste.
Set a large strainer over a bowl. In a food processor fitted with the shredding disk, shred the potatoes, carrot, onion and zucchini in batches.
Add each batch to the strainer. Discard any liquid in the bowl and transfer the shredded vegetables to a clean kitchen towel and squeeze out as much liquid as possible.
Add the shredded vegetables to the bowl and add the egg, protein powder and flour. Stir in some salt and pepper.
Heat a large skillet until very hot. Add half of the oil and heat until it shimmers. Scoop four ½ cup mounds of the vegetable mixture into the pan and use the bottom of the measuring cup to flatten.

Cook the cakes over moderately high heat until golden brown on the bottom, about 4 minutes. Turn the cakes, reduce the heat to medium and cook until golden brown and cooked through, about 4 minutes longer.
Cook the remaining cakes in the remaining oil. Serve immediately with the dip.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Rub the butter into the flour until you have a breadcrumb texture. Add water, a little at a time, to form a dough. Wrap in clingfilm, put in the freezer for 10 minutes.
Lightly flour a worktop and roll the pastry out so it's a little bigger than a 8-in tart tin. Prick the base all over with a fork then return the pastry to the freezer for another 10 minutes.
Blind bake the pie dough for 10-15 minutes, take out and let to cool a bit.
In the meantime mix together the rest of the ingredients in a large bowl and season to taste with salt and pepper. Once the pie dough has cooled slightly, pour the mixture onto the dough.
Place the tin back in the oven, bake for an additional 30-45 minutes or until golden brown on top. Take the quiche out, allow it to cool slightly before serving.

In a large bowl, beat together the cottage cheese, eggs and salt and mix until smooth, then add the milk and stir until creamy. Add the flour and baking powder and stir just until blended. Stir in the melted butter.

Preheat a griddle or skillet over medium heat and brush with butter or oil or spray with nonstick spray. Drop batter onto the pan and cook until bubbles begin to break the surface and the edges no longer appear wet, then flip using a thin spatula and cook until golden on the other side as well.
Serve warm, topped with jam.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Preheat an air fryer to 400°F.
Place veal on a clean work surface and sprinkle with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Place flour in a flat dish. Beat egg and parsley together in a second dish. Place bread crumbs in a third dish.
Dredge each veal cutlet first in flour, then in egg-parsley mixture, followed by bread crumbs, pressing down so that bread crumbs adhere.
Spray the basket of the air fryer with nonstick cooking spray.
Place breaded veal cutlets into the basket, making sure not to overcrowd.
Spray the tops with nonstick cooking spray. Cook for 5 minutes.
Flip, spray any chalky spots with nonstick cooking spray, and cook for 5 minutes longer. Repeat with remaining veal.
Serve with rice, cucumber and lemon wedges.
Make batter by mixing flour, water, and soybean paste in a bowl. Mix well until the batter is smooth.
Place a non-stick pan on the stove and heat it up. Add oil to the pan. Put the green onion on the pan parallel to each other, in the shape of a rectangle.
Pour the batter over the green onion evenly. While the pancake is being cooked, keep patting it, pressing it slightly, and keeping it in a nice shape with your spatula.
A few minutes later, when the bottom of the pancake turns golden brown, turn it over. Cook a few more minutes, and flip it. Cook for 1 minute.
Serve immediately.
Make a smooth yet a little firm dough with all the ingredients mentioned.
Cover and keep aside for 15 minutes. Meanwhile heat olive oil in a pan.
Add ginger garlic paste and finely chopped Onions.
After it gets a little bit translucent and the raw smell vanishes, stir in all the spices including the Chicken and the shredded Cabbage.
Saute until everything gets cooked well for about 15 minutes on a low heat. Don't forget to put a lid on.
Now comes the most interesting part of making the Momos first and then frying.
Divide the dough into equal sized balls that should be smaller in size. Roll out a thin disc.
Place about 1-2 tablespoons of the Chicken filling. Shape into your favourite Momos.
Now instead of steaming, these are to be fried in a air fryer.
Serve with spicy Momos Chutney.
Enjoy!
Place the grated potatoes, sauerkraut and herbs in a bowl. Add the all-purpose flour, salt, pepper and stir in the baking soda. Mix well.
Mix the batter once again thoroughly before beginning to make the latkes, to ensure that the liquid hasn't all pooled at the bottom.
Heat a large cast iron skillet or nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add enough oil to coat the bottom of the pan.
Drop the batter into the skillet with a heaping tablespoon and immediately flatten into a pancake using the back of the spoon or a ladle.
When the edges are browned, check if the bottom of the pancake looks golden-brown. Turn the pancakes over and continue to brown on the other side.
Serve hot.
Gather the ingredients.
Slice tofu into 1- to 1 1/2-inch cubes or rectangles and add to the hoisin, gently turning to saturate. Set in the refrigerator.
Combine stir-fry sauce ingredients in a cup and set aside. Distribute flour over a large dinner plate.
Remove tofu from refrigerator and gently turn each piece in the flour. Let tofu remain sitting in the flour.
Heat a small to medium frying pan over high heat. Add olive oil. When oil is hot, add the floured tofu cubes.
Fry 1 minute, then turn tofu and fry 1 more minute or until medium-browned on both sides.
Remove tofu from oil and set on paper towel to drain. Reduce heat to medium-low and continue frying until all tofu is cooked.
Reserve fried tofu on a sheet pan in a warm oven. Heat a wok or large frying pan over high heat.
Add 1 tablespoon oil plus onion and garlic, and stir-fry 1 minute. Add 2 tablespoons of stir-fry sauce. Stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add broccoli.
Stir-fry 2 minutes, adding more of the stir-fry sauce as you go.
Add red pepper, and remaining stir-fry sauce, frying everything together until vegetables are cooked but still retain some crispness (2 to 3 minutes).
Remove from heat and taste-test. If too salty or sweet for your taste, add a little lime or lemon juice.
Add more soy sauce if not salty or flavorful enough. Some fresh-cut chili or chili flakes can be added for more spice.
To serve, transfer vegetables onto a serving plate or individual plates or bowls.
Tip the pan to drizzle remaining sauce over, then top with the warm tofu. Serve with and egg noodles.
Grate the zucchini into a bowl or colander that is prepared with a clean dish towel. Add a pinch of salt, and leave for 10 minutes. Drain the zucchini.
Mix water with flax seeds in a small bowl. Let the mixture thicken for 5 minutes.
Mix the dry ingredients together. Add the chia mixture, almond milk and olive oil and mix again with a whisk.
Add zucchini, corn , jalapeño, garlic, scallions and parsley. Sprinkle with lime juice. Mix it all together.
Heat up a pan to medium heat and place oil into the pan. Portion out one heaped tbsp of batter and cook in batches over medium heat for 5 minutes per side.
Serve immediately.
In a medium mixing bowl whisk the eggs first, add milk and water and mix well.
Stir in the flour and melted butter. Mix until the batter fully incorporated.
Preheat the pan on medium-high heat and fry the crepes for about 1 minute from each side or until a nice golden color.
Serve the crepes with blueberries, raspberries, blueberries, strawberries. Drizzle with honey and some lemon juice.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Season scallops with salt and pepper, then dredge in flour. Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat and add scallops.
Cook for 1 minute per side. Stir in heavy cream and bring to a boil. Turn off the heat and add pasta and pesto. Mix well. Remove from heat and serve.
Cut Chicken Fillets into 1-1½ inch size pieces.
Combine ginger, garlic, soy sauce, rice wine in a bowl, add chicken pieces, massage, and set aside.
Place 1 egg in ½ cup and add cold water to make it ½ cupful.
Combine flour in a separate bowl, and egg & cold water, and mix lightly to make tempura batter.
Heat Oil from 325°F to 350°F.
Coat the chicken pieces with flour, dip into tempura batter and fry until batter is light golden and crispy.
Take them out and lay them on a rack to drain the oil.
Place all the sauce ingredients in a saucepan and bring to a boil, then simmer for a few minutes.
Half fill a bowl with freshly cooked rice, place chicken, lettuce leaves and spring onion on top, drizzle the sauce over and enjoy.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper and set aside.
In the bowl of your stand-up mixer add the warm water and sugar, sprinkle the yeast on top, let sit for 5 minutes, then stir to combine.
Roll the dough out into a large rectangle. Spread the pizza sauce on top.
Sprinkle the mozzarella cheese evenly over the top. Then add the sliced salami, ham, and pepperoni. Brush the edges of the dough with the egg.
Then roll up the dough like a jelly roll. Pinch the edges together and tuck under. Brush the top of the dough with the egg wash. Sprinkle with oregano.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until bubbly and golden. Remove from oven and slice.
To a small bowl add water, and yeast together. Stir to combine well. The yeast should be activated when it becomes foamy, about 10 minutes.
Transfer the flour to a flat surface and make a well in the middle. Add the yeast mixture, yogurt, salt and oil, knead the dough until the surface becomes smooth and shiny, about 10 minutes.

Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place until it doubles in size, about an hour. Divide the dough into 8 equal portions. Roll the dough to a 8-inch circles using a rolling pin.

Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and lightly grease the surface with oil. Place the dough on the skillet. When it puffs up and bubbles and burnt spots appear, flip it over and cook the other side.
Repeat the same until all dough are done. Brush the naan with the melted butter, serve warm.
wine vinegar: Place potatoes into a large pot and cover with salted water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until tender, about 20 minutes.
Slice potatoes and place them into a large salad bowl. Toss potatoes with onion.
Whisk olive oil, wine vinegar, lemon juice, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, black pepper, oregano, and rosemary in a bowl.
Pour the dressing over the potatoes and toss. Serve immediately or chill until serving time.

Season the chicken with sea salt and black pepper.
In a large skillet, heat the oil over medium-high heat.
Dip the chicken pieces in the egg, shake off the excess.
Working in batches, place a single layer of chicken into the pan. Cook for a few minutes on each side.
Remove the chicken from the pan and cover with foil to keep warm.
In the same skillet, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and saute for about 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the coconut aminos, sesame seeds, wine vinegar, orange zest, and ground ginger.
Bring the sauce to a gentle boil, reduce the heat, and simmer for 10 minutes, until the volume is reduced, and the sauce thickens and looks glossy.
Return the chicken to the pan and toss to coat. The sauce will thicken as it cools from hot to warm. Garnish with parsley.
Arrange eggplant slices in a colander and sprinkle with enough salt to lightly coat, for about 10 minutes.
Rinse eggplant and pat dry with paper towels. Chop eggplant into cubes.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta until tender yet firm to the bite, about 10 to 11 minutes, drain.
Heat 1/2 olive oil in a skillet over medium heat, cook and stir eggplant, adding more oil if eggplant gets dry, until browned, 5 to 10 minutes.
Transfer eggplant to a bowl, reserving oil in the skillet.
Heat remaining oil in the same skillet; cook and stir onion and garlic until golden, about 10 minutes.
Return eggplant to skillet and season with oregano; cook and stir for 1 minute more.
Mix tomatoes, vinegar, salt, and black pepper into eggplant mixture; cover skillet and simmer sauce until eggplant is softened, about 10 minutes.
Serve pasta on a plate and cover with the eggplant mixture.
In a non-stick skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced eggplant and sauté for about 5-7 minutes until it starts to soften and turn slightly brown.
Add the diced onion and continue to sauté for another 2-3 minutes, or until the onion becomes translucent.
Stir in the diced tomatoes, red wine vinegar, capers, salt, and pepper.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and let the mixture simmer for about 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the eggplant is tender and the flavors meld together.
Remove from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
cottage cheese: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook elbow macaroni in the boiling water, about 8 minutes. Drain. 3 minutes before cooking add squash
Place cooked squash in the bowl of a food processor; process until pureed. Transfer to a large bowl.
Stir in cooked pasta, milk, cottage cheese, Cheddar cheese, Parmesan cheese, olive oil, bouillon, nutmeg, cayenne pepper, sea salt, and black pepper until completely blended and creamy.
Melt ghee in a skillet over medium heat. Pour beaten eggs into the skillet, let cook undisturbed until the bottom of the eggs begin to firm, 1 to 2 minutes.
Stir cottage cheese and dill into eggs and season with black pepper. Cook and stir until eggs are nearly set, 3 to 4 minutes more.

In a large bowl, beat together the cottage cheese, eggs and salt and mix until smooth, then add the milk and stir until creamy. Add the flour and baking powder and stir just until blended. Stir in the melted butter and raspberries.

Preheat a griddle or skillet over medium heat and brush with butter or oil or spray with nonstick spray. Drop batter onto the pan and cook until bubbles begin to break the surface and the edges no longer appear wet, then flip using a thin spatula and cook until golden on the other side as well.
Marinate the pieces with salt and ginger paste and keep aside for 5 minutes.
Mix cornflour, flour, ginger paste, salt in a bowl and make a batter using a little water.
Dip the paneer pieces in this batter and fry in hot oil until they turn golden.
Garnish with spring onions and serve.
Preheat oven to 400°F .

Split each zucchini in half, lengthwise, and remove the seeds. Sprinkle with salt and let sit for 10 minutes. Then blot off the drops of liquid with paper towels and place the halves in a greased baking dish.
Chop the ham coarsely and mix with cottage cheese, sour cream, red onion, tomato and spices. Add a third of the grated cheese. Salt and pepper to taste.
Distribute the mixture into the halves of zucchini and sprinkle the remaining cheese on top. Bake for approximately 20–30 minutes.

In a large bowl, beat together the cottage cheese, eggs and salt and mix until smooth, then add the milk and stir until creamy. Add the flour and baking powder and stir just until blended. Stir in the melted butter.

Preheat a griddle or skillet over medium heat and brush with butter or oil or spray with nonstick spray. Drop batter onto the pan and cook until bubbles begin to break the surface and the edges no longer appear wet, then flip using a thin spatula and cook until golden on the other side as well.
Serve warm, topped with jam.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add Greek yogurt, olive oil, cottage cheese, grated halloumi, mint, and parsley to the flour mixture. Mix until the dough comes together.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough into a circle.
Transfer the dough to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Spread crumbled feta cheese over the center of the dough, leaving a border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough over the filling, creating a rustic, free-form shape.
Brush the exposed dough with beaten egg
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes or until the crust is golden and cooked through.
Firstly, in a large kadai heat 2 tbsp oil and roast cottage cheese.
Roast until the paneer turns golden brown. keep aside. In the same oil, add ½ bell pepper, ½ onion and saute on high flame.
Once they slightly shrink, keep the onions and bell pepper aside. With the remaining oil add ½ tsp cumin, cloves.
Saute on low flame until the spices turn aromatic. Further add 1 onion, 1 inch ginger and 2 clove garlic.
Saute until onions shrink and softens. Now add 2 tomato and saute until tomatoes soften and turn mushy.
Cool completely and transfer to the blender. Blend to smooth paste adding water if required.
Filter the puree making sure to have a smooth silky puree. keep aside.
Firstly, in a large kadai heat 1 tbsp butter and saute 1 bay leaf, ½ tsp turmeric, ¾ tsp chilli powder, 1 tsp coriander powder and ¼ tsp cumin powder.
Saute until the spices turn aromatic. Now add in prepared onion tomato puree, 1 tsp salt and mix well.
Cover and cook for 5-10 minutes or until the puree thickens. Add in ¾ cup water and mix well adjusting consistency as required.
Now add in fried paneer, bell pepper and onion. mix well. Cover and simmer for 3 minutes or until the flavours are absorbed well.
Additionally, add 2 tbsp coriander, mix well.
Spread the cottage cheese on slices of bread. Top with prosciutto, slices of peach, blueberries and garnish with basil leaves. Serve.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the cooked pasta to the skillet with the garlic and olive oil. Toss to coat.
Stir in the cottage cheese until warmed through and evenly distributed.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Whisk eggs in a large bowl. Add cottage cheese, cheddar cheese, ghee, spinach, mushrooms and salt, mix.
Pour egg mixture into prepared baking dish. Bake in the preheated oven until eggs are set, 25 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Oil a muffin pan.
Combine egg whites, cottage cheese, garlic powder, salt, and pepper in a blender; blend until smooth, about 15 seconds.
Combine spinach and turkey in a bowl. Pour in egg mixture and stir to combine.
Ladle the mixture evenly into the muffin cups, filling each about 3/4 full. Top each muffin with cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven until egg white bites are set, 18 to 20 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 500°F.
Place 2 pieces of spaghetti squash on a microwave-safe place with cut sides facing down.
Microwave on high until tender, about 15 minutes. Repeat with remaining 2 pieces of spaghetti squash.
Scoop squash out of shells into a large bowl and reserve shells.
Transfer 1/2 the cooked squash to a food processor and finely chop. Return squash to the bowl and set aside.
Place kale in the food processor and chop finely. Transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Puree 1/2 the tomatoes in the food processor. Transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook and stir ground beef, onion, and garlic in the hot skillet until browned and crumbly, 5 to 7 minutes.
Add basil, oregano, salt, red pepper flakes, and black pepper. Let simmer for 5 minutes.
Add spaghetti squash, kale, tomatoes, tomato puree, 1/2 of the mozzarella cheese, 1/2 of the cottage cheese.
Add Parmesan cheese, and cream cheese to the skillet with the ground beef.
Reduce heat to low and simmer until cheeses are melted, about 5 minutes. Remove lasagna sauce from heat.
Spread 2 tablespoons of cottage cheese into each spaghetti squash shell. Scoop lasagna sauce into the shells up to the top.
Top filled shells with remaining mozzarella cheese. Place filled shells on the middle rack of the preheated oven and bake for 10 minutes.
Turn the oven to broil and cook lasagnas until cheese is browned, about 5 minutes more.
Add halved strawberries and cottage cheese to a bowl and enjoy.
Preheat your air fryer to 360°F.
Combine all ingredients except blueberries in a blender.
Process until smooth.
Pour mixture into a baking dish that can fit inside your air fryer basket.
Top with blueberries.
Bake in the air fryer for 25 to 30 minutes or until tested done.
Let cool.
Serve and enjoy.
In a bowl or jar, mash blueberries, raspberries, honey, and vanilla.
Add cottage cheese and stir to combine.
Serve chilled, topped with almonds and a drizzle of honey.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In an oven-safe dish, place a layer of sliced eggplant rounds.
Spread a portion of cooked lentils over the eggplant.
Add a layer of low-fat cottage cheese on top of the lentils.
Pour low-sodium tomato sauce over the cottage cheese.
Sprinkle dried oregano, salt, and pepper on top.
Repeat the layers if you have more ingredients.
Top the final layer with the shredded low-fat mozzarella cheese.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly, and the eggplant is tender.
Let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Cook the lasagna sheets in a bowl of water according to the instructions on the packaging
In a large nonstick skillet over medium heat, cook and stir onion in oil until tender. Add spinach, broccoli, 1/3 cup marinara sauce, garlic powder and cumin. Cook for another 2 minutes.

Stir in cottage cheese and 1/2 cup cheddar cheese. Season to taste. Spoon about 1/3 cup spinach mixture down center of each lasagna cheets. Roll up and place seam side down in a baking dish. Spoon remaining marinar sauce over top.
Cover and bake 20-25 minutes or until heated through. Uncover sprinkle with remaining cheese. Bake 5 minutes longer or until cheese is melted.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Line the bottom of the baking sheet with parchment paper or wax paper and coat it with cooking spray.
Puree spinach, parsley, flour and sour cream in a food processor until smooth. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Add egg yolks and pulse to mix, transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Beat egg whites in a clean mixing bowl, with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form. Fold ⅓ of the beaten whites into the spinach puree with a rubber spatula to lighten it.
Fold the spinach mixture into the remaining whites just until blended, and spread in the prepared pan. Bake for 8-10 minutes, or until springy to touch, cool for 5 minutes before continuing.
Invert the spinach cake onto a moistened towel. Peel off the parchment or wax paper and cover the spinach cake with another towel.
Puree cream cheese and cottage cheese in a food processor until smooth, transfer to a small bowl and set aside.
Uncover the spinach cake and sprinkle the surface with lemon juice. Spread half the cheese mixture on the spinach cake and distribute smoked salmon evenly on top.
Spread the remaining cheese mixture over the salmon. Sprinkle with chives and some black pepper. Roll the cake using the towel to lift and assist in the rolling.
Wrap the roulade in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving.
Place zucchini slices on a baking sheet brush with olive oil on both sides, sprinkle with salt.
Place slices directly on a grill that has been preheated to medium. Grill the slices on both sides until the zucchini is tender, 3 minutes per side.
Remove the slices from the grill and place them on the baking sheet.
Place 1/2 tbsp of the cheese spread, and dill at one end of each zucchini slice and roll-up.
pork shoulder: Heat oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add diced pork shoulder and cook until browned, about 4-5 minutes.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic; cook until softened, about 2-3 minutes.
Stir in ground cumin, chili powder, and dried oregano; cook for 1 minute.
Pour in chicken broth and bring to a simmer.
Add drained and rinsed hominy and canned diced tomatoes.
Simmer for 20-25 minutes until pork is tender and flavors are well combined.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh chopped cilantro.
Marinate the pork with the cornstarch, oyster sauce, and olive oil.
Meanwhile, in a medium pot, bring 4 cups of water to a boil. Add the rice and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat, and cover and cook for about 15 minutes.
Add the ginger and pork. Stir and simmer for another 5 minutes or so. Finally, stir in the pepper and salt to taste.
Serve piping hot with chopped scallions.
Melt the butter in a frying pan or wok, over medium-high heat. Add the pork strips, and stirfry for a couple of minutes, or until lightly browned.
Add the vegetables and chili paste, stirring for a couple of minutes, or until crisp-tender. Season with salt and pepper, to taste.
For serving, use a slotted spoon to distribute the stir-fry to plates, sprinkle with almonds and toss to coat.
In a small bowl, mix together the oil, ground cumin, smoked paprika, dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Rub the spice mixture onto the thinly sliced pork shoulder to marinate it.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the marinated pork slices to the hot skillet. Cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side until they are browned and cooked through.
Warm the corn tortilla in a dry skillet or on a griddle until it is soft and pliable.
Place the cooked pork slices on the warm tortilla.
Top with diced pineapple, diced onion, and chopped fresh cilantro.
Preheat your oven to 325°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the orange juice, lime juice, achiote paste, ground cumin, dried oregano, ground cinnamon, salt, and pepper. Mix until you have a smooth marinade.
Add the cubed pork shoulder to the marinade. Make sure the pork is well-coated. Let it marinate for about 10-15 minutes.
Heat a small amount of cooking oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the marinated pork to the skillet. Sear it for about 2-3 minutes on each side until it's browned.
Cover the skillet with a lid or aluminum foil.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for about 45-60 minutes, or until the pork is tender and fully cooked.
Remove the skillet from the oven.
Season pork with salt, pepper, and any desired spices, then cook it in a pressure cooker for about 45 minutes or until tender. Shred the cooked pork with a fork.
Toast the whole wheat hamburger bun.
Assemble the burger by placing lettuce and the shredded pork on the bottom half of the bun.
Drizzle with barbecue sauce if desired.
Cover with the top half of the bun.
Heat oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add pork chunks and brown on all sides for 5-6 minutes.
Add minced garlic and chopped onion; cook for another 2-3 minutes until softened.
Stir in chicken broth, soy sauce, fish sauce, ground black pepper, and ground ginger.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Add the hard-boiled egg and simmer for another 5 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, mix together sliced pork shoulder with ground cumin, paprika, dried oregano, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Place the seasoned pork slices on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through and slightly crispy around the edges.
Warm the corn tortillas in a dry skillet or microwave.
Fill each tortilla with roasted pork slices, pineapple chunks, and sliced onions.
Top with sliced avocado and a dollop of sour cream.
Place the pork shoulder chunks in the slow cooker.
In a small bowl, mix together ground cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Sprinkle the spice mixture over the pork, rubbing it in to coat evenly.
Pour broth or water and lime juice over the pork.
Cover and cook on high for 3-4 hours, until the pork is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Once cooked, shred the pork using two forks.
Serve the carnitas hot with corn tortillas and your choice of toppings.

Melt the butter in a large pan over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring frequently, until the onion is translucent. Add the garlic and cook, stirring for 2 minutes longer. Add the potatoes, lima beans, corn, chicken stock, and tomatoes and bring to a boil.

Cover, reduce heat, and simmer for about 25 minutes. Add the barbecue sauce, Worcestershire sauce, brown sugar, if using, salt, black pepper, cayenne, chicken, and pork. Mix well to combine. Simmer, uncovered, for 15 minutes.
Place hominy in a large pot, cover with water. Add 1 head garlic and salt to taste. Cook over medium heat for 2 hours.
Place pork shoulder and pork loin in the hominy mixture and cook until meat is tender and cooked through, about 1 hour.
Place tomato and guajillo chiles in a pot and add enough water to cover, bring to a boil. Cook until chiles have softened, 15 to 20 minutes. Drain.
Place tomato and chiles with salt, 1 clove garlic, oregano, and cumin in a blender, add 2 cups water.
Blend until smooth. Strain mixture through a fine-mesh sieve and set chile sauce aside.
Transfer pork to a work surface and shred with 2 forks. Discard the pork bones.
Pour chile sauce into hominy mixture, bring to a boil. Return shredded pork to pot. Simmer pozole until flavors have blended, about 3 minutes.
Ladle pozole into serving bowls and top with lettuce, radish and onion and serve lime wedge on the side.
cumin seed: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
In a blender, combine the onion, ginger, and garlic and blend into a smooth puree with 2 tbsp or more of water.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and cook for 1 minute. Add bay leaf and cook until the bay leaf changes color.
Add the pureed onion and cook until the onion mixture is dry and does not smell raw. Stir occasionally to avoid sticking, 4 minutes.
Add cinnamon, garam masala and cayenne and mix well. Add the coconut milk, mango pulp, salt, and mix well.
Mix, cover and cook until the sauce comes to a boil, 5 minutes. Add chickpeas and mix in. Reduce the heat to low medium and cook for 10 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf and discard. Serve with rice.
Roughly tear the chapatis by hand.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds.
When the seeds start crackling, add the green chilies, curry leaves and chopped onions.
Sauté them for 1-2 minutes.
Now add the potatoes, carrots, salt and turmeric.
Stir and cover. Let it cook for 2-3 minutes, till the potatoes and carrots are tender.
Add a tablespoon of water, peas and rest of the spices.
Stir and then add the crumbled chapati.
Add the lemon juice, stir and cover and cook it for a minute.
Take off the lid and cook for another one minute.
Garnish with chopped coriander.
It tastes great by itself and goes well when paired with yoghurt.
Combine the milk, flour and chili powder in a deep bowl, mix well and keep aside.
Heat the oil in a broad non-stick pan, add the cumin seeds and peas and saute on a medium flame for 30 seconds.
Add the curry and grated onion and saute on a medium flame for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and saute on a medium flame for 1 minute.
Add the milk mixture and salt, mix well and cook on a slow flame for 6 minutes, while stirring occasionally.
Serve immediately.
Place the rinsed lentils, vegetable stock, tomatoes, and salt in a stockpot.
Heat the oil in a small skillet over high heat and add the mustard and cumin seeds. Fry until the mustard seeds begin to pop, about 10 seconds.
Reduce the heat to medium-high and add the garlic. Fry for another 20 seconds. Add the onion and fry until soft, about 5 minutes.
Add the turmeric, coriander, cinnamon, and cloves and stir to combine. Scrape the contents of the skillet into the stockpot. Stir to combine and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for about 20 minutes, or until the lentils are done and the soup is thick, stirring occasionally.
Before serving, add the lime juice, according to taste, and garnish with cilantro
In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over moderate heat. Stir in the cumin seeds and cook until they are fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the shredded cabbage, turmeric and kosher salt and cook, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes. Serve immediately.
Add coconut, roasted gram lentils, garlic, chili, cumin seeds, sugar, lemon juice and water to a blender and blend until you get smooth chutney.
Add more water if needed to get to the desired consistency.
Take out the chutney in a serving bowl.
Heat oil in a tadka pan over medium heat.
Add mustard seeds and allow them to pop.
Turn the heat off and then add asafetida/hing and curry leaves.
Be careful as you add curry leaves as the oil may splatter.
Pour the tadka over the chutney and stir in.
Serve with pita bread.

Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add cumin seeds. Allow to splutter.
Add chicken, ground turmeric, ground coriander, and garam masala. Season with salt. Saute for 3 minutes or until browned.
Add onions, tomatoes, minced ginger and garlic. Pour in 1 cup water. Season with salt. Cook for 10 minutes.
Add chickpeas, and ginger and stir to combine. Simmer for 5 minutes. Garnish with parsley and serve.
Turn Instant Pot to saute mode and heat oil. Add cumin seeds and toast them for 30 seconds. Add turmeric powder and mix well. Add ginger and garlic, mix well.
Add onion and saute for a minute. Cook for 2 minutes with a glass lid on. Add tomatoes, red chili powder, garam masala, salt and ground coriander. Mix well.
Add ground chicken and break it with a spatula. Close Instant Pot with the pressure valve to sealing. Pressure Cook on for 4 minutes.
Garnish with cilantro and serve hot.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat 1 tbsp oil and cook the fish cutlets for 2-3 minutes on each side,keep aside. Heat the remaining oil in pan.
Add cumin seeds and whole spices, let them crackle; add onion,ginger paste. Sauté until onions are golden brown.
Add tomato puree, salt and sauté for 10 minutes. Now,add rest of the spices and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add water and let it boil. Add the cooked fish pieces to the curry and simmer for 5 minutes.
Enjoy with rice.
Soak lentils in ½ cup of water for 10 minutes, then drain out half of the water and blend it to smooth paste. Transfer the content to a mixing bowl.
Add all other ingredients to the paste of lentils. Mix well. Add little water to make a thick and smooth consistent batter. Heat a flat griddle on medium flame.
Grease it with some oil. Put one ladleful of batter and allow it to spread, making a circle. Cook it till the bottom is getting cooked and turns to light golden in color.
Drizzle 1 tsp oil on top of crepe. Once base is done, flip it and cook other side. Remove once cooked evenly on both sides. Repeat with all the batter. Serve with lime slices.
In a mixing bowl, combine the crumbled paneer, cooked peas, mashed potato, cumin seeds, garam masala, turmeric powder, chili powder, and salt. Mix well to combine.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the paneer and pea mixture to the pan and sauté for 2-3 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside to cool.

In a separate bowl, prepare the samosa dough by combining all-purpose flour, salt, and vegetable oil. Gradually add water and knead until a smooth dough forms. Cover with a damp cloth and let it rest for 15-20 minutes.
Divide the dough into small lemon-sized balls and roll each ball into a thin circle.
Cut each circle in half to form semi-circles.
Take one semi-circle and fold it into a cone shape, sealing the edges with water.
Fill the cone with the paneer and pea mixture, then seal the open edge using water to form a triangular samosa.
Repeat the process with the remaining dough and filling.
Heat vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Once the oil is hot, carefully add the samosas in batches and fry until they turn golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove the fried samosas from the oil and drain them on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Serve the paneer and pea samosas hot with your favorite chutney or dipping sauce.
Add all the chopped vegetables to a bowl. Add the rice flour, chickpea flour, salt, spices, cilantro, baking soda and mix everything well.
Add in 1 cup of water to the mixture, stir well until combined. Let this mixture sit for 5 minutes before starting to make waffles.
Oil and preheat your waffle maker. Pour and spread the batter on your waffle maker, spread evenly with a spatula.
Cook until golden brown and crisp, to preference. Remove the waffle from the waffle maker, repeat for all of the batter. Serve immediately.

Heat a skillet over low-medium heat. Add coconut oil.
Once it's hot, add cumin seeds. Stir and let it splutter.
Add onion, garlic, ginger, chili, and saute for 3 minutes. Add cashews and salt. Stir and cook for 2 minutes.
Add spinach. Stir and cook until wilted. Transfer everything to a blender and blend until creamy.
Next, add 1 tbsp olive oil to the skillet. Add cubed tofu. Stir and saute for 3 minutes.
Then add turmeric, cumin powder, coriander powder. Saute for 10 minutes over low heat.
Now add the spinach puree and 1/2 cup water. Stir and cook for 1 minute over low heat.
Turn off the heat, add coconut milk and give it a final stir.
In a large skillet, saute the carrots and cumin in the butter over low heat for about 2 minutes. Season with salt and pepper and coat with protein powder.
Add the orange juice and honey. Bring to a boil. Cover and simmer over high heat for about 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Adjust the seasoning. Sprinkle with the thyme and serve.
In a pan, heat a little oil and add cumin seeds. Once they splutter, add chopped onion, grated ginger, and green chili. Sauté until onions are soft.
Add mashed potato, cooked green peas, garam masala, turmeric powder, ground coriander, ground cumin, and salt. Mix well and cook for a couple of minutes. Remove from heat and let it cool.
Preheat the air fryer to 375°F for 3-5 minutes.
Take one sheet of phyllo dough and fold it in half lengthwise. Cut it into two strips.
Take one strip of phyllo dough and fold it in half widthwise to form a rectangle.
Fold the phyllo dough over the filling diagonally to form a triangle. Continue folding until you reach the end of the strip.
Repeat the process with the remaining strips of phyllo dough and filling.
Spray the samosas lightly with cooking spray.
Place the samosas in the air fryer basket in a single layer, leaving some space between them.
Air fry for 10-12 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the samosas are golden brown and crispy.
Serve the air fryer samosas hot with your favorite chutney or dipping sauce.
Set a pan over medium heat. Add oil.
Once it’s hot, add cumin seeds. Let it splutter for 30-40 seconds.
Then add garlic + ginger + onion + asafoetida and stir-sauté for 3-4 minutes, or until the onion is golden.
Add potatoes and give it a quick mix. Cover the pan and sauté for 5 minutes (stir occasionally).
Next, add cauliflower florets. Stir, cover the pan and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Now add turmeric powder + cumin powder + coriander powder + Kashmiri red chili powder + salt.
Stir and cook for 1 minute. Add tomatoes + green peas.
Mix well, cover the pan, and cook for 9-10 minutes. Stir occasionally.
Garnish with freshly chopped cilantro and serve.
Heat 2 teaspoon of oil in a pan on medium heat.
Add cauliflower florets and cook for 2-3 minutes and then add the sliced potatoes.
Cook on medium-low flame for 7-8 minutes till potatoes and cauliflower have some brown spots on them.
Drain on paper towel and set aside. In the same pan heat 1 1⁄8 teaspoon of oil on medium heat and add cumin seeds and heat until they crackle.
Add in the onions and cook for 2 minutes till translucent. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another 2 minutes or until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and cook for 2 minutes till they become slightly soft.
Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder.
Cover the pan and let the masala cook for 2-3 minutes and then add the potatoes and cauliflower to it and mix. Add chopped coriander leaves and give a good mix.
Add garam masala and cook the potato and cauliflower on medium-low heat for 5-6 minutes.
Add salt and cover the pan and cook more additional 6-7 minutes on low flame or till the potato and cauliflower are tender but not soggy.
If you feel the masala is sticking, you may add some water. Add 1 tablespoon at a time and only add enough to cook the veggies.
Garnish with some more coriander leaves if desired and serve hot.
In a salted boiling water cook rice according to package directions.
Heat the oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. When they crackle, add onion and saute for 3 minutes.
Add the turmeric powder, chickpeas, peas, beans and mix well. Cook on a medium flame for 3 minutes.
Add the green chili paste, salt and 2 tbsp of water. Add the rice, lemon juice, coriander and cook for 3 minutes.
Garnish with some chopped onion and serve.
In a mixing bowl, combine the whole wheat flour with a pinch of salt. Gradually add water and knead to form a smooth dough. Cover and let it rest for 15-20 minutes.
In another bowl, mix together the mashed potatoes, chopped cilantro, chopped green chili, cumin seeds, and salt to taste to prepare the stuffing.
Divide the dough into equal-sized balls and roll each ball into a small circle.
Place a spoonful of the potato stuffing in the center of each circle.
Bring the edges of the dough together to cover the stuffing and pinch to seal. Flatten the stuffed ball with your hands.
Heat a skillet or griddle over medium heat. Place the stuffed paratha on the skillet and cook until golden brown spots appear on one side, then flip and cook the other side.
Brush the cooked paratha with oil on both sides.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile bring the tomatoes, cumin, turmeric, salt, and water to a boil in a four quart saucepan, reduce heat to medium and maintain a simmer.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium-high heat, cook the fish in the oiled skillet until golden brown, 2 to 3 minutes per side. Transfer the fish to the saucepan.
Heat a separate skillet over medium heat, and toast the mustard seeds, cumin seeds and fennel seeds until fragrant.
Stir the spices into the saucepan. Simmer until the flavors integrate, about 10 minutes more. Serve hot.
