corn starch: Make the sauce: In a small bowl, mix together corn starch and 1 tablespoon of water to make slurry.
Set aside. In a small pot, whisk together all ingredients and 2 tablespoons of water until combined. Bring to a boil and lower heat to a simmer.
Whisk in the corn starch slurry and cook 1-2 minutes, until thickened. Set aside in a warm place.
In a steamer set, steam broccoli and carrots over boiling water for 4-5 minutes, until tender with a little bite.
Toss with a splash of soy sauce and mirin. Set aside in a warm place.
Cook the chicken: In a large skillet, heat 1 teaspoon of sesame oil and 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium high heat.
Add diced chicken and cook until golden and cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Pour half of the teriyaki sauce over the chicken and cook 1 more minute. Assemble the bowls:
Top with broccoli, carrots, and chicken. Drizzle with remaining teriyaki sauce, sesame seeds and chives.
In a bowl, prepare the batter by mixing all-purpose flour, cornstarch, baking powder, and cold water until it forms a smooth, thick batter.
Heat a small amount of cooking oil in a skillet or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Dip each peeled and deveined prawn into the batter to coat it evenly.
Carefully place the battered prawns into the hot skillet.
Fry the prawns for 2-3 minutes on each side or until they turn golden brown and crispy.
While the prawns are frying, make the honey sauce. In a small bowl, mix together honey, low-sodium soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, and red pepper flakes.
Once the prawns are crispy, remove them from the skillet and place them on a paper towel to drain any excess oil.
In the same skillet, pour the honey sauce over medium heat.
Add the crispy prawns back to the skillet and stir them in the sauce to coat them evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and glazes the prawns.
Remove the skillet from the heat.
Heat the oil in a soup pot or wok over medium-low heat.
Add the tomato chunks and stir-fry for 5 minutes until the tomatoes are softened and start to fall apart.
Add in 1 cup chicken stock, 2 cups water, 2 tsp light soy sauce, ½ tsp sesame oil, ¼ tsp ground white pepper, and salt to taste.
Bring to a boil, and then lower the heat so that the soup is simmering with the lid on.
Now quickly beat the egg in a small bowl and prepare the cornstarch slurry in a separate bowl.
Use a ladle to slowly swirl the soup in a whirlpool motion.
Keep swirling as you pour in the cornstarch slurry until well incorporated.
Now pour a thin stream of egg into the middle of the whirlpool as you slowly swirl the soup.
This is how you get that pretty egg drop effect.
Serve hot or at room temperature.
Ladle the soup into bowls and garnish with chopped scallions and cilantro, if using.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
Add milk and corn starch into a medium bowl and whisk together until the corn starch is fully dissolved. Next add in mustard, honey, salt, and pepper and whisk together well then set aside.
Place a large pan over medium high heat and add in olive oil, garlic, and onions. Saute for 1 minute then add in the chicken while continuing to saute for another 3 minutes.

Next, add broccoli into the pan and continue to sauté for another 2 minutes. Finally, reduce heat to low and add in the honey mustard mixture. Stir until well combined, remove from heat and add in fresh cilantro , give a final stir then serve.
To serve, divide rice between plates and add chicken and broccoli. Serve hot.
Combine the pandan leaf and water in an electronic blender and blend for a minute.
Wrap the pandan in a cheese cloth or muslin cloth and squeeze to extract the juice or strain through a fine sieve.
Set aside.
Sieve the flour into a mixing bowl.
Make a well in the center and crack in the egg.
Slowly stir in the salted coconut milk.
Set aside.
Combune pandan leaf and water in a pot.
Add in the grated coconut and corn starch and continue to cook for a few minutes.
Dish out into a bowl.
First, we'll bring a pot of water to boil.
Boil the noodles for about 1-2 minutes, and work to spread the noodles apart with chopsticks the entire time.
Then, we'll drain the noodles into a colander.
The goal is to get the noodles to be pretty dry, so shake the colander a few times to get excess water.
Continue separating the noodles with chopsticks for 30-60 seconds. Cover the colander with a plate.
This helps the noodles get to the perfect texture, allowing them to cook a bit longer without introducing excess moisture.
For the chicken, Cut the chicken into little strips. Place the strips in a bowl. Marinate the chicken with cornstarch and water.
Using our hands, we'll mix and massage the bowl for 20-30 seconds.
We'll also crack and beat eggs. For the shrimp, as long as they're peeled and deveined, we don't have to do anything to them.
After washing our vegetables, we'll be cutting carrots, onions, and celery into slices and strips.
For bell pepper, use several different colors of bell peppers, but if you want to keep it simple, you can just use an entire, single bell pepper.
Chop green onions  into roughly 1 inch pieces. We'll also be adding bean sprouts later as well.
The amounts and types of vegetables are really up to you. Anything goes with Singapore Noodles (with some caveats):
Try to avoid veggies with higher water content (like bok choy), since this will lead to a more soggy dish.
Try to avoid having an excessive amount of vegetables, since even lower water content vegetables will leak out water.
We'll create our sauce by mixing curry powder, oyster sauce, salt and water.
As written, the amount of curry powder my dad uses is pretty mild, so if you want a stronger flavor and/or more yellow finish, feel free to increase the amount of curry powder.
Curry powder is absolutely essential for Singapore noodles.

With so many options, the type of curry powder you use for this dish really impacts the final flavor profile, so you might want to experiment with several options until you land on something you love.
When cooking this dish, along with other noodle stir-fries, we'll want to get our wok really hot before adding oil.
Heat the wok for 2-3 minutes on high heat. While we wait, we'll continue loosening up our noodles. Once the wok is hot enough, add 1-2 tsp of oil.
Pour in the eggs: cook the eggs very gently, leaving the first side intact for 20-30 seconds before starting to flip and scramble the eggs.
We want the eggs to be really soft. In total, we're cooking the eggs for about a minute here, and tossing them back in later.
Transfer the eggs onto a plate, add oil: Next, we'll add another tbsp of a high smoke point oil like oil, and roll it around the pan.
Cook the chicken + shrimp: We'll be cooking the chicken now, so remix the chicken + marinade around with chopsticks for a few seconds.
Cook the chicken for 30-45 seconds before adding the shrimp.
Add the shrimp, and cook both meats until the shrimp starts to turn orange, and the chicken starts to turn white.
Transfer the meat onto a plate, add 1 tbsp of oil. Cook the vegetables for about 60-90 seconds.
Transfer the vegetables onto a plate, add 1 tbsp of oil. Cook the noodles for about 1-2 minutes before flipping them.
After flipping, cook the noodles for another 2-3 minutes, occasionally moving them around with your chopsticks.
Add the sauce and cook the noodles for another 2 minutes before adding everything else back in.
Add everything else in: Over the course of about 2 minutes, we'll be progressively adding the vegetables, followed by the chicken and shrimp, the char siu, bean sprouts, and lastly, some eggs.
Evenly mix the ingredients in well with the noodles. Add sesame oil, and mix everything for another 30-45 seconds.
Add the sesame oil and white pepper into the fish paste, stir to combine well.
Clean and rinse the okras and red chilies.
Cut a slit in the middle of the okra to allow an opening to stuff fish paste inside.
For the red chilies, cut a slit down the middle of the red chilies, seed removed.
Cut the tofu puffs in the middle (but not completely broken).
Combine and stir all the ingredients in the Sauce Mixture, set aside.
Using a butter knife, stuff the fish paste inside the okras, red chilies, and tofu puffs.
Heat up a skillet and add 1 tbsp of oil.
One heated, arrange all the yong tow food in one layer and lightly pan-fry the yong tow foo on both sides so the surface is slightly browned.
Transfer the pan-fried yong tow foo onto a plate.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a medium bowl, sprinkle the shrimp with salt, pepper, and cornstarch, and toss to coat.
Set aside to sit for ten minutes at room temperature.
Heat a large sauté pan or wok (a seasoned cast iron pan or hard anodized aluminum works well, they're relatively stick free and can take the heat) on high heat.
When the pan is very hot (a drop of water instantly sizzles when it hits the pan), swirl in one tbsp of the oil to coat the pan.
Add the shrimp to the hot pan, spreading them out quickly in a single layer.
Let them fry in the pan without moving them, for 30 seconds.
Flip the shrimp over and let them fry on the other side for another 30 seconds or until they are mostly cooked through. (Do not cook them all the way!)
Return the pan to the burner and lower the heat to medium.
Add a little more oil if the pan needs it.
Add the beaten eggs and stir them quickly to scramble them while they cook.
When the eggs are not quite cooked through, still a bit runny, transfer them from the pan to the bowl with the cooked shrimp.
Clean out the pan or wok with paper towels and return it to the burner.
Heat the pan on high and when it is hot, swirl in the remaining tbsp or two of oil.
When the oil is shimmering hot (almost smoking), add the green onions and sauté for 15 seconds.
Then add the leftover cooked rice to the pan and stir with the green onions to mix well.
Spread the rice onion mixture over the surface of the pan and let it fry, without moving it. You should hear the rice sizzle.
Cook for about 1 to 2 minutes. Use a spatula to turn over the rice, and spread it over the pan again. Let cook for a minute longer.
Sprinkle soy sauce on the rice and stir to combine. Add the carrots, peas, shrimp, eggs, and sesame oil, stirring to combine well. Heat everything until sizzling hot.
Combine the chopped seafood, pinch of salt and a pinch of ground black pepper in a small bowl. Mix it well and set aside.
Combine flour, starch, salt, and stock in a large enough to accommodate the green onions. Mix with a whisk until smooth.
Heat 2 tbsp of oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and swirl to coat evenly.
Add the green onions to the batter to coat them. Using tongs or your hand, place them side by side in the skillet.
Add the seafood to the leftover batter in the bowl. Using your hands or tongs, spread the battered seafood on top of the battered scallions, scraping out any excess batter remaining in the bowl.
Add the sliced chili and pour the beaten eggs over top of the pancake in the skillet.
Reduce the heat to medium and cook for about 6 minutes, until the bottom is light brown and crispy.
Lift one edge of the pancake, and cook on another side for 3 minutes.
Transfer to a large plate, with the egg and seafood side up.
In a mixing bowl, add the kimchi. Use kitchen shears to cup it into small bits then add the rest of the ingredients.
Whisk and combine well. The batter will be on the thin side.
Preheat skillet, add oil and swirl it around. Take ½ cup of the batter and add it to the pan and immediately use the back of a spoon to spread it.
Cook over medium-low heat about 2 minutes on the first side and 1 minute on the second side. Repeat the process until you finish the batter.
Add rice and water to a pot and cook according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a medium bowl, whisk together soy sauce, chicken stock, honey, vinegar, garlic, sesame oil, cornstarch, sriracha, ginger, red pepper flakes and 1/4 cup water, set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat. Add steak and cook, flipping once, until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in broccoli and soy sauce mixture until tender and slightly thickened, about 3-4 minutes. Serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Clean the crab and cut into 6 pieces, drain the crab and discard the water seeping out of the crab.
Pick out the tamale (or mustard) inside the crab shell, set aside. Discard the shell.
Mix all the ingredients in the Sour and Spicy Sauce in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat up a wok and add the oil.
When the oil is heated, add the minced garlic and ginger and stir fry until aromatic or when they turn slightly brown.
Add the crab and the tamale into the wok, stirring for about 10 seconds.
Add the Sour and Spicy Sauce, stir to blend well with the crab.
Cover the wok with its lid and let cook for about 4-5 minutes.
Uncover the wok and slowly add the cornstarch mixture into the sauce. Stir the sauce until it thickens.
Add the egg into the sauce and stir a few times so the egg is distributed evenly in the sauce.
Cook for another 30 seconds or so, add salt to taste if needed.

In a small bowl, whisk together the low-sodium chicken broth, soy sauce, rice vinegar, hoisin sauce, chili garlic sauce, cornstarch, ground ginger, garlic powder, and crushed red pepper flakes. Set this sauce mixture aside.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and lightly grease it with cooking spray or a small amount of vegetable oil.
Add the small pieces of chicken breast to the skillet. Cook and stir until the chicken is cooked through and no longer pink, which should take about 4-5 minutes.
Pour the sauce mixture into the skillet with the cooked chicken.
Add the chopped scallions to the skillet.
Stir everything together and let it simmer for about 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce thickens and the ingredients are heated through.
Taste and adjust the seasonings, adding more chili garlic sauce if you prefer it spicier.
In a mixing bowl, combine cornstarch, soy sauce, sesame oil, minced garlic, minced ginger, rice vinegar, and black pepper.
Add chicken wings to the bowl and toss until they are evenly coated with the marinade.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Once the oil is hot, add the chicken wings to the skillet and fry until they are golden brown and cooked through, about 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway through.
While the chicken wings are frying, prepare the gochujang sauce. In a small bowl, mix together gochujang and 1 tablespoon of water until smooth.
Once the chicken wings are cooked, remove them from the skillet and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Serve the fried chicken wings hot, drizzled with the gochujang sauce.
Garnish with sesame seeds.
In a saucepan bring water to a boil. Add rice and stir. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 20 minutes.
Slice beef into very thin strips. In a mixing bowl, combine  cornstarch, broth, soy sauce, honey and garlic powder. Mix thoroughly.
In a large skillet, over medium/high heat, saute the beef strips until browned and juices evaporate. Stir in broccoli and broth mixture.
Cook until mixture boils and thickens, stirring frequently. Serve over the cooked rice.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Start by gathering all the aromatics for the spice pouch.
Wrap everything in cheesecloth and tie securely with kitchen string. Set aside.
Rinse the beef. Place the beef in a medium to large pot along with 5 slices of ginger and 2 tbsp of Shaoxing wine.
Fill the pot with enough water to just cover the beef. Bring to a boil.Once boiling, cook for 1 final minute and turn off the heat.
Carefully drain the beef and ginger slices, and wash both the pot and the beef clean.
If there are still visible bristles on the skin, now is a good time to remove them with tweezers.
After the ti pang is clean, pat it very dry with a paper towel. Heat oil in a wok over medium heat.
Brown the ti pang on all sides until the skin is lightly crisp and brown. This process takes 10-15 minutes.
Use your spatula to scoop hot oil over any area that can’t touch the wok’s surface.
Turn off the heat, remove the ti pang, and set aside.
Add ginger, garlic, and scallion whites. Stir and cook for 2 minutes. Add the Shaoxing wine, light soy sauce and water.
Turn up the heat and bring to a boil. Turn off the heat.
Place the ti pang in a deep pot with just enough room to hold it and eventually flip it over. Transfer the sauce from the wok to this pot.
There should be enough liquid to submerge the ti pang about 75%. If not, add a little more water.
Lastly, add the spice pouch. Cover the pot, turn up the heat, and bring to a boil.
Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium low and cook the ti pang for 3 hours with the lid on.
Flip the ti pang every half hour to ensure it’s cooked evenly.
Once the ti pang is so tender that it can barely hold its shape and you can easily pull out the center bone, transfer to a serving bowl.
Skim the fat from the sauce.
Turn up the heat to reduce the sauce until it becomes a thin gravy (you can also thicken it with some cornstarch slurry).
Add salt to taste. Pour the sauce over the ti pang in the serving bowl.
Serve with rice and bok choy.
Sieve the flour and mix with the egg, a pinch of salt and 1 cup hot water. Knead to form a smooth, homogeneous dough. Shape into a ball, cover and leave to rest for around 30 minutes.
For the filling, mix ¾ of the blueberries with the honey and corn starch.

Roll the dough out on a floured surface and cut out circles 3 inch in diameter. Spoon 1 tsp of filling onto one half of each circle and fold into a semicircular shape. Use a fork to press the edges together.
Bring a large pan of water to the boil with a pinch each of salt and sugar and cook the parcels for 5 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and arrange on plates. Top with the remaining blueberries.
Cut the beef steak into thin strips, marinate for 10-15 minutes in a mixture of soy sauce and all-purpose flour.
Chop broccoli into small florets, lightly saute with a tablespoon of cooking oil for 3-4 minutes.
In a bowl, mix together beef stock, soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, garlic and corn starch. Set the stir fry sauce mixture aside.
Heat 3 tablespoons of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Saute the marinated beef and minced garlic for 3 minutes, then add the carrot.
Continue to stir and cook until the beef is close to cooked through, another minute or so. Stir in the broccoli and stir fry sauce.
Turn the heat to low and stir gently until the sauce becomes sticky and thoroughly coat the beef and broccoli stir fry.
Serve hot.
In a large resealable plastic bag, add chicken and ¼ cup corn starch. Season with salt and pepper and shake to coat.
In a large pot over medium-high heat, add 2 tbsp olive oil. Shake off excess cornstarch and add chicken. Cook, in batches, until golden, about 7 minutes. Then drain on a paper towel-lined plate.
Add remaining 1 tbsp oil to skillet. Then add garlic and red pepper flakes cook until fragrant, 30 seconds. Add chicken broth, making sure to scrape up any brown bits with a wooden spoon.

In a medium bowl, whisk hoisin sauce, honey, soy sauce, and lime juice with the remaining 1 tbsp corn starch until combined. Add the mixture to skillet and simmer until slightly thickened, 1 minute. Then add broccoli and cook until tender, 5 minutes.
Return chicken to skillet and stir to coat. Simmer for 1 more minute.
When serving, garnish with sesame seeds.
Mince the fish meat finely, adding salt to taste. Add some ice cubes and continue mincing the fish meat. Add corn starch and water and stir the mixture until it becomes a soft dough.
Dip your hand in the sago flour and roll the dough into a cylindrical shape.
Bring a pot of water to the boil and drop in the keropok lekor. Wait for them to float and remove with a slotted spoon. Set aside to cool.
Cut the pieces diagonally into thin slices. Dry thoroughly, then deep-fry in hot oil. Serve with sweet chili sauce.
pineapple chunks: Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a wok or skillet, heat ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add pork pieces to the wok, cooking until they are browned and cooked through (about 3-4 minutes).
Toss in diced bell peppers, pineapple chunks, and diced onion, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together rice vinegar, low-sodium soy sauce, tomato paste, and cornstarch.
Pour the sauce over the pork and vegetables, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile oil a nonstick skillet and heat over medium-high heat for 1 minute. Add the chicken and stir-fry until it's well browned, stirring often.
Add the garlic and vegetables and stir-fry for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in the soy sauce, stock and orange juice. Cook and stir until the mixture boils and thickens. Serve over the rice. Garnish with green onion.
Add all ingredients to a high-speed blender and blend until smooth. Pour the smoothie in glasses and serve immediately.
Segment grapefruit over a bowl so you can collect all the juice.
Add grapefruit segments, grapefruit juice, banana, frozen pineapple, yogurt, coconut oil, protein powder and ginger into a high-powered blender and blend until smooth.
Serve immediately.
Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and rinse with cold water.
Add ham, cheese, pineapple chunks, and onion to a large bowl.
In another small bowl, whisk the garlic powder, vinegar, salt, pepper, Greek yogurt, and pineapple juice until creamy.
Pour the dressing over the salad, and mix to combine.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Sprinkle pork  with salt and pepper. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
In the same skillet, saute green pepper and onion in remaining 1 teaspoon oil until almost tender, about 2 minutes.
Stir in broth, pineapple, tomato sauce and vinegar. Bring to a boil. Return pork to the pan. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 5 to 7 minutes.
Combine all ingredients in a high-powdered blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
Brown chicken in oil in a large skillet over medium high heat. Add red bell pepper, carrot and garlic and stir fry for 1 to 2 minutes.
In a small bowl, combine cornstarch and soy sauce and mix together; pour mixture into the skillet.
Add along with the pineapple and liquid, vinegar, sugar and ginger. Stir together and bring to a full boil. Serve with rice and garnish with chives.
Place all of the ingredients in your blender. Blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Put the coconut water in the blender bowl. Add the berries, banana chunks, pineapple, kale, protein powder and chia seeds. Puree until very smooth.
Serve immediately.
Combine all ingredients in a high-powdered blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Preheat your grill to medium-high heat.
Thread the chicken chunks, pineapple chunks, and cherry tomatoes alternately onto a skewer.
Brush the skewer with olive oil. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Place the skewer on the preheated grill.
Grill for about 5-7 minutes per side, or until the chicken is cooked through and has grill marks, and the pineapple and tomatoes are slightly caramelized.
Remove the ultra-easy pineapple chicken kabob from the grill.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, mix together sliced pork shoulder with ground cumin, paprika, dried oregano, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Place the seasoned pork slices on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through and slightly crispy around the edges.
Warm the corn tortillas in a dry skillet or microwave.
Fill each tortilla with roasted pork slices, pineapple chunks, and sliced onions.
Top with sliced avocado and a dollop of sour cream.
Place the fresh kale leaves, fresh pineapple chunks, and half a banana into a blender.
Pour in the almond milk.
Blend all the ingredients until smooth and creamy. If the smoothie is too thick, you can add a little more milk to reach your desired consistency.
Add all the ingredients to the blender. Blend until fully incorporated and creamy. Taste and add extra mint leaves. Serve immediately.
Add all ingredients to a high-speed blender and blend until smooth. Pour the smoothie in glasses and serve immediately.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a large microwaveable dish, mix the tomato sauce, vinegar, sugar and garlic thoroughly with the chicken, onion and pepper.
Microwave, uncovered, on high for 8-10 mins until the chicken is starting to cook and the sauce is sizzling.
Stir in the pineapple pieces and bell pepper and return to the microwave for another 3 to 5 minutes until the chicken is completely cooked.
Leave to stand for a few minutes, serve with rice and garnish with parsley.
Combine teriyaki sauce, soy sauce, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic powder, ginger, and sesame seeds in a large glass bowl.
Add salmon and pineapple to the marinade. Marinate in the refrigerator for 20 to 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Thread salmon, bell peppers, onion, and tomatoes onto skewers and place on the prepared baking sheet. Cover wooden skewer ends with aluminum foil.
Bake skewers in the preheated oven just until salmon flakes easily with a fork, about 10 minutes.
jalapeno peppers: Place clam juice and oregano in a saucepa, bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Add shrimp and stir just until shrimp turn pink and are not quite cooked through, about 2 minutes.
Transfer shrimp to a bowl with a slotted spoon. Reserve liquid. Let shrimp cool to room temperature.
Place chopped tomatoes, cucumbers, celery, red onion, and jalapenos is a mixing bowl.
Add ketchup, cooking liquid from the shrimp, lime juice, cilantro, and hot sauce. Gently mix until ingredients are well blended.
Chop shrimp into bite-size pieces, leaving a few whole for garnishes. Stir chopped pieces into the vegetable mixture.
Wrap mixing bowl with plastic wrap. Place reserved shrimp in a bowl, wrap with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate shrimp cocktail and reserved shrimp until thoroughly chilled, 2 to 3 hours.
If mixture seems a bit thick, add a little tomato juice or pickle juice. Stir in avocado cubes.
Serve, if possible, in clear glass bowls. Top with reserved whole shrimp and some cilantro leaves.
Preheat your air fryer to 370°F.
Cut the jalapeño peppers in half lengthwise and remove the seeds and membranes.
In a small bowl, mix together the cream cheese, shredded cheddar cheese, cooked and crumbled bacon (if using), chopped green onions (if using), salt, and pepper until well combined.
Fill each jalapeño half with the cream cheese mixture, dividing it evenly among the peppers.
Wrap each stuffed jalapeño half with a slice of bacon, securing it with a toothpick.
Place the bacon-wrapped jalapeño poppers in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
Lightly spray the jalapeño poppers with cooking spray.
Air fry at 370°F for 12-15 minutes, or until the bacon is crispy and the jalapeños are softened.
Once cooked, remove the jalapeño poppers from the air fryer and let them cool slightly before serving.
Serve the air-fried jalapeño poppers hot as a delicious appetizer or snack.
Preheat your grill or grill pan to 375°F.
Wash the jalapeño peppers and cut off the tops. Remove the seeds and membranes if you prefer milder heat; leave them in for spicier wraps.
Carefully stuff each jalapeño pepper with a slice of lean bacon.
Secure the bacon-wrapped jalapeños with toothpicks to hold them together.
Place the bacon-wrapped jalapeños on the preheated grill.
Grill for about 15-20 minutes, turning occasionally, until the bacon is crispy and the jalapeños are tender.
Once cooked, remove the Grilled Bacon Jalapeño Wraps from the grill, let them cool slightly, and remove the toothpicks.
Serve your Grilled Bacon Jalapeño Wraps as a tasty appetizer or snack.
Combine first 5 ingredients in a blender or food processor; process until smooth.
Place chicken in a zip-top plastic bag. Add broth mixture.
Seal and marinate in refrigerator 2 hours, turning bag occasionally.
Preheat grill. Remove chicken from bag, discard marinade.
Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper. Place chicken and onion on grill rack coated with cooking spray.
Grill onion 3 minutes on each side or until onions are browned. Grill chicken 6 minutes on each side or until done.Cool 5 minutes.
Cut chicken into very thin slices.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Place 1 tortilla on a baking sheet coated with cooking spray. Sprinkle with 3 tbsp cheese, half of chicken, half of onion, and 1½ tsp cilantro.
Top with 1 tortilla. Sprinkle with remaining 3 tbsp cheese, chicken, onion, and 1½ tsp cilantro. Top with remaining tortilla.
Coat tortilla with cooking spray. Bake at 400°F for 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Let stand 5 minutes.
Cut into 4 wedges. Top each serving with 1 tsp sour cream.
Garnish with cilantro sprigs and serve with lime wedges, if desired.
In a large pot, heat the oil over moderately low heat.
Add the shallots, ginger, and jalapeños; cook, stirring occasionally, for 3 minutes.
Add the broth and water; bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the zests and mushrooms; simmer 5 minutes longer.
Add the lime juice, fish sauce, and swordfish to the soup.
Cook until the fish is just done, about 2 minutes.
Serve sprinkled with the tomatoes and cilantro, if using.
Enjoy with rice noodles.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
Cut the jalapeño peppers in half lengthwise and remove the seeds and membranes.
In a small bowl, mix together the cream cheese, shredded cheddar cheese, and bacon crumbles until well combined.
Stuff each jalapeño half with the cheese and bacon mixture.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the stuffed jalapeños in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
Air fry for 10-12 minutes, until the peppers are tender and the filling is bubbly.
Remove the jalapeño poppers from the air fryer and let them cool slightly before serving.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Spray a baking dish with cooking spray.
Cook the rice in a pot of water accoriding to the instructions on the packaging.
Mix1 teaspoon lemon juice, 1 teaspoon lime juice, and 1/2 teaspoon chipotle powder in a large bowl.
Cover the bottom of the baking dish with the mixture and set aside.
Mix tomatoes, red onion, jalapeno peppers, habanero peppers, remaining lemon juice, remaining lime juice, and remaining chipotle powder together in a large bowl. Mix thoroughly.
Place salmon fillets on top of spinach in the baking dish and top with tomato mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven until fish flakes easily with a fork, about 14 minutes.
Preheat broiler to high.
Arrange tortilla chips on a nonstick baking sheet in a single layer but close together; set aside.
Coat a large nonstick skillet with cooking spray; heat over medium heat.
Add beef and cook until browned, breaking up meat as it cooks, about 3 to 4 minutes.
Season with oregano and salt; stir in tomatoes and beans.
Cook until heated through, stirring occasionally, about 3 to 5 minutes.
Spoon beef mixture over chips; sprinkle with cheese.
Broil until cheese melts, about 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Remove from broiler; sprinkle with scallions and jalapenos.
Serve with salsa and sour cream on the side.
Over medium-high heat, cook crumbled sausage in a large skillet until evenly browned.
Wipe down the skillet (but don't wash it).
Set sausage aside and leave the skillet on the heat for the tortillas.
Heat another skillet over medium heat while you whisk together the eggs and buttermilk in a medium bowl.
Pour them into the skillet and cook until almost firm.
Add the sausage crumbles to the eggs, cooking until the eggs are firm.
Using the sausage skillet, warm the tortillas for about 45 seconds per side.
After warming the first side, flip over the tortilla and add some shredded cheese to the tortilla.
After the cheese has melted a little, top it with the scrambled eggs and sausage mixture.
Then, remove from heat and add as many toppings as you like!
Enjoy!
Oil a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add carrot, onion, and jalapeno.
Simmer to your desired level of doneness, 5 to 10 minutes. Add cubed tofu.
Cook and stir until heated through, about 2 minutes. Add soy sauce, sesame seeds and garlic powder, cook about 5 minutes more.
Wrap tortillas in a moist paper towel and heat in the microwave on high until soft, about 30 seconds.
Spread a thin layer of vegan sour cream on each tortilla and top with lettuce. Add tofu and vegetable mixture and roll up, burrito-style.
In a large pot, heat the oil over moderately low heat. Add the shallots, ginger, and jalapenos. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 3 minutes.
Add the broth and water and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes. Stir in the zests and mushrooms; simmer 5 minutes longer.
Add the lemon juice, fish sauce, and seafood to the soup. Cook for about 5 minutes. Serve with cilantro.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Spray a baking sheet with cooking spray.
Fill each jalapeno pepper half with cream cheese, Cheddar cheese, and bread crumbs, and arrange peppers on the prepared baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven until jalapenos are tender and cheese melts, about 20 minutes. Sprinkle with red peppers flakes and serve immediately.

Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and reduce the heat to low. Cook and stir for 10 minutes. Add the peppers and cook and stir for 5 minutes more. Add the garlic and cook for about 1 minute.

Add the beef, breaking it up with a wooden spoon, and increase the heat to medium. Season with salt and pepper. Add the chili powder, cumin, red pepper flakes, and basil, and cook and stir until the beef is thoroughly browned.

Stir in the tomatoes, tomato paste, and broth, and bring to a low boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 45 minutes, until the chili reduces slightly and thickens. Add the beans and chickpeas and continue to simmer for about 15 minutes longer. Serve warm.
Combine tomatoes, onion, lime juice, cilantro, jalapeno pepper, habanero pepper, garlic, and ground black pepper in a food processor.
Pulse several times until desired texture is reached.
Chop the shrimp into ½ inch pieces and transfer to a bowl. Set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the lemon, lime and orange juice to combine. Pour ½ cup of the citrus juice over the shrimp and toss to combine.
Allow the shrimp to marinade in the juice for 15 minutes. Add the tomato, jalapeño, cilantro and red onion to the shrimp.
Toss the ingredients to combine and allow to marinade for an additional 10 minutes. Stir in the avocado and remaining juices.
Season the ceviche with salt and pepper, to taste. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 425°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper, set aside.
Pour everything into your blender, including peppers and vinegar. Blend it until liquefied.
Cut the tops off of your peppers and slice in half lengthwise. Pour the vinegar into a saucepan, add peppers, salt, and garlic. Cook 10 mintues.
For filling, in a medium bowl combine chicken, cheese, taco sauce and cumin, set aside.
Stack tortillas and wrap in damp paper towels. Microwave 40 seconds or until warm and softened.
Spread one-fourth of the filling onto the bottom third of each tortilla. Tightly roll up tortilla. Place, seam sides down, on the prepared baking sheet.
Bake 15 minutes or until edges of tortillas are starting to brown. Serve with the sauce.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Set jalapeno halves on a baking sheet lined with aluminum foil, open side up.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms, onion, and garlic to the skillet and saute for about 8 minutes.
Transfer vegetables to a paper towel-lined plate to soak up the excess moisture.
Mix mushroom mixture, nutritional yeast, cream cheese substitute, 3/4 cup shredded cheese substitute, salt, and pepper together in a bowl.
Spoon into the jalapeno pepper halves on the baking sheet. Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes
Remove from the oven and top with the remaining shredded cheese.
Broil until the cheese is melted and slightly browned, 1 to 2 minutes. Sprinkle with red pepper flakes.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook and stir ground beef, onion, 5 to 7 minutes.
Transfer meat mixture to a slow cooker, add tomato sauce, diced tomatoes, crushed tomatoes, and jalapenos.
Add Worcestershire, taco seasoning, cumin, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, salt, and pepper and stir to combine.
Cover and cook, adding water as needed to thin out chili, on Low for 8 hours. Serve and top with cheese and green onion.
In a small bowl, combine the coriander, cumin, dill and turmeric to make the spice mix. Set aside until needed.
In a large, deep skillet heat oil. Saute the onions for 2 minutes then add the garlic and jalapeno. Cook on medium-high, for 2 more minutes.
Now add the tomatoes and only ½ of the spice mix. Add the tomato paste, lime juice, water, salt and pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring to a high simmer, then turn the heat down to medium-low. Cover and cook the tomato mixture for 10 more minutes, stir occasionally.
Meanwhile, season the fish fillets lightly with salt and pepper and coat on both sides with the remaining ½ of the spice mix.
Gently add the fish fillets to the tomato mixture, nestling the fish pieces nicely in it. Cook on medium-high briefly, then reduce the heat to medium.
Cover and cook for another 10 minutes. Remove from heat and top with the fresh parsley. Serve immediately.
ricotta cheese: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the jumbo pasta shells according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a bowl, combine ricotta cheese, chopped fresh spinach, shredded mozzarella cheese, and grated Parmesan cheese. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Stuff each cooked pasta shell with the spinach and ricotta mixture.
Spread a thin layer of marinara sauce in a small ovenproof dish.
Place the stuffed shells in the dish.
Spoon more marinara sauce over the top of each stuffed shell.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the shells are heated through.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, mix the mixed seafood with marinara sauce, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Spread a small amount of marinara sauce on the bottom of an oven-safe dish.
Place one lasagna noodle at the bottom of the dish.
Spread half of the seafood mixture evenly over the noodle.
Add small dollops of ricotta cheese on top of the seafood mixture.
Sprinkle a portion of mozzarella and Parmesan cheese over the ricotta.
Place another lasagna noodle on top and repeat the layering: remaining seafood mixture, ricotta cheese, and a portion of mozzarella and Parmesan cheese.
Top with the third lasagna noodle and cover with the remaining marinara sauce and shredded mozzarella cheese.
Cover the dish with foil and bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 5-10 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and slightly golden.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Cook the lasagna noodles according to package instructions in a pot of salted boiling water. Drain and set aside.
In a small bowl, mix together the diced mixed vegetables, marinara sauce, salt, black pepper, and Italian seasoning.
Spread a thin layer of the vegetable mixture on the bottom of a small baking dish.
Place one cooked lasagna noodle over the vegetable mixture in the baking dish.
Spread half of the ricotta cheese over the lasagna noodle, followed by half of the remaining vegetable mixture.
Place the second cooked lasagna noodle over the vegetable mixture.
Spread the remaining ricotta cheese over the lasagna noodle, followed by the remaining vegetable mixture.
Sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese over the top.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 5 minutes to brown the cheese slightly.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
Garnish with fresh basil leaves, if desired, and serve.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the whole wheat ziti pasta according to the package instructions until it's al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a small oven-safe dish, add the cooked ziti pasta.
Stir in low-sodium tomato sauce, part-skim ricotta cheese, and season with dried oregano, salt, and black pepper.
Top the mixture with shredded part-skim mozzarella cheese.
Drizzle a little olive oil over the top.
Place the dish in the preheated oven and bake for about 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is bubbly and golden.
Serve your Baked Ziti hot.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Lightly grease a 6-inch pie dish with cooking spray.

Lay one sheet of phyllo dough in the prepared dish, allowing the edges to hang over the sides. Lightly brush the sheet with cooking spray or melted butter. Repeat with the remaining sheets, layering them and brushing each with cooking spray or melted butter.

In a mixing bowl, combine the diced ham, salami, provolone cheese, mozzarella cheese, Parmesan cheese, ricotta cheese, egg, dried oregano, dried basil, salt, black pepper, and diced tomatoes. Mix until well combined.
Spoon the filling mixture into the phyllo dough-lined dish, spreading it out evenly.
Fold the overhanging edges of the phyllo dough over the filling to create a rustic crust.
Bake the pizza rustica in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set.
Remove the pizza rustica from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
Bring large pot of salted water to boil. Add sheets and cook until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in large soup pot over medium heat. Add onion and cook until soft and translucent 5 minutes.
Add beef, garlic and oregano. Cook, breaking up meat with wooden spoon, until browned for about 3 minutes.
Add tomato paste and cook, stirring, until darkened for about 2 minutes.
Add tomatoes and chicken stock. Season with salt and red pepper flakes for taste. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir in cooked noodles, basil, Parmesan. Simmer for 2 minutes.
Divide soup among bowls. Top with ricotta and serve.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Slice the zucchini thinly lengthwise using a mandoline or a sharp knife.
In a bowl, combine raw ground beef, ricotta cheese, grated Parmesan, and shredded smoked mozzarella. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Lay out the zucchini slices and spread a thin layer of the beef and cheese mixture on each slice.
Roll up the zucchini slices into rolls.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the zucchini and beef rolls and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side or until the beef is browned.
In a baking dish, spread a thin layer of marinara sauce.
Place the partially cooked zucchini and beef rolls in the baking dish, seam side down.
Drizzle olive oil over the top of the rolls and sprinkle with dried oregano.
Cover the rolls with the remaining marinara sauce.
Bake in the preheated oven for an additional 15-20 minutes or until the zucchini is tender and the beef is cooked through.
Add the asparagus to a pot of boiling salted water over medium heat. Cook for 5 minutes. Quickly cool the asparagus with cold water, and pat dry. Set aside.
Beat the eggs and 1 tbsp of ricotta in a bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat the oil in a small skillet. Pour in the egg mixture and make sure it covers the entire surface by swirling the pan. Cook over medium-low heat until the edges are cooked through.
Add the remaining ricotta, asparagus, thyme leaves, and chives on one side of the omelet.
Using a non-stick spatula carefully flip the side with no filling over the top of the side with the filling. Cook for another minute.
Remove from the skillet and serve hot, sprinkled with grated Parmesan cheese.
Mix the eggs, milk, flour, spinach, ricotta, baking powder and salt in a blender.
Heat olive oil in a pan over high heat. Once the pan is hot add 1 scoop of the batter for each pancake. Flip pancakes after 2 minutes and cook for another 2 minutes.
While pancakes are cooking mix cream cheese and chives in a bowl. Add salt to taste.
Place pancakes on a plate and top them with cheese mixture and smoked salmon.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a skillet, cook the ground pork over medium heat until browned and cooked through. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Add marinara sauce to the cooked pork and stir until heated through.
Cook the lasagna noodles according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a small baking dish, layer one lasagna noodle at the bottom.
Spread half of the pork and marinara mixture over the lasagna noodle.
Dollop half of the ricotta cheese on top of the pork mixture.
Repeat the layers with the remaining lasagna noodle, pork and marinara mixture, and ricotta cheese.
Sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese evenly over the top layer.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until the cheese is golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Preheated the oven to 400°F.
Cook the spinach on medium heat on olive oil for 3 minutes until it is slightly wilted. Stir in the ricotta and cook for a further 1 minute. Allow to cool.
Cut slits into the chicken breasts about ½ inch apart but don’t cut all the way through. Stuff all of the spinach and ricotta mixture into the slits. Season the chicken with salt and pepper.
Grate the cheddar and sprinkle it generously on top. Bake in the oven for 25 minutes until the meat is done and cheese has melted.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Roll out the store-bought puff pastry sheet on a clean surface.
In a bowl, combine the chopped fresh spinach, ricotta cheese, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix everything together.
Spread the spinach and ricotta mixture evenly over the rolled-out puff pastry, leaving a border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the pastry over the filling, creating a rustic edge.
Brush the pastry with the beaten egg to give it a golden finish.
Place the pastry on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is puffed and golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the lasagna noodles according to package instructions until they are al dente. Drain and set them aside.
In a bowl, combine chopped fresh spinach and low-fat ricotta cheese. Season with salt and black pepper.
Lay out one of the cooked lasagna noodles on a flat surface.
Spread the spinach and ricotta mixture evenly over the noodle.
Roll up the noodle with the filling inside, creating a rotolo.
Grease a small ovenproof dish with olive oil.
Place the rotolo in the dish.
Pour marinara sauce over the rotolo and sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese on top.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Cook the gnocchi according to package instructions until they float to the surface. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced bell peppers to the skillet. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the diced cherry tomatoes to the skillet and cook for another 2-3 minutes until they begin to release their juices.
Stir in the fresh spinach leaves and cook until wilted.
Add the cooked gnocchi to the skillet, along with the garlic powder, lemon zest, and lemon juice.
Pour the heavy cream into the skillet and toss everything together until well combined.
Cook for another 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and coats the gnocchi and vegetables.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove the skillet from heat and stir in the ricotta cheese until incorporated.
Preheat oven to 190°F.
Cook the lasagna sheets in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Lightly grease an ovenproof dish. Pour in 1 cup marinara sauce, spreading evenly over base.
Place ricotta, spinach, parsley, basil, parmesan, salt and pepper in a large bowl, and mix to combine.
Cut each lasagne sheet in half crosswise. Place 1/3 cup of ricotta mixture along length of each lasagne sheet and roll up to form a tube. The mixture should make 8 tubes.
Lay tubes side by side in the dish. Spoon over remaining marinara to coat. Sprinkle with cheeses and bake for 25 minutes or until golden.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a skillet over medium heat, sauté chopped chard, red onion, and minced garlic in olive oil until the chard is wilted and the onion is softened. Set aside to cool.
In a bowl, mix together feta cheese, ricotta cheese, Parmesan cheese, beaten egg, dried dill, salt, and pepper.
Combine the chard mixture with the cheese mixture. Mix well.
Lay one sheet of phyllo dough on a clean surface. Spray lightly with cooking spray.
Place another sheet of phyllo dough on top and spray again. Repeat until you have four layers.
Spoon the chard and cheese mixture onto one end of the phyllo dough.
Roll the phyllo dough over the filling to form a log.
Place the log on a baking sheet, seam side down.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes or until the phyllo is golden brown and crispy.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Cook the lasagna noodles according to package instructions, then drain and set aside.
In a small bowl, mix together the shredded cooked chicken breast, ricotta cheese, shredded mozzarella cheese, grated Parmesan cheese, chopped spinach, marinara sauce, salt, and pepper.
Place one cooked lasagna noodle on a flat surface. Spread half of the chicken and cheese mixture evenly over the noodle.
Roll the lasagna noodle tightly to form a roll-up.
Repeat with the remaining lasagna noodle and chicken and cheese mixture.
Place the roll-ups in a small baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until heated through and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
Garnish with Italian seasoning, if desired.
Preheat your oven to 425°F.
Place the pizza crust on a baking sheet or pizza stone.

Spread the ricotta cheese evenly over the pizza crust. Scatter the torn ham pieces over the ricotta cheese.

Arrange the asparagus pieces on top of the ham.
Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella cheese over the toppings.
Drizzle a little olive oil over the pizza, and season with salt and pepper to taste. Add red pepper flakes if you like some heat.
Transfer the pizza to the preheated oven and bake for about 12-15 minutes or until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbly and slightly browned.
Spread some butter on the baguette slices and toast them in a dry pan over low heat.
In a small mixing bowl, combine the ricotta cheese, lemon zest and honey, mix well.
Spread the ricotta mixture on to the toasted bread and enjoy alongside fruit.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the penne pasta according to package instructions until it's al dente. Drain and set aside.

In a bowl, mix together the cooked penne pasta, marinara sauce, fresh spinach leaves, ricotta cheese, and season with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes if desired. Ensure all ingredients are well combined.
Transfer the mixture into a small baking dish or ramekin.
Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella cheese over the top of the pasta mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven for approximately 15-20 minutes or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Once done, remove from the oven and let it cool for a couple of minutes before serving.
coconut flour: Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper. Line a second cookie sheet with several layers of paper towels.
Mix coconut flour, onion powder, and garlic powder in a small bowl. Whisk eggs thoroughly in a second bowl.
Grind macadamia nuts in a food processor until coarsely chopped. Empty ground nuts into a third bowl, mix in coconut until blended.
Place each shrimp in the coconut flour mixture, lightly coating on all sides.
Then dip in the egg, and finally in the macadamia-coconut mixture. Place on the parchment-lined cookie sheet, repeat with remaining shrimp.
Heat ghee in a large skillet over medium heat until melted, add shrimp. Cook, turning often, until golden brown and evenly cooked.
Drain shrimp on paper towels. Season with salt and pepper and serve.
In a small bowl, combine the egg, coconut flour, psyllium husk powder and salt.
Over low heat, melt the butter and coconut cream. Slowly whisk in the egg mixture, combining until you achieve a creamy, thick texture.
Serve with coconut milk or cream. Top your porridge with blueberries and serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Chop the potatoes into wedges. Add them into baking dish along with the oil, spices, and some salt and pepper.
Bake for 30 minutes, garnish with some coconut flour and serve.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the canned pumpkin puree, almond flour, coconut flour, baking powder, ground cinnamon, ground nutmeg, ground ginger, and a pinch of salt. Mix well.
Add the vanilla extract and crack in the egg. Stir until all the ingredients are fully combined and you have a smooth batter.
Line a muffin tin with a single muffin liner or use a silicone muffin cup.
Pour the muffin batter into the liner, filling it about two-thirds full.
Place the muffin tin in the preheated oven and bake for 18-20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of a muffin comes out clean.
Remove the muffin from the oven and allow it to cool for a few minutes before enjoying.
In a large bowl, whisk together all ingredients.
Let batter sit for 10 minutes to allow liquids to absorb. Grease and heat your griddle to low-medium heat.
Using a 1 tablespoon measuring spoon, scoop batter onto griddle. Cook for 3-4 minutes, flipping when edges look dry. Cook for another 1-2 minutes on the other side.
Serve warm with blueberries, fresh fruit.
Combine banana, pineapple, milk, protein powder, and lemon juice in a blender.
Puree until smooth and pour the smoothie into bowls.
Decorate each bowl with some blueberries, mint leaves and coconut flour.
Preheat the oven to 425°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place the avocado slices on a plate and sprinkle the avocados with lemon juice. This keeps the avocados from turning bitter after cooking.
In a bowl, add coconut flour. In another bowl, whisk the eggs. In the last bowl, stir together pork panko, garlic powder, paprika, and sea salt.
Dredge an avocado slice in coconut flour, shake off excess, then dip in the egg wash. Finally, dredge in the pork rinds.
Place avocado slices on the parchment-lined baking sheet in a single layer and bake for 10 minutes.
Mix almond flour, coconut flour, salt, baking powder, and cinnamon together in a bowl.
Whisk in eggs, heavy cream, ghee, and vanilla extract slowly until batter is just blended.
Heat a lightly oiled griddle over medium-high heat. Drop batter by large spoonfuls onto the griddle and cook until bubbles form and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes.
Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 2 to 3 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.
In a large bowl, whisk to combine Greek yogurt, egg yolks, maple syrup, and flaxseed oil. Add coconut flour, baking soda, and salt, and gently fold until just combined.
In a separate large bowl using a hand or stand mixer, whip egg whites until stiff peaks form, 4 to 5 minutes.
Gently fold whipped egg whites into batter until evenly combined.
Grease a large nonstick pan with coconut oil and place over medium-low heat. Pour about 2 tablespoons of pancake batter onto the pan, spreading it out evenly.
Cook until both sides are lightly golden. Serve with sliced banana.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and oil.
Pour coconut milk into a bowl and stir in apple cider vinegar. Let sit until thickened, about 5 minutes.
Add onions, soak until 'buttermilk' clings to onions, about 5 minutes.
Mix oat flour, almond flour, sesame seeds, salt, garlic powder, paprika, and pepper together in a shallow dish.
Take onion rings out of the buttermilk one by one with a fork and drop on top of the cornmeal mixture.
Spoon mixture over rings. Lift coated rings onto the sprayed foil and space them apart.
Bake in the preheated oven for 8 to 10 minutes. Flip rings over and bake until dark golden, about 5 minutes more.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Sprinkle shrimp with salt and pepper.
Stir together coconut flour, garlic powder, and onion powder in a shallow dish. Thoroughly whisk eggs and water in a second shallow dish.
Dip each shrimp in coconut flour mixture, lightly coating all sides.
Heat 2 tbsp butter and cook, turning often, until shrimp are golden brown and opaque, 5 minutes total.
Transfer shrimp to a paper towel-lined plate to drain. Serve immediately.
Place the shredded coconut in a small skillet and toast on low medium heat stirring or tossing for 5 minutes until golden brown and evenly toasted.
Place apricots, cashews, toasted coconut, hemp seeds, and vanilla extract in the bowl of a food processor and blend until sticky dough forms.
Divide the apricot mixture into a few parts and roll into walnut-sized balls.
Roll the balls in the coconut flour and serve.
Combine coconut flour, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
Whisk together almond milk, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla extract in a separate bowl.
Fold egg mixture into the flour mixture and mix to combine.
Grease a large skillet with coconut oil and heat over medium-high heat. Pour 3 tablespoons of the batter at a time into the hot skillet, making circles.
Cook pancakes until the bottoms are golden brown, about 2 minutes. Carefully flip and cook on the other side until golden.
Repeat with the remaining batter, greasing the pan as needed between batches. Serve with blueberries and drizzle with lemon juice.
In a food processor, add all your ingredients, and pulse until a thick batter remains.
Transfer your pancake batter into a bowl and let sit for 10 minutes to thicken. If the batter is too thick, add a little extra almond milk.
Pre-heat your non-stick pan on medium heat, and spray it lightly with cooking spray. Once hot, pour scant 1/4 cup portions of batter.
Cover the pan and allow to cook for 3 minutes, until the edges bubble and are slightly golden. Flip, cover and repeat for 3 minutes. Serve.
Preheat the ovn to 350°F.
In a small skillet, over medium heat saute onions and half of the garlic in half of the butter until soft, roughly 3 minutes. Set aside and allow to cool.
In a large bowl, combine the salmon, mustard, chives, egg, coconut flour and salt with cooked onion mixture and mix well.
Form into meatballsand bake for 2 minutes or until cooked through as desired.
While meatballs are baking, make your cream sauce. In a large skillet over medium heat, saute remaining garlic in the remaining butter until soft, roughly 3 minutes.
Add lemon juice and whisk until combined. The whisk in heavy cream. Simmer until desired thickness is achieved, stirring frequently.
Remove cooked meatballs from the oven and place in the sauce. Serve with cream sauce.
In a medium mixing bowl, stir together the almond flour, coconut flour, coconut, flax, cinnamon, nutmeg, baking soda and salt.
Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl with an electric hand mixer or using a stand mixer, beat together the oil, almond butter and vanilla extract at medium speed until well combined, about 1 minute.
Beat in the egg on low and mix until well incorporated.
Stir in the flour mixture until well combined.
Then stir in the raisins.
Refrigerate for at least 30-60 minutes or until the dough is firm and easy to roll into balls.
Move the oven rack to the bottom position in the oven.
Preheat the oven to 350 °F and line a baking sheet with a piece of parchment paper.
If you use the Nordic Ware Insulated Baking Sheet, you don't need to use parchment paper.
Roll the dough into 8 balls.
You can also roll them into smaller balls but then you need to reduce the baking time.
Place 4 inches apart on the prepared baking sheet.
Press the cookies down lightly with the palm of your hand.
Bake for 14-18 minutes or until the surface of the center of the cookies no longer appears wet.
They'll be very soft but will continue to cook as they sit on the cookie sheet.
Let cool completely on the baking sheet.
Store in an airtight container for up to 3 days.
Combine sliced almonds, toasted coconut and ¼ teaspoon salt in a shallow dish. Set aside.
Pit then coarsely chop the dates. Place them along with flours in the food processor, and pulse to desired texture.
Add almond butter, maple syrup and the remaining ¼ teaspoon salt.

Process, scraping sides as necessary, until the mixture is evenly mixed. Check to see if the mixture will come together by squeezing a bit of the dough. If it doesn’t, add one to two more teaspoons maple syrup and pulse again.
Form into 12 balls. Roll balls in the almond and coconut mixture.
Store in a glass storage container and refrigerate up to 5 days.
Whisk and stir all ingredients under “Paleo Crepes” in a large mixing bowl until there are no lumps.
Let the batter sit for 5 minutes.
Heat 1 tsp ghee in a cast iron skillet or non-stick skillet over medium heat.
When hot, lower the heat to medium-low, whisk the batter again for an additional 10 seconds.
Pour ¼ cup of the batter into the pan.
Quickly and gently swirl the pan around so that the batter coats the entire pan evenly.
Cook about 1-minute or until the edges start to crisp.
Carefully flip and cook the flip side about 30 seconds.
Transfer to a cooling rack and repeat with the remaining batter.
Combine/stir almond butter, cacao powder, coconut milk, and pinch of fine sea salt until creamy and smoothly.
Taste and adjust to your own liking.
Preheat air fryer to 380°F.
In a shallow bowl, combine coconut flour, salt and pepper. In a second shallow bowl, add the egg whites and whisk together until bubbles form.
In a third shallow bowl, add the coconut. Dredge shrimp in coconut flour mixture, then dip in egg whites, and then coat with coconut.
Spray air fryer with coconut oil cooking spray. Add shrimp to air fryer basket and then spray each shrimp generously with more coconut oil.
Bake for 10 minutes. While shrimp are cooking, prepare sauce by combining the remaining ingredients in a small bowl and mix together.
Serve shrimp with the sauce immediately.
Preheat the oven to 350 ºF (175 ºC).
Prepare a loaf pan by lining with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, combine the coconut flour, baking soda, baking powder and cinnamon. Whisk to combine.
In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs. Add the mashed banana, mixing until smooth, then whisk in the coconut oil and vanilla. Add the banana mixture to the flour mixture, stirring until well mixed.
Scrape the batter into the pan and smooth the top. Bake for 35 minutes.
flax seed: Place all ingredients in a blender and puree until smooth. Serve immediately.
Cut avocado in half and remove the pit. Use a spoon to scoop out the avocado and place in blender.
Add yogurt, strawberries, juice, vanilla extract, flax seed and protein powder to the blender. Blend on high until smooth.
Serve immediately.
Stir flour, soy milk, egg, flax seeds, baking powder and vanilla extract together in a bowl.
Heat a lightly oiled griddle over medium-high heat. Drop batter by large spoonfuls onto the griddle and cook until bubbles form and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes.
Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 2 to 3 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.
Serve sprinkled with chopped walnuts and lemon juice.
In a small saucepan, bring water and salt to a boil. Stir in oats, cook 5 minutes over medium heat, stirring occasionally.
Divide the oatmeal between 2 bowls. Stir in cinnamon, honey and flaxseed. Add peanut butter, blueberries, banana, almonds and chia seeds.
Stir oats, chia seeds, pepitas, pineapple, peanut butter, honey, coconut, mango, blueberries, flax, and wheat germ together in a large bowl. Roll into balls.
Combine blueberries, coconut milk, green apple, mint,  flaxseeds, vanilla extract in a blender, blend until smooth, about 1 minute.
Mix all ingredients together in a high-speed blender until smooth.
Bring a large pot of lightly salter water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.

Process chopped walnuts, toasted flaxseeds, garlic cloves, salt, and black pepper in a food processor 1 minute. Add parsley, half of the basil, Parmesan cheese and lemon juice. Process until smooth. With processor running, gradually add olive oil and water and process until combined.
Mix pesto with the cooked pasta and serve warm, topped with the remaining basil.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a large bowl add the onion, carrot and garlic and mix well.
Add everything else and stir together really well making sure all the spices are distributed well.
You should have a really thick batter that is sticky but that can be squeezed together in your hands. Add water gradually, stirring as you go.
Roll into tight walnut sized balls and place on the prepared baking tray then bake for 25 minutes.
Add the peach, apple, water, and cinnamon stick in a pot and bring to a boil. Turn heat down to medium-low and cook for 20 minutes. Remove the cinnamon stick.
Pour the fruit and its liquid into a mixer and add protein powder and flax seed and blend.
Serve cooled.
Mix oats, almond butter, honey, flax seeds, and chia seeds together in a bowl, refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Scoop mixture into balls and place on a plate or baking sheet, store in refrigerator.
In a mason jar, plastic container or bowl, layer oats, milk, seeds, yogurt, honey, peanut butter and banana.
Stir to combine and refrigerate overnight.
In the morning, serve cold or heat in the microwave for a warm oatmeal.
In a medium mixing bowl, stir together the almond flour, coconut flour, coconut, flax, cinnamon, nutmeg, baking soda and salt.
Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl with an electric hand mixer or using a stand mixer, beat together the oil, almond butter and vanilla extract at medium speed until well combined, about 1 minute.
Beat in the egg on low and mix until well incorporated.
Stir in the flour mixture until well combined.
Then stir in the raisins.
Refrigerate for at least 30-60 minutes or until the dough is firm and easy to roll into balls.
Move the oven rack to the bottom position in the oven.
Preheat the oven to 350 °F and line a baking sheet with a piece of parchment paper.
If you use the Nordic Ware Insulated Baking Sheet, you don't need to use parchment paper.
Roll the dough into 8 balls.
You can also roll them into smaller balls but then you need to reduce the baking time.
Place 4 inches apart on the prepared baking sheet.
Press the cookies down lightly with the palm of your hand.
Bake for 14-18 minutes or until the surface of the center of the cookies no longer appears wet.
They'll be very soft but will continue to cook as they sit on the cookie sheet.
Let cool completely on the baking sheet.
Store in an airtight container for up to 3 days.
Preheat the oven to 350°F and line an 8x8-inch baking pan with parchment paper.
Lightly spray the exposed pan sides with nonstick cooking spray.
Make the fruit filling: In a medium bowl, mix together the strawberries, rhubarb, lemon juice and vanilla.
Make the crumble: In a food processor, combine the oats, nuts, almond flour, flaxseed, cinnamon, and salt and pulse until just combined.
Add the oil and pulse again. Add the water and pulse again.
Press ⅔ of the crumble into the baking pan to form a crust. Bake 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown and firm.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 15 minutes.
Spread the fruit filling over the crust, sprinkle with the remaining crumble, and garnish with some rolled oats.
Bake for an additional 20 minutes, or until the fruit is soft and the crumble topping is lightly crisp. Let cool completely before slicing.
If not serving the same day, store the bars covered in the refrigerator.
In a bowl, mix together all the ingredients (leave some berries to garnish) until you get a smooth batter.
Preheat and lightly grease a frying pan.
Take a ladle half full of batter and pour it in the middle of the pan, spreading very lightly.
Cook for 1 minute then add on a lid to make the pancakes fluffy.
Cook for another minute then flip and leave for about 15 seconds.
Repeat until you've used all the batter.
Top with berries to serve.
Mix together ground flax and water and set aside to thicken. Add potatoes to a pot of water and bring to a boil. Cook for about 10 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Add onions and carrots to a pan over medium heat with a splash of oil, and cook until onions turn translucent.
Add green onion and mushrooms and season with salt and pepper. Stir and turn off heat.
Add white beans to a large bowl and use a fork or potato masher to mash the beans. Add potatoes to the bowl and mash, again leaving some chunks for texture.
Add in veggies, vegan cheese, and flax mixture. Mix until well combined. Mix together milk and arrowroot powder. Form the potato mixture into patties.
Roll each patty in flour, dip in plant milk mixture, then coat with panko. Brush the korokke with oil. Place in the air fryer.
Air fry at 350°F for 15 minutes. Serve immediately.
Mix tahini and vanilla until smooth. Add remaining ingredients and mix well. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Remove from refrigerator and roll into 1 inch balls, place evenly on parchment covered cookie sheet.
Freeze for 30 minutes before serving.
Preheat the oven to 300°F.
Separate the egg yolks from the egg whites, placing each into two separate, large bowls. Add salt to the egg whites and whisk until it becomes very stiff.
Mix the egg yolks and cream cheese together with the electric mixer, and then stir in the ground flax seed and baking powder until well combined. Gently fold the egg whites in the egg yolk mix.
For each serving, place 3 equal mounds of batter on the parchment-lined baking tray. Then bake on the middle rack for about 25 minutes, until golden. Set aside, and let the bread cool on the tray.
Change the oven heat to 350°F.
Mix together the tuna, pickles, sour cream and lemon juice to create a salad mixture.
Take the baking tray with the cooled "bread", and spread the tuna salad on top of each slice. Sprinkle with cheese on top and bake for 15 minutes.
In a large bowl, place the cucumbers, tomatoes, red onions, green peppers, cilantro and parsley.
In a small bowl or jar, whisk together the olive oil, flax seeds, lime juice, salt and pepper until well combined.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine. Serve immediately.
Blend all of the ingredients in a blender until smooth. Pour the shake into glasses and serve.
flax seeds: Toss the tomatoes, cucumbers, and lemon juice in a bowl with 2 tsp of the salt. Set aside.
Put the couscous into a large bowl. Bring the water, 2 tbsp of olive oil, flax seeds, the remaining salt, cayenne, and herb stems to a boil over high heat.
Pour the liquid over the couscous, stir to separate any clumps. Cover with a lid, set aside for 5 minutes. Remove the herb stems and fluff with a fork.
Add the tomato mixture, scallions, mint, parsley, and pine nuts. Drizzle the remaining oil over the salad, toss and serve.
Layer apple, pineapple, cashew butter, coconut, flax meal, vanilla extract, and cinnamon into a blender; add almond milk. Blend mixture until smooth.
Layer orange, mango, cashew butter, coconut, chia seeds, and flax into a blender, add orange juice.
Cover and blend mixture until smooth, adding water for a thinner smoothie.
In a blender, combine bananas, flax seeds, cashew butter, milk, coconut flakes and some ice. Blend well. Add more ice and blend. Serve immediately.
Grate the zucchini into a bowl or colander that is prepared with a clean dish towel. Add a pinch of salt, and leave for 10 minutes. Drain the zucchini.
Mix water with flax seeds in a small bowl. Let the mixture thicken for 5 minutes.
Mix the dry ingredients together. Add the chia mixture, almond milk and olive oil and mix again with a whisk.
Add zucchini, corn , jalapeño, garlic, scallions and parsley. Sprinkle with lime juice. Mix it all together.
Heat up a pan to medium heat and place oil into the pan. Portion out one heaped tbsp of batter and cook in batches over medium heat for 5 minutes per side.
Serve immediately.
Combine almond milk, oats, banana, hemp seeds, chia seeds, ground flax seeds, pear, cinnamon, sea salt, and vanilla extract in a pot, mix until well combined with a wooden spoon.
Cook over low heat, stirring frequently, 20 to 30 minutes.
Pour almond or coconut milk into a blender and add chia seeds to allow the chia seeds to soak for 5 minutes.
Add all remaining ingredients and blend until chunks are liquified and the smoothie is the consistency you desire. Pour into large glasses and enjoy.
Add avocado, sour cream, cilantro, lime juice, a large pinch of salt, and a splash of water to a food processor. Process until it forms a smooth, creamy consistency.
Garnish with flax seeds. Serve with rice cakes.
Add some oil to a large saucepan placed over a medium heat and cook the onion and carrot for 10 minutes. Add the garlic and cook another minute.
Add the tomatoes, tomato paste, basil, and broth. Season to taste with and salt and pepper and stir everything together. Add seeds and stir.
Bring to a boil then lower the heat. Simmer, uncovered, for 30 minutes. Use an immersion blender and blend the soup until smooth. Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a pan and add mushrooms. Add soy sauce. Cook for 6 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Add the cooked rice and parsley and mix well.
To serve, place the rice mixture into a bowl and top with eggs and avocado. Sprinkle with flax seeds.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Mix the ground flax and water in a small bowl and set aside for 5 minutes to thicken.
Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a large skillet. Add chopped onion and saute for 2 minutes. Add mushrooms, thyme, salt, and pepper. Saute for 10 minutes and cover with a lid.
Remove from heat. Add flax mixture and yeast and mix all ingredients together. Add chopped dill, hemp seeds and protein powder.
Grease an oven tray with some oil. Add patties and cook for 20 minutes. Flip them on the other side after 15 minutes. Serve immediately.
Place kale, banana, soy milk, flax seeds into a blender. Cover and purée until smooth. Serve over ice.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Add the dry ingredients to a bowl and mix. Squeeze out excess moisture from zucchini and add it another bowl with the remaining wet ingredients and mix well.
Pour the dry ingredients into the bowl of wet and mix well. Distribute the batter between muffin cups and bake for 15 minutes.
Allow muffins to cool for 10 minutes in the pan, before removing them and cooling on a wire rack. Serve cooled.
Place all ingredients in a blender. Mix well for one minute or until smooth. If the smoothie is too thick, add water. Serve cooled.
Rinse quinoa in a fine strainer with cold water to remove bitterness until water runs clear and is no longer frothy.
Heat milk in a saucepan over medium heat until warm, 2 to 3 minutes. Stir quinoa and salt into the milk, cook for about 20 minutes.
Remove saucepan from heat. Stir maple syrup and lemon peel into the quinoa mixture. Gently fold blueberries into the mixture.
Add quinoa into a bowl top with flaxseed and almonds.
Place all of the ingredients in your blender. Blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Heat the walnuts and seeds in a dry frying pan over a medium heat for 2 minutes until slightly browned and toasted.
Put the walnuts, seeds and yeast in a blender. Blend for 1 minute. Tear up the kale leaves and add to the blender. Blend until the kale is evenly chopped.
With the blender running, add the oil. Add the lemon juice and season to taste. Set aside.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a pan and add the mushrooms. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionaly. Add the pesto and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes. Add pasta and mix well.
Serve immediately.
Add the rinsed groats along with other ingredients into blender and blend until smooth and homogeneous batter forms.
Take a non-stick pan. Add a few drops of oil onto the pan and swipe it off with folded kitchen paper. Let all the oil absorb into the paper and use it to swipe the pan clean between pancakes.
Put a ladleful of batter onto the pan and even it out with a ladle to get the exact shape of your pan bottom. Cook 2 minutes then flip and repeat.
Serve topped with strawberry jam.
Put the oats and milk in a saucepan. Bring it all to a boil, stirring, then remove from heat, cover and let sit for 5 minutes.
Mix in the yogurt, flax seeds and cinnamon and mix well. To serve, top with walnuts and raisins.
Add the oil to a pan and when it's semi hot, add the garlic, onion, seeds and chili. Add the minced pork after 3 minutes.
Cook for 5 minutes, then add the soy sauce and cook for 5 more minutes. Serve topped with chives.
white balsamic vinegar: Whisk together in a small bowl 2 teaspoon extra virgin olive oil, white balsamic vinegar, Dijon mustard & salt/pepper
To a nonstick pan add 1 teaspoon extra virgin olive oil & heat over medium-high heat.
Saute bacon 3-4 minutes.
Add asparagus & red onion cooking another 2-3 minutes until onion is tender.
In a bowl, add greens & top with dressing combining well.
Top with cooked asparagus, onion & bacon.
Add eggs to pan & cook as desired.
To the salad add your cooked eggs and serve with bread.
Feast!
Rinse and halve peppers, remove seeds and ribs, and cut into strips.
Peel and chop garlic and shallots. Rinse basil and shake dry. Cut the leaves from one sprig into thin strips. Strip leaves from remaining sprigs.
Heat olive oil in a pan. Add half of the garlic and all of the shallots and cook until translucent. Add peppers and cook a few minutes more.
Deglaze with chicken broth and bring to a boil. Cook about 10 minutes over low heat. Stir in basil leaves and season with salt and pepper.
Rinse cod fillets and place side by side in a steamer insert. Sprinkle with the remaining garlic and the basil strips.
Place in a pot with a small amount of water. Bring to a boil. Cover and steam 12-15 minutes.
Stir vinegar with pepper mixture, bring to a boil and divide among deep plates or bowls. Top with fish and garnish with garlic and basil.
Season chicken breasts with salt. Cut each breast into 3 to 4 pieces. Place in a large bowl, stir in garlic.
Add olive oil, apple cider vinegar, red wine vinegar, white balsamic vinegar, lemon juice, red pepper flakes, allspice, sumac,. Mix well with your hands.
