Cover chicken mixture with plastic wrap, pressing against the surface to remove any air. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours and up to overnight.
Preheat an outdoor grill and 2 grill baskets with the cover closed for 10 minutes.
Grill until golden brown, 10 to 15 minutes per side.
Combine all ingredients in a big bowl. Taste and add more salt and pepper if needed.
Serve immediately, topped with vinegar and oil.
Whisk the olive oil, balsamic vinegar and mustard in a small bowl until emulsified. Add salt and poppy seeds, stir in salt and poppyseeds. Set aside.
Mix cucumbers and watermelon. Sprinkle with feta.
Make a stack out of the basil leaves. Roll them in either direction and thinly slice them to make ribbons. Toss evenly over the salad.
Drizzle with dressing. Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add the salt and edamame and boil for 3 minutes, then drain.
Blend edamame, cucumber, onion, avocado, jalapeno pepper, garlic, white balsamic vinegar, lime juice, salt, and black pepper in a blender until smooth.
Chill before serving.
Preheat the oven to 450°F.

Line a 15x10x1-inch baking pan with foil and set aside. In a large bowl combine pumpkin chunks, 2 tablespoons olive oil, salt, and pepper, toss to combine. Spread in the prepared baking pan. Roast for 35 minutes. Cool to room temperature.
Meanwhile, arrange arugula on a large serving platter or bowl. Top with cooled pumpkin and cheese.
For dressing, in a small bowl whisk together the remaining olive oil, the vinegar, and maple syrup. Drizzle dressing evenly over the salad. Toss gently to mix.
Cook green beans in boiling water 4 minutes or until crisp-tender. Drain and rinse with cold water.
Combine oil, lemon juice, balsamic vinegar, water, garlic, mustard, honey ¼ teaspoon salt in a bowl; stir with a whisk.
Stir in beans, chickpeas, and tomatoes and toss. Then sprinkle with walnuts and cheese.
Sprinkle tuna with remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt and pepper. Heat a grill pan over medium-high heat and add tuna; cook for 3 minutes on each side or until desired degree of doneness.
Divide bean mixture evenly among 4 plates; top with sliced tuna.
pork ribs: Season the pork ribs with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the pork ribs to the skillet and sear on all sides until browned.
Pour broth into the skillet.
Sprinkle smoked paprika, garlic powder, and onion powder over the pork ribs.
Cover the skillet and reduce heat to low. Simmer for about 30-40 minutes, or until the pork ribs are tender and cooked through, adding more broth if needed to prevent sticking.
Once the pork ribs are cooked, remove them from the skillet and let them rest for a few minutes.
Heat a cast iron pan over medium-high heat. Add oil, ginger and pork rib tips, in that order, to the hot pan.
Stir-fry ribs until the surface of the ribs turns golden on all sides, about 5 minutes
Pour enough boiling water on top of the ribs so the ribs are just covered with water. Cover with a lid and cook for 10 minutes.
Remove lid and keep cooking until the water is almost absorbed, 5 to 20 minutes more.
Be careful not to burn the ribs. Add vinegar, and soy sauce. Stir until well combined and cook until sauce thickens, 1 to 2 minutes.
Remove membrane from back of ribs or score with a sharp knife. Place ribs in a shallow dish and season with salt and pepper.
Puree onion in a blender or food processor. Add kochujang, vinegar, garlic, sesame oil, soy sauce, and minced ginger, puree into a sauce.
Rub 1/3 generously over ribs, reserving the rest of the sauce. Cover ribs with plastic wrap and refrigerate 5 hours to overnight.
Preheat the oven to 325°F.
Scatter sliced ginger root over the bottom of a roasting pan. Place ribs meat-side down on top of ginger slices and pour lager over ribs. Cover with a lid or aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven until meat is loosened from the ribs but not yet falling off the bone, 1 to 1 1/2 hours. Let cool, 5 to 10 minutes.
Preheat an outdoor grill and lightly oil the grate. Place ribs on the grill and cook until browned, about 6 minutes per side.
Coat with 1/2 the reserved sauce during the last 2 minutes of cooking each side. Garnish with white and black sesame seeds.
Combine kosher salt, paprika, onion powder, garlic powder, ginger, and pepper in a small bowl.
Place one layer of aluminum foil on a baking sheet, leaving about a 2-inch overhang on all sides.
Pat the ribs dry using a paper towel and rub spice mixture into both sides of the ribs.
Place rib racks, meaty side up, on the prepared baking sheet. Cover with more aluminum foil, and tightly crimp the edges. Refrigirate for 2 hours.
Preheat the oven to 225°F.
Place the covered baking sheet on the center rack of the oven and bake for 4 hours. Remove from the oven and rest ribs for 10 minutes.
Place wood chips in a bowl and cover with water. Preheat an outdoor grill for medium heat.
Drain wood chips and place in an aluminum foil packet, poke several holes in the packet. Place the wood chip packet on the hottest part of the grill and close the lid.
When the chips are smoking, oil the grate where the rib racks will go. Remove ribs from foil and place rib racks on the oiled grate.
Brush with 1/2 the barbecue sauce. Close the lid and grill for 5 to 10 minutes, monitoring closely for flare-ups.
Turn ribs and baste the other side with the remaining barbecue sauce.
Close the lid and grill, again monitoring for flare-ups, for an additional 5 to 10 minutes.
Keep turning and basting until the ribs reach your desired color. Slice rib racks into portions and serve with additional barbecue sauce.
Preheat your oven to 225 degrees F.
Start by removing the silver skin on the pork loin back ribs. Dig your finger under the middle of the silverskin, and then pull it back.
Season ribs with salt, pepper, garlic powder. Put the ribs into a large baking pan and cover it with foil. Bake them for 2 hours in the oven.
Turn your oven up to 250 degrees F and continue cooking for 1 hour more.
Mix together ketchup, chili sauce, cumin and cayenne pepper to make barbecue sauce.
Brush your ribs well with the BBQ sauce and serve,
Blanch pork ribs in a small pot of boiling water to remove scum. Remove pork ribs from pot and set aside.
Combine all ingredients in a soup pot and bring to a boil for 10 minutes. Then simmer on the lowest flame for 2 hours.
Season with salt to taste and serve hot.
butter beans: Combine the tomatoes, tomato purée, cumin, paprika, and cayenne pepper in a large pan. Bring to a boil then reduce to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the beans and cook, stirring, for 5 minutes until the beans are heated through. Remove from the heat and stir in the lemon zest. Season to taste.
Serve the beans with the toast, garnishing with parsley and cheese.
Blend (using a hand blender) the beans, garlic, lemon juice, cumin and tahini till smooth. Add salt to taste.
Pour into a serving dish and scatter with chives before serving.
Place pasta sheets in a large bowl or baking dish and cover with hot water.
Allow to soak, agitating them occasionally, until malleable, at least 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, place dried mushrooms in a 2 quart liquid measure and cover with 1 1/2 quarts hot water.
Microwave on high power until simmering, about 2 minutes.
Set aside and allow mushrooms to soak until tender, about 10 minutes.
Drain mushrooms and squeeze dry, reserving soaking liquid.
Roughly chop mushrooms into 1/2- to 1-inch pieces.
While past and mushrooms soak, place sweet potato in a medium saucepan and cover with water.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to a simmer and cook until potato shows no resistance when pierced with a knife, about 8 minutes.
Drain and transfer to the bowl of a food processor.
Add 1 tablespoon olive oil and process until smooth.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Alternatively, to make without a processor, pass potato through a ricer into a bowl.
Whisk in olive oil and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Set aside.
Heat canola or vegetable oil in a large skillet over high heat until smoking.
Add fresh and soaked mushrooms and cook, stirring and tossing occasionally, until mushrooms are well browned on all sides, about 4 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium.
Add shallots, garlic, and thyme, and cook, stirring and tossing constantly until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add 1 teaspoon soy sauce and 2 teaspoons lemon juice and toss to coat.
Season to taste with salt and pepper, then transfer to a bowl.
Return skillet to high heat.
Add white wine and cook, scraping up browned bits with wooden spoon.
Allow to reduce by half, then add mushroom soaking liquid along with beans.
Simmer until reduced by 1/3, about 5 minutes.
Add escarole, remaining soy sauce, and remaining lemon juice and season to taste with salt and pepper. simmer until escarole is lightly softened, about 2 minutes.
Pour beans and escarole into a 9- by 13-inch baking dish.
Set aside.
Adjust oven rack to upper middle position and preheat oven to 425°F.
Transfer pasta sheets to a double layer of paper towels and dry them on both sides.

Working one piece at a time, lay pasta flat on cutting board with the short end parallel to the edge of the table, the add 1/8th of potato mixture and 1/8th of mushroom mixture to bottom 1/4 of the sheet.
Roll the sheet up away from you into a cylinder.
Repeat with all 8 rolls.
Nestle rolls into baking dish and moisten tops by spooning a bit of the pan liquid over them.
Cover dish tightly with foil and transfer to oven
Bake fo 20 minutes, remove foil, and continue baking until tops of cannelloni are browned and crisp.
Place immediately, placing two cannelloni on each plate and surrounding them with butter beans and escarole.
Sprinkle with parsley and drizzle with additional extra-virgin olive oil. Serve.
pickle juice: Use a potato masher to crush garbanzo beans in a large mixing bowl.
Add celery, onion, vegan mayonnaise, nutritional yeast, salt, garlic powder, olive oil, pickle juice, vinegar, and lemon juice.
Season with paprika, nori, seafood seasoning, hot sauce, cayenne pepper, and dill. Mix thoroughly.
Serve on toasted bread.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
In a shallow dish, mix together the breadcrumbs, grated Parmesan cheese, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Toss the potato fries in the dill pickle juice to coat them.
Roll each potato fry in the flour mixture until evenly coated.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the coated potato fries in the air fryer basket in a single layer, making sure they are not touching each other.
Air fry at 375°F for 12-15 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the fries are golden brown and crispy.
Once cooked, remove the pickle-flavored potato fries from the air fryer and serve hot.
Place potatoes in a large pot and add water to cover by at least 2”. Add 1 tsp salt and bring to a boil, then reduce to medium-low and simmer 8 minutes.
Using a slotted spoon, transfer the potatoes to a colander set over a bowl. While the potatoes cool, return the water to a boil.
Lower the eggs into the boiling water and boil over medium heat until just hard boiled, about 10 minutes. While potatoes are cooking, prepare an ice bath.
Transfer the hard-boiled eggs to the ice bath to cool. Peel, then dice eggs into bite-sized pieces.
In a large bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, sour cream, mustard, pickle juice, and cayenne until smooth. Add the pickles, onion, celery, potatoes, and eggs.
Season with salt and fold to combine. Season with more salt and pepper to taste. Serve sprinkled with chives.
Start by preparing the sauce.
In a small bowl combine the mayo, sugar-free ketchup, 1 tsp of mustard, diced pickles, pickle juice, and spices.
Flavors do meld better together over time so feel free to adjust.
To prep, the hamburger patties, measure out 2 oz. of meat per patty, and roll into a meatball.
Repeat so you have 10 total meatballs.
Season the tops with sea salt and cracked black pepper.
Preheat your cast iron/ griddle to high heat.
Add a little oil to the pan if necessary.
Place two meatballs onto the griddle or pan, use a wide spatula and press down.
I like to use a potato masher to help me press and smash the burgers as flat as possible.
Baste the top (optional) in mustard before flipping.
Work quickly.
As the edges appear to brown, flip them.
Place one piece of American cheese on one burger patty and stack the second patty over it.
To assemble the patty I start with a bottom piece of lettuce (my bun). add the slice of onion, the double stacked burger patty, tomato, pickles, and the sauce.
Cover with the second lettuce bun and dig in!
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Add your chopped cauliflower, sprinkle with salt and pepper and drizzle with the olive oil. Bake the cauliflower for 15 minutes.
Remove from the oven and let it cool to room temperature. In a small bowl, combine the dressing ingredients and whisk until smooth.
Once the cauliflower has cooled, add it to a bowl with the chopped onion, celery, pickles, and boiled eggs. Mix through the dressing.
Sprinkle with the chopped dill and serve immediately.
Boil the beetroot, carrot and potatoes in separate pots and cook until tender but not mushy. Cool completely. Gently mix with the onion and apple in a bowl.
Mix olive oil and pickle juice with dill, protein powder, salt and pepper. Drizzle over the vegetables and mix well.
Prepare the keto burger buns.
Brush a batch of buns with one egg yolk mixed with a tbsp of water.
In a bowl, mix the ingredients for the burger patties: beef, minced garlic, onion powder, apple cider vinegar, sea salt and black pepper.
Using your hands, create 4 equal patties, ½ to ¾ inch (1¼ to 25/32 inch) thick. Pierce the patties with a fork several times.
This will loosen the patties and help them cook equally without curling or getting tough while enabling maximum caramelization. And set aside.
Prepare the burger sauce by mixing the mayonnaise, ketchup, pickle juice, salt and pepper. And set aside.
To brown the keto burger buns, cut them widthwise.
Brush them with melted butter and cook in a hot skillet cut side down for about a minute or until browned and crispy. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
Grease the hot skillet with a tbsp of ghee and add the burger patties.
Cook over high heat, until a crust forms on the bottom and the burgers are easy to flip on the other side. 2-3 min
Once flipped, cook until the crust forms on the bottom and the sides are browned. 2-3 min
Cook the patties. The thicker the patties, the longer they will take to cook. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
In the same pan, cook the bacon slices.
Cut each of the slices lengthwise and cook until browned and crispy.
To assemble the burger: Spread about 2 tsp of the burger sauce on each bun halve.
Top each the bottom half with 4 slices of pickles and add a quarter of the shredded lettuce.
Add a burger patty, 2 onion slices, 1 slice of tomato and 1 slice of cheese.
Place under a broiler for a minute until the cheese is melted. Otherwise, you can add the cheese directly on top of the burger patty while still hot.
Finally, top with 2 slices of cooked bacon and add the top half of the burger bun.
Enjoy! Serve with leftover burger sauce, or low-carb ketchup and homemade mustard.
Optionally, serve with more pickles.
Wash, peel, and quarter the beetroots.
Bring a medium pot of filtered water or bone broth to a boil. Add the beets and boil for 15 minutes until tender.
Drain the water and reserve in a jar to repurpose. Cool the beets in the fridge in a glass or ceramic container.
Pour the kefir, yogurt, and sour cream into a large bowl. Whisk with a fork until well incorporated. Add all veggies, spices, and herbs.
Serve topped with halved eggs, dill, green onions.
gruyere cheese: Preheat your oven to 350°F.
Lightly grease a small baking dish or oven-safe ramekin.
Spread the chopped asparagus and spinach evenly in the baking dish.
Sprinkle the shredded Gruyère cheese over the vegetables.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and milk until well combined.
Season the egg mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Pour the egg mixture over the vegetables and cheese in the baking dish.
Place the baking dish in the preheated oven and bake for about 20-25 minutes or until the quiche is set and the top is golden brown.
Once cooked, remove the quiche from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, Dijon mustard, 1 tablespoon water and 1/2 teaspoon each salt and pepper.

Heat oil in a skillet and fry the bacon pieces for 4 to 5 minutes. Add eggs and cook, stirring with rubber spatula every few seconds, to desired doneness, 2 to 3 minutes for medium-soft eggs. Fold together with bacon, spinach, and Gruyere cheese.
Preheat your broiler.
Toast the bread slices until lightly golden brown.
In a small saucepan, melt ghee over medium heat.
Stir in flour and cook for about 1 minute until smooth and bubbly.
Gradually whisk in milk until the mixture is smooth and thickened.
Season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in grated Gruyere cheese until melted and smooth.
Place one slice of toast on a baking sheet and top with a slice of ham.
Pour the cheese sauce over the ham.
Place the second slice of toast on top.
Crack an egg onto the top slice of toast.
Place the baking sheet under the broiler and broil for about 3-4 minutes, or until the egg is cooked to your desired level of doneness and the cheese sauce is bubbly and golden brown.
Serve hot.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Using kitchen shears, cut the top of the lobster shell lengthwise, then remove the meat from the shell, keeping it intact. Set aside.
In a small saucepan, melt the ghee over medium heat.
Stir in the all-purpose flour and cook for about 1 minute until smooth and bubbly.
Gradually whisk in the milk until the mixture is smooth and thickened.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the shredded Gruyere cheese until melted and smooth. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Place the lobster meat back into the shell and pour the cheese sauce over the top.
Sprinkle the grated Parmesan cheese over the sauce.
Place the stuffed lobster shell on a baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven for about 12-15 minutes or until the cheese is golden brown and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
In a pot over medium heat, add thinly sliced onions, minced garlic, beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the soup simmer for about 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together and the onions to soften.
While the soup simmers, toast the baguette or bread slice in the oven for about 5 minutes until slightly crisp.
Sprinkle the shredded Gruyère cheese over the toasted bread slice.
Place the cheese-topped bread under the broiler for 1-2 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Once the soup is ready, ladle it into a bowl.
Place the Gruyère toast on top of the soup.
Preheat your oven to 400°F. Grease a glass or ceramic baking dish.
Wash and dry the mushrooms. Use a paring knife to gently remove the stems.
In a medium bowl, use a rubber spatula to thoroughly mix the cream cheese with the salt and the spices. Mix in the gruyere.
Spoon the mixture into the mushroom caps.
Place the mushrooms in the prepared baking dish. Bake until the cheese has melted and the mushrooms are tender, 10 minutes.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
Spread Dijon mustard on one side of each slice of bread.
Place a slice of ham on one of the slices of bread with the mustard side up.
Sprinkle half of the grated cheese over the ham.
Top with the other slice of bread, mustard side down, to form a sandwich.
Brush the top of the sandwich with olive oil.
Sprinkle the remaining grated cheese on top of the sandwich.
Place the sandwich on a baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven for about 10-12 minutes or until the cheese is melted and the bread is toasted.
Once done, remove from the oven and let it cool for a moment.
Heat ghee in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced onion, minced garlic, and dried thyme to the saucepan. Cook until onions are caramelized, about 15-20 minutes.
Pour beef broth into the saucepan and bring to a simmer. Cook for an additional 5-10 minutes to allow flavors to meld. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat your broiler.
Ladle the soup into an oven-safe bowl.
Place a slice of French bread on top of the soup.
Sprinkle shredded Gruyere cheese over the bread.
Place the bowl under the broiler and broil for 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the broiler and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Cook the macaroni pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, about 10-15 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Sprinkle the flour over the onions and garlic, and stir to coat evenly. Cook for 1-2 minutes to remove the raw flour taste.
Gradually pour in the beef broth while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Cook until the sauce thickens, about 3-5 minutes.
Add the cooked macaroni to the skillet with the onion and sauce. Stir to combine.
Sprinkle the grated Gruyère cheese over the macaroni mixture and stir until the cheese is melted and the macaroni is well coated.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the French Onion Mac and Cheese to a serving bowl or plate.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a saucepan, combine the water and ghee. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
Add the flour all at once, stirring constantly until the mixture forms a ball and pulls away from the sides of the pan, about 1-2 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat and let cool for a minute.
Add the egg to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated and smooth.
Stir in the shredded Gruyère cheese, salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
Drop spoonfuls of the dough onto the prepared baking sheet, leaving space between each one.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown and puffed.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Grease a muffin tin with cooking spray or oil.
Distribute the chopped mixed greens evenly among the muffin tin cups.
In a mixing bowl, whisk together the egg, grated Gruyère cheese, milk, salt, pepper, and any optional seasoning or nutmeg.
Pour the egg mixture over the greens in the muffin tin cups, filling each cup about three-quarters full.
Bake in the preheated oven for approximately 15-20 minutes or until the quiches are set and slightly golden on top.
Once done, remove the mini quiches from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Melt ghee in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced onion, minced garlic, and dried thyme to the saucepan. Cook until onions are caramelized, about 15-20 minutes.
Pour beef broth into the saucepan and bring to a simmer. Cook for an additional 5-10 minutes to allow flavors to meld. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat your broiler.
Ladle the soup into an oven-safe bowl.
Place a slice of French bread on top of the soup.
Sprinkle shredded Gruyere cheese over the bread.
Place the bowl under the broiler and broil for 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the broiler and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat the oven to 425°F. Grease a shallow 1.5-quart baking dish with butter or olive oil.
Place the cauliflower florets in a microwave-safe bowl. Add a tablespoon of water. Cover with a microwave-safe plate and microwave on high for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, in a large bowl, mix together the sour cream, mustard, garlic, gruyere, salt and pepper.
Drain the cooked cauliflower well and pat it dry. Add it to the sour cream sauce, gently tossing to combine.
Transfer the mixture to the prepared baking dish. Sprinkle the Parmesan on top and bake until heated through, about 10 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
In a large bowl whisk together flour and salt until combined. Using your hands, add butter, working it into the flour until the mixture resembles pea-size crumbs.
Add ice water by the tablespoon until the mixture forms into a crust. Form into a disc, wrap in plastic wrap, and refrigerate until firm, 30 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out crust until ¼” thick. Loosely drape over a 9 in pie plate and crimp edges. Refrigerate until ready to use.
In a large skillet over medium heat, cook bacon until crispy, 8 minutes. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain.
Scatter cooked bacon on pie crust with 1 cup Gruyère and shallot. In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, cream, cayenne, and nutmeg and season with salt.
Pour mixture over bacon and cheese. Sprinkle with remaining 1⁄2 cup cheese. Bake 40 minutes. Let cool 15 minutes before slicing and serving.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Heat cream, garlic and thyme in a medium saucepan just until bubbles begin to form around edge of pan. Remove from heat, set aside to steep.
Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a medium skillet over medium heat. Add leeks, season with salt and cook, stirring often, for 10 minutes. Transfer to a small bowl and set aside.

Butter a 3-quart gratin dish with remaining 1 tablespoon butter. Layer ⅓ potato slices and ⅓ celery root slices evenly over bottom of baking dish. Cover with ⅓ leeks and ⅓ gruyere. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Repeat layers twice more. Strain cream mixture into a medium pitcher and pour over vegetables.
Bake for 45 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
In a large cast iron skillet over medium heat, heat oil along with butter. Add the mushrooms and saute for about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside in a medium bowl.
Season mushrooms with the garlic powder, protein powder and salt and pepper to taste, stir to combine. Allow to cool to room temperature. Add the gruyere cheese and mix well.

Using a knife or a pizza cutter, cut the puff pastry into 2-inch squares and place them on a lined baking sheet. Using a pastry brush, brush the beaten egg on the squares. Place a tablespoon of mushroom mixture in the center. Bake for 20 minutes. Let cool slightly before serving.
In a shallow dish, combine panko, thyme, salt, and pepper.
After pounding chicken try doing so inside a ziplock bag, dredge chicken in panko mixture. Heat 1/2 tbsp of olive oil in skillet and cook chicken for 4 minutes.
Add remaining olive oil to skillet, turn chicken, and cook for 4 more minutes. Remove chicken and let rest before cutting crosswise.
For the pasta, heat ghee and olive oil over medium heat. Add garlic and ham and saute for 2 minutes.
Add flour, salt, and pepper and cook for 2 minutes stirring constantly.
Gradually add milk and whipped cream reducing heat to medium low.
Stir frequently and cook for 4 to 5 minutes until thickened. Add cheese and cook until it melts. Add cooked pasta and toss to coat.
Serve with chicken. Enjoy!
Preheat oven to 450°F.
Heat plenty of frying oil in a frying pan, or use a deep fryer if you own one.
Peel and slice the potatoes to ½ cm – ⅛ inch thick slices lengthwise. Season with salt and pepper.
Deep fry the potatoes, until they get golden in color and slightly brownish on the edges. Transfer potatoes on paper towels to drain and set aside.
Cut off the top and bottom of the eggplants. Slice the eggplants into 1 cm – ¼ inch thick slices. Fill a large bowl with water and add the eggplants.
Let them soak for at least 15 minutes to remove some of their bitterness. Transfer the eggplants to a strainer, season with salt and pepper, and then deep fry them too.
Once they have a nice golden brownish color, remove from heat and transfer on paper towels as well.
In a 15×10 inch baking pan or dish lay the potatoes. Press a bit with your hands to make them slightly stick together.
Do the same with the eggplants. In a medium cooking pot whisk together the sunflower oil and flour.
Cook over high heat for 2 minutes while whisking constantly until the flour gets a nice sandy color. Reduce heat to medium and add the milk.
Whisk constantly until the cream starts to thicken. Remove from heat. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
Whisk in the eggs one by one and ⅓ of the grated cheese. Add 3-4 tablespoons powdered breadcrumbs to the ground beef sauce to thicken it.
The spread it evenly on top of the eggplants. You may remove most of the spices from the sauce at this point.
Sprinkle ⅓ of the grated cheese on top, and add any remaining eggplants (if there are any). using a ladle, spread the cream evenly on top.

Sprinkle the remaining cheese on top of the cream. Bake for about 30-40 minutes. Until the cream starts to look golden brownish and firm. Let it stand for at least 30 minutes before serving. Cut into 8 pieces and serve.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
Coat a 9-inch pie pan with cooking spray; set aside. Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat; swirl to coat the pan.
Add mushrooms; cook, stirring occasionally, until  browned and tender, about 8 minutes. Add onion and garlic; cook, stirring often, until softened and tender, about 5 minutes.
Add spinach; cook, tossing constantly, until wilted, 1 to 2 minutes. Remove from heat. Whisk eggs, milk, mustard, thyme, salt and pepper in a medium bowl.
Fold in the mushroom mixture and cheese. Spoon into the prepared pie pan. Bake until set and golden brown, about 30 minutes.
Let stand for 10 minutes, slice. Garnish with thyme and serve.
In a 5 to 6 quart thick-bottomed pot, heat olive oil on medium heat. Add the onions and toss to coat with the olive oil.
Cook the onions, stirring often, until they have softened, about 15 to 20 minutes. Increase the heat to medium-high.
Add the remaining tbsp of olive oil and the ghee and cook, stirring often, until the onions start to brown, about 15 more minutes.
Sprinkle with 1 tsp of salt.
Continue to cook until the onions are well browned, about 10 to 15 more minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for a minute more.
Add the wine to the pot and scrape up the browned bits on the bottom and sides of the pot, deglazing the pot as you go.
Add the stock, bay leaves, and thyme. Bring to a simmer, cover the pot and lower the heat to maintain a low simmer.
Cook for about 30 minutes. Season to taste with more salt and add freshly ground black pepper. Discard the bay leaves.
While the soup is simmering, line a sheet pan with parchment paper or foil and preheat the oven to 450°F with a rack in the upper third of the oven.
Brush both sides of the French bread or baguette slices lightly with olive oil (you'll end up using about a tbsp and a half of olive oil for this).
Put in the oven and toast until lightly browned, about 5 to 7 minutes. Remove from oven.
Turn the toasts over and sprinkle with the grated Gruyere cheese and Parmesan.
Return to the oven when it's close to serving time and bake until the cheese is bubbly and lightly browned.
To serve, ladle soup into a bowl and transfer one cheesy toast onto the top of each bowl of soup.
Alternatively, you can use individual oven-proof bowls or one large casserole dish.
Ladle the soup into the bowls or casserole dish.
Cover with the toast and sprinkle with cheese.
Put into the broiler for 10 minutes at 350° F, or until the cheese bubbles and is slightly browned.
vanilla extract: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a cupcake tray with 12 cupcake liners. Set aside.
Sift the flour into a mixing bowl and add sugar, pea proteins, baking soda, salt and matcha powder. Mix together.
Prepare your vegan buttermilk by adding 1 tbsp white vinegar to a measuring jug and then adding soy milk.
Add the yogurt, oil and vanilla extract and mix into a batter. Don’t overmix.
Divide the batter evenly between the 12 cupcake liners in your cupcake tray.
Bake for 20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center of one of the cupcakes comes out clean. Serve with some blueberries.
In a food processor, add the oats, and pulse for 20 seconds in order to break the oats down slightly.
Add the rest of the ingredients and pulse until fully combined.
Roll into 12 balls and refrigerate, then serve.
In a non-stick pan over medium heat, sauté the apple slices until softened.
Add oats, almond butter, chopped nuts, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and a dash of salt to the pan.
Stir well and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the oats are heated through.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and enjoy.
Heat waffle iron and grease with coconut oil.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda and protein powder. In another large bowl, stir together melted ghee, sugar, egg, egg yolk, sour cream, and vanilla.
Add mashed bananas and stir until combined. Gradually add dry ingredients to wet ingredients until just combined.
Add 1 cup batter to waffle iron. Close and cook until golden on both sides. Transfer waffle to a plate and repeat with remaining batter.
Layer apple, pineapple, cashew butter, coconut, flax meal, vanilla extract, and cinnamon into a blender; add almond milk. Blend mixture until smooth.
In a saucepan, bring the coconut milk, and milk to a gentle boil.
Add the rice and simmer over low heat for about 20 minutes. Add vanilla and honey.
Transfer to a bowl and cover with plastic wrap directly on the surface of the rice pudding.
Top with mango and put in the fridge. Serve chilled.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease 6 muffin cups or line with paper muffin liners.
Stir together flour, baking soda and baking powder. In a separate bowl, combine yogurt, egg, vanilla and blueberries.
Stir mixtures together just until combined; batter will be very thick. Scoop into prepared muffin cups.
Bake in preheated oven for 25 minutes.
Bring water and salt to a boil on the stove. Once boiling, stir in oats, protein powder, and vanilla, and reduce heat to medium. Allow to cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
After 15 minutes, reduce heat to low and stir in almond milk, coconut milk and half of the mango.
To serve, top with mango slices, almonds and coconut flakes.
Heat coconut oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add sliced apples to the skillet.
Sprinkle ground cinnamon, vanilla extract, and a dash of salt over the apples.
Saute the apples until they are softened and lightly browned.
Transfer the sauteed apples to a plate.
Enjoy this healthy and flavorful sauteed apple dish!
Pour water into a tall pot; bring to a boil. Add oats; cook, stirring occasionally, in boiling water until a thin oatmeal forms, about 5 minutes. Remove pot from heat.
Stir cold water, condensed milk, and vanilla extract into oat mixture; transfer to a pitcher and chill in the refrigerator until cold, at least 1 hour.
Pour drink into glasses and sprinkle cinnamon over the top.
Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend on high until smooth.
Enjoy immediately or slightly chilled.
Rinse the mixed raspberries and blackberries.
Mash half of the berries in a bowl using a fork.
Add chia seeds, almond milk, honey, and vanilla extract to the mashed berries. Stir well.
Gently fold in the remaining whole berries.
Cover the bowl and refrigerate the mixture for at least 2 hours or overnight, allowing the chia seeds to expand and create a pudding-like consistency.
Once chilled and set, give the mixture a final stir and serve chilled.
Measure flour into a large mixing bowl. Slowly whisk in the milk. Whisk in the salt, eggs, cinnamon and vanilla extract until smooth.
Heat a griddle or frying and melt butter pan over medium heat.
Soak bread slices in mixture until saturated. Cook bread on each side until golden brown. Serve hot with berries.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, and baking powder.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the milk, whisked egg, and vanilla extract.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined. Be careful not to overmix; a few lumps are okay.
Lightly grease a small oven-safe baking dish or ramekin with cooking spray.
Pour the pancake batter into the greased baking dish.
Place the baking dish in the preheated oven.
Bake for approximately 15-18 minutes, or until the pancake is puffed up and golden brown on top. It should no longer be gooey in the center when a toothpick or knife is inserted.
Carefully remove the Baked Pancake from the oven.
Add all ingredients to blender and blend until you reach the desired consistency.
You can add extra water in case you need would like it thinner.  Less if you prefer your shake thicker.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine grated zucchini, egg, unsweetened applesauce, olive oil, and vanilla extract.
In a separate bowl, whisk together almond flour, baking powder, ground cinnamon, and a pinch of salt.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, stirring until well combined.
Pour the batter into a greased mini loaf pan or ramekin.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Allow the zucchini bread to cool slightly before serving.
Stir together kefir and oats in a pint-sized mason jar. Stir in vanilla extract and honey, if using, mix well. Refrigerate overnight.
When ready to serve, top with red currants, some more oats, and enjoy.
Place chia seeds in a bowl.
Whisk coconut milk, unsweetened coconut milk, agave nectar, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and salt together in a bowl, pour over chia seeds and stir well.
Allow coconut milk-chia seed mixture to soak until thickened, at least 20 minutes, or cover bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
Stir and top with blueberries and coconut shavings.
Place rolled oats, steel cut oats and salt in a medium sauce pot.
Combine the milk, water and 1½ cups chopped strawberries in a blender and blend until smooth. Pour the liquid mixture and vanilla extract into the sauce pot.
Cook oats, stirring frequently over medium heat until thickened, about 10-15 minutes. Once thickened, gently stir in remaining ½ cup diced strawberries and serve.
Blend soy milk, oats, strawberries, and banana in a blender until smooth. Add vanilla and blend again until smooth.
Pour into glasses and serve
garam masala: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
In a blender, combine the onion, ginger, and garlic and blend into a smooth puree with 2 tbsp or more of water.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the cumin seeds and cook for 1 minute. Add bay leaf and cook until the bay leaf changes color.
Add the pureed onion and cook until the onion mixture is dry and does not smell raw. Stir occasionally to avoid sticking, 4 minutes.
Add cinnamon, garam masala and cayenne and mix well. Add the coconut milk, mango pulp, salt, and mix well.
Mix, cover and cook until the sauce comes to a boil, 5 minutes. Add chickpeas and mix in. Reduce the heat to low medium and cook for 10 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf and discard. Serve with rice.
Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set aside.
Heat ghee in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and ginger to the pan and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the diced tomato and cook until softened.
Add the minced lamb to the pan and cook until browned.
Stir in the cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, and garam masala, and cook for another minute to toast the spices.
Add the mixed vegetables and rinsed basmati rice to the pan. Stir well to coat the rice and vegetables with the spices.
Pour in the water and season with salt to taste. Bring to a boil.
Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove the pan from the heat and let it sit, covered, for a few minutes before fluffing the pilau with a fork.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the onion and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, 5 minutes.
Add coriander, cumin and turmeric and cook, stirring, for 2 minutes.
Pulse tomatoes in the food processor until finely chopped. Add to the pan along with salt.
Reduce heat to maintain a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
Add chickpeas, spinach, and garam masala, reduce heat to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes more.
Serve topped with cilantro.
Add the cucumber to a bowl with the yogurt. Add in the remaining ingredients. Stir well to combine.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Chop the potatoes into wedges. Add them into baking dish along with the oil, spices, and some salt and pepper.
Bake for 30 minutes, garnish with some coconut flour and serve.
In a bowl, combine the Greek yogurt, tomato paste, grated ginger, minced garlic, garam masala, ground turmeric, ground cumin, cayenne pepper, and salt. Mix well to form a marinade.
Add the chicken pieces to the marinade and toss to coat evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours.
In a skillet, heat the ghee over medium heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
Serve the chicken hot with naan bread.
Heat olive oil in a saucepan over medium-high heat, add onion and garlic and stir occasionally until tender, 5 minutes.
Add ginger, cook until fragrant, 2 minutes, add spices (except garam masala) and stir until fragrant.
Add stock, tomato, cover and simmer until starting to become tender, 5 minutes.
Add cauliflower, cover and simmer until tender, 5 minutes.
Stir in garam marsala, and serve.
Heat up oil in a large pan over medium heat. Sauté the onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger. Saute for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, and red pepper flakes. Cook for  1 minute.
Add in the tomatoes together with their juices and cook for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the tomatoes have softened and broken down.
Add the broth, potatoes, and salt to the pan. Stir well to combine, then turn up the heat and bring to a high simmer. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add in the cauliflower and stir well to combine, cook for 10 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Remove from heat and stir in the lime juice and sprinkle with chopped spinach.
Puree the ginger, garlic, lemon juice, and 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a food processor, adding a little water if necessary.
Cut the chicken breasts into small cubes and pour the mixture over them. Stir and refrigerate to marinate while you prep the rest of the recipe.
Puree all the sauce ingredients in a food processor until very smooth. Set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onions and saute for 5 minutes, then add the chicken and the tomato paste. Saute until fragrant and browned.

Add the korma sauce base, yogurt, half and half, paneer, raisins, cashews, and salt. Simmer for as long as you can before eating so the flavors can develop. Add extra water or chicken broth the thin out the sauce to your desired consistency since it will thicken up a little bit.
Savor to taste and decorate with coriander leaves before serving.

Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add cumin seeds. Allow to splutter.
Add chicken, ground turmeric, ground coriander, and garam masala. Season with salt. Saute for 3 minutes or until browned.
Add onions, tomatoes, minced ginger and garlic. Pour in 1 cup water. Season with salt. Cook for 10 minutes.
Add chickpeas, and ginger and stir to combine. Simmer for 5 minutes. Garnish with parsley and serve.
In a nonstick skillet heat 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger and saute until softened, about 5 minutes
Over medium heat, add the salt, coriander, garam masala, turmeric, cumin, and stir on medium-low heat until fragrant, about 30 seconds or so.
Then stir into the spices in the pan, creating a thick spiced sauce, about 1 minute.
Add the spinach and gently stir to combine, as the skillet will be very full. Cover and raise the heat to medium-high, allowing the spinach to start to wilt.
Meanwhile, wipe the skillet clean with a paper towel, return it to medium-high heat, and add 1 tbsp of oil.
When the oil is hot, add the cubed chicken pieces, sprinkle salt over the top to season, and cook until almost completely cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Pour the spinach back over the chicken and stir.
Turn Instant Pot to saute mode and heat oil. Add cumin seeds and toast them for 30 seconds. Add turmeric powder and mix well. Add ginger and garlic, mix well.
Add onion and saute for a minute. Cook for 2 minutes with a glass lid on. Add tomatoes, red chili powder, garam masala, salt and ground coriander. Mix well.
Add ground chicken and break it with a spatula. Close Instant Pot with the pressure valve to sealing. Pressure Cook on for 4 minutes.
Garnish with cilantro and serve hot.
Chop the scallions and keep the whites and greens seperate. Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan. Add chopped onions along with half of the salt.
Sauté the onions on medium heat until they are translucent and golden brown.
Add ginger, garlic, chili and the white scallions to the onions. Cook for 2 minutes, mixing well.
Add tomatoes and turmeric to the pan and cook for 2 minutes. Whisk the eggs with milk and remaining salt.
Slowly pour the whisked eggs to the pan and keep mixing with a spatula for 5-6 minutes or until the eggs are cooked.
Add the garam masala and garnish with chopped cilantro and remaining scallions. Mix well.
Heat a griddle on medium heat. Apply ghee on both sides of sliced bread.

Cook the bread slices until both sides turn golden-brown
Enjoy egg bhurji with the ghee toasted bread!
Heat the oil in a large pan over a medium-high setting.
Sauté the finely chopped onion until golden.
Add the crushed garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the garlic doesn't smell raw anymore.
Mix in the next four ingredients (ground coriander, turmeric, cumin, and cayenne) and toast for two minutes stirring often.
Add the crushed tomatoes, chickpeas and vegetable stock.
Increase the heat to high and once boiling, lower to medium-low to maintain at a simmer for 10 minutes, stirring every now and then.
Add 1/2 tsp salt, the sugar, and the spinach. If the spinach is frozen increase the heat until the curry is bubbling away again.
Simmer for an additional five minutes.
Add the garam masala, lemon juice and coconut milk and stir.
Sprinkle over the chopped cilantro leaves and serve hot with pita, if desired.
In a bowl, mix chicken pieces with yogurt, garlic, turmeric, cumin, coriander, and chili powder.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add marinated chicken pieces and cook for 5-6 minutes until browned.
Add chopped tomato and cook for another 5-7 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Stir in heavy cream, garam masala, and salt; cook for 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.
To make the paste, heat a little of oil in a frying pan, add diced onion and 1 tsp fresh or dried chilli, and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
In a blender combine garlic, remaining oil, then add ground coriander,  cumin, garam masala, tomato sauce, salt, and the fried onion. Blend to a smooth paste.
Cook the paste in a medium saucepan for 2 minutes over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally so it doesn’t stick.
Tip in chickpeas, tomatoes, and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add coconut cream with a little water, cook for 5 minutes more. Serve garnished with parsley.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add diced onions and cook until softened.
Stir in minced garlic and ginger, and cook until fragrant.
Add ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric powder, paprika, and garam masala to the skillet. Cook for a minute until spices are aromatic.
Add diced tomatoes to the skillet and cook until they break down and form a thick sauce.
Stir in cooked chickpeas and simmer for a few minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Season with salt to taste.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat to 350°F.
Add the diced onion and sauté until it becomes translucent.
Add the minced garlic and ginger. Sauté for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, garam masala, and red chili powder. Cook for an additional minute to release the spices' flavors.
Add the diced tomato and cook until it softens, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the fresh spinach leaves and water to the pan. Cover and let the spinach wilt for about 2-3 minutes.

Use an immersion blender to puree the cooked mixture until it forms a smooth paste. If you don't have an immersion blender, transfer the mixture to a regular blender, blend until smooth, and then return it to the pan.
Stir in the milk and cook for a few minutes to heat through.
Add the paneer cubes and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the paneer is heated.
In a pressure cooker heat oil and saute cinnamon, cardamom, ½ tsp cumin till it turns aromatic.
Add in turmeric, 1 tsp red chilli powder and ½ tsp coriander powder. saute.
Add 1 tsp garlic paste, tomato puree, and saute well.
Add potatoes, peas, 1 cup water, and salt, and saute for a minute.
Mix well and pressure cook for 3 whistles or till potato gets cooked well.
Add in ½ tsp garam masala and serve.
In a mixing bowl, combine the crumbled paneer, cooked peas, mashed potato, cumin seeds, garam masala, turmeric powder, chili powder, and salt. Mix well to combine.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the paneer and pea mixture to the pan and sauté for 2-3 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside to cool.

In a separate bowl, prepare the samosa dough by combining all-purpose flour, salt, and vegetable oil. Gradually add water and knead until a smooth dough forms. Cover with a damp cloth and let it rest for 15-20 minutes.
Divide the dough into small lemon-sized balls and roll each ball into a thin circle.
Cut each circle in half to form semi-circles.
Take one semi-circle and fold it into a cone shape, sealing the edges with water.
Fill the cone with the paneer and pea mixture, then seal the open edge using water to form a triangular samosa.
Repeat the process with the remaining dough and filling.
Heat vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Once the oil is hot, carefully add the samosas in batches and fry until they turn golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove the fried samosas from the oil and drain them on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Serve the paneer and pea samosas hot with your favorite chutney or dipping sauce.
rib of celery: Heat the olive oil until shimmering over medium heat in a large soup pot. Add the onion, chicken, carrots, celery, and salt and pepper.
Cooking, stirring frequently, until the vegetables are very soft, about 15 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the stock and bring to a boil.
Add the noodles and cook 6 minutes, then add the chicken and cook about 2 minutes more, until the noodles are cooked through.
In a large pot, place the potatoes, celery and leaves, bacon and onion. Pour in enough water to cover. Bring to a boil.
Remove the celery leaves, and stir in the bouillon cubes until dissolved. Mix in the corn. Reduce heat to medium-low, and cook 25 minutes. Serve with a basil leaf garnish.
Heat the olive oil in a soup pot, add the onion, celery, and carrot, and cook for 4 minutes until the onion softens. Sprinkle the flour over the veggies, stir and cook for another minute
Pour in the broth to the pot, stir well and bring to a simmer. Reduce the heat to low and cook for 5 more minutes. Add tortellini, spinach, and cream.
Stir everything together and continue cooking for another 5 minutes or until the tortellini is cooked through. Season with salt and pepper as needed. Serve warm.

Season the chicken breasts with salt and black pepper. Place them in a pot, fill the pot with water and set it on medium heat and let it simmer for 8 to 16 minutes or until the chicken is no longer pink.
After the chicken is cooked, remove from pot and place it on a slicing board and shred the chicken. Set aside.
Heat the olive oil until shimmering over medium heat in a large soup pot. Add the onion, carrots, celery, and 1 tsp each salt and pepper.
Cooking, stirring frequently, until the vegetables are very soft, about 15 minutes. Add the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the stock and bring to a boil.

Add the noodles and cook 6 minutes, then add the chicken and cook about 2 minutes more, until the noodles are cooked through and the chicken is warmed through. Season to taste with salt and pepper, then stir in the parsley.
Cool and refrigerate the soup in an airtight container for four days, or in the freezer for up to two months.
In a large pot over medium heat, heat oil. Add carrots, celery, onion, and leek and cook until soft, 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, 1 minute more.
Add broth, water, thyme, rosemary, bay leaf, oregano, and pinch red pepper flakes. Bring to a boil, then add the turkey meat. Cook for 12-15 minutes, or until the meat is cooked through.
Take the turkey out, shred it with two forks and return it to the pot. Add the egg noodles. Cover and cook until pasta is cooked through, 7 minutes.
Discard thyme, rosemary, and bay leaf, and serve.
Add 1 tbsp ghee to bottom sauté pan on medium heat and add garlic, shallots, and celery. Cook until translucent and scrape into your sauce pan.
Add the remaining ingredients except arrowroot to the pan and cook uncovered for 5 minutes. Pull the lobster tails out of the broth and set them aside.
Add the arrowroot powder to the broth and stir together. Once thickened, use an immersion blender to create a smooth broth.
Cut the lobster tails in half using a sharp knife and pull the tail meat out of the shell.
Pour the chowder into bowls, and garnish with lobster.
In a large pot, melt butter over medium heat. Saute celery, onion, bay leaf, dill, salt and pepper for about 5 minutes.
Stir in potatoes and saute for 3 minutes. Add water and bring to a boil. Cover, reduce heat and boil until potatoes are tender.
Whisk flour into milk and stir together into pot. Bring to a simmer, stirring often.
Stir in seafood, lemon juice and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Ladle into bowls and drizzle each serving with olive oil.
Heat oil in a pot and add onion. Saute for 3 minutes, add garlic, celery, carrots, potatoes, and spices. Saute for 1 minute more.
Pour in vegetable broth, 1 cup water and bring the soup to a boil. Simmer for 20 minutes.
Pour about 1/2 soup into a different pot and blend this part until smooth.
Pour the blended soup back into the large pot, add coconut cream and stir to combine. Let simmer for 5 minutes, season and serve.
Preheat the oven to 360°F. Add eggplant on a baking tray and season with salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp of olive oil. Bake for 25 minutes.
Heat 1 olive oil in a pan and add onion and celery. Fry for 5 minutes and add 1/4 cup of water.
Add tomatoes, salt olives and capers, and cook for 10 minutes. Add vinegar and stir,
Add baked eggplants, stir, finish cooking for a couple of minutes, then turn the heat off, garnish with some pine nuts and serve.
In a large pot over medium heat, melt the butter.
Add  in the onion, celery, carrots, and ¼ tsp of the salt; sauté, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5 minutes,
Add in the garlic and cook until fragrant, 30-60 seconds.
Pour in the broth, Italian seasoning, pepper, and remaining 1 ¼ of salt.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and gently simmer for 10 minutes.
Add in the noodles and simmer, stirring occasionally, until tender, about 8-10 minutes.
Stir in the chicken and heat through.
Ladle into bowls and enjoy!
Heat olive oil in a stockpot over medium heat. Cook and stir onion and garlic in hot oil for 5 minutes. Add carrots and celery to stockpot and cook for 10 minutes.

Pour vegetable broth, water, and white wine over the mixture. Stir crushed tomatoes, lentils, paprika, bay leaves, honey, salt, and black pepper into the mixture; bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium-low, cover the stockpot, and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 50 minutes.
In another bowl, heat butter. Add bacon and curry and cook for 6 minutes.
Remove and discard bay leaves from the soup. Ladle into bowls and top with arugula and bacon to serve.
Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Coat pan with cooking spray.
Add onion, carrot, and celery to pan; cook 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While vegetables cook, heat roast beef according to package directions; drain, discarding drippings.
Chop beef into 1-inch pieces.
Stir garlic into vegetables; cook 30 seconds. Stir in beef, barley, and remaining ingredients.
Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat, and simmer 40 minutes or until barley and vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally.
Remove and discard bay leaves.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Oil a baking dish.
Place the potatoes in a pot, cover with cold water, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Turn the heat to medium-low, and boil the potatoes until tender, about 25 minutes, drain.
Stir the vegan sour cream, soy milk, olive oil, vegan cream cheese, and salt into the potatoes.
Mash with a potato masher until smooth and fluffy. Set the potatoes aside.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat, and cook and stir the onion, carrots, celery, peas, and tomato for about 10 minutes.
Stir in the Italian seasoning, garlic, and pepper. Reduce the heat to medium-low, and crumble the vegetarian ground beef substitute into the skillet.
Cook and stir, breaking up the meat substitute, until the mixture is hot, about 5 minutes.
Spread the vegetarian meat substitute mixture into the bottom of the baking dish, and top with the mashed potatoes, smoothing them into an even layer.
Sprinkle the potatoes with the shredded vegan cheese. Bake in the preheated oven until the cheese is melted and slightly browned and the casserole is hot, about 20 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Season beef fillet with salt and pepper. Set aside for 15 to 30 minutes.
Combine pepper, thyme, rosemary, and garlic in a bowl.
Heat a shallow, heavy-bottomed pan over high heat. Add oil and sear beef on both sides until brown and crusty.
Remove beef from pan. Spread mustard on top then sprinkle evenly with herb mixture.
Place carrots, tomatoes, chili pepper, onions, and celery in a roasting pan. Place beef on top of the vegetables.
Roast for 1.5 hours for medium-rare or 3.5 hours for well done. Remove pan from the oven, transfer meat to a platter, and let rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Pat beef dry with paper towels and season well with salt and pepper.
In a large pot over medium heat, heat oil.  Working in batches, add beef and sear on all sides until golden, about 3 minutes per side. Remove from pot and repeat with remaining beef.

To same pot, add mushrooms and cook until golden and crispy, 5 minutes. Add onion, carrots, and celery and cook until soft, 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, 1 minute more. Add tomato paste and and stir to coat vegetables.
Add broth, thyme, rosemary, and beef to pot and season with salt and pepper. Bring to a boil and reduce heat to a simmer. Simmer until beef is tender, 50 minutes to an hour.
Heat oil in a 6-quart Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add chicken thighs and sear, 2 to 3 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add carrots and celery to the pan. Reduce heat to medium and cook until softened, about 10 minutes. Add onions and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and add chicken stock, water, green beans, riced cauliflower, broccoli, bouillon cube, bay leaf, salt, and pepper.
Add chicken thighs and simmer until no longer pink at the bone and the juices run clear, 30 to 45 minutes.
Remove chicken and shred meat. Place shredded chicken back in the soup and serve.
Heat the oil in a saucepan. Brown the onion and garlic for 1 minute.
Add lentils, carrot, celery, and fry for 2 minutes. Deglaze the saucepan with vegetable broth.
Stir in yogurt and cook for 15 minutes. Roughly blend the soup and season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Garnish with parsley and serve.
Preheat oven to 375°F and place rack into the center of the oven.

To make the pastry dough pulse the flour and salt in a food processor. Add butter, egg and ice water. Pulse until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Shape the dough into a ball. Tightly cover the dough in plastic wrap and place in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.

While your empanada dough is resting prepare the filling. Fill a medium size stock pot ¾ full with water and bring to a boil. Add the cubed potato to the pot and boil the potato until tender, about 3 minutes.

While potatoes are boiling, brown the beef in a saute pan over medium heat along with the onions, celery and carrots. Once potatoes are cooked drain and add them to the ground beef mixture and cook until beef is cooked through and vegetables are softened.
Add the garlic, chili powder, cumin, cinnamon and beef broth to the beef and vegetable mixture, sauteing 1 minute longer until spices are fragrant. Season with salt and pepper.

Remove dough from refrigerator and divide it into 10 equal part, roughly 2 oz each. Roll each piece of dough very thin on a lightly floured surface until you have a circle roughly 7-inch wide. Working with one prepared dough at a time add a heaping ⅓ cup of filling onto one side of the dough.

Wet a pastry brush with egg wash and dampen the inside ¼ inch edge of the filled dough. Fold the dough in half. Using a fork, firmly press the edges together. Repeat this process with remaining dough and filling and arrange on a parchment lined baking sheet.
In a small bowl mix together an egg with 1 tbsp of water, brush each empanada with egg wash until coated. Bake for 35 min or until golden brown.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil over medium-high heat in a large sauté pan or wok.
Add chicken and sauté for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and no longer pink on the inside.
Use a slotted spoon to transfer the chicken to a clean plate, and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon oil to the sauté pan, along with the bell pepper, onion, celery and garlic.
Sauté for 5-6 more minutes, stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, as the veggies are sautéing, whisk together the soy sauce, cornstarch, oyster sauce, vinegar, black pepper and ginger in a small bowl until combined.
Once the veggies are ready go to, add the chicken and sauce mixture to the pan, and stir until the veggies and chicken are evenly coated.
Continue to cook for 1-2 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Taste, and season with extra soy sauce and/or black pepper as desired.
Remove from heat, and serve immediately sprinkled with your desired toppings.
Place the cauliflower in a large pot and cover with water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat and boil the cauliflower until it’s just fork tender, 10 minutes.
Drain the cauliflower and cool it to room temperature. Meanwhile, in a medium bowl, whisk together the Greek yogurt, sour cream, vinegar, parsley and dill.
Add the cauliflower, celery, green onions to the bowl and toss well to combine. Season with salt and pepper.
Chill the salad for at least 1 hour before serving.
garlic paste: In a large bowl, combine all the ingredients well.
Add prawns, mix. Cover and allow it to marinade for 30 mins under refrigeration.
Heat 3 tbsps of oil in heavy bottom pan. Then, add sliced onions and fry until just starts picking up golden colour.
Add bay leaf and sliced tomatoes and sauté on high heat for another minute. Then, add marinated prawns and and mix.
Cook on high heat for minute and then low heat for 3 minutes. Do not add any water and do not over cook the prawns.
Add coriander leaves and mix. In a large pot, add enough water. Add bay leaves, oil, lemon juice and salt Bring it to a boil.
Add rice and cook until the rice is 70% done.
When the rice is 70% done, using a large colander or slotted spoon take out the rice and layer it evenly over the prawns layer.
Sprinkle the melted ghee over the rice layer. Place the pot over a high flame and cook for 5-7 minutes.
Place hot tava/griddle below the pot and keep it for Dum(slow cook) for another 10-12 minutes.
After 15 minutes, switch off the heat and leave as it is for 10 minutes.
After 10 minutes, open the lid, dish out yummy hot prawn biryani and serve with raita of your choice.
In a pressure cooker heat oil and saute cinnamon, cardamom, ½ tsp cumin till it turns aromatic.
Add in turmeric, 1 tsp red chilli powder and ½ tsp coriander powder. saute.
Add 1 tsp garlic paste, tomato puree, and saute well.
Add potatoes, peas, 1 cup water, and salt, and saute for a minute.
Mix well and pressure cook for 3 whistles or till potato gets cooked well.
Add in ½ tsp garam masala and serve.
In a large saucepan over medium heat, combine water, miso, garlic paste, dashi and gochujang.
Bring to a boil and let boil 2 minutes. Stir in zucchini, potato, mushrooms and onions and let boil 5 to 7 minutes more.
Stir in tofu and cook until tofu has expanded and vegetables are tender.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced onions and sauté until they become translucent.
Stir in ginger paste and garlic paste, and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add diced chicken breast to the skillet and cook until the chicken is no longer pink.
Add thinly sliced bell pepper and diced tomato to the skillet, and cook for a few more minutes until the vegetables are slightly softened.
Sprinkle cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, chili powder, and salt over the chicken and vegetables. Mix well to combine.
Cook for another 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro before serving, if desired.
Heat oil in a nonstick pan and add cinnamon stick, garlic paste, ginger paste, and saute for 5 seconds in the oil.

Add chopped onions and saute until translucent.
Add chopped tomatoes. Saute and cook covered for couple of minutes until tomatoes are soft and mushy.
Mash the tomatoes with the spatula and saute again for 30 seconds. Fold in jack fruit.
Add turmeric powder, chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala and saute well. Cook covered for 3 minutes on a medium flame.
Remove the lid and saute again for 30 seconds. Add water, mix and cook covered for 2 minutes or until few boils.
Add coconut milk, salt. Mix and cook covered for 10 minutes on a low flame.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve warm.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Mix bread crumbs and dry soup mix together in a bowl.
Mix mayonnaise and garlic paste together in a separate bowl. Microwave on high to thin out consistency, for 20 seconds.
Brush chicken breasts with the mayonnaise mixture. Cover with the crumb mixture. Place on a baking sheet.
Bake on the middle rack of the preheated oven until chicken is no longer pink in the center and juices run clear, about 20 minutes. Serve immediately.
Cook noodles according to package directions, omitting salt and fat. Drain and rinse under cold water.
In a pan heat 1 tbsp olive oil, add chicken breasts, and season with salt and pepper. Cook for 5 minutes on each side, until tender.
Combine rice vinegar, oil, soy sauce, honey, and garlic paste in a large bowl, and stir with a whisk.
Add noodles, chicken, and cucumbers to the bowl. Toss gently to coat. Top with green onions.
In a large bowl, mix all powder ingredients, butter, egg, garlic and ginger paste, scallion, cilantro, almond flour, seasoning, and ground chicken.
When ready to cook, mix in tomatoes. Wet your palms with a little oil or water and shape the chicken mixture into round patties.
Heat oil on medium heat in a skillet and, once oil is hot, place the chicken patties in oil and let them fry for 5 minutes. Flip and cook for another 5 minutes.
Remove to a paper towel-lined plate. Place on a plate, topped with sliced tomatoes. Serve along with salad mix, seasoned with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, and vinegar.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add the diced onions and sauté until they turn translucent.
Add ginger paste and garlic paste, and sauté for another minute.
Add the diced tomatoes and cook until they soften.
Stir in the red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, and coriander powder. Mix well.
Pour in the coconut milk and add salt to taste. Stir until the sauce is well combined.
Gently place the white fish fillet into the curry.
Cover the pan and let it simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily.
Serve your Goan Fish Curry hot, garnished with chopped cilantro with cooked rice.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.

Chop off the stem end of the eggplant and then cut in half lengthwise. Using a spoon, scoop out the meaty flesh of the eggplant, ensuring to leave about ¼ - ½ inch of the flesh and the skin intact. You’re aiming for a hollow boat of eggplant to stuff the mixture into. In a small bowl, place the scooped out eggplant and set aside. Place the eggplant halves in a 9x13 in glass plan, making sure the eggplant halves are in a single layer, flesh side up.

Working quickly, with the flesh side up, brush 1 tbsp of olive oil onto the flesh of each eggplant half. Season each eggplant half well with 1 tsp Italian seasoning, garlic powder and kosher salt. Place the pan in the oven and roast for 40 minutes, or until the eggplant is fork tender all the way through. Depending on how big your eggplants are, cooking time may vary.

While the eggplant is roasting in the oven, place a medium skillet on medium heat. While the skillet is heating, chop the portobello mushroom caps and eggplant flesh into bite sized pieces. Once the skillet is heated, add the remaining olive oil. Stir in mushrooms and eggplant flesh pieces.
Occasionally stir the mushrooms and eggplant until starting to brown, about 10 minutes. It is normal for water to be released, allow it to cook off and then the vegetables will start to brown.

Add the garlic paste and mix well to combine. Next, add the remaining Italian seasoning and salt as needed and taste the mixture, it should be flavorful. If it doesn't, continue to season until it tastes good. Remove the pan from the heat until the eggplant is done cooking.

Once the eggplant halves are fork tender, remove from the oven. Toss the marinara sauce and fontina cheese together with the eggplant mixture. Divide the eggplant mixture into two portions and stuff each roasted eggplant half. Sprinkle freshly grated Parmesan cheese over each half of stuffed eggplant.

Place the glass pan back in the oven for 10minutes to melt the cheese.
Remove from the oven and top with parsley.
Put the onion, garlic and ginger paste, curry powder, cayenne with half the butter in a frying pan over a low-medium heat. Cook until the onions soften.
Pour in the coconut milk and tomatoes and season with a pinch of salt and plenty of black pepper. Bring to the boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Add the beans, lentils and remaining butter. Simmer for about 30 minutes.
Remove from the heat, cover and leave to cool completely. The flavours will continue to cook and develop as it cools.
Garnish with coconut cream and serve,
Heat oil and add cumin seeds. When they splutter, add onions and ginger-garlic pastes. Stir fry till fat separates.
Add the garam masala, coriander powder, chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt and yogurt and saute till fat separates.
Mix in the fish and cook over high heat till opaque. Mix in the browned onions, coriander, green chillies and biryani masala.
To make the rice, heat oil and add cloves, peppercorns, cinnamon and cardamoms. Mix well and cook till rice is tender but still holds its shape.
To serve, put the fish layer at the bottom of the dish, cover with the rice and sprinkle the saffron-milk mixture.
Keep or over low heat for about 15 minutes. Mix to break up the layers and serve.
Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent.
Add the ginger paste and garlic paste to the pot and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the cubed lamb meat to the pot and cook until it's browned on all sides.
Stir in the red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and salt. Mix well to coat the meat with the spices.
Pour in the water and bring the mixture to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for about 1-1.5 hours, or until the meat is tender and cooked through.
Check the seasoning and adjust salt if needed.
Serve the Nihari Lamb hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
First rinse and chop the tomatoes. Use the tomatoes that are ripe with a sweet tangy taste and not overly sour.
In a blender or grinder make a smooth puree of the chopped tomatoes. Set aside.
Melt butter in a pan. First add bay leaf and saute for a few seconds till aromatic.
Add garlic paste, ginger and tomato puree. Stir. Now add the red chili powder and saute this mixture till the fat begins to leave the side of the tomato paste.
When you see the tomato mixture clumping together and the fat leaving the sides, then add water. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir and add the paneer cubes. Mix and switch off the heat. Lastly add cream and gently stir.
Combine rice wine, sesame oil, ginger paste, salt, and pepper together in a glass or ceramic bowl, add chicken and toss to coat.
Cover and let marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Heat enough oil in a frying pan over medium heat to cover the chicken pieces.
Add chicken to the hot oil and fry until no longer pink in the centers, about 5 minutes.
Place a small amount of fried chicken inside each wrapper. Brush all edges with beaten egg and fold to seal. Set aside.
Heat more oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Pan-fry dumplings, working in batches as needed, until golden, 3 to 5 minutes.
Transfer dumplings to the baking sheet rack as you work to drain excess oil.
Whisk together gochujang, lime juice, sesame oil, garlic paste, and soy sauce for sauce in a bowl. Serve with dumplings.
Place the basmati rice in a large container and cover with several inches of cool water, let stand 30 minutes. Drain.
Heat 1/4 cup oil in a large skillet over medium heat, fry cardamom pods, and cinnamon sticks in the hot oil until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the onions cook and stir until the onions are lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Stir the garlic paste into the onion mixture cook until the garlic is fragrant, about 1 minute more.
Sprinkle the cilantro over the mixture and cook 1 minute more. Add the lamb chops to the skillet, season with salt.
Cook and stir the lamb until the meat begins to brown, about 20 minutes.
Stir the tomatoes into the mixture, continue cooking until the oil begins to separate from the gravy, about 10 minutes.
Add the yogurt and lemon juice, cover and cook until the lamb is tender, about 15 minutes.
Add water as needed to keep the mixture from getting too dry.
Bring the rice, 7 1/2 cups water, and 1 teaspoon salt to a boil in a saucepan until the rice is nearly done yet a little chewy, 10 to 15 minutes drain any excess water.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a small skillet, fry the sliced onion in the hot oil until lightly browned.
Layer about half the rice in the bottom of a deep pot with a lid. Spoon the lamb masala over the rice. Spread the fried onion over the lamb masala. Top with the remaining rice.
Stir the saffron and warm milk together in a small bowl pour over the top layer of rice.
Cover the pot with the lid and place the pot over low heat, cook until the rice is thoroughly cooked, about 15 minutes.
Boil the water and add cloves, cardamom, ½ teaspoon black pepper and salt. Then add rice cook it till it is 70% cooked and drain the excess water.

Heat butter in a pan and add cumin, bay leaf and cayenne pepper. Then add ginger and garlic paste and chili paste. Saute for a min before adding the thinly sliced onions. Spring onions and fry them till they are golden brown. Then add salt, ½ teaspoon black pepper and lemon juice, mix well for 2-3 mins.

Place the first layer of cooked onions at the bottom of the pan, add a layer of rice and again the layer of cooked onions. Then seal the pan tightly with aluminium foil and place a heavy lid on top. Cook the biryani on medium heat for 5-10 mins.
Before serving, garnish with coriander.
Dice chicken breasts and season with salt and pepper. Put flour on a plate an dredge chicken shaking off excess flour.
Bring 1½ cup chicken stock to a boil in a medium sauce pan. Add rice and peas and bring back up to boil. Turn off heat and cover.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chicken pieces and lemon zest. Cook turning occasionally until chicken is browned.
Add remaining 1 cup of chicken stock and garlic stir to combine. Lower heat to medium and cook until bubbly and sauce is thick, about 5 minutes.
Add butter to chicken and sauce mix. Turn off heat. Add lemon juice and stir to combine. Serve chicken and sauce with rice and peas.
Combine chicken, chicken broth, water, coconut cream, garlic chile paste, coconut aminos, lime juice, salt, ginger, and basil in a multi-functional pressure cooker.
Close and lock the lid. Select Soup/Broth function according to manufacturer's instructions, set timer for 30 minutes.
Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build. Release pressure carefully using the slow-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, about 10 minutes.
Release remaining pressure using the quick-release method, about 5 minutes. Unlock and remove the lid. Serve soup in bowls and sprinkle with fresh cilantro
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Set aside a rimmed baking sheet with a fitted rack, lined with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whisk together salt and ghee. Add the chicken wings, and toss them together to completely coat.
Using tongs, place wings on the baking tray rack, and bake for 25 minutes. Transfer wings into a large bowl and set aside. Meanwhile, prepare the butter sauce.
Heat the butter, garlic paste, turmeric, ground coriander, garam masala, and cumin in a small saucepan, over low heat. Whisk untill butter is melted and combined.
Add the tomato puree, water, and fenugreek. Stir together, cover, and cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in the salt, and cook for another 5 minutes. Add cream, whisk together, and cook uncovered for a few minutes, until the sauce thickens. Remove from heat.
Pour ¾ sauce over the wings, and toss to coat. Using a slotted spoon, return wings to the baking rack and bake for 10 minutes, or longer for desired crispiness
Transfer baked wings into the bowl containing the remaining butter sauce, and toss to coat. Serve immediately.
pork chop: Preheat your air fryer to 390°F.
Season the pork loin chop with salt and pepper on both sides.
Place the flour, beaten egg, and breadcrumbs in three separate shallow dishes.
Dredge the pork loin chop in the flour, shaking off any excess. Then dip it into the beaten egg, allowing any excess to drip off. Finally, coat it evenly with breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the breaded pork loin chop in the air fryer basket.
