Air fry at 390°F for 12-15 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the pork chop is golden brown and cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the pork loin chop from the air fryer and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the air-fried Wiener Schnitzel hot with lemon wedges and your favorite side dishes.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season both sides of the pork chop with salt, pepper, and dried thyme.
Add the pork chop to the skillet and sear for about 2-3 minutes on each side until it's browned.
Remove the pork chop from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced potatoes and onions. Cook for about 5 minutes, or until they start to brown.
Return the seared pork chop to the skillet, placing it on top of the potatoes and onions.
Pour the low-sodium chicken broth into the skillet.
Cover the skillet and simmer for about 10-15 minutes.
Serve the skillet pork chop with potatoes and onions.
Cook the noodles according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a bowl, mix together low-sodium soy sauce, sesame oil, and Sichuan chili paste to make the sauce. Adjust the amount of chili paste to your preferred level of spiciness.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the thinly sliced pork chop with salt and pepper.
Add the pork to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until it's cooked through and slightly browned.
Remove the cooked pork from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add minced garlic and minced ginger. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped bok choy or Chinese cabbage and sliced green onions. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are slightly wilted.
Return the cooked pork to the skillet and pour the prepared sauce over the ingredients.
Toss everything together in the skillet to coat with the sauce.
Serve the spicy Sichuan pork and vegetable mixture over the cooked noodles.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Season the pork chop with salt and pepper on both sides.
Rub a little olive oil on the pork chop to prevent sticking.
Place the pork chop on the grill and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F for medium-rare or 160°F for medium, depending on your preference.
While the pork chop is cooking, place the corn kernels in a small bowl.
In another small bowl, whisk together the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper to make the sauce.
Once the pork chop is cooked to your liking, remove it from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes.
While the pork chop is resting, place the corn kernels on the grill and cook for about 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lightly charred.
Slice the grilled pork chop and serve it with the grilled corn kernels.
Drizzle the sauce over the pork chop and corn.
Preheat your air fryer to 380°F.
Season the pork loin chop with salt and pepper on both sides.
Place the flour, beaten egg, and breadcrumbs in three separate shallow dishes.
Dredge the pork loin chop in the flour, shaking off any excess. Then dip it into the beaten egg, allowing any excess to drip off. Finally, coat it evenly with breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the breaded pork loin chop in the air fryer basket.
Drizzle the melted ghee over the pork loin chop.
Air fry at 380°F for 10-12 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the pork chop is golden brown and cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the pork loin chop from the air fryer and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the Air Fryer Butter Schnitzel hot with lemon wedges and your favorite side dishes.
Preheat a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the pork with salt and pepper on both sides.

Add a little olive oil to the skillet and cook the pork for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F for medium-rare or 160°F for medium, depending on your preference.
While the pork is cooking, prepare the veggies.
In the same skillet, add the corn, diced tomato, and sliced carrot. Cook for about 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the veggies are tender.
Once the pork is cooked to your liking, remove it from the skillet and let it rest for a few minutes.
Serve the cooked pork with the veggies on the side.
Preheat your air fryer to 380°F.
Season the pork loin chop with salt and pepper on both sides.
In a shallow dish, mix the breadcrumbs with paprika, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Place the flour, beaten egg, and seasoned breadcrumbs in three separate shallow dishes.

Dredge the pork loin chop in the flour, shaking off any excess. Then dip it into the beaten egg, allowing any excess to drip off. Finally, coat it evenly with seasoned breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the breaded pork loin chop in the air fryer basket.
Air fry at 380°F for 10-12 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the pork chop is golden brown and cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the pork loin chop from the air fryer and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the air-fried Pork Schnitzel hot with lemon wedges and your favorite side dishes.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Use a sharp knife to create a pocket in the side of the pork chop, being careful not to cut all the way through.
Season the inside of the pocket with salt and pepper.
Stuff the pocket with pesto sauce, pressing it down gently.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and lightly coat it with cooking spray or olive oil.
Sear the pork chop on both sides until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the pork chop to a baking dish and bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until cooked through.
Remove from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season the pork chop with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
In a skillet over medium-high heat, heat the ghee or oil.
Sear the pork chop for about 3-4 minutes on each side until it's nicely browned and cooked through.
Remove the pork chop from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced mushrooms and sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they're tender.
Pour in the broth and let it simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Return the pork chop to the skillet and let it simmer in the mushroom sauce for another minute or so.
Once the sauce thickens slightly and the pork chop is coated, remove from heat.
Season the pork chop with salt and pepper.
Dredge the pork chop in the all-purpose flour, shaking off any excess.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the pork chop to the hot skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side until it's browned and cooked through.
In the same skillet, add the diced onion and sliced mushrooms. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Pour in the Marsala wine and chicken broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Add the raw fettuccine pasta to the skillet. Make sure it's submerged in the sauce.
Cover the skillet and simmer for about 10-12 minutes, or until the pasta is tender and cooked to your liking, stirring occasionally.
Return the cooked pork chop to the skillet, coating it with the Marsala sauce and pasta.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, allowing the pork chop to absorb the flavors of the sauce.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, whisk together rice vinegar, ketchup,  sauce, and cornstarch. Set the sauce aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Season the pork chop with salt and black pepper.
Add the pork chop to the pan and cook on both sides until it's browned and cooked through (internal temperature should reach 145°F or 63°C).
Remove the pork chop from the pan and set it aside.
In the same pan, add diced onions and diced bell peppers. Sauté until they start to soften.
Stir in the pineapple chunks.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth and the sauce you prepared earlier.
Stir well and let the sauce simmer for a few minutes until it thickens.
Return the cooked pork chop to the pan and coat it with the sweet and sour sauce.
Serve with rice!
Season the pork chop with salt and pepper.
Dredge the pork chop in flour, then dip it into the beaten egg, and finally coat it with breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Heat  oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the breaded pork chop to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the cooked pork chop from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add sliced mushrooms and cook until they are softened.
Pour in broth and stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Return the cooked pork chop to the skillet and let it simmer in the mushroom sauce for a few minutes.
Garnish with chopped parsley if desired.
Serve hot with lemon wedges on the side.
Preheat your air fryer to 380°F.
Season the pork loin chop with salt and pepper on both sides.
Place the flour, beaten egg, and panko breadcrumbs in three separate shallow dishes.

Dredge the pork loin chop in the flour, shaking off any excess. Then dip it into the beaten egg, allowing any excess to drip off. Finally, coat it evenly with panko breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the breaded pork loin chop in the air fryer basket.
Air fry at 380°F for 10-12 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until the pork is golden brown and cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the pork loin chop from the air fryer and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season both sides of the pork chop with a pinch of salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
In an ovenproof skillet, sear the pork chop for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown.
Combine tomato sauce, diced tomatoes, oregano, and basil in a small bowl. Mix well.
Pour the tomato sauce mixture over the pork chop. Add sliced mushrooms and bell peppers around the chop.
Place the skillet in the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes.
While the pork is baking, steam the peas and green beans according to package instructions.
Plate the pork chop with the sauce, mushrooms, and bell peppers. Add the steamed peas and green beans on the side.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the boneless pork chop with salt and black pepper.
In a shallow dish, combine the panko breadcrumbs and grated Parmesan cheese.
Dredge the seasoned pork chop in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing the breadcrumbs onto both sides to coat evenly.
Heat the olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the breaded pork chop to the skillet and cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side until the breadcrumbs are golden brown and the pork is cooked through.
Pour the marinara sauce over the cooked pork chop in the skillet.
Sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese over the top.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for about 5-7 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the Panko-Breaded Pork Parmigiana from the oven.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a small bowl, mix together apple cider, apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard, minced garlic, salt, and pepper to make the glaze.
Heat olive oil in an ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat.
Season both sides of the pork chop with salt and pepper.
Sear the pork chop in the skillet for about 2-3 minutes on each side, until it's browned.
Add the thinly sliced apple and onion to the skillet, and pour the apple cider glaze over everything.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Preheat your air fryer to 400°F.
In a shallow bowl, combine almond flour, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Dip the pork chop into the beaten egg, then coat it with the almond flour mixture, pressing gently to adhere the crumbs to the pork.
Lightly grease the air fryer basket with cooking spray or olive oil.
Place the breaded pork chop in the greased air fryer basket.
Air fry the pork chop at 400°F for 12-15 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until it is golden brown and cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the pork chop from the air fryer and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a small bowl, whisk together the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, dried rosemary, dried thyme, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Place the boneless pork chop on an oven-safe dish or baking sheet lined with parchment paper or foil.
Pour the balsamic vinegar mixture over the pork chop, ensuring it's evenly coated.
If desired, place a fresh rosemary sprig on top of the pork chop for added flavor.
Roast the pork chop in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes.
Remove the Balsamic Roasted Pork Chop from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
salmon fillets: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a lightly greased baking dish.
Season the salmon fillets with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until the salmon flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is cooking, prepare the Creamy Garlic Dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together minced garlic, Greek yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Once the salmon is done, remove it from the oven and let it cool slightly.
In a large salad bowl, arrange the mixed lettuce and tomato slices.
Place the cooked salmon fillets on top of the salad.
Squeeze lemon wedges over the salmon and salad for extra flavor.
Drizzle the Creamy Garlic Dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad gently to combine all the ingredients.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking sheet and season with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until it flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is baking, in a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, and sesame seeds to create the dressing.
In two serving plates, divide the mixed greens and arrange the halved cherry tomatoes and sliced red bell pepper.
Once the salmon is cooked, let it cool slightly and then flake it into bite-sized pieces.
Place the flaked salmon on top of the prepared salads.
Drizzle the Asian Ginger Sesame Dressing over the salads.
Garnish each plate with slices of lime.
Preheat the oven to 375°F. Oil a shallow baking dish.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Whisk soy sauce, hot water, and miso together in a large glass or ceramic bowl until thoroughly combined.
Add salmon and turn several times to coat. Marinate for 10 minutes, occasionally spooning the mixture over the fish to make sure its covered.
Transfer salmon to the prepared baking dish and cover with foil. Leave extra marinade in the bowl.
Bake in the preheated oven for 7 minutes. Uncover, spoon remaining marinade evenly onto the salmon, and bake until fish flakes easily with a fork.
Serve with rice and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Season the salmon with salt and set aside to release water.
Heat half the oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the salmon skin-side down and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Turn the fish over and cook for another 2-3 minutes on the other side. Transfer to a plate.
Heat remaining oil in the pan over medium-low heat. Add the asparagus spears, season lightly with salt and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring twice or three times during the process.
Plate the salmon with asparagus on the side, a drizzle of lemon juice some fresh cracked pepper.
Whisk together the soy sauce, hoisin sauce, chili sauce, lemon juice, ginger, garlic, and olive oil in a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Place the salmon fillets into the marinade, and turn to evenly coat. Cover the dish with plastic wrap, and marinate in the refrigerator for 30 minutes.
Preheat an oven to 350°F.
Cook the rice in a pot od water accoriding to the instruction on the packaging.
Remove and discard the plastic wrap from the salmon, use a spoon to scoop up the marinade that has collected in the bottom of the baking dish, and drizzle it over the salmon fillets.
Bake in the preheated oven until the salmon flakes easily with a fork, about 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F .
Rinse salmon, and arrange in a baking dish. Sprinkle salt, pepper, onion powder, and dill over the fish and potatoes. Oil over top.
Bake in preheated oven for 20 to 25 minutes. Salmon is done when it flakes easily with a fork.
In a medium pot, melt butter. Add chopped and saute for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
When ready, add diced potatoes, salt, black pepper, stock and fresh dill. Mix and cover with a lid. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add salmon chunks and push them down so they are submerged in the liquid.
Stir in cream and cook for 5 minutes more. Garnish with parsley and serve.
Heat coconut oil in a pot over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add green beans and saute until nearly tender, about 3 minutes. Pour in stock and bring to a boil. Add salmon, curry powder, and garlic powder.
Add coconut cream, reduce heat, and simmer until salmon flesh flakes easily with a fork, 3 to 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper, garnish with basil and chili.
Preheat oven to 375˚F.
Arrange salmon fillets on parchment paper–lined baking sheet; sprinkle with salt and pepper. Roast for 10 minutes and slice.
Meanwhile, cook pasta according to package directions. Drain and reserve 1/4 cup pasta water.
In separate pot of boiling water, cook broccoli for 3 minutes or until tender-crisp. Drain and pat dry.
Arrange pasta on serving plate, broccoli, salmon,  basil, lemon juice, and grated Parmesan.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Pour the vegetable stock into a small saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat. Boil for a few minutes to slightly reduce the stock.
Add the cream, butter, chives, parsley, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to the stock, and whisk to combine.
Reduce the temperature to low and keep uncovered until salmon is being prepared.
Season the salmon on both sides with salt and pepper, place in the baking dish, and top it with the lemon sauce. Bake the salmon in the oven for 12 minutes.
Remove salmon from the oven and garnish with chopped parsley. Serve warm.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Rub the butter into the flour until you have a breadcrumb texture. Add water, a little at a time, to form a dough. Wrap in clingfilm, put in the freezer for 10 minutes.
Lightly flour a worktop and roll the pastry out so it's a little bigger than a 8-in tart tin. Prick the base all over with a fork then return the pastry to the freezer for another 10 minutes.
Blind bake the pie dough for 10-15 minutes, take out and let to cool a bit.
In the meantime mix together the rest of the ingredients in a large bowl and season to taste with salt and pepper. Once the pie dough has cooled slightly, pour the mixture onto the dough.
Place the tin back in the oven, bake for an additional 30-45 minutes or until golden brown on top. Take the quiche out, allow it to cool slightly before serving.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile toast sesame seeds in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat once sesame seeds are a golden brown.
Combine miso paste, mirin, soy sauce, ginger, and honey in a small bowl. Mix well. Stir in toasted sesame seeds.
Set an oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler. Line a baking pan with foil and lightly grease with oil.
Coat salmon well in the miso sauce and place skin-side up on the baking pan and broil in the preheated oven, 10 to 15 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 375°F. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and oil.
Place salmon fillets skin-side down on the baking sheet. Brush with remaining oil and sprinkle evenly with 1 1/2 teaspoons Greek seasoning.
Mix crushed pistachios, bread crumbs, and remaining 1 teaspoon Greek seasoning together in a small bowl. Sprinkle evenly over salmon.
Add kale, asparagus and tomatoes. Bake in the preheated oven until salmon flakes easily with a fork, about 20 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350°F and lay baking tray with baking paper.
Place orange juice, honey, ginger and garlic in a small pan over medium heat, and bring to the boil.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes or until slightly reduced and syrupy.
Place salmon on prepared tray. Reserve half the glaze. Use remaining glaze to brush over salmon fillets. Bake for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a large frypan over high heat. Add the baby spinach leaves and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes, until just wilted. Season.
To serve, divide the wilted spinach and salmon fillets among serving plates and garnish with some sprigs of thyme.
Preheat broiler.
Combine the soy sauce, honey, ginger, sesame oil and garlic powder in a small bowl, stirring with a whisk.
Coarsely chop bok choy leaves, and arrange on one end of a jelly-roll pan.
Coarsely chop bok choy stems and arrange in a single layer on opposite end of jelly-roll pan.
Place salmon, skin side down, in a single layer on top of leafy greens.
Pour half of soy sauce mixture evenly over salmon; pour remaining half evenly over bok choy stems.
Broil for 5 minutes; stir stems.
Broil an additional 4 minutes or until desired degree of doneness.
Arrange ⅓ cup bok choy on each of 4 plates.
Top each serving with 1 fillet and 1 tbsp green onions.
Prejeat the onion to 350°F.
Place the salmon in an oven safe skillet. Place the onion rings and onion on top of the filet. Add tomatoes.
Pepper to taste and cook for 15 minutes or until salmon flakes easily with a fork. Garnish with parsley.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season salmon fillets on both sides with lemon pepper.
In a large skillet, melt butter over medium high heat. Stir in garlic. Place salmon in pan. Cook for 10 minutes per inch of thickness, or until fish flakes.
Flip fillets halfway through cooking to brown on both sides. Sprinkle with lemon juice before serving.
Season salmon with salt and pepper and cook on a grill for about 5 minutes on each side. When done, slice the salmon on small chunks.
In a bowl mix all other ingredients and season with olive oil, salt and lemon juice.
Place salad mix and salmon chunks on a platter and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Cook the broccoli in a pot of water for 3 - 5 minutes.
Combine the salt, pepper, mixed herbs and garlic granules, if using, in a bowl, then scatter onto a plate.
Rub salmon fillet with a little olive oil and roll in the seasoning to coat.
Put in the air fryer basket in one layer and cook at 180C for 8-10 mins, until cooked through.
Keep checking after 10 mins and cook in 1-2 min blasts, until ready.
Preheat a gas grill to high. Drizzle salmon and peppers with oil and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Place them on a grill. Grill the salmon until browned, 5 minutes on each side. Cook the peppers, turning frequently, until tender and well browned, 10 minutes total.
Place them on a plate and serve with arugula and slices of lemon.
butter lettuce: Rinse whole lettuce leaves and pat dry, being careful not tear them. Set aside.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook and stir beef and cooking oil in the hot skilletfor 5 to 7 minutes. Drain and discard grease, transfer beef to a bowl.
Cook and stir onion in the same skillet used for beef until slightly tender, 5 to 10 minutes. Stir hoisin sauce, garlic, soy sauce, vinegar and ginger sauce into onions.
Add onions, sesame oil, and cooked beef. cook and stir until the onions just begin to wilt, about 2 minutes.
Arrange lettuce leaves around the outer edge of a large serving platter and pile meat mixture in the center.
Peel, devein and roughly chop half the prawns. Place in a bowl with mango and avocado.

Grate the zest of half a lime, then juice both limes. Place the lime zest and juice in a small bowl with the coriander, chilli and oil. Whisk to combine and season well. Pour over prawn mixture and toss to combine.
Divide lettuce among plates, fill with the salad and serve with the remaining prawns.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet.
Add in diced onion and cook until softened.
Now add in ground chicken, garlic and sesame oil. Cook until the chicken is no longer pink.
Combine hoisin sauce, amino acids (or soy sauce), rice vinegar, sriracha, ginger, water chestnuts, green onions (reserving some for topping the lettuce wraps) and red pepper flakes.
Heath through and then taste and season with salt and pepper, as desired.
Fill butter lettuce with chicken mixture and serve. Enjoy!
Pick your dipping sauce of choice above and mix all the sauce ingredients together.
Taste the sauce and adjust the seasonings as necessary.
I like to prepare the sauce before making the spring rolls so that the flavors have a longer time to develop.
Fill a medium saucepan or small pot with water and bring it to boil on high heat.
Once the water boils, add the shrimp and cook for 2 minutes. Remove the shrimp from the water and let them cool for 5 minutes.
Peel the shells from the shrimp. Lay a shrimp on its side on a chopping board.
Gently press the shrimp down with one hand and use the other to slice the shrimp in half, horizontally. Repeat with the remaining shrimp.
Fill a large saucepan or small pot with water and bring it to boil on high heat.
Turn off the heat. Let the noodles sit in the hot water for about 5 to 8 minutes.
Double check the package directions to see how long they should sit in hot water as the cooking time varies across different brands.
Once the noodles have softened, drain and rinse the noodles under cold water.
I like cutting the noodles with kitchen scissors several times so they are shorter and easier to grab.
Fill a large bowl with lukewarm water to wet the rice paper sheets.
Transfer all the vegetables, shrimp, herbs, and noodles into bowls and arrange them on your work top.
Construct the rolls with the ingredients.
Serve the spring rolls with the dipping sauce.
white rice: Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add rice, cook and stir until heated through, 2 to 3 minutes
Stir in soy sauce and sesame oil. Add onion, corn, peas and carrot, cook and stir until tender, 5 to 10 minutes.
In a pot, cook the rice according to the instructions on the packaging. Once done, fluff with a fork and set aside for plating.
Place the broccoli in a steamer and steam for 5–8 minutes, until bright green and tender-crisp. Remove from heat and set aside.
In a small bowl or liquid measuring cup, whisk together the soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic. Set aside.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat, add the olive oil, and brown the sliced chicken, cooking until fully cooked.
Pour the sauce over the chicken in the skillet and let it simmer for 1–2 minutes, or until the sauce thickens and coats the chicken.

Divide the rice between serving plates, placing it in a small mound. Arrange the cooked chicken and sauce over the rice. Place the steamed broccoli beside the rice and chicken. Optionally, drizzle a bit of extra sauce over the broccoli for added flavor.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to the boil. Add the rice and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool.
Heat 1 tablespoon of the coconut oil in a large skillet. Add the onion and saute for 2 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add chicken breasts and cook until brown on both sides, about 4 minutes per side. Add carrots, potatoes and cherry tomatoes and cook until lightly browned.
Stir in curry powder. Cook for 1 minute or until fragrant. Whisk in coconut milk and chicken stock. Gently simmer for 20 minutes or until potatoes and carrots are cooked through.
Remove from heat, serve with rice and garnish with cilantro.
Bring a pot of water to the boil. Add rice and cook as per package instructions. Drain, set aside and then fluff using two forks.
Heat half the olive oil in a non-stick wok or pan. Add the eggs, season with salt and pepper, and scramble until done. Set aside.

Add the remaining olive oil to the same pan, then the onion and celery, saute for 4-5 minutes. Add the carrot, ham, and scallions. Stir fry for 2-3 minutes on high heat, or until the veggies are tender.
Add the rice and soy sauce, mix well and serve immediately.
Marinate the beef with the soy sauce and ketchup for at least 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, bring a pot of water to a rolling boil, season the water with salt, and cook the rice according to instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat the oil in a wok, then saute the garlic until golden brown, being very careful not to burn it. Add the marinated meat and stir fry for 5 minutes, or less if you want the beef more rare.
On a plate, arrange a layer of cucumbers and beef, top with onion. Enjoy alongside rice.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
For the "egg" mixture, mix the flour, black salt, salt, olive oil and water together with a whisk, until you have a smooth batter.
In a large frying pan, heat up the sesame oil on high heat. Then add the "egg" mixture, let it cook for 30 seconds and then scramble it up with a spatula.
Continue until the egg mixture is all well cooked. Clear some space in the pan, add the peas, carrot, corn and onions. Cook for 30 seconds before stirring in with rice.
Mix the garlic powder with soy sauce, and pour it all over the rice. Stir everything up and serve immediately.
Mix together the fish sauce, soy sauce, kecap manis, oyster sauce and water in a bowl or jug and set aside.
Heat up a large drizzle of oil in a wok or stir-fry pan, then stir-fry the green beans for a few minutes.
Add the chopped chillis and garlic and stir-fry for a couple more minutes.
Add the pork and stir-fry until cooked an additional few minutes.
Pour in the sauce, stir well, then let bubble for a minute or two more.
Stir in the basil until wilted, then serve with the rice and a fried egg, if desired.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instrictions on the packaging.
Add oil to a large skillet. Add garlic and cook for one minute. Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Add chicken breast and cook 5-6 minutes on each side until fully cooked.Pour in the wine and allow to cook for one minute.
Add in the chicken stock and simmer until slightly reduced. Stir in lemon juice, lemon zest, and parsley.
Serve immediately with extra lemon slices and parsley for garnish. Enjoy it with rice.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil and cook the rice to the package instructions. Drain the rice and set it aside.
In a large skillet or wok, over medium-high heat, add the sesame oil and onion, and saute for 2-3 minutes. Add the ham and saute for an additional 3-4 minutes.
Add the carrots and stir continuously for 2 minutes. Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Push the mixture to one side of the pan, add the eggs to the other side, stir to scramble.
Add cooked rice, season with soy sauce to taste and mix well. Serve hot.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to the boil. Add the rice and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool.

Heat 1 tablespoon of the olive oil in a skillet and add the pork belly. Season it with salt and pepper. Cook 5 minutes on one side, 5 minutes on the other side until nicely browned. Remove from the skillet and set aside, leaving the drippings in skillet.
Add the garlic and ginger into the skillet and stir-fry until aromatic, then add the scallions, tomatoes and spinach. Cook for 8min until spinach and tomatoes soften.
Add the pork belly back into the skillet and mix together with the rest of the ingredients. Serve immediately with rice.
Bring a pot of water to the boil. Cook the rice to package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool. Once cooled, fluff the rice using a fork.
In a separate pan, add olive oil and heat on medium heat. Add onion, a pinch of salt, and saute for 2-3 minutes. Add zucchini, saute for additional 3 minutes.
Add tomatoes, and simmer on low heat for 5 minutes. A minute or so before finishing the sauce off, add corn and stir the mixture well. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Divide the rice between plates, top with vegetable sauce and enjoy.
Bring water and rice to a boil in a saucepan. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes.
Combine cooked rice, protein powder, cashew milk, coconut milk, coconut flakes, maple syrup, chia seeds, cinnamon, and vanilla extract in a bowl, mix well.
Serve topped with blueberries and raspberries
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile heat oil in large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and cook chicken, stirring frequently, 8 minutes or until chicken is almost done.
Stir in bell peppers and mushrooms and taco seasoning, cook, stirring occasionally, 5 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Stir in prepared rice and chicken broth; heat through. Season, if desired, with salt and pepper.
Bring a pot of water to the boil. Add the rice and cook it to the package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool.
In a large skillet, saute meat and onion in oil until lightly browned, about 3 minutes. Add carrots and potatoes. Add 3 cups of water and bring to the boil.
Reduce heat, cover and simmer until meat and potatoes are tender, about 10 minutes. Add curry and saute to coat.
Simmer for another 5 minutes or so, stirring constantly if curry sauce becomes too thick, just add water. To serve, ladle the curry over rice.
Bring a pot of water to the boil. Add rice, cook to packet instructions, then strain and set aside. Place a non-stick pan on medium heat, add 1 tbsp olive oil.
Add chicken breasts, season with salt and pepper, cook for 4-6 minutes on each side, or until the chicken is done. Set aside to cool before cutting into strips.
To a small mixing bowl, add avocado, 1 tbsp olive oil, salt and pepper, shallot and lemon juice. Mash it well using a fork to make guacamole.
Warm the tortillas in a dry pan. Add the rice, chicken, guacamole, tomatoes and black beans. Add a drizzle of olive oil and some black pepper before wrapping the burritos and enjoying.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile in a large skillet, melt the teaspoon of butter and fry the salmon fillets until they are flaky on the inside and a little golden on the outside.
In a medium skillet, place the oil, peas and mushrooms then saute for 3 minutes. Set aside. Place cooked mushrooms on top of fillets.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil. Add the rice and cook to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a non-stick skillet or wok. Add the onion and saute for 3-4 minutes. Add the carrots and peppers and saute for another minute or two.
Add the meat, season with salt and pepper to taste, and cook for 6-7 minutes, or until the meat is cooked through. Add the tomatoes and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
Divide the rice between two plates, add the meaty sauce, top with parsley and serve.
Prepare the beef by trimming fat, and dicing it into one-inch cubes. Toss into the slow cooker, and sprinkle with pepper, salt, garlic powder, and oregano.
Stir to coat the beef cubes. Top with the sliced onion and cook on high for 4-6 hours.
20 minutes before the hearts are ready, bring a pot of water to a boil and cook the rice to the package instructions.
Serve over rice.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, cook and stir until onion is translucent.
Stir in the chili powder and cumin, and cook for about 30 seconds.
Add the rice, chicken broth and tomato paste, and bring to a boil. Transfer to a 1 quart casserole dish, and cover with aluminum foil or a lid.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, or until liquid has been absorbed and rice is tender.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile oil a nonstick skillet and heat over medium-high heat for 1 minute. Add the chicken and stir-fry until it's well browned, stirring often.
Add the garlic and vegetables and stir-fry for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in the soy sauce, stock and orange juice. Cook and stir until the mixture boils and thickens. Serve over the rice. Garnish with green onion.
oyster sauce: Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, marinate thinly sliced chicken breast with soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce. Set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and minced ginger to the skillet and cook until fragrant.
Add sliced onion and sliced bell peppers to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add marinated chicken to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through.
Stir in fresh Thai basil leaves and cook until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, topped with egg made to taste, and with cooked rice on the side.
In a wok or skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add thinly sliced chicken to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 3-4 minutes).
Toss in mixed vegetables, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil.
Pour the sauce over the chicken and vegetables, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Fill a large pot with lightly salted water and bring to a rolling boil. Drop in soba noodles and return to a boil for about 7 minutes, drain.
Meanwhile, combine soy sauce, oyster sauce, and mirin in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add peas, onion, and green beans and saute until softened but still crisp, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add soba noodles and toss with the cooked vegetables. Mix in soy sauce mixture and cook for an additional 1 to 2 minutes
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add noodles and cook about 2 to 4 minutes, drain.
In a large skillet or wok over high heat with a small amount or oil, cook carrot, bean sprouts and garlic.
Mix in salt, broth, water, soy sauce and oyster sauce, stir. Pour over noodles and toss lightly.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, and fish sauce. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant.
Add sliced chicken breast to the skillet and cook until no longer pink.
Add sliced chicken breast to the skillet and cook until no longer pink.
Pour the sauce mixture into the skillet and stir to coat the chicken and vegetables evenly.
Add sliced red chili pepper and fresh basil leaves to the skillet. Cook for another minute until basil is wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the spicy basil chicken hot.
Mix all the ingredients for the sauce in a small bowl and set aside.
Remove your udon noodles from its package and add it to a pot of boiling water.
Frozen udon noodles can be added directly to the pot of boiling water. Stir gently until your udon noodles are separated and pliable.
Rinse and drain your noodles under cold water. If not using right away, drizzle on a little bit of oil to keep them from sticking.
Heat your pan over high heat and add the oil. Add in the ground meat and stir fry for 1 minute before adding the rest of your vegetables.
Stir fry together for 2 to 3 minutes and then add in the noodles and sauce. Continue stir frying for about 3 minutes or until well combined.
Serve immediately.
Marinate the pork with the cornstarch, oyster sauce, and olive oil.
Meanwhile, in a medium pot, bring 4 cups of water to a boil. Add the rice and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat, and cover and cook for about 15 minutes.
Add the ginger and pork. Stir and simmer for another 5 minutes or so. Finally, stir in the pepper and salt to taste.
Serve piping hot with chopped scallions.
First, we'll bring a pot of water to boil.
Boil the noodles for about 1-2 minutes, and work to spread the noodles apart with chopsticks the entire time.
Then, we'll drain the noodles into a colander.
The goal is to get the noodles to be pretty dry, so shake the colander a few times to get excess water.
Continue separating the noodles with chopsticks for 30-60 seconds. Cover the colander with a plate.
This helps the noodles get to the perfect texture, allowing them to cook a bit longer without introducing excess moisture.
For the chicken, Cut the chicken into little strips. Place the strips in a bowl. Marinate the chicken with cornstarch and water.
Using our hands, we'll mix and massage the bowl for 20-30 seconds.
We'll also crack and beat eggs. For the shrimp, as long as they're peeled and deveined, we don't have to do anything to them.
After washing our vegetables, we'll be cutting carrots, onions, and celery into slices and strips.
For bell pepper, use several different colors of bell peppers, but if you want to keep it simple, you can just use an entire, single bell pepper.
Chop green onions  into roughly 1 inch pieces. We'll also be adding bean sprouts later as well.
The amounts and types of vegetables are really up to you. Anything goes with Singapore Noodles (with some caveats):
Try to avoid veggies with higher water content (like bok choy), since this will lead to a more soggy dish.
Try to avoid having an excessive amount of vegetables, since even lower water content vegetables will leak out water.
We'll create our sauce by mixing curry powder, oyster sauce, salt and water.
As written, the amount of curry powder my dad uses is pretty mild, so if you want a stronger flavor and/or more yellow finish, feel free to increase the amount of curry powder.
Curry powder is absolutely essential for Singapore noodles.

With so many options, the type of curry powder you use for this dish really impacts the final flavor profile, so you might want to experiment with several options until you land on something you love.
When cooking this dish, along with other noodle stir-fries, we'll want to get our wok really hot before adding oil.
Heat the wok for 2-3 minutes on high heat. While we wait, we'll continue loosening up our noodles. Once the wok is hot enough, add 1-2 tsp of oil.
Pour in the eggs: cook the eggs very gently, leaving the first side intact for 20-30 seconds before starting to flip and scramble the eggs.
We want the eggs to be really soft. In total, we're cooking the eggs for about a minute here, and tossing them back in later.
Transfer the eggs onto a plate, add oil: Next, we'll add another tbsp of a high smoke point oil like oil, and roll it around the pan.
Cook the chicken + shrimp: We'll be cooking the chicken now, so remix the chicken + marinade around with chopsticks for a few seconds.
Cook the chicken for 30-45 seconds before adding the shrimp.
Add the shrimp, and cook both meats until the shrimp starts to turn orange, and the chicken starts to turn white.
Transfer the meat onto a plate, add 1 tbsp of oil. Cook the vegetables for about 60-90 seconds.
Transfer the vegetables onto a plate, add 1 tbsp of oil. Cook the noodles for about 1-2 minutes before flipping them.
After flipping, cook the noodles for another 2-3 minutes, occasionally moving them around with your chopsticks.
Add the sauce and cook the noodles for another 2 minutes before adding everything else back in.
Add everything else in: Over the course of about 2 minutes, we'll be progressively adding the vegetables, followed by the chicken and shrimp, the char siu, bean sprouts, and lastly, some eggs.
Evenly mix the ingredients in well with the noodles. Add sesame oil, and mix everything for another 30-45 seconds.
In a small bowl, whisk together chicken broth, soy sauce, oyster sauce, rice vinegar, and cornstarch until smooth. Set aside.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add diced onion, carrot, celery, and bell pepper to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are tender, about 5-7 minutes.
Add shrimp to the skillet and cook until they turn pink and opaque, about 2-3 minutes.
Stir the sauce mixture once more and then pour it into the skillet with the shrimp and vegetables. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens, about 1-2 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic. Cook, stirring, for 3 minutes. Add beef strips. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes.
Add carrot. Cook, stirring for 5 minutes. Stir in stock. Season with pepper. Bring to the boil. Reduce heat to medium-low. Simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, place noodles in a pot filled with water. Cook  for 3 to 5 minutes. Drain.
Add green beans, soy sauce and oyster sauce. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 3 minutes. Add noodles. Toss to combine. Serve.
Place your steamer rack in a wok on high heat.
Pour enough water so that the top of the rack isn't submerged, and start boiling water.
Take the rack out, separate and lay out noodles (12 oz) on top of the rack.
Once boiling, set the steamer rack + noodles back in the wok and cover for 10 minutes. Leave the stove on high heat.
If you're using a steamer rack without holes (i.e. steaming on a plate where the steam can't easily access the bottom, steam for 2-3 extra minutes.)
Once the 10 minutes is up, quickly dump the noodles in the water for 15-30 seconds.
Drain noodles through a colander, and spend about 1-2 minutes fluffing and separating the noodles with a chopstick.
Let it cool for 3-5 minutes.
We'll wash and chop our vegetables:
Mix dark soy sauce (1 tbsp), light soy sauce (1 tbsp), oyster sauce (1 tbsp), sugar (2 tsp), water (1 tbsp) in a bowl until the sugar dissolves.
We'll be adding the sesame oil later.
We'll start by heating our wok to around 350-400°F.
Depending on your stove, this should take around 2-3 minutes.
.Make room for the bean sprouts by pushing the noodles aside in the wok
Set the bean sprouts in the wok, and then cover them with the noodles.
This helps trap some of the heat inside to help them cook more evenly and quickly.
Let the bean sprouts cook underneath the noodles for 30-45 seconds.
Add the green onions (everything, the cooked roots + raw shoots) and onions, and cook for about 1-2 minutes.
Continue separating the noodles with chopsticks and a spatula.
Pour in the sauce mix, and mix everything around for 1 minute.
We'll want to let the sauce evaporate as much as we can.
Add sesame oil (1 tsp), and mix and separate the noodles for another 1-2 minutes, and then we can start plating.
Transfer the noodles onto a plate, and call your loved ones over.
Time to eat!
Season pork chops with pepper and salt. Dredge the meat into the flour, shake excess flour and then dip it into the beaten egg, shake excess.
Dredge in panko, then cover the meat evenly with panko breadcrumbs by patting with your hands to make the meat is throughly covered.
Working in batches, deep fry the cutlet, until golden on the bottom for about 3 minutes. Flip and cook on the other side for 3 minutes.
In a medium bowl stir ketchup, Worcestershire, soy and oyster sauce.
Serve sauce and pork chops with shredded cabbage.
Cook the lo mein noodles according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet or wok, heat the vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for about 30 seconds, until fragrant.
Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook until pink and opaque, about 2-3 minutes per side. Remove the shrimp from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the broccoli florets and sliced red bell pepper. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes, until tender-crisp.
Return the cooked shrimp to the skillet with the vegetables.
Add the cooked lo mein noodles to the skillet, along with the soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, and red pepper flakes, if using. Toss everything together until well combined and heated through.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving plate.
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve hot.
Cook the noodles following packet directions. Drain well.

Meanwhile, heat the oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat. Stir-fry the chicken, in 2 batches, for 2 minutes or until golden brown and cooked through, transferring to a plate between batches.
Combine the chicken, onion, garlic, ginger and broccoli florets and capsicum in the wok or pan. Stir-fry for 4 minutes or until broccoli is tender.
Add the oyster sauce with 1/4 cup water. Stir-fry for 2 mins or until heated through.
Divide noodles and stir-fry among serving bowls. Serve immediately
Combine broth, soy sauce, oyster sauce, and chili garlic sauce, stirring with a whisk.
Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add pork. Cook 5 minutes or until done, stirring to crumble.
Add ginger and garlic. Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly. Add tofu and cook 5 minutes or until golden, stirring frequently.
Add broth mixture to pan. Bring to a boil. Cook 1 minute or until mixture thickens. Remove from heat.
Sprinkle with onions and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, marinate thinly sliced beef with soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce. Set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and minced ginger to the skillet and cook until fragrant.
Add sliced onion and sliced bell peppers to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add marinated beef to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through.
Stir in fresh Thai basil leaves and cook until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, topped with egg, and with cooked rice on the side.
Cook the egg noodles according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and thinly sliced onion to the skillet. Cook until fragrant and the onions become translucent.
Add the julienned carrots and sliced cabbage to the skillet. Stir-fry for a few minutes until the vegetables are slightly softened.
Add the sliced chicken or shrimp to the skillet and cook until heated through.
Push the ingredients to one side of the skillet and add the cooked egg noodles to the other side. Break up any clumps of noodles with a spatula.
Mix the noodles with the other ingredients in the skillet, combining them well.
Add the soy sauce, oyster sauce, ground white pepper, and salt to taste. Stir-fry to evenly distribute the sauces and seasonings.
Continue cooking and stirring for a few more minutes until the noodles are heated through and well-coated with the sauce.
Remove from heat and transfer the Bami Goreng to a plate.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a wok or skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add thinly sliced beef to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 2-3 minutes).
Toss in broccoli florets, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until the broccoli is slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil.
Pour the sauce over the beef and broccoli, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Heat large skillet on medium heat. Add oil, then garlic. Cook garlic until translucent and fragrant, then add chicken.
Cook chicken for about 2 minutes, then add sesame seed oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar and oyster sauce.
Cook mixture for about another 5 minutes or until chicken is cooked. Add zucchini and cook until tender, about 2 minutes.
Before serving, sprinkle with sesame seeds, and ground black pepper.
Mix together the fish sauce, soy sauce, kecap manis, oyster sauce and water in a bowl or jug and set aside.
Heat up a large drizzle of oil in a wok or stir-fry pan, then stir-fry the green beans for a few minutes.
Add the chopped chillis and garlic and stir-fry for a couple more minutes.
Add the pork and stir-fry until cooked an additional few minutes.
Pour in the sauce, stir well, then let bubble for a minute or two more.
Stir in the basil until wilted, then serve with the rice and a fried egg, if desired.
pork loin: Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a wok or skillet, heat ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add pork pieces to the wok, cooking until they are browned and cooked through (about 3-4 minutes).
Toss in diced bell peppers, pineapple chunks, and diced onion, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together rice vinegar, low-sodium soy sauce, tomato paste, and cornstarch.
Pour the sauce over the pork and vegetables, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Combine chili powder and cocoa powder in a bowl, sprinkle over both sides of pork loin
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and brown pork loin on both sides, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer pork and juices to a baking dish.
Roast in the preheated oven until pork is slightly pink in the center, about 12 minutes.
Combine mango, shallot, cilantro, and lemon juice in a bowl. Slice pork loin and top with juices from the baking dish and mango mixture. Top with cheese slices.
In a skillet or wok, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the thinly sliced pork loin to the skillet and cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chopped kimchi, thinly sliced onion, and minced garlic to the skillet.
Stir-fry for another 3-4 minutes, until the onion is softened and the pork is cooked through.
Add the soy sauce and sesame oil to the skillet, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Continue to stir-fry for another 1-2 minutes, until everything is well combined and heated through.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add diced pork loin and cook until browned and cooked through.
Push the pork to one side of the skillet and crack the egg into the other side. Scramble the egg until cooked through.
Add mixed vegetables to the skillet and cook until heated through.
Add cooked rice to the skillet and toss to combine with the pork, egg, and vegetables.
Stir in low-sodium soy sauce until evenly distributed.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a pot, bring chicken broth to a simmer over medium heat.
Add minced garlic, grated ginger, soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper.
Cook for 5 minutes to infuse flavors.
Meanwhile, cook ramen noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a separate pot of boiling water, cook sliced pork for 2-3 minutes until cooked through. Remove from water and set aside.
Soft-boil the egg by cooking it in boiling water for 6-7 minutes. Peel and halve.
Divide cooked noodles into a bowl. Pour the hot broth over the noodles.
Arrange sliced pork, soft-boiled egg halves, sliced green onions on top.
Use a food processor or grater to turn the cauliflower florets into rice-sized pieces.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and add a small amount of olive oil.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until they begin to soften.
Add the cubed pork loin to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides.
Sprinkle paprika over the pork and vegetables, stirring to coat evenly.
Pour in the chicken or vegetable broth and season with salt and pepper. Stir well.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and cook for about 10-12 minutes or until the pork is cooked through and the sauce thickens.
In a separate pan, heat a little olive oil over medium heat.
Add the riced cauliflower to the pan and sauté for 4-5 minutes until it's tender but not mushy. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the Pork Paprikash over the cauliflower "rice". Garnish with chopped parsley if desired.
In a small saucepan, heat a little oil over medium heat.
Add the diced pork loin to the saucepan and cook until browned, about 2-3 minutes.
Stir in the diced butternut squash, sliced red bell pepper, and sliced onion. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until the vegetables start to soften.
Add the red curry paste to the saucepan and cook, stirring constantly, for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the coconut milk and vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the soup simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the fish sauce and lime juice. Taste the soup and adjust the seasoning if necessary.
Remove the saucepan from heat.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Sprinkle pork  with salt and pepper. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
In the same skillet, saute green pepper and onion in remaining 1 teaspoon oil until almost tender, about 2 minutes.
Stir in broth, pineapple, tomato sauce and vinegar. Bring to a boil. Return pork to the pan. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 5 to 7 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in a skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add thinly sliced pork and cook for 3-4 minutes until browned.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic; cook for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Stir in frozen peas and carrots mix; cook for another 2 minutes.
Push the mixture to one side of the skillet, and pour the beaten egg into the other side. Scramble the egg until fully cooked, then mix with the vegetables and pork.
Add cooked rice to the skillet, breaking up any clumps.
Stir in soy sauce, salt, and black pepper; cook for 3-4 minutes until everything is well combined and heated through.
Heat a small amount of cooking oil in a skillet or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the diced pork loin to the hot skillet and cook until it's browned and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes. Remove the pork from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add a bit more oil if needed. Sauté the diced onions, green bell pepper, and red bell pepper for about 3-4 minutes until they become tender.
Return the cooked pork to the skillet and add the canned black beans, kidney beans, and diced tomatoes. Stir to combine.
Season the mixture with chili powder, paprika, salt, and pepper. Adjust the seasonings to your taste.
Simmer the Southern Pork & Beans for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the dish to heat through.
Once everything is heated, transfer the mixture to a serving dish.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Season the pork loin with salt and pepper.
Grill the pork loin for approximately 4-5 minutes per side or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F.

While the pork is grilling, prepare the white bean puree: In a blender or food processor, combine the drained white beans, olive oil, balsamic vinegar, lemon juice, chopped herbs, salt, and pepper. Blend until smooth.
Once the pork loin is cooked through, remove it from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes.
Plate the grilled pork loin and serve it alongside the white bean puree.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Place the pork in a medium bowl, seasoning all over with salt. Combine rice wine vinegar, tomato sauce and soy sauce. Whisk to combine. Set aside.
Add 1 tbsp of the olive oil to a large skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot and shimmering, add in the onion, tomatoes, and garlic. Cook for 1 minute, then set aside.
Return the skillet to medium-high heat. Add in the remaining olive oil. Once hot and shimmering, add in the pork. Cook the pork for 2 minutes.
Pour the sweet and sour sauce into the skillet with the pork. Increase heat to high. Cook, stirring often, for 2 minutes. Add the vegetables and stir to combine.
Cook for 5 minutes before serving on top of the rice.
Place pork, sweet potatoes, onion, tomatoes, chiles, chicken broth, cumin, salt, and pepper into the bottom of a slow cooker.
Cover and cook on Low for 6 to 7 hours. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of the pork should read at least 145°F.
Add lime juice and stir well before serving.
In a bowl, combine minced garlic, soy sauce, kecap manis, tomato ketchup, lime juice, honey, ground coriander, ground ginger, ground cumin, ground white pepper, and chili flakes
Place pork slices in a shallow dish and pour the marinade over them. Let it marinate for at least 1 hour.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Heat vegetable oil in an ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear pork slices on both sides until browned. Pour the reserved marinade into the skillet and bring it to a simmer.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 15-20 minutes. Baste the pork occasionally with the marinade.
Once cooked, remove the skillet from the oven and let the pork rest for a few minutes. Slice the Babi Panggang and serve it hot.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat. Cook and stir onion and garlic in the hot oil until tender, about 5 minutes. Stir in pork and cook 5 minutes.
Stir shrimp into the mixture, season with salt and pepper. Continue cooking until shrimp turn pink, about 5 minutes.
Add tomato to the pot, cover, and let cook for 5 minutes. Stir zucchini, okra, green beans, eggplant, and bitter melon into the mixture.
Cover, and cook until the vegetables are all soft to the touch, about 10 minutes more. Serve hot.
Place the roast in a slow cooker, and season with salt. Place chile peppers and garlic on top of roast. Pour in the chipotle sauce and 1/2 cup water.
Cover, and cook 7 hours on Low.
In a pot, bring remaining 2 3/4 cups water and rice to a boil. Mix in the lime juice and cilantro. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer 20 minutes.
Remove roast from the slow cooker, and use two forks to shred.
Return pork to the slow cooker, and allow to sit 15 minutes to absorb some of the liquid. Serve over the cooked rice.
In a bowl, mix together soy sauce, gochujang, rice vinegar, sesame oil, minced garlic, and minced ginger.
Add the thinly sliced pork loin to the bowl and toss until well coated with the marinade.
Let the pork marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the marinated pork slices to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Once cooked, remove the pork from the skillet and transfer to a serving plate.
Garnish with thinly sliced green onions and sesame seeds.
Serve the Korean spicy marinated pork hot.
Heat oven to 350°F. Trim fat from pork. Place pork in 13x9 in glass baking dish. Season well with salt and pepper.
In small skillet, heat preserves, apple juice, rosemary and garlic over medium heat, stirring frequently, until thickened.
Spoon ¼ cup apricot mixture onto pork, reserve remaining apricot mixture.
Bake uncovered 75 to 90 minutes or–if using a meat thermometer–until the thermometer reads 155°F. Take the meat out of the oven.
Cover pork with foil, let stand 15 to 20 minutes or until thermometer reads 160°F.
Cut pork into slices. Heat remaining apricot mixture, pour it over the meat and serve.
Cook tomato on a ridged grill pan over medium-high heat until slightly blackened, about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and cool until easily handled.
Peel off the skin and remove seeds.
Bring a small pot of water to a boil.
Add guajillo and ancho chile peppers; cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Drain.

Combine tomato flesh, softened chile peppers, 2 slices pineapple, orange juice, quartered onion, vinegar, chipotle peppers, salt, garlic, cloves, cumin seeds, and oregano in a blender; blend until smooth.
Arrange pork slices in a glass or ceramic baking dish.
Pour blended mixture over pork, ensuring all sides are evenly coated.
Cover baking dish with plastic wrap.
Marinate pork in the refrigerator, 4 hours to overnight.
Cook remaining pineapple slices on a ridged grill pan over medium-high heat until slightly blackened and soft, about 5 minutes per side.
Chop into small pieces.
Wipe out the grill pan and preheat over medium-high heat.
Cook marinated pork in the hot pan, turning once, until browned, 4 to 5 minutes.
Chop pork coarsely into small pieces against the grain.
Spoon onto taco shells and serve with pineapple, chopped onion, and cilantro.
Season the pork loin generously with salt and pepper.
Set your Instant Pot to "Sauté" mode and add a small amount of olive oil.
Once the Instant Pot is hot, sear the pork loin on all sides until browned, for about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the pork loin from the Instant Pot and set it aside.
In the same Instant Pot, add minced garlic and chopped rosemary. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the chicken broth or water, deglazing the bottom of the pot by scraping up any browned bits with a wooden spoon.
Return the seared pork loin to the Instant Pot, ensuring it's coated with the garlic, rosemary, and liquid.

Close the Instant Pot lid, seal the valve, and set it to "Pressure Cook" mode. Cook on high pressure for about 20-25 minutes for a small pork loin (adjust time based on thickness), followed by a natural release for 10 minutes.
After the natural release, carefully perform a quick release to release any remaining pressure.
Use a meat thermometer to ensure the internal temperature of the pork loin reaches at least 145°F (63°C) for safe consumption.
Remove the pork loin from the Instant Pot, let it rest for a few minutes before slicing, and serve.
beef tenderloin: Arrange the thinly sliced beef tenderloin on a serving plate in a single layer.
In a small bowl, whisk together the extra virgin olive oil and lemon juice to make a dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the beef slices.
Season the beef with salt and black pepper to taste.
Scatter the arugula over the beef slices.
Top with shaved Parmesan cheese.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Season the beef tenderloin steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the beef tenderloin steak to the skillet and cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side until browned. Remove from the skillet and let it rest on a plate.
In the same skillet, add sliced mushrooms and diced onion. Cook until softened.
Roll out the puff pastry sheet on a lightly floured surface.
Spread Dijon mustard over the center of the puff pastry sheet.
Place the cooked beef tenderloin steak on top of the mustard.
Spoon the cooked mushrooms and onions over the beef.
Fold the edges of the puff pastry over the beef and seal the seams.
Brush the top of the puff pastry with beaten egg for an egg wash.
Place the beef en croûte on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the puff pastry is golden brown and puffed up.
Remove from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing.
Serve hot.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Season the beef tenderloin steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in an ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the beef tenderloin steak for about 2-3 minutes on each side until it forms a golden-brown crust.
Transfer the skillet with the seared steak to the preheated oven.
Roast for about 8-10 minutes for medium-rare doneness, or adjust the cooking time according to your preference for doneness.
While the steak is cooking, in a separate skillet, sauté the sliced mushrooms until they are tender.
Add beef broth and Cabernet Sauvignon (or red wine) to the skillet with the mushrooms. Let it simmer and reduce slightly.
If you prefer a thicker sauce, mix a teaspoon of cornstarch with a tablespoon of cold water in a small bowl. Add this mixture to the skillet and stir until the sauce thickens slightly.
Once the steak is cooked to your liking, remove it from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes.
Serve the beef tenderloin steak topped with the Cabernet-mushroom sauce.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat 1/2 tsp oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add beef.
Cook for 3 - 4 minutes until cooked and there's some caramelised bits, then remove from skillet. Keep warm until required or reheat to warm.
Soak mushrooms in a large bowl of boiling water for 30 minutes, or until rehydrated. Drain, squeeze out excess water, then slice.
Place carrot and zucchini in separate bowls, sprinkle each with ¼ tsp salt, toss, leave for 20 minutes then drain excess liquid.
Get 2 skillets going if you can. Heat 1/2 tsp oil in a skillet over medium high heat. Cook mushrooms for 2 minutes.
Add ½ tsp soy, ½ tsp of garlic. Stir for 1 minute, then remove. Add 1/2 tsp oil into the skillet, cook carrot until just tender 5 to 8 minutes, then remove.
Cook as with carrot for 4 minutes. Heat 1/2 tsp oil. Sauté until starting to wilt.
Add ½ tsp garlic, and salt to taste, stir, then remove. When cool, squeeze to drain out excess liquid.
Simmer in water for 5 min or steam in microwave for 3 min until floppy. Drain under cold water, then cool.
Squeeze out excess liquid with hands, place in bowl. Mix with 1 tsp olive oil, 1 tsp garlic.
Vegetables can cool, they are meant to be at room temp or slightly warm. Fry eggs in a skillet to your taste.
Place warm rice in bowls. Top with vegetables and beef.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Mix marinade ingredients. Mix the marinade in a bowl, then add beef. Mix the marinade in a bowl, then add beef.
Heat 2 tsp oil in a large skillet over high heat. Let excess marinade drip off then add beef.
Cook for 3 - 4 minutes until cooked and there's some caramelised bits, then remove from skillet.
Keep warm until required or reheat to warm. Soak mushrooms in a large bowl of boiling water for 30 minutes, or until rehydrated.
Drain, squeeze out excess water, then slice.
Place carrot and zucchini in separate bowls, sprinkle each with ¼ tsp salt, toss, leave for 20 minutes then drain excess liquid.
Get 2 skillets going if you can! Heat 2 tsp oil oil in a skillet over medium high heat. Cook mushrooms for 2 minutes.
Add 1 ½ tsp soy, ½ tsp of garlic. Stir for 1 minute, then remove.
Add 2 tsp oil into the skillet, cook carrot until just tender 5 to 8 minutes, then remove. Cook as with carrot for 4 minutes.
Heat olive oil. Sauté until starting to wilt. Add ½ tsp garlic, and salt to taste, stir, then remove
When cool, squeeze to drain out excess liquid.
Simmer in water for 5 min or steam in microwave for 3 min until floppy. Drain under cold water, then cool.
Squeeze out excess liquid with hands, place in bowl. Mix with 1 tsp garlic, ¼ tsp fish sauce.
Vegetables can cool, they are meant to be at room temp or slightly warm. Fry eggs in a skillet to your taste.
Place warm rice in bowls. Top with vegetables and beef, as pictured in post, then lastly, the egg.
Serve with Bibimbap Sauce.
Season your beef steak with soy sauce and pepper.
Let it sit for 15 minutes.
Grill your steak over a very hot grill to get the grill marks.
Cook to your desired done-ness.
Let the steak rest for 5 minutes, then slice thinly across the grain.
For the salad dressing, mix together the fish sauce, sugar, lime juice and chili in a small bowl.
Taste and adjust sweetness, saltiness and tartness according to your taste.
In a large mixing bowl, combine beef, cucumber, tomato, shallot, and herbs.
Drizzle about 3 tbsp of dressing and toss together.
Taste and add more dressing if needed.
Serve and enjoy with bread.
Freeze beef tenderloin until very firm but not rock solid, about 1-2 hours.
While the beef is in the freezer, place the plates in the fridge to chill.
Once the beef is firm, slice very thinly against the grain and place slices on chilled plates.
Top each serving with a handful of arugula, capers, and zucchini.
Drizzle with vinegar and olive oil and add a pinch of freshly cracked black pepper and sea salt.
Serve immediately.
red bell pepper: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking sheet and season with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until it flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is baking, in a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, and sesame seeds to create the dressing.
In two serving plates, divide the mixed greens and arrange the halved cherry tomatoes and sliced red bell pepper.
Once the salmon is cooked, let it cool slightly and then flake it into bite-sized pieces.
Place the flaked salmon on top of the prepared salads.
Drizzle the Asian Ginger Sesame Dressing over the salads.
Garnish each plate with slices of lime.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season the chicken breast with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Bake the chicken breast in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, or until it's cooked through and reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the pasta according to the package instructions. Once cooked, drain and set it aside to cool.
Once the chicken is done, let it rest for a few minutes, then dice it into bite-sized pieces.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked pasta, diced chicken, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, red bell pepper, baby spinach or arugula, black olives, and red onion.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, balsamic vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper. This is your salad dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Place the veg stock, garlic and ginger in a blender and whizz until smooth.
If your blender isn't big enough, do this in two batches and make sure to keep the lid on tight.
Pour the blended stock into a medium-sized pot and bring to the boil.
Once it's boiling, add the noodles and cook as per the packet instructions.
Chop the button mushrooms or roughly tear the oyster mushrooms.
Add the oil to a frying pan set on a high heat, then add the mushrooms along with the soy sauce and cook for 3 minutes, stirring regularly.
Once the noodles are cooked, remove them from from the stock with a slotted spoon and divide them between four large bowls, then pour over the broth.
Now layer up the bowls by placing some of the red pepper strips, grated carrot, red cabbage and spring onions in each bowl, along with some beansprouts.
Each bowl should be very colourful and rainbow-like in terms of colour.
Top with the fried mushrooms, then top each bowl with a sprinkle of sesame seeds and serve with a lime wedge.
Add boiled eggs, 1 per serving.
Heat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium heat.
Add the diced tofu to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes until golden brown and slightly crispy. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the diced onion, bell peppers, and minced garlic. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
Add the cooked quinoa, chopped kimchi, soy sauce, sesame oil, grated ginger, and red pepper flakes to the skillet. Stir well to combine all the ingredients.
Cook the fried rice mixture for another 1-2 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Add the cooked tofu back into the skillet and toss gently to incorporate it with the fried rice.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Remove from heat and garnish with sliced green onions and fresh parsley.
Combine olive oil, vegetable oil, cider vinegar, and sugar in a saucepan, bring to a boil, remove from heat, and cool to room temperature.
Stir pinto beans, black-eyed peas, corn, onion, celery, red bell pepper, and cilantro together in a large bowl.
Pour cooled oil mixture over bean mixture and toss to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate, stirring occasionally, for 24 hours.
Place a saucepan on medium heat and add olive oil, the chopped garlic and fry until golden brown, add tomato sauce and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat, simmer.
Place frypan on heat and add olive oil, add eggplant and cook until coloured and almost soft, drain eggplant and set aside.
Place the pan back on heat and add olive oil, add the onion and toss, add pepper and zucchini, cook until nicely colored, remove from heat and drain oil.
Add the vegetables to the reduced tomato, add basil and keep warm.
Place grill pan on heat, brush salmon with oil, when pan is hot add salmon and grill on each side. Serve with warm ratatouille.
Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes, or until it's no longer pink and slightly browned. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the minced garlic and grated fresh ginger. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced red bell pepper, green bell pepper, and onion to the skillet. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until the vegetables start to soften.
Return the cooked chicken to the skillet.
In a small bowl, whisk together the low-sodium chicken broth, low-sodium soy sauce, and hoisin sauce. Pour this sauce over the chicken and vegetables.
Add the cashews to the skillet and stir everything together.
Let the mixture simmer for about 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
In a medium size mixing bowl add the black-eyed peas, black beans, corn, tomatoes, bell pepper, and red onion. Stir to combine.
In a jar, add olive oil, vinegar, salt and lemon juice, shake to combine. Pour the dressing over the veggies and stir to combine. Add in the avocado, give it another mix and enjoy.
Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add a pinch of salt and the noodles.
Cook the noodles according to the package instructions until they are al dente. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
Drain the cooked noodles and rinse them under cold water to stop the cooking process. Let them cool completely.
In a mixing bowl, combine the noodles, thinly sliced cucumber, thinly sliced red bell pepper, shredded carrot, chopped scallions, and sesame seeds.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, olive oil, until well combined.
Pour the dressing over the salad ingredients.
Gently toss everything together until the salad is evenly coated with the dressing.
Toss cucumbers, tomatoes, onion, bell pepper, garlic, parsley, and mint together in a bowl.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the salad and toss to coat; season with salt and pepper.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a bowl, combine the cooked quinoa, diced tomatoes, diced onion, diced red bell pepper, canned chickpeas, olive oil, ground cumin, ground paprika, ground cinnamon, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Lay the cabbage leaf flat on a clean surface.
Place the quinoa and chickpea mixture in the center of the cabbage leaf.
