Carefully fold the sides of the cabbage leaf over the filling, and then roll it up to create a neat cabbage roll.
Place the cabbage roll seam-side down in a small oven-safe dish.
Pour the tomato sauce over the cabbage roll.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 30-35 minutes, or until the cabbage is tender and the filling is heated through.
In a medium bowl, mix mango, red bell pepper, green onion, cilantro, jalapeno, lime juice, and lemon juice.
Cover, and allow to sit at least 30 minutes before serving with tortilla chips.
In a pot or saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the cubed lamb and brown it for a few minutes.
Stir in the cubed eggplant and sliced red bell pepper.
Cook the vegetables and lamb for about 5 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the diced tomatoes to the pot and mix.
Pour in the low-sodium vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cover the pot and let the stew simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and the flavors have melded together.
Combine golden raisins and red wine vinegar in a bowl.
Heat olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
Cut 8 oz. eggplant into 3/4-inch cubes.
Add chopped red bell pepper, chopped white onion, and eggplant cubes to skillet.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, 10 to 12 minutes.
Stir in chopped tomato, chopped drained capers, crushed red pepper, and raisin mixture.
Simmer until slightly thickened, about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle with chopped fresh mint.
Serve with garlic bread.
Cook fettuccine pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add diced red and yellow bell peppers and cook until softened.
Stir in cayenne pepper and vegetable broth. Simmer for a few minutes.
Add cooked fettuccine pasta to the skillet and toss to coat evenly with the sauce.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cut the top off the butternut squash and scoop out the seeds.
Place the hollowed-out butternut squash in a baking dish.
In a small saucepan, combine quinoa and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the quinoa is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced red bell pepper, diced red onion, and diced zucchini. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until the vegetables begin to soften.
Stir in the cooked quinoa, black beans, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Stuff the quinoa and vegetable mixture into the hollowed-out butternut squash.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 30-35 minutes or until the butternut squash is tender when pierced with a fork.
In a skillet over medium heat, add olive oil.
Add sliced bell peppers, red onion, and zucchini, sautéing for about 3-4 minutes until slightly softened.
Add cauliflower rice to the skillet and cook for an additional 4-5 minutes until the cauliflower is tender.
Stir in fajita seasoning and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Add sliced cooked beef to the skillet, heating through for 2-3 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Place chicken in an oiled skillet over medium-high heat and cook doe 9 to 10 minutes.
Meanwhile place bell peppers, red chilli pepper, green onions, and garlic in a mixing bowl.
Mix chicken broth, hoisin sauce, soy sauce, tomato sauce, sriracha sauce, and red pepper flakes together in a separate mixing bowl.
Place dry rice noodles in a large mixing bowl. Cover with about 6 cups boiling water; add a pinch of salt. Stir from time to time until noodles are soft, about 10 minutes.
Drain, cool with cold water. Drain thoroughly. Transfer to a mixing bowl. Add chicken, drizzle with oil. Toss to coat.
Heat large skillet over high heat, drizzle in oil. When oil is hot, add the vegetable mixture. Cook and stir until vegetables are hot and steamy, 1 or 2 minutes.
Pour in sauce, cook and stir over high heat until sauce starts to bubble, about 1 minute. Add noodles and chicken.
Cook and stir to blend ingredients and ensure ingredients are hot, about 1 minute. Remove from heat.
Preheat a toaster oven or a regular oven to 350°F.
Lay out the two slices of whole-grain bread on a clean surface.
In a small bowl, combine the honey mustard and sriracha sauce. Mix well to create a sweet and spicy sauce.
Spread the sweet and spicy sauce evenly on one side of each bread slice.
Lay the thinly sliced turkey breast on one of the bread slices.
Add the thinly sliced cucumber and red bell pepper on top of the turkey.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Place the mixed salad greens on top of the vegetables.
Place the mixed salad greens on top of the vegetables.
Wrap the sandwich in aluminum foil or parchment paper.
Place the wrapped sandwich in the preheated toaster oven or regular oven for about 5-7 minutes, just until it's heated through and slightly crispy.
Remove the Sweet and Spicy Turkey Sandwich from the oven, unwrap it, and let it cool for a minute before serving.
In a mixing bowl, combine the chopped hard-boiled eggs, diced cucumber, diced red bell pepper, and chopped chives or green onions.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the light mayonnaise and Dijon mustard until well combined.
Pour the dressing over the egg and vegetable mixture.
Gently toss everything together until the egg salad is coated with the dressing.
Season the egg salad with salt and black pepper to taste. Adjust the seasoning if needed.
wonton wrappers: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the wonton wrappers on a clean surface.
In a small bowl, mix together the drained crab meat and small pieces of Brie cheese.
Spoon half of the crab and Brie mixture onto the center of each wonton wrapper.
Moisten the edges of the wrappers with a bit of water using your finger.
Fold the wrappers diagonally over the filling, forming a triangle shape, and press the edges firmly to seal.
Place the sealed wontons on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or lightly greased with oil.
Lightly spray the top of the wontons with cooking spray or brush them with a little oil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 10-12 minutes or until the wontons are golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let them cool for a minute before serving.
In a saucepan, heat the low-sodium chicken broth over medium heat.
Add sliced bok choy, sliced green onions, sliced mushrooms, minced ginger, and minced garlic to the broth.
Season with low-sodium soy sauce, salt, and black pepper to taste.
Bring the broth to a gentle simmer and let it cook for about 5-7 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
While the soup is simmering, prepare the wonton wrappers according to the package instructions.
Once the vegetables are tender, drop the cooked and shredded chicken and the cooked wonton wrappers into the soup.
Cook for another 2-3 minutes to heat the chicken and wontons.
Serve your Chicken Wonton Soup hot.
In a mixing bowl, combine the ground chicken, chopped green onions, minced garlic, grated ginger, and a pinch of salt.
Place a small amount of the meat mixture in the center of each wonton wrapper.
Fold the wonton wrappers in half to form a triangle and press the edges to seal.
In a saucepan, bring 1/2 cup of water to a boil.
Carefully add the wontons to the boiling water and cook for about 5-6 minutes, or until they float to the surface and are cooked through.
While the wontons are cooking, prepare the spicy sauce. In a separate bowl, combine the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and chili oil.
Once the wontons are cooked, remove them from the boiling water and drain any excess water.
Drizzle the spicy sauce over the cooked wontons.

If making homemade wontons, mix ground chicken with minced garlic, minced ginger, and soy sauce. Place a small amount of the mixture in the center of each wonton wrapper, moisten the edges with water, fold and seal the edges.
In a pot, bring chicken broth to a boil over medium heat.
Add wontons and cook for 3-4 minutes until they float to the top.
Add chopped bok choy or spinach, sliced mushrooms, soy sauce, and sesame oil. Simmer for another 2-3 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with sliced green onions.
Combine mince, coriander, spring onion, soy sauce, rice wine or sherry, ginger and garlic in a bowl. Season with pepper.
Place 1 wonton wrapper on a clean work surface. Top with 1 tsp of mince mixture. Brush edges with a little water. Bring the corners together to enclose the filling and pinch the centre to seal.
Repeat with remaining wrappers and mince mixture.
Heat oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Remove from heat. Arrange wontons over the base of the frying pan. Cook over high heat for 2 minutes or until the bases are golden.
Pour 1/4 water evenly over wontons. Cover and cook for 3 to 4 minutes or until the wontons are cooked through and water evaporates.
Mix chicken, scallions and soy sauce together in a bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
Place 1/2 teaspoonful of chicken mixture in the center of each wonton wrapper. Dip your finger in water and run it along the edge of each wrapper.
Bring corners together in the center and press to seal. Pinch edges together.
Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook pot stickers for 2 to 3 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium, pour in chicken broth and cover skillet. Steam pot stickers  5 to 6 minutes. Garnish with the remaining scallions and sesame seeds.

In a bowl, combine the ground chicken, chopped shiitake mushrooms, chopped water chestnuts, chopped green onions, minced garlic, minced ginger, low-sodium soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to form the filling.
Take one dumpling or wonton wrapper and place a spoonful of the filling in the center.
Gather the sides of the wrapper up around the filling, leaving the top exposed.
Carefully press down the filling to flatten the top, so it forms a small cup shape.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Steam the Siu Mai dumplings in a steamer for about 10-12 minutes until they are cooked through. You can line the steamer with cabbage leaves or parchment paper to prevent sticking.
Serve your homemade Siu Mai hot.
In a large bowl, mix together beef, cabbage, carrot, and onion. Stir in the egg, salt, soy sauce, and vegetable oil.
Place a large teaspoonful of filling in the center of a dumpling skin. Moisten the edges of a wonton with a few drops of water.
Then fold the dumpling in half, and pinch the edges together to seal.
Create a ripple pattern along the pinched edge by pinching and gently pushing together small segments of it. Repeat with remaining dumplings.
Boil the dumplings in water until they float to the top, about 5 minutes.
In a mixing bowl, combine canned crab meat, cream cheese, green onions, Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Place a spoonful of the crab mixture in the center of each wonton wrapper.
Moisten the edges of the wonton wrappers with water, then fold them over to form triangles, pressing the edges to seal.
Preheat the air fryer to 375°F for about 3-5 minutes.
Lightly coat both sides of the crab Rangoon with cooking spray.
Place the crab Rangoon in the air fryer basket in a single layer, making sure they are not touching.
Air fry the crab Rangoon for 6-8 minutes, flipping halfway through, or until golden brown and crispy.
Serve the air-fried crab Rangoon hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
Soak dried prawns in a bowl of hot water for 30 minutes. Drain and finely chop.
Soak rice vermicelli in a bowl of warm water for 10 minutes. Drain, squeeze out excess water, and finely chop.
Beat eggs and 1 pinch salt together in a bowl until light and foamy. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat, add eggs.
Cook until set, about 2 minutes. Flip the omelet and cook 1 minute more. Cool omelet on a work surface, cut into small pieces.
Combine egg, prawns, vermicelli, chives, mushrooms, 1 tablespoon oil, 1/2 teaspoon salt, sesame oil, and black pepper in a bowl.
Wet the edge of a wonton wrapper with water and place 1 tablespoon egg mixture in the center.
Fold wonton wrapper in half and pleat the edges with your fingers. Repeat with remaining wrappers and egg mixture.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat; add half of the dumplings and fry for 2 minutes.
Pour in boiling water, cover, and cook until bottoms of dumplings are golden brown, about 10 minutes.
Remove and keep warm. Repeat with remaining dumplings.
Heat 1 1/2 teaspoons oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add cabbage, bean sprouts, and carrots, saute until tender, 6 to 8 minutes. Transfer vegetable mixture to a large bowl.
Place beef in the same skillet; cook and stir until no longer pink, 5 to 7 minutes. Drain beef into the bowl with the vegetable mixture.
Add green onions, beaten egg, garlic, sesame oil, sesame seeds, salt, pepper, and ginger. Mix filling well.
Separate and place wonton wrappers onto a flat work surface. Spoon 1 tablespoon of the filling onto the center of each wrapper.
Use your finger or a pastry brush to lightly moisten edges of wonton wrappers with water.
Fold one corner of the wrapper over the filling onto the opposite corner to form a triangle. Press edges together to seal wontons.
Heat remaining oil in a large saucepan. Place wontons in the hot oil, fry until lightly browned, 1 to 2 minutes per side.
Combine rice wine, sesame oil, ginger paste, salt, and pepper together in a glass or ceramic bowl, add chicken and toss to coat.
Cover and let marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Heat enough oil in a frying pan over medium heat to cover the chicken pieces.
Add chicken to the hot oil and fry until no longer pink in the centers, about 5 minutes.
Place a small amount of fried chicken inside each wrapper. Brush all edges with beaten egg and fold to seal. Set aside.
Heat more oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Pan-fry dumplings, working in batches as needed, until golden, 3 to 5 minutes.
Transfer dumplings to the baking sheet rack as you work to drain excess oil.
Whisk together gochujang, lime juice, sesame oil, garlic paste, and soy sauce for sauce in a bowl. Serve with dumplings.
Mash green onion and ginger in a bowl with water. Mix in pork, 2 tablespoons rice wine, soy sauce, 2 teaspoons salt, sesame oil, and white pepper.
Combine shrimp, 1 tablespoon rice wine, 1 teaspoon salt, mushrooms, and celery in a bowl. Add to the pork mixture, stir until filling is well combined.
Separate and place wonton wrappers onto your work surface. Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the pork-shrimp filling onto the center of each wrapper.
Use your finger or a pastry brush to lightly moisten edges of wonton wrappers with water.
Fold one corner of the wrapper over the filling onto the opposite corner to form a triangle. Press edges together to seal.
Bring the chicken stock to a rolling boil. Add wontons and cook until they float to the top, about 5 minutes.
rice flour: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Combine the gluten-free flour, baking powder, salt, and pumpkin spice inside a sifter and sift into a large bowl. Give it a mix to combine.
Using a grater, grate the butter into the dry mixture. Combine the butter and dry ingredients with your hands until there are no large lumps.
In a medium bowl, whisk the pumpkin and eggs until smooth. Pour the wet ingredients into dry ingredients and mix with a wooden spoon until a dough forms.
Transfer the dough onto a lightly floured surface and make a large circle around 1-inch thick. Cut the dough into triangles.
Place each scone onto the lined baking sheet. Bake 14 minutes until they are light brown. Remove from oven and allow them to cool before serving.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Add onions to a large bowl and separate. Add in all the spices, salt, chickpea flour and rice flour. Sprinkle in the oil and mix in.
Add in water, just a little bit at a time so that you get a pasty pakora mix. Take spoonfuls of this mixture and place on a parchment lined baking sheet.
Bake for 17 minutes. Once the pakoras are cooked, let them cool for a few minutes.
Mix tomato paste, vinegar, onion powder, oregano and some salt in a bowl. Serve with pakoras.
Add some salt to the grated zucchini and let it sit for 10 minutes. This way the zucchini will release all it's moisture.
After 20 minutes, squeeze the zucchini, remove all the water and place it in another bowl. Add scallion, broccoli, and coriander. Also add corn, dill and garlic.
Add salt, chaat masala and rice flour and mix everything together. Take the mixture of a size of a lemon ball and make a patty out of it. Repeat till the mixture is finished.
In a pan heat oil on medium heat. Once the oil is hot place the patties on the pan. Cook for 2 minutes per side. Set aside.
In a blender, mix chili flakes, basil, tomato puree, avocado and walnuts and blend until smooth. Serve in a smal bowl along with the veggie patties.
Use a pair of kitchen shears to cut lengthwise about halfway into the shrimp, without cutting through the tail.
Devein, pat dry, and place in a large bowl. Toss shrimp with 2 teaspoons oil and season with salt, pepper, paprika, and cayenne. Marinate in the refrigerator, 20 minutes.
Sprinkle shrimp with rice flour and toss until fully coated.
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat remaining ¼ cup oil until shimmering.
Add shrimp in a single layer and fry until shrimp are pink and rice flour is golden, Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate.
Melt butter over medium heat until it begins to foam, then add garlic. Cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant and golden, 2 to 3 minutes. Add shrimp and toss until fully coated.
Serve with lemon wedges. Spoon over remaining garlic butter.
In a mixing bowl, combine chickpea flour, rice flour, turmeric powder, chili powder, cumin powder, garam masala, baking soda, and salt.
Gradually add water to the dry ingredients to form a smooth batter with a thick consistency.
Heat oil in a deep frying pan or wok over medium-high heat.
Dip the chicken pieces into the batter, ensuring they are evenly coated.
Carefully drop the coated chicken pieces into the hot oil, a few at a time, and fry until they turn golden brown and crispy. Make sure not to overcrowd the pan.
Remove the fried chicken pakoras using a slotted spoon and place them on a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
Repeat the frying process with the remaining chicken pieces.
In a saucepan, whisk together the rice flour and water until well combined.
Place the saucepan over medium heat and bring the mixture to a simmer while stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Once the mixture starts to thicken, reduce the heat to low and let it cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
Stir in the milk (or milk alternative) and continue cooking for another 1-2 minutes until the cereal reaches your desired consistency.
Remove the saucepan from heat and stir in the ghee (or vegetable oil) and a pinch of salt.
Pour the Cream of Rice Cereal into a serving bowl. Top with cinnamon.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Trim the ends off the eggplant, slice into ½-inch rounds. Transfer the rounds to a baking sheet lined with paper towels and sprinkle with salt. Let sit for 10 minutes.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the almond meal, Italian seasoning, and a pinch of salt. Beat the eggs in another medium bowl with a splash of water.
Add the rice flour to another medium bowl. Coat the salted eggplant rounds in the rice flour, then in the egg, then in the seasoned almond meal.
Set the rounds on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Drizzle on both sides with olive oil.
Bake the eggplant, flipping halfway through for even browning, for 20 minutes.
Heat a drizzle of olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium-low heat. Add the garlic and red pepper flakes and cook for 3 minutes. Add basil and stir.
Add the crushed tomatoes and stir to combine. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes.
To assemble, spread a bit of sauce in the bottom of a 9x13-inch dish, then add a layer of eggplant.
Top with more sauce and sprinkle with the mozzarella and Parmesan cheese. Repeat with the remaining ingredients. Bake for 10 minutes, serve immediately.
Heat water in a small saucepan over high heat. Bring to a boil and add walnuts. Boil for 2 minutes, then drain and place walnuts on a cookie sheet to dry.
Whip egg whites in a medium bowl until foamy. Stir in rice flour until it has a pasty consistency.
Heat oil in a heavy deep skillet over medium-high heat. Dip shrimp into the flour batter, and then cook in the hot oil until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Stir together mayonnaise, honey, and sweetened condensed milk in a medium serving bowl. Add fried shrimp and toss to coat with the sauce.
Sprinkle the walnuts on top and serve.
Make the batter by placing the chickpea flour and rice flour in a bowl with the rosemary, parsley and jalapeno pepper. Add salt to taste.
Whisk in the water until a smooth batter forms. The batter consistency should be thick but easily pourable.
Heat the oil in the skillet. Add the shallots and garlic with a pinch of salt and ground black pepper. Saute until the shallots start to soften but are not brown.
Add the mushrooms and saute until they are softened, about 3 minutes over medium heat. Stir in the matzo crackers. Then pour in the chickpea batter.
Cook over medium to medium-high heat until the edges of the brei appear dry and multiple bubbles rise in the center.
Start the broiler. Place the skillet carefully under for 3 minutes or until the top is set and the crackers are slightly charred. Serve immediately.
cilantro leaves: Add avocado, sour cream, cilantro, lime juice, a large pinch of salt, and a splash of water to a food processor. Process until it forms a smooth, creamy consistency.
Garnish with flax seeds. Serve with rice cakes.
In a bowl, combine the (drained) tuna, mayo, sliced onion, with some salt and pepper, to taste.
Mix until well combined.
Next, cut sheets of nori into quarters.
Place nori shiny side down, place about a tbsp of the tuna mixture on the lower corner of the nori, a stick or two of carrots and cucumber, a slice of serrano, and few sprigs of cilantro.
Roll up the hand roll into a cone shape, starting at the lower left-hand corner sometimes, the nori doesn't seal.
Repeat for the other sheets and serve immediately!
Enjoy!
Heat the oil in a large pan over a medium-high setting.
Sauté the finely chopped onion until golden.
Add the crushed garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the garlic doesn't smell raw anymore.
Mix in the next four ingredients (ground coriander, turmeric, cumin, and cayenne) and toast for two minutes stirring often.
Add the crushed tomatoes, chickpeas and vegetable stock.
Increase the heat to high and once boiling, lower to medium-low to maintain at a simmer for 10 minutes, stirring every now and then.
Add 1/2 tsp salt, the sugar, and the spinach. If the spinach is frozen increase the heat until the curry is bubbling away again.
Simmer for an additional five minutes.
Add the garam masala, lemon juice and coconut milk and stir.
Sprinkle over the chopped cilantro leaves and serve hot with pita, if desired.
Place all the ingredients in a bowl and stir to combine.
Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.
Serve with tortilla chips.
Prepare rice noodles according to package directions, taking care they are a slightly crunchy/al dente because they will cook more in the sauce.
Rinse noodles in cold water, drain, and toss in with 1 tsp sesame oil. Set aside.
Add all of the Sauce Ingredients to a medium mixing bowl. Whisk together and set aside.
While rice noodles are soaking, heat 1 tbsp oil a deep large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
Once very hot, add chicken and let cook undisturbed for 1 minute then continue to stir fry just until cooked. Transfer to a plate. Wipe out the pan.
Heat one tbsp oil in the same skillet and heat over medium-high heat. Add bell peppers and shallots and sauté for 1 minute.
Add garlic and sauté for 1 additional minute.
Reduce heat to medium and pour beaten eggs over vegetables and cook, stirring constantly, just until scrambled.
Add sauce and noodles and increase heat to medium-high.
Toss until evenly combined and continue to stir and cook until noodles are al-dente 1-2 minutes.
Stir in chicken, bean sprouts, green onions and heat through. Taste and add additional chili sauce to taste. Top with cilantro and serve.
In a small saucepan, combine the low-sodium beef broth, cinnamon stick, whole cloves, fennel seeds, and coriander seeds.
Heat the broth mixture over medium-low heat until it comes to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer gently for about 10-15 minutes to infuse the flavors. Remove the spices if preferred or leave them in for a stronger flavor.
While the broth is simmering, prepare the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Usually, you'll need to soak them in hot water for a few minutes until they soften. Drain and set aside.
Thinly slice the raw beef sirloin or eye of round.
Once the broth is ready, strain it to remove the spices if you left them in.
Place the cooked rice noodles in a serving bowl.
Arrange the thinly sliced raw beef over the noodles.
Pour the hot broth over the beef and noodles. The heat from the broth will partially cook the beef.
Top your Homemade Pho with thinly sliced onions, fresh bean sprouts, cilantro leaves, basil leaves, mint leaves, and sliced jalapeño peppers.
Preheat oven to 450 °F.
Add the eggplant slices in the baking pan and cover with tomatoes, garlic, parsley, and paprika.
Sprinkle with salt, paprika, pepper, parsley, and drizzle with oil. Bake for 15 minutes.
Turn off the heat, drizzle with lemon juice, and garnish with cilantro leaves.
Scoop out some of the avocado from the pitted area to widen the "bowl" area.
Place the scooped avocado into a medium-size mixing bowl. Mash it with a fork.
Add the tuna, bell pepper, jalapeno, and cilantro to the mixing bowl. Pour lime juice over and stir it all together.
Scoop the tuna into the avocado bowls. Season with salt and pepper and serve.
Prepare all the ingredients and have them ready for assembly.
Fill a shallow dish with warm water.
Dip one rice paper wrapper into the warm water for a few seconds until it becomes pliable.
Lay the rice paper wrapper flat on a clean surface.
Arrange the cooked shrimp, rice vermicelli noodles, shredded lettuce, shredded carrots, julienned cucumber, and fresh herbs in the center of the rice paper wrapper.
Fold the sides of the rice paper wrapper over the filling, then tightly roll it up like a burrito.
Repeat the process with the remaining rice paper wrapper and filling ingredients.
Slice the baguette or French roll in half lengthwise.
In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise, sriracha sauce, rice vinegar, salt, and pepper to make a spicy mayo.
Spread the spicy mayo on both sides of the bread.
Layer the cooked chicken breast slices, shredded carrots, thinly sliced cucumber, fresh cilantro leaves, and jalapeño slices onto the bottom half of the bread.
Place the top half of the bread on top to form a sandwich.
Place the chopped cucumber, green bell pepper, red onion, minced garlic, fresh spinach, cilantro, and parsley in a blender or food processor.
Add the low-sodium vegetable broth, red wine vinegar, and olive oil to the blender.
Blend the ingredients until smooth and well combined. If the mixture is too thick, you can add a bit more vegetable broth to achieve your desired consistency.
Season the Green Gazpacho with salt and black pepper to taste. Adjust the seasoning if needed.
Pour the Green Gazpacho into a bowl or serving glass.
In a large bowl, combine the chicken, pineapple and grapes.
In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise and curry; pour over chicken mixture; toss to coat.
Cover and refrigerate until serving.
Serve with bread.
Pour oil into a pan on medium to medium-high and saute the peppers and onions until just softened.  Salt and pepper to taste.
Add the cooked chicken and cilantro to the pan and stir,
Once the chicken is hot, place 1/4 of the fajita mixture on each tortilla.  If you prefer your tortillas warm, you can place them in the microwave for 8-10 seconds.
Top each tortilla with fresh cilantro if desired and enjoy!
Heat the fat on a high heat in a large skillet and tip in the pork.
Cook for 5 minutes until the liquid has all but evaporated and the pork is beginning to brown.
Add the ginger, garlic and shallots and cook for 3 minutes, stirring very frequently.
Now stir in the lime zest and juice, fish sauce, and cook a further minute.
Remove from the heat, throw in the herbs, chili flakes and give it all a good mix and serve.
In a large pot, heat the oil over moderately low heat.
Add the shallots, ginger, and jalapeños; cook, stirring occasionally, for 3 minutes.
Add the broth and water; bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the zests and mushrooms; simmer 5 minutes longer.
Add the lime juice, fish sauce, and swordfish to the soup.
Cook until the fish is just done, about 2 minutes.
Serve sprinkled with the tomatoes and cilantro, if using.
Enjoy with rice noodles.
In a large bowl toss together all of the ingredients for the salad.
Set aside.
In a small jar add all of the ingredients for the salad dressing.
Shake until well combined.
Pour salad dressing over the salad, tossing to combine until everything is well coated.
In a pot, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and minced ginger. Sauté for a minute or until fragrant.
Pour in low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth and bring it to a boil.
Add raw shrimp, sliced mushrooms, sliced tomatoes, sliced onions, and sliced bell peppers to the boiling broth.
Simmer until the shrimp turn pink and the vegetables are tender, which should take about 5-7 minutes.
Season the soup with red chili flakes, salt, fish sauce, and lime juice, adjusting to your taste preferences.
Serve your Tom Yum Soup hot, garnished with fresh cilantro leaves.
Squeeze juice from limes into large bowl. Add jalapeño Let stand 2 minutes.
Meanwhile, trim and discard top and bottoms from persimmons. Thinly slice persimmons.
Toss persimmons, pomegranate seeds, pine nuts, olive oil, and cilantro in lime juice. Season to taste with salt.
Let stand 30 minutes or up to 2 hours. Top with cilantro sprigs.
Serve immediately.
In a large pot, heat the oil over moderately low heat. Add the shallots, ginger, and jalapenos. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 3 minutes.
Add the broth and water and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes. Stir in the zests and mushrooms; simmer 5 minutes longer.
Add the lemon juice, fish sauce, and seafood to the soup. Cook for about 5 minutes. Serve with cilantro.
In a small bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, paprika, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Cut the fish fillet into smaller pieces suitable for tacos.
Dredge the fish pieces in the flour mixture, making sure they are evenly coated.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and lightly coat it with cooking spray.
Add the coated fish pieces to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until the fish is crispy and cooked through.
Warm the corn tortillas in the skillet for about 10-20 seconds on each side.
Assemble your Baja Fish Tacos by placing the crispy fish pieces on the warm tortillas.
Top the fish with shredded cabbage, diced tomatoes, diced red onions, and fresh cilantro leaves.
Squeeze fresh lime juice over the tacos and add a dollop of low-fat Greek yogurt if desired.
red lentils: Pour cleaned lentils into a medium sauce pan. Add 3 cups of water. Bring lentils and water to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 10 minutes.
While the lentils are cooking, bring a separate small pot of water to a boil. Place the tomatoes in the boiling water and blanch for one minute.
Remove the tomatoes to a bowl to cool. Once cool, peel the tomatoes and cut out and discard the tough stem end. Mash them, and set aside.
In a medium saucepan, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions. Cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add garlic paste and cook for 1 minute more, stirring continuously, making sure that the garlic does not burn. Add turmeric and stir.
To the onions and spices, proteins, add the cooked lentils along with the lentil cooking water. Add salt. Cook for 10 minutes.
Add lime juice and tomatoes. Cook for 3 more minutes. Adjust salt if necessary.
Stir in chopped cilantro and remove from heat. Garnish with more chopped cilantro.
Place the rinsed lentils, vegetable stock, tomatoes, and salt in a stockpot.
Heat the oil in a small skillet over high heat and add the mustard and cumin seeds. Fry until the mustard seeds begin to pop, about 10 seconds.
Reduce the heat to medium-high and add the garlic. Fry for another 20 seconds. Add the onion and fry until soft, about 5 minutes.
Add the turmeric, coriander, cinnamon, and cloves and stir to combine. Scrape the contents of the skillet into the stockpot. Stir to combine and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat, cover, and simmer for about 20 minutes, or until the lentils are done and the soup is thick, stirring occasionally.
Before serving, add the lime juice, according to taste, and garnish with cilantro
In a medium skillet bring salted water to a boil, add lentils and cook until tender, 10 minutes. Drain.
Add the olive oil to a large soup pot over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic and saute until fragrant and softened, about 5 minutes.
Add tomato sauce, beans, lentils, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cover with a lid and allow to simmer for 15 minutes, until the beans are soft.
Puree with a hand blender or transfer to a stand alone blender and puree until smooth.
Heat a pot over medium-high heat.
Add the pieces of lean lamb and sear them for about 2-3 minutes, until they are browned on all sides.
Add the diced onions, celery, and carrots to the pot. Sauté for about 3-4 minutes, until the vegetables begin to soften.
Stir in the minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, ground cinnamon, ground turmeric, and paprika. Cook for about 1-2 minutes until the spices become fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes and red lentils to the pot. Mix everything together.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and let the soup simmer for about 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the lamb is cooked through.
Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste.
Start by rinsing the dried red lentils in cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set them aside.
Heat the oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the finely chopped onion and minced garlic to the saucepan. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they become translucent and fragrant.
Stir in the ground turmeric, ground cumin, ground coriander, ground paprika, and cayenne pepper. Sauté for another 30 seconds to release the spices' flavors.
Add the diced tomato, diced carrot, and diced bell pepper to the saucepan. Sauté for an additional 2-3 minutes until the vegetables start to soften.
Pour in the low-sodium vegetable broth and add the rinsed red lentils. Stir to combine.

Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and let it cook for about 15-20 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the curry has thickened. Stir occasionally during cooking.
Season the red lentil curry with salt and pepper to taste, adjusting as needed.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it sit for a minute or two to allow the flavors to meld.
Heat the olive oil in a pot on medium-high heat then add the onion ginger and garlic and let cook for 2 minutes.
Add the carrot and celery and cook for 5 minutes until all the veggies are tender.
Add in the lentils, rice, red curry paste, soy sauce lime juice, and vegetable broth and mix together.
Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and let simmer for 20 minutes until the lentils are tender.
Sort and wash lentils; place in a bowl.
Cover with water to 2 inches above lentils.
Cover and refrigerate 8 hours or overnight
Drain well.
Place lentils and 3 tbsp water in a food processor; process until smooth, scraping bowl occasionally.
Combine the pureed lentils, spinach, and next 7 ingredients (spinach through garlic), stirring well.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
Drop lentil mixture by heaping tbsp into pan.
Cook 2 minutes on each side or until lightly browned.
Remove from pan
Cover and keep warm.
Repeat procedure with remaining oil and lentil mixture.
To prepare dip, combine ½ cup water and remaining ingredients in a blender; process until well blended.
Serve with puffs.
Garnish with cilantro sprigs, if desired.
Serve with rice, if desired.
Cook the red lentils according to the instructions on the package. Peel and grate the potatoes and the carrot.
In a large bowl, combine them with the cooked red lentils, the garlic, the onion, the flour, and the spices and stir well.
Heat some oil in a large pan, and add about 2 heaped tablespoons for each fritter. Cook them on medium heat in a son-sticky pan for 3 minutes on each side.
Bring chicken stock and lentils to a boil in a large saucepan over high heat. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Stir in onion and garlic; cook until onion has softened and turned translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir onion mixture into lentils; season with cumin and cayenne. Continue simmering until lentils are tender, about 10 minutes.
Purée soup in a standing blender or with a stick blender until smooth. Stir in lemon juice and cilantro.
Heat the olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onions and sauté until they become soft and translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the diced carrots and tomatoes and sauté for a few minutes until they start to soften.
Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth.
Season the soup with ground cumin, ground paprika, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for about 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are soft.
Use an immersion blender or a regular blender to purée the soup until smooth.
If using a regular blender, return the soup to the pot and heat through.
Serve your Turkish Red Lentil Soup with Mint hot, garnished with fresh mint leaves.
In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until it becomes translucent.
Add the minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, and cayenne pepper to the pot. Sauté for another minute to release the spices' flavors.
Add the chopped butternut squash, carrot, and celery to the pot, and sauté for a few minutes.
Pour in the red lentils and vegetable or chicken broth. Stir to combine.
Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Cover the pot and let it simmer for about 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are tender.
Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste.
Use an immersion blender or transfer the soup to a blender in batches to puree it until smooth.
Serve the Red Lentil and Squash Soup hot, garnished with fresh cilantro.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large saucepan or pot over medium-high heat. Saute onion and bell pepper until tender and golden, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, curry paste, chili garlic sauce, garam masala, curry powder, and salt. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring often, until fragrant.
Next add lentils, broth, and tomatoes. Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer gently, stirring occasionally. Cook until lentils are soft but not mushy.
Add juice from 1/2 a lime and stir. For an ultra creamy soup, feel free to add all the coconut milk to the soup.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and chili, and add some extra chili garlic sauce if you like things spicy. Enjoy!
Rinse 1/2 cup of red lentils under cold water and drain.
In a medium-sized pot, heat 1/2 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add 1/4 cup of diced onion and minced garlic to the pot. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until the onion becomes translucent.
Stir in the diced red and green bell peppers and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until they begin to soften.

Add 1 tablespoon of chili powder, 1/2 teaspoon of ground cumin, 1/4 teaspoon of paprika, 1/8 teaspoon of cayenne pepper (adjust to taste), salt, and pepper to the pot. Stir well to coat the vegetables in the spices.
Pour in the rinsed red lentils, 1/2 can of kidney beans (drained and rinsed), and 1/2 can of diced tomatoes (with their juices). Mix everything together.
Add 1/2 cup of vegetable broth to the pot. Stir well.
Bring the chili to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the lentils are soft and the chili has thickened. Stir occasionally.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Once the rice and chili are ready, serve the chili over the cooked rice.
In a stock pot heat oil over medium-low heat. Cook onion, garlic, carrot, celery, parsley, tomato, red pepper and saute  for about 5 minutes.
Add paprika, cumin, and lentils and stir. Add the vegetable soup and bring to boil. Simmer until lentils are tender.
Puree soup with a stick blender or food processor until smooth and strain it through a sieve. Add salt and pepper.
Pour the hot soup into warmed bowls and garnish with cream cheese and parsley.
Bring water to a boil, add salt and penne, and cook according to package instructions.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add the onion and saute for 3 minutes.
Add carrots, celery, salt, pepper and herbs. Saute 5 minutes.
Add the tomato paste, browning it just a bit in the pan. Add the tomatoes and cook them for 3 minutes.
Add the lentils, bring to a boil, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes. Serve with pasta.
Add olive oil to a large heavy soup pan over medium heat. Add chopped onions, carrot, salt, pepper and cook for 8 minutes, stir occasionally. Add garlic and cook for 2 minutes, stir occasionally.

Add cumin, turmeric, coriander and onion powder to the cooked vegetables and stir for 1 minute until fragrant. Add water, lentils, bay leaves, nutritional yeast and stir. Bring to a boil, reduce to a simmer and cook uncovered for 15 minutes.
Take off the heat and add lemon juice, parsley, scallions, fresh dill and red pepper flakes.
Melt the butter in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add the onion, carrot, and jalapeno, then sautee for 5 minutes.

Add the garlic, ginger, apples, and diced tomatoes to the pot. Sautee for another 3 minutes, then add in all of the spices and toss to coat. Add in the lentils and broth and let the contents come to a boil. Turn the heat down to medium-low and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes.

Puree about 75% of the ingredients using either an immersion blender or by transferring a portion of the contents to the bowl of a standard blender. Leave some of the chunks whole, as it adds a nice texture and consistency to the soup.
Return the soup to the pot if needed, then stir in the coconut milk. Taste, and adjust salt and black pepper as needed.
Serve topped with cashew and cilantro.
Place 4 cups water in a pot and bring to a boil. Add peas, reduce heat to low, and cook, 3 to 5 minutes. Remove peas with a slotted spoon. Reserve broth in the pot.
Add lentils to the broth, bring to a boil. Cook until tender, 15 to 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat a frying pan over medium-high heat. Add coconut oil to melt.
Add potatoes, carrots, and bell pepper, and saute until soft, 7 to 10 minutes.
Once lentils have finished cooking, add the potato mixture, cooked peas, Swiss chard, garlic, salt, nutmeg, and pepper to the pot. Squeeze in lemon juice and serve.
Place 4 cups water in a pot and bring to a boil. Add peas, reduce heat to low, and cook, 3 to 5 minutes.
Remove peas with a slotted spoon. Reserve broth in the pot.
Add lentils to the broth; bring to a boil. Cook until tender, 15 to 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat a frying pan over medium-high heat. Add coconut oil to melt.
Add potatoes, carrots, and bell pepper, and saute until soft, 7 to 10 minutes.
Once lentils have finished cooking, add the potato mixture, cooked peas, Swiss chard, garlic, salt, nutmeg, and pepper to the pot. Squeeze in lemon juice and serve.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.

Heat the oil in a saucepan over a moderate heat, add the onion and celery, and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes. Add tomatoes and spinach and stir well to combine. Cook for about 10 minutes.
Stir in the lentils and add half of the stock. Bring to the boil, then reduce the heat and cover. Simmer for 20 minutes and gradually add stock.
Spread the oats and cheese over the top. Place the dish in the oven and bake for about 10 minutes. Serve hot.
pork sausage: Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Combine pork sausage, Italian sausage and mozzarella cheese in a bowl.
Mix well and form into about 24 equal-sized meatballs. Arrange meatballs in a shallow baking pan.
Bake in the preheated oven meatballs are nicely browned for about 20 minutes. Serve and garnish with basil.
Fill a large pot with lightly salted water and bring to a rolling boil over high heat. Once the water is boiling, stir in the penne, and return to a boil.
Cook the pasta uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the pasta has cooked through, but is still firm to the bite, about 11 minutes.

Meanwhile, heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook and stir the sausage pieces, garlic, and red pepper flakes in the hot pan until the sausage is no longer pink on the outside, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the broccoli and Parmesan cheese. Pour 1/2 water. Cover, and cook until the sausage is no longer pink.
Stir the cooked pasta, broccoli, and 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese together until evenly mixed. Season to taste with salt and pepper to serve.
Preheat air fryer to 390°F. Spray basket of the air fryer with cooking spray and place eggs into the basket.
Combine yogurt, chutney, mayonnaise, salt, pepper, curry powder, and cayenne in a small bowl. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Divide pork sausage into 6 even portions. Flatten each portion into a thin patty. Place one egg in the middle and wrap the sausage around, sealing all sides. Set aside.
Place flour into a bowl and beaten eggs into another bowl. Place breadcrumbs on a plate.
Dip each wrapped egg into flour, then into beaten egg, letting the excess drip off. Roll in bread crumbs and place onto a plate.
Cook for 12 minutes, turning eggs over halfway through. Repeat with remaining eggs. Serve immediately.
Heat olive oil in a pot. Add chicken and sausage and cook 5 minutes on each side. When sausage is cool to handle, slice them.
Saute vegetables, add garlic, 1 cup water and chopped tomatoes. Season with thyme, paprika, chili flakes, salt and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook 15 minutes more. Serve in bowls.
Bake biscuits according to package directions.
Meanwhile in an oiled skillet break apart and cook the sausage for 7 to 8 minutes. Gradually add milk and heavy whipping cream.
Cook until mixture comes to a boil and thickens, stirring constantly. Reduce heat to medium-low, simmer 2 minutes, stirring constantly. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Split biscuits in half. Place 2 halves on each of 4 plates; top with about 1/4 cup gravy.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat and cook onion until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes. Stir in sausage.
Cook and stir until browned, about 5 minutes. Add mushrooms and wine and bring to a simmer.
Stir in tomatoes and bring to a simmer. Cook until sauce starts to thicken, 8 to 10 minutes. Season with salt, pepper and red pepper flakes.
Meanwhile, bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add penne and cook for about 11 minutes. Garnish with parsley.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly grease 4 muffin cups.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat and stir in sausage, cook and stir until sausage is crumbly, evenly browned, and no longer pink, 10 to 15 minutes and drain.
Beat eggs in a large bowl. Stir in onion, bell pepper, salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Mix in sausage and Cheddar cheese. Spoon by 1/4 cupfuls into muffin cups.
Bake in preheated oven until a knife inserted near the center comes out clean, 20 to 25 minutes.
In a frying pan, cook the pork sausage over medium heat until it is browned and cooked through. Set aside.
In a small saucepan, place the potato chunks and cover them with water. Bring to a boil and cook until the potatoes are tender, approximately 15-20 minutes.
Drain the cooked potatoes and return them to the saucepan.
Add the ghee, milk, salt, and pepper to the potatoes.
Mash the potatoes with a potato masher or fork until smooth and creamy. Adjust the consistency by adding more milk if needed.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the mashed potatoes with the cooked sausage and optionally with gravy on top.
Turn on a multi-functional pressure cooker and select Saute function.
Melt ghee and cook onion until soft and tender, about 3 minutes. Add sausage, cook until golden and crispy, about 5 minutes.
Place diced tomatoes, kale, chicken broth, salt, garlic powder, oregano, and black pepper in the pot.
Close and lock the lid. Select high pressure according to manufacturer's instructions, set timer for 10 minutes.
Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build. Release pressure using the natural-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, about 10 minutes.
Complete releasing pressure carefully using the quick-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, about 5 minutes. Unlock and remove the lid.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Drizzle oil over the chopped sausage. Roast for 10 minutes. Mix in the red onion and mushrooms, season with salt and pepper and cook for a further 10 minutes.
Bring a large pan of water to the boil and cook the pasta for 2 minutes.

Drain and toss in the tin with the sausages, onions and mushrooms. Spoon over the cream and stir it through, along with half of the cheese. Sprinkle the remaining cheese on top and bake for 10 minutes.
Serve immediately, topped with basil.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a large skillet, cook sausage over medium heat until no longer pink and drain.
Add the eggs, milk, cheese, and mustard. Transfer to a greased baking dish.
Bake for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350°F and grease glass baking dishes.
Place bread in prepared dish. Top with sausage and cheese.
Beat together eggs and milk. Season with pepper. Pour over sausage mixture.
Bake casserole until puffed and center is set, about 50 minutes.
In a large skillet, cook sausage over medium heat until no longer pink; drain.
Add the eggs, milk, bread, cheese, bacon and mustard, stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a greased shallow 3-qt. baking dish.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Remove from the refrigerator 30 minutes before baking.
Cover and bake at 350° for 60-65 minutes or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
Preheat oven to 325°F. Lightly grease a baking dish.
Place sausage in a large, deep skillet. Cook over medium-high heat until evenly brown. Drain, crumble, and set aside.
Line the bottom of the prepared baking dish with crescent roll dough, and sprinkle with crumbled sausage.
n a large bowl, mix beaten eggs, mozzarella, and Cheddar. Season the mixture with oregano, and pour over the sausage and crescent rolls.
Bake 25 minutes in the preheated oven, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean. Garnish with parsley.
Preheat oven to 450°F.
Prepare a floured surface for shaping the dough and have an un-greased baking sheet ready (lined with parchment sheets if you have them).
Whisk together flour, sugar and salt in a medium-sized bowl.
Using a fork or a pastry blender, cut in the shortening and chilled butter.
Work quickly, you don’t want the fats to melt – the key to fluffy biscuits is minimal handling. The mixture should be crumbly.
Make a well in the middle of the flour mixture, and pour in the buttermilk.
Stir with a spoon and blend just until the liquid is absorbed and the dough comes away from the sides of the bowl – add 1-2 tbsp more buttermilk if the dough is dry.
Do not over mix; the dough will be tacky, neither wet nor dry.
With lightly floured hands, turn out the dough onto a lightly-floured surface and gently fold it over on itself 2 or 3 times.
Shape into a ¾” thick round with a floured rolling pin.
Using a 2-inch biscuit cutter, cut out the biscuits pressing straight down (avoid the temptation to twist the cutter as twisting keeps the biscuits from rising).
Dip the cutter in flour between cuttings to keep the dough from sticking to the cutter.
Place biscuits on the baking sheet so that they just touch (for crunchy sides, leave space in between).
Reshape scrap dough and continue cutting. Remember to handle the dough as little as possible.
Bake the biscuits 450°F for 15-18 minutes or until lightly golden brown on top.
Turn the baking sheet around halfway through baking, and brush the tops of the biscuits with melted butter.
While they are baking, start making the sausage gravy.
Preheat a 4-quart saucepan over medium high heat (put a few drops of water in the pan – when they evaporate, you know the pan is ready).
Crumble the sausage into the pan and let it brown for a minute or two, then turn down to medium heat.
Continue cooking, breaking up the sausage into smaller pieces, until no pink remains.
Stir in the onions and cook until they are transparent.
Remove sausage with a slotted spatula or spoon, leaving the drippings in the pan.
If less than 3 tablespoons of drippings remain, add enough butter to equal about 3 tablespoons of drippings.
Add the cooked sausage back to the pan on medium heat, and sprinkle the flour over the sausage.
Stir in the flour and cook for about 6-8 minutes, until the mixture starts bubbling and turns slightly golden brown.
Stir in poultry seasoning, nutmeg, Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce and salt – cook for 1 minute to deepen the flavors
Slowly add the milk and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until thickened (about 15 minutes). Be patient, it will thicken!
To serve, break a biscuit in half and put it in a bowl or on a plate.
Spoon a generous portion of sausage gravy over the biscuit half and top with the other biscuit half.
Spray a 9×13 casserole dish with non-stick spray. Preheat the oven to 350ºF.
In a large skillet over medium heat, brown the sausage. Use a spatula or wooden spoon to break up the sausage into smaller pieces as it cooks.
Spoon the browned sausage evenly into prepared casserole dish. Set aside.
In a medium-size bowl, whisk together eggs, heavy cream, and 1 cup of shredded cheese. Season with garlic powder, black pepper, and salt.
Pour egg mixture evenly over sausage and stir to combine. Sprinkle with the remaining 1/2 cup of shredded cheese.
Bake for approximately 35 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the edges start to brown. Allow to cool at least 10 minutes before serving. Garnish with parsley.
In a Dutch oven over medium-high heat, add oil and onion. Saute for about 5 minutes, then add in sausage, kraut. Cook for about 5 minutes more.
Add in carrots, and potatoes and cook, stirring occasionally for about 10 minutes.
Add in chicken stock, cover. When it comes to a boil, turn heat down to medium low. Cook covered for about 30 minutes, or until veggies are desired tenderness.
Season with salt and pepper, and serve.
Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Cook pork sausage, breaking it onto smaller pieces with a wooden spoon, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add ground beef, cook and stir until beef is browned and crumbly, about 5 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Pour remaining olive oil over sausage mixture, add eggplant, onion, garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Cook and stir until lightly browned, about 10 minutes. Add mushrooms and continuing cooking until tender, about 5 minutes.
Mix crushed tomatoes, diced tomatoes, parsley, oregano, and basil into sausage mixture; bring to a boil.
Cover Dutch oven, reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer for 30 minutes.
Stir zucchini noodles into the sauce with tongs, cook and stir until the zucchini is slightly tender, 2 to 3 minutes. Top with Parmesan.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Crumble sausage into a medium skillet. Cook over medium heat until evenly brown, drain.
In a medium bowl, mix together mustard powder, salt, eggs and milk. Add the sausage, bread cubes, and cheese, and stir to coat evenly.
Pour into a greased 9x13 inch baking dish. Cover, and bake 45 to 60 minutes.
Uncover, and reduce temperature to 325°F. Bake until set.
Preheat air fryer to 390°F.
Boil eggs in a pot of water for 15 minutes or until prefered way.
Divide pork sausage into even portions. Flatten each portion into a thin patty.
Place one egg in the middle and wrap the sausage around the eggs, sealing all sides. Set eggs aside on a plate.
Place flour into a small bowl and beaten eggs into another small bowl. Place panko bread crumbs onto a plate.
Dip each sausage-wrapped egg into flour, then dip into beaten egg, letting the excess drip off. Roll in bread crumbs and place onto a plate.
Oil the air fryer and place eggs into the basket. Cook for 12 minutes, turning eggs over halfway through. Repeat with remaining eggs.
oat flour: Combine milk with vinegar in a medium bowl and set aside for 5 minutes to "sour".
Combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large mixing bowl. Whisk egg and ghee into "soured" milk.
Pour the flour mixture into the wet ingredients and whisk until lumps are gone.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat, and coat with coconut oil. Pour batter onto the skillet, and cook until bubbles appear on the surface.
Flip with a spatula, and cook until browned on the other side. Garnish with fruit and pecans.
Toss zucchini and salt together in a large colander and place in sink to drain for 10 minutes.
Transfer zucchini to the center of a piece of cheesecloth, wrap cheesecloth around zucchini and squeeze to drain as much moisture as possible.
Mix flour, Parmesan cheese, egg, and garlic together in a large bowl. Stir in zucchini, then season with kosher salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Working in batches, scoop tablespoonfuls of zucchini mixture into the hot skillet and fry until golden brown, about 2 minutes per side.
Blend chia seeds in a blender until powdery, 3 to 5 seconds. Add banana, eggs, protein powder, flour, cocoa powder, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and sea salt.
Blend, occasionally scraping sides down, until batter is smooth.
Heat a skillet over medium heat; add coconut oil. Pour 1/2 of the batter into the hot oil and sprinkle 1/2 of the blueberries onto the batter.
Cook until bubbles form and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes. Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 2 to 3 minutes.
Repeat with remaining batter and blueberries, using more coconut oil if needed.
Heat the ghee in a small pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pot. Cook until the onions are translucent and fragrant.
Sprinkle the flour over the onions and garlic. Stir well to combine and cook for another minute to cook off the raw flour taste.
Slowly whisk in the chicken or vegetable broth, ensuring there are no lumps.
Pour in the milk and continue stirring until the mixture thickens slightly.
Add the Dijon mustard and whole grain mustard to the pot. Stir well to combine.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Continue cooking the soup over low heat for about 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Remove from heat and ladle the Mosterdsoep into a bowl.
Preheat waffle iron according to manufacturer's instructions.
Beat egg whites in a glass or metal bowl using an electric mixer on high until stiff peaks form.
Whisk flour, cornmeal, chia seeds, baking powder, baking soda, white sugar, and salt together in a bowl.
Whisk almond milk, coconut oil, and egg yolks together in a separate bowl, stir into flour mixture until batter is smooth.
Gently fold egg whites into batter until just mixed.
Pour about 1/4 cup batter into preheated waffle iron, cook for about 5 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.
Stir flour, soy milk, egg, flax seeds, baking powder and vanilla extract together in a bowl.
Heat a lightly oiled griddle over medium-high heat. Drop batter by large spoonfuls onto the griddle and cook until bubbles form and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes.
Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 2 to 3 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.
Serve sprinkled with chopped walnuts and lemon juice.
Drain kimchi thoroughly through a sieve, reserving juice. Chop kimchi finely and set aside.
Whisk together egg, water, 3 tablespoons reserved kimchi juice, salt in a large bowl. Mix in kimchi and green onions. Stir in flour until just combined.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add a few spoonfuls of batter to the skillet.
Cook pancake in hot oil until edges are lightly browned, about 3 minutes.
Flip and cook until crispy, 2 to 3 minutes more. Transfer pancake to a serving plate and repeat with remaining batter.
Sprinkle bonito flakes on top of pancakes to serve.
Drain kimchi thoroughly through a sieve, reserving juice. Chop kimchi finely and set aside.
Whisk together egg, water, 3 tablespoons reserved kimchi juice, salt in a large bowl.
Mix in kimchi and green onions. Stir in flour until just combined.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add a few spoonfuls of batter to the skillet.
Cook pancake in hot oil until edges are lightly browned, about 3 minutes. Flip and cook until crispy, 2 to 3 minutes more.
In a mixing bowl whisk together 2 eggs and milk, then add the flour and baking powder, salt, and mix until you have a smooth batter.
Heat a drizzle of oil in a frying pan. Working in batches, spoon about 4 tbsp of the batter into the pan for each pancake.
Fry for 3 minutes until bubbles start to appear on the surface before turning over and frying the other side for a further 3 minutes until golden brown.
Bring a pan of water to a boil then stir the water using a spoon to create a vortex before gently dropping the egg into the center of the pan.
Turn off the heat and leave it to sit in the water for 2 minutes until the egg white is translucent. Take the egg out of the water and place it on top of your pancakes.
Serve the pancakes with smoked salmon, sliced cucumbers, tomatoes, and  poached eggs. Season with a drizzle of lemon juice and ground black pepper.
Add broccoli to a pot of boiling water; continue cooking  about 3 minutes. Drain and keep warm.
Trim beef and slice into thin bite-size strips.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add beef, onion, and garlic; cook and stir until onion is tender and beef is browned.
Remove from heat. Stir in flour, salt, and black pepper.
Return skillet to the heat. Add beef broth, tomato paste, and Dijon mustard.
Cook and stir until thick and bubbly, 5 to 8 minutes. Continue cooking for 1 minute more. Remove from heat.
Serve broccoli and beef in a bowl.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a baking sheet.
Mash black beans in a bowl; add onion, garlic, carrots, and green bell pepper. Mix.
Whisk cornstarch, water, chile-garlic sauce, chili powder, cumin, seafood seasoning, salt, and black pepper together in a separate small bowl.
Stir cornstarch mixture into black bean mixture. Mix whole-wheat bread into bean mixture.
Stir flour, 1/4 cup at a time, into bean mixture until a sticky batter forms.
Spoon 'burger-sized' mounds of batter onto the prepared baking sheet, about a 3/4-inch thickness per mound. Shape into burgers.
Bake in the preheated oven until cooked in the center and crisp in the outside, about 10 minutes on each side.
Serve patties on top of buns with tomato, lettuce and onion.
Melt the ghee in a non-stick frying pan over a low-medium heat. Meanwhile, add the 2 1/2 bananas, eggs, flour and baking powder to a blender and blitz for 20 seconds.

Pour little puddles straight from the blender into the frying pan. Cook for 1 min or until the tops start to bubble, then flip with a fork or a fish slice and cook for 20-30 seconds more. Garnish with banana slices.
Measure flour into a large mixing bowl. Slowly whisk in the milk. Whisk in the salt, eggs, cinnamon and vanilla extract until smooth.
Heat a griddle or frying and melt butter pan over medium heat.
Soak bread slices in mixture until saturated. Cook bread on each side until golden brown. Serve hot with berries.
In a mixing bowl, combine the grated butternut squash, egg, flour, Parmesan cheese, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and add enough oil to coat the bottom.
Spoon the butternut squash mixture into the skillet, forming small fritters with about 2 tablespoons of mixture for each.
Flatten the fritters slightly with the back of a spoon and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crispy.
Once cooked, transfer the fritters to a plate lined with paper towels to drain any excess oil.
Serve the butternut squash fritters warm.
Cook bacon in a large skillet and over medium heat, turning occasionally, until crispy, 10 to 12 minutes.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate with a slotted spoon, leaving bacon grease in the skillet. Crumble bacon when cool to the touch.
Add celery and garlic to the skillet and cook in the bacon grease until celery is tender, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add cauliflower, water, and onion powder, cover skillet to steam cauliflower. Cook, stirring every so often, until cauliflower is tender, about 5 minutes.
Add milk, 1 3/4 cups chicken broth, and hot sauce. Bring to a boil.
Meanwhile, whisk flour into the remaining chicken broth in a small bowl and drizzle into the boiling chowder.
Add Cheddar cheese and 1/2 of the crumbled bacon. Stir until smooth and cheese has melted.
Season with salt, pepper, and hot sauce. Ladle into bowls and top with remaining bacon and green onion.
Heat waffle iron and grease with coconut oil.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda and protein powder. In another large bowl, stir together melted ghee, sugar, egg, egg yolk, sour cream, and vanilla.
Add mashed bananas and stir until combined. Gradually add dry ingredients to wet ingredients until just combined.
Add 1 cup batter to waffle iron. Close and cook until golden on both sides. Transfer waffle to a plate and repeat with remaining batter.
Combine flour, baking powder and salt. In a separate bowl, mix together egg, milk, cream cheese, oil and mashed bananas.
Stir flour mixture into banana mixture, batter will be slightly lumpy
Heat a lightly oiled griddle or frying pan over medium high heat. Pour or scoop the batter onto the griddle.
Cook until pancakes are golden brown on both sides, serve hot and garnish with sliced banana and walnuts.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease 6 muffin cups or line with paper muffin liners.
Stir together flour, baking soda and baking powder. In a separate bowl, combine yogurt, egg, vanilla and blueberries.
Stir mixtures together just until combined; batter will be very thick. Scoop into prepared muffin cups.
Bake in preheated oven for 25 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 375°F. Coat 2 baking sheets lightly with olive oil.
Mix flour, yeast, cornmeal, Italian seasoning, salt, black pepper, and garlic powder together in a bowl.
Lightly spray both sides of zucchini rounds with cooking spray. Dredge rounds in flour mixture, slightly pressing it into the zucchini.
Arrange coated rounds in a single layer on the prepared baking sheets.
Bake in the preheated oven until browned on the bottom, 5 to 10 minutes. Flip and bake until browned on the second side, 5 to 10 minutes more.
Place chicken breasts, 1 at a time, between 2 sheets of parchment paper and flatten with a mallet.
Combine bread crumbs, salt, and black pepper in a shallow bowl. Place flour in a separate shallow bowl and beaten egg in a third shallow bowl.
Dip chicken breasts first in flour, then in beaten egg, and finally coat with bread crumb mixture.
Preheat an air fryer to 375°F.
Place breaded chicken in the air fryer basket, making sure they are not touching, work in batches if necessary.
marsala wine: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente, drain.
Meanwhile in a large skillet, saute the chicken breasts in butter. Once chicken is lightly browned on all sides, add the soup and Marsala wine.
Cover and simmer until chicken is no longer pink inside, about 20 minutes. Garnish with parsley.
Season the pork chop with salt and pepper.
Dredge the pork chop in the all-purpose flour, shaking off any excess.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the pork chop to the hot skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side until it's browned and cooked through.
In the same skillet, add the diced onion and sliced mushrooms. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Pour in the Marsala wine and chicken broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Add the raw fettuccine pasta to the skillet. Make sure it's submerged in the sauce.
Cover the skillet and simmer for about 10-12 minutes, or until the pasta is tender and cooked to your liking, stirring occasionally.
Return the cooked pork chop to the skillet, coating it with the Marsala sauce and pasta.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, allowing the pork chop to absorb the flavors of the sauce.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper. Then, coat it evenly with the all-purpose flour, shaking off any excess.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the floured chicken breast to the skillet and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until it's cooked through and golden brown. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced mushrooms and sauté them for about 2-3 minutes until they begin to brown.
Pour in the Marsala wine, chicken broth, and heavy cream. Stir well to combine.
Let the sauce simmer for about 5-7 minutes, or until it thickens slightly and reduces.
Return the cooked chicken breast to the skillet and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes to heat the chicken through.
Taste the sauce and adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper, then dredge it in the flour, shaking off any excess.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken breast to the skillet and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until it's cooked through and golden brown. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced mushrooms and cook for 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Pour in the Marsala wine and chicken broth. Stir, scraping the bottom of the skillet to release any browned bits.
Let the sauce simmer for about 5 minutes or until it reduces by half.
Stir in the ghee to thicken the sauce and make it glossy.
Return the cooked chicken to the skillet and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes to heat the chicken through.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the chicken breast and cook until browned on both sides and cooked through. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add sliced mushrooms and cook until softened.
Pour in Marsala wine and chicken broth. Bring to a simmer and cook until the sauce is slightly reduced.
While the sauce is simmering, boil the cubed potato in salted water until tender. Drain.
Mash the cooked potato with ghee until smooth.
Serve the mashed potatoes alongside the cooked chicken breast.
Pour the Marsala mushroom sauce over the chicken.
Pound the chicken breast to an even thickness, then season with salt and pepper.
Dredge the chicken breast in flour, shaking off any excess.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken breast to the skillet and cook until golden brown on both sides and cooked through, about 4-5 minutes per side. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add sliced mushrooms and cook until they are tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Pour in Marsala wine and chicken broth, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and let it cook until the sauce reduces slightly, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in ghee until melted and well combined.
Return the cooked chicken breast to the skillet and spoon the sauce over it.
Cook for another 1-2 minutes to heat through.
Remove from heat and sprinkle chopped fresh parsley over the chicken.
Serve the chicken Marsala hot.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and pepper, cook in hot oil until browned, 3 to 5 minutes.
Stir onion with the chicken; continue cooking until the onion is translucent, 5 to 7 minutes.
Pour cream of mushroom soup and Marsala wine over the chicken mixture; cook until hot for 3 to 5 minutes.
Slice chicken breasts in half widthwise using a sharp knife.
Cover with plastic wrap, pound with the flat side of a meat mallet to 1/4-inch thickness. Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil and 2 teaspoons butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add 3 chicken pieces; cook until golden, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer to a large platter. Cook remaining chicken pieces in batches, adding 1/3 tablespoon olive oil and 2 teaspoons ghee for each batch.
Cook and stir shallot in the hot skillet until shallots soften, about 1 minute. Add mushrooms, cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper. Pour in Marsala wine, cook until slightly reduced.
Pour in chicken stock, simmer until slightly thickened, about 1 minute.
Stir 1 tablespoon ghee into the skillet. Return chicken to the skillet, simmer until heated through, about 1 minute. Garnish with chopped parsley.
Sauté 2 tsp ghee, garlic, and rosemary until garlic begins to brown.
Add Marsala wine and reduce by one-third.
Add remaining ingredients, reduce by half of the original volume. Set aside.
Sauté pancetta until it begins to brown. Add ghee, shallots, and shrimp. Cook until shrimp are evenly pink but still translucent.
Add chicken, salt, pepper, and mix thoroughly. Add gratinata sauce and ½ cup parmesan cheese.
Simmer until sauce thickens.
In a large bowl, combine shrimp & chicken mixture with the cooked pasta. Pour into a large casserole dish or roaster.
Top with remaining cheese, pimientos and sprinkle with paprika.
Bake at 475°F for 10 to 15 minutes.
Remove and garnish with fresh rosemary sprig.
Add wine and broth to a saucepan. Heat over medium-high heat, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium and let boil until reduced to ¾ cup.
Meanwhile season both sides of chicken with salt and pepper. Dredge each side in arrowroot powder.
Melt half of the butter with half of the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and let sear for 4 minutes per side.
Transfer chicken to a plate. Tent with foil. Reduce burner temperature to medium heat. Melt remaining butter with olive oil, add mushrooms.
Saute mushrooms, just tossing occasionally, until mushrooms have shrunk and are golden brown. Add garlic during last 1 minute of sauteing.
Remove pan from heat then pour in marsala reduction, thyme and oregano. Return to heat, bring to a simmer, while scraping up browned bits from the bottom.
Off heat stir in heavy cream, season sauce with salt and pepper to taste. Return chicken breasts to pan, spoon sauce over top.
Sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately.
Season chicken breasts with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper. Turn on a multi-functional pressure cooker and select Saute function.
Heat 1 ghee butter and 1 tablespoon olive oil, cook chicken breasts on both sides until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Remove chicken from the pot. Melt remaining ghee and  olive oil, add garlic and shallots and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Stir in mushrooms and cook until tender, about 3 minutes. Pour chicken broth into the pot and bring to a boil.
Return chicken to the pot and cover with Marsala wine. Close and lock the lid. Select Poultry function, set timer for 10 minutes.
Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build. Release pressure using the natural-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, about 10 minutes.
Release remaining pressure using the quick-release method, about 5 minutes. Unlock and remove the lid. Select Saute function.
Stir heavy whipping cream in the pot. Season with salt and pepper.
Cook until sauce has slightly thickened, about 5 minutes. Serve and sprinkle with fresh parsley.
whole wheat bread: Spray the slices of bread with water to moisten them. Roughly grate the tomato or chop it into small cubes.
Spread the grated tomato on the cracker and top with cheese. Sprinkle with pepper and a good amount of oregano.
Drizzle with olive oil, add a couple of olives to each slice of bread and enjoy!
Bring a small pot of water to the boil. Add the eggs, boil for 10 minutes, then transfer them to ice cold water to quickly cool off. Peel, slice and set aside.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium-low heat. Add a glug of olive oil, then the bread cubes. Cook until golden brown, about 3-5 minutes, flipping occasionally.
Add the mayonnaise, half the olive oil, Italian seasoning and lemon juice to a small mixing bowl. Season using salt and pepper and mix well to combine.
Heat the remaining olive oil over medium heat in a non-stick pan. Season the scallops using salt and pepper and cook 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they're no more translucent.
Add the greens to a large mixing bowl, add the dressing, eggs and apples, mix well, then transfer to a serving bowl. Add the scallops and croquettes and enjoy.
Chop the scallions and keep the whites and greens seperate. Heat oil in a heavy bottomed pan. Add chopped onions along with half of the salt.
Sauté the onions on medium heat until they are translucent and golden brown.
Add ginger, garlic, chili and the white scallions to the onions. Cook for 2 minutes, mixing well.
Add tomatoes and turmeric to the pan and cook for 2 minutes. Whisk the eggs with milk and remaining salt.
Slowly pour the whisked eggs to the pan and keep mixing with a spatula for 5-6 minutes or until the eggs are cooked.
Add the garam masala and garnish with chopped cilantro and remaining scallions. Mix well.
Heat a griddle on medium heat. Apply ghee on both sides of sliced bread.

Cook the bread slices until both sides turn golden-brown
Enjoy egg bhurji with the ghee toasted bread!
Heat the olive oil in a large pan. Add the onion and celery and fry gently until soft, about 3-5 minutes.
Add the stock, bring to the boil, lower heat and simmer gently for half an hour. Process with a stick blender until completely smooth, add salt and pepper to taste.
Meanwhile, heat a splash of olive oil in a non stick frying pan on medium heat. Add the cubed bread, fry until golden and crispy. Serve the soup hot with croutons.
To a small mixing bowl, add ½ oz mayonnaise and tuna. Season with salt and pepper to taste and mix well.
