Spread the mayonnaise on both slices of bread. Add slice of cheese to bottom half, top with leafy greens. Add tuna mixture and cherry tomatoes, close with second slice of bread. Enjoy.
In a large bowl, cover the lentils with boiling water and allow to sit for 15 minutes, drain.
In a large stockpot on medium heat, saute the onions, leeks, and garlic with the olive oil, salt, pepper, thyme, and cumin for 20 minutes, until the vegetables are translucent.
Add the celery and carrots and saute for 10 more minutes. Add the chicken stock, tomato paste, and lentils, cover and gently bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes, until the lentils are cooked through, season to taste at the end.
Serve hot, drizzled with olive oil, sprinkled with grated Parmesan with some bread.
halloumi cheese: Rinse the quinoa under cold water and cook it according to the package instructions. Typically, this takes about 15 minutes. Once cooked, let it cool slightly.
While the quinoa is cooking, slice the raw halloumi cheese into thin slices.
In a non-stick skillet, heat a small amount of olive oil over medium heat.
Once the skillet is hot, add the halloumi slices and cook for about 1-2 minutes on each side until they develop a golden brown crust.
In a bowl, combine the raw spinach, sliced cucumber, and fresh pomegranate seeds.
In a small cup, whisk together the remaining olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to create the dressing.
Once the quinoa is cooked and slightly cooled, add it to the salad mixture.
Place the cooked halloumi on top of the prepared salad.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss until well combined.
Mix up the tomatoes, garlic, basil, red pepper flakes, salt and pepper. Set aside.
Heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add halloumi and cook for 2 minutes, then flip and cook for another 1 minute. Remove from pan.
Toss the zoodles in the same pan for a minute. Add the tomatoes to the zoodles and cook for a few minutes until they have softened.
Top with Halloumi, sesame seeds and arugula and serve.
Cut the halloumi cheese into 1/3 inch slices. You don't want to make them too thin since they will shrink a little in the grill.
Grill the halloumi for about 5 minutes on each side. You should see nice grill marks along the sides. May also be pan fried as well.
Wash and dry the lettuce, place on a plate along with the avocado and soft boiled egg. Place the halloumi on top.
Sprinkle with salt and sesame seeds. Finish with olive oil and balsamic vinegar on top and serve.
Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add halloumi slices to the pan and fry on each side until golden.
Spread mashed avocado onto the toasted bread.
Sprinkle with lime juice, and chili flakes.
Top with fried halloumi.
Heat a grill pan over medium-high heat or heat a grill to medium heat.
Brush the halloumi slices with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, coating both sides of the cheese.
Place the halloumi on the grill pan or grill and cook on each side for 2 to 3 minutes or until grill lines form and the cheese is melting.
Toss the sliced cucumbers, halved tomatoes, greens, and mint together.
Spread them across a large, low bowl or large plate.
Mix the remaining olive oil, lemon juice, sea salt, and pepper together.
Drizzle across the salad.
When the halloumi is done cooking, add it to the salad and serve with bread immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the quinoa in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Slice the top off the bell pepper and remove the seeds and membranes.
In a bowl, combine the diced halloumi cheese, diced tomatoes, diced cucumber, cooked quinoa, olive oil, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Stuff the mixture into the hollowed-out bell pepper.
Place the stuffed bell pepper in an oven-safe dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the pepper is tender and the halloumi cheese is golden brown.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add Greek yogurt, olive oil, cottage cheese, grated halloumi, mint, and parsley to the flour mixture. Mix until the dough comes together.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough into a circle.
Transfer the dough to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Spread crumbled feta cheese over the center of the dough, leaving a border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough over the filling, creating a rustic, free-form shape.
Brush the exposed dough with beaten egg
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes or until the crust is golden and cooked through.
Preheat your air fryer to 390°F.
Meanwhile, pat the halloumi dry with a paper towel to remove any excess moisture.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, honey, red pepper flakes, and black pepper.
Brush both sides of the halloumi with the honey mixture.
Once the air fryer is preheated, arrange the halloumi in a single layer in the air fryer basket.
Air fry the halloumi for 5-6 minutes, flipping halfway through the cooking time, or until golden brown and crispy on the outside.
Remove the hot honey halloumi from the air fryer and transfer it to a serving plate.
Serve immediately as a delicious and flavorful appetizer or snack!
Place slices of bread in a toaster and toast for 3 minutes until golden brown.
Heat grill over medium-high heat. Add halloumi slices to the grill and fry on each side until golden.
Make guacamole from avocado, pea protein, lime juice, salt, and pepper. Spread on a slice of toasted bread. Top with halloumi and sesame seeds.
Place sliced halloumi cheese on a grill about 3 minutes on each side.
Place vegetables on a plate, and season with salt and pepper.
On the vegetables place grilled halloumi, and drizzle with olive oil. Serve.
Add orange juice, mustard, vinegar, 4 tbsp olive oil, salt and pepper into a bowl and mix well. Add peppers and tomatoes and toss gently.
Heat the remaining of oil in a frying pan and cook the halloumi for a few mins until golden on both sides.
Toss the lettuce through the dressed carrots. Arrange the lettuce mixture on plates and top with the halloumi.
Toast your bread using whichever method you prefer.
Heat a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat on the stove.
Add the butter and let it melt.
Swirl the pan to coat, then add the sliced halloumi.
You can do this in a couple of batches if all of the cheese doesn’t fit at once.
Grill the halloumi a couple of minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Remove to a plate while you prepare the rest of the toast.
Spread half of the avocado over each slice of toast, smashing down with a fork if necessary.
Squeeze the lemon juice evenly over the avocado, being sure not to lose any seeds.
Place the grilled halloumi slices over each piece of toast.
You may have one or two slices extra, eat these or save them for later use.
Finish off the toast by drizzling the honey evenly over both pieces of toast and sprinkling on red pepper flakes, based on your spice tolerance.
Enjoy immediately.
Wash and trim the ends of the zucchini. Make the zucchini pasta using a spiralizer or julienne peeler and set aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the halloumi cheese to the pan and cook, turning occasionally, for 2 minutes. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
Put the zucchini noodles, arugula, and pesto in a pan. Cook, tossing, for 3 minutes or until heated through. Remove from heat.
Place halloumi cheese on a plate, along with zucchini noodles mixture. Garnish with cherry tomatoes.
Heat the olive oil in a large non-stick frying pan over a medium-high heat.
Fry the cheese until golden underneath, then use tongs to turn the cheese over and cook until golden.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the cheese while it's frying.
Place the cheese on warmed plates, scatter over the fresh herbs, and season with black pepper. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Cut the halloumi cheese strips into fry-like shapes.
In a shallow dish, combine the all-purpose flour and paprika.
Dredge the halloumi fries in the flour mixture, ensuring they are evenly coated.
Place the coated halloumi fries on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Lightly spray the fries with cooking spray or drizzle with a small amount of vegetable oil to help them crisp up.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 10-12 minutes or until the halloumi fries are golden and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let them cool slightly.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the tomato halves to the pan, shaking to distribute everything. Cook until they are beginning to soften and break down a bit, about 2 minutes.
Add the spiralized zucchini and cook, stirring constantly, until they are warm and just barely soft.
Add the halloumi on a grill and bake for 5 minutes.
Place arugula on a plate, top with zucchini, tomatoes, and  halloumi cheese. Spinkle with salt and pepper and serve.
Dice the halloumi into ~1-inch cubes, large enough to slide onto skewers without falling apart.
Keep the tomatoes intact, cut the red onion and pepper into squares, and slice the zucchini into circles or ribbons.
In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, and parsley.
Add some salt and pepper to taste (keep in mind that halloumi is already very salty).
Place the veggies into the bowl with the marinade and toss until coated.
Cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes and up to 12 hours in advance.
When you are ready to prepare the skewers, preheat the grill to medium.
Thread 12 skewers with the marinated veggies and halloumi.
Cook under the grill for 10 to 12 minutes, rotating as needed, until the veggies are tender and the cheese is browned to your liking.
In a small bowl combine the pesto with 1.5 tbsp olive oil to thin it out.
In another bowl, mix the cherry tomatoes with about 1 tbsp of the pesto/olive oil mixture and set aside.
Heat up ½ tbsp olive oil in a frying pan and add the sliced mushrooms.
Let cook without disturbing them until they are browned on one side, then add salt and pepper and give them a stir.
Cook over medium heat, stirring often until they are done.
Remove to a plate and set aside.
In the same pan, fry the sliced halloumi cheese until it is golden on both sides then remove to a plate.
Heat up the last ½ tbsp olive oil in a pan and fry the four eggs to your liking.
Toast the bread, and lay each flat on a plate. Spread each piece with the remaining olive oil and pesto mixture.
Top each piece of toast with a handful of arugula, half of the tomatoes, half of the mushrooms, half of the avocado, half of the halloumi, and one fried egg.
Season with pepper then serve warm.
red chilies: Toast the peanuts in a dry pan for just 2 minutes, until fragrant.
In a stone bowl, combine the fish sauce, chili, garlic, lime juice, and tamarind and use the pestle to pound them enough to mash them into a paste.
Add papaya and beans and mix well. Then add the tomatoes, peanuts, and mix.
Add the sesame oil and white pepper into the fish paste, stir to combine well.
Clean and rinse the okras and red chilies.
Cut a slit in the middle of the okra to allow an opening to stuff fish paste inside.
For the red chilies, cut a slit down the middle of the red chilies, seed removed.
Cut the tofu puffs in the middle (but not completely broken).
Combine and stir all the ingredients in the Sauce Mixture, set aside.
Using a butter knife, stuff the fish paste inside the okras, red chilies, and tofu puffs.
Heat up a skillet and add 1 tbsp of oil.
One heated, arrange all the yong tow food in one layer and lightly pan-fry the yong tow foo on both sides so the surface is slightly browned.
Transfer the pan-fried yong tow foo onto a plate.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions of the packaging.
Remove the thick layer of fat running down the side of the steaks and discard.
Slice the steak into 1cm-thick, long strips and toss with the cornflour and some seasoning.
Heat the oil in a large frying pan or wok over a high heat then add the steak, frying for 3-5 mins or until golden brown on the outside.
Remove with a slotted spoon and transfer to a plate.
Add the onion and peppers to the pan and fry for 6-7 mins or until beginning to soften.
Stir through the garlic, ginger and chilli and cook for a further min.
Return the beef to the pan and stir through the black bean sauce, rice wine vinegar and 2 tbsp water to loosen a little.
Bring to a simmer and then remove from the heat.
Serve the stir-fry in deep bowls with mounds of sticky rice and top with coriander.
Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Add pasta and cook according to package directions. Drain.
In a large skillet over medium heat, heat sesame oil. Add green onions and cook until fragrant, 1 minute. Add shrimp, then season with salt and pepper. Cook for 3 minutes, then remove from skillet.
To same skillet, heat olive oil. Add sliced jalapeno and chili and cook until soft, 5 minutes, then add garlic and cook until fragrant, 1 minute more.
Pour in soy sauce, chicken broth, sweet chili sauce and rice vinegar adn whisk until combine. Simmer for 1 to 2 minutes then toss in pasta and shrimp, garnish with cilantro before serving.
In a small pot, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until it becomes translucent.
Add the minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, salt, and pepper. Cook for a minute to release the spices' flavors.
Add the chicken pieces and cook until they are no longer pink.
Stir in the cracked wheat (bulgur) and continue to cook for a couple of minutes.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer, cover the pot, and let it cook for about 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked, and the wheat is tender.
Adjust the seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed.
Serve your Spiced Chicken and Wheat Porridge hot, garnished red chili and green onion.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Tip the farfalle into a pan of boiling, salted water, stir once, then cook for 10 minutes until 'al dente' or according to packet instructions.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a saute pan and fry the bacon for a couple of minutes until crispy.
Add the onion and cook for another minute or two until softened, stirring occasionally.
Add the garlic with paprika and chilli and cook for about 30 seconds, stirring. Stir in the passata and cook for 2 minutes until heated through.
When the pasta is cooked, drain well and then return to the pan.
Pour in the tomato mixture, then fold everything together until well combined. Bake for 5 minutes and garnish with some grated Parmesan.
Heat oil in a wok over medium heat. Add chili, garlic, and cook, stirring, for 2 minutes or until fragrant.
Add chicken and cook over medium high heat, stirring frequently for 5 minutes or until cooked through. Stir in fish sauce.
Meanwhile pour boiling water over the noodles.
Remove from heat and add fish sauce, lime juice, mint, red onion, and mix well. Season with pepper and salt.
Serve with drained noodles and mix all together. Garnish with remaining mint leaves and onion.
Add the coconut cream to a saucepan and heat up to near boiling, stirring frequently. Mix in the curry paste and stir, combining the paste with the milk.
Add the sliced chicken and chicken stock and cook for 15 minutes. Toss in the vegetables and cook for 5 more minutes.
Serve immediately.
In a mixing bowl, combine the thinly sliced cucumber, red onion, and red chili.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the fresh lime juice, fish sauce.
Pour the lime dressing over the cucumber mixture.
Toss everything together to combine the ingredients and coat them with the dressing.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in a heavy based skillet or pot over medium high heat.
Add curry paste (and garlic, ginger and lemongrass extras, if using) and cook for 2 to 3 minutes until it mostly "dries out"
Add chicken broth and coconut milk, mix to dissolve paste. Curry in a jar seasonings, Add 1 tsp fish sauce.  Mix then bring to simmer.
Add chicken, stir then lower heat to medium so it's bubbling gently. Cook 7 minutes.
Add eggplants, cook 5 minutes until soft. Taste sauce.
Add snow peas, cook 2 minutes until a bit softened, then stir through basil and lime juice.
Sauce should have reduced but will still be a be on the thin side, not thick - that's how it's should be. DO NOT keep simmering - sauce will darken.
Serve curry over jasmine rice or roti bread with garnishes of choice.
Mix together the fish sauce, soy sauce, kecap manis, oyster sauce and water in a bowl or jug and set aside.
Heat up a large drizzle of oil in a wok or stir-fry pan, then stir-fry the green beans for a few minutes.
Add the chopped chillis and garlic and stir-fry for a couple more minutes.
Add the pork and stir-fry until cooked an additional few minutes.
Pour in the sauce, stir well, then let bubble for a minute or two more.
Stir in the basil until wilted, then serve with the rice and a fried egg, if desired.
In a mixing bowl, combine the drained tuna, diced cucumber, chopped fresh mint leaves, diced red onion, and red chili for heat.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Pour the dressing over the salad ingredients.
Gently toss everything together until the salad is evenly coated with the dressing.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging,
In a medium pot of boiling water, simmer tofu for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and let drain in a colander lined with paper towels. When cool enough to handle, gently squeeze and pat dry.
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat 2 tablespoons oil. Add ginger and garlic and fry until fragnant.
Add tofu and let cook until golden on all sides. Season with salt and pepper, and remove from skillet and set aside.
Add mushrooms and edamame in the skilled and fry for 3 minutes or until tender, add the tofu and flaxseed oil and cook for another 2 minutes.
Serve on a plate with rice and garnish with red chilli pepper.
Blend all spice paste ingredients with 2 tablespoons water until smooth.
Place asam jawa in a small metal strainer over a bowl. Pour 1 cup hot water over asam jawa.
Stir and press down asam jawa with a spoon to dissolve. Pour spice paste into a medium clay pot or non reactive pot.
Pour strained tamarind juice into the pot. Stir to combine. Add crushed lemongrass, daun kesum, salt to taste, and pineapple.
Again stir to combine. Place lid on the pot and allow sauce to come to a boil.
Reduce heat and continue to simmer for 10 minutes. Remove the lid. Do a taste test.
Add okra and fish. Place the lid back onto the pot. Continue to cook for another 3 to 5 minutes or until okra is tender.
Turn off stove and serve warm with steamed rice.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Prepare the Aromatics & Vegetables: Chop the aromatics and vegetables as indicated in the “Ingredients” section.
Clean and rinse the prawns: Peel and devein the prawns and rinse thoroughly. (Pro-tip: Buy already peeled and deveined prawns to save time)
Sichuan Stir-fry Sauce: Combine all the sauces in a medium bowl and stir with a spoon to combine.
Cook the noodles: Cook the noodles according to package instructions in a large wok filled with boiling water. Drain and set aside.
Wipe out the wok with a paper towel and set it back on the stovetop. Cook the aromatics: Heat oil, chili oil and oil in the wok over medium-high heat.
Once hot, add the onion, garlic, and spring onion whites and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
Add chilies & baby corn: Add the fresh red chilies and baby corn and continue stir-frying for a 1-2 minutes to combine.
Cook the prawns: Add the jumbo prawns, season with freshly cracked black pepper, and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until no longer translucent and grey.
Add the noodles & sauce: Add the noodles and immediately pour the contents of the sauce bowl over them.
Toss to combine and coat everything in the sauce. The sauce will start to thicken.
Add the bok choy: Add the bok choy and cook for 1-2 minutes until the green parts have wilted and the white parts have softened slightly.
Taste and season with more black pepper if needed, then switch off the heat.
To Serve: Divide the Sichuan Sauce Noodle Stir-fry with Jumbo Prawns in four plates and garnish with the chopped spring onion greens toasted sesame seed if desired.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix together fish sauce, lime juice, grated ginger, minced garlic, ground coriander, ground turmeric, chili flakes, sliced red chili (if using), salt, and pepper.
Place the chicken thigh or breast in a shallow dish or resealable plastic bag.
Pour the marinade over the chicken, ensuring it is evenly coated. Marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours.
Preheat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Remove the chicken from the marinade and discard any excess marinade.
Grill the chicken for about 6-8 minutes per side, or until cooked through and nicely charred.
Once cooked, remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Add 1 cup of cold water to the Instant Pot and place a trivet/steamer rack inside the pot.
Place the fish filets inside a cake tin or other heat-proof bowl that fits inside the pot.
Sprinkle each fillet with diced garlic, ginger and chili, plus a little salt and pepper. Pour soy sauce over the salmon fillets.
Place the tin with salmon on top of the trivet, so the tin doesn’t touch the water inside the pot.
Place and lock the lid, press Manual (High Pressure) and set the timer to 2 minutes.
Place cut up veggies inside a steam basket and sprinkle evenly with garlic.
Once the timer is up, use the quick release method to let out the pressure in the pot and open the lid.
Place the steam basket with the vegetables on top of the salmon tin. Drizzle them with lime juice, tamari sauce and olive oil. Sprinkle a little salt and pepper as well
Place the lid back on, lock it, press Manual (HIGH Pressure) and set the timer to 0 minutes (ZERO).
Once the timer goes off, release the pressure as quickly as possible.
Remove the steamer basket with the vegetables and set aside.
Carefully remove the tin with the salmon and transfer the salmon to a plate.
Pour any remaining juice over the top and serve with rice and vegetables on the side.
Combine all the ingredients in Chili Paste and blend well using a food processor.
Heat a wok and add "tumis" or stir fry the Chili Paste until the oil separates from chili. Set aside.
In a small bowl, combine all the ingredients of the Sauce together. Set aside.
Heat up a wok on high heat and add 1 tbsp of olive oil. Add the garlic, 3 tbsp of Chili Paste, potatoes, bean curd and squid.
Stir fry until fragrant. Add the yellow noodles and the Sauce and continue stirring.
Push the noodles to the side of the wok. Add the remaining ½ tbsp olive oil and crack the eggs on top of the oil.
Scramble the eggs and mix in with the noodles. Add the bean sprouts and quick stir for about 1 minute.
Dish out and garnish with sliced lettuce leaves and lime wedges. Squeeze some lime juice all over the noodles before serving.
Heat oil in a skillet on medium-high heat. Add the beef, onion, bell pepper, and garlic. Cook until the ground beef is no longer pink and the veggies are tender.
Add in paprika, salt, pepper, tomato sauce, chili, and rice. Bring to a boil and cover and reduce heat and let simmer 5-7 minutes or until rice is tender.
If the rice is uncooked, add some more water and simmer until done. Sprinkle with cheese and stir until cheese has melted.
In a large bowl toss together, lime juice, chili, coriander stalks, yogurt, onion, carrot and cabbage.
Whisk all the eggs together in a bowl, with a splash of water to loosen and a pinch of sea salt and black pepper.
Put a large non-stick frying pan on a medium-low heat. Once fairly hot, put in a tiny drizzle of oil followed by a quarter of the egg mixture.
Slide it on to a plate, spoon over a quarter of the vegetable slaw, then gently fold up the sides and roll over. Serve with a few extra coriander leaves.
beef broth: In a skillet over medium-high heat, cook the diced bacon until it becomes crispy. Remove it from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet with the bacon grease, add the thinly sliced beef. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Cook the beef for about 2-3 minutes, or until it's browned and cooked to your desired level of doneness. Remove it from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the chopped onion and sauté for about 2 minutes, or until it becomes translucent.
Return the cooked bacon to the skillet.
Add the cooked pinto beans to the skillet and stir everything together.
Pour in the beef broth and bring it to a simmer. Let it cook for about 5 minutes to heat everything through and allow the flavors to meld.
Add the cooked beef back to the skillet and simmer for an additional 2 minutes to warm the beef.
Stir in the chopped cilantro.
Serve your Carne en su Jugo in a bowl with a wedge of lime on the side.
Heat the olive oil in a small pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until it becomes translucent.
Add the minced garlic, harissa paste, ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook for a minute to release the flavors.
Add the lamb pieces and brown them on all sides.
Stir in the cauliflower florets, sliced carrots, and continue to cook for a few minutes.
Pour in the beef broth and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer, cover the pot, and let it cook for about 20-25 minutes, or until the lamb is tender, and the vegetables are cooked.
Adjust the seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed.
Heat oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add cubed beef and cook until browned.
Add minced garlic and sauté briefly. Stir in chopped onion and diced bell peppers. Sauté until they begin to soften.
Sprinkle paprika and caraway seeds over the ingredients. Mix well.
Add diced tomatoes and beef broth. Season with salt and pepper.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for about 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beef is tender.
In a bowl, mix together ground beef, grated onion, chopped fresh parsley, chopped fresh cilantro, ground cumin, paprika, ground cinnamon, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Form the mixture into small meatballs, about 1 inch in diameter.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the meatballs to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
Pour in low-sodium beef broth and bring to a simmer.
Let the meatballs simmer in the broth for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Serve the Algerian Kefta hot.
In a small saucepan, cook the lean ground beef over medium heat until browned.
Add the beef broth, diced tomatoes, and kidney beans to the saucepan. Stir to combine.
Sprinkle chili powder, salt, and black pepper over the mixture. Stir to incorporate.
Bring the chili to a simmer and let it cook for about 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Once the chili is heated through and flavors are blended, remove the saucepan from the heat.
Transfer the chili to a serving bowl.
Serve hot.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Sprinkle pork  with salt and pepper. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
In the same skillet, saute green pepper and onion in remaining 1 teaspoon oil until almost tender, about 2 minutes.
Stir in broth, pineapple, tomato sauce and vinegar. Bring to a boil. Return pork to the pan. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 5 to 7 minutes.
Cook noodles in a pot of water according to package directions.
In a large skillet, saute mushrooms and onion in oil until tender. Add broth, roast beef and pepper.
Bring to a boil. Reduce heat; simmer, uncovered, for 10 minutes. Drain noodles and stir into skillet.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Season the beef tenderloin steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in an ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the beef tenderloin steak for about 2-3 minutes on each side until it forms a golden-brown crust.
Transfer the skillet with the seared steak to the preheated oven.
Roast for about 8-10 minutes for medium-rare doneness, or adjust the cooking time according to your preference for doneness.
While the steak is cooking, in a separate skillet, sauté the sliced mushrooms until they are tender.
Add beef broth and Cabernet Sauvignon (or red wine) to the skillet with the mushrooms. Let it simmer and reduce slightly.
If you prefer a thicker sauce, mix a teaspoon of cornstarch with a tablespoon of cold water in a small bowl. Add this mixture to the skillet and stir until the sauce thickens slightly.
Once the steak is cooked to your liking, remove it from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes.
Serve the beef tenderloin steak topped with the Cabernet-mushroom sauce.
Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add cubed beef stew meat and brown on all sides for 4-5 minutes.
Add chopped onion and cook for another 2-3 minutes until softened.
Stir in sliced carrot and diced potato; cook for another 3 minutes.
Pour in beef broth and bring to a boil.
Add chopped dried apricots, dried cranberries, ground cumin, cinnamon, salt, and black pepper.
Reduce heat and simmer for 25-30 minutes until the beef and vegetables are tender.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh chopped parsley.
In a pot, bring beef broth to a boil over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced beef, carrot, mushrooms, garlic, and ginger. Simmer for 5-7 minutes until the beef is cooked and vegetables are tender.
Stir in soy sauce and sesame oil.
Meanwhile, cook noodles according to package instructions.
Drain noodles and add them to the soup.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh chopped cilantro.
In a bowl, combine ground beef, breadcrumbs, 1/2 milk, finely chopped onions, minced garlic, ground allspice, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Shape the mixture into small meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the meatballs and cook for about 8-10 minutes, turning them occasionally until they are browned and cooked through.
Remove the meatballs from the skillet and set them aside.
In the same skillet, add  beef broth, 1/2 milk, flour, salt, and black pepper. Stir well to create a creamy gravy.
Return the cooked meatballs to the skillet and simmer in the gravy for a few minutes until the gravy thickens.
Serve your Swedish Meatballs hot, with the gravy poured over them.
In a small saucepan, combine the low-sodium beef broth, cinnamon stick, whole cloves, fennel seeds, and coriander seeds.
Heat the broth mixture over medium-low heat until it comes to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer gently for about 10-15 minutes to infuse the flavors. Remove the spices if preferred or leave them in for a stronger flavor.
While the broth is simmering, prepare the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Usually, you'll need to soak them in hot water for a few minutes until they soften. Drain and set aside.
Thinly slice the raw beef sirloin or eye of round.
Once the broth is ready, strain it to remove the spices if you left them in.
Place the cooked rice noodles in a serving bowl.
Arrange the thinly sliced raw beef over the noodles.
Pour the hot broth over the beef and noodles. The heat from the broth will partially cook the beef.
Top your Homemade Pho with thinly sliced onions, fresh bean sprouts, cilantro leaves, basil leaves, mint leaves, and sliced jalapeño peppers.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook ground beef until browned. Add diced onion and bell pepper, and cook until softened.
Stir in diced tomatoes and beef broth. Simmer for a few minutes until slightly thickened.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the beef mixture to a small baking dish. Mix in cooked fusilli pasta.
Sprinkle shredded cheddar cheese over the top.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Season the beef short rib with salt and pepper.
In a small bowl, mix together the balsamic vinegar, beef broth, minced garlic.
Place the seasoned short rib in the slow cooker.
Pour the balsamic vinegar mixture over the short rib, ensuring it's evenly coated.
Cover and cook in the slow cooker on high heat for 3-4 hours, until the meat is tender and easily pulls apart.
Once done, remove the short rib from the slow cooker and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Season the steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.

Add the seasoned steak to the skillet and cook to your desired level of doneness, around 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare, depending on the thickness of the steak. Remove the steak from the skillet and set it aside to rest.
In the same skillet, add the sliced mushrooms and minced garlic. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the mushrooms start to brown.
Pour in the beef broth and Worcestershire sauce, scraping the bottom of the skillet to deglaze it.
Let the sauce simmer and reduce for about 2-3 minutes until it slightly thickens.
Place the steak back into the skillet with the mushroom sauce for a brief moment to warm it up again.
Remove the steak and sauce from heat, plate the steak, and pour the mushroom sauce over it. Optionally, garnish with fresh parsley.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and carrot to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add the ground turkey to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Stir in frozen peas,  broth, tomato paste, and Worcestershire sauce. Simmer for a few minutes until the mixture thickens slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the turkey mixture to a small baking dish.
Spread the mashed potatoes evenly over the turkey mixture.
Use a fork to create ridges on the surface of the mashed potatoes.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the mashed potatoes are golden brown on top.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Heat a small saucepan over medium-low heat and add the olive oil.
Add the thinly sliced onion to the saucepan and sauté for about 5 minutes, or until they start to become translucent.
Sprinkle the sugar over the onions and continue to cook for another 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the onions are caramelized and golden brown.
Pour in the low-sodium beef broth and Worcestershire sauce. Bring the mixture to a simmer and let it cook for an additional 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together. Season with black pepper.
While the soup is simmering, preheat your oven to 350°F.
Place the slice of whole-grain bread on a baking sheet and toast it in the preheated oven for about 5 minutes, or until it becomes crispy.
Ladle the hot onion soup into an oven-safe bowl.
Place the toasted bread slice on top of the soup.
Sprinkle the shredded Swiss cheese over the bread.
Transfer the bowl to the oven and broil for about 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is bubbly and lightly browned.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and grease with cooking spray or olive oil.
Add cubed beef to the skillet and cook until browned.
Add diced onion and bell pepper to the skillet and cook until softened.
Stir in diced tomatoes, beef broth, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the beef is tender and the flavors have melded.
Serve hot.
In a pot, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the cubed beef stew meat and minced garlic. Cook until the beef is browned on all sides.
Add diced onions, diced carrots, diced celery, and diced green beans. Sauté for a few minutes until they start to soften.
Pour in low-sodium beef broth and diced tomatoes.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Cover the pot and let the soup simmer for about 30-40 minutes, or until the beef is tender and the vegetables are cooked.
Serve your Vegetable Beef Soup hot.
Season the pork chop with salt and pepper.
Dredge the pork chop in flour, then dip it into the beaten egg, and finally coat it with breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Heat  oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the breaded pork chop to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the cooked pork chop from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add sliced mushrooms and cook until they are softened.
Pour in broth and stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Return the cooked pork chop to the skillet and let it simmer in the mushroom sauce for a few minutes.
Garnish with chopped parsley if desired.
Serve hot with lemon wedges on the side.
vegetable stock: Place the veg stock, garlic and ginger in a blender and whizz until smooth.
If your blender isn't big enough, do this in two batches and make sure to keep the lid on tight.
Pour the blended stock into a medium-sized pot and bring to the boil.
Once it's boiling, add the noodles and cook as per the packet instructions.
Chop the button mushrooms or roughly tear the oyster mushrooms.
Add the oil to a frying pan set on a high heat, then add the mushrooms along with the soy sauce and cook for 3 minutes, stirring regularly.
Once the noodles are cooked, remove them from from the stock with a slotted spoon and divide them between four large bowls, then pour over the broth.
Now layer up the bowls by placing some of the red pepper strips, grated carrot, red cabbage and spring onions in each bowl, along with some beansprouts.
Each bowl should be very colourful and rainbow-like in terms of colour.
Top with the fried mushrooms, then top each bowl with a sprinkle of sesame seeds and serve with a lime wedge.
Add boiled eggs, 1 per serving.
Bring a large pot of lightly salter water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.

In a large saute pan, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add the asparagus, season with the salt and pepper, and cook for 3 minutes. Add the cherry tomatoes and cook for 2 more minutes.
Transfer the asparagus mixture to a large serving bowl. Add the cooked pasta and Parmesan. Serve immediately.
In a saucepan bring water to boil. Add uncooked buckwheat groats. Cover the pot and simmer for 10 minutes. Set aside.
Heat the butter and oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Saute the onion until softened.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook for about 5 minutes on all sides. Make sure the mushrooms render as much moisture as possible.
Once mushrooms are well browned, and garlic and continue cooking on medium-low until garlic is fragrant.
Pour in the 2 tbsp of vegetable stock and cook for 2 minutes, to reduce the sauce slightly.
Add 1 tbsp of parsley. Cook the mushrooms for half a minute, until garlic is fragrant.
Season with salt and pepper, to taste. Serve porridge with mushrooms in two bowls and sprinkle with remaining parsley.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes or until onion softens.
Add curry paste and cook, stirring, for 1 minute or until fragrant.
Add the cauliflower, stock and water and bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes.
Transfer half the cauliflower mixture to a clean saucepan. Blend the remaining cauliflower mixture, until smooth.
Add to saucepan with unblended cauliflower mixture. Add coconut cream and stir over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat, garnish with parsley and season with salt .
Heat the olive oil in a large pan. Add the onion and celery and fry gently until soft, about 3-5 minutes.
Add the stock, bring to the boil, lower heat and simmer gently for half an hour. Process with a stick blender until completely smooth, add salt and pepper to taste.
Meanwhile, heat a splash of olive oil in a non stick frying pan on medium heat. Add the cubed bread, fry until golden and crispy. Serve the soup hot with croutons.
Heat the ghee in a pan, add the spring onions and zucchini and stir well. Cover and cook for 3 mins, add the peas and stock and return to the boil.
Cover and simmer for a further 4 mins, then remove from the heat and stir in the watercress and mint until they are wilted.
Puree in a food processor, adding yogurt with the second batch. Return to the pan, then add seasoning to taste. Serve hot or cold
Heat the oil in a deep saucepan, then soften the onion for 4-5 mins. Stir in the curry paste and cook for 1 min more until fragrant.
Add the grated sweet potato and stock, then bring quickly to the boil, simmering for 5 mins until the potato is tender.
Remove the soup from the heat, stir in the coconut and seasoning, then cool briefly before whizzing with a stick blender until smooth. Sprinkle with coriander.
Boil the asparagus tips for a few minutes in lightly salted water. Cool and place them to one side.
Fry in a pan together with the rest of the asparagus and spinach.
Add the vegetable stock and double cream, then bring to the boil. Allow to simmer until the asparagus is tender.
Blend it all into a smooth consistency with a hand blender. Season with salt, pepper and lemon juice.
Add the frozen prawns and allow them to thaw in the hot soup. Sprinkle the asparagus tips over the soup.
Put your stock or broth into a saucepan, and bring it to a simmer over low heat with a ladle nearby or in the pot.
Heat the olive oil over medium heat in a wide, heavy skillet or in a large, wide saucepan.
Add the onion and a generous pinch of salt, and cook gently until it is just tender about 3 minutes.
Add the rice and the garlic, and stir until the grains separate and begin to crackle. Add the wine, and stir until it has been absorbed.
Begin adding the simmering stock, a couple of ladlefuls (about 1/2 cup) at a time. Cook, stirring often until it is just about absorbed.
Stir in the diced broccoli and peas. Continue to add broth and stir the rice for another 5 minutes. Continue to add broth and stir the rice for another 10 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper, Parmesan, stir for a minute and serve.
Heat oil and half the butter in a large, deep frypan on medium-low heat. Cook onion and garlic for 5 minutes until soft.
Turn heat to medium-high, add rice and stir to coat. Add wine and stir for 1 minute.
Add stock a ladleful at a time, stirring and allowing each to absorb before adding the next, until rice is cooked but still al dente.
Add asparagus with a final ladleful of stock, stir in cheese, butter, and 1/2 cup water, cook for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, stir in rind and parsley. Season and serve with remaining parsley.
Boil noodles in the vegetable broth until al dente.
Add cloves of minced garlic, steamed broccoli, greens, and spices.
Add cooked chicken.
Simmer for five minutes to warm the chicken, then serve.
Combine the chopped seafood, pinch of salt and a pinch of ground black pepper in a small bowl. Mix it well and set aside.
Combine flour, starch, salt, and stock in a large enough to accommodate the green onions. Mix with a whisk until smooth.
Heat 2 tbsp of oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and swirl to coat evenly.
Add the green onions to the batter to coat them. Using tongs or your hand, place them side by side in the skillet.
Add the seafood to the leftover batter in the bowl. Using your hands or tongs, spread the battered seafood on top of the battered scallions, scraping out any excess batter remaining in the bowl.
Add the sliced chili and pour the beaten eggs over top of the pancake in the skillet.
Reduce the heat to medium and cook for about 6 minutes, until the bottom is light brown and crispy.
Lift one edge of the pancake, and cook on another side for 3 minutes.
Transfer to a large plate, with the egg and seafood side up.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add rice and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat the coconut oil in a medium skillet over medium-low heat. Add the onions, season with salt and pepper and cook for about 5 minutes.
Stir in garlic, turmeric and curry powder and cook for 30 seconds. Pour in the vegetable stock, chickpeas, coconut milk and honey.
Bring to a boil, reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes. Taste and adjust the seasoning.  Serve the curry over the rice.
Heat the stock in a jug pot. Leave it on low heat, it should remain hot while you cook the risotto, but it should not boil.
Melt 1 tbp of the butter in a pot. Chop the onion very finely and cook it for about 2 minutes until translucent.
Add the rice and stir well to coat it with the butter. Stir for about 2 minutes, it should start looking glossy.
Pour white wine and stir until all the wine is evaporated. Slowly, start adding the stock and stir well after each addition.
Stir in remaining butter. Stir well to melt the butter, the rice should be creamy and glossy.
Remove from the heat, cover, and let stand while you sear the salmon in the pan.
Heat the olive oil in a non-stick pan. Add the fish to the pan and sear, stirring often but gently, for 5 minutes.
Add the salmon to the rice and combine. Garnish with basil leaves and serve.
Add the oil to a pot with the chopped onion, garlic and ginger and saute.
Add in the coconut cream, protein powder, vegetable stock, and pumpkin, and bring to a boil.
Turn down the heat and simmer until the pumpkin is soft and cooked, 10 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to blend it smooth inside the pot.
Add salt and pepper to taste. Serve with leaves of parsley.
Heat the oil in a large pan over a medium-high setting.
Sauté the finely chopped onion until golden.
Add the crushed garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the garlic doesn't smell raw anymore.
Mix in the next four ingredients (ground coriander, turmeric, cumin, and cayenne) and toast for two minutes stirring often.
Add the crushed tomatoes, chickpeas and vegetable stock.
Increase the heat to high and once boiling, lower to medium-low to maintain at a simmer for 10 minutes, stirring every now and then.
Add 1/2 tsp salt, the sugar, and the spinach. If the spinach is frozen increase the heat until the curry is bubbling away again.
Simmer for an additional five minutes.
Add the garam masala, lemon juice and coconut milk and stir.
Sprinkle over the chopped cilantro leaves and serve hot with pita, if desired.
Turn on a multi-functional pressure cooker and select Saute function.
Heat olive oil and add onion, cook until translucent, about 7 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute more.
Combine tomatoes, vegetable broth, basil, nutritional yeast, salt, ground black pepper, and Italian seasoning in the pot.
Close and lock the lid. Select high pressure according to manufacturer's instructions, set timer for 5 minutes.
Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build.
Release pressure carefully using the quick-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, about 5 minutes. Unlock and remove lid.
Blend with an immersion blender until creamy.
Heat the onion with the olive oil and butter until the onion is translucent and soft, 5 minutes.
Add the rice and stir to toast until it makes a squeaking sound, 5 minutes.
Add 1/3 cup stock and stir until it evaporates. Add the tomatoes, salt, and another 1/3 cup stock.
When all the broth is absorbed, add remainingr. Keep enough liquid in the pan and stir continuously so that the rice does not stick.
Remove the rice from the heat. Serve topped with burrata cheese and serve.
Finely chop the shallot, mint and garlic.
Heat the olive oil over low heat in a small pot and add the mint, shallot and garlic mix. Let it cook softly for about 2-3 minutes, until the shallots turn translucent.
Add the peas and vegetable stock. Stir and let it simmer for about 15 minutes. Take off the heat and set aside.
Blend the soup using a stick blender until creamy. Season with salt, pepper to taste, and a drizzle of olive oil. Garnish with mint leaves before serving.
Heat olive oil in a soup pot. Add chopped onion and garlic. Cook for a minute.
Add crushed tomato, tomato paste, basil, salt, pepper, bay leaf, and stock to the pot. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in ½ cup of white beans to the pot. Using a hand blender blitz everything until smooth.
Stir in the rest of the bean to the soup. Give everything a good mix.
beef steak: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Wash and scrub the potato. Cut it into small cubes.
Toss the potato cubes with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Spread the seasoned potato cubes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Place the baking sheet in the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes until the potatoes are golden brown and tender.
While the potatoes are baking, season the steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat.
Once the skillet is hot, add the steak and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or longer to your desired level of doneness.
While the steak is cooking, steam the broccoli florets until tender, about 5-7 minutes.
Once the steak is cooked to your liking, remove it from the skillet and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing.
Serve the sliced steak with the roasted potatoes and steamed broccoli.
To prepare the dressing, coming the olive oil, vinegar, lemon juice, tamari, garlic and cayenne in a blender. Puree until smooth and season with salt to taste.
Preheat the skillet on high heat. Liberally rub ghee on both sides of the skirt steak and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the steak for 3 minutes per side with the lid closed. Allow the steak to rest for at least 5 minutes and then slice against the grain into strips.
Layer the steak over arugula and tomatoes. Top with dressing and Parmesan cheese.
In a large skillet over high heat, add 1 tbsp avocado oil. Add in the sliced steak and brown on both sides. Remove from pan to a plate and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add in 1 tbsp avocado oil. Add in the broccoli florets and saute until tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the steak back to the pan with the broccoli. Add in the soy sauce, swerve, garlic, ginger and broth. Stir to combine and let thicken for a few minutes.
Serve immediately.
Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
Zest and juice the orange into a small bowl.
Add olive oil, fresh thyme leaves, salt, and pepper to the bowl and mix well to combine.
Season the beef steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Brush the orange and thyme mixture over the steak, coating it evenly.
Grill the steak for 3-4 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or longer to your desired level of doneness.
Remove the steak from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes.
Slice the steak against the grain and serve.
Optionally, you can drizzle any remaining orange and thyme mixture over the sliced steak for extra flavor.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl combine butter, garlic, and dried herbs and set aside. Season steaks generously with salt, pepper, and garlic powder on both sides.
Place skillet over medium-high heat and drizzle with oil. Sear steaks for 1-2 minutes one each side until nice and browned.
Transfer the meat to a plate and set aside to rest. Add potatoes to the skillet, season generously with salt, pepper, and garlic powder and saute 3-5 minutes until browned.
Return the steak to the skillet, and transfer it to the oven to cook for 15-20 minutes, until potatoes are fork-tender and steak is cooked to your preferred doneness.
Serve hot with a sprinkling of salt.
Season the steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and add olive oil.
Once the skillet is hot, add the steak and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or longer to your desired level of doneness.
While the steak is cooking, prepare the mustard sauce by mixing Dijon mustard and balsamic vinegar in a small bowl.
Remove the steak from the skillet and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing.
In a serving plate, arrange the arugula and cherry tomatoes.
Place the sliced steak on top of the arugula and cherry tomatoes.
Marinate the beef with the soy sauce and ketchup for at least 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, bring a pot of water to a rolling boil, season the water with salt, and cook the rice according to instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat the oil in a wok, then saute the garlic until golden brown, being very careful not to burn it. Add the marinated meat and stir fry for 5 minutes, or less if you want the beef more rare.
On a plate, arrange a layer of cucumbers and beef, top with onion. Enjoy alongside rice.
In a pot, bring beef broth to a boil over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced beef, carrot, mushrooms, garlic, and ginger. Simmer for 5-7 minutes until the beef is cooked and vegetables are tender.
Stir in soy sauce and sesame oil.
Meanwhile, cook noodles according to package instructions.
Drain noodles and add them to the soup.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh chopped cilantro.
Preheat your air fryer to 400°F.
Season the thinly sliced beef steak with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Lightly coat the steak slices with cooking spray.
Place the seasoned steak slices in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
Air fry the steak slices at 400°F for 5-6 minutes, flipping halfway through, until they reach your desired level of doneness.
Meanwhile, place the bread slices on a plate and sprinkle shredded cheese evenly over each slice.
Once the steak slices are cooked, remove them from the air fryer and place them on top of the cheese-covered bread slices.
Place the assembled steak and cheese melts back in the air fryer basket.
Air fry at 400°F for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the steak and cheese melts from the air fryer and serve immediately.
Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced beef and cook for 3-4 minutes until browned. Remove beef and set aside.
In the same pot, add chopped onion, carrot, red bell pepper, green bell pepper, and garlic; cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Stir in the rice and cook for 2 minutes until lightly toasted.
Add beef broth, ground cumin, salt, and pepper.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes until the rice is cooked and liquid is absorbed.
Return the cooked beef to the pot and stir to combine.
In a small bowl whisk together gochujang, green onions, soy sauce, rice vinegar, garlic, ginger and sesame oil.
Pour half of mixture into a large ziplock bag.
Add steak to bag, seal, and chill for 30 minutes or up to overnight.
Cover remaining sauce and chill until ready to use.
Drizzle oil into a large pan or skillet and bring to medium-high heat.
Use tongs to transfer steak from bag to pan (keep marinade in the bag) and cook for 5-8 minutes on each side until well-browned on the outside.
Transfer steak to a large cutting board and allow to rest while you move on to the next step.
Add onions, mushrooms, ramen noodles, ramen seasoning, and beef marinade from the bag to the pan.
Pour water over the top and bring to a simmer. Allow to cook for 5-7 minutes until noodles tender and cooked through.
Thinly slice steak across the grain.
Add steak strips and reserved sauce to the pan and stir everything to combine.
Cook for a few minutes just until steak is hot throughout, garnish with green onions if desired and serve immediately.
Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
Season the beef steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Grill the steak for 3-4 minutes on each side for medium-rare, or longer to your desired level of doneness.
While the steak is grilling, peel and chop the potato into chunks.
Boil the potato chunks in a pot of salted water until tender, about 15-20 minutes.
Drain the cooked potatoes and mash them with a fork or potato masher until smooth.
Season the mashed potatoes with salt and pepper to taste.
In a bowl, toss together the mixed salad greens, cherry tomatoes, and cucumber slices.
Drizzle the salad with olive oil and balsamic vinegar, and season with salt and pepper.
Remove the grilled steak from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes.
Serve the grilled steak with a side of mashed potatoes and the mixed salad.
Add rice and water to a pot and cook according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a medium bowl, whisk together soy sauce, chicken stock, honey, vinegar, garlic, sesame oil, cornstarch, sriracha, ginger, red pepper flakes and 1/4 cup water, set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat. Add steak and cook, flipping once, until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in broccoli and soy sauce mixture until tender and slightly thickened, about 3-4 minutes. Serve immediately.

In a bowl, combine the thinly sliced beef, low-sodium soy sauce, fresh orange juice, orange zest, grated ginger, chili garlic sauce, minced garlic, and cornstarch. Mix well and let it marinate for 10-15 minutes.
Heat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add the oil.
Once the oil is hot, add the thinly sliced bell peppers to the skillet. Stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes or until they start to soften.
Push the bell peppers to one side of the skillet and add the marinated beef to the other side. Stir-fry the beef for about 2-3 minutes or until it's cooked through and slightly caramelized.
Combine the cooked beef and bell peppers in the skillet and stir-fry together for another minute to heat through.
Transfer the Spicy Orange Zest Beef with bell peppers to a serving plate.
Garnish with sliced green onions.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, cornstarch, red pepper flakes, minced garlic, salt, and pepper. This will be your stir-fry sauce.
Heat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add the olive oil.
Add the thinly sliced beef to the hot skillet and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes, or until it's no longer pink. Remove the beef from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the mixed stir-fry vegetables. Stir-fry them for about 3-4 minutes, or until they begin to soften but are still crisp.
Return the cooked beef to the skillet with the vegetables.
Pour the stir-fry sauce over the beef and vegetables. Stir-fry everything together for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce thickens and coats the ingredients evenly.
Taste the stir-fry and adjust the seasoning if needed.
Preheat the oven to 400°F
Cut the potato into fries.
Toss the potato fries with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, and any additional seasonings of your choice.
Spread the seasoned potato fries in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the potato fries in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway through, until crispy and golden brown.
While the potato fries are baking, season the beef steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat.
Once the skillet is hot, add the seasoned beef steak and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side until browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake the beef steak for an additional 5-7 minutes for medium-rare, or longer to your desired level of doneness.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let the beef steak rest for a few minutes before slicing.
Serve the beef steak with the crispy potato fries.
Enjoy your delicious and satisfying meal!
Season the steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.

Add the seasoned steak to the skillet and cook to your desired level of doneness, around 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare, depending on the thickness of the steak. Remove the steak from the skillet and set it aside to rest.
In the same skillet, add the sliced mushrooms and minced garlic. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the mushrooms start to brown.
Pour in the beef broth and Worcestershire sauce, scraping the bottom of the skillet to deglaze it.
Let the sauce simmer and reduce for about 2-3 minutes until it slightly thickens.
Place the steak back into the skillet with the mushroom sauce for a brief moment to warm it up again.
Remove the steak and sauce from heat, plate the steak, and pour the mushroom sauce over it. Optionally, garnish with fresh parsley.
Heat oil in skillet over high heat. Once hot, add in steak bites and season with salt and pepper. Cook 2 minutes undisturbed. Stir, cook another 2 minutes. Set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add butter to same pan. Once melted, add garlic and parsley and cook about a minute, stirring.
Remove from heat. Add steak bites back into pan and toss well. Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil. Add the rice and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside before fluffing using a fork.
Slice the steak in thin strips and brown in a frying pan or wok with the oil. Remove and set aside.
Add soy sauce and water to the pan and saute onion and garlic until softened. Add the sliced carrot and peppers. Heat long enough to soften peppers, about 3-5 minutes, then add the sliced steak.
Cook until everything is hot and then serve over rice.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, combine the thinly sliced beef, low-sodium soy sauce, grated ginger, minced garlic, cornstarch, and honey (if using). Mix well and let it marinate for about 10 minutes.
Heat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat and add the oil.
Once the oil is hot, add the marinated beef to the skillet. Stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes or until the beef is cooked through and slightly caramelized.
Add the cut spring onions to the skillet and stir-fry for an additional 1-2 minutes until they become slightly tender.
Serve the Mongolian Beef and Spring Onions over cooked rice.
flank steak: Prepare the beef by trimming fat, and dicing it into one-inch cubes. Toss into the slow cooker, and sprinkle with pepper, salt, garlic powder, and oregano.
Stir to coat the beef cubes. Top with the sliced onion and cook on high for 4-6 hours.
20 minutes before the hearts are ready, bring a pot of water to a boil and cook the rice to the package instructions.
Serve over rice.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and cornstarch.
Add thinly sliced beef to the bowl, ensuring each piece is coated in the mixture. Let it marinate for a few minutes.
In a wok or skillet, heat ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add the marinated beef to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 2-3 minutes).
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds over the beef, stirring to combine.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Cut the flank beef steak into medium/thin strips, marinate with coconut aminos, salt, and arrowroot flour.
While the beef is marinating, clean then chop broccoli into small florets, lightly saute with 1 - 2 tablespoons of cooking oil for 3 - 4 minutes while they still appear to be bright green.
In a small bowl, mix together all the ingredients for the stir fry sauce.
Set aside.
Heat 2 - 3 tbsp of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sautee the marinated beef and minced garlic for 3- 4 minutes then add sliced carrots.
Continue to stir and cook until the beef is close to cooked through (another ~2 - 3 minutes depending on how thin you cut the beef).
Stir in broccoli then the stir fry sauce.
Turn the heat to low and stir gently until the sauce becomes sticky and thoroughly coat the beef and broccoli stir fry.
Serve the beef stir fry on a bed of rice and garnish with sesame seeds or chopped green onion (optional).
Serve with rice.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, oyster sauce, and cornstarch.
Add thinly sliced beef to the bowl, ensuring each piece is coated in the mixture. Let it marinate for a few minutes.
In a wok or skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add the marinated beef to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 2-3 minutes).
Toss in thinly sliced bell pepper and onion, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
Adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with sesame seeds.
Preheat the oven to 425°F.
On a sheet pan, arrange the sliced steak, shrimp, onion, and bell pepper.
Drizzle olive oil over the ingredients on the sheet pan.
Sprinkle chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper over the ingredients. Toss everything together to coat evenly.
Spread out the ingredients on the sheet pan in a single layer.
Place the sheet pan in the preheated oven and roast for 8-10 minutes, or until the steak is cooked to your desired doneness and the shrimp are pink and opaque.
While the steak and shrimp are cooking, warm the corn tortillas in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 1 minute on each side.
Once the steak and shrimp are cooked, remove the sheet pan from the oven.
Divide the cooked steak, shrimp, onions, and bell peppers between the warmed tortillas.
In a large pot with boiling water, cook the noodles according to package directions.
Add the broccoli the last 5 minutes of cooking and let them cook until tender. Drain the noodles and broccoli.

While the pasta is cooking, add olive oil to a medium sized skillet. Cook the steak until no longer pink.
Add the garlic, and carrots and cook for a minute.
In a small bowl whisk together soy sauce, hoisin sauce, ginger, and pepper.
Add the spaghetti to the skillet and pour the sauce on top and toss until incorporated.
In a medium saucepan bring water to a boil. Add rice,  and a pinch of salt. Cook, covered, about 15 minutes, fluff with a fork, and set aside.
In a medium bowl, season meat with salt, pepper and, then toss with cornstarch until well-coated. Set aside.
In a large skillet heat 1 tsp oil. Add eggs and scramble using a rubber spatula and transfer to a plate.
Turn heat on high, and add another tsp of oil. Add meat, working in batches, and cook until seared all over, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate.
In the same skillet over medium-low heat, add 2 tbsp oil, onion, garlic, ginger, and cook until starting to soften.
Add peas, pepper and 2 tbsp water, and cook until tender. Add cooked rice and break up using a spatula, cooking 3 minutes more.
Stir in soy sauce, white vinegar, pepper, and sesame oil. Add back meat and eggs, then toss together and serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 400˚F.
Cut the flank steak against the grain into ⅛-inch thick slices.
In a large skillet over medium heat, heat the olive oil, until shimmering. Add the steak, paprika, garlic powder, ½ teaspoon of pepper, 1 teaspoon of salt, and the Worcestershire sauce.
Mix to coat the meat with the spices and cook until the meat is cooked through, about 3 minutes. Transfer the meat a bowl, leaving the juices in the pan. Cover and set aside until ready to use.

Add the butter to the pan with the meat juices. Add the onions and cook until tender and shiny, about 10 minutes. Add the remaining ½ teaspoon of pepper and teaspoon of salt, and cook until the onions are caramelized, about 10 minutes.
Cut the top off of a red bell pepper. Cut around the seed pod, then pull it out of the pepper. Remove any remaining seeds. Repeat with the rest of the peppers.
Place the peppers in a baking dish, cut side up. Scoop a bit of steak filling into each pepper, followed by the onions. Repeat with the rest of the steak, then the rest of the onions.
Bake for 30 minutes, until the skin of the peppers is shriveling, and the cheese is golden.
Top with some grated Parmesan cheese and parsley.
Cook the rice in pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.

Place the beef in a shallow dish. Combine soy sauce, , green onion, garlic, sesame seeds, sesame oil, and ground black pepper in a small bowl. Pour over beef. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour or overnight.
Preheat an oiled skillet on medium to high heat and grill beef until slightly charred and cooked through, 1 to 2 minutes per side.
Combine the beef and marinade ingredients and marinate for about 1 hour.

Some fresh rice noodles come as large sheets, while others are already cut. If you have the sheets, slice the rice noodles so they're about 1 inch wide. If the noodles are very stiff and stuck together, bring a wok full of water to a boil, and add the noodles. Blanch them for 30 seconds to loosen. Transfer to an ice bath and drain thoroughly.
Heat your wok over high heat until smoking, and add 1 1/2 tablespoons oil to coat the wok. Add the beef and sear until browned. As long as your wok is hot enough, the meat shouldn't stick. Set aside
Add 1 1/2 tablespoons more vegetable oil to the wok. Add the ginger to infuse the oil with its rich flavor for about 15 seconds. Add the scallions.
Spread the noodles evenly in the wok and stir-fry on high for about 15 seconds. Add the Shaoxing wine around the perimeter of the wok.

Next, add the sesame oil, soy sauces, pinch of sugar and the seared beef. Stir-fry, making sure your metal wok spatula scrapes the bottom of the wok. Lift the noodles in an upward motion to mix well and coat them evenly with the sauce.
In a food processor, add the garlic. Pulse until minced. Add fresh parsley and cilantro and pulse until finely chopped.
Add oil, lemon juice, crushed red pepper, salt and pepper sauce ingredients and pulse until well combined. Set aside.
Heat a large cast iron skillet over medium-high heat for about 3 minutes. Pat dry the steak and sprinkle with a modest amount of salt.
Add avocado oil to the skillet. When the oil is shimmering, use a pair of long tongs and carefully place steaks in skillet. Cook 3 minutes on each side.
Remove steak from skillet and let rest on a cutting board for 5 minutes. After resting, cut the steak into ½" slices against the grain.
Arrange on a large serving platter and top with fresh chimichurri sauce. Serve immediately.
Mix all fajita seasoning ingredients in a bowl.
Marinate steak with 2 tbsp of fajita seasoning, lime juice, cilantro, garlic, and half of the olive oil and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
When ready to cook, heat the remaining oil in skillet and saute bell peppers along with 1 tsp of fajita seasoning. Cook for 5 minutes, then set aside.
In the same skillet, stir in marinated steak strips. Keep on stirring on high flame to give it a quick sear. Cook for 2 minutes.
Arrange the fajitas along with peppers in skillet or platter and serve.
Place the steak into a sealable bag.
Place the peppers and onion into a separate sealable bag.
Add the olive oil, lime juice, chili powder, ground cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, black pepper, and garlic to a jar with a screw top or tight fitting lid.
Shake until well combined.
Pour ⅓ of the marinade over steak, ⅓ of marinade over vegetables and reserve the remaining marinade in the jar for using when cooking the steak fajitas.
Seal the bags tightly and refrigerate for one hour to overnight.
When ready to cook, heat a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Pour the vegetables into the skillet and cook until just tender crisp, about 5 minutes.
Remove the vegetables from the skillet to a plate and add steak strips to the same skillet.
When the steak is cooked throughout (about 7-10 minutes), add vegetables back to the skillet along with the reserved marinade.
Serve with warm tortillas and desired optional garnishes.
pork chops: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a shallow dish, combine the breadcrumbs, dried oregano, dried basil, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Mix these dry ingredients together.
Dip the boneless pork chop into the beaten egg, making sure it's coated evenly.
Place the egg-coated pork chop into the breadcrumb mixture and press the breadcrumbs onto both sides of the chop, ensuring it's well coated with the Italian-seasoned breadcrumbs.
Lightly spray a baking sheet or ovenproof skillet with cooking spray.
Place the breaded pork chop on the prepared baking sheet or skillet.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Bake the pork chop in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes. The breading should be golden brown and crispy.
Remove pork chop from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat an air fryer to 390°F.
Place pork chops on a plate and season both sides with Cajun seasoning.
Lightly beat eggs in a separate shallow dish. Dip pork chops into eggs, letting excess drip off.
Coat chops in breading and set on a plate.
Oil air fryer basket and place chops inside, making sure to not overcrowd the fryer.
You may have to do two batches depending on the size of your air fryer.
Cook for 5 minutes. Flip chops and mist again with cooking spray if there are dry or powdery areas.
Cook 5 minutes more. Repeat with remaining chops.
Place pork chops on a paper towel to absorb any excess moisture. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Whisk egg and soy sauce together in a small bowl. Place flour on a small plate and bread crumbs in another.

Dredge a pork chop in the flour, pressing it in using your fingertips to cover all crevices along the surface. Turn to evenly coat all sides. Dip the pork into the egg mixture, coating completely. Transfer immediately to the bowl of bread crumbs, pressing in to evenly coat. Repeat process with the second pork chop.

Melt the butter in a small frying pan over high heat. Once it begins to foam, stir it and watch it carefully, it will brown in 1-2 minutes. Stir the butter a few times to ensure it browns evenly. Remove the pan from the heat. Add the olive oil to the pan then and set it aside for the butter to cool slightly.

In a medium-sized bowl, whisk the maple syrup, balsamic vinegar, apple cider vinegar, and mustard. Slowly drizzle in the brown butter and oil, whisking the entire time. Once all the oil and butter have been added, whisk the dressing quickly for several seconds so it becomes creamy.
Store the dressing at room temperature until you are ready to use it.

Put the cabbage, carrots and spring onions in a large bowl and stir to combine. Mix the mayonnaise with the mustard in another small bowl and drizzle over. Fold everything together to coat in the creamy sauce, then season with salt and pepper.

Heat oil in a wide pan or wok on medium-high heat to 350°F. Lower a pork chop into the oil. Fry until bottom side is golden, 2 to 3 minutes. Turn; cook until other side turns golden and pork is slightly pink in the center.
Pick up the cutlets and hold them on their side for a few seconds to let the oil drip off. Drain on a paper towel.
Place the cutlets on the plate and serve them with brown dressing and coleslaw.
Preheat the over to 390°F.
Season and oil the potatoes and place them in the oven for 35 minutes. Add the broccoli and bake for another 10 minutes.
Preheat grill for medium heat and lightly oil the grate.
Whisk tomato sauce, soy sauce, and garlic together in a bowl to make a glaze.
Sear the pork chops on both sides on the preheated grill. Lightly brush glaze onto each side of the chops as they cook, grill until no longer pink in the center, about 7 to 9 minutes per side.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onions to the skillet and sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the chunks of boneless pork shoulder to the skillet.
Season the pork with ground cumin, chili powder, salt, and black pepper.
Cook the pork for about 2-3 minutes on each side until it's browned.
Stir in the diced tomatoes and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Pour in the low-sodium chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.

Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and let the pork simmer for about 20-25 minutes, or until it's tender and cooked through. You can add a little more chicken broth if needed to prevent sticking.
Warm the tortillas in a dry skillet or microwave them for a few seconds until they are soft and pliable.
Once the pork is cooked and the sauce has thickened, fill the tortillas with the Pork Carnitas mixture. Top with onion and parsley.
Serve your Pork Carnitas Tacos.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Using a sharp knife, carefully cut a pocket horizontally in the side of the pork chop, being careful not to cut all the way through. The pocket should be large enough to stuff with pesto.
Season the inside of the pork chop pocket with a pinch of salt and black pepper.
Spoon the pesto sauce into the pocket, filling it as evenly as possible.
Use toothpicks to secure the open end of the pork chop, sealing the pocket with the pesto inside.
Heat an ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat and lightly spray it with cooking spray.
Place the stuffed pork chop in the hot skillet and sear it for about 2-3 minutes on each side, until it's browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast the pork for approximately 15-20 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Remove the Pesto Stuffed Pork Chop from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Season pork chops with salt, pepper, and garlic powder to taste. In a large nonstick skillet, brown the chops over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and mushrooms, and saute for 1 minute. Pour cream of mushroom soup over chops. Add rosemary, cover skillet, and reduce temperature.
Simmer 20 minutes, or until chops are cooked through. Serve imemdiately.
Set uppermost oven rack 5 inches from heat source, and preheat broiler.
Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Stir in onions, and cook until soft and golden.
Remove from heat. In a bowl, stir together 1/2 teaspoon paprika, onions, and sour cream.
Season pork chops with pepper, garlic salt, and 1 teaspoon paprika.
Broil pork chops 6 minutes. Turn, and broil 5 to 6 minutes more. Remove from oven.
Spread onion mixture over pork chops, and broil 1 or 2 minutes more.
Combine paprika, garlic powder, salt and pepper in a small bowl. Pat the pork chops dry with kitchen paper then season generously with the spice mix.
Heat olive oil and butter in a skillet over medium-high until butter is melted. Sear the pork chops for 5 minutes per side. Transfer the pork chops to a warm plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the mushrooms and stir fry until golden brown, scraping up any bits leftover from the chops.
Add garlic, parsley then stir fry for another 30 seconds before pouring in the broth. Add the cream, give a good stir and simmer for 3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Place pork chops and their juices back into the sauce and simmer fot 3 minutes. Garnish with remaining fresh parsley and serve.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook noodles in the boiling water, about 2 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Meanwhile, combine soy sauce and fish sauce in a small bowl, set aside.
Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat for 1 to 2 minutes. Pour in oil. Add curry paste, garlic, and ginger paste, cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add coconut milk in intervals, stirring as you pour it in to keep the sauce from separating.
Add pork strips, carrots and kale and cook until pork is cooked through, about 5 minutes.
