Add drained noodles to the skillet, then add soy sauce mixture. Simmer until sauce has thickened, 4 to 5 minutes.
Preheat oven to 450°F.
Toss potatoes with oil, 1/2 tsp. salt, and 1/4 tsp. pepper on a rimmed baking sheet.
Add corn cobs and roast, tossing halfway through, until potatoes are tender and corn is cooked through and beginning to brown, 15–20 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix butter, herbs, and remaining 1 tsp. salt and 1/2 tsp. pepper in a small bowl.
Whisk barbecue sauce and honey mustard in another small bowl.
Preheat broiler on high and set oven rack about 4" from heat.
Line broiler pan or another rimmed baking sheet with foil.
Using a pastry brush, slather pork chops with about half of barbecue sauce mixture and transfer to prepared pan; reserve remaining barbecue sauce mixture.
Broil pork chops, turning and brushing with remaining barbecue sauce mixture halfway through, until cooked through, about 15 minutes.
Spread each corn cob with 1 Tbsp. herb butter; serve remaining herb butter alongside.
Top potatoes with Parmesan.
Divide pork chops and potatoes among plates.
Serve with corn alongside.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat. Stir in the onion and cook and stir until the onion has softened and turned translucent, about 5 minutes.
Season with salt. Stir in the ginger, tomatoes, potatoes, carrot and pork chops. Cover and reduce heat to medium-low. Turn the pork occasionally, until browned.
Pour in the water and tamarind soup base. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat. Continue simmering until the pork is tender and cooked through, about 30 minutes.
Stir in green beans and cook until tender. Serve imemdiately.
Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil, add potatoes and cook for 12 to 15 minutes until tender.
Heat grill to medium-high. In large bowl, whisk together oil, vinegar, horseradish, celery seeds, and ¼ teaspoon salt. Toss with tomatoes, celery, and onion.
Season pork chops with ½  teaspoon each salt and pepper and grill until golden brown and just cooked through, 5 to 7 minutes per side.
Fold parsley into tomatoes and serve over pork, potatoes and greens.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the kale, and cook until tender, about 5 minutes. Transfer the kale to the ice water for a minute, drain and set aside.
In a medium saucepan over medium heat melt the butter. Add the rice and cook for 1 minute.  Add the bell pepper and garlic, cook for 2 additional minutes
Add salt, turmeric, garlic powder, paprika and the chicken stock, turn up the heat and bring to a boil. Then reduce heat to low and simmer covered for 15-18 minutes.
Remove from the heat and let sit, with the lid on for 10 minutes. Fluff the rice with a fork and set aside. Meanwhile cook the pork chops.
In a large skillet, heat the oil over medium-high. Pat the pork dry, season heavily with salt and pepper. Cook, turning occasionally, until browned and cooked through, 8 to 12 minutes.
Transfer pork to a cutting board, let it rest for 5 minutes, then thinly slice it. Divide the kale and pork among plates, drizzle pork with the pan juices and add the rice. Enjoy!
Season the pork generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
In a deep skillet, add the olive oil and 1 tbsp of the butter over medium-high heat.
Let the pan heat up for a few minutes, and once it's hot, cook the pork chops for 5 minutes/side or until golden.
Add the remaining butter and the garlic to the skillet. Turn the heat down to medium and cook 5 minutes.
Sprinkle with flour and stir. Add in the chicken broth and lemon juice and cream. Stir and let it cook for about a minute or until the sauce is noticeably thickened.
Add the pork back in and cook for another 5 minutes or until the sauce has thickened up a bit.
Combine paprika, garlic powder, salt and pepper in a small bowl. Pat the pork chops dry with kitchen paper then season generously with the spice mix.
Heat olive oil and butter in a skillet over medium-high until butter is melted.
Sear the pork chops for 5 minutes per side until well browned on both sides. Transfer the pork chops to a warm plate and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the mushrooms and stir fry until golden brown, scraping up any bits leftover from the chops.
Add garlic and Italian seasoning then stir fry for another 30 seconds before pouring in the broth.
Add the cream, give a good stir then allow simmering for 3 minutes until slightly thickened and adjust seasoning if needed.
Place pork chops and their juices back into the sauce and allow to simmer for 3 minutes, and serve.
Cook the pasta in a pot according to the instruction on the packaging or until al dente.
Season both sides of pork chops with paprika, salt, and pepper.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat, add 2 tbsp ghee. Sear pork chops, 2 to 4 minutes per side.
Melt remaining ghee in the same skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and cook until golden about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and mustard; cook until garlic is fragrant, about 1 minute. Slowly add beef broth and cream, whisking until incorporated.
Season with salt and pepper. Reduce heat to medium and simmer, stirring often, until sauce thickens, about 5 minutes.
Return pork chops to the skillet and cook until heated through, about 1 minute. Serve hot. Garnish with parsley.
Combine pork chops, salt, thyme, rosemary, and pepper in a resealable plastic bag.
Seal and shake until pork chops are coated with seasoning.
Turn on a multi-functional pressure cooker and select the Brown function. Pour in oil.
Add pork, with seasoning, and cook until browned, about 5 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate.
Melt 1 tbsp ghee in the pot. Cook onions until soft and browned, 8 to 10 minutes.
Add potatoes and garlic. Balance pork chops over potatoes.
Whisk apple cider, mustard and nutmeg in a bowl. Pour over pork chops.
Close and lock the lid. Select high pressure according to manufacturer's instructions; set timer for 8 minutes.
Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build. Let pressure release naturally for 5 minutes.
Nudge valve slightly open to release remaining pressure more rapidly. Transfer pork chops to a plate.
Transfer potatoes and onions to a bowl using a slotted spoon, leaving gravy in the pot.
Add remaining 1 tbsp ghee and milk to the potatoes; mash until smooth. Divide mashed potatoes between serving plates.
Top with 1 pork chop. Spoon gravy on top.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Spray a sheet pan with cooking spray.
Combine salt, pepper, and garlic powder together in a small bowl and set aside
Place onion, red bell pepper and green bell pepper on the sheet pan and drizzle with olive oil. Season with 1 tsp of the spice mix. Toss together.
In a large tupperware with fitted lid or a ziptop bag, combine pork rinds, almond flour and remaining spice mix.
Place about ⅓ of the pork pieces into the bag and shake vigorously to coat all of the pieces on all sides. Remove from the bag and place on the sheet pan.
Continue with the rest of the pork. Discard the remaining pork rind coating. Bake for 10 minutes.
While pork and veggies are cooking, prepare the sweet and sour sauce by combining avocado oil, vinegar and tomato paste and whisk together.
Take the pan out and pour the sauce evenly over the pork and veggies. Toss everything together. Place back into the oven for 5 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Oil a baking dish.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Sprinkle pork chops with salt and black pepper, and brown them on both sides, about 5 minutes per side. Set chops aside.
Mix the water, rice, tomato sauce, picante sauce, and taco seasoning in the prepared baking dish.
Lay the chops into the rice mixture. Cover the dish.
Bake in the preheated oven until the chops are no longer pink inside and the rice is tender, about 1 hour.
phyllo pastry: Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a skillet, cook the ground beef over medium heat until browned. Add the chopped onion and diced potato, and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Season the beef mixture with ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine.
Cut each phyllo pastry sheet in half lengthwise to create four strips.
Place a spoonful of the beef mixture at one end of each strip of phyllo pastry.
Fold one corner of the phyllo pastry over the filling to form a triangle. Continue folding the pastry in a triangle shape until you reach the end of the strip.
Repeat the process with the remaining beef mixture and phyllo pastry strips.
Place the samosas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the samosas with olive oil.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the samosas are golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Lay out the filo pastry sheets and cut each sheet into 4 squares.
In a mixing bowl, combine the cooked and drained spinach, chopped artichoke hearts, grated Parmesan cheese, Greek yogurt, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Place a spoonful of the spinach and artichoke mixture in the center of each filo square.
Fold the corners of each filo square over the filling to create a cup shape.
Place the filled filo cups on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10-12 minutes, or until the filo pastry is golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Thaw the phyllo pastry sheet according to package instructions, if frozen.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped spinach and cook until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
Remove the skillet from heat and let the spinach cool slightly.
In a mixing bowl, combine the cooked spinach, crumbled feta cheese, chopped fresh dill, chopped fresh parsley, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Lay the phyllo pastry sheet flat on a clean surface. Cut it in half widthwise to create two rectangles.
Place half of the spinach mixture along one edge of each rectangle.
Fold the sides of the pastry over the filling, then roll it up tightly to form a log shape.
Place the spanakopita rolls on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a pot of boiling water, cook the diced potato, green peas, and carrot until tender, about 5-7 minutes. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, cook the chopped onion until softened.
Add the cooked potato, green peas, and carrot to the skillet with the onion.
Stir in the ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Mix well to combine.
Cut each phyllo pastry sheet in half lengthwise to create four strips.
Place a spoonful of the vegetable mixture at one end of each strip of phyllo pastry.
Fold one corner of the phyllo pastry over the filling to form a triangle. Continue folding the pastry in a triangle shape until you reach the end of the strip.
Repeat the process with the remaining vegetable mixture and phyllo pastry strips.
Place the samosas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the samosas with olive oil.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the samosas are golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
pink salmon: Place the mashed potato in a bowl.
Flake the salmon into the potatoes, add the parsley and lemon zest and season with salt and freshly ground pepper.
Mix well. Shape the mixture into 8 small cakes with damp hands. Dust in flour.
You can make the fishcakes in advance and chill at this stage until ready to cook.
Heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan and fry the fish cakes for 4-5 mins on each side over a medium heat until cooked through.
white onion: In a small bowl, mix together the coconut aminos, honey, vinegar, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic powder.
Place the marinade and chicken into a large Ziploc bag and toss to coat. Marinate.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat. Place the marinated chicken, onion, and bell pepper alternately onto the skewers.
Grill the kabobs for approximately 10 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken reaches desired doneness
Heat a skillet and add 2 tbsp olive oil and tofu. Cook tofu for 5 minutes, until it is crispy and golden. When cooked, transfer on a plate.
Heat the remaining oil in the wok and add the onion and mushrooms. Stir fry for 3 minutes then add the broccoli and carefully pour in ¼ cup water.
Pour in the teriyaki sauce and stir fry for another 5 minutes. Garnish with sesame seeds and serve.
Slice top and bottom ends off oranges; cut off peel and white pith, following the shape of the fruit.
Cut orange segments from the membrane to remove and roughly chop.
Place in a medium bowl. Discard peel, pith, and membrane.
Stir in onion, cilantro, chile, salt, and 1 tbsp lime juice.
Sprinkle both sides of snapper evenly with Cajun seasoning.
Heat oil in a large nonstick or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the snapper; cook until golden brown and cooked through, 3 to 4 minutes per side.
Transfer to a plate and flake into large pieces using 2 forks. Sprinkle with the remaining 1 tbsp lime juice.
Divide the snapper and cabbage evenly among warm tortillas. Top with the orange salsa.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
Place the mango, peach, pineapple, onion, red pepper, yellow pepper, and cilantro in a mixing bowl.
Stir in the garlic, jalapeno, lime juice, salt, sugar, and water. Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving.
Gather the ingredients.
Slice tofu into 1- to 1 1/2-inch cubes or rectangles and add to the hoisin, gently turning to saturate. Set in the refrigerator.
Combine stir-fry sauce ingredients in a cup and set aside. Distribute flour over a large dinner plate.
Remove tofu from refrigerator and gently turn each piece in the flour. Let tofu remain sitting in the flour.
Heat a small to medium frying pan over high heat. Add olive oil. When oil is hot, add the floured tofu cubes.
Fry 1 minute, then turn tofu and fry 1 more minute or until medium-browned on both sides.
Remove tofu from oil and set on paper towel to drain. Reduce heat to medium-low and continue frying until all tofu is cooked.
Reserve fried tofu on a sheet pan in a warm oven. Heat a wok or large frying pan over high heat.
Add 1 tablespoon oil plus onion and garlic, and stir-fry 1 minute. Add 2 tablespoons of stir-fry sauce. Stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add broccoli.
Stir-fry 2 minutes, adding more of the stir-fry sauce as you go.
Add red pepper, and remaining stir-fry sauce, frying everything together until vegetables are cooked but still retain some crispness (2 to 3 minutes).
Remove from heat and taste-test. If too salty or sweet for your taste, add a little lime or lemon juice.
Add more soy sauce if not salty or flavorful enough. Some fresh-cut chili or chili flakes can be added for more spice.
To serve, transfer vegetables onto a serving plate or individual plates or bowls.
Tip the pan to drizzle remaining sauce over, then top with the warm tofu. Serve with and egg noodles.
In a skillet or wok heat the olive oil and add the chicken. Toss for 5 minutes until thoroughly cooked.
Add the water and onions and mix together. Cover and cook the wok for 3 minutes.
Add the mushroom, broccoli, tomato puree and stir well. Add the salt, pepper, oregano, red chili powder and stir for 3 minutes.
Cook for 5 minutes or until the broccoli is soft. Serve warm.
Rinse the chickpeas and soak for 8 hours or overnight in enough water to cover.

Drain and rinse, then transfer to a medium saucepan and cover with fresh water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until the chickpeas are tender. Drain and transfer to a large bowl.
Meanwhile, rinse the lentils and transfer to a medium saucepan. Cover with 4 cups of water, then bring to a boil.
Drain and add to the bowl of chickpeas. Add the onion, red pepper and dried tomatoes, and pour with olive oil, vinegar and salt and pepper.
Toss until everything is evenly coated with the dressing. Now add the fresh parsley and toss again.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to the boil. Add the rice and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool.
Heat ½ tsp of the sesame oil in a medium nonstick skillet over medium-high. Add scallion whites and diced onion and cook, stirring, until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add sliced carrot and cook about 5 min. Add the rice in an even layer. Cook, without stirring, 2 to 3 minutes, or until the bottom becomes slightly crispy.
With a spoon or spatula, push the rice to one side of the skillet. Crack the egg onto the other side. Cook, constantly stirring the egg, 30 to 60 seconds or until cooked through.
Mix the rice and egg to thoroughly combine. Stir in the soy sauce and sesame oil. Gently fold in the reserved salmon and toss.
Melt butter in a small skillet. Add breadcrumbs and cook them until lightly browned, about 3-5 minutes.
Serve immediately, and garnished with scallion greens and bread crumbs.
Place your steamer rack in a wok on high heat.
Pour enough water so that the top of the rack isn't submerged, and start boiling water.
Take the rack out, separate and lay out noodles (12 oz) on top of the rack.
Once boiling, set the steamer rack + noodles back in the wok and cover for 10 minutes. Leave the stove on high heat.
If you're using a steamer rack without holes (i.e. steaming on a plate where the steam can't easily access the bottom, steam for 2-3 extra minutes.)
Once the 10 minutes is up, quickly dump the noodles in the water for 15-30 seconds.
Drain noodles through a colander, and spend about 1-2 minutes fluffing and separating the noodles with a chopstick.
Let it cool for 3-5 minutes.
We'll wash and chop our vegetables:
Mix dark soy sauce (1 tbsp), light soy sauce (1 tbsp), oyster sauce (1 tbsp), sugar (2 tsp), water (1 tbsp) in a bowl until the sugar dissolves.
We'll be adding the sesame oil later.
We'll start by heating our wok to around 350-400°F.
Depending on your stove, this should take around 2-3 minutes.
.Make room for the bean sprouts by pushing the noodles aside in the wok
Set the bean sprouts in the wok, and then cover them with the noodles.
This helps trap some of the heat inside to help them cook more evenly and quickly.
Let the bean sprouts cook underneath the noodles for 30-45 seconds.
Add the green onions (everything, the cooked roots + raw shoots) and onions, and cook for about 1-2 minutes.
Continue separating the noodles with chopsticks and a spatula.
Pour in the sauce mix, and mix everything around for 1 minute.
We'll want to let the sauce evaporate as much as we can.
Add sesame oil (1 tsp), and mix and separate the noodles for another 1-2 minutes, and then we can start plating.
Transfer the noodles onto a plate, and call your loved ones over.
Time to eat!
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions of the packaging.
Remove the thick layer of fat running down the side of the steaks and discard.
Slice the steak into 1cm-thick, long strips and toss with the cornflour and some seasoning.
Heat the oil in a large frying pan or wok over a high heat then add the steak, frying for 3-5 mins or until golden brown on the outside.
Remove with a slotted spoon and transfer to a plate.
Add the onion and peppers to the pan and fry for 6-7 mins or until beginning to soften.
Stir through the garlic, ginger and chilli and cook for a further min.
Return the beef to the pan and stir through the black bean sauce, rice wine vinegar and 2 tbsp water to loosen a little.
Bring to a simmer and then remove from the heat.
Serve the stir-fry in deep bowls with mounds of sticky rice and top with coriander.
In a small bowl whisk together gochujang, green onions, soy sauce, rice vinegar, garlic, ginger and sesame oil.
Pour half of mixture into a large ziplock bag.
Add steak to bag, seal, and chill for 30 minutes or up to overnight.
Cover remaining sauce and chill until ready to use.
Drizzle oil into a large pan or skillet and bring to medium-high heat.
Use tongs to transfer steak from bag to pan (keep marinade in the bag) and cook for 5-8 minutes on each side until well-browned on the outside.
Transfer steak to a large cutting board and allow to rest while you move on to the next step.
Add onions, mushrooms, ramen noodles, ramen seasoning, and beef marinade from the bag to the pan.
Pour water over the top and bring to a simmer. Allow to cook for 5-7 minutes until noodles tender and cooked through.
Thinly slice steak across the grain.
Add steak strips and reserved sauce to the pan and stir everything to combine.
Cook for a few minutes just until steak is hot throughout, garnish with green onions if desired and serve immediately.
Toss all the ingredients in a large mixing bowl, season with salt and pepper to taste, and serve.
Heat olive oil in a pot. Add chicken and sausage and cook 5 minutes on each side. When sausage is cool to handle, slice them.
Saute vegetables, add garlic, 1 cup water and chopped tomatoes. Season with thyme, paprika, chili flakes, salt and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook 15 minutes more. Serve in bowls.
In a pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add onion, saute for 3-4 minutes, add mushrooms and continue saute until softened.
Next, add the spinach. Once it wilts, add the eggs and cheese. Scrape the mixture toward the center of the pan as the eggs start to cook.
When there is hardly any liquid left, cover the pan for 60 seconds. Remove the cover, fold the eggs in half, and press down to release any liquid.
Flip the omelet over, press down again, transfer to a serving plate and enjoy.
Heat the oven to 450°F.
Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and spread half of a bag of tortilla chips in an even layer.
Sprinkle half of the cheddar cheese over the chips. Repeat for another layer. Bake until the cheese is melted and bubbly, about 5 minutes
Meanwhile, add the avocado to a small mixing bowl. Season using salt and pepper to taste, add olive oil, 1/2 of the lime juice, and mash with a fork.
Prepare the pico de gallo by mixing the diced tomatoes, cilantro, lime juice, white onion, and jalapeno in a separate mixing bowl. Set aside.
Remove from the oven and evenly top with warm corn, guacamole, and pico de gallo.
Combine golden raisins and red wine vinegar in a bowl.
Heat olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high.
Cut 8 oz. eggplant into 3/4-inch cubes.
Add chopped red bell pepper, chopped white onion, and eggplant cubes to skillet.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, 10 to 12 minutes.
Stir in chopped tomato, chopped drained capers, crushed red pepper, and raisin mixture.
Simmer until slightly thickened, about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle with chopped fresh mint.
Serve with garlic bread.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook couscous according to package instructions. Mix in the parsley and season with salt and pepper.
Put onion, tomato puree and lemon juice into a blender. Blend until smooth and season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
In a large bowl, gently mix together the salmon, green onion, garlic, dill, spinach, flour, egg, paprika, salt and pepper.
Form ½ inch thick patties. Heat olive oil over medium high heat in a large skillet. Cook the patties until nicely browned on both sides, about 3 minutes per side.
Serve the patties along with tomato dip and couscous.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil and add rice. Cook according to instructions and set aside.
Bring water to boil in a saucepan. Throw in spinach, gently stir to ensure its well soaked in water. Cook for about a minute, strain and let cool.
In a medium saute pan, heat oil. Add in onion and let cook until translucent and starts to turn slightly brown.
Add in garlic, spinach and let cook for 5 minutes, then mix with sauce. Let cook on medium heat for about 10 minutes.
Rinse and pad dry salmon with paper towel and season with salt. Heat stove top grill, drizzle olive oil and sear skinless side of salmon for 3 minutes. Flip and cook for another 3 minutes.
Serve on spinach stew with rice.
Bring a pot of water to the boil and cook the rice to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat a skillet on medium heat and add about a tbsp of olive oil, add veggies and saute for 2-3 minutes.
Add chicken, soy sauce and lemon juice, stir fry for 10-13 minutes or until chicken is completely cooked.
Serve the stir fry hot over rice.
Place the potatoes in a pot of cold water and bring to the boil. Boil until nice and tender, drain and set aside to cool.
In a large mixing bowl, add canned tuna with its olive oil, onion, cucumber and cherry tomatoes, toss well.
Cut the cooled potatoes into small cubes and add them to the mixing bowl. Add balsamic vinegar, season with salt and pepper, and mix well.
Serve slightly chilled with wedges of lemon.
tamarind paste: Preheat your oven to 400°F.
In a bowl, toss the diced sweet potato with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Spread the seasoned sweet potato on a baking sheet in a single layer.

Roast the sweet potato in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until it's tender and slightly caramelized. Stir or flip the sweet potato halfway through for even cooking. Once done, remove it from the oven and let it cool.

While the sweet potato is roasting, prepare the tamarind dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together 1 tablespoon of tamarind paste, 1 tablespoon of maple syrup, 1 tablespoon of olive oil, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
In a serving bowl, place 2 cups of chopped kale.
Once the roasted sweet potato has cooled slightly, add it to the bowl with the kale.
Drizzle the tamarind dressing over the sweet potato and kale.
Toss all the ingredients together until the sweet potato and kale are coated with the dressing.
Toast the peanuts in a dry pan for just 2 minutes, until fragrant.
In a stone bowl, combine the fish sauce, chili, garlic, lime juice, and tamarind and use the pestle to pound them enough to mash them into a paste.
Add papaya and beans and mix well. Then add the tomatoes, peanuts, and mix.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced onions and cook for about 2-3 minutes, or until they become translucent.
Add ground beef and cook until it's browned and crumbled.
Stir in the chopped dried apricots, cooked rice, ground cinnamon, salt, and pepper. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes to combine the flavors.
While the filling is cooking, carefully remove the central stem from the Swiss chard leaves, creating a V-shape.
Place the Swiss chard leaves on a work surface and add half of the beef and apricot filling to each leaf.
Fold the sides of the leaves over the filling and roll them up, creating a dolma.
Place the dolmas seam side down in a baking dish.
In a small bowl, mix the tamarind paste with beef or vegetable broth to create a tamarind sauce.
Pour the tamarind sauce over the dolmas in the baking dish.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the dolmas are tender.
Serve your Beef Dolmas with Apricots and Tamarind as a flavorful and satisfying meal.
Blend all spice paste ingredients with 2 tablespoons water until smooth.
Place asam jawa in a small metal strainer over a bowl. Pour 1 cup hot water over asam jawa.
Stir and press down asam jawa with a spoon to dissolve. Pour spice paste into a medium clay pot or non reactive pot.
Pour strained tamarind juice into the pot. Stir to combine. Add crushed lemongrass, daun kesum, salt to taste, and pineapple.
Again stir to combine. Place lid on the pot and allow sauce to come to a boil.
Reduce heat and continue to simmer for 10 minutes. Remove the lid. Do a taste test.
Add okra and fish. Place the lid back onto the pot. Continue to cook for another 3 to 5 minutes or until okra is tender.
Turn off stove and serve warm with steamed rice.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
cauliflower florets: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Steam or boil the cauliflower florets until they are tender but still firm.
In a mixing bowl, combine the cooked cauliflower, plain Greek yogurt, grated Parmesan cheese, everything bagel seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Mix all the ingredients together until the cauliflower is evenly coated.
Transfer the mixture into a small baking dish or oven-safe dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until the top is golden brown and the cauliflower is cooked through.
Once done, let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
In a bowl, toss the finely chopped cauliflower florets with olive oil, chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, lime juice, salt, and pepper until well coated.
Spread the seasoned cauliflower on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Roast in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes until the cauliflower is tender and slightly browned.
Remove the cauliflower from the oven and set it aside.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Place one tortilla in the skillet and sprinkle half of the shredded cheese on top of the tortilla.
Add the roasted chile-lime cauliflower on top of the cheese.
Sprinkle the remaining cheese over the cauliflower and cover it with the second tortilla.
Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side until the tortilla turns golden brown and the cheese melts.
Once cooked, remove the quesadilla from the skillet and let it cool for a minute before slicing.
Heat olive oil in a small pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened.
Add diced potato, chopped cauliflower florets, and vegetable broth to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until the vegetables are tender.
Use an immersion blender or transfer the soup to a blender and blend until smooth.
Stir in milk and heat until warmed through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the cauliflower and potato soup hot,garnished with chopped parsley.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Combine the cauliflower rice, egg, onion, pea protein powder, almond flour, mozzarella, chives, and garlic powder.
Season with a generous pinch of salt and several turns of freshly ground pepper, and stir it all up.
Form the mixture into tots and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Space them evenly. Bake 15 minutes.
In a small bowl mix together yogurt, lime juice, salt, and pepper. Serve with tots.
Pulse cauliflower florets in a food processor until chopped into rice-sized pieces, about 10 seconds.
Transfer cauliflower 'rice' to a large bowl; add lemon juice, olive oil, and garlic.
Add tomatoes, scallions, mint, parsley, salt, pomegranate seeds and pepper and mix well.
Heat olive oil in a saucepan over medium-high heat, add onion and garlic and stir occasionally until tender, 5 minutes.
Add ginger, cook until fragrant, 2 minutes, add spices (except garam masala) and stir until fragrant.
Add stock, tomato, cover and simmer until starting to become tender, 5 minutes.
Add cauliflower, cover and simmer until tender, 5 minutes.
Stir in garam marsala, and serve.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil over high heat. Add the cauliflower florets to the water and cook for 10 minutes.
Drain the cauliflower well and then turn it out onto paper towels to dry completely.
In the same pot, add the cream cheese and garlic.  Saute the garlic until very fragrant, softened, but not browned.
In the bowl of a food processor, combine the cauliflower, cream cheese, and garlic mixture, Parmesan cheese, salt, and pepper.

Pulse until the mixture is smooth and fluffy.
Spoon the mixture into a serving bowl.

Stir in the sour cream and sprinkle the top of the cauliflower with chives and a spoon of butter.
Serve immediately.
Heat up oil in a large pan over medium heat. Sauté the onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger. Saute for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, and red pepper flakes. Cook for  1 minute.
Add in the tomatoes together with their juices and cook for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the tomatoes have softened and broken down.
Add the broth, potatoes, and salt to the pan. Stir well to combine, then turn up the heat and bring to a high simmer. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add in the cauliflower and stir well to combine, cook for 10 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Remove from heat and stir in the lime juice and sprinkle with chopped spinach.
In a heavy bottom pan, heat the butter over medium heat, add the onion and saute for 5 minutes. Add the garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Add the cauliflower, thyme, salt and pepper, and 2 cups water.
Bring the chunky soup to a boil, cover, reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes, or until cauliflower is fork tender.
Stir in the milk and lemon juice. Once soup is done, blend until desired consistency.
Season and serve into bowls, and garnish with some florets.
In a medium saucepan, combine the cauliflower and broth and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to a simmer, cover with a lid, and cook for 15 minutes.
Use a slotted spoon to transfer the cauliflower to a food processor and puree until silky smooth.
Transfer to a bowl and stir in the Parmesan and chopped chives and season with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Serve warm, topped with butter.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
In a mixing bowl, combine the chopped cauliflower, grated Parmesan cheese, almond flour (or breadcrumbs), egg, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Shape the cauliflower mixture into small tots or cylinders.
Lightly spray the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Place the cauliflower tots in the air fryer basket in a single layer, making sure they are not touching each other.
Air fry at 375°F for 10-12 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through, or until the tots are golden brown and crispy.
Once cooked, remove the cauliflower tots from the air fryer and serve hot.
Preheat your grill to medium-high heat, around 375-400°F.
Season the chicken breast with ground cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper on both sides.
Grill the chicken breast for about 5-6 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 165°F and is cooked through. Set it aside to rest.
While the chicken is grilling, prepare the cauliflower "rice." Place the cauliflower florets in a food processor and pulse until they resemble rice-like grains.
In a bowl, combine the cauliflower "rice," chopped cucumber, chopped tomatoes, chopped parsley, chopped mint, olive oil, and lemon juice. Mix well to combine. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the chicken is rested, slice it.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In an oven-safe skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the cauliflower florets, sliced mushrooms, and chopped kale to the skillet. Sauté for about 5 minutes until they begin to soften and the kale wilts slightly. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a bowl, beat the two eggs and then pour them over the sautéed vegetables in the skillet.
Sprinkle the grated Parmesan cheese evenly over the eggs and vegetables.
Let the frittata cook on the stovetop for 2-3 minutes without stirring.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for about 10-12 minutes, or until the frittata is set and slightly golden on top.
Carefully remove the skillet from the oven (remember, the handle will be hot), and let it cool for a minute.
Slide the frittata out of the skillet onto a plate.
In a large frying pan, heat 2 tbsp of the oil over moderate heat. Add the bread crumbs and cook, stirring, until golden, about 5 minutes. Remove.
Heat remaining oil, add garlic and parsley. Cook for 1 minute.
In a large pot of boiling, salted water, cook the tagliatelle until almost done, about 8 minutes.
Add the cauliflower and bring it back to a boil. Cook until the cauliflower and pasta are just done, about 5 minutes longer. Drain.
Toss with the garlic mixture, salt, bread crumbs, walnuts, and Parmesan. Top with additional Parmesan.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and cook, stirring, for 3 minutes or until onion softens.
Add curry paste and cook, stirring, for 1 minute or until fragrant.
Add the cauliflower, stock and water and bring to the boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes.
Transfer half the cauliflower mixture to a clean saucepan. Blend the remaining cauliflower mixture, until smooth.
Add to saucepan with unblended cauliflower mixture. Add coconut cream and stir over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat, garnish with parsley and season with salt .
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Heat a large oven-safe saute pan over medium-high heat. Add 1 tbsp of oil to the pan, followed by the sausage.
Add onion and garlic to the pan, season with salt and pepper, and saute for 3 minutes or until onions are translucent and meat is well-browned.
Increase heat slightly. Add the remaining oil to the pan.. Add cauliflower to pan, and season with salt and pepper. Cook for 10 minutes.
Garnish with green onions, sesame seeds, and serve.
Preheat oven to 400°F.

Cook cauliflower in boiling, salted water for 10 minutes.  
Drain and allow to cool slightly.
In a medium bowl beat eggs well. Add yogurt, milk, and oregano. Mix well.

Arrange cauliflower in a lightly oiled baking dish.
Pour egg mixture over the top and sprinkle with cheddar.
Bake for 20 minutes, and serve.
Preheat your oven to 425°F.
Place cauliflower florets into bowl. Add the oil, salt, pepper, and garlic powder and mix well.
Place on a cookie sheet and roast for 20 minutes, then turn the cauliflower over and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Boil cauliflower florets over high heat for 5 minutes, or until just tender. Drain and place into baking dish. Add uncooked grated cauliflower.
Combine eggs and milk in a large bowl and beat with a fork until smooth. Add cheddar cheese, garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper.
Pour egg mixture over cauliflower. Top with reserved cheese. Bake for 25 minutes.
Position a rack in upper third of oven; preheat broiler to high.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium heat. Add onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until starting to brown, 3 minutes.
Add cauliflower and water. Cover and cook until just tender, about 5 minutes.
Add kale, garlic, thyme and 1/4 teaspoon each salt and pepper; cook, stirring often, until the kale is wilted, 3 minutes.
Whisk eggs, paprika, and the remaining salt and pepper in a large bowl. Add the vegetables to the egg mixture and gently stir to combine.
Wipe the pan clean; add the remaining 1 tablespoon oil and heat over medium heat. Pour in the egg mixture and top with cheese.
Cover and cook until the edges are set and the bottom is brown, 5 minutes.
Transfer the pan to the oven and broil until the top of the frittata is just cooked, 3 minutes.
iceberg lettuce: Heat a non-stick pan on medium heat. Add the bacon and cook until the fat has rendered out and the bacon is nice and crispy. Transfer the bacon aside, then chop it roughly.
Add the chicken to the same pan, utilizing the bacon fat, season well with salt and pepper. Cook the chicken for 5-6 minutes, turning frequently, or until it's cooked through. Take off the heat.
To a mixing bowl, add the lettuce, halved cherry tomatoes, chopped bacon and the chicken meat. Add the Caesar dressing and toss well to combine. Serve immediately.
Drain water from the canned tuna and add to a bowl. Use only flaked tuna in water.
Add 3-4 tbsp mayonnaise. You can add more or less according to preference.
Stir the tuna well, so that it becomes easily spreadable.
Add the mustard and minced shallots.
Use a fork to mix and stir the tuna to get it more smooth and spreadable.
Once the texture is to your liking, add in the freshly chopped dill, salt, pepper and paprika.
Stir till thoroughly incorporated.
Chill the tuna spread for at least 30 minutes in the fridge for best results. The filling tastes better chilled.
Apply a thin layer of mayo on each slice of Un-toasted bread.
Lay  the lettuce and sliced tomato, and enjoy.
Cook and stir ground chicken in a large oiled skillet for about 10 minutes. Drain excess grease.
Whisk soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, ginger,  hot pepper sauce, and sesame oil in a bowl and pour over chicken.
Cook and stir chicken mixture with sauce over low heat until flavors have blended, 5 to 10 minutes.
Form lettuce leaves into cup shapes and fill with chicken mixture. Top lettuce wraps with shredded carrots and sprinkle with green onion.
Slice 8 large rounds from the edges of the head of iceberg to create buns.
In a large skillet over medium heat oil, cook bacon until crispy. Transfer bacon to a paper towel-lined plate to drain, reserving bacon fat in pan.
Add onion slices and cook until tender, about 3 minutes per side. Set aside red onions and wipe skillet clean.
Return skillet over medium-high heat. Shape ground beef into 4 large burger patties. Season both sides of patties with salt and pepper and add to skillet.

Cook until seared on both sides and cooked to your liking, about 4 minutes per side for medium. Top each burger with a slice of cheese, then cover skillet with a lid and cook until cheese has melted, about 1 minute more.

To build each burger, top one iceberg round with the cooked cheeseburger, jalapenos, and a tomato slice, then spread tomato sauce. Top with second iceberg round. Repeat with remaining ingredients and serve.
Toss all the salad vegetables together.
Put the tikka pieces into a bowl and microwave on high for to heat through.
Heat the naan bread in the microwave on medium for 1 min until puffed up.
Spread each piece of naan with 2 tbsp yogurt and then fill with salad and warm chicken tikka masala.
Enjoy warm.
Wash lettuce, dry and cut into thin shreds. Slice tomatoes into thick slices.
Cook bacon until crisp, drain on paper towel and set aside.
Toast bread until lightly browned, butter if desired and set aside.
Spread each slice of bread with 1 tbsp mayonnaise. Top with bacon, tomatoes and lettuce. . Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cut in half and serve.
Mix together the cream cheese and Dijon mustard until smooth.
Spread each tortilla with about 2 tablespoons of the cream cheese mixture, spreading to within 1/4 inch of the edge of the tortillas.
Arrange about 1/4 cup of shredded lettuce on each tortilla, and press the lettuce down into the cream cheese mixture.
Place 2 turkey slices per tortilla over the lettuce, and sprinkle with 2 tablespoons of shredded Swiss cheese.
Top each tortilla evenly with tomato, avocado slices, and crumbled bacon
Roll each tortilla up tightly, and cut in half across the middle with a slightly diagonal cut.
Start by preparing the sauce.
In a small bowl combine the mayo, sugar-free ketchup, 1 tsp of mustard, diced pickles, pickle juice, and spices.
Flavors do meld better together over time so feel free to adjust.
To prep, the hamburger patties, measure out 2 oz. of meat per patty, and roll into a meatball.
Repeat so you have 10 total meatballs.
Season the tops with sea salt and cracked black pepper.
Preheat your cast iron/ griddle to high heat.
Add a little oil to the pan if necessary.
Place two meatballs onto the griddle or pan, use a wide spatula and press down.
I like to use a potato masher to help me press and smash the burgers as flat as possible.
Baste the top (optional) in mustard before flipping.
Work quickly.
As the edges appear to brown, flip them.
Place one piece of American cheese on one burger patty and stack the second patty over it.
To assemble the patty I start with a bottom piece of lettuce (my bun). add the slice of onion, the double stacked burger patty, tomato, pickles, and the sauce.
Cover with the second lettuce bun and dig in!
Place hominy in a large pot, cover with water. Add 1 head garlic and salt to taste. Cook over medium heat for 2 hours.
Place pork shoulder and pork loin in the hominy mixture and cook until meat is tender and cooked through, about 1 hour.
Place tomato and guajillo chiles in a pot and add enough water to cover, bring to a boil. Cook until chiles have softened, 15 to 20 minutes. Drain.
Place tomato and chiles with salt, 1 clove garlic, oregano, and cumin in a blender, add 2 cups water.
Blend until smooth. Strain mixture through a fine-mesh sieve and set chile sauce aside.
Transfer pork to a work surface and shred with 2 forks. Discard the pork bones.
Pour chile sauce into hominy mixture, bring to a boil. Return shredded pork to pot. Simmer pozole until flavors have blended, about 3 minutes.
Ladle pozole into serving bowls and top with lettuce, radish and onion and serve lime wedge on the side.
pineapple juice: Blend pineapple juice, spinach, pear, apple, avocado, and broccoli florets together in a blender until smooth.
Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and rinse with cold water.
Add ham, cheese, pineapple chunks, and onion to a large bowl.
In another small bowl, whisk the garlic powder, vinegar, salt, pepper, Greek yogurt, and pineapple juice until creamy.
Pour the dressing over the salad, and mix to combine.
Add pineapple, banana, and pineapple juice into the container of a blender. Puree on high until smooth.
Prepare the sauce by adding the coconut sugar, vinegar, coconut aminos, sesame, pineapple juice, and chicken stock to a medium saucepan. Stir and bring to a boil.
Add chicken pieces and beaten egg to a large ziplock bag. Seal and shake to coat chicken.
Then add the arrowroot starch to the bag, shaking again to lightly coat all the chicken pieces.
Add oil to a large non-skillet. Add the coated chicken. Fry over medium heat, a couple of minutes on each side until the coating begins to crisp.
Add the sauce to the chicken. Cover and reduce the heat down to a simmer and allow the juices to soak into the chicken.
Place all the sauce ingredients apart from the cornflour and water in a saucepan on medium heat. Whisk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Mix the cornflour and water in a bowl then add the slurry to the sauce, continuing to whisk it all together until it is thick.
Place the tofu chunks into a bowl with the cornflour, garlic, ginger, salt and pepper. Toss well so all the edges of the tofu is fully coated in the flour.
Add the tofu and fry for around 10 minutes, flipping occasionally, until golden brown and crispy.
Pour over the majority of the sauce, stir the tofu and leave to heat through for a minute.
In a bowl, whisk together pineapple juice, soy sauce, rice vinegar, minced garlic, grated ginger, and black pepper.
Place the chicken in a shallow dish or resealable plastic bag. Pour the marinade over the chicken, ensuring it is evenly coated. Let marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Remove the chicken breast from the marinade and discard any excess marinade.
Grill the chicken breast for about 6-8 minutes per side, or until cooked through and juices run clear.
Once cooked, remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanshile saute chicken in a large skillet over medium high heat until cooked through.
In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, garlic powder, pineapple juice, orange juice, pecans and pepper.
Pour over chicken. Let simmer for 10 to 15 minutes, or until sauce is thickened to taste. Garnish with parsley.
Combine strawberries, apples, yogurt, pineapple juice, orange juice, and milk in a blender; blend until smooth.
Cook chicken in an oiled skillet for 7 to 8 minutes. Meanwhile cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a large saucepan over medium-low heat, blend reserved pineapple juice, soy sauce, vinegar, and honey.
Stir broccoli, red chili into the saucepan. Mix in chicken. Cook and stir until heated through. Sprinkle with chives.
Add ice cubes, pineapple, mango, strawberries, banana slices, yogurt, pineapple juice, orange juice, and coconut milk to a blender.
Cover and process on medium speed for about 30 seconds.
Increase to high speed for 15 to 30 seconds until thick and smooth.
Adjust with more coconut milk, pineapple, or orange juice as needed to reach the desired consistency. Serve.
dill pickles: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Lay the slice of beef flat on a cutting board. Season with salt and pepper.
Place the slice of bacon on top of the beef.
Sprinkle finely chopped onion over the bacon.
Place strips of dill pickle on top of the onion.
Roll up the beef tightly, enclosing the filling inside. Secure with toothpicks if needed.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the rolled-up beef to the skillet and cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side or until browned.
Pour beef broth into the skillet, cover, and let it simmer for about 45 minutes to 1 hour or until the beef is tender.
While the beef is baking, wash the potato and poke it a few times with a fork. Rub olive oil over the potato and sprinkle with salt.
Place the potato directly on the oven rack and bake for about 45 minutes to 1 hour, or until tender when pierced with a fork.
Serve the cooked beef rouladen with the baked potato.
Garnish with chopped parsley if desired.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Grease a baking dish with ghee.
Cook ground beef and onion in a large, nonstick skillet over medium heat until beef is browned and onions are soft and translucent, 5 to 10 minutes.
Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper. Stir in 1/2 cup Cheddar cheese until well combined.
Remove from heat. Add ketchup, mayonnaise, pickles, and mustard. Transfer mixture to buttered baking dish and top with remaining 1/2 cup Cheddar cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted, 7 to 10 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a large skillet over medium heat, cook beef and 1 cup onion, crumbling meat, until beef is no longer pink and onion is tender, 6-8 minutes; crumble meat; drain.
Stir in tomato sauce, Velveeta, mustard and Worcestershire sauce until cheese is melted, 4-6 minutes.
Transfer to a greased 13 x 9 inches or 3 ½ quarts baking dish.
Sprinkle with cheddar cheese and bacon.
Top with Tater Tots.
Bake, uncovered, 35-40 minutes or until bubbly.
Top with tomatoes, pickles and remaining onion.
In small bowl, mix mustard and mayonnaise. Open the sliced bread, spread the mixture on both pieces.
Add the lettuce, meats and tomatoes, close the sandwich with the second half of bread and enjoy.
Place the diced potato in a small saucepan and cover with cold water. Add a pinch of salt.
Bring the water to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Cook the potato for about 10-12 minutes, or until tender but not mushy. Drain and let cool.
In a mixing bowl, combine mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, white vinegar, sugar, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Add the cooled potato, egg, onion, celery, dill pickles and bell pepper to the bowl. Gently stir to coat the ingredients with the dressing.
Taste and adjust the seasoning with additional salt, pepper, or other seasonings as desired.
Refrigerate the potato salad for at least 30 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld.
Place 4 eggs, milk, water, butter, salt and flour in a blender and mix until well combined. Place in the refrigerator and chill for at least 20 minutes.

Place the remaining 3 eggs in cold water, bring them to the boil, take off the heat and leave in hot water for 13 minutes. Afterwards run under cold water to cool. Grate the eggs using a cheese grater, set aside.
Meanwhile, heat the olive oil in a non-stick pan on medium heat. Add the onion, saute for 3 minutes, add the port, season with salt and pepper and cook 5-8 minutes, or until the pork is cooked.
Heat some butter in a nonstick skillet on medium heat. Pour about ¼ cup of batter into the hot skillet and tilt the skillet until the batter spreads evenly.
Cook for about a minute, until slightly golden, flip the crepe over and cook for another 30 seconds on the second side. Set aside and cover with a damp towel.
Take a pancake, spread a tbsp of sour cream on it, add pork meat, some grated egg and a few slices of pickle, then roll tightly. Enjoy warm or cold.
Preheat a sandwich press or a skillet over medium heat.
Slice the Cuban bread or French bread in half horizontally.
Spread mustard on one side of each bread slice.
Layer the roasted pork, ham, Swiss cheese, and dill pickle slices on one bread slice.
Top with the other bread slice to form a sandwich.
Lightly brush the outside of the sandwich with olive oil.
Place the sandwich in the preheated sandwich press or skillet and cook until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is melted, about 3-5 minutes per side.
Remove from the heat and let it cool for a minute before slicing.
Serve the Classic Cuban Midnight Sandwich hot.
Heat a large soup pot over medium-high heat and add bacon. Cook until crisp, about 10 minutes. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate. Set aside to cool.
Add beef to the soup pot and flatten with a spatula. Cook until bottom is browned, about 5 minutes. Flip and cook other side, about 5 minutes more.
Add broth, Cheddar cheese, cream cheese, pickles, tomatoes, steak sauce, mustard, onion powder, chili powder, and black pepper.
Mix well to combine, breaking meat into smaller pieces. Reduce heat to low, cook and stir until cream cheese is melted
Cover and cook soup for 25 minutes more.
Ladle soup into individual bowls. Divide lettuce between bowls and fold into soup. Crumble cooked bacon and top with parsley.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Place bread slices in the oven to toast while it preheats, and while you make the tuna salad.
In a small bowl, mix together the tuna, mayonnaise, salt, balsamic vinegar, mustard and dill pickle until well blended.
Remove bread from the oven, and pile the tuna mixture onto one slice. Sprinkle cheese over the other slice of bread.
Bake for 7 minutes in the preheated oven, or until cheese is melted and tuna is heated through.
Place the cheese side of the sandwich on top of the tuna side. Cut in half and serve immediately.
Mash tuna with a fork. Mix with mayonnaise, dill pickles, green onion, celery, nutritional yeast, and soy sauce.
Place diced potatoes and beets in a medium pot, cover with water, cover with a lid and boil until tender. Drain and add cooked potatoes to a mixing bowl. Cool slightly.
Add diced beets, apples, onion, pickle, and ¼ cup of water. Mash slightly with a potato masher. Consistency should be smooth with chunks.
Whip vegan cream cheese and soymilk with an electric mixer until smooth and fluffy.
Fold vegan whipped cream cheese mixture into salad and stir just until well combined. Season as desired with salt and black pepper. Serve immediately.
Place the potatoes in a pot with enough water to cover. Bring to a boil, and cook for about 10 minutes, or until easily pierced with a fork.
Drain, and transfer to a large bowl to cool.
In a medium bowl, mix the sour cream, mayonnaise, mustard, vinegar, eggs, pickle, celery, green onions, and hot sauce.
Season with dill, garlic powder, onion salt, salt, and pepper. Pour over the potatoes, and gently toss to coat. Chill at least 3 hours in the refrigerator before serving.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Add your chopped cauliflower, sprinkle with salt and pepper and drizzle with the olive oil. Bake the cauliflower for 15 minutes.
Remove from the oven and let it cool to room temperature. In a small bowl, combine the dressing ingredients and whisk until smooth.
Once the cauliflower has cooled, add it to a bowl with the chopped onion, celery, pickles, and boiled eggs. Mix through the dressing.
Sprinkle with the chopped dill and serve immediately.
Peel and dice the carrots and potatoes. Add the potatoes to a large pot of cold water and bring them to a boil. After 5 minutes add the carrots and continue to boil for 5-7 minutes.
Drain the potatoes and carrots, set aside to cool.
In a separate mixing bowl add the pickles, hard boiled eggs, ham and mayonnaise, mix well.
Add the remainder of the vegetables and canned peas, mix well to coat all the vegetables.
Serve slightly chilled, with a garnish of dill.
curry sauce: Add fish to food processor along with the chillies, garlic, spring onion, coriander, fish sauce, flaxseed oil and egg yolk and blitz to a smooth paste. Tip into a bowl.
Mix the paste together with basil, lime leaves, green beans and zest of a lime. Set aside in fridge for half an hour.
Remove from fridge and form the fish paste into little patties with wet hands.
Fry in a frying pan oiled with olive oil till golden on each side, drain on kitchen paper and serve.
Preheat your air fryer to 450°F. Remove the tofu from it’s package, rinse and pat it dry with some paper towels.
Bring a pot to medium-high heat, add 1 tbsp coconut oil. Next, add the onions and carrots, cook until the onions become semi-translucent.
Add water and bring to a boil, then reduce it to medium heat and simmer covered for 10-15 minutes or until the carrots soften.

Season tofu with salt and pepper on both sides, then lightly coat it in flour, cover it in the egg and finally, evenly coat all sides with the panko. Air fry the tofu for 3-4 minutes, then set aside to cool.
Break the curry block into pieces and dissolve them in the simmering pot, mix well. Add water to dilute if desired.
Cook your udon in boiling water for about 4-5 minutes or according to the package instructions. Scoop out the udon and place it in a large bowl, then spoon some of the curry over top.
Place a couple pieces of your tofu katsu in the bowl and add some chopped scallion, enjoy!
sesame oil: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking sheet and season with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until it flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is baking, in a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, and sesame seeds to create the dressing.
In two serving plates, divide the mixed greens and arrange the halved cherry tomatoes and sliced red bell pepper.
Once the salmon is cooked, let it cool slightly and then flake it into bite-sized pieces.
Place the flaked salmon on top of the prepared salads.
Drizzle the Asian Ginger Sesame Dressing over the salads.
Garnish each plate with slices of lime.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Prepare the tofu by draining it and cutting it into small cubes.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.

In a bowl, combine the cubed tofu with 2 tablespoons of low-sodium soy sauce, 2 tablespoons of rice vinegar, 1 tablespoon of sesame oil, and 1/2 teaspoon of grated ginger. Toss to coat the tofu evenly.
Spread the marinated tofu cubes on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Bake the tofu in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until it's lightly browned and slightly crispy.

While the tofu is baking, assemble your salad bowls. Divide 1 cup of cooked rice, 1/2 cup of thawed peas, 1/2 cup of broccoli florets, 1/2 cup of halved cherry tomatoes, and 1/2 cup of shelled edamame between two serving bowls.
When the tofu is done baking, remove it from the oven and allow it to cool slightly.
Once the tofu has cooled, distribute the baked tofu cubes evenly over the salad bowls.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small pot, combine broth, chopped kimchi, tofu, sliced mushrooms, green onion, sesame oil, gochujang, minced garlic, and minced ginger.
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat.
Add the bite-sized chicken pieces to the pot and reduce the heat to medium-low.
Simmer the stew for about 10-12 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the flavors have melded.
Remove from heat and serve the spicy Korean chicken stew hot with cooked white rice.
Cook the glass noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant.
Add carrots, spinach, onion, bell pepper, and mushrooms to the skillet. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
Add cooked noodles to the skillet.
Drizzle soy sauce and sesame oil over the noodles and vegetables. Toss everything together until well combined.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot or at room temperature.
Preheat the oven to 375°F. Oil a shallow baking dish.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Whisk soy sauce, hot water, and miso together in a large glass or ceramic bowl until thoroughly combined.
Add salmon and turn several times to coat. Marinate for 10 minutes, occasionally spooning the mixture over the fish to make sure its covered.
Transfer salmon to the prepared baking dish and cover with foil. Leave extra marinade in the bowl.
Bake in the preheated oven for 7 minutes. Uncover, spoon remaining marinade evenly onto the salmon, and bake until fish flakes easily with a fork.
Serve with rice and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Preheat an air fryer to 400°F.
Place green beans in a bowl. Whisk together sesame oil, soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, garlic, and red pepper flakes in a separate bowl.
Pour over green beans and toss to coat and let marinate for 5 minutes.
Place half the green beans and half of the lemon in the air fryer basket. Cook 12 minutes, shaking basket halfway through cooking time.
Repeat with remaining green beans and remaining lemon.
Heat sesame oil in a skillet over medium heat; cook and stir eggplant until softened, 8 to 10 minutes.
Whisk miso, soy sauce, ginger, vinegar, water, and garlic together in a bowl until sauce is evenly combined, add to eggplant mixture.
Cook and stir eggplant mixture until sauce is thickened and glossy, 3 to 4 minutes. Top mixture with sesame seeds and green onion.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Add ground beef to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon, about 4-5 minutes.
Add shredded cabbage and carrots to the skillet. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together sesame oil, soy sauce, grated ginger, and black pepper.
Pour the sauce mixture over the beef and vegetables in the skillet. Stir until everything is evenly coated.
Cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until heated through.
Meanwhile, in a separate non-stick skillet, fry the egg to your desired doneness.
Serve the cooked rice in a bowl and top with the Korean ground beef mixture.
Place the fried egg on top.
Garnish with chopped green onions and optional toppings if desired.
In a wok or skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add thinly sliced chicken to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 3-4 minutes).
Toss in mixed vegetables, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil.
Pour the sauce over the chicken and vegetables, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
In a bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, gochujang, honey, minced garlic, grated ginger, and black pepper.
Place the chicken breast in a shallow dish or resealable plastic bag. Pour the marinade over the chicken, ensuring it is evenly coated. Let marinate for at least 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
Preheat a skillet or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Once the skillet is hot, add the marinated chicken breast. Cook for about 6-8 minutes per side, or until the chicken is cooked through and caramelized on the outside.
Once cooked, remove the chicken from the skillet and transfer to a cutting board. Let it rest for a few minutes.
Bring a large pot of lightly salter water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a large pot heat sesame oil. Add pasta and toast for 2 minutes.
To serve, divide pasta between plates and top each with cherry tomatoes, spinach and sprinkle with sesame seeds. Serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey, sesame oil, minced ginger, minced garlic, red pepper flakes, and cornstarch. Set the sauce aside.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken pieces and stir-fry until they're no longer pink and are lightly browned.
Pour the sauce over the chicken and stir well.
Cook for a few more minutes until the sauce thickens and everything is heated through.
Serve the General Tso's chicken over the cooked rice.
In a small bowl, stir together the soy sauce, mirin, and sesame oil. Divide into two equal parts. Stir the rice vinegar into one part and set aside as a dipping sauce.
Spread the sesame seeds out on a plate. Coat the tuna steaks with the remaining soy sauce mixture, then press into the sesame seeds to coat.
Heat olive oil in a cast iron skillet over high heat until very hot. Place steaks in the pan, and sear for about 30 seconds on each side.
Serve immediately, garnished with lemon slices.
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F.
Place green beans in a bowl. Whisk together sesame oil, soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, garlic, and red pepper flakes in a separate bowl and pour over green beans.
Toss to coat and let marinate for 5 minutes. Place half the green beans in the air fryer basket.
Cook 12 minutes, shaking basket halfway through cooking time. Repeat with remaining green beans. Serve immediately, topped with sesame seeds.
Preheat oven to broiler settings on high heat 400°F.
Wash and rinse chicken wings and pat dry. Cut the skin off the wing drumettes, and leave it on for the wingettes.
In a shallow dish, whisk together the soy sauce, honey, sesame oil and garlic. Add the chicken and mix into the sauce to coat.
Refrigerate to marinade for at least 1 hour. Remove wings from marinade and reserve liquid.
Arrange chicken wings skin side up onto an oven tray lined with aluminum foil, and drizzle with a little extra honey.
Broil for 8-10 minutes, or until they are golden brown. Turn the wings and broil for a further 5-8 minutes, or until they are cooked through.
While the wings are in the oven, pour the reserved marinade into a small saucepan, and bring to the boil for 5 minutes.
To serve, pour honey sauce over the wings and garnish with sesame seeds. Enjoy with carrots and celery sticks.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions. Drain and rinse under cold water to cool.
In a bowl, combine the cooked pasta, thinly sliced cucumber, and thinly sliced red bell pepper.
Pour the sesame oil over the ingredients and toss to coat.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with sesame seeds.

In a bowl, combine the ground chicken, chopped shiitake mushrooms, chopped water chestnuts, chopped green onions, minced garlic, minced ginger, low-sodium soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to form the filling.
Take one dumpling or wonton wrapper and place a spoonful of the filling in the center.
Gather the sides of the wrapper up around the filling, leaving the top exposed.
Carefully press down the filling to flatten the top, so it forms a small cup shape.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Steam the Siu Mai dumplings in a steamer for about 10-12 minutes until they are cooked through. You can line the steamer with cabbage leaves or parchment paper to prevent sticking.
Serve your homemade Siu Mai hot.
In a wok or skillet, heat ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add shrimp to the wok, cooking until they turn pink and opaque (about 2-3 minutes).
Toss in broccoli florets and sliced red bell pepper, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and crushed Szechwan peppercorns.
Pour the sauce over the shrimp and vegetables, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Cook the noodles according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a bowl, mix together low-sodium soy sauce, sesame oil, and Sichuan chili paste to make the sauce. Adjust the amount of chili paste to your preferred level of spiciness.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the thinly sliced pork chop with salt and pepper.
Add the pork to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until it's cooked through and slightly browned.
Remove the cooked pork from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add minced garlic and minced ginger. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped bok choy or Chinese cabbage and sliced green onions. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are slightly wilted.
Return the cooked pork to the skillet and pour the prepared sauce over the ingredients.
Toss everything together in the skillet to coat with the sauce.
Serve the spicy Sichuan pork and vegetable mixture over the cooked noodles.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken pieces and potato cubes to the skillet. Cook until the chicken is browned and the potatoes are slightly tender, about 5-6 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, gochujang, sesame oil, rice vinegar, minced garlic, minced ginger, and water.
Pour the sauce mixture over the chicken and potatoes in the skillet.
Stir well to coat the chicken and potatoes evenly with the sauce.
Cover the skillet and let it simmer for about 10-12 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the potatoes are tender.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving plate.
Garnish with sliced green onions.
Serve the Korean spicy chicken and potato hot and enjoy!
onion powder: Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper. Line a second cookie sheet with several layers of paper towels.
Mix coconut flour, onion powder, and garlic powder in a small bowl. Whisk eggs thoroughly in a second bowl.
Grind macadamia nuts in a food processor until coarsely chopped. Empty ground nuts into a third bowl, mix in coconut until blended.
Place each shrimp in the coconut flour mixture, lightly coating on all sides.
Then dip in the egg, and finally in the macadamia-coconut mixture. Place on the parchment-lined cookie sheet, repeat with remaining shrimp.
Heat ghee in a large skillet over medium heat until melted, add shrimp. Cook, turning often, until golden brown and evenly cooked.
Drain shrimp on paper towels. Season with salt and pepper and serve.
In a bowl, combine lean ground beef or turkey with garlic powder, onion powder, dried oregano, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the seasoned ground meat and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, until browned and cooked through.
Warm the whole wheat pita bread in the microwave or oven.
Place the cooked ground meat on the warmed pita bread.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, diced cucumber, and diced red onion.
Drizzle plain Greek yogurt over the toppings.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, dried thyme, salt, and pepper.
Rub the spice mixture evenly over the chicken thighs, ensuring they are well coated.
Place the seasoned chicken thighs on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and juices run clear.
Once cooked, remove the chicken thighs from the oven and let them rest for a few minutes.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a bowl, combine the olive oil, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add the beef cubes to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the seasoning mixture.
Thread the seasoned beef cubes onto skewers, alternating with the red onion, cherry tomatoes, zucchini slices, and eggplant pieces.
Place the skewers on the preheated grill and cook for about 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until the beef is cooked to your desired doneness and the vegetables are tender.
Remove the skewers from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the beef shish kabobs hot with your favorite sides.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix together breadcrumbs, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Dredge the chicken breast in the breadcrumb mixture, pressing the breadcrumbs onto the chicken to adhere.
Place the coated chicken breast on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the coating is golden brown and crispy.
Remove from the oven and let the chicken rest for a few minutes.
Preheat your air fryer to 350°F.
Season both sides of the baby back ribs with salt, pepper, and your favorite dry rub seasoning.
Place the seasoned ribs in the air fryer basket, bone side down.
Air fry the ribs at 350°F for 25-30 minutes.
After the initial cooking time, remove the ribs from the air fryer and brush them with barbecue sauce if desired.
Return the ribs to the air fryer and continue to cook for an additional 5-10 minutes, or until the ribs are cooked through and tender.
Once cooked, remove the ribs from the air fryer and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Place cashews in a bowl and cover with water. Soak until soft, at least 4 hours but preferably overnight.
Drain and rinse cashews; place in the bowl of a food processor. Add apple cider vinegar, salt, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Blend until smooth and creamy, occasionally scraping down bowl sides with a spatula, 3 to 5 minutes.
Cook bacon in a large skillet and over medium heat, turning occasionally, until crispy, 10 to 12 minutes.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate with a slotted spoon, leaving bacon grease in the skillet. Crumble bacon when cool to the touch.
Add celery and garlic to the skillet and cook in the bacon grease until celery is tender, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add cauliflower, water, and onion powder, cover skillet to steam cauliflower. Cook, stirring every so often, until cauliflower is tender, about 5 minutes.
Add milk, 1 3/4 cups chicken broth, and hot sauce. Bring to a boil.
Meanwhile, whisk flour into the remaining chicken broth in a small bowl and drizzle into the boiling chowder.
Add Cheddar cheese and 1/2 of the crumbled bacon. Stir until smooth and cheese has melted.
Season with salt, pepper, and hot sauce. Ladle into bowls and top with remaining bacon and green onion.
Preheat your oven to 400°F. Grease a glass or ceramic baking dish.
Wash and dry the mushrooms. Use a paring knife to gently remove the stems.
In a medium bowl, use a rubber spatula to thoroughly mix the cream cheese with the salt and the spices. Mix in the gruyere.
Spoon the mixture into the mushroom caps.
Place the mushrooms in the prepared baking dish. Bake until the cheese has melted and the mushrooms are tender, 10 minutes.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
Start with a small oven-safe dish or ramekin. Place the uncooked egg noodles at the bottom of the dish.
Layer the drained tuna on top of the noodles. Sprinkle the frozen peas evenly over the tuna.
In a separate bowl, mix together the low-fat or fat-free Greek yogurt, shredded cheddar cheese, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Pour the yogurt and cheese mixture over the layers of noodles, tuna, and peas.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes or until the casserole is heated through, and the top is lightly browned and bubbly.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Bake the tortillas by placing them over two bars on the oven rack to create "hard shell" taco shells.
Mix minced onion, salt, chili powder, cumin, red pepper flakes, cayenne pepper, dried minced garlic, and oregano in a bowl.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Crumble ground beef into the hot skillet. Cook for 7 to 10 minutes. Drain and discard any excess grease.
Return ground beef to heat. Pour seasoning mixture and water over the beef and stir to combine. Bring to a simmer and cook until the moisture absorbs into the meat, about 5 minutes.
Place the beef onto the tortillas and add lettuce, tomato and cheese.
Place potatoes into a large pot and cover with salted water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until tender, about 20 minutes.
Slice potatoes and place them into a large salad bowl. Toss potatoes with onion.
Whisk olive oil, wine vinegar, lemon juice, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, black pepper, oregano, and rosemary in a bowl.
Pour the dressing over the potatoes and toss. Serve immediately or chill until serving time.
In a mixing bowl, combine ground venison, ground sage, dried thyme, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Divide the mixture into small portions and shape them into patties.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and add a little oil.
Cook the sausage patties in the skillet until browned and cooked through, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Once cooked, remove the sausage patties from the skillet and drain on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Serve hot with your favorite breakfast sides.
Preheat oven to 400°F .
Rinse salmon, and arrange in a baking dish. Sprinkle salt, pepper, onion powder, and dill over the fish and potatoes. Oil over top.
Bake in preheated oven for 20 to 25 minutes. Salmon is done when it flakes easily with a fork.
Preheat a grill to medium-high heat 375°F.
Rub tilapia fillets with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder and grill for 5 minutes on each side.
Serve with tomatoes, bell peppers, seasoned with salt, pepper, olive oil and vinegar.
Preheat oven on 375°F.
Add the spinach to a non-stick pan over medium heat and stir until wilted. Add softened cream cheese and stir until well incorporated.
Let it cool slightly and combine with Parmesan cheese, mozzarella, minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
Use a sharp knife and slice through the chicken breast, creating a pocket. Season the chicken with chili powder, onion powder salt, pepper, garlic powder.
Fill the chicken with the spinach filling and fold it, and use toothpicks to seal the cream cheese.
Place the chicken breast into a baking dish, drizzeled with oil and bake for 25 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl, mix together the grated Parmesan cheese, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Pat the chicken breast dry with paper towels.
Lightly coat the chicken breast with cooking spray or olive oil.
Sprinkle the Parmesan cheese mixture evenly over the chicken breast, pressing it gently to adhere.
Place the chicken breast on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or aluminum foil.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the coating is golden brown and crispy.
Remove the chicken from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Place ground beef, parmesan cheese, olive oil, egg, salt, and spices in a bowl and blend thoroughly. Form the mixture into meatballs, about 1 oz each.
Melt the butter in a large skillet and saute the meatballs until golden brown on all sides, about 10 minutes. Lower the heat and add the canned tomatoes.
