Let simmer for 15 minutes. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve with mozzarella cheese on top
Fry the bacon in a large pan until the desired crispiness. Drain from the fat, chop and set aside.
Mix onion powder, garlic powder in a small bowl.
In a large pot add water, chicken broth, cauliflower with a pinch of salt and ground black pepper. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Simmer, add butter and cream to the cauliflower and mix well. Then add the cheese and stir slowly until melted.
Add 2/3 of the cooked bacon and season with the mixed spices and herbs. Blend in a blender and season with salt and black pepper. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Divide the cauliflower cheese soup into bowls. Top the soup with the remaining crispy bacon. Drizzle with olive oil and serve.
Combine chili powder, thyme, smoked paprika, oregano, cayenne pepper, cumin, onion powder, and salt in a bowl.
Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium heat, cook and stir onion in hot ghee until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook until soft, about 2 minutes, stir shredded cabbage and sliced carrot into onion and garlic.
Cook and stir cabbage mixture until cabbage starts to soften, about 5 more minutes.
Stir spices into cabbage mixture and cook, stirring often, until spices are evenly mixed in and cabbage is tender, 3 more minutes.
spinach leaves: Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Place the diced sweet potato on a baking sheet. Drizzle with a little olive oil, salt, and pepper, and toss to coat.
Roast the sweet potato in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender and slightly crispy.
While the sweet potatoes are roasting, prepare the dressing. In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, balsamic vinegar, minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
In a large salad bowl, add the fresh spinach leaves.
Once the sweet potatoes are done roasting, remove them from the oven and let them cool slightly.
Add the roasted sweet potatoes, chickpeas, and diced avocado to the salad bowl with the spinach.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Gently toss everything together until the salad is well coated with the dressing.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a medium saucepan, combine the green or brown lentils and 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 20-25 minutes.
While the lentils are cooking, toss the diced pumpkin with a drizzle of olive oil, ground cinnamon, salt, and black pepper on a baking sheet.
Roast in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes, or until the pumpkin is tender and slightly caramelized. Remove from the oven and let it cool.
In a large salad bowl, place the fresh spinach leaves and sliced sun-dried tomatoes.
Add the cooked lentils and roasted pumpkin to the salad bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dressing ingredients: olive oil, honey, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients to ensure they are well coated with the dressing.
Cook the quinoa in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a large salad bowl, place the fresh spinach leaves.
Add the sliced strawberries on top of the spinach.
Sprinkle the chopped nuts over the salad.
Scatter the crumbled feta cheese over the salad.
Add the cooked quinoa to the salad.
In a small bowl or jar, prepare the vinaigrette. Combine olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, salt, and black pepper. Whisk or shake well to emulsify the dressing.
Taste the vinaigrette and adjust the sweetness or seasoning according to your preference.
Drizzle the vinaigrette over the salad.
Gently toss all the ingredients in the salad bowl to coat them with the dressing.
Cook fusilli pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant.
Add sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add frozen peas to the skillet and cook until heated through.
Add fresh spinach leaves to the skillet and cook until wilted.
Add cooked fusilli pasta to the skillet and toss to combine with the vegetables.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Place the chicken breast between two sheets of plastic wrap and pound it to an even thickness, about 1/2 inch thick.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Spread pesto sauce evenly over the surface of the chicken breast.
Layer spinach leaves over the pesto sauce.
Place a slice of mozzarella cheese on top of the spinach.
Roll up the chicken breast tightly, starting from one end.
Secure the roll with toothpicks if necessary.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and lightly coat it with cooking spray or olive oil.
Sear the chicken roll on all sides until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the chicken roll to a baking dish and bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until cooked through.
Remove from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve hot.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat to 350°F.
Add the diced onion and sauté until it becomes translucent.
Add the minced garlic and ginger. Sauté for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, garam masala, and red chili powder. Cook for an additional minute to release the spices' flavors.
Add the diced tomato and cook until it softens, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the fresh spinach leaves and water to the pan. Cover and let the spinach wilt for about 2-3 minutes.

Use an immersion blender to puree the cooked mixture until it forms a smooth paste. If you don't have an immersion blender, transfer the mixture to a regular blender, blend until smooth, and then return it to the pan.
Stir in the milk and cook for a few minutes to heat through.
Add the paneer cubes and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the paneer is heated.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken breast to the skillet and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side until golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the chicken breast from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add minced garlic and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add fresh spinach leaves to the skillet and cook until wilted.
Stir in heavy cream and grated Parmesan cheese, and cook until the sauce thickens slightly.
Return the cooked chicken breast to the skillet and spoon the sauce over the top.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until the chicken is heated through and the sauce is bubbling.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, mix together balsamic vinegar, garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Brush the balsamic mixture onto both sides of the sliced strawberries and kiwi.
Grill the strawberries and kiwi slices for about 1-2 minutes on each side, until grill marks appear.
Arrange baby spinach leaves on a plate.
Top the spinach with grilled strawberries and kiwi.
Drizzle any remaining balsamic mixture over the salad.
Serve immediately.
Heat olive oil in a pot or saucepan over medium heat.
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the pot. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until onions are translucent.
Add diced butternut squash to the pot and cook for another 3-4 minutes until slightly softened.
Stir in paprika and cumin, mixing well with the ingredients.
Add vegetable broth to the pot and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to simmer and let it cook for about 10-12 minutes or until the butternut squash is tender.
Add canned chickpeas to the stew and cook for an additional 3-4 minutes to warm them through.
Finally, add fresh spinach leaves to the pot, stirring until the spinach wilts and combines with the stew.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Once everything is cooked and combined, remove from heat.
Cook the orzo pasta according to the package instructions until it's al dente. Drain and set aside to cool.
In a mixing bowl, combine the cooked and cooled orzo pasta, fresh spinach leaves, crumbled feta cheese, chopped red onion, and chopped sundried tomatoes.
Drizzle olive oil and balsamic vinegar over the salad ingredients.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss the salad gently to ensure all the ingredients are evenly coated with the dressing.
Serve your Spinach and Orzo Salad as a delicious and satisfying meal.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, cook the chopped bacon over medium heat until crispy. Remove the bacon from the skillet and set aside, leaving the bacon fat in the skillet.
In the same skillet with the bacon fat, add the minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the fresh spinach leaves to the skillet and cook until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
Return the cooked bacon to the skillet and add the cooked pasta. Toss to combine.
Drizzle the olive oil over the pasta and toss again until everything is evenly coated.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with grated Parmesan cheese.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the mixed lettuce, arugula, and spinach leaves.
Add the sliced radishes and cucumber to the bowl.
Drizzle the olive oil and balsamic vinegar over the salad.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Toss the salad gently to coat all the ingredients evenly with the dressing.
Serve immediately.
Combine spinach, kale, pear, banana, blueberries, yogurt, ice cubes, and ground flax seeds in a blender.
Blend on high until mixture is smooth. Mix with a wooden spoon to redistribute ingredients if needed.
Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
In a bowl, toss the raw shrimp with barbecue sauce until well coated.
Add olive oil to the preheated skillet.
Add minced garlic to the skillet and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the barbecue-coated shrimp to the skillet. Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side or until the shrimp are opaque and cooked through.
Remove the cooked shrimp from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add fresh spinach leaves. Cook briefly until wilted.
Season the spinach with salt and pepper to taste.
Arrange the garlicky spinach on a plate and top with the BBQ shrimp.
Serve your BBQ shrimp with garlicky spinach immediately.
In a pot, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pot. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until the onion is translucent.
Add the chopped celery, broccoli florets, and zucchini to the pot. Sauté for another 2-3 minutes.
Pour in the low-sodium vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the pot and let it simmer for about 10-12 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in the fresh spinach leaves and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the spinach is wilted.
Remove the pot from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly.
Use an immersion blender or a regular blender to puree the soup until it's smooth.
Return the pureed soup to the pot and place it over low heat.
Stir in the plain Greek yogurt and heat the soup until it's warmed through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a bowl, beat the eggs together with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil.
Add the thinly sliced zucchini to the skillet and sauté for about 2-3 minutes until slightly tender.
Add the fresh spinach leaves to the skillet and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until wilted.
Spread the sautéed zucchini and spinach evenly on the skillet.
Pour the beaten eggs over the vegetables in the skillet.
Sprinkle the diced tomatoes over the egg mixture.
Let the frittata cook on the stovetop for 2-3 minutes until the edges start to set.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for about 8-10 minutes until the frittata is fully set and lightly golden on top.
Once done, remove the frittata from the oven and let it cool for a minute.
Cook the gnocchi according to package instructions until they float to the surface. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, melt the ghee over medium heat.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they start to brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the fresh spinach leaves and cook until wilted.
Add the cooked gnocchi to the skillet and toss everything together until well combined.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer to a serving plate.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil and add rice. Cook according to instructions and set aside.
Bring water to boil in a saucepan. Throw in spinach, gently stir to ensure its well soaked in water. Cook for about a minute, strain and let cool.
In a medium saute pan, heat oil. Add in onion and let cook until translucent and starts to turn slightly brown.
Add in garlic, spinach and let cook for 5 minutes, then mix with sauce. Let cook on medium heat for about 10 minutes.
Rinse and pad dry salmon with paper towel and season with salt. Heat stove top grill, drizzle olive oil and sear skinless side of salmon for 3 minutes. Flip and cook for another 3 minutes.
Serve on spinach stew with rice.
Cook the spaghetti according to the package instructions until it's al dente. Drain and set it aside.
In a blender or food processor, combine the fresh spinach leaves, fresh basil leaves, minced garlic, low-fat Greek yogurt, grated Parmesan cheese, low-sodium vegetable broth, and lemon zest.
Blend the ingredients until you have a smooth and vibrant green sauce. You can add a little more vegetable broth if needed to achieve your desired consistency.
Season the green sauce with salt and black pepper to taste.
Toss the cooked whole wheat spaghetti in the green sauce until it's well-coated.
Transfer the Green Spaghetti to a plate.
In a bowl, marinate the diced chicken breast in Greek yogurt, salt, and pepper. Set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until soft and translucent.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the pan and cook for another minute.
Add diced chicken to the pan and cook until it is browned on all sides.
Add ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, paprika, and cayenne pepper to the pan. Stir well to coat the chicken with the spices.
Pour tomato sauce and broth into the pan. Stir to combine and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Simmer the chicken tikka masala for about 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in fresh spinach leaves and cook until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish the chicken and spinach tikka masala with chopped cilantro before serving.
fava beans: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add bell pepper and beans and cook for 4 minutes. Season with garlic, salt and pepper.
Add the eggs, season with salt, then cover and cook for 10 more minutes. Serve immediately.
In a small saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add minced garlic to the saucepan and cook until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes.
Add cooked fava beans to the saucepan and cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Mash the fava beans with a fork or potato masher until you achieve a spreadable consistency.
Stir in ground cumin, salt, and pepper to taste.
Cook for another 1-2 minutes, then remove from heat.
Squeeze a bit of lemon juice over the fava bean spread and stir to combine.
Serve the fava bean breakfast spread warm or at room temperature with slices of toasted bread.
Remove the skin of the cod and place it in a small saucepan with a glass of water and bring nearly to boiling point. Remove the fish from the saucepan and preserve the liquid.
Heat the olive oil in a large pan over a medium heat and add the sliced garlic. Fry for a minute before adding the sliced onion and continue to cook for 10 minutes.
Drain the peppers of their juices and slice them thickly, making sure there are no seeds attached. Add to the pan and cook for a further 5 minutes.
Add the paprika, salt, and tomato puree. Simmer until the sauce has reduced by half.
Rinse the cod in cold water, pat dry with kitchen paper and then cut into ¾-inch pieces. Season with a little salt and then add to the sauce in the pan.
Cook for about 5 minutes, until the cod is just cooked, and then serve.
Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente, 10 minutes.
Place beans in a sieve, rinse and let drain. Blanch beans in salted boiling water, 5 minutes. Drain and rinse thoroughly with cold water.
In a pan, saute garlic until translucent. Stir in tomato sauce, fava beans, and tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper, and saute 5 minutes.
Drain pasta and add to pan with vegetables. Toss briefly to combine and divide among bowls. Garnish with some hemp seeds.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the pasta and cook according to package instructions. Drain and set aside. Reserve about ½ cup of the pasta cooking water.

Bring a medium pot of salted water to a boil for the favas. Prepare an ice bath. Add the favas to the medium pot of boiling water. Boil for 5 minutes, then drain, reserving ½ cup of the cooking water.
Transfer the favas to the ice bath. Drain and peel the favas. In a food processor or blender, puree half of the favas with the reserved water.
Heat a large skillet over medium. Add the oil. Add the leeks and garlic and cook, stirring often, for 2 minutes. Add the wine, bring to a simmer and cook, for 2 minutes.
Stir in the fava puree, the remaining whole favas, the chopped herbs, and the lemon juice. Season the sauce with salt and pepper.
Stir the pasta water into the sauce, then add the pasta, butter, and cheese. Toss until coated.
Serve immediately.
almond milk: In a bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, and ground cinnamon.
Add applesauce, almond milk, and the egg to the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined.
Gently fold in the grated apple.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Spoon batter onto the skillet to form pancakes of your desired size.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface, then flip and cook the other side until golden brown.
Repeat until all the batter is used.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Place hashbrown in each case of a muffin tin, and smush them down to form a base. Bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown.
Place 1 tbsp of beans on top of each cooked hash brown, followed by a few pieces of sausages and bacon.
Beat the eggs and milk together and pour some into each cup, being careful not to overfill. Top with grated cheese, parsley, and pepper.
Bake for 5 minutes, and serve.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
To make the crepes, beat all ingredients well. Cook in a pan, 3 minutes on each side.
In a large skillet, saute the onion and garlic in butter until golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the spinach, salt, pepper and mozzarella.
Fill the crepes with the spinach mixture, top with some mozzarella and bake for 15 minutes.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a mixing bowl, combine the cooked pasta, fresh spinach leaves, plain Greek yogurt, almond milk, lemon zest, grated Parmesan cheese, salt, and pepper. Mix everything thoroughly.
Transfer the pasta mixture into an oven-safe dish or ramekin.
Place the dish in the preheated oven and bake for about 15-20 minutes or until the top is lightly golden and the mixture is heated through.
Once baked, remove from the oven and let it cool for a couple of minutes before serving.
Layer apple, pineapple, cashew butter, coconut, flax meal, vanilla extract, and cinnamon into a blender; add almond milk. Blend mixture until smooth.
Chop onions and carrots with a knife or a blender, let them simmer in a frying pan with a small amount of oil and cool.
Pour milk over the loaf, allow some time to swell and squeeze.
Mince the fillet, bread, onion, and carrot through a meat grinder, put an egg in there, salt and pepper. Form patties. Roll in crackers.
Fry under cover until cooked, 10 minutes.
In a bowl or jar, combine the rolled oats and almond milk.
Stir well until the oats are fully coated with the milk.
Add the mixed berries on top of the oat mixture.
Cover the bowl or jar and refrigerate it overnight, allowing the oats to soak and soften.
In the morning, take out your muesli from the refrigerator.
Give it a good stir to mix the softened oats and the berries.
Combine pumpkin puree, almond milk, water and pumpkin pie spice in a blender. Blend until well combined, 5 to 10 seconds.
Bring water and salt to a boil on the stove. Once boiling, stir in oats, protein powder, and vanilla, and reduce heat to medium. Allow to cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
After 15 minutes, reduce heat to low and stir in almond milk, coconut milk and half of the mango.
To serve, top with mango slices, almonds and coconut flakes.
In a blender, combine bananas, flax seeds, cashew butter, milk, coconut flakes and some ice. Blend well. Add more ice and blend. Serve immediately.
Place banana slices, chopped apple, almond milk, Greek yogurt, ground cinnamon, and chia seeds in a blender.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour the smoothie into a glass and enjoy this healthy, no-sugar-added Apple Banana Smoothie!
Rinse the mixed raspberries and blackberries.
Mash half of the berries in a bowl using a fork.
Add chia seeds, almond milk, honey, and vanilla extract to the mashed berries. Stir well.
Gently fold in the remaining whole berries.
Cover the bowl and refrigerate the mixture for at least 2 hours or overnight, allowing the chia seeds to expand and create a pudding-like consistency.
Once chilled and set, give the mixture a final stir and serve chilled.
In a pot with boiling water add the tagliatelle.  Follow the instruction of your spaghetti brand. It usually takes about 8 minutes to cook.
Place the oil in a pan and let it warmup. Saute the onion and garlic.
Roast the jalapeno by toasting by bathing it in a little oil and then placing it straight into the stove flame.
In the blender add the cream, the milk, salt, and jalapeno.
Blend and the taste. Add more jalapeno if you prefer it spicier or add more salt to taste.
Get rid of the water were the pasta cooked and place the pasta back in the pot. Add the lender mix to the pasta as well as the cheese.
Mix all together and garnish with orange, arugula, and few slices od jalapeno.
In a pot, heat ghee over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic to the pot, sautéing until softened (about 2-3 minutes).
Add diced chicken to the pot, cooking until it's browned on all sides (about 3-4 minutes).
Stir in almond flour, ensuring the chicken is well coated.
Pour in chicken broth, almond milk, diced vegetables, and dried thyme, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Let it simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through, and the vegetables are tender.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
While the chicken mixture is simmering, prepare the dumplings according to the package instructions (if using store-bought) or use your preferred low-carb dumpling recipe.
Add the cooked dumplings to the pot just before serving.
Add all ingredients to a high-speed blender, and blend on high for 2 minutes or until completely smooth.
Serve immediately.
In a blender combine lentils, ginger, spinach and cinnamon, blend until smooth.
Add fruit and avocado, begin to blend, adding Ripple slowly until mixture is smooth and well blended. Serve imemdiately.
Sift flour and baking powder into a bowl. Add sliced spring onions, chopped parsley, corn and ham. Season with salt and pepper and stir to combine.
Make a well in the centre, add eggs and milk, then stir to form a batter.
Heat a frying pan, add a film of oil and drop heaped tablespoonfuls of the batter into the pan. Cook for 3 minutes on each side.
Mix oil, egg, sliced banana, flour, raisins, protein powder honey, milk and baking powder. Give it a good whisk with a fork. Make sure the banana gets crushed.
Pop the mug or bowl into the microwave for 1 minute 30 seconds, using a 1000w microwave. Take it out, turn it round and give it another 1 minute blast.
Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 425°F.
Place the pork sausages in a small baking dish and bake in the preheated oven for about 10 minutes.
While the sausages are baking, prepare the batter. In a mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, milk, beaten egg, salt, and pepper. Mix until smooth.
After 10 minutes, remove the baking dish from the oven and carefully pour the batter over the partially cooked sausages.
Return the baking dish to the oven and bake for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the batter is puffed up and golden brown.
Remove from the oven and serve hot.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
coconut oil: Blend chia seeds in a blender until powdery, 3 to 5 seconds. Add banana, eggs, protein powder, flour, cocoa powder, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and sea salt.
Blend, occasionally scraping sides down, until batter is smooth.
Heat a skillet over medium heat; add coconut oil. Pour 1/2 of the batter into the hot oil and sprinkle 1/2 of the blueberries onto the batter.
Cook until bubbles form and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes. Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 2 to 3 minutes.
Repeat with remaining batter and blueberries, using more coconut oil if needed.
Preheat waffle iron according to manufacturer's instructions.
Beat egg whites in a glass or metal bowl using an electric mixer on high until stiff peaks form.
Whisk flour, cornmeal, chia seeds, baking powder, baking soda, white sugar, and salt together in a bowl.
Whisk almond milk, coconut oil, and egg yolks together in a separate bowl, stir into flour mixture until batter is smooth.
Gently fold egg whites into batter until just mixed.
Pour about 1/4 cup batter into preheated waffle iron, cook for about 5 minutes. Repeat with remaining batter.
In a mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, salt, and ground cinnamon.
In another bowl, whisk together the milk, egg, and vanilla extract.
Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, whisking until smooth.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and lightly grease with oil.
Pour about 1/4 cup of the pancake batter onto the skillet for each pancake.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface of the pancake, then flip and cook until golden brown on the other side.
Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil or butter to the skillet as needed.
Serve the Moroccan pancakes warm with your favorite toppings such as honey, fruit, or yogurt.
Add all of the ingredients except coconut oil to a blender and blend on high speed until completely smooth. Let the batter sit in your blender while you heat your pan.

Lightly coat a pan with coconut oil and place over medium heat. Once the pan is hot, add 1/3 cup of the batter for each pancake and cook for 2-4 minutes or until pancakes slightly puff up. Flip pancakes and cook until golden brown on the underside.
Transfer the pancakes to a plate and top them with honey and blueberries.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
Line a baking dish with aluminum foil, letting foil hang down over the sides of the pan, oil pan with coconut oil.
Stir oats, wheat germ, flax seed, protein powder, cinnamon, and salt together in a bowl.
Mash bananas in a separate large bowl and stir 2 tbsp peanut butter, honey, and vanilla extract into bananas, mixing well.
Stir dry ingredients into banana mixture and spread into the prepared baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
Let cool in the pan and lift bars onto a work surface using aluminum foil as handles. Cut into bars, wrap in plastic wrap, and store in refrigerator.
Make the granola first. Preheat the oven to 300 °F.
Mix the dry ingredients - oatmeal, 1 tbsp cocoa powder, and salt - together in a medium mixing bowl.
Stir to combine.
Add vanilla extract to the dry ingredients. Stir to combine.
Melt coconut oil in the microwave until completely liquid (approximately 30-40 seconds). Stir to combine.
Melt honey in the microwave until completely liquid (approximately 15-20 seconds).
Stir to combine.
Line a 9 x 13 inch cookie sheet with parchment paper.
Spread a thin layer of the mixture on the cookie sheet.
Bake for 20 minutes, stirring halfway through.
Remove from oven and allow to completely cool for 30 minutes.
While the granola is baking, mix the banana, avocado, almond milk, remaining cocoa powder and the ice cubes in a blender.
Pour into bowls, and top with cooled granola
Preheat oven to 350ºF.
Spray coconut oil on Madeleine pan to prevent sticking.
In a large bowl, add dry ingredients and whisk to blend.
In a small bowl, add wet ingredients and mix well. Pour them into dry ingredients and mix.
Put a spoonful of dough into each Madeleine pan cake mold.
Bake for 10 minutes until done and serve.
Add the milk and half of the honey in a small sauce pan and combine well. Heat on medium heat until the milk is warm to touch.
Beat the eggs in a large bowl. Then slowly add a small amount of warm milk while whisking. While whisking, slowly add the melted butter to the mixture.

Set up a fine-mesh sieve over the bowl and add cake flour, matcha powder, protein powder, and baking powder. Sift half of the dry ingredients into the batter and whisk to combine the dry and wet ingredients together.
Sift the rest of dry ingredients and mix until there is no lump in the batter. Be careful not to overmix the batter, as it causes a rubbery crepe texture.
Cover with plastic wrap to refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Set a 12-inch non-stick frying pan on low heat. Once hot, brush with coconut oil. Pour the batter just enough to cover the bottom of the frying pan, about 1/3 cup for my pan.
Cook for 2 minutes without touching. By now you’d see the outside edge of the crepe gets a tiny bit crispier. Using a chopstick, spatula, or hand, flip the crepe.
Cook the other side for 30 seconds and transfer to a working surface lined with parchment paper. Continue this process until all the batter is used.
Whip the cream using a hand mixer, gradually adding the remaining honey.
To assemble the cake, place a single crepe on a plate. This will be the top layer when we flip. Put the freshly whipped cream in the middle and spread evenly, leaving the outer 2-inches.
Place another crepe on top and spread whip cream evenly, leaving outer ½ inch. Continue this process until the last crepe is placed on top.
Prepare a round curved shallow dish and place a large piece of plastic wrap on top. Transfer the crepe stack to the shallow dish.
Wrap the mille crepe with plastic wrap from one corner to clockwise. This will make the final cake evenly round, instead of wrap randomly.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours until the cake is set. Once chilled, remove the plastic wrap and flip onto the serving platter. Dust more matcha powder over the cake, cut it and enjoy!
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a bowl, combine the rolled oats, baking powder, and ground cinnamon.
Add the lemon juice, lemon zest, unsweetened applesauce, and honey or maple syrup to the dry ingredients. Mix until well combined.
Gently fold in the fresh blueberries into the oat mixture.
Lightly grease a muffin tin or ramekin with oil.
Spoon the oatmeal mixture into the muffin tin or ramekin, filling it to the top.
Place the tin or ramekin in the preheated oven and bake for about 20-25 minutes or until the oatmeal cakes are firm and slightly golden on top.
Once baked, remove the oatmeal cakes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Carefully remove the oatmeal cakes from the muffin tin or ramekin.
Combine milk with vinegar in a medium bowl and set aside for 5 minutes to "sour".
Combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large mixing bowl. Whisk egg and ghee into "soured" milk.
Pour the flour mixture into the wet ingredients and whisk until lumps are gone.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat, and coat with coconut oil. Pour batter onto the skillet, and cook until bubbles appear on the surface.
Flip with a spatula, and cook until browned on the other side. Garnish with fruit and pecans.
In a bowl, combine ricotta cheese, all-purpose flour, baking powder, and the egg.
Gently fold in the fresh blueberries.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and lightly grease it with coconut oil.
Pour small scoops of the pancake batter onto the skillet to form pancakes.
Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Transfer the pancakes to a plate.
Combine spinach, kale, pear, banana, blueberries, yogurt, ice cubes, and ground flax seeds in a blender.
Blend on high until mixture is smooth. Mix with a wooden spoon to redistribute ingredients if needed.
In a blender or food processor, blend the rolled oats until they form a flour-like consistency.
In a mixing bowl, combine the oat flour, almond flour, baking powder, egg, almond milk, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt. Mix until well combined and smooth.
Let the batter sit for a couple of minutes to allow the oats to absorb some of the liquid.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and lightly grease it with cooking spray or oil.
Pour a portion of the pancake batter onto the skillet, using about 1/4 cup of batter for each pancake.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface of the pancake and the edges start to look set, usually about 2-3 minutes.
Flip the pancake and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes on the other side until golden brown and cooked through.
Repeat with the remaining batter, making additional pancakes.
Serve with fruit!
reheat your oven to 350°F.
In a small mixing bowl, combine the mashed ripe banana, oil, vanilla extract, and whisked egg. Mix well.
In another bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, baking powder, baking soda, and a pinch of salt.
Pour the wet banana mixture into the dry ingredients. Stir until just combined. Be careful not to overmix; a few lumps are okay.
Lightly grease a muffin tin or a single muffin cup with cooking spray.
Pour the muffin batter into the greased muffin cup.
Place the muffin tin or cup in the preheated oven.
Bake for about 18-20 minutes, or until a toothpick or knife inserted into the center of the muffin comes out clean.
Carefully remove the Banana Crumb Muffin from the oven.
Allow it to cool for a minute before serving.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly softened, about 3 minutes.
Add frozen vegetables, cook and stir until tender yet firm to the bite, about 5 minutes. Drain into a bowl.
Place coconut oil in the warm pot. Add pasta mixture, garlic powder, sea salt, and pepper, toss until combined, 1 to 2 minutes. Top with Parmesan cheese.
In a mixing bowl, combine the applesauce, almond milk,  oil, and vanilla extract. Mix until well combined.
Add the flour, baking powder, and a pinch of salt to the wet ingredients. Stir until just combined. Do not overmix; it's okay if the batter is slightly lumpy.
Preheat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and lightly coat with cooking spray or olive oil.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface of the pancakes and the edges look set, about 2-3 minutes.
Flip the pancakes and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until golden brown on both sides.
Repeat with the remaining batter.
Put the coconut oil in a non-stick pan over a medium heat. Lightly fry the garlic, add the spinach leaves and wilt for a few mins – add a splash of water if they stick.
Whisk the eggs with the coconut milk and turmeric. Season well. Add to the pan and let it cook for 3 minutes.
When it begind to get firm, use a spatula, carefully ease around the edges of the omelet, and then fold it over in half.
In a small bowl, combine 2 tbsp ground flax or chia with 6 tbsp water.
Stir together and set aside to thicken. In a large bowl, whisk together the quinoa flour, baking powder, cinnamon and salt.
In a large measuring cup or medium size bowl, combine almond milk, and vanilla extract.
Pour the liquid mixture into the large bowl with flour. Add the walnuts, carrots, and melted coconut oil. Stir together until a smooth batter forms.
Allow batter to rest as you preheat the waffle iron. Lightly grease the iron then fill with batter.
Cook until your iron says the waffles are ready, or possibly a little longer so that the waffles are golden with crisp edges.
Place cooked waffles on a cooling iron as you cook the rest of the batch.
Reheat in the toaster or oven for extra crispy edges. Serve warm with mixed frozen berries.
Cook the oatmeal according to package directions.

Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the maple syrup, protein powder, cinnamon, and vanilla, and let it bubble around for a little bit to get incorporated. Add bananas and simmer for a few minutes on each side until soft and plump. Remove from heat.
To serve, top oatmeal with bananas.
Heat coconut oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add sliced apples to the skillet.
Sprinkle ground cinnamon, vanilla extract, and a dash of salt over the apples.
Saute the apples until they are softened and lightly browned.
Transfer the sauteed apples to a plate.
Enjoy this healthy and flavorful sauteed apple dish!
corn kernels: In a mixing bowl, combine 1 cup of drained and rinsed black beans, 1 cup of halved cherry tomatoes, and 1/2 cup of corn kernels.
Add 1/2 diced avocado to the bowl.
Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of chopped fresh dill over the ingredients.
Squeeze the juice of half a lime into the bowl to add a zesty flavor.
Season the salad with a pinch of salt and pepper to taste. If you like it spicy, you can also add 1/4 teaspoon of chili powder.
Toss all the ingredients together until they are well combined.
In a large mixing bowl, combine drained black beans, diced cucumber, chopped red onion, diced tomatoes, chopped fresh parsley, and cooked corn kernels.
In a small bowl, whisk together the lime juice and olive oil.
Pour the lime and olive oil dressing over the salad.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined and evenly coated with the dressing.
Let the salad chill in the refrigerator for about 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.

In a food processor, combine the chickpeas, diced red onion, 1 garlic, fresh parsley, fresh cilantro, ground cumin, ground coriander, salt, pepper, baking powder, and 2 tablespoons of flour. Pulse until the mixture forms a coarse paste. If needed, add the extra tablespoon of flour to achieve the right consistency.
Form the mixture into 8 falafel patties.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the falafel patties and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side or until they are golden brown and crispy.
While the falafel is cooking, prepare the salad. In a large bowl, combine the torn lettuce, thinly sliced red onion, corn kernels, diced tomatoes, and diced cucumber.
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the Lemon-Tahini Dressing by combining tahini, fresh lemon juice, water, 1 minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
Once the falafel is cooked, remove them from the skillet or oven.
Divide the salad between two plates or bowls.
Top each salad with four hot homemade falafel patties.
Drizzle the Lemon-Tahini Dressing over the salad.
Serve immediately and enjoy your delicious Homemade Falafel Salad with Lemon-Tahini Dressing.
In a large salad bowl, combine the chopped Romaine lettuce, chopped cooked chicken breast, chopped hard-boiled eggs, diced tomato, crumbled blue cheese, corn, crumbled bacon, and diced avocado.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste. Drizzle with greek yogurt.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined and evenly coated with the dressing.
Cut the avocado in half, remove the pit, and scoop the flesh into a bowl.
Add cooked corn kernels, chopped onion, diced tomato, and chopped jalapeno pepper to the bowl with the avocado.
Drizzle lime juice over the ingredients.
Use a fork to mash the avocado and mix all the ingredients together until desired consistency is reached.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a pot or saucepan, heat a bit of olive oil over medium heat.
Add diced chicken and cook until it's no longer pink, about 5-7 minutes.
Add diced onion and minced garlic to the cooked chicken. Sauté for another 2-3 minutes until the onion softens.
Stir in the white beans, corn, broth, ground cumin, chili powder, dried oregano, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to blend.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
In a bowl, combine fresh or frozen corn kernels, all-purpose flour, baking powder, salt, and black pepper.
Stir in milk to form a thick batter.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Drop spoonfuls of the corn batter into the hot skillet, spreading them out to form fritters.
Cook for about 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the corn fritters from the skillet and place them on a paper towel to drain any excess oil.
Serve your Very Crispy Corn Fritters hot.
Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, bell pepper, carrot, and minced garlic to the pot. Cook until vegetables are softened.
Stir in chili powder, ground cumin, paprika, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add canned diced tomatoes, kidney beans, black beans, corn kernels, and vegetable broth to the pot. Stir to combine.
Bring the chili to a simmer and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until flavors are well combined and vegetables are tender.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
In a skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add 4 ounces of thinly sliced raw chicken breast to the skillet. Season with salt, pepper, 1/2 teaspoon of chili powder, and 1/2 teaspoon of cumin.

Cook the chicken slices for about 3-4 minutes per side or until they are no longer pink in the center and have a nice sear on the outside. Make sure the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, prepare the remaining ingredients. Dice 1/4 cup each of cucumber, red bell pepper, avocado, and tomatoes.
In a serving bowl, place the 1 cup of raw cauliflower rice.
Top the cauliflower rice with the cooked chicken slices, diced cucumber, diced red bell pepper, diced avocado, diced tomatoes, 1/4 cup of drained and rinsed black beans, and 1/4 cup of corn kernels.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice if desired.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
In a small baking dish, layer diced potatoes, cooked corn kernels, black beans, diced tomatoes, diced bell peppers, and cooked ground beef.
Sprinkle shredded cheese on top of the casserole.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
In a bowl, mix together the cooked shredded chicken, black beans, corn kernels, diced tomatoes, diced onion, shredded cheese, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Place the flour tortilla on a flat surface.
Spoon the filling mixture onto the center of the tortilla.
Fold in the sides of the tortilla, then roll it up tightly to form a chimichanga.
Lightly grease the air fryer basket with cooking spray or olive oil.
Place the chimichanga in the air fryer basket, seam side down.
Air fry the chimichanga at 375°F for 10-12 minutes, or until it is golden brown and crispy.
Once cooked, remove the chimichanga from the air fryer and let it cool slightly before serving.
Place the diced carrots, celery, onion, garlic, potato, frozen corn, and frozen peas in the slow cooker.
Add the chicken thigh pieces on top of the vegetables.
Pour broth over the chicken and vegetables in the slow cooker.
Season with dried thyme, salt, and pepper to taste.
Cover and cook on low for 4-5 hours or on high for 2-3 hours, until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary before serving.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
For the "egg" mixture, mix the flour, black salt, salt, olive oil and water together with a whisk, until you have a smooth batter.
In a large frying pan, heat up the sesame oil on high heat. Then add the "egg" mixture, let it cook for 30 seconds and then scramble it up with a spatula.
Continue until the egg mixture is all well cooked. Clear some space in the pan, add the peas, carrot, corn and onions. Cook for 30 seconds before stirring in with rice.
Mix the garlic powder with soy sauce, and pour it all over the rice. Stir everything up and serve immediately.
In a mixing bowl, combine diced chicken, mixed salad greens, red onion, cucumber, tomato, corn kernels, avocado, and cilantro.
In a small bowl, whisk together lime juice, olive oil, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to coat everything evenly.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
In a blender, combine the fresh corn kernels, plain Greek yogurt, vegetable broth, cold water, and minced garlic.
Blend until the mixture is smooth and creamy.
Transfer the blended mixture to a bowl.
Stir in the diced cucumber, red bell pepper, red onion, fresh lime juice, and ground cumin.
Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste. Mix well.
Cover the bowl and refrigerate the soup for at least 1 hour to chill.
Before serving, garnish with fresh cilantro leaves.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Season the pork chop with salt and pepper on both sides.
Rub a little olive oil on the pork chop to prevent sticking.
Place the pork chop on the grill and cook for about 4-5 minutes on each side, or until the internal temperature reaches 145°F for medium-rare or 160°F for medium, depending on your preference.
While the pork chop is cooking, place the corn kernels in a small bowl.
In another small bowl, whisk together the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper to make the sauce.
Once the pork chop is cooked to your liking, remove it from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes.
While the pork chop is resting, place the corn kernels on the grill and cook for about 3-4 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lightly charred.
Slice the grilled pork chop and serve it with the grilled corn kernels.
Drizzle the sauce over the pork chop and corn.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook ground turkey until browned.
Add cooked quinoa, black beans, corn kernels, diced tomatoes, diced bell pepper, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture into a small baking dish.
Sprinkle shredded cheese on top.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cut the butternut squash in half lengthwise and scoop out the seeds and membranes.
Place the butternut squash halves, cut side down, on a baking sheet. Roast them in the preheated oven for about 30-35 minutes, or until they are tender when pierced with a fork.
While the butternut squash is roasting, rinse the quinoa under cold water.

In a small saucepan, bring the vegetable broth to a boil. Add the rinsed quinoa and reduce the heat to low. Cover and simmer for about 15 minutes, or until the quinoa is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced bell pepper, msuhrooms and diced zucchini to the skillet. Sauté for about 3-4 minutes until they begin to soften.
Stir in the cooked quinoa, black beans, corn kernels, ground cumin, salt, and pepper. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, mixing well.
Once the butternut squash halves are tender, remove them from the oven.
Stuff each butternut squash half with the quinoa and vegetable mixture.
Return the stuffed butternut squash halves to the oven and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes to heat the stuffing through.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.
In a small baking dish, spread a thin layer of enchilada sauce on the bottom.
Cut one of the corn tortillas into smaller pieces and place them in the baking dish to cover the bottom.
Layer half of the black beans, corn, and diced bell peppers on top of the tortilla pieces.
Sprinkle half of the shredded cheese over the beans and veggies.
Pour a little more enchilada sauce over the cheese layer.
Repeat the layers using the remaining tortilla, black beans, corn, bell peppers, and cheese.
Top it off with the remaining enchilada sauce.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for about 20-25 minutes, until the casserole is heated through and the cheese is melted.
Remove the foil and bake for an additional 5 minutes to slightly brown the cheese on top.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a couple of minutes.
Garnish with chopped cilantro.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Mix in the Cheddar until the cheese melts then add corn. Set aside.
Mash the avocado and lime juice together in a small bowl with a fork until it becomes a spreadable consistency. Lay the tortilla out on a cutting board.
Spread the mashed avocado on the tortilla, leaving a 1-inch outer border. In the bottom third of the tortilla, lay out the rice, beans, carrots and salsa.
Roll over once tightly, then tuck in the sides and continue rolling. Cut in half. Wrap in a paper towel, followed by a layer of aluminum foil. Serve at room temperature.
heavy whipping cream: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Season scallops with salt and pepper, then dredge in flour. Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat and add scallops.
Cook for 1 minute per side. Stir in heavy cream and bring to a boil. Turn off the heat and add pasta and pesto. Mix well. Remove from heat and serve.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta, and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente, drain.
In a large skillet, cook pancetta in oil until just beginning to brown. Stir in shallots, garlic, and mushrooms, cook for one minute.
Season with pepper and oregano, and pour in cream. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat , and simmer for 1 to 2 minutes. Cover, and continue to simmer for 5 to 7 minutes.
Divide pasta onto individual serving plates, and top with equal portions of pancetta and mushrooms. Garnish with freshly grated Parmesan.
Bring a large pot of lightly-salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook until al dente, 8 to 10 minutes; drain well. Place the pasta in a large bowl.
Heat a skillet over medium heat. Cook the ham in the skillet for 3 minutes. Add the cream, tomato paste, soy sauce, and salt, stir.
Reduce heat to medium low and cook and stir until the mixture reaches a low boil. Pour the sauce over the drained pasta.
Melt ghee in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, cook and stir until softened and browned, 10 to 15 minutes.
Stir chicken broth, squash, curry powder, salt, cumin, and cayenne pepper into the onion mixture and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer until squash is tender, about 15 minutes. Remove from the heat. Stir cream into the squash mixture.
Fill blender halfway with the squash mixture. Cover and hold the lid down with a potholder, pulse a few times before leaving on to blend.
Pour into a pot. Repeat with remaining squash mixture. Ladle soup into serving bowls.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil, add pasta and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 10 minutes.
Meanwhile in a large, heavy skillet over medium heat combine mushroom soup, mushrooms, onion, garlic powder, salt, pepper, nutritional yeast, water and olive oil.
Stir until ingredients are well mixed. Reduce heat to low and simmer until thickened, about 10 minutes. Garnish with parsley.
Bring a large pot of lightly-salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook until al dente, 8 to 10 minutes, drain well.
Combine the onion, and garlic in a saucepan over medium heat, cook until onion is translucent, 5 to 7 minutes.
Stir together the pumpkin puree, nutritional yeast, cream, ginger, salt, and pepper in a saucepan over medium-low heat.
Add the onion and garlic and stir. Simmer until heated through, about 3 minutes. Toss with drained pasta to serve.
Cook the spaghetti according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced ham and sliced mushrooms, and sauté until the mushrooms are tender.
Add  broth to the skillet. Cook for a few minutes until the peas are heated through.
Stir in the cooked spaghetti, grated Parmesan cheese, and heavy cream. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Bring a pan of water to the boil and cook the fusilli according to the pack instructions.
Meanwhile, heat a knob of butter in a saucepan, then add the shallot and cook for 5 mins or until softened.
Add the peas, salmon, cream and 1/4 cup of water. Crumble in the stock cube.
Cook for 3-4 mins until cooked through, stir in the chives and some black pepper. Then stir through to coat the pasta. Serve in bowls. Add Parmesan cheese.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Brown chicken in oil on both sides. Stir in oranges, and heavy cream.
Reduce heat to medium, and simmer until liquid has reduced by half. Remove chicken from pan when not longer pink in center.
Allow the sauce to reduce another 5 minutes, season to taste with salt and pepper.
Heat the oven to 400°F, place on a baking tray, drizzle 1 tbsp with oil and roast for 12 minutes until opaque and the flesh starts to flake when pressed.
While the salmon is cooking, boil the pasta for 10 mins until al dente, then drain, reserving 1/2 cup of the pasta water.
Heat the oil on a medium heat ddd the garlic and fry for a minute until fragrant. Add the wine, bubble for a few minutes until reduced by half.
Add the cream and season well. Bring to a very gentle simmer.
Flake the cooked salmon into big chunky pieces. Add to the sauce with the cooked pasta. Add the lemon zest, a squeeze of the juice.
Toss gently to keep the salmon in big pieces. Grind over some black pepper.
Heat ghee in a large pot over medium heat, cook and stir onion until tender, about 5 minutes.
Stir garlic, curry powder, cumin, ginger, salt, turmeric, cardamom, cinnamon, cayenne pepper, and nutmeg into onion, cook and stir until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Stir chicken into onion mixture, cook for 2 minutes. Stir carrots, corn, green beans, coconut milk, cashews, and tomato paste into chicken mixture, simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir cauliflower into chicken mixture, simmer, covered, until vegetables are tender and chicken is cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Stir peas, red bell pepper, and cream into chicken mixture, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until heated through, about 10 minutes.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
In a medium bowl whisk together eggs, heavy cream, and mozzarella. Season with salt, pepper, and a pinch red pepper flakes.
In a large skillet over medium heat, heat oil. Add shallot and garlic and cook until soft, 5 minutes then add mushrooms and cook until soft, another 5 minutes more.
Add spinach and cook until wilted, 2 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Pour egg mixture into skillet and bake untik eggs are just set, 12 minutes.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti in the boiling water, about 12 minutes. Drain.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a skillet over low heat. Add leek and mushroom and cook until softened, 3 to 5 minutes. Add cream.
Add cooked spaghetti, nutritional yeast and season with salt and pepper. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and serve.
Put zucchini in large Dutch oven with a few inches of water. Simmer zucchini over medium-low heat until tender when poked with a fork, about 20 minutes.
Use a slotted spoon to transfer zucchini to a blender, leaving about 1 inch of water in the bottom of the pot.
Blend zucchini until no large chunks remain, return to the pot. Combine bell pepper, red onions, and basil in the blender, blend until no large pieces remain.
Stir bell pepper mixture into the blended zucchini, add cream, Swiss cheese, salt, and pepper.
Cook over medium-low heat until warmed through and the cheese is melted, 5 to 7 minutes.
Bring a pot of water to a boil. Boil asparagus for 3 to 4 minutes, drain.
In a large saucepan melt ghee over medium heat. Saute garlic and asparagus for 3 to 4 minutes. Stir in the cream and simmer for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add linguine and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente, drain and transfer to a serving dish.
Stir lemon juice into asparagus mixture, pour mixture over pasta. Sprinkle with Parmesan.
Cook chicken in an oiled skillet over medium-high heat for7 to 8 minutes.
Meanwhile in a large saucepan cook and stir broccoli, mushrooms, carrot, and onion in ghee 6-8 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Stir in basil, and pepper. Add milk and cream all at once, add Worcestershire sauce and bouillon granules.
Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Add chicken, heat through.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Meanwhile bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook lasagna noodles in boiling water until al dente. Drain, and cut noodles in half crosswise.
Melt ghee in a skillet over medium heat. Saute garlic just until fragrant. Stir in cream. Gradually mix in milk, and season with thyme. Simmer sauce gently until thick. Remove from heat.
Grease a baking dish. Layer noodles, sauce, asparagus, ham, and mozzarella cheese in three layers, each starting with noodles, and ending with shredded cheese on the top.
Cover the dish, and cook in the oven for 10 minutes, or until cheese is melted and bubbly. Let stand for 5 to 10 minutes before serving.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Season chicken breasts and thighs with salt and pepper.
Melt ghee in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken in the hot butter until golden brown, 5 to 10 minutes.
Transfer to a baking dish. Add feta cheese and garlic. Mix heavy cream and pesto together in a bowl. Pour over the chicken mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven, uncovered, until top is golden brown, 20 to 30 minutes.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes. Stir asparagus into pot in the last five minutes of cooking; drain.
Meanwhile heat ghee and cream in a medium saucepan over medium heat. When mixture begins to bubble, stir in Parmesan, garlic powder, pepper and cayenne.
Continue cooking until mixture thickens, stirring occasionally. Stir in shrimp and heat for 5 to 6 minutes until shrimp are cooked.
Toss pasta and asparagus with sauce and serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Combine almond flour, parsley and baking powder in a large bowl. Mix in Cheddar cheese by hand.
Create a small well in the middle of the bowl, add eggs and heavy whipping cream to the center.
Use a large fork, spoon, or your hands to blend in the flour mixture until a sticky batter forms.
Drop 8 portions of batter onto the prepared baking sheet. Bake in the preheated oven until golden, about 20 minutes.
crimini mushrooms: Season the chicken with salt and pepper. In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until it’s well browned, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside. Add the bacon to the skillet and cook until the fat begins to render, about 3 minutes.
Add the onion and sauté until it becomes translucent, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic and mushrooms, and sauté until the mushrooms are tender, 5 minutes.
Add the browned chicken back to the skillet. Pour the wine into the skillet, stir in the mustard and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the skillet and simmer until the chicken is almost fully cooked, 15 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Combine the thinly sliced onion and flour. Heat a skillet over low heat and add the butter. Add the onion and cook until no longer powdery and white.
Add the milk a little bit at a time, stirring well after each addition. Once the milk is added, continue to stir until thickened. Salt to taste.
Cut salmon fillets into bitesized pieces and salt. Heat a nonstick pan over medium heat and brown the salmon pieces. Remove salmon and saute the mushrooms.
Put the mushrooms and salmon into a casserole dish. Pour white sauce on top and lightly stir to help bring pieces to the top.
Sprinkle the cheese over the top and cook in oven until melted. Serve immediately.
Combine buckwheat and vegetable broth in a medium pot. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper. Bring to a boil, cover, and reduce heat.
Cook for about 10 minutes, or until the buckwheat is tender.  Drain off extra liquid if there is still liquid remaining.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat and add the sliced onion. Saute for 5 minutes, or until the onion starts to soften and turn golden brown.
Add the mushrooms, and saute for 5 minutes. When the mushrooms start releasing juices add cooked buckwheat and chopped parsley to the skillet.
Mix well over medium heat to combine all the flavors and serve warm.
Melt the butter in a medium-sized saucepan over medium heat. Stir in the sliced mushrooms and cook for 7 minutes.
Stir in the milk and flour, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil and begins to thicken.
Add the cheese and stir until completely melted. Transfer to a plate, garnish with parsley and serve.
Put the oats in a pan and add  2 cups water and ½ cup milk. Stir very well and heat the porridge mixture on a low to medium flame. Simmer for 5 minutes. Cover oats with a lid and set aside.

Heat olive oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add brussels sprouts and spinach, cook until soft and brown, 4 minutes. Add sliced cremini mushrooms and cook until tender, 4 minutes. Stir in minced garlic, salt, and black pepper. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
In the same skillet crack eggs and cook them for 3 minutes or until white is set. Flip and cook 3 minutes more, until the yolk is completely set. Remove from pan and season with salt and pepper.
Serve warm oats porridge along with vegetables and eggs. Garnish with chives.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Butter a 2-quart casserole dish on the bottom and sides. Heat the butter in a large deep skillet on medium heat.
Add the onion, season with salt and pepper. Cook for 3 minutes, until the onion is tender.
Add the fresh mushrooms and garlic. Cook for 6 minutes, until the mushrooms are golden.
Add the buckwheat to the skillet and roast it in the skillet with the mushrooms for about 2 minutes.
Pour in the 1½ cups of water, bring to a boil. Reduce to a simmer, cover, and cook on low heat for about 10 minutes, just until most of the liquid is absorbed.
Transfer the buckwheat mixture to the prepared casserole dish and spread it out in an even layer.
Place the chicken on top of the buckwheat. Spread the sour cream or mayonnaise over the chicken and top it all with the cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 25 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through and the cheese is bubbly and golden.
Preheat your oven to 400°F. Grease a glass or ceramic baking dish.
Wash and dry the mushrooms. Use a paring knife to gently remove the stems.
In a medium bowl, use a rubber spatula to thoroughly mix the cream cheese with the salt and the spices. Mix in the gruyere.
Spoon the mixture into the mushroom caps.
Place the mushrooms in the prepared baking dish. Bake until the cheese has melted and the mushrooms are tender, 10 minutes.
Add buckwheat flour, eggs, almond milk, and salt in the jar of a high-speed blender and blend until smooth. Heat a non-stick pan or crepe pan over medium-high heat.
Spray the pan with cooking spray. Once the pan is heated, pour about ¼ cup of the batter into the pan while turning it in a circular motion to spread the batter thinly around the pan.
Cook for 2 minutes on one side until set. Flip and cook for another 1 minute on the other side. Stack the cooked crepes on a plate and continue with the rest of the batter.
Ground mushrooms up in a food processor. Once done, add them to a large heated skillet with olive oil, carrot, and beef. Cook until beef is browned, about 15 minutes.
Stuff the crepes with beef mixture and roll them. Garnish with dill and serve warm.
Place the quinoa in a strainer and rinse several times with cold water. Place in a medium saucepan with 1 1/2 cups of water and salt to taste.
Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the water is absorbed. Remove from the heat, and let sit for 10 minutes
Heat olive oil in a pan, over medium heat. Add mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes, until browned. Add spinach, green peas, walnuts, and quinoa.
Transfer to a plate, garnish with parmesan and serve warm.
Slice chicken breasts in half widthwise using a sharp knife.
Cover with plastic wrap, pound with the flat side of a meat mallet to 1/4-inch thickness. Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil and 2 teaspoons butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add 3 chicken pieces; cook until golden, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer to a large platter. Cook remaining chicken pieces in batches, adding 1/3 tablespoon olive oil and 2 teaspoons ghee for each batch.
Cook and stir shallot in the hot skillet until shallots soften, about 1 minute. Add mushrooms, cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper. Pour in Marsala wine, cook until slightly reduced.
Pour in chicken stock, simmer until slightly thickened, about 1 minute.
Stir 1 tablespoon ghee into the skillet. Return chicken to the skillet, simmer until heated through, about 1 minute. Garnish with chopped parsley.
To cook barley, combine 1 cup pearled barley with 3 cups water and 1/2 tsp salt.
Bring to a boil then reduce heat and simmer until barley is tender about 40 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Chop onion into small pieces. Slice the carrots and mushrooms.
Saute the vegetables in heated olive oil for 7 minutes. Add the cubed chicken and cook for another 15 minutes.
Add barley porridge to the vegetables and chicken. Mix everything and serve warm. Garnish with parsley and chili pepper.
Add wine and broth to a saucepan. Heat over medium-high heat, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium and let boil until reduced to ¾ cup.
Meanwhile season both sides of chicken with salt and pepper. Dredge each side in arrowroot powder.
Melt half of the butter with half of the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and let sear for 4 minutes per side.
Transfer chicken to a plate. Tent with foil. Reduce burner temperature to medium heat. Melt remaining butter with olive oil, add mushrooms.
Saute mushrooms, just tossing occasionally, until mushrooms have shrunk and are golden brown. Add garlic during last 1 minute of sauteing.
Remove pan from heat then pour in marsala reduction, thyme and oregano. Return to heat, bring to a simmer, while scraping up browned bits from the bottom.
Off heat stir in heavy cream, season sauce with salt and pepper to taste. Return chicken breasts to pan, spoon sauce over top.
Sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a large saucepan heat the olive oil and onion on medium-high heat. Saute for a couple of minutes until the onions have softened.
Add in the carrots, and cook for 5 minutes until they’re soft. Add in the mushrooms and Italian herb mix, stirring well, and cook for another couple of minutes.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the lentils, cooking for 2-3 minutes. Add in the tomato paste, tomatoes, and ½ cup of water.
Season with salt and give it a good stir and cook for 15 minutes. In a large baking dish-16.54 inches x 9.05 inches, start with the lentil sauce, then the lasagna sheets, followed by bechamel sauce.
Repeat the process and at the end top it with cheese. Bake for 40 minutes or until pasta is cooked through. Remove from oven and let cool for 10 minutes before cutting and serving.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Chop onion and peppers.
Cut broccoli into small florets and slice carrots into small sticks or thin disks.
Slice mushrooms.
Make the sauce: mix all ingredients in a bowl or glass jar. Set aside.
In a large skillet, heat up olive oil.
Add minced garlic and ginger.
Saute for 2 minutes, stirring often.
Add onions and peppers.
Saute until onions are translucent, about 5 minutes. Stir often.
Add cabbage and cook until soft.
Add broccoli and carrots.
Cook until broccoli is vibrant green and softer.
Add mushrooms, beans and spinach.
Cook until the spinach is wilted.
Add stir fry sauce and cook until it thickens.
Serve stir fry over rice or noodles.
Pour the broth into a saucepan set over medium heat. Add bay leaves, reduce heat to a simmer and keep warm.
Meanwhile, heat oil in a large high-sided skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms and season with salt and pepper. Saute for 6 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
Reduce heat to low. In same pan, heat 2 tbsp butter  until melted. Add onion, celery, carrot and garlic. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
Add barley, salt and pepper. Cook for 2 minutes Stir in the wine and cook for 2 more minutes.
Ladle in 1 cup broth. Cook, stirring constantly, until almost all the liquid is absorbed. Continue to add all broth, 1 cup at a time.
Cook, stirring often for 35 minutes. Stir in the mushrooms. Cook for 3 minutes. Stir in remaining butter, parsley and Parmesan cheese. Serve immediately.
Cut about a 3-inch wide pocket into the thick side of each breast almost to the other side, but do not cut all the way through.
Heat butter in a large skillet over medium-low heat. Place the spinach in the skillet and saute the spinach for about 30 seconds. Remove from heat.
In a medium bowl, mix the cheese and spinach until combined. Stuff the chicken breasts with the spinach mixture and secure the breasts with toothpicks.
Place the breasts in the hot skillet and leave for 8 minutes. Turn the breasts over and cook for another 8 minutes. Remove from the pan and set aside.
Saute onion on olive oil until softened, about 3 minutes. Add mushrooms, salt, and pepper. Cook until mushrooms have released juices.
Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add the chicken broth and cream. Simmer for 5 minutes or until reduced by half.
Let simmer for 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Add stuffed chicken breasts and serve.
Position racks in the upper and lower thirds of your oven.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Lightly coat 2 rimmed baking sheets with nonstick spray.
Coat a 9 × 13 inch baking dish with nonstick spray and set aside.

Arrange sliced eggplant in a single layer on the 2 sheet pans—if some of your end-most eggplant slices are very curved, trim off the eggplant outermost curvy portion so that the slices lay mostly flat.
Brush 2 ½ tsp of the oil over both sides of all of the slices.
Sprinkle ½ tsp kosher salt and ¼ tsp pepper over the top.
Roast the eggplant until it is soft and golden, about 25 minutes, flipping the slices over and swapping the pans’ positions once halfway through.
Remove the slices from the oven and reduce the oven temperature to 350°F.
Meanwhile, in a large skillet heat the remaining ½ tbsp oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced mushrooms.
Saute until the mushrooms are soft, about 7 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, next ½ tsp kosher salt, and oregano.
Cook for another 2 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat.
Add the marinara pasta sauce and stir to combine.
In a large bowl add the ricotta, ¼ cup Parmesan, egg, and remaining ½ tsp salt and remaining ¼ tsp pepper.
Mix well, until the mixture is evenly combined.
Stir in the spinach, using a fork to break it up and distribute it as evenly with the ricotta mixture as possible.
Spoon half of the mushroom marinara sauce on the bottom of the prepared baking dish and spread it into an even layer.
Lay 4 eggplant slices on top followed by all of the ricotta mixture.
Layer on another 4 slices of eggplant and finish with the remaining mushroom marinara sauce.
Top with the mozzarella and remaining ¼ cup Parmesan.
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, until the cheese is melted and the lasagna is hot and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with fresh herbs.
Let rest 5 to 10 minutes, then serve.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a bowl add all the mushrooms and veggies. Drizzle with olive oil then add Italian seasoning, salt, pepper and toss until well combined.
Dump the veggies to a baking sheet and place in the preheat oven. Roast for 20 minutes or until mushrooms are golden brown.
Mix all together and garnish with fresh parsley before serving.
In a small bowl combine the pesto with 1.5 tbsp olive oil to thin it out.
In another bowl, mix the cherry tomatoes with about 1 tbsp of the pesto/olive oil mixture and set aside.
Heat up ½ tbsp olive oil in a frying pan and add the sliced mushrooms.
Let cook without disturbing them until they are browned on one side, then add salt and pepper and give them a stir.
Cook over medium heat, stirring often until they are done.
Remove to a plate and set aside.
In the same pan, fry the sliced halloumi cheese until it is golden on both sides then remove to a plate.
Heat up the last ½ tbsp olive oil in a pan and fry the four eggs to your liking.
Toast the bread, and lay each flat on a plate. Spread each piece with the remaining olive oil and pesto mixture.
Top each piece of toast with a handful of arugula, half of the tomatoes, half of the mushrooms, half of the avocado, half of the halloumi, and one fried egg.
Season with pepper then serve warm.
Slice tops off peppers and place peppers on a microwave-safe plate. Remove stems from pepper tops and discard.
Dice flesh from pepper tops and set aside 1/2 of the diced peppers, save remaining diced peppers for another use.
Microwave peppers on high for 3 minutes until partially cooked.
Place frozen riced cauliflower in a microwave-safe bowl and microwave on high for 4 minutes, stirring halfway through.
Heat olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat until hot, about 1 minute.
Add beef, mushrooms, carrots, celery, onion, and reserved diced peppers and cook, about 5 minutes.
Stir in Italian seasoning, red pepper flakes, oregano, salt, and pepper, cook for 1 minute.
Add cooked riced cauliflower and garlic; stir and cook, 2 to 3 more minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Add drained tomatoes, tomato paste, and fresh basil to the skillet and cook until nearly all liquid is absorbed, about 5 more minutes.
Turn off heat and allow beef mixture to cool slightly, about 5 minutes.
Stuff beef mixture evenly into peppers. Stand peppers upright in a baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven until peppers are tender and filling is hot, about 30 minutes.
cider vinegar: Combine yogurt, sugar, lemon juice, vinegar, salt, pepper and hot sauce in a large bowl. Add cabbage and toss until fully coated.
Combine chicken and barbecue sauce in a medium microwavable bowl, toss until chicken is coated. Microwave on High until heated through, about 1 minute.
Heat tortillas according to package directions. Fill each tortilla with some chicken and top with the slaw and avocado. Garnish with cilantro and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Sprinkle pork  with salt and pepper. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
In the same skillet, saute green pepper and onion in remaining 1 teaspoon oil until almost tender, about 2 minutes.
Stir in broth, pineapple, tomato sauce and vinegar. Bring to a boil. Return pork to the pan. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 5 to 7 minutes.
Brush the tuna with a little oil.
Heat a non-stick pan.
Add the tuna and cook for 1 min each side, or a min or so longer for a thicker fillet.
Transfer to a plate to rest. Halve and stone the avocado and scoop the flesh into a small bowl.
Add the mustard powder and vinegar, then mash well so that the mixture is smooth like mayonnaise. Stir in the capers.
Spoon into two small dishes and put on serving plates with the lettuce leaves, and tomatoes. Slice the tuna.
Spoon some ‘mayo’ on the lettuce leaves and top with tuna and cherry tomatoes and a few extra capers.
To eat, roll up into little wraps.
Combine onion and both bell peppers in the bowl of a food processor, pulse until blended into a uniform size but not liquid.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onion-bell pepper mixture, carrot, and zucchini. Cook and stir until soft and heated through, 6 to 8 minutes.
Add broth and lentils; bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered and stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender, about 15 minutes.
Stir in tomatoes, chili powder, mustard, vinegar, maple syrup, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper, bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer until thickened, about 10 minutes. Serve on buns.
Peel, wash and cut the potatoes into the desired shape.
I fancied slices this time. Chunky chips or wedges will be as good.
Season the fish with salt and pepper and place some herbs and a little garlic in the inside of the fish.
Take two ideally non-tick pans out for simultaneous cooking, place both on medium fire.
When both pans are fairly hot, add a drizzle coconut or olive oil into each pan and add the fish in one and the cut potatoes into the other.
Cover the potatoes but not the fish.
Lower the heat for the fish and cook for at least 4-7 min on one side, until the fish will no longer stick to the pan.
Meanwhile, after 4-5 min of cooking the potatoes covered, remove the lid, season them and give them a good old tossing.
Using a wooden spoon it's also okay at least at this point as the potatoes are still sturdy.
Ideally, would be to avoid using any utensils so that potatoes remain whole and are not smashed.
If they stick do allow them to cook further until they get a crusty layer on the outside.
They will release naturally. Add a drizzle of extra oil if you feel it is necessary.
Once the fish cooked on one side turn gently on the other side and cook for a further 5-6 min on medium fire.
If you are using fillet, this will cook in half the time.
Give the potatoes another toss and cook uncovered for another 5-6 minutes tossing them occasionally until they have cooked thoroughly.
Paste the garlic and add to the potatoes and the fish in the last minute of cooking. Season more if you think it's needed.
Boil the lentils following pack instructions, drain, rinse well, then drain thoroughly.
Meanwhile, mix the citrus juices, vinegar and a pinch of salt in a salad bowl, then toss in the onion. Whisk together the oil, cumin, garlic and chutney.
Toss into the onions with the cooled lentils, coriander, tomatoes, and plenty of seasoning. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 400°F
Thaw fish, if frozen. Cut into bite-sized strips; set aside. Lightly grease a baking sheet.
Place polenta on baking sheet. Lightly coat with nonstick cooking spray. Bake 15 to 20 minutes or until golden brown on the edges.
In a large nonstick skillet, cook catfish in hot oil over medium heat for 4 to 6 minutes or until lightly browned and pieces flake when tested with a fork.
