Sprinkle with 1 tsp of the Cajun seasoning. On a serving platter arrange lettuce. Top with polenta, catfish, tomato and sweet pepper.
In a small bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, vinegar, mustard, and remaining 1 tsp Cajun seasoning. Serve dressing with salad.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the chopped bacon until it becomes crispy.
Once the bacon is crispy, remove it from the skillet and set it aside, leaving the bacon drippings in the pan.
Add the halved Brussels sprouts to the skillet with the bacon drippings.
Sauté the Brussels sprouts in the bacon drippings until they are tender and slightly caramelized, about 8-10 minutes.
Season the Brussels sprouts with salt and pepper to taste.
Add the cooked crispy bacon back to the skillet with the Brussels sprouts.
Pour cider vinegar over the Brussels sprouts and bacon.
Toss everything together for about a minute until the vinegar coats the sprouts and bacon evenly.
Remove the skillet from heat and transfer the Brussels sprouts and bacon to a plate.
Enjoy your flavorful Brussels sprouts with bacon and cider vinegar as a delicious single-serving dish!
Preheat oven to 450°F.
Place beef bones, carrots, leek, onion, and garlic on a roasting pan or rimmed baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes.
Toss the contents of the pan and continue to roast until deeply browned, about 20 minutes more.
Fill a large (at least 6-quart) stockpot with 12 cups of water (preferably filtered).
Add celery, bay leaves, peppercorns, and vinegar.
Scrape the roasted bones and vegetables into the pot along with any juices.
Add more water if necessary to cover bones and vegetables.
Cover the pot and bring to a gentle boil.

Reduce heat to a very low simmer and cook with lid slightly ajar, skimming foam and excess fat occasionally, for at least 8 but up to 24 hours on the stovetop. (Do not leave on stovetop unattended, simply cool and continue simmering the next day.)
The longer you simmer it, the better your broth will be.
Add more water if necessary to ensure bones and vegetables are fully submerged.
Alternately, you can cook the broth in a slow cooker on low for the same amount of time.
Remove the pot from the heat and let cool slightly.
Strain broth using a fine-mesh sieve and discard bones and vegetables.
Let continue to cool until barely warm, then refrigerate in smaller containers overnight.
Remove solidified fat from the top of the chilled broth.
Broth can be stored for up to 5 days in the refrigerator and up to 6 months in the freezer.
In your slow cooker, combine the onion, ketchup, apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, tomato paste, paprika, Worcestershire, salt and pepper.
Whisk to combine.
Add the chicken thighs and make sure they are all evenly spread out and coated with the sauce ingredients.
Place the lid on the slow cooker and turn to high heat for the Shredded BBQ Chicken to be done in 4-5 hours, and low for Shredded BBQ Chicken to be done in 7-8 hours.
Once the timer is up, remove the lid and using 2 forks, shred the chicken while still in the crock pot.
Turn the crockpot to warm and serve the shredded BBQ chicken in burger buns with lettuce.
In a medium skillet bring water to a boil. Add eggs and cook until hard-boiled. Peel them and slice.
Cook in the steamer casserole for 5 minutes. Drain the vegetables and then plunge them into cold water with ice to stop them cooking.
Place all of the ingredients in a large salad bowl, wash and drain the spinach leaves and add those too.
Mix the mustard and vinegar in a bowl. Then stir in the olive oil, salt and pepper.
Pour the dressing into the salad bowl, toss carefully and serve.
vegan cheese: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Spread taters on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10 minutes. Turn taters over, bake until crispy, about 10 minutes more.
Transfer taters to a shallow, oven-safe serving dish. Spread vegan bacon bits and sprinkle vegan cheese on top.
Set oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler.
Place baking dish in the oven. Broil, watching closely to prevent burning, until vegan cheese is melted, about 3 minutes.
Top with sour cream and green onions.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add pasta and cook for 8 to 10 minutes or until al dente; drain.
In a 1 1/2 quart glass casserole dish, layer noodles, 1/2 of cheese, 1/2 of the tuna, 1/2 of peas and 1/2 of the soup.
Repeat the layering with the remaining ingredients. Top the casserole with bread crumbs and drizzle with flaxseed oil.
Bake for 10 to 15 minutes.
Mix all ingredients together. Season with salt, and olive oil, and toss. Serve.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
In a mixing bowl, onion, drained tuna, vegan cheese, and mayonnaise. Mix thoroughly.
Spread tuna mixture on slices of French bread to form a sandwich. Place sandwiches on a cookie sheet. Bake in a preheated oven for 10 minutes.
In a pan, melt some butter. Add the shallots and cook 3 minutes. Add all the mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the gnocchi and cook at high heat. Add the spices. Add the soy milk a bit of broth. Add the spinach. Continue to cook at high heat.
Add the cheese, vegan butter and simmer for 5 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Spray a 12 muffin tin or line with muffin cups.
Combine flax and water, set aside to thicken. In small bowl, whisk together cashew milk and apple cider vinegar and set aside.
In large mixing bowl, combine flour, baking powder, salt, and grated cheese. Add flax mixture, cashew milk mixture, and melted butter, stirring to combine.
Spoon batter into muffin tins, filling about half way. Add  broccoli florets to each muffin tin.
Bake at for 25 minutes. Serve hot or cooled.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
In a very large saucepan, cook the cauliflower in boiling salted water for 3 minutes. Then add the broccoli and cook for 3 minutes more.
Drain well and transfer to a large ovenproof dish.
To make the cheese sauce, melt the butter in a small pan. When foaming, add flour and mustard powder, and cook for 1 min, stirring constantly.
Take off the heat and add the milk, a little at a time, stirring constantly to get rid of any lumps. Once half the milk has been added, return to the heat and add the remaining milk.
Keep stirring and bring to the boil, then turn down to a simmer and cook for 2 mins, until thickened and smooth.
Add most of the cheese and some seasoning then take off the heat.
Pour the sauce over the vegetables and sprinkle with the remaining cheese.
Cook in the oven for 30 minutes until bubbling.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Oil a baking dish.
Place the potatoes in a pot, cover with cold water, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Turn the heat to medium-low, and boil the potatoes until tender, about 25 minutes, drain.
Stir the vegan sour cream, soy milk, olive oil, vegan cream cheese, and salt into the potatoes.
Mash with a potato masher until smooth and fluffy. Set the potatoes aside.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat, and cook and stir the onion, carrots, celery, peas, and tomato for about 10 minutes.
Stir in the Italian seasoning, garlic, and pepper. Reduce the heat to medium-low, and crumble the vegetarian ground beef substitute into the skillet.
Cook and stir, breaking up the meat substitute, until the mixture is hot, about 5 minutes.
Spread the vegetarian meat substitute mixture into the bottom of the baking dish, and top with the mashed potatoes, smoothing them into an even layer.
Sprinkle the potatoes with the shredded vegan cheese. Bake in the preheated oven until the cheese is melted and slightly browned and the casserole is hot, about 20 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
First prepare the enchilada sauce. In a medium sauce pot combine the oil, arrowroot powder, and chili powder. Heat the mixture until it begins to bubble. Cook 1 minute.
Slowly pour in some water while whisking. Add the tomato paste, cumin, garlic powder, cayenne pepper, cocoa powder, and salt.
Whisk until smooth and continue to heat over a medium flame. Let the sauce come up to a simmer. Once thickened, turn off the heat and set the sauce aside.
In a bowl, combine beans, avocado, tomato and corn. Stir everything together. Season with salt and garlic powder. Coat a casserole dish with non-stick spray.
Fill each tortilla with some filling and roll tightly. Once all of the filled enchiladas are in the dish, pour the enchilada sauce over top. Top with cheese.
Bake for 25 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Mix warm water and egg replacer together in a large bowl. Stir in vegan cheese, tofu, nutritional yeast, salt, and garlic powder.
Add onion, pecans, Italian seasoning, basil, and sage, mix until well combined. Stir in enough bread crumbs to reach a moist, crumbly texture.
Form mixture into 1 1/2-inch balls and place in an 8-inch baking pan.
Mix grape jelly, tomato sauce, olive oil, vinegar, garlic, and oregano in a separate bowl. Pour over meatballs.
Bake in the preheated oven until meatballs are firm, 35 to 40 minutes.
Heat buttery spread and oil in a large, heavy saucepan over medium heat. Add onions, garlic, and salt.
Cook for 5 minutes, stirring often. Reduce heat to very low and cook, stirring occasionally, 50 to 60 minutes.
Add flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 3 to 4 minutes. Blend in broth, sherry, and Dijon mustard.
Cover and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes. Stir in sherry vinegar.
Set an oven rack about 6 inches from the heat source and preheat the oven's broiler. Ladle soup into 3 oven-proof bowls.
Top each bowl with a slice of toast and some vegan cheese. Broil until cheesy is bubbly and golden, 1 to 2 minutes. Serve hot.
Mix together ground flax and water and set aside to thicken. Add potatoes to a pot of water and bring to a boil. Cook for about 10 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Add onions and carrots to a pan over medium heat with a splash of oil, and cook until onions turn translucent.
Add green onion and mushrooms and season with salt and pepper. Stir and turn off heat.
Add white beans to a large bowl and use a fork or potato masher to mash the beans. Add potatoes to the bowl and mash, again leaving some chunks for texture.
Add in veggies, vegan cheese, and flax mixture. Mix until well combined. Mix together milk and arrowroot powder. Form the potato mixture into patties.
Roll each patty in flour, dip in plant milk mixture, then coat with panko. Brush the korokke with oil. Place in the air fryer.
Air fry at 350°F for 15 minutes. Serve immediately.
Combine water, TVP, and soy sauce in a large pan over high heat. Cook and stir until water is mostly evaporated.
Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic, sazon, adobo sauce, oregano, and cumin. Continue cooking until TVP starts to firm up, about 5 minutes.
Add cayenne and liquid smoke and cook until the mixture starts sticking to the pan, about 5 minutes. Stir in tomato paste and mix vegan meat well.
Mix flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda together in a large bowl. Make a well in the center and add water and 1/4 cup vegetable oil.
Mix with your hands and knead until all flour is incorporated. Cut dough into 3 pieces and roll into balls.
Place dough balls in the bowl and cover, let sit for 3 to 4 minutes. Roll a dough ball out into a 6-inch circle and fill with vegan meat and shredded cheese.
Dip a finger in water and moisten the edges of the dough before folding over and crimping empanada edges using a fork. Repeat.
Heat 1 cup vegetable oil in a deep pan over medium-high heat. Add 2 or 3 empanadas as a time to the hot oil and fry for about 5 minutes total.
Transfer fried empanadas to a plate lined with paper towels to absorb any excess oil.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
In a small mixing bowl, combine tuna, egg, parsley, cheese, salt and pepper with flaxseed oil.
Flatten the crescent rolls one at a time, and place a small amount of tuna mixture on each roll. Fold over and seal edges.
Arrange stuffed crescent rolls in the preheated oven. Bake in preheated oven until the dumplings are richly browned, about 30 minutes.
pasta water: Bring a large pot of water to the boil, salt heavily. Drop the sliced asparagus in and blanch for 1-2 minutes. Remove them from the water and set aside.
Bring the pot of water back to a boil again, cook the fettuccine until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water, then drain the pasta and set it aside.
In a sauce pan, add the olive oil, and heat it on medium heat. Add the minced garlic and saute it for 30 seconds. Add the asparagus and cooked pasta, stir well to coat the pasta thoroughly.
Season to taste with salt and pepper and serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
, Boil pasta for 3-5 min or to al dente.
Drain pasta and reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
While pasta is cooking, in a large saucepan (4 quart) or medium pot melt butter over medium heat then add flour, cook 1 minute whisking constantly.
While whisking slowly pour in milk, add in mustard and garlic powder.
Increase heat slightly, then bring mixture to a gentle simmer whisking constantly.
Reduce heat to low add in cream cheese and whisk until melted.
Remove saucepan from heat add in the shredded cheeses half at a time, and whisk to melt, while returning to warm heat as needed to melt fully.
Season sauce with salt to taste.
Toss pasta with cheese sauce, thin with pasta water a splash at a time as needed.
Serve immediately.
In a small bowl, combine salt, Creole seasoning, paprika powder, onion powder, garlic powder, and flour.
Dredge the chicken in the seasoning mixture and shake off excess.
Reserve any leftover spice/flour mix for the sauce.
Preheat large saute pan, add olive oil and melt ghee over medium-high heat.
Cook the chicken tenders until done, about 8 minutes.
Add more paprika and heavy cream into the skillet.
Bring to a boiling point.
Lower heat and simmer 3-4 minutes.
Taste the sauce and adjust seasoning to your liking.
Boil pasta in salty water, drain, but do not rinse.
Reserve ½ cup of pasta water.
If you think the sauce needs thinning, add reserved pasta water to the sauce.
Add it slow - as much as you would like to have according to your liking.
Add cooked pasta into the sauce and stir until fully coated.
Add chicken into the skillet with pasta to reheat.
Serve with sauce spooned over chicken and pasta, and sprinkled with chopped parsley.
pork tenderloin: Heat the oil in a large pan and cook the onions and garlic for 2 minutes.
Add the pork and sausage and fry for 3 minutes until the pork turns creamy white.
Stir in the coconut milk, water, and curry paste and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the soy sauce, fish sauce, cook for a minute and serve.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
Place the diced potato on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and drizzle with olive oil. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Roast the potatoes in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes until they start to soften.
While the potatoes are roasting, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Season the pork tenderloin with salt, pepper, and minced garlic.
Sear the pork tenderloin in the skillet for about 3-4 minutes on each side until browned.
Remove the pork from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the halved Brussels sprouts and cook for about 5-7 minutes until they start to caramelize.
Return the pork tenderloin to the skillet with the Brussels sprouts.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the pork and Brussels sprouts and sprinkle with lemon zest.
Continue to cook for another 5-7 minutes until the pork is cooked through and the Brussels sprouts are tender.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil, cook the linguine at a boil until tender yet firm to the bite, 8 to 9 minutes then drain.
Whisk soy sauce, vinegar, cornstarch, sugar, and sesame oil together in a small bowl.
Heat canola oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat; cook and stir spinach and onion in the hot oil until onion is softened, about 2 minutes.
Add pork, pepper,garlic, and ginger, cook until pork is no longer pink, about 2 minutes.
Pour soy sauce mixture over pork mixture, cook and stir until sauce thickens, about 1 minute.
Remove skillet from heat. Add linguine to the mixture; toss to coat. Sprinkle with green onions.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in a skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the thinly sliced pork to the skillet and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until it's no longer pink.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and stir-fry for an additional 30 seconds until fragrant.
Toss in the sliced bell pepper and chopped bok choy. Stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until the vegetables are slightly tender but still crisp.
Drizzle the soy sauce over the stir-fry and mix well to coat everything evenly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the pork is fully cooked and the vegetables are tender, remove the skillet from heat.
In a large saucepan, heat the coconut milk over medium heat until it reaches a gentle boil. Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes. Set aside.

In a separate skillet or wok, heat a small amount of oil over medium-high heat. Add the curry paste and sliced pork, stirring frequently for about 5 minutes until the pork is browned and coated in the curry paste.
Pour the warmed coconut milk into the skillet with the pork and curry paste, add the eggplant, peas, red pepper, fish sauce, and salt. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Serve garnished with some sliced bell pepper and basil leaves.
Place the oil and red curry paste in a large saucepan and fry over medium-high heat, stirring for 30 seconds. Add pork and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the lemongrass, stock, coconut milk, and ginger and bring to a simmer. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the prawns and cook for 2 minutes. Stir in the lime juice and fish sauce to taste.
Ladle the soup into bowls and serve.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over high heat. Season the pork with 1 tsp salt. Stir-fry the pork until golden brown all over, aroung 5 minutes.
In a small bowl mix the soy sauce, rice vinegar, garlic and ginger. Transfer all of the pork pieces back to the skillet, along with the diced pineapple and pepper.
Pour the sauce over the pork and simmer sauce, stirring often, until sauce thickens and slightly reduces, about 3 minutes.
Serve immediately, topped with sesame seeds.
Melt the butter over medium heat in a large saute pan. Add the spinach and onion and cook until the spinach is wilted down and the onion is soft, about 4 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds, until fragrant. Remove to a bowl and set aside.
Season the butterflied and flattened pork tenderloin with salt and pepper, and then thinly and evenly spread the cheese on top. Top with the spinach mixture.
Tightly roll up the pork tenderloin, letting it sit on the seam. Lay out your bacon slices, slightly overlapping the edges, until it's the same width as the length of the pork tenderloin.
Wrap the bacon around the tenderloin, then secure with toothpicks.
Place the tenderloin on a wire rack set over a rimmed baking sheet lined with foil. Roast at 350ºF for about 1 hour.
Heat oil in a skillet. Add sliced onion, minced ginger, and garlic. Saute for a minute until it is aromatic.
Add red curry paste. Saute for 3 minutes until oil starts oozing out on the side, and the mixture is aromatic.
Add coconut milk, lemon peel, brown sugar, fish sauce. Mix well and let everything come to a boil. Simmer and let it cook for 3 minutes.
Add pork and let it simmer for 15 minutes. Add lemon juice and basil and serve.
Combine chili paste, honey, ginger, garlic, two tablespoons soy sauce, and teaspoons sesame oil in a resealable plastic bag.
Meanwhile bring a large pot of water to the boil and cook the rice to the package instructions.
Add the pork to the marinade, seal the bag, and shake to thoroughly coat. Let sit at least 10 minutes and up to 2 hours.
Heat 1 tablespoon coconut oil oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high. Add half of the pork in a single layer, season very lightly with salt.
Cook the pork for about a minute, increasing heat to high if needed, until dark brown underneath. Repeat for the 2nd half of the marinated pork.
Wipe the skillet clean, add the remaining coconut oil and heat over medium-high heat. Add the carrots, cook until they begin to soften, about 3 minutes.
Combine vinegar and the remaining soy sauce and sesame oil in a small bowl.
Divide the rice between bowls, add pork and carrots, and enjoy!
plain flour: Cut Chicken Fillets into 1-1½ inch size pieces.
Combine ginger, garlic, soy sauce, rice wine in a bowl, add chicken pieces, massage, and set aside.
Place 1 egg in ½ cup and add cold water to make it ½ cupful.
Combine flour in a separate bowl, and egg & cold water, and mix lightly to make tempura batter.
Heat Oil from 325°F to 350°F.
Coat the chicken pieces with flour, dip into tempura batter and fry until batter is light golden and crispy.
Take them out and lay them on a rack to drain the oil.
Place all the sauce ingredients in a saucepan and bring to a boil, then simmer for a few minutes.
Half fill a bowl with freshly cooked rice, place chicken, lettuce leaves and spring onion on top, drizzle the sauce over and enjoy.
In a shallow bowl, season flour with salt and pepper. In another shallow bowl, combine bread crumbs and coconut. Place eggs and flaxseed oil in a third shallow bowl.
Working with one at a time, dip prawns in flour, then eggs, then coconut mixture.
Place prawns in the basket of an air fryer and heat to 400°F . Bake until prawns are golden and cooked through, 10 to 12 minutes. Work in batches as necessary.
Place the mashed potato in a bowl.
Flake the salmon into the potatoes, add the parsley and lemon zest and season with salt and freshly ground pepper.
Mix well. Shape the mixture into 8 small cakes with damp hands. Dust in flour.
You can make the fishcakes in advance and chill at this stage until ready to cook.
Heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan and fry the fish cakes for 4-5 mins on each side over a medium heat until cooked through.
Serve with salad.
Heat the oven to 400°F.
In a large bowl mix together all the ingredients for the filling
To make the pastry, put the flour in a large bowl, then put the ghee and water into a small pan and heat gently until the ghee melts.
Bring just to the boil and then stir into the flour using a wooden spoon.
When the mixture is cool enough to handle, (it should still feel very warm) knead well until smooth.
Cut off 1/4 of the dough, wrap in cling film and reserve for the lid.
Roll out the remaining dough to a circle and then place in the base of a non-stick 20cm springform cake tin.
Working quickly while the dough is warm and pliable, press the dough evenly over the base and up the sides of the tin.
Make sure there are no holes. Fill with the meat mixture and pack down well. Roll out the dough for the lid.
Place on top of the pie. Pinch all around the edge to seal the pie. Make a hole for steam in the centre, using the handle of a wooden spoon.
Cook in the oven for 30 mins then reduce the heat to 350°F and cook for 90 minutes.
Brush the top with beaten egg and return to the oven for a further 20 mins.
Leave until cold.
Soak the gelatine in cold water for about 5 mins, then remove and squeeze out the excess water.
Heat the stock until almost boiling. Remove from the heat and stir in the gelatine. Leave to cool to room temperature.
Use a small funnel to pour the stock into the pie through the hole in the top.
Pour in a little at a time allowing a few seconds before each addition. Place in the fridge to set overnight.
Melt the butter with the olive oil in a large pan. Add the onions and fry with the lid on for 10 mins until soft.
Add the garlic cloves for the final few minutes of the onions’ cooking time, then sprinkle in the plain flour and stir well.
Increase the heat and keep stirring as you gradually add the wine, followed by the beef stock. Cover and simmer for 15-20 mins.
To serve, turn on the grill, and toast the bread. Ladle the soup into heatproof bowls.
Put a slice or two of toast on top of the bowls of soup, and pile on the gruyere. Grill until melted.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat two tablespoons of oil in a heavy pan and cook the onion, celery, capsicum and chili for 10 minutes until softened.
Add the sausage and okra and cook for five minutes or until browned.
To make the roux, heat remaining two tablespoons of oil in a saucepan.
Gradually sprinkle in the flour and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, for 15 minutes or until caramel colored.
Gradually add the stock, stirring constantly.
Add the garlic, tomatoes, tomato paste, dried oregano and cayenne.
Season generously and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes.
Add the vegetable and sausage mixture and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes or until the okra is cooked. Skim off any excess oil.
Add the prawns and parsley, simmer for 5 minutes, and serve with rice.
vanilla essence: Peel and finely grate the carrots. Pour the almond milk into a saucepan, add the carrot, raisins, cinnamon and vanilla essence.
Simmer gently on a low heat for 5 minutes until the carrots are tender, giving it a stir every now and then.
Stir in the rolled oats and carry on stirring until the porridge starts to thicken up. Divide between your bowls and top with almonds. Serve immediately.
In a bowl, mix the white chocolate with the matcha and the vegetable whipping cream, whisk until it integrates and freeze.
In a bowl mix the flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, matcha, oil, vanilla essence, almond milk until all ingredients are integrated.
Grease your molds with butter and cover with matcha, then form small balls of 10 grams.
Add the mixture by half, place the ganache ball and cover with the rest, bake for 18 minutes and remove from mold.
Sprinkle with matcha, white chocolate, apple, kiwi and blueberries.
Combine chia seeds, milk, vanilla essence. Stir well until combined. Leave for 5 minutes.
Stir again to avoid any chia-lumps. Cover and leave in fridge for 2 hours.
Serve topped with blueberries, strawberries and pomegranate seeds.
In a pot, mix together the quinoa, coconut milk, pea protein powder, water, vanilla extract, cinnamon and maple syrup. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low and cook for 20 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix together the shredded coconut and chia seeds.
To serve, pour the quinoa into each bowl, add the raspberries and garnish with the chia and shredded coconut.
peanut oil: Preheat oven to 425°F.
This can be done in a bamboo steamer similar to below, but it is easier en papillote.
Get yourself a large piece of parchment paper and fold it in half.
Pat the fish dry and remove any bones.
Sprinkle all over with salt and place on in the center of one half of the parchment.
Sprinkle the fish with the cooking wine.
Working from one end of the parchment, fold it over and roll it up and along to form a sealed parchment bundle - en papillote.
Place in a oven on a cookie sheet. Bake for 8-12 minutes, depending on the thickness of your fish.
Remove from the oven, open parchment and transfer gently to a plate including any juice.
Top with half of the green onion.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instuctions on the packaging.
Mix rice wine, soy sauce, and cornstarch together in a bowl. Add pork, toss until evenly mixed.
Preheat a wok or deep saucepan over medium-high heat. Pour in peanut oil.
Cook pork in the hot oil in batches until evenly browned, 2 to 3 minutes per batch.
Transfer to a plate using a slotted spoon. Cook onion in the remaining oil until softened, 2 to 3 minutes.
Mix curry powder and water in a bowl to form a paste.
Stir curry paste, spring onion, and salt into the wok. Add rice, stirring to break up any lumps. Stir in peas and pepper.
Return pork to the wok. cook and stir until rice and pork are heated through, 4 to 5 minutes.
Cook the noodles following packet directions. Drain well.

Meanwhile, heat the oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat. Stir-fry the chicken, in 2 batches, for 2 minutes or until golden brown and cooked through, transferring to a plate between batches.
Combine the chicken, onion, garlic, ginger and broccoli florets and capsicum in the wok or pan. Stir-fry for 4 minutes or until broccoli is tender.
Add the oyster sauce with 1/4 cup water. Stir-fry for 2 mins or until heated through.
Divide noodles and stir-fry among serving bowls. Serve immediately
Remove any old leaves from the cabbage, cut stem off base, slice finely and add to a salad bowl.
Add carrot matchsticks and onion.
Toast almond flakes in a pan on medium until they turn a light golden brown, about 3-4 minutes, and add to salad.
Make the dressing by combining salt, pepper, 2 tsp peanut oil, vinegar, soy sauce, lime juice, garlic, ginger, and honey in a small bowl or glass jar, and stir thoroughly to combine. Set aside.
Cut tofu in half, then into small cubes.
Add 2 tsp peanut oil to fry pan, add tofu, season with chili flakes, salt and pepper.
Bring the pan to medium heat and cook until edges the tofu are a light golden brown and crispy.
Add tofu to salad bowl.
Dress and toss to combine. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Stir balsamic vinegar, lemon juice, and soy sauce with salt, paprika, pepper, and red pepper flakes until the salt has dissolved.
Stir in garlic, green onions and peanut oil until well combined.
Pour marinade into a resealable plastic bag or glass bowl. Add salmon and pineapple to marinade and gently toss to coat. Place into refrigerator and marinate.
Prepare an outdoor grill for medium-high heat.
Drain excess marinade from salmon fillets and pineapple. Grill until firm and opaque, about 4 minutes per side.
Mix vegetarian beef crumbles, napa cabbage, soy sauce, green onion, chili garlic sauce, sesame oil, salt, and pepper together in a bowl to make filling.
Place a spoonful of the filling in the center of each pot sticker wrapper.
Brush edges of each wrapper with water, fold over to enclose filling. Squeeze edges and crimp together to seal.
Heat peanut oil in a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Place potstickers flat-side down in the skillet; cook until golden brown, about 3 minutes.
Flip potstickers. Pour in 1/4 cup water; cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue cooking until water evaporates, about 5 minutes.
Combine mince, coriander, spring onion, soy sauce, rice wine or sherry, ginger and garlic in a bowl. Season with pepper.
Place 1 wonton wrapper on a clean work surface. Top with 1 tsp of mince mixture. Brush edges with a little water. Bring the corners together to enclose the filling and pinch the centre to seal.
Repeat with remaining wrappers and mince mixture.
Heat oil in a large frying pan over high heat. Remove from heat. Arrange wontons over the base of the frying pan. Cook over high heat for 2 minutes or until the bases are golden.
Pour 1/4 water evenly over wontons. Cover and cook for 3 to 4 minutes or until the wontons are cooked through and water evaporates.
In a large bowl, whisk together lime juice, peanut oil, soy sauce, and grated ginger.
Gently toss in the spiralized zucchini and carrot. Serve immediately.
Place the noodles in a large bowl and cover with warm water. Soak for at least 20 minutes until soft. Drain in a colander. With kitchen scissors, cut into 6-inch lengths and set aside.

Combine the broth, 2 teaspoons of the soy sauce and 1 tablespoon of the rice wine or sherry and sugar in a small bowl. Combine the garlic, 1 tablespoon of the ginger and the minced jalapeño in another bowl.

In a medium bowl, combine the cornstarch, 1 teaspoon soy sauce, 1 tablespoon wine and 1 tablespoon of the ginger. Stir together well. Lightly salt the shrimp and toss with the cornstarch mixture until coated.
Heat a skillet over high heat and add the oil to the sides of the pan and tilt the pan to distribute.

Add the garlic, ginger and jalapeño, and stir-fry for 10 seconds. Add the peppers, brussel sprouts, spinach and mushroom and stir-fry for 1 minute. Add the shrimp along with any liquid in the bowl, and stir-fry for 3 minutes.

Add the scallions, drained noodles and the broth mixture, and stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add the cilantro and stir-fry for another 30 seconds. Stir in the sesame oil, remove from the heat, top with sesame seeds and serve.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil over medium-high heat in a large sauté pan or wok.
Add chicken and sauté for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and no longer pink on the inside.
Use a slotted spoon to transfer the chicken to a clean plate, and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon oil to the sauté pan, along with the bell pepper, onion, celery and garlic.
Sauté for 5-6 more minutes, stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, as the veggies are sautéing, whisk together the soy sauce, cornstarch, oyster sauce, vinegar, black pepper and ginger in a small bowl until combined.
Once the veggies are ready go to, add the chicken and sauce mixture to the pan, and stir until the veggies and chicken are evenly coated.
Continue to cook for 1-2 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Taste, and season with extra soy sauce and/or black pepper as desired.
Remove from heat, and serve immediately sprinkled with your desired toppings.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Pulse peanuts in food processor (or grind by hand) until they are the consistency of small pebbles. Set aside.
Heat peanut oil in a large Dutch oven or pot over medium-high heat.
Once hot, add lamb and brown on all sides, turning occasionally for 5 minutes. Use a slotted spoon to remove lamb and set aside.
Add sweet potato to the same pot and cook until tender and softened. 10-12 minutes.
Add bay leaf, onion and jalapeno, and cook until softened and tender, 8-10 minutes. During the last 2 minutes, add minced garlic.
Season with salt and pepper.
Add curry and red chili powders, cumin, coriander, cardamom and turmeric, and cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in fire roasted tomatoes and cook for another 4 minutes, then return lamb to the pot.
Slowly stir in coconut milk, cover, reduce heat to low and simmer for 1-2 hours, or until meat is cooked through all the way and sauce has thickened.
Fold in peanuts, taste and, if necessary, adjust seasoning. Plate with white rice and serve hot.
Stir soy sauce, tomato sauce, barbeque sauce, garlic, vinegar, Sriracha, pepper, sesame oil, and ginger together in a saucepan, bring to a boil.
Heat oil in a deep-fryer or large saucepan to 360°F. Season chicken wings with lemon-pepper seasoning.
Season chicken wings with lemon-pepper seasoning
Fry chicken wings in batches of 6 to 8 at a time in hot oil until no longer pink at the bone and the juices run clear, 6 to 8 minutes per batch.
Put cooked wings into a large mixing bowl. Ladle sauce over the wings and toss to coat.
Put the chicken in a bowl, add the corn flour and some seasoning, and toss.
Heat 1 tbsp of the groundnut oil in a wok or large frying pan, add the chicken and cook until golden all over.
Scoop out of the pan with a slotted spoon and add the rest of the oil, spring onions, ginger and pepper.
Stir-fry until the pepper has softened a little then add back the chicken along with the chilli flakes, soy sauce, honey and stock.
Bubble for 2 minutes then add the noodles and sesame oil, and toss.
Sprinkle with a little more spring onions before serving.
romaine lettuce: Preheat your grill or a grill pan to 375°F.
Season the boneless, skinless chicken breast with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Grill the chicken breast for about 6-8 minutes per side, or until it's cooked through and has nice grill marks. Make sure the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
While the chicken is cooking, you can grill the small pita bread pieces until they are crispy and lightly browned.
Once the chicken is done, remove it from the grill and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped romaine lettuce, diced cucumbers, diced tomatoes, diced onions, chopped fresh parsley, and chopped fresh mint.
Add the grilled chicken strips to the salad.
Drizzle the salad with olive oil and fresh lemon juice.
Toss everything together to ensure the salad is evenly coated with the dressing.
In a large salad bowl, combine the chopped Romaine lettuce, chopped cooked chicken breast, chopped hard-boiled eggs, diced tomato, crumbled blue cheese, corn, crumbled bacon, and diced avocado.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste. Drizzle with greek yogurt.
Toss all the ingredients together until well combined and evenly coated with the dressing.
Season the raw chicken breast with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and add a small amount of olive oil to prevent sticking.
Place the seasoned chicken breast in the hot skillet and cook for about 6-8 minutes on each side, or until the chicken is cooked through and no longer pink in the center.
While the chicken is cooking, you can prepare the rest of the salad.
In a large salad bowl, arrange the chopped romaine lettuce as the base.
Once the chicken is cooked, let it rest for a few minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
Place the sliced cooked chicken on top of the lettuce.
Add the chopped hard-boiled eggs, diced tomatoes, diced cucumber, crumbled blue cheese, crumbled bacon, and diced red onion to the salad bowl.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, minced garlic, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the Cobb Salad.
Gently toss everything together until the salad is well coated with the dressing.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper.
Place the seasoned chicken breasts on a baking sheet and roast in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until they are cooked through and no longer pink in the center.
While the chicken is roasting, cook the fusilli pasta according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool.
Once the chicken is done, let it cool slightly before slicing it into thin strips.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooked fusilli pasta, chopped Romaine lettuce, tomato, grated Parmesan cheese, and sliced chicken.
Drizzle the greek yogurt over the salad.
Toss everything together until the pasta, lettuce, and chicken are evenly coated with the dressing.
Preheat a skillet over medium heat and add olive oil.
Season the shrimp and trout with salt and pepper.
Cook the shrimp for about 2-3 minutes on each side until pink and opaque. Remove from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, cook the trout fillet for about 3-4 minutes on each side until cooked through. Remove from skillet and set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the romaine lettuce, diced tomato, sliced hard-boiled egg, cooked shrimp, and flaked trout.
Add grated Parmesan cheese and Caesar dressing to the salad.
Toss everything together gently to combine.
Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cut the pita bread into small squares and place them on a baking sheet.
Bake the pita squares for about 8-10 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown. Remove from the oven and let them cool.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped romaine lettuce, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, diced red onion, chopped parsley, and sliced radishes.
Add the cooled pita squares to the bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad.
Toss everything together gently to combine.
To prepare dressing, combine water. lemon juice, ground black pepper, Sriracha, Worcestershire sauce, olive oil, and garlic.
To prepare salad, combine croutons, 2 tablespoons cheese, shrimp, lettuce, onion and olives in a large bowl. Add dressing; toss well to coat.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped romaine lettuce, diced tomatoes, cucumbers, red bell peppers, red onions, fresh parsley, and fresh mint leaves.
In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and pepper to create the dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad and toss to combine.
Add the toasted and broken pita pieces to the salad and gently toss again.
Preheat the oven to 450°F. Rub the olive oil on all sides of the salmon and place it skin-side down on the prepared baking sheet.
Bake for 10 minutes, or until salmon is cooked through at the thickest part and flakes easily with a fork.
On a serving platter, arrange the lettuce, tomatoes, cucumber slices, and red onion.
Top with the salmon fillet, drizzle with olive oil, wholegrain mustard, and sprinkle with feta cheese.
Toast the bread in the toaster to your desired doneness.
Evenly spread bread slices with dressing, then top with chicken, bacon, tomatoes and lettuce.
To serve, cut sandwiches into halves.
In a large bowl toss together all of the ingredients for the salad.
Set aside.
In a small jar add all of the ingredients for the salad dressing.
Shake until well combined.
Pour salad dressing over the salad, tossing to combine until everything is well coated.
Whisk together the lemon juice, garlic, sumac, pomegranate molasses, dried mint, salt and pepper.
Slowly stream in olive oil, whisking continuously until emulsified.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Set aside.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil on medium heat.
Add the pita bread and season with kosher salt and freshly cracker peppers.
Cook stirring frequently until the pieces the bread crispy and golden in color, about 5-7 minutes.
Set aside.
Place the lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, green peppers, radishes, green onions and parsley in a large serving bowl.
Pour the dressing on top and toss to combine.
Add the fried pita bread to the salad immediately before serving and gently toss again.
Heat the olive oil a non-stick frying pan on medium-high heat. Heavily salt chicken breasts, add to pan and cook for 4-5 minutes on each side, set aside.
In the same pan cook the bacon on medium-low heat until the fat renders out. Transfer the bacon to a paper towel, then cut it into small pieces.
After the chicken has cooled off for 3-5 minutes, slice it into bite-sized pieces and season it with black pepper.
Assemble the two salads, dividing the lettuce, tomatoes, avocado and corn between two bowls. Add the chicken, bacon, a drizzle of olive oil, a squirt of lemon juice, toss and serve.
In a large salad bowl, combine the romaine lettuce, onion, olives, bell peppers, tomatoes, cucumber and cheese.
Whisk together the olive oil, oregano, lemon juice, and black pepper in a small bowl.
Pour dressing over salad, toss and serve.
In a small bowl, combine the ingredients for the marinade (yogurt, lemon juice, garlic, cinnamon, oregano, salt, nutmeg, and clove).
Add chicken to the marinade, coat well, cover and refrigerate for 4-24 hours.
If desired, slice the chicken into strips before marinating to maximize the flavor.
Prepare the yogurt sauce for the sandwiches. Open a 7 oz. container of plain greek yogurt and stir in the garlic, dill, and salt.
Refrigerate until ready to use.
After marinating the chicken, grill until cooked through (I use a George Foreman, cooked for about 7 minutes).
Prepare the vegetables for the sandwich.
Chop, rinse, and drain the lettuce in a colander.
Wash and slice the cucumber and tomato.
Build the chicken shawarma sandwiches.
Spread the yogurt sauce on your flat bread, top with the chicken and vegetables.
Roll the sandwich closed.
You can wrap the sandwich in foil to help it stay closed as you eat.
Preheat a grilled over medium-high heat to 425°F.
Pound chicken to an even thickness using the flat side of a meat mallet.
Brush chicken breasts lightly with about 1 tbsp olive oil, season with salt and pepper.
Brush grilled grates lightly with oil, and grill chicken, rotating once halfway through cooking, until it registers 165 in center on an instant read thermometer, about 6 - 8 minutes.
Transfer to a plate, cover with foil, let rest 10 minutes then dice into cubes.
Transfer chicken to a bowl, pour vinaigrette over chicken and toss to evenly coat.
To assemble wraps spread about 3 tbsp hummus over each tortilla, top with chicken, lettuce, tomatoes, cucumber and feta cheese and wrap tortillas.
In your slow cooker, combine the onion, ketchup, apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, tomato paste, paprika, Worcestershire, salt and pepper.
Whisk to combine.
Add the chicken thighs and make sure they are all evenly spread out and coated with the sauce ingredients.
Place the lid on the slow cooker and turn to high heat for the Shredded BBQ Chicken to be done in 4-5 hours, and low for Shredded BBQ Chicken to be done in 7-8 hours.
Once the timer is up, remove the lid and using 2 forks, shred the chicken while still in the crock pot.
Turn the crockpot to warm and serve the shredded BBQ chicken in burger buns with lettuce.
coconut sugar: Prepare the sauce by adding the coconut sugar, vinegar, coconut aminos, sesame, pineapple juice, and chicken stock to a medium saucepan. Stir and bring to a boil.
Add chicken pieces and beaten egg to a large ziplock bag. Seal and shake to coat chicken.
Then add the arrowroot starch to the bag, shaking again to lightly coat all the chicken pieces.
Add oil to a large non-skillet. Add the coated chicken. Fry over medium heat, a couple of minutes on each side until the coating begins to crisp.
Add the sauce to the chicken. Cover and reduce the heat down to a simmer and allow the juices to soak into the chicken.
Preheat the oven to 350ºF. Line a baking sheet with a sheet of parchment paper.
Then, in a mixing bowl, mash the banana until it becomes a bit creamy but still has a few smallish chunks.
Next, add the vanilla, salt, cardamom, cinnamon, maple syrup, and coconut sugar to the mashed banana and stir.
And now, add the oats, cranberries, and walnuts to the mashed banana mixture and stir until combined.
Now, using a teaspoon, generously scoop the oat mixture and then, using your hands, gently shape each scoop into a ball and place on the prepared pan.
Gently pressing the top of each cookie lightly with two fingers to flatten slightly.
Bake cookies for 15 minutes.
Rub the poblano pepper with olive oil. Roast over the gas flame of your stove, turning often, until the skin is black, 7 to 10 minutes.
Place pepper into a paper bag and seal; set aside to cool, about 10 minutes.
Combine cucumber and onion in a bowl. Set aside.
Take cooled poblano from the bag and scrape off the burned skin. Remove stem and seeds, roughly chop the pepper.
Place tomatoes, poblano, jalapeno, garlic, lime juice, sugar, salt, pepper, and cumin into the bowl of a food processor and pulse until thick and not runny.
Add cucumber and onion, pulse 3 to 4 times to incorporate. Refrigerate until chilled, about 30 minutes. Serve.
In a saucepan, combine apples, water, sugar, and cinnamon. Cover, and cook over medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes.
Allow to cool, then mash with a fork or potato masher.
Place the ribs in a large stockpot and cover with cold water. Soak ribs, refrigerated, for 1 hour to pull out any blood. Drain.
Combine garlic, onion, and Asian pear in a blender and puree. Pour into a large bowl and stir in the soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, and black pepper.
Marinate ribs in the soy mixture, covered, overnight.
Preheat an outdoor grill for high heat, and lightly oil the grate.
Grill ribs until the meat is tender and the outside is crusty, 5 to 10 minutes per side.
Mix water, soy sauce, and sugar together in a small bowl until sugar is dissolved. Add egg and beat until egg mixture is combined.
Heat skillet over medium heat until a drop of water evaporates almost immediately. Pour egg mixture into pan.
Cook until the bottom of the omelet has solidified, 3 to 5 minutes. Flip and fold omelet into a square and transfer to a plate.
Place all the veggies (onion, red bell pepper, mushrooms, garlic, ginger and chili), broth, coconut milk and sugar in a large pot.
Bring it to a boil and then cook over medium heat for about 5 minutes. Add the tofu and cook for 5 minutes more.
Remove from the heat, add the soy sauce, lime juice and parsley. Stir and serve.
Place pork, flour, and sesame oil in a bowl and toss to coat. Let sit for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, preheat the air fryer to 350°F.
Oil the basket of the air fryer. Place pork in the basket of the air fryer and oil top of pork.
Cook for 10 minutes. Shake the basket and cook pork until desired crispness, about 10 minutes more.
While the pork is cooking, combine sugar, vinegar, tomato sauce, and soy sauce in a saucepan over medium-high heat.
Whisk constantly until sugar is dissolved and mixture comes to a boil. Reduce heat to low and keep sauce warm until ready to use.
Transfer pork to a bowl. Pour sauce on top of the pork and toss to coat. Top with sesame seeds.
Combine sugar, soy sauce, water, onion, garlic, pepper, and sesame oil in a blender, process until well blended.
Put chicken into a large glass or ceramic bowl. Pour marinade over top and toss to evenly coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and marinate in the refrigerator for 12 hours.
Cook the rice a pot of water accoridng to the intrucstions on the packaging.
Preheat an outdoor grill for medium-high heat and lightly oil the grate.
Remove chicken from the marinade and shake off excess. Discard the remaining marinade.
Cook chicken on the preheated grill until slightly crisp on the outside, no longer pink in the center, and the juices run clear, about 4 to 8 minutes per side.
In a large bowl, stir together the soy sauce, sugar, sesame seeds, sesame oil, shallots, garlic, and mirin.
Add the meat, and stir to coat. Cover and refrigerate for 12 to 24 hours.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Fry the meat for 5 to 10 minutes, or until no longer pink.
Prehet the oven to 350°F.
In a medium bowl add the eggs, milk, sugar, vanilla extract and ground cinnamon. Mix until fully combined.
Add bread that has been torn into 1 inch pieces, raisins and seeds. Mix until you see that all the bread pieces are in touch with the liquid.
Brush a small baking mould with oil or give it a light coating with oil spray. Transfer the bread pudding mix and spread evenly.
Bake for 10 minutes in the middle rack of the oven. Then transfer to the highest rack, turn on the broiler setting at high and bake about 3 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
In a large bowl, mix together oats, brown sugar, cinnamon, baking powder, and salt. Beat in milk, eggs, melted ghee, and vanilla extract.
Stir in dried pumpkin puree. Spread into a baking dish and top with pecans. Bake for 40 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Place chickpeas in a fine mesh strainer.  Rinse well with water.  Remove and discard any remaining skins by pinching the chickpea with your fingers.
Place dried and peeled chickpeas and oil in a 12-inch cast iron skillet. Toss well so chickpeas are evenly coated in the oil.
Roast for 30 minutes tossing halfway. While the chickpeas roast, whisk together the coconut sugar and spices.
Remove the skillet from the oven. Immediately sprinkle sugar/spice mix overtop and toss well to combine. Serve immediately.
In a large bowl, mix together soy sauce, wine, sugar, garlic, sesame oil, sesame seeds and black pepper
Place beef and onions into the mixture, and stir to coat. Cover, and refrigerate for 1 hour.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Preheat grill pan over high heat. Brush oil over grill pan, and add beef and onions. Cook, turning to brown evenly, for 3 to 6 minutes, or until done.
In a large mixing bowl, sift together the dry ingredients. Set aside.
Blend the water, bananas and vanilla extract in a blender just enough to create a smooth banana milk. Measure out 2 cups of homemade banana milk.
Add the banana milk and oil to the bowl of dry ingredients. Gently whisk together until just combined and slightly lumpy.
Heat a large pan or griddle over medium-high heat. Add a bit of oil to the heated pan and pour about ⅓ cup of batter down.
Flip the pancake when the outer edge begins to appear cooked and bubbles form and pop on top. Cook on the other side for 1-2 more minutes, or until the bottom is golden brown, serve.
In a medium pot, heat the coconut oil over medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, jalapeno, ginger, curry paste and cook, stirring frequently, for 5 minutes.
Add chicken stock and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes. Strain out the the garlic, onions, and ginger and discard.
Add in coconut cream, chicken breast, and mushrooms. Simmer until chicken breast pieces are just cooked through, then add fish sauce, sugar and lime juice.
Cook 2 minutes, then ladle into serving bowls and top with fresh parsley.
Grease and flour pans. Preheat oven to 325°F.
Sift flour, salt, baking powder, soda, and cinnamon together in a bowl.
Beat eggs, oil, vanilla, and sugar together in a large bowl. Add sifted ingredients to the creamed mixture, and beat well.
Stir in zucchini and nuts until well combined. Pour batter into prepared pans. Bake for 40 to 60 minutes,
In a large pot, combine the zucchini, garlic, vinegar, soy sauce, water, yeast, sugar and sesame oil.
Season with black pepper. Mix to blend, then cover and cook over medium heat until zucchini is tender, about 20 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Boil the sweet potatoes until tender, about 10 minutes. Drain and return to pot.
Pour in the remaining ingredients for the mash and blend with an immersion blender. Place in a 1.5-quart baking dish.
For the streusel, pulse the bacon and pecans a few times in a food processor. Mix with the coconut sugar and coconut oil, and sprinkle over the sweet potatoes.
Bake for 10 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 325°F. Grease a loaf pan.
Combine bananas, sugar, egg, and butter together in a bowl. Mix flour and baking soda together in a separate bowl.
Stir into banana mixture until batter is just mixed. Stir salt into batter. Pour batter into the prepared loaf pan.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted in the center of the bread comes out clean, about 1 hour.
elbow macaroni: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
, Boil pasta for 3-5 min or to al dente.
Drain pasta and reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
While pasta is cooking, in a large saucepan (4 quart) or medium pot melt butter over medium heat then add flour, cook 1 minute whisking constantly.
While whisking slowly pour in milk, add in mustard and garlic powder.
Increase heat slightly, then bring mixture to a gentle simmer whisking constantly.
Reduce heat to low add in cream cheese and whisk until melted.
Remove saucepan from heat add in the shredded cheeses half at a time, and whisk to melt, while returning to warm heat as needed to melt fully.
Season sauce with salt to taste.
Toss pasta with cheese sauce, thin with pasta water a splash at a time as needed.
Serve immediately.
Cook the elbow macaroni according to the package instructions until it's al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a small saucepan over low heat, warm the low-fat milk. Be careful not to boil it; just heat it gently.
Add the shredded sharp cheddar cheese and shredded mozzarella cheese to the warm milk. Stir until the cheeses are melted and the mixture is smooth.
Season the cheese sauce with garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Stir well to combine.
Add the cooked elbow macaroni to the cheese sauce. Stir until the macaroni is evenly coated with the cheese sauce.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Cook your macaroni according to the package directions and then rinse and drain.
Add the vegan butter to a pot and let it melt. When it’s melted add in flour and stir it vigorously with the melted butter.
Then pour in the cans of coconut milk and the vegetable stock. Use a hand whisk to whisk out the lumps.
Remove it from the heat and add in the dijon mustard, nutritional yeast, onion powder, garlic powder, salt, and pepper, and whisk in.
Add the cooked macaroni into the pot of sauce and toss it up with the sauce.
Bake for 20 minutes and serve.
Cook the elbow macaroni according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a small saucepan, heat the milk over medium-low heat until warm but not boiling.
Gradually add the shredded cheddar cheese to the warm milk, stirring constantly until the cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Season the cheese sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
Add the cooked macaroni to the cheese sauce and stir until well coated.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the macaroni is heated through.
Transfer to a bowl and serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook elbow macaroni in the boiling water for 8 minutes, drain.
Brown bacon pieces in a large, oven-proof skillet over medium heat. Remove with a slotted spoon. Pour off all but 1 tablespoon of bacon grease.
Saute shallots in skillet over medium heat until translucent. Return cooked bacon to skille, stir in broccoli.
Add cooked pasta and cheddar cheese and mix until cheese has melted.
Combine bread crumbs, 1 tbsp ghee, paprika and cheese in a small bowl and sprinkle over the top of the macaroni and cheese.
Bake until topping is lightly browned, 5 to 10 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the elbow macaroni according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a microwave-safe bowl, combine the low-fat milk, shredded cheddar cheese, shredded mozzarella cheese, Dijon mustard, a pinch of salt, and a pinch of black pepper.
Microwave the cheese mixture in 30-second intervals, stirring each time until the cheese is fully melted and the mixture is smooth. This should take about 1-2 minutes.
Add the cooked and drained macaroni to the cheese sauce. Stir to combine, ensuring all the pasta is coated with the cheese sauce.
Transfer the macaroni and cheese mixture to an oven-safe ramekin or small baking dish that has been lightly coated with cooking spray.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the cheese is bubbly.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook elbow macaroni in the boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 8 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine tuna, bell pepper, tomatoes, carrot, radishes, olives, peas, chives and parsley in a large bowl.
Drain pasta and rinse in cold water. Drain again and add to the bowl with the tuna mixture.
Combine mayonnaise, black pepper, and salt in a small bowl. Pour dressing over salad and toss to coat.
Cook the elbow macaroni according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet over medium heat, cook the ground beef, breaking it apart with a spoon, until browned.
Add the chopped onion and bell pepper to the skillet and cook until they soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the canned diced tomatoes, beef broth, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for about 10 minutes, allowing the flavors to blend.
Add the cooked elbow macaroni to the skillet and stir until everything is combined and heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Cook the elbow macaroni according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a small saucepan, melt the ghee over medium heat.
Stir in the flour and cook for about 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the milk, stirring constantly until smooth and thickened.
Reduce the heat to low and add the shredded cheddar cheese. Stir until the cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Season the cheese sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
Add the cooked macaroni to the cheese sauce and stir until well coated.
Transfer the macaroni and cheese mixture to a small baking dish coated with cooking spray.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and bubbly.
Let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Preheat oven to 450°F.
In a large pan, melt the butter. Add garlic, and spinach cooking until spinach is wilted. Add artichoke hearts. Add the milk, salt, and pepper. Stir until milk is boiling.
Add the macaroni, cooking until the milk coats the macaroni like a glaze. Add cheddar and half of the mozzarella, stirring until the cheese is melted.
Sprinkle the remaining mozzarella on top. Bake for 7 minutes. Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a large saucepan, add oil over medium heat. Add in the onion, garlic, carrots, and celery, and cook until the onion is translucent. Then add in the tomato paste and mix in.
Add in the tomatoes, and the herbs, and mix in, and cook until the tomatoes are tender.
Then add in broth, beans, salt, and pepper, and mix in, and simmer for 15 minutes. Add in pasta to the soup, mix in, taste and adjust flavor, and garnish with basil.
Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Melt 2 tbsp of butter in a large skillet. Add the leeks and cook over high heat, stirring, for 5 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Cook over low heat for 20 minutes.

Meanwhile, in a large saucepan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Add the flour and cook over moderately high heat, whisking, for 2 minutes. Add milk and bring to a boil, whisking until thickened, about 5 minutes. Remove from the heat. Add 2 cups of the cheese, season with salt and pepper and whisk the cheese sauce until melted.
Add the macaroni and the cheese sauce to the leek greens and stir until combined.
Transfer the macaroni to an 8-by-11-inch baking dish and sprinkle with the remaining cheese. Bake for 40 minutes. Let stand for 15 minutes before serving.
basmati rice: Heat the olive oil in a pot on medium-high heat then add the onion ginger and garlic and let cook for 2 minutes.
Add the carrot and celery and cook for 5 minutes until all the veggies are tender.
Add in the lentils, rice, red curry paste, soy sauce lime juice, and vegetable broth and mix together.
Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and let simmer for 20 minutes until the lentils are tender.
In a large bowl, combine all the ingredients well.
Add prawns, mix. Cover and allow it to marinade for 30 mins under refrigeration.
Heat 3 tbsps of oil in heavy bottom pan. Then, add sliced onions and fry until just starts picking up golden colour.
Add bay leaf and sliced tomatoes and sauté on high heat for another minute. Then, add marinated prawns and and mix.
Cook on high heat for minute and then low heat for 3 minutes. Do not add any water and do not over cook the prawns.
Add coriander leaves and mix. In a large pot, add enough water. Add bay leaves, oil, lemon juice and salt Bring it to a boil.
Add rice and cook until the rice is 70% done.
When the rice is 70% done, using a large colander or slotted spoon take out the rice and layer it evenly over the prawns layer.
Sprinkle the melted ghee over the rice layer. Place the pot over a high flame and cook for 5-7 minutes.
Place hot tava/griddle below the pot and keep it for Dum(slow cook) for another 10-12 minutes.
After 15 minutes, switch off the heat and leave as it is for 10 minutes.
After 10 minutes, open the lid, dish out yummy hot prawn biryani and serve with raita of your choice.
Combine the rice, salt, and water in a medium pot. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce the heat, and simmer for 15 minutes. Remove from the heat and set aside.
Heat the oil over medium-low heat in a large nonstick pan. Add garlic, and jalapeno and cook, stirring frequently, until softened, 3 minutes.
Add the coconut milk, red curry paste, fish sauce and whisk together. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer until thickened, a few minutes.
Add the chicken and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally to promote even cooking, until the chicken is cooked through, 5  minutes.
Stir in the lime juice, season, and serve with quinoa.
Heat the olive oil in a large stockpot. Add the onion and sauté for 7-8 minutes, until translucent. Reduce to low heat, add the crushed tomatoes, water, and salt.
Turn heat to medium-high and bring to a boil. Add the rice and stir well. Reduce the heat to low again, cover, and let simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the rice starts to soften.
Take the pot off the heat and keep it covered for another 10 minutes or until the rice has cooked completely.
Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced onion, minced garlic, and minced ginger to the skillet. Cook until the onions are softened and translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chicken leg pieces to the skillet and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5-6 minutes.
Stir in ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, and garam masala. Cook for another 1-2 minutes.
Add the rinsed basmati rice to the skillet and stir to combine with the chicken and spices.
Pour in low-sodium chicken broth and season with salt and pepper to taste. Bring to a simmer.
Cover the skillet with a lid and reduce the heat to low. Let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the chicken is tender.
Remove from heat and let it sit, covered, for an additional 5 minutes.
Fluff the chicken biryani with a fork before serving.
Serve the chicken biryani hot.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Laq butternut squash chunks on a baking tray and drizzle with oil. Roast for 15 minutes.
In a medium skillet bring water to a boil and prepare the rice according to packet instructions.
In a large pan heat the rest of the oil over a medium heat, add in the beans and garlic, fry for a few minutes until fragrant.
Add in the roasted squash and the fajita seasoning and stir well to combine. Add a little water to stop it sticking and create a bit of sauce.
Next add in the cooked rice and stir in the beans, mix through the coriander leaves and serve.
Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set aside.
In a pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and sauté until they become soft and translucent.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are no longer pink. Season with salt, pepper, and the spices (cumin, coriander, paprika).
Stir in the chopped tomatoes and bell peppers and cook for a few minutes until they start to soften.
Add the chickpeas and mix well.
Add the rinsed rice to the pan and sauté for a couple of minutes to toast the rice.

Pour in the water and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat, cover the pan, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the liquid is absorbed. Make sure the lid is secure to trap the steam.
Fluff the Al Kabsa with a fork and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile heat the oil in a very large nonstick frying pan, add the onions and ginger, and cook for 5 mins. Add the curry powder and allspice.
Pour in the tomatoes plus a can of water, the coconut milk, thyme and bouillon.
Add the sweet potatoes, beans and peppers, cover the pan and simmer for 20-25 mins or until all the vegetables are tender.
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F.
Spray a baking sheet with cooking spray and bake the salmon for 10 minutes. While the salmon is cooking, spray a large saucepan cooking spray.
Over a medium heat, saute the onions for 5 minutes until softened, but not colored. Add garlic and curry powder and stir through. Add rice, and stir for a minute until the rice is well coated.
Pour over the stock and add the green beans to the pan. Stir well. Bring to the boil, reduce the heat and allow to simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the lemon juice, cook for another minute and then cover the pan and turn off the heat. Leave for 10 minutes. During this time, the rice will absorb the remaining liquid.
While the rice is standing, poach 2 eggs in a separate pan. Uncover the rice and fluff it up with a fork. Divide the rice between 2 plates, add salmon and a poached egg.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and pepper, cook in hot oil until browned, 3 to 5 minutes.
Stir onion with the chicken; continue cooking until the onion is translucent, 5 to 7 minutes.
Pour cream of mushroom soup and Marsala wine over the chicken mixture; cook until hot for 3 to 5 minutes.
Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set aside.

In a small bowl, mix the plain Greek yogurt with minced garlic, grated ginger, ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric powder, and chili powder. Add the chicken pieces to the yogurt mixture, making sure they are well coated. Let marinate for at least 15 minutes.
In a small saucepan or skillet, heat a little oil over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced onion and sauté until translucent.
Add the marinated chicken pieces to the saucepan and cook until they are no longer pink, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the rinsed basmati rice to the saucepan and stir to combine with the chicken.
Pour in the water. If using saffron, dissolve it in a tablespoon of warm water and add it to the saucepan. Season with salt to taste.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
Once cooked, fluff the biryani with a fork.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile bring a cup of water to a boil in a saucepan. Stir chicken, curry powder, and red pepper flakes into boiling water, cover saucepan.
Cook for 10 minutes. Uncover and reduce heat to low. Gradually stir cream into chicken mixture. Add yogurt; cook and stir for 10 minutes. Garnish with parsley.
Turn the Instant Pot to saute mode and heat oil. Add whole spices–cardamom, cloves, black pepper, and cumin. Saute for 30 seconds.
Add onion and mix well. Saute for 2 minutes covered with glass lid. Add ginger, garlic, turmeric, red chili powder, ground cumin, ground coriander, and salt, mix well.
Add chicken and water. Mix well to evenly coat all the spices to the chicken. Close Instant Pot with pressure valve to sealing.
Pressure Cook for 8 minutes followed by 5-minute Natural Release. Open the Instant Pot, add a few spoonfuls of hot curry to the yogurt and mix well.
Cook on Saute mode to thicken the sauce. Meanwhile, cook the basmati rice according to the package instructions. Set aside to cool a bit, then fluff the rice using a fork.
Serve the curry alongside rice with a garnish of cilantro.
Rinse the rice in cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set aside.
In a pan, heat the oil over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds.
Add the sliced onions and sauté until they become translucent and slightly brown.
Add the chicken or lamb pieces and cook until they are no longer pink. Season with salt, pepper, and the spices (coriander, cayenne pepper, turmeric).
Stir in the sliced carrots and cook for a few minutes until they begin to soften.
Add the chickpeas and stir well.
Pour in the rice and sauté for a couple of minutes to toast the rice.

Add water and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat, cover the pan, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the liquid is absorbed. Ensure the lid is tight to trap the steam.
Fluff wiht fork. Serve!
Rinse the Basmati rice in cold water until the water runs clear. Drain the rice and set it aside.
In a small bowl, steep the saffron threads in the hot water for a few minutes to infuse the water with saffron flavor and color.
In a saucepan, combine the rinsed rice, saffron-infused water, vegetable oil, and a pinch of salt. Stir to combine.
Place the saucepan over medium-high heat and bring the mixture to a boil.
Once it boils, reduce the heat to low and cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid.
Allow the rice to simmer for about 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it sit, covered, for an additional 5 minutes to allow the rice to steam and fully absorb the saffron flavors.
Fluff the saffron rice with a fork.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile put the ginger, soy, mirin, garlic, chilli flakes  and 1 tsp of the sesame oil in a food processor and blend until fine.
Pour the marinade into a bowl, add the meat, mix well and leave to sit while you prepare the onion.
Heat the remaining sesame oil in a large wok or frying pan until very hot. Add the onion and stir-fry for a few mins.
Add the beef and the marinade, stirring constantly until it’s cooked through, about 5 mins. Serve with rice and garnish with onions.
Heat butter over medium heat in a large saucepan. Add parsley, carrot, turmeric and basmati rice and cook for one minute while stirring.
Add balance of ingredients, cover and cook over low heat for 20 minutes
Remove bay leaf and fluff up the rice with a fork, before serving.
Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear. Drain and set aside.
Heat ghee in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and ginger to the pan and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the diced tomato and cook until softened.
Add the minced lamb to the pan and cook until browned.
Stir in the cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, and garam masala, and cook for another minute to toast the spices.
Add the mixed vegetables and rinsed basmati rice to the pan. Stir well to coat the rice and vegetables with the spices.
Pour in the water and season with salt to taste. Bring to a boil.
Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pan, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove the pan from the heat and let it sit, covered, for a few minutes before fluffing the pilau with a fork.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
Add milk and corn starch into a medium bowl and whisk together until the corn starch is fully dissolved. Next add in mustard, honey, salt, and pepper and whisk together well then set aside.
Place a large pan over medium high heat and add in olive oil, garlic, and onions. Saute for 1 minute then add in the chicken while continuing to saute for another 3 minutes.

Next, add broccoli into the pan and continue to sauté for another 2 minutes. Finally, reduce heat to low and add in the honey mustard mixture. Stir until well combined, remove from heat and add in fresh cilantro , give a final stir then serve.
To serve, divide rice between plates and add chicken and broccoli. Serve hot.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil. Add rice and cook to package instructions. Drain and set aside to cool before fluffing using a fork.
To make the curry paste, peel, roughly chop and place the garlic, shallots and ginger into a food processor.

Trim the lemongrass, remove the tough outer leaves, then finely chop and add to the processor. Trim and add the chilis along with the cumin and half the coriander (stalks and all). Blitz until finely chopped, add the fish sauce and blitz again.
Slice the chicken into strips. Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a large pan on a medium heat, add the chicken and fry for 5 to 7 minutes, or until just turning golden, then transfer to a plate.
Tear the mushrooms into even pieces. Return the pan to a medium heat, add the mushrooms and fry for 4 to 5 minutes, or until golden. Transfer to a plate using a slotted spoon.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and add the Thai green paste for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.

Pour in the coconut milk and a cup of boiling water, crumble in the stock cube and add the lime leaves. Turn the heat up and bring gently to the boil, then simmer for 10 minutes, or until reduced slightly.
Stir in the chicken and mushrooms, reduce the heat to low and cook for a further 5 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper. Pick, roughly chop and stir through the basil leaves and remaining coriander leaves. Serve with lime wedges and steamed rice.
green bell pepper: De-seed the bell peppers and chop into 1” square pieces. Cut the firm tofu into 1” square pieces.⁠ Heat a barbecue or grill pan until hot.⁠
Meanwhile, mix the seasoning spices and oil in a small bowl. Thread the vegetables and tofu, alternating, onto the skewers and brush with the marinade.⁠
Cook the skewers on each side until the vegetables are cooked. Turn and repeat on all sides. Serve immediately.
Mix the chicken with the lime juice, chilli powder and garlic in a zipper bag, leave to marinate for 10 minutes.
Heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan on medium-high, fry the chicken for 2-3 minutes, or until cooked through. Transfer the chicken to a plate and set aside.
Add the rest of the olive oil to the frying pan. Add the sliced onion and bell peppers, season with salt and pepper, and stir fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add the chicken back to the pan to warm it up.
Meanwhile warm the tortillas in a separate non-stick pan on medium heat. Once nice and hot, remove from the heat.
Divide the vegetable-chicken mixture and add it to the warm tortillas. Enjoy immediately.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add turkey breast, green pepper, garlic, Italian seasoning, ground black pepper, salt, and red pepper flakes,
Cook and stir until turkey is lightly browned, 4 to 5 minutes.
Stir marinara sauce and baby spinach into the turkey mixture, cook and stir until marinara sauce is warm through, about 3 minutes.
Stir zucchini noodles into the sauce with tongs; cook and stir until the zucchini is slightly tender, 2 to 3 minutes.
Serve and top with grated Parmesan.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In an oven-safe skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken drumsticks and chorizo slices. Cook until they start to brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the diced potato, chopped onion, and chopped bell peppers to the skillet. Sauté for another 3-4 minutes.
Sprinkle the smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper over the mixture. Stir to coat everything evenly.
Place the skillet in the preheated oven and roast for about 30-35 minutes or until the chicken drumsticks are cooked through, and the potatoes are tender.
Remove from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile oil a nonstick skillet and heat over medium-high heat for 1 minute. Add the chicken and stir-fry until it's well browned, stirring often.
Add the garlic and vegetables and stir-fry for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in the soy sauce, stock and orange juice. Cook and stir until the mixture boils and thickens. Serve over the rice. Garnish with green onion.
Cook turkey in a skillet over medium heat until completely browned, 5 to 7 minutes, season with taco seasoning mix.
Add green bell pepper; cook and stir until pepper is tender, about 5 minutes
Arrange salad greens onto 2 plates. Fill tortilla cups with greens, cheese, and onion. Spoon turkey over the each salad. Top with salsa and sour cream.
Preheat a grill to medium-high heat 375°F.
