Rub tilapia fillets with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder and grill for 5 minutes on each side.
Serve with tomatoes, bell peppers, seasoned with salt, pepper, olive oil and vinegar.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
In a large pot of boiling salted water, cook potatoes until parboiled for 10-12 minutes; drain well.
Arrange steak, potatoes, broccoli, bell pepper, and onion on a large sheet pan.
In a small bowl, whisk the garlic, olive oil, Italian seasoning and salt & pepper. Brush the mixture on the steak and veggies.

Place into the oven in the upper-center rack and broil until the steak is browned and charred at the edges and the veggies are lightly charred, about 4-5 minutes per side, flipping once, for medium doneness.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté for a couple of minutes until it becomes translucent.
Add the chopped red and green bell peppers and continue to sauté for another couple of minutes.
Add the chopped tomato, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper. Cook for a few more minutes until the tomato softens and the mixture is well combined.
Make two wells in the vegetable mixture and crack the eggs into them.
Cover the skillet and cook until the egg whites are set, but the yolks are still slightly runny.
Sprinkle fresh parsley on top for garnish.
Serve your delicious Menemen immediately with your favorite bread or pita. Enjoy your low-calorie meal!
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil, cook the rotini at a boil until tender yet firm to the bite, about 10 minutes, drain.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground turkey and break it up with a wooden spoon.
Add chili powder, garlic, cumin, cayenne pepper, oregano, and black pepper, mix thoroughly.
Cook and stir until turkey is browned and crumbly, 5 to 7 minutes.
Add corn, diced tomatoes and chiles, green bell pepper, onion, celery, and carrot; mix thoroughly.
Place lid on skillet. Cook until vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally, about 8 minutes.
Add tomato sauce, let simmer until thickened, about 5 minutes. Transfer turkey mixture to a casserole dish and add the cooked pasta.
Mix everything together. Top with cheese. Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is melted, about 10 minutes.
Add the steak to a mixing bowl and season with salt and pepper. Stir until well combined.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Once the skillet is hot, you can add in the oil.
Next, add the steak bites to the skillet cook for 5 minutes until browned on all sides. Remove the meat from the pan and set aside.
Then, add the minced garlic, onion, and diced peppers. Cook the veggies for 10 minutes.
Add the steak. When the beef is back in the pan, give everything a good stir until well combined and cook for 3 more minutes until the steak is hot.
Season meat with salt, pepper, paprika powder, and cumin. Heat 1 tsp oil in a frying pan. Fry the meat over high heat for 5 minutes until crumbly. Set aside.
Mix all vegetables with oil, vinegar and season with salt and pepper. Add minced meat and mix. Serve.
Place the chopped cucumber, green bell pepper, red onion, minced garlic, fresh spinach, cilantro, and parsley in a blender or food processor.
Add the low-sodium vegetable broth, red wine vinegar, and olive oil to the blender.
Blend the ingredients until smooth and well combined. If the mixture is too thick, you can add a bit more vegetable broth to achieve your desired consistency.
Season the Green Gazpacho with salt and black pepper to taste. Adjust the seasoning if needed.
Pour the Green Gazpacho into a bowl or serving glass.
Heat a small amount of cooking oil in a medium-sized pot over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced chorizo sausage to the pot and sauté for about 3-4 minutes until it's browned and has released some of its flavorful oils. Remove the chorizo from the pot and set it aside.
In the same pot, add a bit more oil if needed. Sauté the diced onions, red bell pepper, and green bell pepper for about 3-4 minutes until they become tender.
Add the diced potatoes to the pot and cook for another 2-3 minutes, allowing them to start softening.
Return the cooked chorizo to the pot and add the canned diced tomatoes, chicken or vegetable broth, paprika, and cayenne pepper. Stir to combine.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Simmer the Chorizo Potato Stew for about 15-20 minutes or until the potatoes are tender and the flavors meld together.
Once the stew is ready, transfer it to a serving bowl.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
In a skillet, cook the ground beef over medium heat until browned.
Add the diced onion, red bell pepper, green bell pepper, and minced garlic. Sauté until the vegetables are tender.
Stir in cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for another minute.
Roll out the refrigerated pie crust and use a round cutter to make several dough circles.
Place a spoonful of the beef mixture on each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form half-moon shapes and press the edges to seal.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven for about 15-20 minutes or until they are golden brown.
Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Lightly coat the skillet with cooking spray or a small amount of olive oil.
Season the chicken with salt and pepper to taste.
Add the seasoned chicken to the skillet. Cook until no longer pink and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Once the chicken is cooked, remove it from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced green bell pepper and onion. Cook until vegetables are tender, about 3-5 minutes.
While the vegetables are cooking, toast the bread.
Stuff the chicken and veggies in the bread.
Top with cheese.
In a small saucepan, heat a bit of water or vegetable broth over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion, red bell pepper, and green bell pepper to the saucepan. Sauté for about 3-4 minutes until the vegetables start to soften. Add more water or broth if needed to prevent sticking.
Stir in the minced garlic, cumin powder, chili powder, and paprika. Sauté for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the drained and rinsed black beans to the saucepan.
Pour in the low-sodium vegetable broth.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and let it cook for about 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Season the soup with salt and pepper to taste, adjusting as needed.

Using an immersion blender or a regular blender, carefully blend the soup until it reaches your desired consistency. If using a regular blender, be sure to allow the soup to cool slightly before blending, and blend it in batches if necessary.
Return the blended soup to the saucepan and heat it through if needed.
In a small saucepan over medium heat, add the diced onion and green bell pepper. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Add the lean ground beef to the saucepan and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, until it's browned and cooked through.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes, beef broth, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Add the uncooked elbow macaroni to the saucepan, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover and simmer for about 15-20 minutes, or until the macaroni is tender and has absorbed the flavors of the sauce.
Taste and adjust the seasoning if needed.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it sit for a couple of minutes to thicken slightly.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Cook and stir lean ground beef in the hot skillet until some of the fat starts to render, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add onion and bell pepper; continue to cook until vegetables have softened and beef is cooked through, 3 to 5 more minutes.
Stir in ketchup, mustard, and garlic powder; season with salt and pepper. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes.
Load the hamburger bun.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
Prepare a large baking sheet by placing a metal cooling rack on the tray and spraying with olive oil.
Combine chicken, peppers, onion, oil, seasoning, and lime juice together in a large bowl.  Toss well.
Place mixture on the rack in an even layer.  Bake for 15 minutes, until chicken is cooked through.
Turn the oven broiler on and place under the broiler for about 2 minutes.
sirloin steak: Preheat your air fryer to 400°F for about 3-5 minutes.
Season the steak bites with salt and black pepper.
Place the seasoned steak bites in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
Cook the steak bites in the air fryer for 6-8 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through cooking, or until the steak reaches your desired level of doneness.
While the steak is cooking, melt the ghee in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Once the steak is cooked, transfer it to a serving dish and pour the garlic ghee over the steak bites.
Toss the steak bites in the garlic ghee until evenly coated.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Serve immediately and enjoy!
Add two parts water to one part rice and cook over low to medium-low heat in a saucepan until all the water has been absorbed and rice is tender (15 to 20 minutes).
Cut steak into thin slices and set aside. You may want to freeze the steak for 10 minutes to make slicing it a little easier.
Cut the stems off the enoki mushrooms, and slice them horizontally into three equal parts.
In a small bowl, add 2 tablespoons of mirin, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, 2 tablespoons of sake, 3 tablespoons of dashi (or water), and set aside.
Cut green onion into thin slices. Separate the green part in one bowl, and the white part of the onion in another bowl.
Heat pan on medium/low and add the white part of the onion with mirin, soy sauce, sake, and dashi, and cook for 1 minute.
Add slices of beef and cook until medium.
Add enoki mushrooms and half of the green onions and cook for 1 minute.
Crack an egg, separating the yolk from the white, and carefully add the white part of the egg in the middle of the pan first.
After 30 seconds, add the yolk, and cook for an additional 1 minute on medium.
Place beef on the edges of the rice bowl and top with egg in the middle.
Sprinkle on the remaining green onion, sesame seeds, black pepper, and serve.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
In a large pot of boiling salted water, cook potatoes until parboiled for 10-12 minutes; drain well.
Arrange steak, potatoes, broccoli, bell pepper, and onion on a large sheet pan.
In a small bowl, whisk the garlic, olive oil, Italian seasoning and salt & pepper. Brush the mixture on the steak and veggies.

Place into the oven in the upper-center rack and broil until the steak is browned and charred at the edges and the veggies are lightly charred, about 4-5 minutes per side, flipping once, for medium doneness.
Add the steak to a mixing bowl and season with salt and pepper. Stir until well combined.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Once the skillet is hot, you can add in the oil.
Next, add the steak bites to the skillet cook for 5 minutes until browned on all sides. Remove the meat from the pan and set aside.
Then, add the minced garlic, onion, and diced peppers. Cook the veggies for 10 minutes.
Add the steak. When the beef is back in the pan, give everything a good stir until well combined and cook for 3 more minutes until the steak is hot.
In a mixing bowl, combine soy sauce, olive oil, salt, thyme, red pepper flakes, pepper and lemon juice and add steak bites and mix well.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over high heat. Drain steak bites from the marinade.
Place the steak bites in the skillet in a single layer.  Cook steak bites for 4 minutes, stirring occasionally until steak bites are golden brown.
Once all the steak bites are golden brown, add butter and garlic to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes, stirring to coat the meat in the sauce. Serve immediately.
Cut the steak into strips across the grain with a sharp knife, then mix with the miso, grated ginger, 1/4 garlic. Add some black pepper and toss really well to coat the meat.
Bring a medium pan of water to the boil, add the broccoli and blanch for 2 mins. Add noodles and beans to the water, and cook for 5 mins until the noodles soften.
Meanwhile, heat the oil in a non-stick wok and stir-fry the steak for 1 min until it just changes color. Remove from the pan and add the garlic slices and remaining ginger.
Return the steak to the wok, add the broccoli and toss well over the heat to warm through, adding a little more water to loosen the sauce a little.
Drain the noodles and pile onto plates. Top with the stir-fry and serve with sesame seeds and sliced spring onion.
Blanch the broccoli and beans for 2 minutes in a large pan of lightly salted boiling water, then drain well.
Drizzle the steak with oil, season really well and sprinkle over the fajita seasoning, patting to coat.
Heat a non-stick pan over a high heat and cook the steak for 3 minutes on each side.
Add the blanched broccoli, onion, beans and the peppers to the pan and fry for 3 minutes until the broccoli is starting to char. Serve.
Season the steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the steak to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until it reaches your preferred level of doneness. Remove the steak from the skillet and let it rest.
In the same skillet, add the sliced bell peppers, onions, and minced garlic. Sauté for about 2-3 minutes until the vegetables start to soften.
Stir in the canned diced tomatoes (with their juice), tomato sauce, dried oregano, dried basil, and red pepper flakes. Season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Let the tomato mixture simmer for about 3-4 minutes to heat through and meld the flavors.
Return the cooked steak to the skillet and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes to heat the steak through and allow it to absorb the flavors of the sauce.
Serve your Steak Pizzaiola with the tomato-pepper sauce spooned over it.
Mince garlic and rosemary.
Add 1 teaspoon salt (or to taste) and mince it a little longer.
You can also do this with a mortar and pestle.
Rub the paste over both sides of each steak.
Heat a skillet over medium/high heat and melt butter.
Add oil to prevent it from burning too much.
Add steaks, season them with pepper and cook 1 or 2 minutes on each side.
Turn it over, cook it 2 or 3 more minutes and add the wine (you can add extra garlic and rosemary now too to the skillet).
It will steam furiously.
Cook the steak 2 or 3 additional minutes for medium rare, remove steaks and let rest for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the wine until reduced and a kind of sauce if formed.
Slice the steaks and serve.
Place the steaks in a marinade bowl. Mix all the ginger to lime juice together and pour over the steaks, cover and let stand 20 minutes at room temperature.
Meanwhile mix and arrange the salad ingredients on a large serving plate.
Heat the sesame oil in a skillet, remove the steaks from the marinade (keep the marinade aside) and quickly brown each side over high heat.
Turn down the heat and cook the steaks to your preference. When done, cover with foil and let rest 10 minutes before slicing into thin strips.
Arrange the steak strips over the salad ingredients.
Pour the retained marinade into the skillet and let simmer until reduced by a third. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
Drizzle over the salad just before serving and garnish with sesame.
In a saucepan bring water to a boil. Add rice and stir. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 20 minutes.
Slice beef into very thin strips. In a mixing bowl, combine  cornstarch, broth, soy sauce, honey and garlic powder. Mix thoroughly.
In a large skillet, over medium/high heat, saute the beef strips until browned and juices evaporate. Stir in broccoli and broth mixture.
Cook until mixture boils and thickens, stirring frequently. Serve over the cooked rice.
Start by prepping the steak.
Trim any fat and cut the steak into 3 inch wide strips, targeting 8 strips.  Use any leftovers for another meal.
Season the steak pieces generously on both sides with salt, pepper, and Worcestershire sauce and place in the refrigerator for 30 minutes for the flavors to marinate.
While the steak is marinating, prep the filling.
Chop up the carrot, bell pepper, and zucchini into matchstick-size pieces, slightly longer than the width of the steak strips.
Cut the green onion in a similar size and sliced them in half length-wise. Set all vegetables aside.
Moving on to the sauce, melt the butter in a small saucepan on medium heat.
Add the finely chopped shallot and sautee until they turn soft and translucent.
Add the balsamic vinegar, brown sugar, and beef stock to the pan and stir to combine.
Allow the sauce to come to a boil.
You’ll notice it starts becoming thicker and have the consistency of syrup.
Once the volume has reduced by half, remove from heat and transfer the sauce to a bowl.
Peel and crush the garlic with the flat side of your knife and add to a skillet with olive oil on medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
Turn the heat up to high and toss in the carrots, bell pepper and zucchini (there’s no need to cook the green onion) and stir fry the veggies for no longer than 2-3 minutes.
Season them with the the itlalian herb seasoning and salt and transfer the veggies to a bowl.
To assemble the steak rolls, simply take a strip of the marinated steak and lay it with the short side towards you.
Place the veggies (don't forget the green onion!) in the middle and roll the beef up over the filling, securing it with toothpick.
Repeat the same with the other rolls.
Reheat your skillet - no need for a new pan -over medium-high heat and add olive oil to coat.
When hot, add beef rolls, seam side down, with space between each roll.  Pan fry for 2-3 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Remove the toothpick from the rolls and transfer to your plate.  Serve with the balsamic glaze sauce spooned over the top of each roll.
Cook star anise and cinnamon in a large skillet over medium until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Transfer to a 6-quart slow cooker. Cut 1 onion in half; place in skillet, cut side down; add ginger. Thinly slice remaining onion, and set aside.
Cook onion and ginger over medium, undisturbed, until onion is charred on cut side, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer to slow cooker. Increase heat under skillet to medium-high.
Add short ribs; cook until browned on all sides, 14 to 16 minutes. Transfer to slow cooker. Remove skillet from heat; discard drippings.
Pour 1 cup water into skillet, scraping to loosen browned bits. Pour through a strainer into slow cooker; discard solids. Stir in beef stock and salt.
Cover and cook on low 8 hours. Remove short ribs from slow cooker. Pour broth through a strainer lined with cheesecloth into a bowl; discard solids.
Skim and discard fat from surface of broth; pour broth back into slow cooker. Remove meat from bones of short ribs; shred meat, and return to slow cooker.
Stir in fish sauce and hoisin sauce. Turn slow cooker to high; cover to keep hot. Cook noodles according to package directions.
Place 1/2 cup cooked noodles in each of 3 bowls. Top each with 1 ounce raw sirloin and 1 1/3 cups hot broth.
Top each with 1/3 cup short rib meat and 1/3 cup thinly sliced onion. Sprinkle with bean sprouts, basil. Serve with lime wedges; garnish with Fresno chile.
Slice the sirloin into thin strips.
Cut bell peppers into long strips, onion into half rings and mushrooms into bite-sized pieces.
Preheat an oiled skillet over high-heat.
Add onions, bell peppers and mushrooms to the skillet.
Sauté until all veggies are golden brown
Remove from the skillet and set aside.
Add sliced beef to the same skillet and season with salt and pepper
Sauté until the beef is fully cooked.
Add vegetables back to the skillet and stir to combine.
Top with cheese slices and let mixture sit for a minute to melt.
Stuff toasted or untoasted hoagie buns with the mixture.
Serve immediately and enjoy!
Heat a deep skillet on high heat with 1 tbsp oil. Once it's hot, fry the beef for just a few moments.
Flip the pieces over and cook for another moment or two, then remove and set aside. Continue until all of the beef has been seared.
Add another spoon of oil, onion and pepper. Cook until softened. Stir in the minced garlic for a moment, until fragrant, and then add the beef broth.
Add the rutabaga into a pot of salted water. Bring it to a boil and cook it until tender. Add beef back and simmer for a minute.
Drain the rutabaga and using a hand mixer or potato masher, mash until perfectly smooth.
Add the melted butter. Slowly add the warmed heavy cream until thick and creamy.
Serve beef with hot with mashed rutabagas and garnish with parsley.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl combine butter, garlic, and dried herbs and set aside. Season steaks generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Place a cast iron skillet ob medium heat and drizzle with oil. Sear steaks for 2-3 minutes on each side until nice and browned. Transfer to a plate and set aside.

Add potatoes, tomatoes, zucchini, red onion, and celery to the skillet. Season generously with salt and pepper, and saute 3-5 minutes until browned. Push vegetables to one side of the pan and return steaks to the other side of the pan.
Transfer to oven and cook for 15-20 minutes until potatoes are tender and steak is cooked to your preferred doneness.
Immediately after removing from the oven, place a dollop of the butter on each steak and serve.
Make the sauce.
Add the lime juice, chipotle paste, garlic powder, cumin, cinnamon, and salt and pepper to a small bowl or jar and mix well to combine.
Set aside. Salt and pepper your steak.
Heat a large cast-iron skillet (it needs to be large) over medium heat. Add 1 tbsp oil and let it get hot.
Add steak in a single layer and cook for 3-5 minutes until browned on the outside, flipping as needed.
If needed, you may need to cook in 2 batches. Remove from the pan, leaving the juices behind.
Add 1 tbsp oil (you may need more depending on how well seasoned your pan is).
Add sweet potatoes and onions and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
If needed, cover with a lid to help speed up the process, but you don’t want them to steam too much.
Add peppers and mushrooms and cook for another 5 minutes.
Add steak and juices and mix well to combine.
Add chipotle sauce and mix well until combined and warmed through.
Top with optional toppings and enjoy!

Season beef cubes with salt and pepper. In non-stick skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of the oil over medium-high heat. Add beef, and cook 2 to 3 minutes, turning frequently, until browned on outsides, but still rare in the middle. Remove from skillet.
Heat remaining 1 tablespoon oil in skillet over medium-high heat. Add mushrooms, potatoes and carrots, cook and stir 3 to 4 minutes or until the mushrooms are tender. Remove from skillet.
Reduce heat to medium, add bacon and onion. Cook and stir 3 to 4 minutes or until onions are softened and beginning to brown. Add tomato paste, garlic and thyme.

Cook and stir 1 minute. Add cooked mushrooms, broth and wine, heat to boiling. Reduce heat to low, simmer 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until sauce is slightly thickened and potatoes are cooked.
Return beef to skillet, cook 2 to 3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until beef is medium done. Serve hot.
Place a large deep pan over medium heat and add oil. Once oil is hot add beef strips, cooking 1 minute per side without stirring. Remove beef to a plate and cover.
Add butter, chopped onion and sliced mushrooms. Saute for 10 minutes until liquid has evaporated and onions and mushrooms are soft.
Add 1 minced garlic clove and saute 1 minute until fragrant. Add flour and saute another minute stirring constantly.
Pour in 1 cups beef broth, then add 3/4 cup cooking cream and simmer another 2 minutes or until slightly thickened.
Stir in sour cream and dijon mustard and season with salt and pepper and continue simmering until sauce is creamy.
Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a large pan over high heat. Add the steak, season with salt and pepper, and cook until browned, 5-6 minutes.
Remove from the pan.
Heat the remaining tbsp of oil in the same pan, then add the red onion and cook for about 5 minutes, until softened and browned.
Add the tomato, garlic, and aji amarillo paste, and cook for another 5-7 minutes, until the tomatoes have released some of their juices, but are still intact.
Add the soy sauce and vinegar and stir to combine, let cook for 1 minute. Add the steak, fries, and cilantro.
Toss gently to coat the fries in the sauce. Serve with rice, if desired.
Enjoy!
beef sirloin: Preheat your grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Thread the beef cubes, onion chunks, bell pepper chunks, and tomato chunks onto the skewers, alternating between them.
Season the skewers with salt and pepper.
In a small bowl, mix together the olive oil and minced garlic.
Brush the olive oil mixture over the skewers.
Place the skewers on the grill or grill pan and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until the beef is cooked to your desired level of doneness and the vegetables are tender.
Once cooked, remove the skewers from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a bowl, combine the olive oil, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add the beef cubes to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the seasoning mixture.
Thread the seasoned beef cubes onto skewers, alternating with the red onion, cherry tomatoes, zucchini slices, and eggplant pieces.
Place the skewers on the preheated grill and cook for about 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until the beef is cooked to your desired doneness and the vegetables are tender.
Remove the skewers from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the beef shish kabobs hot with your favorite sides.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat, cook and stir beef until browned, 3 to 4 minutes.
Move beef to the side of the wok and add broccoli, bell pepper. Cook and stir vegetables for 2 minutes.
Stir beef into vegetables and season with soy sauce and sesame seeds. Continue to cook and stir until vegetables are tender, about 2 more minutes.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, hoisin sauce, grated fresh ginger, and sesame oil. Set this sauce aside.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and lightly spray it with cooking spray.
Add the thinly sliced lean beef to the skillet. Cook for about 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beef is browned and cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Remove the cooked beef from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add minced garlic and sauté for about 30 seconds until it becomes fragrant.
Add the snow peas to the skillet. Stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes until the peas are crisp-tender.
Return the cooked beef to the skillet.
Pour the prepared sauce over the beef and snow peas.
Stir-fry everything together for another 1-2 minutes, ensuring the beef and snow peas are coated with the sauce and heated through.
Remove from heat.
Serve your Asian Beef with Snow Peas hot.
In a bowl, mix together low-sodium soy sauce, fish sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, and a pinch of salt and pepper to create the marinade.
Add the bite-sized beef cubes to the marinade and let them sit for about 10-15 minutes.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat until it's smoking hot.
Add the marinated beef cubes to the skillet, ensuring they are in a single layer to promote searing.

Sear the beef cubes for about 1-2 minutes without moving them, allowing them to develop a nice crust. Then, stir and cook for another 1-2 minutes until they are browned on all sides but still slightly pink in the center.
Add the minced garlic and minced ginger to the skillet and stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Serve your beef on top of lettue and tomato, and top with onion.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, marinate thinly sliced beef with soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce. Set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and minced ginger to the skillet and cook until fragrant.
Add sliced onion and sliced bell peppers to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add marinated beef to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through.
Stir in fresh Thai basil leaves and cook until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, topped with egg, and with cooked rice on the side.
Preheat a skillet over medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, mix the chili powder, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Toss the sliced beef with half of the spice mixture.
Add olive oil to the hot skillet and cook the beef until it reaches your desired doneness, about 3-5 minutes for medium-rare. Remove from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced bell pepper and onion along with the remaining spice mixture. Cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the cooked beef back into the skillet with the peppers and onions, stir to combine, and cook for an additional minute.
Warm the tortillas in the skillet or microwave.
Serve the beef fajita mixture on the warm tortillas.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a wok or skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add thinly sliced beef to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 2-3 minutes).
Toss in broccoli florets, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until the broccoli is slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil.
Pour the sauce over the beef and broccoli, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, cornstarch, vegetable oil, salt, and pepper.
Add thinly sliced beef to the marinade and let it sit for about 10 minutes.
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F for 3-5 minutes.
Place broccoli florets in the air fryer basket and cook for 5 minutes, shaking halfway through.
Remove the broccoli from the air fryer basket and set aside.
Place the marinated beef slices in the air fryer basket in a single layer. Cook for 5-7 minutes, shaking halfway through, until the beef is cooked through and slightly crispy.
Add the cooked broccoli back to the air fryer basket with the beef. Toss everything together and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes to heat through.
Serve the air fryer beef and broccoli hot, garnished with sliced green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced beef strips to the skillet and cook for about 2-3 minutes, or until they are browned. Remove the beef from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the diced onions and sliced mushrooms. Sauté for about 3-4 minutes, or until they become tender and slightly browned.
Sprinkle the flour and paprika over the mushrooms and onions. Stir well to coat.
Pour in the beef broth and return the cooked beef strips to the skillet. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for about 5 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken and the flavors to meld.
Remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the sour cream until well combined. Make sure not to boil the sour cream, as it can curdle.
Season the beef stroganoff with salt and pepper to taste.
Marinate beef in soy sauce, lemon (or calamansi), and ground black pepper for at least 1 hour. Note: marinate overnight for best result.
Heat the cooking oil in a pan then pan-fry half of the onions until the texture becomes soft. Set aside.
Drain the marinade from the beef. Set it aside.
Pan-fry the beef on the same pan where the onions were fried for 1 minute per side. Remove from the pan. Set aside. Add more oil if needed.
Saute garlic and remaining raw onions until onion softens. Pour the remaining marinade and water. Bring to a boil. Add beef.
Cover the pan and simmer until meat is tender. Note: Add water as needed. Season with ground black pepper and salt as needed.
Top with pan-fried onions. Transfer to a serving plate. Serve hot with a flatbread, share and enjoy!
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, minced garlic, and chopped cilantro (if using). Set aside.
Season the thinly sliced beef with salt and pepper.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and add the beef slices. Cook for 2-3 minutes on each side until browned and cooked through. Remove from the skillet and let it rest for a few minutes.
In a large bowl, combine shredded cabbage, julienned carrot, thinly sliced red bell pepper, and sliced green onion.
Pour the prepared dressing over the vegetables and toss to combine.
Slice the cooked beef into thin strips and add it to the bowl with the vegetables.
Toss everything together until well combined.
Serve the Asian Beef Slaw garnished with sesame seeds if desired.
In a medium bowl, combine the sirloin with 1 tablespoon of the soy sauce, 1 teaspoon of the sesame oil, and a pinch of red-pepper flakes.
Bring a medium pot of salted water to a boil. Stir in the rice and boil until just done, about 10 minutes. Drain the rice and return it to the pot.
Meanwhile, heat a large nonstick frying pan over moderately high heat.
Put the beef mixture in the pan and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned and just cooked, 1 to 2 minutes.
Put the watercress and the teaspoon of soy sauce in the pan and cook until the watercress is just wilted for about 30 seconds.
Reduce the heat to moderate. Put a few drops of sesame oil in the frying pan. Add the egg to the pan and cook until just done, about 30 seconds.
Remove from the pan and cut into thin strips. Heat the cooking oil in the pan over moderately high heat.
Add the scallions and 1/8 teaspoon red-pepper flakes and cook, stirring, for 1 minute.
Add the cooked rice to the pan and heat, stirring, for 3 minutes.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons soy sauce, the beef, watercress, and egg. Heat, stirring, 1 to 2 minutes longer.
Drizzle the remaining teaspoon of sesame oil over the top.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, oyster sauce, and coarsely ground black pepper.
In a wok or skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add the thinly sliced beef to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 2-3 minutes).
Toss in thinly sliced cabbage, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until the cabbage is slightly tender.
Pour the sauce mixture over the beef and cabbage, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
In a wok or skillet, heat ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add thinly sliced beef to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 2-3 minutes).
Toss in snow peas and sliced red bell pepper, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil.
Pour the sauce over the beef and vegetables, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
In a bowl, mix together soy sauce, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and black pepper.
Add the beef slices to the marinade and toss until well coated. Let it marinate for 15-20 minutes.
Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the marinated beef to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until beef is cooked through.
Once cooked, transfer the beef to a plate.
Garnish with chopped green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, rice vinegar, minced garlic, minced ginger, and cornstarch. Set aside.
Heat oil in a skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add sliced beef to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 2-3 minutes.
Add snow peas and sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until they are tender-crisp.
Pour the sauce mixture into the skillet and stir well to coat the beef, snow peas, and mushrooms.
Cook for another minute, or until the sauce thickens slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the Asian beef with snow peas and mushrooms hot, garnished with optional toppings if desired.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and cornstarch.
Add thinly sliced beef to the bowl, ensuring each piece is coated in the mixture. Let it marinate for a few minutes.
In a wok or skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add the marinated beef to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 2-3 minutes).
Toss in sliced green onions, stir-frying for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with sesame seeds.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Cook onions, garlic and cubed sirloin in oil for 10 minutes.
Mix in the diced tomatoes with juice, coffee, tomato paste and beef broth.
Season with, chili powder, oregano, cayenne pepper and salt.
Stir in cans of the beans and jalapeno. Reduce heat to low, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
Stir in the beans, and simmer for another 30 minutes.
Heat olive oil in stock pot over medium heat; sear roast in the hot oil until slightly browned, about 2 minutes per side.
Remove roast from pot and place on a plate. Cook and stir onion and garlic in the hot oil until onion is lightly browned, 10 to 15 minutes.
Return seared roast to the stock pot; pour coffee and 1 1/2 cup water over roast. Add beef bouillon, basil leaves, 1 tablespoon salt, and pepper.
Bring broth to a boil; reduce heat to medium low, cover stock pot, and simmer until meat is falling apart, 2 to 3 hours.
Remove and discard basil leaves. Transfer roast to a serving dish and cover with a lid or aluminum foil, keeping the broth in the stock pot over medium heat.
Whisk flour and 1/2 cup water together in a bowl until smooth. Slowly whisk flour mixture into broth until gravy is smooth and somewhat clear, season with salt.
Pour about half the gravy over the roast and pour the rest into a gravy boat for serving.
vegetable broth: Bring broth to a boil. Reduce heat to medium and simmer for about 5 minutes. Add mushrooms and shrimp and cook until shrimp have heated through, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the fish sauce, lime juice, green onion, cilantro and chile pepper. Taste and adjust salt and pepper if needed.
Soup should be sour, salty, spicy and hot. Serve imemdiately.
In a pot, bring the broth and water to a boil over medium-high heat.
Add the uncooked egg noodles, shredded rotisserie chicken, chopped carrots, celery, onion, dried thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for about 10-12 minutes, or until the noodles and vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the heavy cream and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small pot, combine broth, chopped kimchi, tofu, sliced mushrooms, green onion, sesame oil, gochujang, minced garlic, and minced ginger.
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat.
Add the bite-sized chicken pieces to the pot and reduce the heat to medium-low.
Simmer the stew for about 10-12 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the flavors have melded.
Remove from heat and serve the spicy Korean chicken stew hot with cooked white rice.
In a saucepan, melt the ghee over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and minced garlic, and sauté until softened, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the chopped asparagus to the saucepan and cook for another 3-4 minutes.
Sprinkle the flour over the asparagus mixture and stir well to combine.
Gradually pour in the vegetable broth while stirring constantly to prevent lumps from forming.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for about 10-12 minutes, or until the asparagus is tender.
Use an immersion blender or transfer the soup to a blender and blend until smooth.
Return the soup to the saucepan (if necessary) and stir in the milk. Heat gently until warmed through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a small saucepan, melt the ghee over medium heat. Add the diced onion, celery, and minced garlic, and sauté until softened, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the diced potato, diced ham, and broth to the saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the potato is tender.
Stir in the milk and continue to simmer for another 2-3 minutes.
Using a potato masher or fork, mash some of the cooked potato in the soup to thicken it slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and add a bit of olive oil.
Add diced chicken breast to the skillet and cook until it's no longer pink and fully cooked through.
Add pasta, marinara sauce, low-sodium chicken broth, garlic powder, dried basil, salt, and pepper to the skillet with the cooked chicken. Stir to combine all ingredients.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, cover the skillet, and cook for about 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is cooked and most of the liquid is absorbed.
Once the pasta is cooked, sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese and grated Parmesan cheese on top.
Once the pasta is cooked, sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese and grated Parmesan cheese on top.
Once done, remove from heat and let it cool for a minute before serving.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the sliced mushrooms and minced garlic, and sauté until the mushrooms are golden brown and tender.
In a small bowl, whisk together the white miso paste and vegetable broth until smooth.
Pour the miso broth mixture into the skillet with the mushrooms and garlic. Stir well to combine.
Add the cooked pasta to the skillet and toss to coat the pasta evenly with the mushroom-miso sauce.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve and enjoy your delicious Mushroom-Miso Pasta!
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Season the chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken breast and cook until golden brown and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes per side. Remove from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add cherry tomatoes, bell peppers, carrots, minced garlic, dried oregano, and dried basil. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender-crisp, about 5-6 minutes.
Pour broth into the skillet and stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Add the cooked farfalle pasta to the skillet and toss to coat in the sauce.
Slice the cooked chicken breast and add it back to the skillet, stirring to combine with the vegetables and pasta.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until everything is heated through.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add the celery, onion, and garlic and saute for about 5 minutes. Add in the vegetable broth and water.
Add in the raw cashews, bay leaf, and thyme. Bring to a simmer and cook for about 15 minutes. Remove the bay leaf, then blend.
Make sure to blend very well so that the cashews are completely blended. Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately, garnished with dill.
In a pot, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add chopped onion, minced garlic, and diced bell pepper to the pot, sautéing until softened (about 2-3 minutes).
Add chicken to the pot, cooking until they are browned on all sides (about 3-4 minutes).
Stir in rinsed rice, tomato sauce, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and dried oregano, ensuring the rice and chicken are well coated.
Pour in broth, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the chicken is tender.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
In a pot over medium heat, add olive oil.
Sauté diced onion and minced garlic for about 2-3 minutes until the onions are soft.
Add diced carrot and celery to the pot. Cook for another 3-4 minutes until they start to soften.
Stir in shredded cabbage and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until it begins to wilt.
Pour in vegetable broth and diced tomatoes into the pot.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Let the soup simmer for about 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Once done, remove from heat.
In a small pot or saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced onion, sliced eggplant, and sliced zucchini to the pot.
Sauté the sliced vegetables for about 5 minutes or until they start to soften.
Add the diced fresh tomato and canned diced tomatoes to the pot. Stir to combine.
Pour in the low-sodium vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Sprinkle dried oregano, salt, and pepper over the stew.
Cover the pot and let the stew simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and the flavors have melded together.
In a large pot, heat the oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and bell pepper and cook until softened.
Add garlic, ginger and curry paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the broth, coconut milk, and fish sauce.
Bring to a boil.
Add the shrimp and rice noodles.
Cook until shrimp is pink and opaque.
Stir in the lime juice and serve immediately, topped with optional garnishes if using.
In a small pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add chopped onion, minced garlic, and diced carrot. Cook until softened, about 2-3 minutes.
Add fideos (thin noodles) to the pot and cook, stirring frequently, until lightly toasted, about 2-3 minutes.
Pour chicken broth and crushed tomatoes into the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 7-10 minutes, or until the noodles are tender.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Ladle the tomato sopa de fideos into a bowl.
In a large skillet, cook the onion and mushrooms in oil, over medium heat, until all of the liquid has evaporated.
Remove half of the mushrooms from the skillet and reserve for later.
Add the garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute. Then add the couscous, salt, pepper, and veggie broth.
Bring the broth to a boil, cover it with a lid, and then turn off the heat. Let it rest for 5 minutes. Open the lid and add the mushrooms back in. Stir and serve immediately.
In a small saucepan, bring the vegetable broth to a boil.
Gradually pour the instant polenta into the boiling liquid, stirring constantly to prevent clumps from forming.
Reduce the heat to low and continue to stir the polenta until it thickens, which should take about 3-5 minutes. Remove it from the heat.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add minced garlic to the skillet and sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and bell peppers to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes until they start to soften.
Slice the prepared polenta into squares or desired shapes.
Add the polenta to the skillet with the vegetables, arranging it in a single layer.
Cook the polenta for 3-4 minutes on each side until it develops a light golden crust.
Season the polenta and vegetables with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika if using.
Heat ghee in a pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and garlic; cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add sliced mushrooms and thyme; cook for another 5-6 minutes until tender.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to purée the soup until smooth.
Stir in heavy cream, salt, and black pepper. Heat through but do not boil.
Garnish with fresh parsley.
Boil broth in a pot and keep warm.
Heat the oil in another pot over medium heat. Cook the onion, thyme and garlic for 2 minutes. Add the rice and saute, stirring, for 2 minutes.
Ladle in 1 cup of hot broth and continue to cook the rice until almost all the liquid has evaporated, stirring often. Continue adding broth and wait for it to evaporate.
When the rice is cooked, mix in the cream cheese and season with salt and pepper. Mix well so everything is combined. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cut the butternut squash in half lengthwise and scoop out the seeds and membranes.
Place the butternut squash halves, cut side down, on a baking sheet. Roast them in the preheated oven for about 30-35 minutes, or until they are tender when pierced with a fork.
While the butternut squash is roasting, rinse the quinoa under cold water.

In a small saucepan, bring the vegetable broth to a boil. Add the rinsed quinoa and reduce the heat to low. Cover and simmer for about 15 minutes, or until the quinoa is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
In a skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced bell pepper, msuhrooms and diced zucchini to the skillet. Sauté for about 3-4 minutes until they begin to soften.
Stir in the cooked quinoa, black beans, corn kernels, ground cumin, salt, and pepper. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, mixing well.
Once the butternut squash halves are tender, remove them from the oven.
Stuff each butternut squash half with the quinoa and vegetable mixture.
Return the stuffed butternut squash halves to the oven and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes to heat the stuffing through.
In a small pot or saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced zucchini, red bell pepper, yellow bell pepper, and diced onion to the pot.
Sauté the vegetables for about 5 minutes, or until they begin to soften.
Add the diced tomatoes, dried oregano, salt, and pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
Pour in the low-sodium vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let the stew simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender and the flavors have melded together.
oatmilk: Combine the vegetable broth and oat milk in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil.
Add the orzo and cook for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally. Reduce the heat to medium-low and add the nutritional yeast, salt, pepper, and spinach.
Keep cooking for about 3 minutes, until the spinach wilts down. Add salt and pepper to taste. Stir, and remove from heat. Serve immediately.
Heat olive oil in a small pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened.
Add diced potato, chopped cauliflower florets, and vegetable broth to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until the vegetables are tender.
Use an immersion blender or transfer the soup to a blender and blend until smooth.
Stir in milk and heat until warmed through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the cauliflower and potato soup hot,garnished with chopped parsley.
Put spinach, banana, carrot, yogurt in a blender; blend until smooth.
Melt ghee in a small pot over medium heat.
Add chopped leeks to the pot and cook until softened.
Add diced potato and vegetable broth to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until the potato is tender.
Use an immersion blender or transfer the soup to a blender and blend until smooth.
Stir in milk and heat until warmed through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cook the macaroni pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a small saucepan, melt the ghee over medium heat.
Add the cooked macaroni pasta to the saucepan and stir to coat with the melted butter.
Reduce the heat to low and add the shredded cheddar cheese, shredded mozzarella cheese, and milk to the saucepan.
Cook, stirring constantly, until the cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth and creamy.
Season with salt, pepper, paprika, and cayenne pepper, to taste.
Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
While the pasta is cooking, steam the cauliflower florets until tender, about 7-8 minutes.
In a blender or food processor, combine the steamed cauliflower florets, vegetable broth, and milk. Blend until smooth and creamy. Set aside.
In a skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the chopped mixed vegetables to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes, or until tender.
Pour the cauliflower sauce into the skillet and stir to combine with the vegetables.
Add the cooked pasta to the skillet and toss until the pasta is evenly coated with the cauliflower sauce.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
In a medium sized bowl add the couscous and milk.
Add to the bowl the egg, yogurt, vanilla extract, salt. Mix until incorporated. Then add the flour and mix until there are no lumps and it is fully incorporated.
In a pan melt the butter. Once it’s very hot add the kaiserschmarrn batter and fry on medium-low heat for 13 minutes with a lid on.
Flip after half the time roughly or when the surface becomes dry.
Once cooked, tear the pieces and scoop into plates. Serve with raisins.
Prehet the oven to 350°F.
In a medium bowl add the eggs, milk, sugar, vanilla extract and ground cinnamon. Mix until fully combined.
Add bread that has been torn into 1 inch pieces, raisins and seeds. Mix until you see that all the bread pieces are in touch with the liquid.
Brush a small baking mould with oil or give it a light coating with oil spray. Transfer the bread pudding mix and spread evenly.
Bake for 10 minutes in the middle rack of the oven. Then transfer to the highest rack, turn on the broiler setting at high and bake about 3 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Cut the zucchini in half and then in half again, creating medium sized sticks.
Prepare the batter. In a medium bowl, whisk the flour, oat milk, paprika, onion powder, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Dip the zucchini sticks into the batter and then cover them with breadcrumbs.
Place the zucchini fries onto a large parchment-lined baking tray and bake for about 35 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Bake sweet potato in the preheated oven until tender and cooked through, about 1 hour. Remove skin, then cool in the refrigerator 8 hours to overnight.
Blend sweet potato, banana, soy milk, and cinnamon together in a blender until smooth.
Peel and coarsely grate the potatoes, squeezing out as much liquid as you can. Transfer the shredded potatoes to a mixing bowl.
Stir in the crushed garlic, salt, pepper, marjoram, and caraway seeds.
Beat the eggs with the milk. Add the egg mixture to the potatoes and stir well to combine. Gradually mix in the flour to form a thick but still pourable batter.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium-high heat; the oil should be about 1/4-inch deep.
Spoon about 1/4 cup of batter into the hot oil, flattening it slightly. Fry the pancake until golden brown, about 3 minutes on each side.
Drain on paper towels. Taste the first pancake and adjust the seasoning if necessary; repeat with remaining batter.
snapper fillets: Slice top and bottom ends off oranges; cut off peel and white pith, following the shape of the fruit.
Cut orange segments from the membrane to remove and roughly chop.
Place in a medium bowl. Discard peel, pith, and membrane.
Stir in onion, cilantro, chile, salt, and 1 tbsp lime juice.
Sprinkle both sides of snapper evenly with Cajun seasoning.
Heat oil in a large nonstick or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the snapper; cook until golden brown and cooked through, 3 to 4 minutes per side.
Transfer to a plate and flake into large pieces using 2 forks. Sprinkle with the remaining 1 tbsp lime juice.
Divide the snapper and cabbage evenly among warm tortillas. Top with the orange salsa.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
jasmine rice: Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging. Drain and set aside.
In a bowl, stir together water, ketchup, soy sauce, honey, crushed red pepper, and ground ginger. Set aside.

Cook rice in an oiled skillet for 4 to 5 minutes. Stir in garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Stir in sauce. Cook and stir until sauce is bubbly and thickened. Top with onions, red pepper and sesame seeds.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging. Let cool and divide among 4 bowls.
Mix soy sauce, rice vinegar and sesame oil in a bowl to make dressing. Mix salmon with the dressing. Divide among the 4 bowls.
Top bowls with avocado, carrot, seaweed and garnish with a sprinkling of sesame seeds.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instuctions on the packaging.
Mix rice wine, soy sauce, and cornstarch together in a bowl. Add pork, toss until evenly mixed.
Preheat a wok or deep saucepan over medium-high heat. Pour in peanut oil.
Cook pork in the hot oil in batches until evenly browned, 2 to 3 minutes per batch.
Transfer to a plate using a slotted spoon. Cook onion in the remaining oil until softened, 2 to 3 minutes.
Mix curry powder and water in a bowl to form a paste.
Stir curry paste, spring onion, and salt into the wok. Add rice, stirring to break up any lumps. Stir in peas and pepper.
Return pork to the wok. cook and stir until rice and pork are heated through, 4 to 5 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat the oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add the tofu and stir-fry until golden brown. Remove and set aside, leaving the remaining oil in the wok.
Stir the ginger and curry paste into the hot oil for a few seconds until the curry paste is fragrant and the ginger begins to turn golden.
Add the zucchini, bell pepper and broccoli, cook and stir for 1 minute. Pour in the lime juice, soy sauce, coconut milk, and tofu.
Bring the coconut milk to a simmer, and cook a few minutes until the vegetables are tender and the tofu is hot.
Heat a non-stick skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the vegetable oil and shrimp to the hot skillet. Stir-fry the shrimp until they turn pink and opaque, about 2-3 minutes. Remove the shrimp from the skillet and set them aside.
In the same skillet, add the minced garlic and stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the shredded cabbage, thinly sliced carrot, and frozen peas to the skillet. Continue to stir-fry for 2-3 minutes or until the vegetables start to become tender.
Add the cold cooked jasmine rice to the skillet. Break up any clumps and stir-fry for about 2-3 minutes to heat the rice through.
Return the cooked shrimp to the skillet.
In a small bowl, mix together the low-sodium soy sauce, gochujang, sesame oil, and black pepper.
Pour the sauce mixture over the rice and shrimp. Stir-fry for another 2 minutes to evenly distribute the flavors.
Season with additional soy sauce or gochujang to taste if desired.
Transfer the Korean Saewoo Bokkeumbap to a serving plate.
Garnish with thinly sliced green onions.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Remove the skin, then brush on oil and grind on some black pepper. Simply grill, broil, microwave or bake the fish until desired doneness.
Combine pineapple, banana, jam, red bell pepper, cilantro, onion, pepper flakes, coconut flakes and lemon juice.
Serve fish with a big dollop of fruit salsa and rice.
cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add garlic and cook for 2-3 minutes until golden. Remove using a slotted spoon and set aside.
Add pork to the garlic oil in the wok and cook, breaking up mince with a wooden spoon, for 6-7 minutes until lightly caramelized.
Add chili bean sauce and cook for 2 minutes or until lightly caramelized.
Add chives and rice and cook, tossing in the pan, for 3 minutes or until colored.
To serve, top with fried eggs and scatter with crisp garlic.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanshile saute chicken in a large skillet over medium high heat until cooked through.
In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, garlic powder, pineapple juice, orange juice, pecans and pepper.
Pour over chicken. Let simmer for 10 to 15 minutes, or until sauce is thickened to taste. Garnish with parsley.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat the oil in a large wok or frying pan on a medium heat. Pour in the egg and scramble. Set aside.
Heat the curry paste with 1 tbsp water until hot, then tip in the rice, stir to break up and toss to coat. Add the prawns and heat through.
Add back the scrambled eggs and line juice and stir through.
In a 2 quart saucepan, combine water and rice. Cover, and bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat, and simmer for 20 minutes.
In a medium size bowl, combine the chicken and curry powder, and toss to coat.
Coat a large nonstick skillet with oil Cook the chicken, stirring frequently, over medium-high heat for 4 minutes.
Mix in the zucchini and green onions, cook for 3 minutes. Pour in the coconut milk , continue cooking until sauce is hot, and chicken is cooked through. Garnish with parsley.
Cook the rice according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile heat oil in a large frying pan. Add the prawns, the jerk seasoning and rosemary, and cook for 1-2 mins.
Add rice to the pan and fry for 3-4 mins, then season with salt to taste and spoon into two bowls to serve. Garnish with lemon wedges.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Brown chicken in oil on both sides. Stir in oranges, and heavy cream.
Reduce heat to medium, and simmer until liquid has reduced by half. Remove chicken from pan when not longer pink in center.
Allow the sauce to reduce another 5 minutes, season to taste with salt and pepper.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Sprinkle pork  with salt and pepper. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes.
In the same skillet, saute green pepper and onion in remaining 1 teaspoon oil until almost tender, about 2 minutes.
Stir in broth, pineapple, tomato sauce and vinegar. Bring to a boil. Return pork to the pan. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 5 to 7 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in a heavy based skillet or pot over medium high heat.
Add curry paste (and garlic, ginger and lemongrass extras, if using) and cook for 2 to 3 minutes until it mostly "dries out"
Add chicken broth and coconut milk, mix to dissolve paste. Curry in a jar seasonings, Add 1 tsp fish sauce.  Mix then bring to simmer.
Add chicken, stir then lower heat to medium so it's bubbling gently. Cook 7 minutes.
Add eggplants, cook 5 minutes until soft. Taste sauce.
Add snow peas, cook 2 minutes until a bit softened, then stir through basil and lime juice.
Sauce should have reduced but will still be a be on the thin side, not thick - that's how it's should be. DO NOT keep simmering - sauce will darken.
Serve curry over jasmine rice or roti bread with garnishes of choice.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Mix together honey, soy sauce, ginger, garlic and hot sauce in a small bowl. Lightly salt and pepper the chicken strips.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chicken strips and brown on both sides, about 1 minute per side.
Pour the sauce over the chicken. Simmer uncovered until the sauce thickens, 8 to 10 minutes. Top with sesame seeds and onion.
Add the rice, a pinch of salt and the measured water to a pan with a lid and bring to a boil over a high heat. Cover and cook for 10 minutes.
In a bowl, combine the soy sauce, honey, ginger paste and garlic.
Heat a pan over a medium high heat with a drizzle of oil. Add the chicken pieces with a pinch of salt and fry for 5 minutes.
Add the teriyaki sauce and cook for 3 min further until the sauce is thickened and coating the chicken.
Cook the broccoli in lightly salted boiling water for 5 min or until tender. Drain once cooked.
Serve the chicken teriyaki over the rice with the broccoli to the side. Garnish with sesame seeds and the spring onion.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and minced chili to the skillet. Sauté for a few seconds until fragrant.
Add ground chicken to the skillet and cook until it's no longer pink, breaking it into smaller pieces as it cooks.
Stir in low-sodium soy sauce, fish sauce, and sugar. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and well-coated with the sauce.
Add fresh Thai basil leaves to the skillet and cook for about 1-2 minutes until they wilt.
Season with a pinch of salt to taste.
Serve the Pad Krapow Gai over cooked jasmine rice.
In a large saucepan, cook and stir onion in oil until tender and translucent, but not brown. Add bell pepper, beans and garlic powder.
Bring to boil. Stir in rice, return mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to simmer, and cover.
Let mixture simmer for 5 minutes. Remove pan from heat and let stand 5 minutes before serving.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Preheat your grill to medium-high heat.
Brush the tilapia fillet with olive oil.
Season both sides of the tilapia with salt and black pepper to taste.
Place the tilapia fillet on the preheated grill.
Grill for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until the tilapia is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
While the tilapia is grilling, in a bowl, combine the diced mango, diced red bell pepper, diced red onion, minced jalapeño pepper, chopped cilantro, and lime juice.
Mix well to create the mango salsa.
Place the rice on a plate.
Top the rice with the grilled tilapia fillet.
Spoon the mango salsa over the tilapia.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging,
In a medium pot of boiling water, simmer tofu for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and let drain in a colander lined with paper towels. When cool enough to handle, gently squeeze and pat dry.
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat 2 tablespoons oil. Add ginger and garlic and fry until fragnant.
Add tofu and let cook until golden on all sides. Season with salt and pepper, and remove from skillet and set aside.
Add mushrooms and edamame in the skilled and fry for 3 minutes or until tender, add the tofu and flaxseed oil and cook for another 2 minutes.
Serve on a plate with rice and garnish with red chilli pepper.
fennel seeds: In a small saucepan, combine the low-sodium beef broth, cinnamon stick, whole cloves, fennel seeds, and coriander seeds.
Heat the broth mixture over medium-low heat until it comes to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer gently for about 10-15 minutes to infuse the flavors. Remove the spices if preferred or leave them in for a stronger flavor.
While the broth is simmering, prepare the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Usually, you'll need to soak them in hot water for a few minutes until they soften. Drain and set aside.
Thinly slice the raw beef sirloin or eye of round.
Once the broth is ready, strain it to remove the spices if you left them in.
Place the cooked rice noodles in a serving bowl.
Arrange the thinly sliced raw beef over the noodles.
Pour the hot broth over the beef and noodles. The heat from the broth will partially cook the beef.
Top your Homemade Pho with thinly sliced onions, fresh bean sprouts, cilantro leaves, basil leaves, mint leaves, and sliced jalapeño peppers.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile bring the tomatoes, cumin, turmeric, salt, and water to a boil in a four quart saucepan, reduce heat to medium and maintain a simmer.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium-high heat, cook the fish in the oiled skillet until golden brown, 2 to 3 minutes per side. Transfer the fish to the saucepan.
Heat a separate skillet over medium heat, and toast the mustard seeds, cumin seeds and fennel seeds until fragrant.
Stir the spices into the saucepan. Simmer until the flavors integrate, about 10 minutes more. Serve hot.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Combine the spinach, parsley, basil, sage, garlic, fennel seeds, kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper in the bowl of a food processor and pulse until very finely chopped.
Set aside.
Place the ground turkey and egg in a large mixing bowl and use your hands to gently combine the turkey and egg but be careful not to overwork.

Add the finely chopped spinach and herb mixture, breadcrumbs and parmesan cheese and mix with your hands until thoroughly combined then shape into golf ball-sized meatballs and place on a baking sheet.
Mix 2 tablespoons tomato sauce with 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil and baste each of the meatballs.
Bake the meatballs for 15-20 minutes or until cooked through and golden brown.

Meanwhile, heat the jar of marinara sauce in a large pot over medium heat and prepare the instant polenta according to package directions. If you prefer your polenta thick and chunky the polenta is done.
For a creamier polenta, whisk in 1/2 cup milk at a time until the polenta is of desired consistency.
Season the polenta with kosher salt to taste.
Transfer the cooked meatballs to the warmed marinara sauce and cook for 3-5 minutes.
Serve over the cooked polenta and more Parmesan cheese.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a large bowl add the onion, carrot and garlic and mix well.
Add everything else and stir together really well making sure all the spices are distributed well.
You should have a really thick batter that is sticky but that can be squeezed together in your hands. Add water gradually, stirring as you go.
Roll into tight walnut sized balls and place on the prepared baking tray then bake for 25 minutes.
In a large dutch oven or stock pot, heat oil over medium heat, add leeks and saute until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add in carrots, mushrooms, thyme and fennel seeds and cook for 4 more minutes, stirring frequently.
Add in the buckwheat groats, broth, lemon juice and salt and pepper, bring to a boil, partially cover, reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Serve immediately.
Bring salted water to a boil and cook potatoes until tender. Drain and peel them.
Heat 1 tsp of oil in a large pan over medium heat. Fry the potatoes until lightly browned and crispy on all sides. Set aside on a paper towel to drain.
Fry fennel seeds just until the seeds crackle and darken slightly and then add in the cinnamon, cardamom, bay leaf.
Add the onion, garlic, ginger, aminos, and green chili to the spices and cook until translucent. Add the tomatoes and cook until soft. Puree in a blender.
Shimmer cumin seeds, add onion-tomato puree. Cook until the liquid evaporates and it forms a thick paste.
Add in the dry spices: chili powder, coriander, turmeric, and a pinch of salt. Cook for a minute and stir the cashew paste.
Poke the potatoes all over with a fork to allow the sauce to penetrate and add them to the pan. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Serve garnished with cilantro leaves.
In a large heavy-bottomed saucepan or Dutch oven, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Add the onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for another 30 seconds. Add the salt, cumin, fennel seeds, oregano, and chili powder.
Cook, stirring frequently, about 3 minutes. Stir arrowroot into the mixture.
Add the beans, chard, corn, and broth. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for about 30 minutes. Add the red pepper flakes and simmer for another 10 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately.
Cook the hash browns, heat the olive oil in a large oven-proof or cast iron skillet and add the hash browns, flipping occasionally until browned.
At 6 minutes, add the chili powder, garlic powder, cumin, fennel seeds, and kosher salt.
Cook another about 6 minutes until browned, continuing to flip so they don’t stick.
Add the cheese.
Reduce the heat to low.
Add the green onions (reserving a handful for the garnish) and cheese and mix it in.
Make divots for the eggs (all the way down to the bare pan) and add ¼ tsp butter into each well.
Crack 1 egg into each well, and top each with a pinch of kosher salt.
Add a sprinkle of additional shredded cheese on top.
Cook the eggs on the stovetop.
Increase the heat to medium low and cook for 3 minutes on the stove.
Meanwhile, preheat the broiler.
Finish in the broiler.
Transfer the skillet to the broiler and cook for 3 to 4 minutes until the whites of the eggs are set.
Garnish with salsa, reserved green onions, and fresh cilantro.
Serve warm.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Quickly saute shrimp until they turn pink, but are not cooked through, remove from skillet and set aside.
Add the red peppers, onion, garlic, fennel seeds, and thyme, cook until the onion softens, about 8 minutes.
Stir in white wine, tomato paste, and diced tomatoes. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer until the flavors blend, about 10 minutes.
Add the shrimp, and simmer until they are cooked and turn opaque, about 3 minutes. Stir in the basil, and season to taste with salt and pepper before serving.
Serve with rice.
Add 4 oz onion, ginger, garlic in a grinder jar and pulse to form a smooth paste and set aside
To create the spice mix, add all the spices in a grinder jar and pulse to make a smooth powder and set aside.

Heat oil in a large non-stick pot. Add the onion paste and saute for 4 minutes over medium heat. Then add the remaining onion, green bell pepper, spice mix and saute for 2 minutes. Now add the tomato, ketchup and saute for another 2 minutes. Add yogurt and mix everything.
Cover and cook this for 3 minutes on medium heat. Add the cubed paneer and saute. Let this simmer for 2 minutes then turn off the heat.
Transfer to serving plate and sprinkle some parsley on top. Serve hot.
Crush fennel seeds with the bottom of a glass or chop with a knife to break apart the seeds.
Combine crushed fennel with the garlic, chopped sun-dried tomatoes, salt, sage, thyme, pepper, red pepper flakes and water.
Add pork to a large bowl and add seasonings. Mix until seasoning is evenly incorporated but do not overmix.
Portion mixture into 2 oz each. Form into 3-inch patties using your hands. Place on a flat plate or tray and separate layers with parchment.
Heat oil in a skillet. Add patties to the pan without overcrowding and cook for 4 minutes per side. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
In a Dutch oven, cook sausage, ground beef, onion, and garlic over medium heat until well browned.
Stir in crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, tomato sauce, and water. Season with basil, fennel seeds, Italian seasoning, salt, pepper and half of parsley.
Simmer, covered, for about 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook lasagna noodles in boiling water for 8 to 10 minutes.
Drain noodles, and rinse with cold water. In a mixing bowl, combine ricotta cheese with egg, remaining parsley, and salt.
To assemble, spread 1 1/2 cups of meat sauce in the bottom of a baking dish. Arrange noodles lengthwise over meat sauce.
Spread with one half of the ricotta cheese mixture. Top with a third of mozzarella cheese slices.
Spoon 1 1/2 cups meat sauce over mozzarella, and sprinkle with 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese.
Repeat layers, and top with remaining mozzarella and Parmesan cheese.  Cover with foil.
Bake in preheated oven for 25 minutes. Remove foil, and bake an additional 25 minutes.
Add the olive to a deep pot over a medium high heat, add the chicken and a pinch of salt and black pepper and frying until lightly golden. Remove and set aside.
Add the onion and red pepper and fry until softened. Add in the garlic and fry for a further 30 seconds. Return the chicken to the pot and add the fennel seeds.
Add paprika and tomato paste and stir to coat. Pour in the chopped tomatoes, beans and stock, bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover and simmer for 20 minutes.
Taste and season as needed with salt and black pepper. Serve immediately.
Heat olive oil in a pot. Add the crushed spices, onion, garlic and ginger. Saute till the onion softens and starts browning.
Add the lamb cubes and get a nice sear. Once done, add a little water, about ¼ cup and deglaze the pot.
Add the tomatoes, salt, turmeric powder, coriander powder and meat masala powder. Mix well. Cook for 30 minutes.
Serve into bowls and add garnish with scallion.
In a mixing bowl, combine chickpea flour, baking powder, turmeric powder, cumin seeds, fennel seeds, chili powder, and salt.
Gradually add water to the dry ingredients and whisk until you get a smooth, thick batter. The consistency should be similar to pancake batter.
Add the sliced fennel and onion to the batter and mix until the vegetables are well coated.
Heat oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Once the oil is hot, drop spoonfuls of the fennel and onion batter into the oil. Fry in batches, making sure not to overcrowd the pan.
Fry the pakoras for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the pakoras from the oil and transfer them to a plate lined with paper towels to drain any excess oil.
Serve the fennel and onion pakoras hot with your favorite chutney or dipping sauce.
buckwheat groats: Add the rinsed groats along with other ingredients into blender and blend until smooth and homogeneous batter forms.
Take a non-stick pan. Add a few drops of oil onto the pan and swipe it off with folded kitchen paper.
Put a ladleful of batter onto the pan and even it out with a ladle. Fry until it is done on both sides, flipping in the middle. Serve immediately.
In a saucepan bring water to boil. Add uncooked buckwheat groats. Cover the pot and simmer for 10 minutes. Set aside.
Heat the butter and oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Saute the onion until softened.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook for about 5 minutes on all sides. Make sure the mushrooms render as much moisture as possible.
Once mushrooms are well browned, and garlic and continue cooking on medium-low until garlic is fragrant.
Pour in the 2 tbsp of vegetable stock and cook for 2 minutes, to reduce the sauce slightly.
Add 1 tbsp of parsley. Cook the mushrooms for half a minute, until garlic is fragrant.
Season with salt and pepper, to taste. Serve porridge with mushrooms in two bowls and sprinkle with remaining parsley.
Add buckwheat groats and milk to a saucepan, bring it to a boil and cook for about 10 minutes over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally. Put on a plate and set aside,
In the second saucepan place eggs and fill the pan with cold water, 1 inch above the eggs. Bring the water to a boil and cook eggs for 7 minutes.
Remove the eggs from the hot water and place them in cold water. When they cool down, gently tap the eggs against a hard surface and peel away the shell.
