Cut the eggs in half and place them on a plate next to a buckwheat porridge. Add the cubed feta cheese and arugula mixed with cornsalad. Season with salt and pepper.
In a large dutch oven or stock pot, heat oil over medium heat, add leeks and saute until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add in carrots, mushrooms, thyme and fennel seeds and cook for 4 more minutes, stirring frequently.
Add in the buckwheat groats, broth, lemon juice and salt and pepper, bring to a boil, partially cover, reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Serve immediately.
Bring a saucepan of water to a boil. Prepare a bowl of ice water. Blanch parsley in the boiling water for 20 seconds. Transfer to ice water.
Drain the parsley, squeeze out the excess water and pat thoroughly dry. In a blender, puree the blanched parsley with2 tbsp olive oil. Season with salt.
Bring a medium saucepan of water to a boil. In a large skillet, toast the buckwheat groats over moderately high heat, shaking the pan often, for 3 minutes.
Let cool slightly, then transfer to the boiling water and simmer over low heat for 5 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Meanwhile, heat the remaining oil in the large skillet. Add mushrooms and season with salt and pepper. Cover and cook over moderate heat for 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix the parsley oil with the balsamic vinegar, lemon juice, garlic and soy sauce.
In a large bowl, toss the cooked buckwheat groats with the dressing and mushrooms. Serve immediately.
Place the mushrooms onto a large dry pot and cook them 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove and set aside.
Add the olive oil to the same pan and cook the onions over low-medium heat, 2 minutes.
Decrease heat to low-high. Add the chicken pieces and sear them for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until no longer pink.
Rinse under cool running water until the water runs clear. Drain it well. Add the buckwheat to the chicken, cook few minutes, to toast more. Add 2 cups of water right away.
Add the remaining water in 3 settings. Cook buckwheat for about 13 minutes. While cooking, season the buckwheat with herbs and seasonings.
When the buckwheat is ready, return the mushrooms, turn the heat off, and set aside. Serve immediately.
ginger paste: Add the rice, a pinch of salt and the measured water to a pan with a lid and bring to a boil over a high heat. Cover and cook for 10 minutes.
In a bowl, combine the soy sauce, honey, ginger paste and garlic.
Heat a pan over a medium high heat with a drizzle of oil. Add the chicken pieces with a pinch of salt and fry for 5 minutes.
Add the teriyaki sauce and cook for 3 min further until the sauce is thickened and coating the chicken.
Cook the broccoli in lightly salted boiling water for 5 min or until tender. Drain once cooked.
Serve the chicken teriyaki over the rice with the broccoli to the side. Garnish with sesame seeds and the spring onion.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add thinly sliced onions and sauté until they become translucent.
Stir in ginger paste and garlic paste, and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add diced chicken breast to the skillet and cook until the chicken is no longer pink.
Add thinly sliced bell pepper and diced tomato to the skillet, and cook for a few more minutes until the vegetables are slightly softened.
Sprinkle cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, chili powder, and salt over the chicken and vegetables. Mix well to combine.
Cook for another 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro before serving, if desired.
Heat oil in a nonstick pan and add cinnamon stick, garlic paste, ginger paste, and saute for 5 seconds in the oil.

Add chopped onions and saute until translucent.
Add chopped tomatoes. Saute and cook covered for couple of minutes until tomatoes are soft and mushy.
Mash the tomatoes with the spatula and saute again for 30 seconds. Fold in jack fruit.
Add turmeric powder, chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala and saute well. Cook covered for 3 minutes on a medium flame.
Remove the lid and saute again for 30 seconds. Add water, mix and cook covered for 2 minutes or until few boils.
Add coconut milk, salt. Mix and cook covered for 10 minutes on a low flame.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve warm.
Marinate the pieces with salt and ginger paste and keep aside for 5 minutes.
Mix cornflour, flour, ginger paste, salt in a bowl and make a batter using a little water.
Dip the paneer pieces in this batter and fry in hot oil until they turn golden.
Garnish with spring onions and serve.
Make the sauce: In a small bowl, mix together corn starch and 1 tablespoon of water to make slurry.
Set aside. In a small pot, whisk together all ingredients and 2 tablespoons of water until combined. Bring to a boil and lower heat to a simmer.
Whisk in the corn starch slurry and cook 1-2 minutes, until thickened. Set aside in a warm place.
In a steamer set, steam broccoli and carrots over boiling water for 4-5 minutes, until tender with a little bite.
Toss with a splash of soy sauce and mirin. Set aside in a warm place.
Cook the chicken: In a large skillet, heat 1 teaspoon of sesame oil and 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium high heat.
Add diced chicken and cook until golden and cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Pour half of the teriyaki sauce over the chicken and cook 1 more minute. Assemble the bowls:
Top with broccoli, carrots, and chicken. Drizzle with remaining teriyaki sauce, sesame seeds and chives.
In a large skillet over high heat, add 1 tbsp avocado oil. Add in the sliced steak and brown on both sides. Remove from pan to a plate and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add in 1 tbsp avocado oil. Add in the broccoli florets and saute until tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the steak back to the pan with the broccoli. Add in the soy sauce, swerve, garlic, ginger and broth. Stir to combine and let thicken for a few minutes.
Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In an oven-safe skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat on the stovetop.
Add diced onions, minced garlic, and ginger paste. Sauté until the onions become translucent.
Add the diced meat and cook until it's browned.
Stir in diced tomatoes and cook for a few minutes until they start to soften.
Add ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, chili powder, salt, and black pepper. Cook for another minute to release the spices' flavors.
Lower the heat and gently stir in plain yogurt. Simmer for a few more minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Place the skillet in the preheated oven and bake for about 15-20 minutes until the meat is fully cooked and tender.
Serve the Rogan Josh over the cooked rice.
In a large bowl, mix all powder ingredients, butter, egg, garlic and ginger paste, scallion, cilantro, almond flour, seasoning, and ground chicken.
When ready to cook, mix in tomatoes. Wet your palms with a little oil or water and shape the chicken mixture into round patties.
Heat oil on medium heat in a skillet and, once oil is hot, place the chicken patties in oil and let them fry for 5 minutes. Flip and cook for another 5 minutes.
Remove to a paper towel-lined plate. Place on a plate, topped with sliced tomatoes. Serve along with salad mix, seasoned with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, and vinegar.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat 1 tbsp oil and cook the fish cutlets for 2-3 minutes on each side,keep aside. Heat the remaining oil in pan.
Add cumin seeds and whole spices, let them crackle; add onion,ginger paste. Sauté until onions are golden brown.
Add tomato puree, salt and sauté for 10 minutes. Now,add rest of the spices and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add water and let it boil. Add the cooked fish pieces to the curry and simmer for 5 minutes.
Enjoy with rice.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meabwhile whisk teriyaki sauce, green onions, sherry, garlic, Sriracha sauce, and ginger paste together until smooth. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook tofu for 5 minutes. Flip with tongs and cook until browned on the second side, about 5 minutes more.
Pour reserved sauce into the skillet and cook until slightly thickened, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat.
Return the tofu to the skillet and toss to combine. Transfer the tofu to serving plates and spoon sauce on top just before serving with rice.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook noodles in the boiling water, about 2 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Meanwhile, combine soy sauce and fish sauce in a small bowl, set aside.
Heat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat for 1 to 2 minutes. Pour in oil. Add curry paste, garlic, and ginger paste, cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add coconut milk in intervals, stirring as you pour it in to keep the sauce from separating.
Add pork strips, carrots and kale and cook until pork is cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Add drained noodles to the skillet, then add soy sauce mixture. Simmer until sauce has thickened, 4 to 5 minutes.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add the diced onions and sauté until they turn translucent.
Add ginger paste and garlic paste, and sauté for another minute.
Add the diced tomatoes and cook until they soften.
Stir in the red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, and coriander powder. Mix well.
Pour in the coconut milk and add salt to taste. Stir until the sauce is well combined.
Gently place the white fish fillet into the curry.
Cover the pan and let it simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily.
Serve your Goan Fish Curry hot, garnished with chopped cilantro with cooked rice.
Heat a heavy bottomed vessel with oil. Add onions and fry until translucent.
Add ginger paste and fry until fragrant. Add carrot along with slit green chili, salt.
Fry till tomatoes turns mushy and add all the powders. Cook for a minute.
Add beans, 3 cups of water, bay leaves, and cook for 35 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F.

In a food processor, puree ½ red onion, serrano chilis, cashews, yogurt, ginger, garlic and lime juice until smooth. Remove lid and add the Kashmiri chili powder, fenugreek leaves, salt, cumin, garam masala, coriander and turmeric. Pulse several times until blended well.
In a large bowl add the chicken, marinade and mix well. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate to marinate for 30 minutes.

Thread marinated chicken, peppers and onions on skewers, alternating between each (see image). Spray both sides of kebabs with cooking spray. Place a wire rack over a lined baking sheet. Spray with cooking spray to avoid sticking and lay kebabs on top.
Cook for 15 minutes. Turn oven broiler on and broil skewers for 5 minutes, or until slightly charred and cooked through.
Remove from oven and serve with mint chutney and red onion.
To a medium sized bowl, add plum jam, hoisin, gochujang, soy sauce, ginger and garlic. Mix the vinegar and flax, then whisk it into the jam mixture. Set aside.
Over high heat, add oil to the wok or skillet and wait a minute for it to get hot. Add pork and toss to cook, stirring to quickly brown on all sides.
Add the vegetables and cook for 3 minutes, or until tender. Add the stir fry sauce. Cook for 3 more minutes, stirring frequently. Serve immediately.
Heat oil and add cumin seeds. When they splutter, add onions and ginger-garlic pastes. Stir fry till fat separates.
Add the garam masala, coriander powder, chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt and yogurt and saute till fat separates.
Mix in the fish and cook over high heat till opaque. Mix in the browned onions, coriander, green chillies and biryani masala.
To make the rice, heat oil and add cloves, peppercorns, cinnamon and cardamoms. Mix well and cook till rice is tender but still holds its shape.
To serve, put the fish layer at the bottom of the dish, cover with the rice and sprinkle the saffron-milk mixture.
Keep or over low heat for about 15 minutes. Mix to break up the layers and serve.
Puree tomato and onion in a blender.

In a pan heat oil on a low flame and add ginger paste and saute for 1 minutes. Add masala powder and tomato puree and wait for it to boil. Add potato and peas with little water and let it cook for few minutes with covered lid.
Now add Paneer and gently mix. Do not over mix as Paneer will crumble to pieces. Add salt to taste.
Let the curry cook for few minutes and when the oil starts to float above garnish with a handful of parsley leaves.
Serve hot.
Heat oil in a pot over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent.
Add the ginger paste and garlic paste to the pot and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add the cubed lamb meat to the pot and cook until it's browned on all sides.
Stir in the red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and salt. Mix well to coat the meat with the spices.
Pour in the water and bring the mixture to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for about 1-1.5 hours, or until the meat is tender and cooked through.
Check the seasoning and adjust salt if needed.
Serve the Nihari Lamb hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.

Make a few slits on the chicken and mix with 2 cups of yogurt, ginger garlic paste, ½ tbsp garam masala, ½ tsp salt, 1 tsp red chili powder, turmeric and lemon juice. Cover and set this aside for 1 hour.
Meanwhile, add rice to a large pot and rinse it. Drain and set aside.
Heat oil in a large pot and add thinly sliced onion. Fry them on medium heat and stir often.
Add marinated chicken and sautee for 5 minutes. Lower the frame completely. Cover and cook until the chicken is soft, tender and completely cooked.
Add 1 cup of plain yogurt, ½ tsp garam masala, ½ tsp red chili powder and peppermint leaves and mix well.
Layer rice all over the chicken and add salted water. Cover the pot and cook on a medium heat until the rice is completely done. When the rice is cooked, gently fluff it up with a fork.
Take yogurt in a pan in which you are going to make kadhi. Add besan, ginger paste, green chilies, turmeric powder, and salt.
Whisk it well so there are no lumps. You can use a hand blender instead. Then add water. And mix it very well and make a lump-free, thin batter.
Put it on medium heat to boil with stirring continuously. You must stir constantly to avoid curdling and separating yogurt.
When it comes to a boil lower the heat to simmer and prepare the tempering.
Heat the ghee or oil in a small pan. Once hot add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Let them sizzle.
Then add dry red chili, cinnamon stick, cloves. After a few seconds, you will get a nice fragrance from whole spices.
Let it simmer for another 5 minutes so raw taste and smell of besan goes away.
buffalo sauce: Preheat your oven to 450°F.
In a bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, water, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika until you have a smooth batter.
Dip each cauliflower floret into the batter, coating it evenly, and place it on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the cauliflower florets in the preheated oven for about 20 minutes or until they start to brown and become crispy.
While the cauliflower is baking, mix the buffalo sauce with melted butter (if using) in a separate bowl.
Once the cauliflower is done baking, remove it from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Toss the baked cauliflower in the buffalo sauce mixture until they are well coated.
Return the coated cauliflower to the oven for an additional 5-10 minutes or until they reach your desired level of crispiness.
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Pat dry the chicken wings with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
In a bowl, toss the chicken wings with olive oil, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.
Arrange the chicken wings in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the wings are golden brown and crispy.
Once the wings are done baking, transfer them to a clean bowl.
Pour buffalo sauce over the wings and toss until they are evenly coated.
Cook the pasta in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a separate saucepan over medium heat, combine the chicken, shredded cheddar cheese, buffalo sauce, and Greek yogurt or sour cream.
Stir until the cheese is melted and the mixture is heated through, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked pasta to the saucepan and stir until well combined.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the macaroni is heated through.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cook the macaroni pasta according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.

In a small saucepan, combine the cooked chicken breast, shredded cheddar cheese, buffalo sauce, and plain Greek yogurt. Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the cheese is melted and the mixture is well combined.
Add the cooked macaroni pasta to the saucepan with the buffalo chicken mixture. Stir until the pasta is evenly coated with the sauce.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat your oven to 350°F
In a small mixing bowl, combine the cooked chicken and buffalo sauce, stirring until the chicken is evenly coated.
In another small bowl, mix together the softened cream cheese and sour cream until smooth.
Add the cream cheese mixture to the chicken mixture, stirring until well combined.
Fold in the shredded cheddar cheese and crumbled blue cheese.
Transfer the mixture to a small oven-safe dish.
Transfer the mixture to a small oven-safe dish.
Garnish with chopped green onions.
Preheat your air fryer to 390°F.
In a bowl, toss the chickpeas with olive oil until evenly coated.
Add the buffalo sauce and ranch to the chickpeas, and toss until they are evenly coated with the seasoning mixture.
Place the seasoned chickpeas in the air fryer basket in a single layer.
Air fry at 390°F for 15-20 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through, or until the chickpeas are crispy and golden brown.
Once done, remove the chickpeas from the air fryer and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Place the chicken breast in the slow cooker.
Pour the hot sauce over the chicken.
Add the ghee.
Cover and cook on low for 3-4 hours or on high for 1.5-2 hours until the chicken is tender and cooked through.
Shred the chicken using two forks directly in the slow cooker, allowing it to soak up the sauce.
Prepare the lettuce leaves for wrapping.
Scoop the buffalo chicken onto the lettuce leaves.
Add optional toppings if desired.
Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 425°F.
Place the chicken wingettes in a bowl and drizzle them with olive oil. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Toss to coat evenly.
Arrange the seasoned chicken wingettes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or a greased baking rack placed on a baking sheet for crispier wings.
Bake the chicken wings in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, turning them halfway through the cooking time, or until they are cooked through and crispy.
While the wings are baking, mix the Buffalo sauce with ghee in a separate bowl.
Once the wings are done baking, remove them from the oven and let them cool slightly.
Toss the baked chicken wingettes in the Buffalo sauce and ghee mixture until they are well coated.
arrowroot powder: Pat the chicken breasts dry with paper towels and season them on both sides with sea salt and black pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add chicken breasts and sear for 10 minutes per side, until golden brown and almost cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the pan, cover with foil to keep warm, and set aside.
Meanwhile, in a small bowl, vigorously whisk the arrowroot powder into the chicken broth.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet. Cook for about 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the arrowroot/broth mixture, cream, sun dried tomatoes, and spinach. Increase the heat to bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 2 minutes, until the sauce thickens.
Add the chicken back to the pan and spoon the sauce over it. Cook for 2 minutes, until chicken is fully cooked through.
Wash pistachios, and blend with leek to make a fine powder.
Heat butter in a deep non-stick pan, add arrowroot powder and cook on a medium flame for about 1 minute. Add the vegetable stock, pistachio powder, and salt.
Mix gently and cook on a medium flame for a 3 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
On a large rimmed baking sheet, toss Brussels with olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Bake for 15 minutes. Transfer themto a large bowl and preheat broiler.
In a small skillet over medium heat, heat sesame oil. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute. Stir in arrowroot powder. Add soy sauce, water, vinegar and hoisin sauce.
Season with salt and pepper. Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until thickened, about 3 minutes.
Pour sauce over brussels sprouts and toss to combine. Return brussels sprouts to baking sheet and broil until brussels sprouts are glazed and sticky.
Garnish with sesame seeds and serve.
Add 1 tbsp ghee to bottom sauté pan on medium heat and add garlic, shallots, and celery. Cook until translucent and scrape into your sauce pan.
Add the remaining ingredients except arrowroot to the pan and cook uncovered for 5 minutes. Pull the lobster tails out of the broth and set them aside.
Add the arrowroot powder to the broth and stir together. Once thickened, use an immersion blender to create a smooth broth.
Cut the lobster tails in half using a sharp knife and pull the tail meat out of the shell.
Pour the chowder into bowls, and garnish with lobster.
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F.
Season the chicken breasts on both sides with salt and pepper. Place the flour, egg and panko crumbs into separate shallow dishes.
Coat the chicken breasts in flour, shaking off any excess. Dip them into the egg, and then press into the panko crumbs until well coated on both sides.
Place chicken in the hot air fryer, and cook 5 minutes per side, or until golden brown. Serve imemdiately.
Cover a baking sheet with paper towels. Set aside.
Drain the tomatoes by squeezing in a strainer. In a bowl, combine the arrowroot powder, yeast and baking powder. Set aside.
In another bowl, combine the drained tomatoes with the remaining ingredients, except for the oil. Stir in the arrowroot powder mixture.
Season with salt and pepper. With your hands, shape into 2 tbsp patties.
In a large non-stick skillet, heat the oil. Fry the patties over medium heat for about 2 minutes per side.
Drain on the baking sheet. Serve with yogurt.
Preheat the oven to 350°F. Spray a 2-quart casserole dish with cooking spray and set aside.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add cauliflower to the boiling water and allow to cook for 5 minutes. Remove and place it in a bowl filled with ice water.
In a large-size saucepan melt butter over medium heat. Whisk in arrowroot, salt, pepper, onion powder, and garlic powder. Allow to cook for 2 minutes while stirring.
Whisk in milk, continue to cook over medium heat while whisking until thickened. Remove from heat and stir in half of cheese until melted.
Mix cauliflower with cheese sauce and add to casserole baking dish. Top cauliflower with the remaining cheese. Bake for 20 minutes. Serve immediately.
Place potato in a large saucepan. Cover with water. Bring to the boil over high heat. Boil for 10 minutes or until tender. Drain. Return to pan.
Roughly mash. Set aside to cool completely. Combine tuna, peas, chives, parsley, dill, garlic and potato in a large bowl. Add arrowroot powder and egg.
Season well with salt and pepper. Mix well to combine. Shape some mixture into a patty. Toss lightly in breadcrumbs. Place on a tray.
Repeat with remaining potato mixture and breadcrumbs.
Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Cook patties, in batches, for 4 minutes each side or until golden and heated through.
Drain on paper towel. Serve immediately.
In a large pot, melt butter on meidum heat, then add onion, garlic, stir. Cook for 4 minutes, stirring time to time. Then, add potatoes, cook 5 minutes.
Next add arrowroot and flax, whisk well. Then pour vegetables broth, bay leaves, salt and pepper. Stir. Let simmer until soft and tender potatoes about 20 minutes.
Remove bay leaves. Adjust seasonning if needed. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
First prepare the enchilada sauce. In a medium sauce pot combine the oil, arrowroot powder, and chili powder. Heat the mixture until it begins to bubble. Cook 1 minute.
Slowly pour in some water while whisking. Add the tomato paste, cumin, garlic powder, cayenne pepper, cocoa powder, and salt.
Whisk until smooth and continue to heat over a medium flame. Let the sauce come up to a simmer. Once thickened, turn off the heat and set the sauce aside.
In a bowl, combine beans, avocado, tomato and corn. Stir everything together. Season with salt and garlic powder. Coat a casserole dish with non-stick spray.
Fill each tortilla with some filling and roll tightly. Once all of the filled enchiladas are in the dish, pour the enchilada sauce over top. Top with cheese.
Bake for 25 minutes. Serve immediately.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Once hot, add in the bacon to cook. Cook for 4 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon to a paper towel lined plate.
Add arrowroot starch to the coconut milk and mix well. Leaving at least 1 tbsp bacon grease in the pan, add in the chicken to cook. Season with salt and pepper.
Cook, stirring occasionally, for 6 minutes. Remove the cooked chicken from the pan and place on a plate.
Next, add in the onion. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add in the garlic and mushrooms. Cook, stirring frequently, for another 2 minutes.
Mix up the coconut milk and arrowroot starch again. Add in the coconut milk to the pan, stirring constantly for 2 minutes. Add spinach and tomatoes and stir.
Finally, add the chicken and bacon back into the pan. Turn the heat down to low and simmer for 2 minutes. Serve immediately, topped with parsley.
Whisk and stir all ingredients under “Paleo Crepes” in a large mixing bowl until there are no lumps.
Let the batter sit for 5 minutes.
Heat 1 tsp ghee in a cast iron skillet or non-stick skillet over medium heat.
When hot, lower the heat to medium-low, whisk the batter again for an additional 10 seconds.
Pour ¼ cup of the batter into the pan.
Quickly and gently swirl the pan around so that the batter coats the entire pan evenly.
Cook about 1-minute or until the edges start to crisp.
Carefully flip and cook the flip side about 30 seconds.
Transfer to a cooling rack and repeat with the remaining batter.
Combine/stir almond butter, cacao powder, coconut milk, and pinch of fine sea salt until creamy and smoothly.
Taste and adjust to your own liking.
In a large heavy-bottomed saucepan or Dutch oven, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Add the onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for another 30 seconds. Add the salt, cumin, fennel seeds, oregano, and chili powder.
Cook, stirring frequently, about 3 minutes. Stir arrowroot into the mixture.
Add the beans, chard, corn, and broth. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for about 30 minutes. Add the red pepper flakes and simmer for another 10 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to to 425°F.
Heat up a pan with half of the olive oil and cook the onions until they are transparent. Add the garlic and stir for a few seconds.
Add the cod. When it start cooking you can press with a wooden spoon and separate the flakes.
Add the arrowroot powder and stir until it has some color. Start adding the milk slowly while you stir. Add salt. Let it cool, then fill the piquillo peppers.
Heat up a pan with the remaining olive oil and cook the shallots until they are transparent. Add the red bell peppers and stir for 2 minutes.
Add the white wine and cook for another 2 minutes. Add the cream and stir. Blend the whole mix and put back on the pan to cook for a few minutes. Season well.
Put the peppers in an oven proof recipient and pour the sauce in. Cook for 10 minutes. Serve immediately.
Add wine and broth to a saucepan. Heat over medium-high heat, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium and let boil until reduced to ¾ cup.
Meanwhile season both sides of chicken with salt and pepper. Dredge each side in arrowroot powder.
Melt half of the butter with half of the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and let sear for 4 minutes per side.
Transfer chicken to a plate. Tent with foil. Reduce burner temperature to medium heat. Melt remaining butter with olive oil, add mushrooms.
Saute mushrooms, just tossing occasionally, until mushrooms have shrunk and are golden brown. Add garlic during last 1 minute of sauteing.
Remove pan from heat then pour in marsala reduction, thyme and oregano. Return to heat, bring to a simmer, while scraping up browned bits from the bottom.
Off heat stir in heavy cream, season sauce with salt and pepper to taste. Return chicken breasts to pan, spoon sauce over top.
Sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately.
Heat a stock pot over medium heat and add ghee. Add onion and stir to coat, cook 2 minutes. Add garlic, season with salt and pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add the arrowroot powder to the mixture and stir, then slowly pour in the broth, stirring continuously. Bring to a boil, stirring.
Once boiling and thick, lower to a simmer and stir in the chicken, coconut cream, nutritional yeast, and lemon juice. Raise the heat to medium and cook 3 minutes.
Stir in the spinach in batches, then the artichoke hearts, and simmer another 2 minutes. Taste and season with salt and pepper as desired. Serve hot.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain and reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water.
In a blender combine the cashews, garlic, nutritional yeast, salt, and water. Blend until smooth and creamy, pausing to scrape down the sides as needed.
Melt 1 tbsp of olive oil in a medium pot over medium-low and add the arrowroot powder. Let the mixture cook until the powder is golden and a couple of shades darker.
Slowly pour in the garlic-cashew mixture while whisking to prevent clumps. Turn up the heat to medium and bring the sauce to a simmer and let it simmer until thickened.
Pour the sauce into the pasta and stir to coat. Add some of the reserved pasta water if necessary. Add salt and pepper to taste.
Trim off the ends of the sprouts and remove any damaged leaves. Cut the sprouts in half, then thinly slice.
Heat the remaining oil in a skillet over medium heat and add the shaved sprouts with some salt. Cook about 6 minutes.
Place the pasta in a serving dish. Top with the cooked sprouts and serve.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package instructions. Drain, reserve 1 cup of the pasta water and set aside.
Heat oil in a large covered skillet over medium heat. Add the asparagus and cook for a minute or two. Add ¼ cup water and quickly cover.
Allow the asparagus to steam for 2 minutes. Uncover and add the mushrooms. Add a splash of water if it seems dry and cover. Cook for about 2 minutes.
Uncover, add the garlic, and cook for a minute or two. Add the vegetables to the pasta and combine well. Keep warm on the lowest heat setting.
Put pasta water into a small saucepan. Place it over medium high heat and add the bouillion, basil and nutritional yeast.
As it comes to a boil, whisk together the milk and arrowroot in a small bowl and add it to the saucepan. Cook, stirring, until it boils and thickens slightly.
Pour it over the pasta and vegetables, and stir to coat. Serve immediately.
Combine the beef, ginger, soy sauce, and arrowroot powder in a medium bowl. Stir well. Add sesame oil and stir until the beef is lightly coated.
Combine oyster-flavored sauce and the sherry in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat a 14-inch flat-bottomed carbon-steel wok over high heat. Swirl in olive oil. Add beef and cook for 2 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
Add bok choy and broth to the pan. Cover and cook until the bok choy greens are bright green and almost all the liquid has been absorbed, about 1 minute.
Return the beef to the pan, add the reserved sauce, and stir-fry until the beef is just cooked through and the bok choy is tender-crisp, about 1 minute. Serve immediately.
Combine ghee, onions, and salt and cook on low for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add in the arrowroot and stir. Add in the stock, coconut aminos, apple cider vinegar, thyme and bay leaf.
Turn the heat up to high and cook for an additional 15 minutes. Remove the bay leaf and serve immediately.
chickpea flour: Heat 1 tbsp of the olive oil in a large medium pan and add the chopped onions and potatoes.
Cook on a low heat for about 20 minutes until the potatoes are cooked through.
Meanwhile, mix the chickpea flour and 1 cup water together in a bowl with the salt and pepper
Once the potatoes and onions are cooked, add them to the chickpea mix and stir gently to combine.
Rinse the frying pan and heat with the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the chickpea mixture and cook on a medium heat for 5 minutes.
Flip it over and cook on another side.
Make sure the beets are small enough to go through the juicer, then proccess them and make beetroot juice. Set aside.
Mix together the chickpea flour, beetroot juice, eggs, baking powder and salt. Set aside.
Heat up a non-stick pan to high heat for a few minutes until hot, then reduce heat to medium. For each crepe add a ladle-full of batter into to pan and spread out.
Cook for 2 minutes, then flip and cook for another minute. Repeat process with remaining batter. To serve, spread sour cream on crepes, fold them and sprinkle with chives.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Add onions to a large bowl and separate. Add in all the spices, salt, chickpea flour and rice flour. Sprinkle in the oil and mix in.
Add in water, just a little bit at a time so that you get a pasty pakora mix. Take spoonfuls of this mixture and place on a parchment lined baking sheet.
Bake for 17 minutes. Once the pakoras are cooked, let them cool for a few minutes.
Mix tomato paste, vinegar, onion powder, oregano and some salt in a bowl. Serve with pakoras.
Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a baking tray with greaseproof paper.
Add the peas to a medium saucepan, cover with water and boil, according to packet's instructions and drain well.
Heat the olive oil in a frying pan and saute the onion and garlic for a few minutes, until softened.
Add the peas, onions and garlic to a food processor and pulse until it becaomes a thick paste.
Stir in the chickpea flour, bicarbonate of soda.Make patties and brish fritters with oil. Bake for 15 minutes.
Preheat oven to 480°F.
In a bowl, add chickpea flour, salt, 1 cup water and blend with an immersion blender for 5 minutes till completely smooth.
Add the oil to the baking sheet and spread it out evenly.
Gently pour the chickpea batter into the center of the pan, but first over a spoon and not pouring directly into the pan.
Bake for 15 minutes and serve.
Add all the chopped vegetables to a bowl. Add the rice flour, chickpea flour, salt, spices, cilantro, baking soda and mix everything well.
Add in 1 cup of water to the mixture, stir well until combined. Let this mixture sit for 5 minutes before starting to make waffles.
Oil and preheat your waffle maker. Pour and spread the batter on your waffle maker, spread evenly with a spatula.
Cook until golden brown and crisp, to preference. Remove the waffle from the waffle maker, repeat for all of the batter. Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook rice according to package instructions. Set aside.
For the "egg" mixture, mix the flour, black salt, salt, olive oil and water together with a whisk, until you have a smooth batter.
In a large frying pan, heat up the sesame oil on high heat. Then add the "egg" mixture, let it cook for 30 seconds and then scramble it up with a spatula.
Continue until the egg mixture is all well cooked. Clear some space in the pan, add the peas, carrot, corn and onions. Cook for 30 seconds before stirring in with rice.
Mix the garlic powder with soy sauce, and pour it all over the rice. Stir everything up and serve immediately.
In a mixing bowl, combine chickpea flour, rice flour, turmeric powder, chili powder, cumin powder, garam masala, baking soda, and salt.
Gradually add water to the dry ingredients to form a smooth batter with a thick consistency.
Heat oil in a deep frying pan or wok over medium-high heat.
Dip the chicken pieces into the batter, ensuring they are evenly coated.
Carefully drop the coated chicken pieces into the hot oil, a few at a time, and fry until they turn golden brown and crispy. Make sure not to overcrowd the pan.
Remove the fried chicken pakoras using a slotted spoon and place them on a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.
Repeat the frying process with the remaining chicken pieces.
Finely blend half of the arugula, basil and milk and strain into a bowl. Stir in the egg and ¾ of the olive oil. Mix the flour and salt in a mixing bowl.
Gradually whisk in the milk mixture until a smooth dough forms. Cover and leave to rest. Heat the remaining oil over medium heat in a pancake pan. Bake the crepes.
Serve the crepes along with avocado slices, cherry tomatoes, arugula and topped with sour cream and sesame seeds.
Preheat the oven to  350°F. Roast the asparagus for 10 minutes.
While the asparagus is cooking, mix the chickpea flour with all dry ingredients in a medium mixing bowl. Add the water and chives.
Check the batter and add water by the tablespoon if it is not a pourable consistency. It should be like thick pancake batter.
Preheat oil in an iron skillet over high heat. Once it’s hot, reduce the heat to medium and pour half of the batter into the center of the skillet.
Cover tightly and cook for 4 minutes. Add half the asparagus to one side of the omelet and fold the other half over it. Cover and cook for another 3 minutes.
Repeat with remaining ingredients for one more omelette. Serve warm.
Place the dried chickpeas in a bowl and cover them with water. Let them soak overnight. Drain before using.
In a food processor, add the soaked and drained chickpeas along with chopped parsley, cilantro, diced onion, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and baking soda.
Pulse the mixture until everything is well combined and forms a coarse paste. Scrape down the sides of the processor bowl if needed.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and stir in the flour. The mixture should hold together when formed into a ball. If it's too wet, add a bit more flour.
Shape the falafel mixture into small balls or patties using your hands. If the mixture is sticky, wet your hands slightly to prevent sticking.
Heat vegetable oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Make sure the oil is hot enough before frying the falafel.
Carefully place the shaped falafel into the hot oil, cooking them in batches to avoid overcrowding the pan. Fry for 2-3 minutes on each side or until they're golden brown and crispy.
Once done, transfer the fried falafel to a plate lined with paper towels to absorb excess oil.
Once the falafel is cooked, warm the whole-grain pita bread in the oven for a few minutes.
Carefully open the warm pita halves.
Stuff each pita half with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, diced cucumber, and diced red onion.
Place the baked falafel balls into the pita pockets on top of the vegetables.
Drizzle the prepared tzatziki sauce over the falafel.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Combine chickpea flour, water, 1 tablespoon olive oil, garlic powder, onion powder, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper in bowl whisk until smooth. Let sit for 15 minutes.
Heat a cast iron skillet over medium heat. Add 1 tablespoon olive oil and swirl around to coat skillet.
Pour batter into skillet and cook until edges are golden, about 2 minutes.
Place skillet in the preheated oven and bake until socca is set, about 12 minutes.
Remove from the oven and top with tomato and parsley. Drizzle with remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil.
Return to the oven and bake until golden and crispy, about 5 minutes.
Preheat the air fryer to 350°F.
Combine cauliflower, potatoes, chickpea flour, water, red onion, salt, garlic, curry powder, coriander, cayenne, and cumin in a large bowl. Set aside to rest for 10 minutes.
Spray the basket of the air fryer with cooking spray. Spoon 2 tbsp of cauliflower mixture into the basket and flatten. Mist the top of each pakora with non-stick spray.
Cook for 8 minutes. Flip and cook 8 additional minutes. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate. Repeat with remaining batter. Serve immediately.
Preheat your oven to 375°F .
In a bowl, whisk together chickpea flour, baking powder, 1/2 cumin, 1/2 coriander, 1/2 turmeric, and salt.
Add water to the dry ingredients and mix until a thick batter forms.
In a small saucepan, combine crushed tomatoes, cumin, coriander, turmeric, paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper. Heat over medium heat until it begins to simmer.
In an oven-safe dish, heat olive oil in the oven until it's hot.
Carefully remove the dish from the oven and pour the chickpea batter into the hot oil.
Pour the simmering tomato sauce over the chickpea batter.
Return the dish to the oven and bake for about 20-25 minutes or until the chickpea dumplings are cooked through and the sauce is bubbly.
Carefully remove from the oven.
In a mixing bowl, combine the chickpea flour, water, ground cumin, ground coriander, turmeric powder, chili powder, and salt. Mix until you have a smooth batter.
Add the finely chopped mixed vegetables to the batter and stir until they are evenly coated.
Preheat your air fryer to 375°F.
Lightly grease the air fryer basket with cooking spray.
Drop spoonfuls of the pakora batter into the air fryer basket, leaving space between each pakora.
Air fry at 375°F for 8-10 minutes, or until the pakoras are golden brown and crispy on the outside.
Once done, remove the pakoras from the air fryer and let them cool slightly before serving.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, combine the canned chickpeas, chickpea flour, and water. Mix until you have a thick batter.
In a bowl, combine the canned chickpeas, chickpea flour, and water. Mix until you have a thick batter.
Heat the olive oil in a small pan over medium heat.
Add the diced onions and sauté for about 2-3 minutes, or until they become translucent.
Add the diced tomatoes and curry powder to the pan. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to soften and the curry powder to become fragrant.
Pour the chickpea batter into the pan, spreading it evenly to cover the tomato mixture.
Cover the pan and let it cook for about 5-7 minutes, or until the chickpea batter has set and cooked through.
Once the chickpea dumpling is cooked, remove it from the pan and cut it into serving portions.
Garnish with fresh cilantro, if desired.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Add cauliflower rice, breadcrumbs, chickpea flour, protein powder, garlic powder, ginger powder, salt, and pepper to a large bowl and then mix everything.
Once evenly mixed, scoop 1 tbsp of batter and roll in your palm to form a meatball shape.
Make all the meatballs and keep in the fridge for 15 minutes. Place the meatballs in a parchment-lined baking tray, spray with oil, and bake for 30 minutes.
Heat a nonstick pan and add the marinara sauce in it. Once the marinara sauce has simmered for a few minutes, turn off the heat. Mix in the meatballs.
Serve hot, garnished with basil leaves.
Preheat the oven to 425°F. Grease the bottom of a baking dish with olive oil, then set aside.
In a mixing bowl, combine the ground chicken, flour, half of the peas, chopped Italian parsley, parmesan, sea salt, pepper, garlic powder, and egg and mix very well.
Using your hands, form the mixture into 2-inch meatballs. Place the meatballs into the baking dish, separating them a little bit so they can evenly bake.
Put into the oven and bake for 15 minutes, until almost cooked through, and then turn the oven to broil for 3 minutes to create a crispy, golden-brown top.
Heat the remaining olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the peas and cook for 5 minutes, season with salt and pepper. Serve the meatballs with peas.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Heat a medium pan on medium-high heat, add olive oil, onion, and garlic. Cook for 3 minutes until the onion and garlic become translucent.
Add in basil, parsley, oregano, and thyme, cook for 3 minutes until the herbs are well combined and aromatic.
Add sun-dried tomatoes and olives, cook through for 1 minute, and then remove from the heat. Let cool slightly.
To a large bowl, add flour, baking powder, and salt. Mix to combine. Then add water, olive oil, and apple cider vinegar to the dry ingredients. Mix.
Add the onion and sun-dried tomato mixture from the pan to the bowl and carefully mix it through the batter until just combined. Careful not to over-mix.
Divide the batter between the muffin tin wells. Put an olive and a sprig of fresh thyme on top for decoration. Bake for 20 minutes.
Cut the cauliflower into small-ish florets so they are wither bite-sized or slightly smaller.
Wash and pat dry.
Mix together the chickpea flour, salt, turmeric, garlic, ginger and water to make the batter.
It should stick to the back of a spoon but not be so thick it doesn't run off at all.
Put the pieces of cauliflower into the batter and mix well so the pieces of cauliflower are well coated in the batter.
Spoon bits in to the gaps if needed.
Heat some vegetable or other flavorless oil in a small skillet/frying pan - around ⅓ in (1 cm) deep or a little more.
Once it is warm at a medium heat, take spoonfuls of the cauliflower and place in the pan (generally 1-2 pieces of cauliflower in each spoonful, depending on size).
Once the pakora have been cooking around 2-3 minutes and are going golden on the bottom, turn over with a slotted spoon and cook on the other side.
Turn to any other side not yet cooked if needed.
Once golden all over, remove from the pan with a slotted spoon to drain off excess oil then put on paper towel to drain.
Repeat with the rest of the batter.
Serve either as they are or with raita (spiced yogurt dip).
white vinegar: Heat sesame oil in a skillet over medium heat; cook and stir eggplant until softened, 8 to 10 minutes.
Whisk miso, soy sauce, ginger, vinegar, water, and garlic together in a bowl until sauce is evenly combined, add to eggplant mixture.
Cook and stir eggplant mixture until sauce is thickened and glossy, 3 to 4 minutes. Top mixture with sesame seeds and green onion.
In a large bowl combine red cherry tomatoes, onion, cucumber, ciabatta bread and basil leaves.
Make dressing with black pepper, olive oil, white wine vinegar and salt, mix until combined.
Add the dressing to the salad and toss gentnly.
In a medium sized pot bring water to boil, add rice and cook according to box instructions. Remove from heat and set aside.
Add pieces of chicken breasts, olive oil, paprika, cumin, ground chili pepper, salt and black pepper. Mix well until chicken is coated with spices and oil.

Heat a non-stick pan on medium heat.
Add chicken in pan and cook for 10 minutes on each side until thoroughly cooked and set aside.
In a large bowl add salad mix, corn, beans, rice, tomato and cooked chicken.
For salsa finely chop cilantro and onion. Add in a small bowl and garnish with vinegar, lime juice and salt. Mix well until everything is combined.
Add salsa, yogurt and cheese on top.
In a mixing bowl, combine the thinly sliced cucumber, sour cream, white vinegar, chopped fresh dill (if using), salt, and pepper.
Toss until the cucumber slices are evenly coated with the sour cream mixture.
Serve immediately or refrigerate for a few minutes to chill before serving.
Heat the oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the mushrooms, season with salt and pepper, and cook for about 5 minutes.
Bring a saucepan of water to the boil. Add vinegar and remove from heat. Break 2 eggs into a saucer.
Slide into water 1 at a time. Cover and leave for 3 minutes for soft poached. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towel. Repeat with remaining eggs.
Place lettuce and mushrooms onto toast and top with eggs. Sprinkle with pepper and serve,
In a medium bowl, stir together the olive oil, ranch dressing, Worcestershire sauce, rosemary, salt, lemon juice, white vinegar and pepper.
Let stand for 5 minutes. Place chicken in the bowl, and stir to coat with the marinade. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Preheat the grill for medium-high heat. Thread chicken onto skewers and discard marinade.
Lightly oil the grill grate. Grill skewers for 8 to 12 minutes, or until the chicken is no longer pink in the center, and the juices run clear.
Cook rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile mix chicken broth, soy sauce, vinegar, and cayenne pepper together in a bowl until sauce is smooth.
Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat, saute chicken and garlic until chicken is no longer pink in the center, 5 to 10 minutes.
Add bell peppers and saute for about 1 minute. Stir sauce into chicken chicken mixture and cook for1 minute. Stir peanuts into chicken mixture and garnish with green onions.
In a medium bowl, stir together vinegar, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.
Heat oil in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Stir in sesame seeds, and reduce heat to medium. Cook until seeds are brown, about 5 minutes.
Remove seeds with a slotted spoon, and stir into vinegar mixture. Mix in cucumber, green onions, and carrot. Cover, and refrigerate at least 5 minutes.
In a large bowl place arugula and top with white beans, green olives, red onion, and tuna.
Season with olive oil, salt and vinegar, and serve.
Combine milk with vinegar in a medium bowl and set aside for 5 minutes to "sour".
Combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large mixing bowl. Whisk egg and ghee into "soured" milk.
Pour the flour mixture into the wet ingredients and whisk until lumps are gone.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat, and coat with coconut oil. Pour batter onto the skillet, and cook until bubbles appear on the surface.
Flip with a spatula, and cook until browned on the other side. Garnish with fruit and pecans.
Mix flour and water together in a bowl until smooth. Stir in cabbage, bacon, eggs, sausage, green onions, shrimp, and cheese.
Preheat a griddle to 400°F and coat with oil. Pour in flour mixture in the shape of a pancake. Cook 6 minutes per side. Transfer to a serving plate.
Mix soy sauce, tomato sauce, and vinegar together in a small bowl to make okonomiyaki sauce. Drizzle over pancake. Garnish with panko and mayonnaise.
Place the diced potato in a small saucepan and cover with cold water. Add a pinch of salt.
Bring the water to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Cook the potato for about 10-12 minutes, or until tender but not mushy. Drain and let cool.
In a mixing bowl, combine mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, white vinegar, sugar, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Add the cooled potato, egg, onion, celery, dill pickles and bell pepper to the bowl. Gently stir to coat the ingredients with the dressing.
Taste and adjust the seasoning with additional salt, pepper, or other seasonings as desired.
Refrigerate the potato salad for at least 30 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld.
Remove any old leaves from the cabbage, cut stem off base, slice finely and add to a salad bowl.
Add carrot matchsticks and onion.
Toast almond flakes in a pan on medium until they turn a light golden brown, about 3-4 minutes, and add to salad.
Make the dressing by combining salt, pepper, 2 tsp peanut oil, vinegar, soy sauce, lime juice, garlic, ginger, and honey in a small bowl or glass jar, and stir thoroughly to combine. Set aside.
Cut tofu in half, then into small cubes.
Add 2 tsp peanut oil to fry pan, add tofu, season with chili flakes, salt and pepper.
Bring the pan to medium heat and cook until edges the tofu are a light golden brown and crispy.
Add tofu to salad bowl.
Dress and toss to combine. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Heat olive oil on medium heat in deep skillet. Saute onion along with bay leaf till onion becomes translucent.
Add in garlic and cook for a minute. Stir in tomato paste and cook for 3 minutes.
Add in red chili flakes, salt, pepper, cumin, vinegar and combine everything.
Add in the wine and cook for 3 minutes. Add in mussels along with the chicken broth. Cook for 5 minutes till all mussels are open.
Remove the mussels to a separate pot or bowl. To the sauce, stir in butter and cook for a few minutes till the sauce thickens up.
Add back in mussels along with parsley and scallions. Stir well.
Soak the thinly sliced potatoes in a bowl of cold water with white vinegar for about 30 minutes. This helps to remove excess starch and adds vinegar flavor.
After soaking, drain the potatoes and pat them dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
Preheat your air fryer to 370°F.
In a bowl, toss the dried potato slices with olive oil until evenly coated.
Arrange the potato slices in a single layer in the air fryer basket, making sure they are not overlapping.
Air fry the potato slices at 370°F for 12-15 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Once cooked, remove the potato chips from the air fryer and immediately sprinkle them with salt to taste.
Let the chips cool slightly before serving.
Enjoy your homemade Air Fryer Salt and Vinegar Potato Chips as a delicious snack!
In a large pot add a pinch of salt and bring water to a boil. Stir in the shrimp, cover, and remove the pot from heat.
Let sit for 5 minutes, until all the shrimp are cooked through to a bright pink shade.
In a large salad bowl, toss the spinach, arugula, tomatoes, avocados, and shrimp.
Drizzle with salt, garlic powder, olive oil, vinegar and sesame seeds.
Preheat oven on broiler setting.
To Make Hollandaise: Fill the bottom of a double boiler part-way with water. Make sure that water does not touch the top pan.
Bring water to a gentle simmer. Whisk together egg yolks, lemon juice, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, and 1 tablespoon water.
Add the melted ghee to egg yolk mixture 1 or 2 tablespoons at a time while whisking yolks constantly.
Continue whisking until all ghee is incorporated. Whisk in salt, then remove from heat. Place a lid on pan to keep sauce warm.
To Poach Eggs: Fill a large saucepan with 3 inches of water. Bring water to a gentle simmer, then add vinegar.
Carefully break eggs into simmering water, and allow to cook for 2 1/2 to 3 minutes.
Yolks should still be soft in center. Remove eggs from water with a slotted spoon and set on a warm plate.
While eggs are poaching, brown the bacon in a medium skillet over medium-high heat and toast the English muffins on a baking sheet under the broiler.
Top each one with a slice of bacon, followed by one poached egg. Drizzle with hollandaise sauce. Sprinkle with chopped chives and serve immediately.
Whisk water, vinegar, oil, salt, and pepper together in a large bowl until smooth, add cucumbers, tomatoes, parsley, radish and onion and stir to coat.
Cover bowl with plastic wrap, refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
Combine 1 tbsp white vinegar in skillet filled with about 2 inches simmering water.
Crack 4 large eggs into separate small bowls/ramekins; season each bowl with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Gently slip first egg into simmering water at the 12 o'clock position. Adjust heat as needed to maintain gentle simmer.

Set timer for 4 ½ minutes:
- After 30 sec, add second egg at 3 o'clock.
- After another 30 sec, add third egg at 6 o'clock.
- After another 30 sec, add fourth egg at 9 o'clock.
Remove first egg after 1 ½ min left on timer using fish spatula/slotted spoon; place on towel-lined plate and blot dry. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Repeat procedure every 30 sec clockwise for remaining eggs.
Toast two slices of whole-grain bread; drizzle each slice with 1 tsp olive oil.
Place 2 prepared eggs on each toast slice and serve immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, white vinegar, minced garlic, bay leaf, and black peppercorns.
Place the diced chicken thigh in a shallow dish or bowl, and pour the soy sauce mixture over it. Marinate the chicken for at least 15 minutes, or up to 1 hour in the refrigerator.
In a small skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Add the marinated diced chicken thigh (reserving the marinade) and sear until golden brown on all sides, about 5-6 minutes.
Pour the reserved marinade and water into the skillet with the diced chicken.
Cover the skillet and let the chicken simmer over low heat for about 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender, and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Once the chicken is cooked, remove it from the skillet and place it on a serving plate.
Increase the heat to medium-high and let the sauce reduce for a few more minutes.
soybean sprouts: Boil the noodles in salted water for 3 mins until soft. Drain and rinse in cold water. To make the sauce, mix soy sauce, fish sauce and lime
Heat a wok with half the oil. Add the prawns and spring onions, and cook, stirring quickly, for 1 min or until the prawns turn pink.
Push to the side and add the remaining oil. Add the egg and let sit for 30 secs, then scramble until cooked. Add bean sprouts.
Add the noodles and sauce, and cook, stirring continuously, for 3 mins or until everything is hot.
Boil some water in a large saucepan. Add 1 cup cold water and the udon noodles. Cook for 2 minutes or until al dente. Drain and leave in the colander.
Heat oil, add the onion and bean sprouts and saute for 5 mins until softened. Add spring onion and saute for 1 more minute.
Pour in the remaining oil and the noodles. Add soy sauce and mix.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add noodles and cook about 2 to 4 minutes, drain.
In a large skillet or wok over high heat with a small amount or oil, cook carrot, bean sprouts and garlic.
Mix in salt, broth, water, soy sauce and oyster sauce, stir. Pour over noodles and toss lightly.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Cook the egg sunny side up to desired doneness.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and add the vegetable oil.
Add the ground beef to the skillet and cook, breaking it apart with a spoon, until it's browned and cooked through. Drain any excess fat.
In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, sesame oil, and garlic powder to create a sauce.
Pour the sauce over the cooked ground beef and stir to coat. Cook for an additional minute, then remove the beef from the skillet and set it aside.
In the same skillet, add the sliced carrots and cook for about 2-3 minutes until they start to soften. Remove them from the skillet and set them aside.
Repeat the process with the sliced mushrooms, cooking for about 2-3 minutes until they become tender. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the baby spinach and cook briefly until it wilts, about 1-2 minutes. Remove and set aside.
To assemble your Bibimbap, start by placing the cooked rice in a bowl.
Arrange the cooked ground beef, carrots, cucumber, mushrooms, bean sprouts, and wilted spinach on top of the rice in separate sections.
Top your Bibimbap with a fried egg, cooked sunny-side up.
Garnish with sesame seeds.
First, we'll bring a pot of water to boil.
Boil the noodles for about 1-2 minutes, and work to spread the noodles apart with chopsticks the entire time.
Then, we'll drain the noodles into a colander.
The goal is to get the noodles to be pretty dry, so shake the colander a few times to get excess water.
Continue separating the noodles with chopsticks for 30-60 seconds. Cover the colander with a plate.
This helps the noodles get to the perfect texture, allowing them to cook a bit longer without introducing excess moisture.
For the chicken, Cut the chicken into little strips. Place the strips in a bowl. Marinate the chicken with cornstarch and water.
Using our hands, we'll mix and massage the bowl for 20-30 seconds.
We'll also crack and beat eggs. For the shrimp, as long as they're peeled and deveined, we don't have to do anything to them.
After washing our vegetables, we'll be cutting carrots, onions, and celery into slices and strips.
For bell pepper, use several different colors of bell peppers, but if you want to keep it simple, you can just use an entire, single bell pepper.
Chop green onions  into roughly 1 inch pieces. We'll also be adding bean sprouts later as well.
The amounts and types of vegetables are really up to you. Anything goes with Singapore Noodles (with some caveats):
Try to avoid veggies with higher water content (like bok choy), since this will lead to a more soggy dish.
Try to avoid having an excessive amount of vegetables, since even lower water content vegetables will leak out water.
We'll create our sauce by mixing curry powder, oyster sauce, salt and water.
As written, the amount of curry powder my dad uses is pretty mild, so if you want a stronger flavor and/or more yellow finish, feel free to increase the amount of curry powder.
Curry powder is absolutely essential for Singapore noodles.

With so many options, the type of curry powder you use for this dish really impacts the final flavor profile, so you might want to experiment with several options until you land on something you love.
When cooking this dish, along with other noodle stir-fries, we'll want to get our wok really hot before adding oil.
Heat the wok for 2-3 minutes on high heat. While we wait, we'll continue loosening up our noodles. Once the wok is hot enough, add 1-2 tsp of oil.
Pour in the eggs: cook the eggs very gently, leaving the first side intact for 20-30 seconds before starting to flip and scramble the eggs.
We want the eggs to be really soft. In total, we're cooking the eggs for about a minute here, and tossing them back in later.
Transfer the eggs onto a plate, add oil: Next, we'll add another tbsp of a high smoke point oil like oil, and roll it around the pan.
Cook the chicken + shrimp: We'll be cooking the chicken now, so remix the chicken + marinade around with chopsticks for a few seconds.
Cook the chicken for 30-45 seconds before adding the shrimp.
Add the shrimp, and cook both meats until the shrimp starts to turn orange, and the chicken starts to turn white.
Transfer the meat onto a plate, add 1 tbsp of oil. Cook the vegetables for about 60-90 seconds.
Transfer the vegetables onto a plate, add 1 tbsp of oil. Cook the noodles for about 1-2 minutes before flipping them.
After flipping, cook the noodles for another 2-3 minutes, occasionally moving them around with your chopsticks.
Add the sauce and cook the noodles for another 2 minutes before adding everything else back in.
Add everything else in: Over the course of about 2 minutes, we'll be progressively adding the vegetables, followed by the chicken and shrimp, the char siu, bean sprouts, and lastly, some eggs.
Evenly mix the ingredients in well with the noodles. Add sesame oil, and mix everything for another 30-45 seconds.
In a frying pan, with the melted ghee, brown the duck fillets for 4 to 5 minutes over high heat, turning them over.
Salt and pepper.
Remove and keep them warm.
Wash and dry the zucchini, without peeling them, and cut them into thin slices using a mandolin or a sharp knife.
Steam them for a few minutes, keeping them “al dente”.
Rinse, sponge and thin out the tarragon.
After discarding the cooking ghee, deglaze the pan with the balsamic vinegar.
Add cracked pepper.
Return the duck aiguillettes with the rendered juice to the pan and let them caramelize lightly.
Add the zucchini to warm them up and coat them with the sauce.
Season with salt and pepper and divide them between hot plates.
Serve immediately sprinkled with tarragon and fingerling potatoes. Top with bean sprouts.
Prepare rice noodles according to package directions, taking care they are a slightly crunchy/al dente because they will cook more in the sauce.
Rinse noodles in cold water, drain, and toss in with 1 tsp sesame oil. Set aside.
Add all of the Sauce Ingredients to a medium mixing bowl. Whisk together and set aside.
While rice noodles are soaking, heat 1 tbsp oil a deep large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
Once very hot, add chicken and let cook undisturbed for 1 minute then continue to stir fry just until cooked. Transfer to a plate. Wipe out the pan.
Heat one tbsp oil in the same skillet and heat over medium-high heat. Add bell peppers and shallots and sauté for 1 minute.
Add garlic and sauté for 1 additional minute.
Reduce heat to medium and pour beaten eggs over vegetables and cook, stirring constantly, just until scrambled.
Add sauce and noodles and increase heat to medium-high.
Toss until evenly combined and continue to stir and cook until noodles are al-dente 1-2 minutes.
Stir in chicken, bean sprouts, green onions and heat through. Taste and add additional chili sauce to taste. Top with cilantro and serve.
Place the veg stock, garlic and ginger in a blender and whizz until smooth.
If your blender isn't big enough, do this in two batches and make sure to keep the lid on tight.
Pour the blended stock into a medium-sized pot and bring to the boil.
Once it's boiling, add the noodles and cook as per the packet instructions.
Chop the button mushrooms or roughly tear the oyster mushrooms.
Add the oil to a frying pan set on a high heat, then add the mushrooms along with the soy sauce and cook for 3 minutes, stirring regularly.
Once the noodles are cooked, remove them from from the stock with a slotted spoon and divide them between four large bowls, then pour over the broth.
Now layer up the bowls by placing some of the red pepper strips, grated carrot, red cabbage and spring onions in each bowl, along with some beansprouts.
Each bowl should be very colourful and rainbow-like in terms of colour.
Top with the fried mushrooms, then top each bowl with a sprinkle of sesame seeds and serve with a lime wedge.
Add boiled eggs, 1 per serving.
In a small saucepan, combine the low-sodium beef broth, cinnamon stick, whole cloves, fennel seeds, and coriander seeds.
Heat the broth mixture over medium-low heat until it comes to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer gently for about 10-15 minutes to infuse the flavors. Remove the spices if preferred or leave them in for a stronger flavor.
While the broth is simmering, prepare the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Usually, you'll need to soak them in hot water for a few minutes until they soften. Drain and set aside.
Thinly slice the raw beef sirloin or eye of round.
Once the broth is ready, strain it to remove the spices if you left them in.
Place the cooked rice noodles in a serving bowl.
Arrange the thinly sliced raw beef over the noodles.
Pour the hot broth over the beef and noodles. The heat from the broth will partially cook the beef.
Top your Homemade Pho with thinly sliced onions, fresh bean sprouts, cilantro leaves, basil leaves, mint leaves, and sliced jalapeño peppers.

Wash the bean sprouts in cold water, removing any bean husks. Place them in a pot and add about 1 cup water. Do not fill the pot. Bring to a boil and cook for about 5 minutes. Remove from heat, rinse in cold water, and drain.
Add green onions, garlic, toasted sesame seeds, sesame oil, salt, and chili powder and combine thoroughly, adjusting the salt and chili powder to taste. Serve warm or chilled.
Arrange the mixed raw vegetables and boiled egg on a plate.
In a separate bowl, combine the peanut sauce, chili paste (if using), and lime juice. Mix well.
Drizzle the peanut sauce mixture over the vegetables and egg.
Sprinkle a pinch of salt over the top, if desired.
Toss the salad gently to coat the vegetables and egg with the peanut sauce.
Serve your Gado Gado immediately.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Meanwhile beat together the eggs, bean sprouts, half of the green onions and garlic powder in a bowl until well-combined.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat, and scoop about 1/2 cup of the egg mixture into the skillet to make a patty.
Fry until golden brown, about 4 minutes per side, and repeat with the remaining egg mixture. Set the patties aside.
Whisk together the chicken broth, vinegar, and soy sauce in a saucepan over medium-low heat until the sauce simmers and thickens, about 5 minutes.
Spoon the sauce over the patties and sprinkle with sesame seeds and green onions.
Boil the egg to prefered doneness in a pot of water. Slice.
Wash and cut the mixed raw vegetables into bite-sized pieces.
In a small bowl, whisk together raw peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, honey, and chili flakes until well combined.
Arrange the mixed vegetables on a plate and top with the sliced hard-boiled egg.
Drizzle the dressing over the vegetables and egg.
Sprinkle crushed peanuts on top for extra crunch and flavor.
Combine all the ingredients in Chili Paste and blend well using a food processor.
Heat a wok and add "tumis" or stir fry the Chili Paste until the oil separates from chili. Set aside.
In a small bowl, combine all the ingredients of the Sauce together. Set aside.
Heat up a wok on high heat and add 1 tbsp of olive oil. Add the garlic, 3 tbsp of Chili Paste, potatoes, bean curd and squid.
Stir fry until fragrant. Add the yellow noodles and the Sauce and continue stirring.
Push the noodles to the side of the wok. Add the remaining ½ tbsp olive oil and crack the eggs on top of the oil.
Scramble the eggs and mix in with the noodles. Add the bean sprouts and quick stir for about 1 minute.
Dish out and garnish with sliced lettuce leaves and lime wedges. Squeeze some lime juice all over the noodles before serving.
Heat 2 tbsp of olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add brussels sprouts, salt, pepper, paprika and basil and cook, stirring, for 10 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix some salt, the remaining oil, sesame seeds and lemon juice.
Mix together salad  sprouts and brussels sprouts and coat with dressing. Serve immediately.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add the bean sprouts, and cook uncovered until tender yet still crisp, about 15 seconds.
Drain in a colander, then immediately immerse in ice water for several minutes until cold to stop the cooking process.
Whisk soy sauce, sesame oil, chile powder, garlic, and sesame seeds together in a large bowl.
Stir in bean sprouts and toss until well coated with the sauce.
Sprinkle with green onions and season with rice wine vinegar. Refrigerate before serving.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Add the bean sprouts, and cook uncovered until tender yet still crisp, about 15 seconds.
Drain in a colander, then immediately immerse in ice water for several minutes until cold to stop the cooking process. Drain well, and set aside.
Whisk soy sauce, sesame oil, chile powder, garlic, and sesame seeds together in a large bowl.
Stir in bean sprouts and toss until well coated with the sauce. Sprinkle with green onions and season with rice wine vinegar. Serve immediately.
Soften the vermicelli noodles in a large bowl by covering with boiling water and soaking for 3-4 minutes or until tender.
Rinse under cold water, drain, and add to a large bowl.
Add the shredded carrots, cucumbers, bean sprouts and chopped cilantro to the noodles.
In a glass jar fitted with a lid or a bowl, mix together the fish sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, garlic and crushed red pepper.
Pour ¾ of the dressing over the noodles and toss to coat.
Sprinkle with more cilantro and green onion and a squeeze of lime if desired.
Salad can be made ahead and refrigerated overnight, however if making ahead, add the cilantro just before serving.
Place the rice and water in the rice cooker to soak.
In a sauté pan, quickly saute the pork belly (or bacon), kimchi, and bean sprouts.
Add to the rice and water and mix to combine.
Close the rice cooker and cook as you normally would.
After rice is done, stir the rice to make sure everything is evenly distributed and portion into bowls.
Cook star anise and cinnamon in a large skillet over medium until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Transfer to a 6-quart slow cooker. Cut 1 onion in half; place in skillet, cut side down; add ginger. Thinly slice remaining onion, and set aside.
Cook onion and ginger over medium, undisturbed, until onion is charred on cut side, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer to slow cooker. Increase heat under skillet to medium-high.
Add short ribs; cook until browned on all sides, 14 to 16 minutes. Transfer to slow cooker. Remove skillet from heat; discard drippings.
Pour 1 cup water into skillet, scraping to loosen browned bits. Pour through a strainer into slow cooker; discard solids. Stir in beef stock and salt.
Cover and cook on low 8 hours. Remove short ribs from slow cooker. Pour broth through a strainer lined with cheesecloth into a bowl; discard solids.
Skim and discard fat from surface of broth; pour broth back into slow cooker. Remove meat from bones of short ribs; shred meat, and return to slow cooker.
Stir in fish sauce and hoisin sauce. Turn slow cooker to high; cover to keep hot. Cook noodles according to package directions.
Place 1/2 cup cooked noodles in each of 3 bowls. Top each with 1 ounce raw sirloin and 1 1/3 cups hot broth.
Top each with 1/3 cup short rib meat and 1/3 cup thinly sliced onion. Sprinkle with bean sprouts, basil. Serve with lime wedges; garnish with Fresno chile.
english cucumber: In a bowl, combine the (drained) tuna, mayo, sliced onion, with some salt and pepper, to taste.
Mix until well combined.
Next, cut sheets of nori into quarters.
Place nori shiny side down, place about a tbsp of the tuna mixture on the lower corner of the nori, a stick or two of carrots and cucumber, a slice of serrano, and few sprigs of cilantro.
Roll up the hand roll into a cone shape, starting at the lower left-hand corner sometimes, the nori doesn't seal.
Repeat for the other sheets and serve immediately!
Enjoy!
Boil salted water, add the pasta to cook for 3-5 min.
Drain the pasta under cold water until it is no longer hot, you can drizzle a little olive oil on top to coat to keep it from sticking.
Transfer the pasta to a large mixing bowl and add the rest of the ingredients.
Toss to combine.
Drizzle the vinaigrette on top and toss until all of the ingredients are evenly coated with the dressing.
Whisk all ingredients together until combined.
Take the steak out of your fridge and evenly coat both sides with the salt and pepper.
Add all the salad ingredients to a salad bowl.
Heat a cast iron or heavy-bottomed pan over high heat.
Drizzle the avocado oil over both sides of the steak.
When the pan starts to smoke, add the steak and let it cook undisturbed for 2 minutes.
Flip the steak over and let it cook for 2-3 minutes more.
Transfer the steak to a cutting board and let it rest.
While the steak is resting, whisk the salad dressing ingredients in a small bowl.
Thin the dressing with up to a tbsp of water, as needed.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss.
Thinly slice the steak across the grain and serve it over the salad.
Serve and enjoy with bread.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add the salt and edamame and boil for 3 minutes, then drain.
Blend edamame, cucumber, onion, avocado, jalapeno pepper, garlic, white balsamic vinegar, lime juice, salt, and black pepper in a blender until smooth.
Chill before serving.
Prepare the dressing by whisking together extra-virgin olive oil, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, oregano, basil, garlic, honey and salt in a medium mixing bowl, set aside.
Cook pasta to al dente according to package instructions, bring salted water to a boil, let the pasta cook for 3-5 min.
Drain and place on a tray until cool.
Meanwhile add cucumber, tomatoes, onions, black olives, bell pepper and parsley to a large bowl.
Add cooled pasta to bowl, whisk dressing once more then pour over mixture in bowl.
Toss then add in feta and toss again.
Thin mixture with up to ¼ cup water.
Chill up to 1 day if desired or serve right away.
Wash rice in a large bowl under cold water for 2-5 minutes until the water runs clear.
Allow to soak for at least 5 minutes.
Boil 3.½ cups of water in a large sauce pan, pour rice and let simmer on low heat for 10 minutes or until the water has dried into the rice and the rice is soft.
Place 2 tablespoons of butter on the rice to melt with the heat off, Cover and set aside.
Combine lemon juice, parsley, garlic, and oregano in large plastic food storage bag.
Pierce chicken with fork several times and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Add to bag and coat with dressing, marinate 20 minutes or up to two days in the fridge.
Remove chicken from bag. Grill or broil for about 5-6 minutes per side.
Or, cook in a grill pan over medium-high heat, 5 to 6 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Place the tomatoes, cucumber, olives, and onion; set aside.
Place the oil, measured lemon juice, oregano, measured salt, and pepper to taste in a small bowl and whisk to combine.
Pour over the salad and toss to combine. Sprinkle with the feta and serve.
In a large bowl, combine the cucumbers, tomatoes, mozzarella, red onions, beets, and basil.
Whisk together the olive oil, red wine vinegar, garlic, and honey.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Add to the salad and gently toss to combine.
Roll ham and serve with salad.
Add the diced vegetables to a salad bowl or mixing bowl, along with chopped parsley, crumbled feta cheese and drained and rinsed canned chickpeas.
Pour over olive oil and lemon juice, along with the salt and pepper. Stir to combine the ingredients evenly, and serve.
coconut extract: Prepare oatmeal according to package directions on stove top or in microwave, using oats and water.
Peel and slice banana in half. In a separate bowl, mash half banana with a fork and stir into the oatmeal along with coconut extract. Stir in honey. Slice the other half of banana into slices.
Top oatmeal with coconut and banana slices.
basil leaves: In a bowl, arrange the arugula as the base for your salad.
Slice the strawberries and scatter them over the arugula.
Tear the fresh mozzarella into bite-sized pieces and add it to the salad.
Place the fresh basil leaves on top of the other ingredients.
Slice 1/2 avocado and add it to the salad.
For the avocado dressing, remove the pit, and scoop the flesh into a blender or food processor.
Add water, olive oil, fresh lemon juice, salt, and pepper to the blender with the avocado.
Blend until the dressing is smooth and creamy. If it's too thick, you can add more water to reach your desired consistency.
Drizzle the avocado dressing over the salad.
Toss the salad gently to combine all the ingredients with the dressing.
Cook the farfalle pasta according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain, rinse with cold water, and set aside to cool.
In a large salad bowl, combine the cooled farfalle pasta, chopped lettuce, sliced strawberries, and diced avocado. Toss gently to mix.
Season the sliced chicken breast with salt and black pepper.
In a skillet over medium-high heat, add a little olive oil. Cook the thinly sliced chicken breast for about 4-5 minutes on each side.
