While the chicken is cooking, prepare the dressing. In a blender or food processor, combine all the dressing ingredients: 1 ripe avocado, fresh basil leaves, plain Greek yogurt, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, extra-virgin olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Blend until the dressing is smooth and creamy. If it's too thick, you can add a little water or more lemon juice to achieve your desired consistency.
Once the chicken is cooked, remove it from the skillet and let it cool for a few minutes. Then, slice it into thin strips.
Drizzle the creamy avocado dressing over the salad.
Gently toss the salad to coat all the ingredients with the dressing.
Top the salad with the sliced cooked chicken.
Preheat your oven to 400°F.
In a small baking dish, add the cherry or grape tomatoes. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle minced garlic over the top. Toss to coat.
Place the block or crumbled feta cheese in the center of the baking dish, surrounded by the tomatoes.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the tomatoes are burst and the feta cheese is softened and starting to turn golden brown around the edges.
While the tomatoes and feta are baking, cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Once the tomatoes and feta are done baking, carefully remove the baking dish from the oven.
Using a fork, mash the softened feta cheese and tomatoes together until well combined and creamy.
Add the cooked pasta to the baking dish with the tomato-feta mixture. Toss until the pasta is evenly coated.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with torn fresh basil leaves.
Serve immediately and enjoy!
Slice the bread in half, drizzle it with olive oil and toast it on a grill or non-stick pan, about 2-3 minutes. Take the bread off and rub it with garlic.
Open the can of sardines, place the sardines on the toasted bread. Add a drizzle of olive oil, a sprinkling of basil, if you desire, and enjoy.
Assemble caprese salad by alternating tomato and mozzarella slices on a medium platter.
Top the slices with strips of basil and drizzle with olive oil.
Sprinkle salt and pepper evenly over the salad and drizzle with balsamic glaze.
Serve immediately with bread.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta at a boil for about 12 minutes, drain. Transfer to a large bowl.
Put cheese in the bowl of a food processor, add garlic into the food processor and process until the cheese and garlic are combined.
Add spinach, basil, walnuts, lemon juice, lemon zest, and seasoning process until the mixture comes together into a thick paste. Scrape down the sides of the bowl with a spatula.

Fill a large pot with 2 quarts of water. Bring to a boil. 
Core the tomatoes and use a sharp knife to cut a small X into the bottom end of each tomato. When the water is boiling, use a slotted spoon to lower the tomatoes into the water.
Peel off the tomato skins from the tomatoes. Cut the tomatoes crosswise in half, and squeeze out most of the seeds and juices. Chop the tomatoes into 1-inch pieces.
Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil in a large pan on medium high heat. Add the shrimp to the pan and cook for 1 minute. Turn the shrimp over in the pan. Lower the heat to medium.
Add the garlic, cook a minute more. Add the chopped tomatoes and basil and sprinkle with salt. Cook for a minute more and remove from heat.
Add a tbsp of salt to the water you used to blanch the tomatoes. Bring to a boil again and add pasta and cook for 5 minutes.
Drain the pasta and immediately add to pot with the shrimp and tomatoes. Drizzle the pasta with 2 tbsp of olive oil and then stir it with the shrimp and tomatoes.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Combine the herbs in a shallow bowl with salt and pepper and oil. Coat both sides of each chicken fillet with the oil.
Add the fillets and pan fry on each side until golden. Spoon the tomato sauce and cheese over chicken filets.
Bake for 10 minutes and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, marinate thinly sliced beef with soy sauce, oyster sauce, fish sauce. Set aside for 10-15 minutes.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and minced ginger to the skillet and cook until fragrant.
Add sliced onion and sliced bell peppers to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add marinated beef to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through.
Stir in fresh Thai basil leaves and cook until wilted.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, topped with egg, and with cooked rice on the side.
In a pot or deep skillet, combine the pasta, canned diced tomatoes (with their juices), water or vegetable broth, minced garlic, chopped basil leaves, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Place the pot over medium-high heat and bring the mixture to a boil.
Once it reaches a boil, reduce the heat to a simmer and cover the pot. Cook for about 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is al dente and most of the liquid is absorbed.
Keep an eye on it towards the end of the cooking time and add a bit more water if needed.
Once the pasta is cooked, remove it from the heat.
Serve the One-Pot Tomato Basil Pasta hot, garnished with additional chopped basil leaves and grated Parmesan cheese if desired.
Bring a large stockpot with water to boil. Cook pasta according to package directions until al dente.
While the pasta is cooking, whisk together the lemon juice, olive oil, Parmesan, salt and ground black pepper to taste in a large bowl until smooth.
Add the cooked pasta to the sauce along with ½ cup of the pasta cooking water and stir to coat the pasta with the sauce.
Garnish with some chopped basil, lemon zest and slices, and serve.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Heat oil in a heavy based skillet or pot over medium high heat.
Add curry paste (and garlic, ginger and lemongrass extras, if using) and cook for 2 to 3 minutes until it mostly "dries out"
Add chicken broth and coconut milk, mix to dissolve paste. Curry in a jar seasonings, Add 1 tsp fish sauce.  Mix then bring to simmer.
Add chicken, stir then lower heat to medium so it's bubbling gently. Cook 7 minutes.
Add eggplants, cook 5 minutes until soft. Taste sauce.
Add snow peas, cook 2 minutes until a bit softened, then stir through basil and lime juice.
Sauce should have reduced but will still be a be on the thin side, not thick - that's how it's should be. DO NOT keep simmering - sauce will darken.
Serve curry over jasmine rice or roti bread with garnishes of choice.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Slice off top of tomato, hollow out (leaving 1/2 inch thick shell), and chop pulp and set aside.
In a medium bowl, combine bread crumbs, butter, basil, salt and pepper. Next stir in cheese and tomato pulp. Fill each tomato shell and then wrap each tomato individually in foil.
Bake for 15 minutes. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 325°F.
In a bowl, combine the tomatoes, lemon juice and salt. Let marinate for 5 minutes. Drain.
In a baking dish, combine the tomatoes and oil. Bake for about 25 minutes.
Transfer to a serving dish. Refrigerate for 30 minutes or until completely chilled.
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, brown the garlic in the butter.
Place the burrata over the tomatoes and cut in half. Season with pepper and top with the basil. Serve immediately.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Add the eggs, milk, salt and pepper to a medium bowl, and beat until combined. Stir cheese into the egg mixture, and set aside.

Spray a 10 inch non-stick skillet with cooking pray, and preheat over medium low heat. Add the diced onions and garlic, and cook for 2 minutes. Place the tomato slices in the pan. Pour over the egg mixture.
Use the spatula to loosen and pull the edges inward, allowing more liquid eggs to move to the hot pan. Repeat this several times. Remove the pan from the heat.
Place the basil leaves on the top. Place the skillet in the oven for 10 minutes. Remove the skillet from the oven, and let it sit about 5 minutes to cool slightly.
Serve immediately.
In a small saucepan, combine the low-sodium beef broth, cinnamon stick, whole cloves, fennel seeds, and coriander seeds.
Heat the broth mixture over medium-low heat until it comes to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer gently for about 10-15 minutes to infuse the flavors. Remove the spices if preferred or leave them in for a stronger flavor.
While the broth is simmering, prepare the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Usually, you'll need to soak them in hot water for a few minutes until they soften. Drain and set aside.
Thinly slice the raw beef sirloin or eye of round.
Once the broth is ready, strain it to remove the spices if you left them in.
Place the cooked rice noodles in a serving bowl.
Arrange the thinly sliced raw beef over the noodles.
Pour the hot broth over the beef and noodles. The heat from the broth will partially cook the beef.
Top your Homemade Pho with thinly sliced onions, fresh bean sprouts, cilantro leaves, basil leaves, mint leaves, and sliced jalapeño peppers.
Cook gnocchi according to package directions in a large stockpot of generously-salted boiling water.
Drain, and set aside.
Meanwhile, as the gnocchi is cooking, melt the butter in a large sauté pan over medium-high heat.
Add the mushrooms and sauté for about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms are soft and slightly browned.
Then add in the cooked gnocchi and garlic and continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until the garlic is fragrant and the gnocchi is lightly toasted and browned around the edges.
Carefully add in the wine, and give the mixture a good stir.
Continue cooking for 3-4 minutes, until the wine has reduced by half.
Remove pan from the heat, stir in the fresh basil and Parmesan until combined.
Serve immediately, garnished with extra Parmesan if desired.
Put the potatoes in a large pot and add water to cover. Bring to a boil and boil until they are thoroughly cooked, about 15 minutes.
Using a slotted spoon and/or tongs, remove the potatoes, let cool slightly, then peel off the skins.
Briefly add the peas to same pot of boiling water until they are cooked, about 1 minute. Drain the peas in a colander and put the potatoes and peas into the pot.
In a small saucepan, warm the buttermilk over medium heat. When hot but not simmering, add to the pot with potatoes and half of the peas.
Using a potato masher, mash the potatoes, peas, pesto and buttermilk together and season with salt and pepper.
Scoop the mashed potatoes and peas into a serving bowl. Top with the remaining peas, green peas and basil. Serve immediately.
Using your digital scale, measure 2 cups of 90 to 95°F water into a 6-quart dough tub or large mixing bowl.
Measure 15 grams of fine sea salt, add it to the water, and stir or swish it around in the tub until it is dissolved. Add yeast. Add flour.
Mix by hand, first by stirring your hand around inside the dough tub or bowl to integrate the flour, water, salt, and yeast into a single mass of dough.
et the dough rest for 20 minutes, then knead it on a work surface with a very light dusting of flour for about 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Hold the dough for 2 hours at room temperature 70 to 74°F  for the first rise.
Shape each piece of dough into a medium-tight round, working gently and being careful not to tear the dough.
Pour the entire contents of the can of tomatoes into the blender.
Add the sea salt and pulse on the lowest speed setting very, very briefly, just until the tomatoes are blended.
Preheat the oven to 550°F for 45 minutes.
Set up your pizza assembly station. Have the sauce, oil, cheeses, and basil at hand, with a ladle or large spoon for the sauce.
Switch the oven to broil 10 minutes before loading the pizza.
To shape the pizza, put the dough ball on the floured work surface and flip to coat both sides.
Spread the ¼-⅓ cups of the tomato sauce over the dough to within ½ inch of the edge, smoothing it with the back of the spoon or ladle.
Turn off the broiler, then gently slide the pizza onto the pizza stone or steel.
Close the oven door and change the setting to bake at 550°F.
Let the pizza bake for about 4 minutes, until the rim is just starting to turn golden.
Layer ¼  of the mozzarella and 3-5 basil leaves evenly over the pizza.
Change the oven setting from bake to broil and let the pizza bake until the cheese is softly melted and the crust is golden.
Serve and eat immediately.
Combine blueberries, water, basil, lime juice, and protein powder in a blender, blend until smooth.
cod fillets: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Combine tomatoes, olives, olive oil, and basil in a bowl and mix well.
Lay parchment paper on a work surface and place 1 cod fillet in the center of each. Spoon tomato mixture evenly on top of the 4 fillets.
Drizzle with lemon juice and season with salt and pepper. Place a second sheet of parchment on top and seal the edges to make a parcel.
Repeat with the remaining fillets and tomato mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven until cod flakes easily with a fork and asparagus is tender, about 20 minutes.
Remove from the oven and carefully unwrap the parcels. Spoon onto warmed plates and serve immediately.
Marinate cod in 1/4 cup coconut aminos in a glass or ceramic bowl for 20 minutes.
Combine Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese, bread crumbs, coconut flakes, sweetener, salt, and chipotle pepper together.
Blend into a moderately fine powder in a food processor.
Mix egg yolks, cream, and remaining 2 tablespoons coconut aminos together in another bowl for egg wash.
Dredge fish fillets in the egg wash then cover completely in the bread crumb mixture until thickly coated.
Oil the air fryer and cook at 360°F until fish flakes easily with a fork, 7 to 8 minutes. Serve with lemon.
In a medium glass bowl, whisk together the oil and lime zest. Add the fish, turning to coat.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, but no more than 12 hours, turning occasionally if marinating for more than 1 hour.
When the fish is almost ready to be cooked, in a separate medium bowl, gently fold together the relish ingredients. Set aside.
Preheat the grill on medium-high. Drain the fish, discarding the marinade.
Grill for 1 to 2 minutes, or until dark grill marks appear on the fish. Rotate the fish so that the short end is facing 2 o’clock.
Grill for 1 to 2 minutes. Turn over the fish. Turn off the heat directly under the fish but keep all the other burners on medium-high.
Close the grill lid. Cook the fish until it flakes easily when tested with a fork but is still very moist.
Transfer the fish to plates. Top with the relish. Serve immediately with diced tomato.
In a large skillet, heat oil over medium high heat until hot. Add fillets and squeeze ½ of the lemon's juice over the tops.
Sprinkle with pepper to taste. Cook for 4 minutes and turn. Squeeze with the remaining lemon's juice and sprinkle with pepper to taste.
Continue to cook until fillets flake easily with a fork. Serve immediately.
Preheat conventional oven to 350°F.
Squeeze pouch to separate rice; pour into a skillet. Add water and heat; stir to separate rice if necessary.
Transfer to a bowl, add lemon juice and 1½ tsp. salt (or to taste) and stir to combine.
Sweat onions in vegetable oil over medium heat until translucent and soft.
Add tomatoes and cook, stirring occasionally until slightly softened, approximately 2 minutes.
Add onion, tomatoes, capers, parsley, and olive oil to rice and gently stir to combine.
Hold in refrigeration until ready to serve. Season cod with salt and white pepper to taste.
Heat vegetable oil in skillet over high heat. Sear fish on presentation side until golden brown.
Carefully turn over fillets and place pan in oven, cooking fish to desired doneness.
To serve, plate 1 cup rice salad with 1 cod fillet.
Garnish cod with 1 tsp tapenade.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Combine tomatoes, olives, onion, olive oil, basil, and garlic in a bowl and mix well.
Lay 4 sheets of aluminum foil on a work surface and place 1 cod fillet in the center of each. Spoon tomato mixture evenly on top of the 4 fillets.
Drizzle with lemon juice and season with salt and pepper. Place a second sheet of foil on top and seal the edges to make a parcel.
Repeat with the remaining fillets and tomato mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven until cod flakes easily with a fork, about 20 minutes.
Remove from the oven and carefully unwrap the parcels. Spoon onto warmed plates and serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Lightly oil the bottom of a cast iron skillet. Place the fish in the skillet. Season with salt and pepper.
Combine the lemon juice, mayonnaise, garlic powder and onion powder. Spoon the mixture over the fish.
Combine the breadcrumbs and Parmesan. Sprinkle it all over the fish. Drizzle the melted butter over the top.
Bake until fish is cooked through and topping is golden brown, about 20. Serve warm.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Brush both sides of the fillets with oil. Place in a greased baking dish and then sprinkle with salt and pepper. Pour orange juice over the fish.
Bake, covered until fish just begins to flake easily with a fork or for 17-20 minutes.
Boil rice in a separate bowl for 5 minutes on high flame or until soft. Serve rice with salsa and cherry tomatoes on top. Garnish with lemon.
Preheat oven to 400°F.
To make a pie crust, mix flour, butter, salt, and water. Roll out dough, place in a pie dish and bake for 15 minutes.
Put the fish into a baking dish that's been sprayed with nonstick cooking spray. Season with salt and pepper.
Combine the milk, cheese, lemon juice, eggs, onion, and spinach in a bowl. Mix well. Spread the mixture over the fish and put it into pie crust.
Bake in oven for about 20 minutes.
Heat oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Stir in onion and bell pepper. Cook and stir until onion is tender, about 5 minutes.
Mix in curry powder, and continue to cook and stir 2 to 5 minutes. Blend cream into the onion mixture, and simmer until thickened.
Mix in cod, cooking 3 to 5 minutes, until easily flaked with a fork. Season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper and parsley.
Heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan.
Add the onion and fry for 5 minutes until softened.
Add all the remaining ingredients (except the cod), season with black pepper, bring to the boil, leave uncovered and simmer gently for 15 minutes.
Lay the cod on top of the sauce, cover, and continue to simmer for a further 15 minutes until the fish is cooked.
Remove the bay leaves.
In a medium pot, combine the rice, saffron, a big pinch of salt, and 1 cup of water. Heat to boiling on high.
Separate the kale leaves from the stems; discard the stems, then roughly chop the leaves.

Once boiling, place the chopped kale on top of the rice. Reduce the heat to medium. Cover and cook, without stirring, until the water has been absorbed and the rice is tender. Turn off the heat and stir to combine.
Blend the parsley, onion powder, garlic powder and smoked paprika in the blender.
While the rice and kale cook, pat the fish dry with paper towels. Season on both sides with salt, pepper, and enough of the spice blend to coat.
In a medium pan, heat 1 tbsp of olive oil on medium-high until hot. Add the seasoned fish. Cook 2 to 3 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked through.
To the pan of reserved fond, add the juice of 2 lemon wedges, 1 tbsp  of water, red pepper flakes.
Cook on medium, stirring constantly and scraping up any fond,  until combined and the liquid is slightly reduced. Turn off the heat and stir in the butter.
To the pot of cooked kale rice, stir in the cream. Serve the cooked fish over the finished kale rice. Top the fish with the sauce and serve.
Season the white fish chunks with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet or a saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion, red bell pepper, and green bell pepper to the skillet. Sauté for about 3-4 minutes until the vegetables start to soften.
Stir in the minced garlic, paprika, and cayenne pepper. Sauté for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes (with their juice), low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth, and light coconut milk to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer and let it cook for about 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Gently add the seasoned white fish chunks to the simmering broth. Cook for about 3-4 minutes, or until the fish is opaque and cooked through.
Add the lime juice into the stew and stir to incorporate.
Taste and adjust the seasoning with more salt, pepper, or cayenne pepper if needed.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Cut one lemon quarter into 1/4-inch thick slices remove any rind and quarter each slice. Juice the other half.
Combine cod, feta cheese, 1/2 diced bell pepper, zucchini, onion and lemon slices in a mixing bowl.
Mix in oregano and season with salt and pepper. Add lemon juice and olive oil; toss gently to coat all ingredients.
Stuff bell peppers with the cod mixture. Wrap all bell peppers together in heavy-duty aluminum foil, creating a sealed pouch.
Bake in the oven until peppers are tender and cod is cooked through, about 45 minutes. Remove from the oven and let sit for 5 minutes before serving.
Make beer batter: Combine flour, cornstarch, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
Blend beer and egg in a separate bowl, then quickly stir into flour mixture until combined with a few lumps remaining.
Make white sauce: Mix together yogurt and mayonnaise in a medium bowl.
Gradually stir in fresh lime juice until consistency is slightly runny. Season with jalapeño, capers, cayenne, oregano, cumin, and dill.
Preheat air fryer at 350°F.
Dust fish pieces lightly with flour. Dip into beer batter, then cook until crisp and golden brown.
Place fish in tortillas; top with shredded cabbage and white sauce.
Heat oven to 400°F.
Peel potatoes, and cut them into slices. Put them in a baking pan with 4 tbsp of olive oil. Season the potatoes and spread them evenly and place the pan in the oven.
Bake for about 40 minutes, checking once or twice, until the potatoes are tender when pierced with a thin-bladed knife and have begun to brown on top.
Remove potatoes from the oven and top them with the fish, drizzle with the remaining oil or butter and sprinkle with some more salt and pepper.
Broil until the fish is done, 6 to 10 minutes, depending on its thickness. Remove the fish. Serve alongside potatoes and cherry tomatoes.
Place the flour on a plate and toss the pieces of cod in it. Set aside.
Heat a nonstick frying pan on medium-high heat and add the olive oil.
Add the cod pieces and fry them 3 minutes on each side. Transfer them into a separate dish and set them aside.
Add the diced onions and garlic and mix them with the oil.
Add the potatoes, red bell pepper and cook together for about 5 minutes. Add salt and pepper to your taste and the smoked or regular paprika.
Add the can of tomatoes, olives, and bay leaf and mix together until well combined.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the sauce until the peppers are cooked, about 5 minutes.
Return the cod to the pan and allow the sauce and fish to simmer together for 2 more minutes. Taste the sauce for salt and pepper.
Combine olive oil, onion, and bell peppers in a large skillet over medium heat.
Cook gently until softened, stirring occasionally, 5 to 10 minutes.
Add plum tomatoes and lightly crush with the back of a spoon until broken apart.
Add water, then season with salt and pepper. Simmer gently until sauce is thickened, about 10 minutes.
Increase heat to medium-high. Nestle cod fillets in the sauce and spoon some sauce over top.
Cook, turning once halfway through, until fish flakes easily with a fork, 8 to 10 minutes.
On a clean cutting board, thoroughly pat both sides of fish dry. Sprinkle with salt. In a small bowl combine 2 tbsp lime juice and chilis. Set aside.
Set a paper towel lined plate aside for the fried ginger. In a deep skillet set over medium-high heat, add the oil. Saute the ginger for 30 seconds
Remove pan from heat and pour ginger and oil into a sieve set over a bowl. Scatter the strained ginger from the sieve to a plate and set aside. Reserve the oil.
Wipe the pan clean, removing any residual browned bits of crisped ginger. Add strained ginger oil back into the pan and set to medium-low heat.
Stir in coconut milk, fish sauce, the lime zest, and the remaining 1 tbsp of lime juice to the pan. Boil for 10 minutes.
Reduce the heat and stir in the bok choy. Cook for 2 minutes. Divide among bowls. Add the dish and cook for 6 minutes. Remove and add to the bok choy.
Simmer the coconut milk for an additional 3 minutes. Divide the hot poaching liquid into each bowl equally. Top with the ginger, chilies, lime, and cilantro. Serve.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a small saucepan heat olive oil over medium heat. Saute shallot and garlic for 4 minutes.
Add tomatoes, chicken stock, salt and red pepper flakes. Simmer, uncovered, for 15 minutes. Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the butter and thyme.
Drizzle the bottom of a casserole dish lightly with olive oil. Pat dry the cod really well and place in a single layer. Season with salt and pepper.
Drizzle the top lightly with olive oil and squeeze of fresh lemon. Add slices of lemon and cover tightly with aluminum foil and bake for 12 minutes.
Remove from the oven and evenly spread the tomato butter over the cod.
Cover back with aluminum foil and let stand at room temperature for 1 minute before serving.
tiger prawn: Heat oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Fry prawns in the hot oil until golden. Remove to paper towels to drain.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the chile sauce, pomegranate juice, Sriracha hot sauce,tomato sauce, soy sauce, lemon juice and lemon zest.
Pour the sauce into a wok, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Cook until the sauce has reduced by about 10 percent and has thickened slightly.
Mix the prawns into the sauce, and toss to coat. Serve on top of spinach and sprinkle with red chili pepper.
Wash prawns thoroughly and leave on shells and heads. In a bowl, mix the soy sauce, garlic, and brandy.
Arrange the prawns on a dish, and cover with the soy sauce mixture.
Fill a pot or wok about 1/2 full with water. Place a steamer basket over the water, and bring water to a boil.
Place the dish with the shrimp onto the steamer basket. Cover wok, and steam prawns for 10 minutes, until opaque. Serve hot.
Peel, devein and roughly chop half the prawns. Place in a bowl with mango and avocado.

Grate the zest of half a lime, then juice both limes. Place the lime zest and juice in a small bowl with the coriander, chilli and oil. Whisk to combine and season well. Pour over prawn mixture and toss to combine.
Divide lettuce among plates, fill with the salad and serve with the remaining prawns.
Prepare the Aromatics & Vegetables: Chop the aromatics and vegetables as indicated in the “Ingredients” section.
Clean and rinse the prawns: Peel and devein the prawns and rinse thoroughly. (Pro-tip: Buy already peeled and deveined prawns to save time)
Sichuan Stir-fry Sauce: Combine all the sauces in a medium bowl and stir with a spoon to combine.
Cook the noodles: Cook the noodles according to package instructions in a large wok filled with boiling water. Drain and set aside.
Wipe out the wok with a paper towel and set it back on the stovetop. Cook the aromatics: Heat oil, chili oil and oil in the wok over medium-high heat.
Once hot, add the onion, garlic, and spring onion whites and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
Add chilies & baby corn: Add the fresh red chilies and baby corn and continue stir-frying for a 1-2 minutes to combine.
Cook the prawns: Add the jumbo prawns, season with freshly cracked black pepper, and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until no longer translucent and grey.
Add the noodles & sauce: Add the noodles and immediately pour the contents of the sauce bowl over them.
Toss to combine and coat everything in the sauce. The sauce will start to thicken.
Add the bok choy: Add the bok choy and cook for 1-2 minutes until the green parts have wilted and the white parts have softened slightly.
Taste and season with more black pepper if needed, then switch off the heat.
To Serve: Divide the Sichuan Sauce Noodle Stir-fry with Jumbo Prawns in four plates and garnish with the chopped spring onion greens toasted sesame seed if desired.
rice vinegar: Preheat your oven to 375°F.
Place the salmon fillets on a baking sheet and season with salt and pepper.
Bake the salmon in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes or until it flakes easily with a fork.
While the salmon is baking, in a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, and sesame seeds to create the dressing.
In two serving plates, divide the mixed greens and arrange the halved cherry tomatoes and sliced red bell pepper.
Once the salmon is cooked, let it cool slightly and then flake it into bite-sized pieces.
Place the flaked salmon on top of the prepared salads.
Drizzle the Asian Ginger Sesame Dressing over the salads.
Garnish each plate with slices of lime.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
Prepare the tofu by draining it and cutting it into small cubes.
Preheat your oven to 350°F.

In a bowl, combine the cubed tofu with 2 tablespoons of low-sodium soy sauce, 2 tablespoons of rice vinegar, 1 tablespoon of sesame oil, and 1/2 teaspoon of grated ginger. Toss to coat the tofu evenly.
Spread the marinated tofu cubes on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Bake the tofu in the preheated oven for about 20-25 minutes or until it's lightly browned and slightly crispy.

While the tofu is baking, assemble your salad bowls. Divide 1 cup of cooked rice, 1/2 cup of thawed peas, 1/2 cup of broccoli florets, 1/2 cup of halved cherry tomatoes, and 1/2 cup of shelled edamame between two serving bowls.
When the tofu is done baking, remove it from the oven and allow it to cool slightly.
Once the tofu has cooled, distribute the baked tofu cubes evenly over the salad bowls.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.

In a bowl, combine 1/2 lime juice, lime zest, grated ginger, minced garlic, honey, salt, and black pepper. Add the chicken cubes to the marinade and toss to coat. Let marinate for at least 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

While the chicken is marinating, prepare the peanut sauce. In a small bowl, whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, rice vinegar, lime juice, grated ginger, and red pepper flakes (if using). Add water gradually to reach desired consistency.
Preheat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated chicken cubes onto the soaked bamboo skewers.
Grill the chicken kabobs for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked through and lightly charred.
Once cooked, remove the chicken kabobs from the grill and let them rest for a few minutes.
Serve the grilled lime-ginger chicken kabobs with peanut sauce on the side for dipping.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a wok or skillet, heat ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add pork pieces to the wok, cooking until they are browned and cooked through (about 3-4 minutes).
Toss in diced bell peppers, pineapple chunks, and diced onion, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together rice vinegar, low-sodium soy sauce, tomato paste, and cornstarch.
Pour the sauce over the pork and vegetables, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey, sesame oil, minced ginger, minced garlic, red pepper flakes, and cornstarch. Set the sauce aside.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken pieces and stir-fry until they're no longer pink and are lightly browned.
Pour the sauce over the chicken and stir well.
Cook for a few more minutes until the sauce thickens and everything is heated through.
Serve the General Tso's chicken over the cooked rice.
In a small bowl, stir together the soy sauce, mirin, and sesame oil. Divide into two equal parts. Stir the rice vinegar into one part and set aside as a dipping sauce.
Spread the sesame seeds out on a plate. Coat the tuna steaks with the remaining soy sauce mixture, then press into the sesame seeds to coat.
Heat olive oil in a cast iron skillet over high heat until very hot. Place steaks in the pan, and sear for about 30 seconds on each side.
Serve immediately, garnished with lemon slices.
In a wok or skillet, heat ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add shrimp to the wok, cooking until they turn pink and opaque (about 2-3 minutes).
Toss in broccoli florets and sliced red bell pepper, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and crushed Szechwan peppercorns.
Pour the sauce over the shrimp and vegetables, stirring to coat evenly. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken pieces and potato cubes to the skillet. Cook until the chicken is browned and the potatoes are slightly tender, about 5-6 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, gochujang, sesame oil, rice vinegar, minced garlic, minced ginger, and water.
Pour the sauce mixture over the chicken and potatoes in the skillet.
Stir well to coat the chicken and potatoes evenly with the sauce.
Cover the skillet and let it simmer for about 10-12 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the potatoes are tender.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving plate.
Garnish with sliced green onions.
Serve the Korean spicy chicken and potato hot and enjoy!
Heat a skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the  oil and let it heat for about 30 seconds.
Add the minced garlic and ginger to the hot oil. Stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the trimmed green beans to the skillet. Stir-fry the green beans for about 2-3 minutes until they start to blister and turn bright green.
In a small bowl, mix together the low-sodium soy sauce, red pepper flakes, rice vinegar, and water.
Pour the sauce mixture over the green beans and stir to coat them evenly.
Reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the skillet, and let the green beans cook for an additional 3-5 minutes. They should be tender yet slightly crisp.
If you prefer your green beans softer, you can cook them for an additional 1-2 minutes with the lid on.
Taste the green beans and adjust the seasoning with a pinch of salt if needed.
In a small bowl, mix together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sriracha sauce, sesame oil, minced garlic, minced ginger, lemon juice, and chopped fresh parsley.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook until pink and opaque, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Pour the sauce mixture over the cooked shrimp in the skillet.
Stir well to coat the shrimp evenly with the sauce.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes to heat through and allow the flavors to meld.
Remove from heat and transfer to a serving plate.
Serve the sweet and spicy shrimp hot.
Cook the gnocchi according to package instructions until they float to the surface. Drain and set aside.
In a bowl, mix together the peanut butter, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic powder, and ground ginger to make the sauce. Set aside.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and add a splash of oil.
Add the diced chicken breast to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Once the chicken is cooked, add the diced green onions to the skillet. Cook for another 1-2 minutes.
Add the cooked gnocchi to the skillet.
Pour the peanut sauce over the chicken and gnocchi mixture in the skillet.
Toss everything together until well combined and heated through.
Transfer the Peanut Chicken with Gnocchi to a serving plate.
Garnish with chopped peanuts and fresh cilantro.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar, minced garlic, minced ginger, crushed red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper.
Thread the chicken cubes, red bell pepper chunks, and onion chunks onto skewers, alternating between the ingredients.
Place the skewers in a shallow dish and pour the marinade over them, ensuring they are evenly coated. Allow them to marinate for at least 15 minutes.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Once the grill is hot, place the skewers on the grill and cook for about 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are slightly charred.
Once cooked, remove the skewers from the grill and serve immediately.
Enjoy your sweet, tangy, and spicy chicken kabobs!
In a mixing bowl, combine the ground beef, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, minced garlic, grated ginger, and black pepper. Mix well until everything is evenly combined.
Shape the mixture into small meatballs, about 1 inch in diameter.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Once the skillet is hot, add the meatballs to the skillet in a single layer, making sure they are not overcrowded.
Cook the meatballs for about 5-6 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are browned on all sides and cooked through.
Once cooked, remove the meatballs from the skillet and place them on a plate lined with paper towels to drain any excess oil.
Garnish with chopped green onions.
Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, whisk together hoisin sauce, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, grated ginger, minced garlic, red pepper flakes (if using), salt, and pepper.
Place the chicken thighs in a shallow dish or a resealable plastic bag.
Pour the hoisin glaze over the chicken thighs, ensuring they are evenly coated. Marinate for about 15-20 minutes at room temperature.
Heat an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the chicken thighs skin side down and sear for about 2-3 minutes until browned.
Flip the chicken thighs skin side up and transfer the skillet to the preheated oven.
Bake for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken thighs are cooked through, and the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Once done, remove the chicken thighs from the oven and let them rest for a few minutes.
Garnish with parsley.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and cornstarch.
Add thinly sliced beef to the bowl, ensuring each piece is coated in the mixture. Let it marinate for a few minutes.
In a wok or skillet, heat ghee or olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add the marinated beef to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 2-3 minutes).
Sprinkle toasted sesame seeds over the beef, stirring to combine.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Cook the rice in a pot of water according to the instructions on the packaging.
In a small bowl, mix together the low-sodium soy sauce, rice vinegar, hoisin sauce, and brown sugar until well combined.
Heat the vegetable oil in a pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced chicken to the heated pan and cook until it's no longer pink and starts to brown, usually about 5-6 minutes.
Add the diced bell pepper to the pan and stir-fry for an additional 2-3 minutes until it begins to soften.
Lower the heat to medium-low and add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pan. Cook for about 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Pour the prepared sauce over the chicken and bell peppers in the pan.
Toss everything together in the pan, coating the chicken and peppers evenly with the sauce. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
If using, sprinkle chopped peanuts over the Kung Pao Chicken for added crunch and flavor.
Optionally, add red pepper flakes for extra heat.
Remove the pan from the heat.
Serve with rice!
In a saucepan, combine low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth, sliced mushrooms, diced tofu, sliced bamboo shoots, minced garlic, and minced ginger.
Bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for about 5 minutes or until the mushrooms and tofu are tender.
Stir in rice vinegar, chili garlic sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and salt.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes to combine the flavors.
Top with sliced green onions.
In a bowl, mix orange juice, soy sauce, and rice vinegar.
In a wok or skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir-frying for about 30 seconds.
Add thinly sliced chicken to the wok, cooking until it's browned and cooked through (about 3-4 minutes).
Pour the orange juice mixture over the chicken, stirring to coat evenly.
Toss in bell pepper, carrot and onion, stir-frying for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables are slightly tender.
Adjust seasoning if needed.
Place pork, flour, and sesame oil in a bowl and toss to coat. Let sit for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, preheat the air fryer to 350°F.
Oil the basket of the air fryer. Place pork in the basket of the air fryer and oil top of pork.
Cook for 10 minutes. Shake the basket and cook pork until desired crispness, about 10 minutes more.
While the pork is cooking, combine sugar, vinegar, tomato sauce, and soy sauce in a saucepan over medium-high heat.
Whisk constantly until sugar is dissolved and mixture comes to a boil. Reduce heat to low and keep sauce warm until ready to use.
Transfer pork to a bowl. Pour sauce on top of the pork and toss to coat. Top with sesame seeds.
Combine the ingredients for the teriyaki marinade in small bowl and whisk together.
Cut the tempeh into triangles or dice — it really doesn’t matter; the triangles look cool but this will taste amazing either way.
Heat the teriyaki marinade in a stir-fry pan or medium skillet.
When it begins to bubble, add the tempeh and stir quickly to coat all sides.
Turn the heat up to medium-high and cook, stirring often, until the marinade is completely reduced and coats the tempeh nicely, about 8 minutes.
Continue to cook until the tempeh begins to brown here and there, another minute or two.
Drizzle in some sriracha if you’d like a spicier effect; otherwise just sprinkle in the sesame seeds.
Serve on its own as a protein-packed side dish, arrange on a rice bowl with a vegetable or two; or toss into a stir-fry at the last minute.
protein powder: Combine quinoa, oats and almonds in a pot, cover with water.
Bring to a boil and cover. Reduce heat add protein powder and simmer until quinoa and oats are soft, 10 to 15 minutes.
Serve and top with blueberries.
Heat waffle iron and grease with coconut oil.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda and protein powder. In another large bowl, stir together melted ghee, sugar, egg, egg yolk, sour cream, and vanilla.
Add mashed bananas and stir until combined. Gradually add dry ingredients to wet ingredients until just combined.
Add 1 cup batter to waffle iron. Close and cook until golden on both sides. Transfer waffle to a plate and repeat with remaining batter.
Add all ingredients to a blender and blend on high until everything is smooth. Serve immediately.
Combine pumpkin puree, almond milk, water and pumpkin pie spice in a blender. Blend until well combined, 5 to 10 seconds.
In a food processor, combine the chopped dried apricots, almond butter, and ghee.
Pulse until the mixture becomes a paste-like consistency.
Transfer the mixture to a mixing bowl.
Add the unsweetened shredded coconut, mixed seeds, unsweetened protein powder, and a pinch of salt to the bowl.
Mix everything together until well combined.
Line a small dish or container with parchment paper, leaving some excess paper hanging over the edges for easy removal.
Transfer the mixture into the lined dish and press it down firmly to create an even layer.
Refrigerate for at least 1 hour to allow the bars to set.
Once set, use the parchment paper overhang to lift the bars out of the dish.
Cut the mixture into individual protein bars.
Place frozen berries, banana, protein powder, strawberries, coconut, and chia seeds in a blender, pulse briefly to combine. Pour in orange juice, blend until smooth.
Place rolled oats, steel cut oats and salt in a medium sauce pot.
Combine the milk, water and 1½ cups chopped strawberries in a blender and blend until smooth. Pour the liquid mixture and vanilla extract into the sauce pot.
Cook oats, stirring frequently over medium heat until thickened, about 10-15 minutes. Once thickened, gently stir in remaining ½ cup diced strawberries and serve.
Layer bananas, peach slices, almond butter, and hemp seeds in a blender, pour in water. Cover and blend until smooth.
In 3 cups evently divide oats, milk, greek yogurt, chia seeds, strawberries, protein powder. Top with peanut butter and top with the remaining strawberries.
Let rest in a fridge for 2 hours to overnight.
Pour cashew milk into a saucepan and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Add oats and reduce heat to a simmer.
Stir in cocoa powder, salt, and cinnamon until well combined. Add protein powder and stir once more.
Cook until oatmeal has desired consistency, 1 to 2 minutes more. Pour oatmeal into a bowl and top with banana.
Combine coconut milk, water, orange juice, avocado, spinach, protein powder and vanilla powder in a blender. Blend until smooth.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
Line a baking dish with aluminum foil, letting foil hang down over the sides of the pan, oil pan with coconut oil.
Stir oats, wheat germ, flax seed, protein powder, cinnamon, and salt together in a bowl.
Mash bananas in a separate large bowl and stir 2 tbsp peanut butter, honey, and vanilla extract into bananas, mixing well.
Stir dry ingredients into banana mixture and spread into the prepared baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
Let cool in the pan and lift bars onto a work surface using aluminum foil as handles. Cut into bars, wrap in plastic wrap, and store in refrigerator.
Combine pumpkin puree, applesauce, apple juice, and soy milk in a blender; blend until smooth.
Blend chia seeds in a blender until powdery, 3 to 5 seconds. Add banana, eggs, protein powder, flour, cocoa powder, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and sea salt.
Blend, occasionally scraping sides down, until batter is smooth.
Heat a skillet over medium heat; add coconut oil. Pour 1/2 of the batter into the hot oil and sprinkle 1/2 of the blueberries onto the batter.
Cook until bubbles form and the edges are dry, 3 to 4 minutes. Flip and cook until browned on the other side, 2 to 3 minutes.
Repeat with remaining batter and blueberries, using more coconut oil if needed.
In a blender or food processor, add all ingredients. Blend until smooth, adding more nutmilk or water if needed to reach desired consistency. Serve immediately.
Combine all ingredients in a high-powdered blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Combine all ingredients in a high-powdered blender and blend until smooth. Serve immediately.
Combine spinach, kale, pear, banana, blueberries, yogurt, ice cubes, and ground flax seeds in a blender.
Blend on high until mixture is smooth. Mix with a wooden spoon to redistribute ingredients if needed.
Peel the ripe banana and place it in a blender.
Add almond milk to the blender.
Spoon in the chocolate protein powder.
Add chia seeds for extra texture and nutrition.
Blend the ingredients until smooth and creamy.
Pour the chocolate-banana protein smoothie into a glass.
Add the milk and half of the honey in a small sauce pan and combine well. Heat on medium heat until the milk is warm to touch.
Beat the eggs in a large bowl. Then slowly add a small amount of warm milk while whisking. While whisking, slowly add the melted butter to the mixture.

Set up a fine-mesh sieve over the bowl and add cake flour, matcha powder, protein powder, and baking powder. Sift half of the dry ingredients into the batter and whisk to combine the dry and wet ingredients together.
Sift the rest of dry ingredients and mix until there is no lump in the batter. Be careful not to overmix the batter, as it causes a rubbery crepe texture.
Cover with plastic wrap to refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Set a 12-inch non-stick frying pan on low heat. Once hot, brush with coconut oil. Pour the batter just enough to cover the bottom of the frying pan, about 1/3 cup for my pan.
Cook for 2 minutes without touching. By now you’d see the outside edge of the crepe gets a tiny bit crispier. Using a chopstick, spatula, or hand, flip the crepe.
Cook the other side for 30 seconds and transfer to a working surface lined with parchment paper. Continue this process until all the batter is used.
Whip the cream using a hand mixer, gradually adding the remaining honey.
To assemble the cake, place a single crepe on a plate. This will be the top layer when we flip. Put the freshly whipped cream in the middle and spread evenly, leaving the outer 2-inches.
Place another crepe on top and spread whip cream evenly, leaving outer ½ inch. Continue this process until the last crepe is placed on top.
Prepare a round curved shallow dish and place a large piece of plastic wrap on top. Transfer the crepe stack to the shallow dish.
Wrap the mille crepe with plastic wrap from one corner to clockwise. This will make the final cake evenly round, instead of wrap randomly.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours until the cake is set. Once chilled, remove the plastic wrap and flip onto the serving platter. Dust more matcha powder over the cake, cut it and enjoy!
